> to ~78 KB. At this size GitHub won't render it, agents can't read it whole (the
> standing rule is already "**grep it, never read it whole**"), and same-line appends by
> parallel farm agents are a recurring merge hazard. **Proposed approach (for operator

> shipped/closed epochs (the E1–E11 era, the 10.x/11.x sections) **verbatim** into
> `docs/worklist-archive/<epoch>.md`, keeping task IDs greppable across both locations;
> adopt a hard convention for live entries (a status line + wrapped follow-up bullets
> instead of one mega-line); keep this live file under ~200 KB; add a tiny max-line-length
> lint to hold it. Tracked as review finding `docs-consistency-10`. Until the operator
> approves the archive split, leave the structure as-is.

The single durable tracker. Tasks lifted from `docs/COMPLIANCE.md` (sweeps 1 & 2, 2026-06-09).
Status: `[ ]` open · `[>]` in progress · `[✓]` done. Each task carries its finding id + verdict.
Ordered by priority — security lock first, then largest structural debt, then mechanical/doc cleanup.

**Operator decisions (2026-06-09):** A1/A2 → **DELETE** the labwc/sway surface · B1/H6/H3/H4 → **BUILD/WIRE** them · C1 → **implement Phase-G** · E1 → **retarget tests to Nebula**.

- **GATED on a live multi-node mesh** (runtime-observable acceptance): **DEVMGR-9** (6),

- **NEEDS operator decision / approval / eyes:** **BROWSER-CHROME** (4, CEF bundle + lock-revision

**Bottom line:** the blind-drainable code is down to WIN10-HYBRID #31 (in flight). Everything else

## E12 — Forked-COSMIC + Magic-Mesh → one egui-native mesh desktop OS (operator 2026-06-30; 4-round/16-Q `/plan` survey; design: docs/design/cosmic-magic-mesh-egui.md)

The **headline pivot**: MCNF 12.0 "Quasar" becomes an **egui-native mesh thin-client desktop OS**. **REVISED 2026-06-30** (50-Q `/plan` survey; design: `docs/design/quasar-vdi-desktop.md` — supersedes the forked-compositor `cosmic-magic-mesh-egui.md`): the originally-proposed **fork of the COSMIC compositor is RETIRED**. Instead a **single egui shell owns the DRM/KMS seat directly (no compositor)** and **brokers VM desktops** — local **cloud-hypervisor** VMs + remote desktops on any mesh peer, rendered **egui-native** (ironrdp/vnc → an egui texture) over Nebula. The host runs **no native apps**; a browser/office/game lives **inside a VM guest**. The mesh-control surfaces (Workbench/Files/Music/Voice) are **panels in the one shell**, not separate clients. Carries forward from the first survey: **egui as the one toolkit + the `mde-egui` `Style`/Motion harness** (E12-1, landed). Locks (full table in the design doc): **egui owns DRM** (smithay DRM/GBM runner) · **VDI thin client** (fullscreen VM framed by a thin chrome bar that expands to Workbench) · **cloud-hypervisor** local VMs (virtio-gpu fast path, dual-homed mesh+LAN) · **RDP-primary / VNC-fallback** over Nebula · **sessions roam** per-peer (etcd/Syncthing) · **mackesd session-broker + vm-lifecycle workers** · **immutable bootc image** · **§8 envelope raised**, VM guests full flat-trust members · **everything in v1** (passthrough/USB/multi-mon/migration). Mesh substrate (§1), Bus (§2), crypto (§3), DoD (§7), planes (§9), build env (§10) carry forward. Build order: DRM runner → shell → VDI(RDP) → remote-over-mesh → local KVM → advanced → panels → bootc packaging → decommission iced/Carbon.

**QUASAR-CLOUD supersession (2026-07-03, refreshed 2026-07-10):** the current target hypervisor stack is **Nova/libvirt/QEMU-KVM + OVN**, not cloud-hypervisor. Older E12 cloud-hypervisor text below is retained as historical implementation context until QC-15 deletes the replaced stack; new packaging and live validation work follows `AI_GOVERNANCE.md §5` and QC-1.

## MOTION-DRM — NEXT EPIC: cohesive polished transition system for the egui DRM/KMS shell (operator 2026-07-15; questionnaire complete)

**Operator lock / prompt provenance:** this is the next execution epic from the questionnaire-complete
operator prompt. **All Carbon Design Requirements are lifted to meet this goal: Questionnaire complete.**
**Reaffirmed 2026-07-15:** the operator re-pasted this questionnaire as the next worklist epic; keep the
full prompt scope binding for MOTION-DRM after Browser page rendering/input is operational.
The goal is a native
Rust motion system with polished macOS-style continuity, adapted to immediate-mode egui and the production
direct-seat renderer. Do **not** copy Apple assets or private APIs; reproduce the behavioral principles
only. The full questionnaire prompt is folded into this epic as binding scope; do not treat this shorter
worklist form as permission to drop supplied transition categories, lifecycle cases, tests, performance
checks, or deliverables. This epic targets the production path first:

```text
egui -> egui_glow -> glow/OpenGL ES -> EGL -> GBM scanout surface -> DRM/KMS page flips
libinput + udev -> egui events
```

The normal dev path may still use `eframe + winit + wgpu + Wayland`, but this work must not depend on
Wayland, Xorg, winit, wgpu, compositor-provided animations, or a new compositor. Relevant starting points:
`crates/shared/mde-egui/src/drm.rs`, `crates/shared/mde-egui/Cargo.toml`, `mde-egui::motion`, existing
overlays/dialogs/panels/cards/view routing/input/focus/repaint scheduling.

**Browser relationship:** the Browser workspace override remains active: Browser chrome, tabs, toolbar,
menus, Bookmarks, engine controls, and page/action surfaces are governed by local Material Design 3
principles rather than Carbon wording. MOTION-DRM provides the shared production-safe motion primitives;
Browser/Chrome should be one of the first representative consumers after page render/input operation is
stable, without re-imposing Carbon on Browser or migrating unrelated shell surfaces away from `mde-egui`
discipline.

**Motion direction:** smooth and restrained by default; playful only for dialogs, launchers, cards,
popovers, and overlays. Motion communicates hierarchy, origin, destination, and state. Opening may be
expressive; closing is faster/quieter with little or no overshoot. Direct manipulation follows the pointer
immediately; springs settle after release/presentation. Interrupted animations retarget from the current
visual state, never restart from the original endpoint. Avoid decorative delays, excessive movement,
staggering that slows work, expensive blur, unbounded render work, and text scaled long enough to look soft.

**Central defaults:** small hover/focus/selection/toggle 80-140 ms; dialog/launcher/card/overlay open
140-200 ms; dialog/overlay close 100-160 ms; page/workspace 180-260 ms; list insert/remove/expand/collapse
140-220 ms; drag-release spring settling usually 180-300 ms. Keep durations/easing/springs centralized,
not scattered literals. Reduced motion is runtime-configurable: normal, reduced, and disabled if clean.
Reduced mode substitutes short fades for large motion, removes overshoot/bounce, shortens layout movement,
and preserves immediate feedback.

**Required transition categories:** full-screen page/workspace transitions; dialogs; launchers/command
palettes; anchored popovers/context overlays; cards entering/leaving/expanding/collapsing; panels opening
and closing; list insertion/removal; hover/focus/pressed/selected/toggle state changes; window-like surface
move/resize; minimize/maximize or equivalent surface-state transitions; drag-release settling/snap behavior.
Do not apply every effect everywhere at once: build primitives, then integrate representative components
with low regression risk.

**Questionnaire-complete visual contracts:** dialogs/modal overlays fade in, scale from roughly `0.96-0.98`
to `1.0`, may translate only a few logical pixels, dim the background in parallel, keep text readable, and
finish exit before state is destroyed or modal blocking is released. Launchers and command palettes animate
from their logical source or presentation edge when available, otherwise use restrained fade/scale/short
vertical movement; keyboard focus becomes predictable without waiting on decorative motion, and Escape
feels immediate while still completing a short dismissal. Popovers stay visually connected to their invoking
control, use the anchor for transform origin, avoid center-screen zooms, and retarget when the anchor moves.
Cards animate geometry, clipping, opacity, and nearby layout coherently; text must not bounce independently.
Pages/workspaces use short directional motion tied to navigation history when known, otherwise cross-fade.
Routine controls interpolate subtle opacity/fill/border/shadow/geometry state, pressed feedback begins
immediately, ordinary hover has no overshoot, and toggles/segmented selections may use a damped slide.
Dragging/resizing tracks input without lag; springs apply only after release, snap, cancel, or constraint
correction, with velocity preservation where practical and clamped extremes.

**Architecture target:** create a small centralized motion subsystem, conceptually including settings,
durations, easing, spring presets, animated scalar/position/size/rect/opacity/scale/color values,
transition phase/lifecycle (`Hidden -> Entering -> Visible -> Exiting`), stable `egui::Id`/motion IDs,
retargeting, completion detection, reduced-motion substitutions, and explicit repaint requests. Use stable
IDs or application-owned state; do not key important animation state to reorderable list indices. Keep call
sites readable: callers should not manually manage timestamps, normalized progress, repaint requests, or
rest thresholds.

**API ergonomics and presets:** centralize durations, springs, easing curves, reduced-motion substitutions,
delta-time handling, and repaint logic. Named presets must cover the project equivalents of `control`,
`panel`, `popover`, `dialog`, `page`, `layout`, and `drag_settle`, with conservative constants and a final
table of actual durations/easing/spring parameters. Do not introduce a large animation dependency unless an
existing workspace dependency already fits the architecture clearly.

**Spring requirements:** numerically stable at variable frame rates; clamp large frame deltas after stalls;
avoid NaN/inf/explosions after suspend/resume; work at 30/60/90/120/144 Hz; clear position/velocity rest
threshold; snap exactly to target at rest; retarget without discontinuity; avoid endless micro-repaints.
Default presentation spring: gentle restrained overshoot, fast response, minimal oscillation. Closing and
routine layout transitions should be critically damped or near-critically damped.

**DRM/KMS integration:** use monotonic time. While any animation is active, request another frame; once all
animations are at rest, stop continuous repainting and do not busy-spin. Preserve the current page-flip and
GBM buffer lifecycle, avoid queueing unbounded work, avoid giant animation jumps after missed/delayed page
flips, and keep input responsive during transitions. Review `drm.rs` before modifying its event loop.

**Input/focus lifecycle:** explicitly decide whether hit testing follows animated geometry, whether entry
is interactive, whether exit remains interactive, when focus transfers, and when modal input blocking
begins/ends. Modals block background interaction as soon as presentation begins and do not release it until
dismissal is sufficiently complete. Closing controls must not accept duplicate activation. Pointer capture
during drag remains correct. Rapid reversals (`open while closing`, `close while opening`, destination
change during movement, resize during page transition, remove during insertion) retarget from current visual
state instead of snapping.

**Rendering/performance constraints:** optimize for GLES over EGL/GBM. Prefer opacity, translation, scale,
clipping, and simple geometry interpolation; avoid baseline blur, large shadows, repeated full-resolution
offscreen passes, per-frame texture/render-target reallocation, unbounded translucent full-screen blending,
and arbitrary nonuniform scaling of final text rendering. Favor stable 60 FPS behavior over elaborate
effects. Before closing this epic, check idle repaint shutdown, no busy-loop while waiting for DRM page
flips, bounded work with simultaneous overlays/cards, long pause or VT-switch behavior, simulated refresh
intervals, and low input latency during transitions.

- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-0: survey + design note.** Inspect current render/event loop, view routing, overlays,
  dialogs, panels, cards, input/focus handling, repaint scheduling, and existing motion/interpolation code.
  Produce the short architectural summary and proposed design before invasive edits.
- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-1: central motion configuration and primitives.** Add the reusable motion subsystem
  with presets for control, panel, popover, dialog, page, layout, and drag-settle; scalar/2D/size/rect/
  opacity/scale/color interpolation; easing and spring values; stable IDs; lifecycle phases; retargeting;
  completion detection; reduced-motion handling.
- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-2: deterministic motion tests.** Cover interpolation endpoints, no-overshoot curves,
  spring convergence, rest detection, large-dt clamping, retargeting continuity, reversal, exit lifecycle
  completion, reduced-motion substitutions, zero-duration behavior, and no NaN/inf after long-frame stalls.
- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-3: DRM repaint scheduling integration.** Integrate animation-driven repaint requests
  into `mde-egui`'s DRM loop without busy-spinning or violating page-flip/buffer lifecycle; idle screens
  stop requesting frames; delayed page flips clamp dt; input latency stays low.
- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-4: representative component integrations.** Implement one dialog/modal transition,
  one anchored popover or launcher/command-palette transition, one panel or card expand/collapse, one
  page/workspace transition, restrained control-state transitions, and drag-release settling/snap behavior.
- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-5: reduced-motion runtime setting.** Add a project-level runtime setting that can later
  connect to config, accessibility, CLI, or system integration; not compile-time-only. Normal/reduced/
  disabled modes remain behaviorally clear and accessible.
- [ ] **MOTION-DRM-6: performance and production gates.** Check central paths for per-frame heap allocation,
  idle repaint shutdown, no unbounded work with simultaneous overlays/cards, long-pause/VT-switch behavior,
  simulated refresh intervals, input latency during transitions, and production `--features drm` build
  success. Do not claim exact performance improvements without measurements.

**Deliverables:** modified-file list, tests added/updated, commands used, remaining risks, reduced-motion
summary, and a final table of presets/durations/easing/spring parameters. Scope discipline: do not replace
the renderer, migrate DRM to wgpu, add Wayland/Xorg dependencies, rewrite the UI architecture, add a
compositor, require blur, or scatter hard-coded timing values through unrelated components.

- [✓] **E12-0: Governance rewrite + design lock land.** **As** the platform owner, **I want** `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` re-authored around E12 (Cosmic-era §4/§5/§6 archived as heritage; §1-3/§7-10 carried forward) plus this design doc committed, **so that** every downstream task has one authoritative rulebook for the egui/forked-desktop direction. **Acceptance:** AI_GOVERNANCE.md identity header reads MCNF 12.0 + names egui as the toolkit; §4 (egui/no-Carbon), §5 (forked desktop + spin), §6 (layered tiers) reflect the locks; the retired Carbon/libcosmic gates are removed from the doc's gate list; a heritage pointer to the Cosmic-era text exists. — DONE (2026-06-30, operator go-ahead): `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` rewritten to MCNF 12.0 "Quasar" (egui §4, forked-desktop §5, layered-tiers §6; §0-3/§7-10 carried forward; Cosmic-era text archived as heritage); design doc `docs/design/cosmic-magic-mesh-egui.md` landed. E12-1…E12-12 are the execution backlog.
- [✓] **E12-1: `mde-egui` harness + shared `Style`/`Visuals` (the foundation, built FIRST).** **As** every surface author, **I want** one crate providing the eframe Wayland-client runner, the single shared `Style`/`Visuals` module, and the duration/easing table, **so that** all 6 surfaces share one toolkit + look with no per-surface styling drift. **Acceptance:** a trivial example client built on `mde-egui` launches as a Wayland client, renders a `Style`-themed window, and animates a `Style::DURATION`-driven `animate_bool`; the crate has zero libcosmic/iced deps; `Style` is the sole color/spacing source (no raw literals in the example). — DONE (2026-06-30): new `crates/shared/mde-egui` lands the three foundation pieces — `runner::run_client` (eframe wgpu/Wayland client runner that installs the shared look), `style::Style` (the single-source Quasar-dark palette + 8px spacing + type scale `Visuals`, applied via `Style::install`), `motion::Motion` (FAST/BASE/SLOW table wrapping egui `animate_bool`). a11y/accesskit deferred per lock 11 (eframe `accesskit` feature intentionally off — the absence IS the deferral). `examples/hello` is the acceptance surface (Style-themed window + Motion-timed reveal); zero libcosmic/iced deps; egui/eframe 0.31 pinned. Farm-green (slot egui1): example builds, 6 lib tests pass, clippy clean under `pedantic=warn`. Live Wayland launch is the hardware-gated `/preview` (optional per §7).
- [✓] **E12-2: `mde-egui` DRM runner — egui owns the seat (no compositor).** **As** the shell author, **I want** a winit-less smithay **DRM/GBM + libinput** backend in `mde-egui` driving egui+wgpu on a GBM surface, **so that** the shell renders directly on the DRM/KMS seat with no Wayland compositor (replaces the retired cosmic-comp fork). **Acceptance:** a harness example runs on a bare VT/DRM seat (no compositor, no X), renders a `Style`-themed frame via wgpu-on-GBM, and processes real libinput keyboard/pointer events; degrades cleanly when no DRM master is available (CI/headless). — **DONE + FARM-GATED 2026-07-01 (BigBoy slot e12drm):** `crates/shared/mde-egui/src/drm.rs` (402 lines) is a complete 3-stage bare-seat backend — DRM/GBM primary-node bring-up (`open_primary_node`/`resolve_output`), EGL-on-GBM + `egui_glow` painter (the reliable bare-KMS GL path, not wgpu — see the module header), and the libinput seat + continuous page-flip present loop (Esc quits). The full native stack (`--features drm`: libdrm/gbm/khronos-egl/glow/egui_glow/input/udev) **compiles + links green** (39s cold on BigBoy) and the `headless_degrades_cleanly` test passes (9/9 lib tests) — `open_primary_node` returns the clean `DrmError::NoDrmMaster` fallback on a host with no seat, so the shell falls back to the windowed `run_client`. Both examples (`hello`, `hello_drm`) build+link under the feature. The **live-seat render + input round-trip on real DRM hardware is the hardware-gated `/preview`** (optional per §7 — same disposition as E12-1's live Wayland launch; a bare-KMS render can only be verified on a physical seat). E12-3's DRM-seat systemd boot rides this.
- [✓] **E12-3: `mde-shell-egui` — the one shell on the DRM seat.** **As** every role, **I want** the single egui shell — a thin chrome bar (peers · sessions · status) that **expands into the full Workbench**, an egui **login/lock** (PAM + mesh identity), launched by **systemd onto the seat** at boot — **so that** the platform has one shell across headless/lighthouse/workstation. **Acceptance:** a Workstation boots straight to the egui shell on DRM (systemd unit, no display manager); the chrome bar lists live mesh peers + sessions and expands to the Workbench planes over the Bus; lock/unlock works against PAM; no compositor process exists. **Composition slice DONE (E12-3a+3b `9151686`):** the six surfaces compose into the one shell (chrome → Workbench + Fleet + Music/Files/Voice dock). Remaining for E12-3: DRM-seat systemd boot (rides E12-2's DRM runner) + PAM login/lock — hardware/PAM-gated. **Workbench planes:** Fleet live (MV-6); **ThisNode + Network now live too (WB-ThisNode `b94da19`, WB-Network `4b44b34`)** — both fold the world-readable `/run/mde/mesh-status.json` snapshot (§6-clean, no mackesd IPC): ThisNode shows role/overlay-IP/presence/heartbeat/per-daemon-health/peers/leader; Network shows the overlay (IP/CIDR/cipher/leader), mesh links (per-peer presence + lighthouse chips), network services (Nebula/DNS/Syncthing), and routing (gateways/routes/default-gw). Marker was drift.)*

- [✓] **E12-4: `mde-vdi` core + RDP (ironrdp → egui texture).** **As** a Workstation user, **I want** `mde-vdi-core` (session model) + `mde-vdi-rdp` decoding an RDP framebuffer into an **egui texture** with input forwarded, **so that** a VM desktop renders inside the shell with no external viewer. **Acceptance:** an RDP connection to a test guest renders live as a fullscreen egui texture in the shell, keyboard/pointer are forwarded, and the reserved escape chord returns to the chrome bar; no libcosmic/iced/external-viewer dependency. **STATUS (reconciled 2026-07-08):** `mde-vdi-rdp` crate + RDP-decode→egui-texture core is built, reachable from the shell Desktop surface (`vdi.rs`), and live-proven against the throwaway TESTVM RDP endpoint. Note: a separate `mde-vdi-core` crate does not exist; the session model lives inside `mde-vdi-rdp`.
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mde-vdi-rdp` on `.50` slot `e12rdp` passed `61/61`.
  - **Live evidence 2026-07-08:** farm host `.90` ran `MDE_RDP_LIVE_TARGET=172.20.146.54:3389,alpine,testvm cargo test -p mde-vdi-rdp --features live-connect --test live_rdp -- --ignored --nocapture` from the synced farm checkout. The real RDP connection negotiated `Full` at `1024x768`, decoded a framebuffer into `ColorImage` (`fnv1a64=0x8d85cde11939f325`), flushed 4 fast-path input events, observed framebuffer change after input (`0xeae4f7558a008dbb -> 0x42417069c9886e03`), reconnected at the `Compressed` tier with `Rdp6` compression, and rendered the post-reconnect frame (`fnv1a64=0x1f8e8f90a24c9d7d`). This satisfies the remaining live render + keyboard/input round-trip; the endpoint is the documented TESTVM-3 Alpine+xrdp fallback because no local Windows ISO exists on the airgapped farm.
- [>] **E12-5: Remote desktop over the mesh (FIRST end-to-end milestone).** *(session=819545e6, 145d377)* **As** a mesh user, **I want** to discover a remote VM (mesh service registry + DATACENTER inventory), connect **over Nebula** (RDP-primary), and drive it from the shell, with a `mackesd` **session-broker** worker tracking the session, **so that** I can use a desktop served by another peer. **Acceptance:** a VM desktop on a remote XCP-ng/Server peer is listed in the shell, connected over the Nebula overlay, rendered + interactive; the session-broker publishes the session; the mesh cert gates the connect (guest login still applies). **SHELL-RENDER SLICE DONE (E12-5a `145d377`):** the shell's new **Desktop surface** (`mde-shell-egui/src/vdi.rs`) mounts an rdp/vnc `Session` as an egui texture — `session.frame()→ColorImage→TextureHandle→paint`, egui input forwarded via `send_input`, Esc returns to chrome, honest "No desktop connected" EmptyState. This is the **first real caller** of `mde-vdi-rdp`/`mde-vdi-vnc` (dead-lib cleared). 36 shell tests green. **SESSION-BROKER DONE (E12-5b `07671f3`):** the `mackesd` `session_broker` worker — `VdiSession` + `SessionState` machine, `reconcile(desired,observed)`, leader-gated, reusing `NodeId`/`VmId` (no parallel model), `MeshSessionStore` gated with typed errors; spawned beside the scheduler (reachable). 20 tests. **DISCOVERY DONE (E12-5b-discovery `e1e2e17`):** the shell's remote-desktop picker (`mde-shell-egui/src/discovery.rs`, 450 lines) — lists mesh VMs from the reused Fleet inventory (a `read_inventory` extraction in datacenter.rs, no second source), and "Connect" emits a `session_broker::SessionRequest::Open` on `action/vdi/session` + hands the target to the Desktop surface's `VdiState`; honest "No remote desktops available" EmptyState. **REMAINING:** only the live Nebula connect to a remote guest (integration-gated) — the whole buildable E12-5 chain (discover → request → broker-track → render) is now wired end-to-end in-shell.
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi` on `.90` slot `e12shell` passed `13/13`; `cargo test -p mackesd session_broker --features async-services` on `.130` slot `e12broker` passed `34/34`.
  - **Live-transport shell slice 2026-07-08:** the Chooser now carries a typed `DesktopEndpoint` (`host` + protocol port, including `host:port` recovery) into `ConnectRequest`, and the Desktop surface has a `live-vdi` feature that starts an off-UI-thread IronRDP worker for sealed direct RDP endpoints, pumps frames into the egui texture path, and forwards input through the existing RDP session mapper. The shipped RPM build hooks now relink `mde-shell-egui --features drm,live-helper,live-vdi`, so the live transport is present in release candidates instead of stopping at the gated caption. Farm evidence: `.90` slot `e12vdi-shell2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi` passed `14/14`; `.130` slot `e12vdi-live2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi` passed `14/14`; `.130` slot `e12chooser-endpoint` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui an_external_endpoint_prompts_once_seals_then_connects_without_a_broker_open` passed `1/1`; direct warmed BigBoy run `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui a_pinned_endpoint_with_a_stored_credential_connects_without_a_prompt` passed `1/1`. **Still remaining for full E12-5 close:** live Nebula mesh-brokered/mesh-identity attach to the remote guest, with the session-broker publishing the live session.
  - **Broker shared-session store slice 2026-07-08:** `MeshSessionStore` is no longer a fake `IntegrationGated` stub. The leader now publishes the broker roster to the Syncthing-backed workgroup root as one atomic JSON record per VDI session under `sessions/vdi/records`, lists rows deterministically, and removes closed/stale rows idempotently; the trait still leaves room for a future etcd lease-backed implementation. This closes the "session-broker publishes the session" software gap for the replicated fallback plane. Farm evidence: `.90` slot `e12broker-store-smoke` `cargo test -p mackesd mesh_session_store_round_trips_sorted_records_and_removes_idempotently --features async-services` passed `1/1`; `.130` slot `e12broker-store-serial` `cargo test -p mackesd session_broker --features async-services -- --test-threads=1` passed `35/35`. **Still remaining for full E12-5 close:** live Nebula mesh-brokered/mesh-identity attach to the remote guest and mesh-cert-gated connect proof.
  - **Shell broker-lifecycle wire slice 2026-07-08:** the shell's public `action/vdi/session` mirror now emits the broker lifecycle verbs it owns (`open`, `active`, `disconnect`, `close`) without depending on `mackesd`; mesh-brokered chooser connects retain the minted broker session id when `Open` publishes successfully, and the `live-vdi` RDP worker reports `active` on connect plus `disconnect`/`close` on teardown for broker-tracked sessions. Farm evidence: `.90` slot `e12life-discovery` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui discovery` passed `8/8`; `.90` slot `e12life-chooser` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui a_mesh_peer_connect` passed `2/2`; `.130` slot `e12life-vdi` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi` passed `14/14`. **Still remaining for full E12-5 close:** the live Nebula mesh-brokered/mesh-identity attach to the remote guest and mesh-cert-gated connect proof.
  - **Mesh guest-login live-RDP slice 2026-07-08:** mesh-brokered connects now model the design lock precisely: mesh identity gates the route, while an optional remembered sealed guest credential can feed the RDP OS login. Missing guest credentials still stay no-prompt SSO; remembered guest credentials ride inside `DesktopAuth::MeshIdentity` so the live RDP worker can attach without dropping broker lifecycle/session identity. Farm evidence: `.90` slot `e12guest-auth2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui auth` passed `20/20`; `.90` slot `e12guest-chooser2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui a_mesh_peer` passed `3/3`; `.130` slot `e12guest-vdi2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi` passed `16/16`. **Still remaining for full E12-5 close:** a real remote guest over Nebula with an actual sealed guest credential, mesh-cert-gated attach, rendered interactive proof, and post-connect broker `Active` observed on the live Bus/store.
  - **Mesh overlay endpoint proof 2026-07-08:** the worker-published overlay host and RDP protocol port now have a focused shell regression proving they survive the Chooser path into the `ConnectRequest` consumed by `live-vdi`, closing the last software-only gap between peer discovery and the live RDP worker endpoint contract. Farm evidence: `.90` slot `e12endpoint-chooser` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui a_mesh_peer` passed `4/4`; `.130` slot `e12endpoint-vdi` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi` passed `17/17`. **Still remaining for full E12-5 close:** a real remote guest over Nebula with an actual sealed guest credential, mesh-cert-gated attach, rendered interactive proof, and post-connect broker `Active` observed on the live Bus/store.
- [✓] **E12-6: `mde-vdi-vnc` — VNC/XAPI-console fallback.** **As** a user, **I want** a `vnc-rs` path rendering the XCP-ng/QEMU VNC console into an egui texture, **so that** any guest (no RDP server, pre-boot, BIOS) is still reachable. **Acceptance:** a guest with no RDP renders + is interactive via VNC in the shell; the XAPI console location is resolved via the existing DATACENTER verb. **STATUS (reconciled 2026-06-30, session=819545e6):** `mde-vdi-vnc` crate + RFB-decode→egui-texture core BUILT + green (**63 tests**, farm-verified on .90). ✅ **NOW REACHABLE (E12-5a `145d377`):** the shell Desktop surface mounts a `VncSession` via the same `Session::Vnc(s) => s.frame()/s.send_input()` path (dead-lib cleared). **✅ LIVE-PROVEN 2026-07-01 (`c1b1831`):** a REAL XCP-ng guest console (QEMU, mcnf-build-51 on KVM-XCP1, resolved via the existing `vm-console` DATACENTER verb — no new resolver needed) rendered through the crate's public session path: RFB 3.8 handshake → 1024×768 ServerInit → Raw rect → `apply_rect` → egui ColorImage, and **input echoed** (`send_input` keystrokes changed the next framebuffer checksum, reproduced twice). Landed as the env-gated `live_console` test (`MDE_VNC_LIVE_TARGET=…`; XCP-ng consoles are dom0-local unix sockets — `/var/run/xen/vnc-<domid>`, NOT tcp 59xx — so the test needs a tunnel). **✅ LIVE SHELL-PROVEN 2026-07-09:** the in-shell `live-vdi` VNC worker connected to the KVM-XCP1 XAPI console socket via the same DATACENTER `vm-console`/domid path, rendered the live 1024×768 console frame, and input echoed through the shell worker (`fnv1a64 0xface601842022325 → 0x9ca76c76d58f3087`).
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mde-vdi-vnc` on `.170` slot `e12vnc` passed `91/91`; `live_console` remained ignored without `MDE_VNC_LIVE_TARGET`.
  - **In-shell live VNC pump slice 2026-07-09:** `mde-vdi-vnc` now has a `live-connect` feature with the real TCP RFB open/pump path: resolved-address retry, protocol/security negotiation for the XCP-ng/QEMU `None`-security console path, `ClientInit`/`ServerInit`, initial control-message flush, framebuffer-update requests, Raw/CopyRect/RRE/Hextile payload reads through `VncSession::apply_rect`, and queued egui input flush over the same `VncSession::take_input` surface. The shell `live-vdi` feature now enables that transport, starts an off-UI-thread VNC worker for `VdiProtocol::Vnc`, pumps decoded frames into the Desktop texture upload path, forwards input to the live worker, and publishes the same broker active/disconnect lifecycle used by RDP. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `e12vnc-livecrate` `cargo test -p mde-vdi-vnc --features live-connect` passed `94/94`; `.50` slot `e12vnc-clippy2` `cargo clippy -p mde-vdi-vnc --features live-connect --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings` passed; `.130` slot `e12vnc-shell-live` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi` passed `19/19`; `.90` slot `e12vnc-shell-auth` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui live_vnc --features live-vdi` passed `2/2`; live `.90` slot `e12vnc-liveproof` `MDE_VNC_LIVE_TARGET=127.0.0.1:15903 cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-vdi live_vnc_worker_renders_real_console_and_accepts_input -- --ignored --nocapture` passed against KVM-XCP1 `mcnf-build-kvm-xcp1` (domid `1`, `/var/run/xen/vnc-1`) over a temporary SSH bridge, with `FRAME OK 1024x768 fnv1a64=0xface601842022325` and input echo `after=0x9ca76c76d58f3087`.
- [✗] **E12-7: `mde-kvm` — local cloud-hypervisor VMs. — DROPPED / SUPERSEDED (reconciled 2026-07-10).** **As** a Workstation, **I want** to create/run local VMs on **cloud-hypervisor** with the **virtio-gpu zero-copy** fast path into the egui texture, each **dual-homed** (own Nebula cert + a LAN NIC), from **mesh golden images**, via a `mackesd` **vm-lifecycle** worker folded into the DATACENTER/Instances surface, **so that** local desktops run without a remote peer. **Acceptance:** a local Windows 10 golden-image VM boots on cloud-hypervisor, renders in the shell via virtio-gpu (dmabuf→wgpu), is dual-homed (its Nebula peer appears + it has a LAN IP), and is managed from the Instances panel; running disk lives in `~/Local`. — WAS IN PROGRESS before the QC-15 deletion (farm agent, salvaged + verified; kept below as historical record only): `crates/services/mde-kvm` broker core DONE — `VmSpec` + dual-homed `Nic` model, the pure `build_ch_config` (VmSpec→cloud-hypervisor VmConfig JSON), an injectable `ChTransport`/`UnixSocketTransport` (HTTP/1.1 over the CH api-socket), and a `Vm` create/boot/shutdown/info lifecycle. Farm-green: 18 tests pass, clippy clean. **✅ Instances-panel wiring DONE (E12-7-instances `1da4370`):** the shell's new `Surface::Instances` (`mde-shell-egui/src/instances.rs`, 748 lines) is the **first real caller of `mde-kvm`** (dead-lib cleared) — lists this node's cloud-hypervisor VMs via the real `ChBroker`/`UnixSocketTransport` over each VM's api-socket, shows the dual-homed NICs (Nebula peer + LAN), Boot/Shutdown, honest "No local VMs" EmptyState, and folds mde-kvm's typed `KvmError::Connect` into a "gated — no VMM" pip + banner (never fake-running). 42 shell tests. Kept separate from the Fleet plane (that's a Bus-fed libvirt projection; this is the node-local CH broker). **NO LONGER A LIVE REMAINDER (2026-07-10):** the local-VM-boot + virtio-gpu-render items once tracked here targeted cloud-hypervisor, which QC-15 has since deleted outright — this is not a task for a future drainer to resume on the old stack; see the QUASAR-CLOUD supersession note immediately below for where the functionality continues.
  - **QUASAR-CLOUD supersession 2026-07-10:** `mde-kvm`/cloud-hypervisor is no longer the target local VMM path, and `crates/services/mde-kvm` is now deleted outright (QC-15), not merely pending deletion. Treat this entry as historical/replaced-stack evidence — do not resume it as written; the buildable code it describes (`VmSpec`, `ChBroker`, the Instances panel's cloud-hypervisor caller) built on a foundation that no longer exists. Successor functionality lives in **QC-13** (instance access — console + keys), **QC-15** (this cutover), **QC-16** (verb-level CI on the farm dev cloud), **QC-18** (object tiers + media re-platform), **QC-22** (SELinux tightening), and **QC-23** (the QEMU virtio-gpu→egui local-desktop fast path — the direct successor to this entry's virtio-gpu-render acceptance bullet); point future local/virtual-desktop work at those instead of reviving mde-kvm.

- [>] **E12-8: Session roaming + persistence.** *(session=819545e6, 5a2c164)* **As** a user, **I want** open sessions + monitor layout persisted **per-peer in mesh state** (etcd/Syncthing) and a disconnect policy, **so that** my desktops follow me to any Workstation and survive disconnect. **Acceptance:** sessions + multi-monitor mapping persist and reappear on a second Workstation; a disconnected VM **keeps running** by default and reconnects; a per-VM suspend/shutdown policy is honored. **POLICY CORE DONE `5a2c164`:** the `mackesd` `session_roaming` worker (1537 lines) — `RoamingState` + `MonitorLayout` (serde), `DisconnectPolicy` (default **KeepRunning**; Suspend/Shutdown per-VM honored), `on_node_loss` holds a connected session `Disconnected` for reconnect (never closes it), `reconcile_roaming` = the "desktops follow me" diff (mirrors scheduler `replace_decisions`). Reuses the broker's `VdiSession`/`SessionState`/`SessionStore` (no parallel model); leader-gated; spawned reachable (mackesd.rs:6280). 24 tests, 2379 mackesd total. **REMAINING (integration-gated):** the live per-peer persist + reappear-on-second-Workstation (rides the broker's gated `MeshSessionStore` + a `LayoutStore`).
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mackesd session_roaming --features async-services` on `.130` slot `e12roam` passed `43/43`.
  - **Restart-persistent layout preload slice 2026-07-10:** `SessionRoamingWorker` now preloads persisted `MonitorLayout`s from `LayoutStore::list()` before replaying `action/vdi/roaming`, validates/refuses conflicted rows, and re-keys a pre-existing old-peer layout onto the arriving Workstation on `Arrive`, so saved monitor mappings survive daemon restart before the next roam. `MeshLayoutStore` is documented as the Syncthing/workgroup-root layout plane, not an integration-gated stub. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `e12roam-preload-suite` `cargo test -p mackesd session_roaming --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `46/46`; `.90` slot `e12roam-preload-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_preloads_persisted_layouts_before_roaming_arrivals --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `e12roam-broker-retry` `cargo test -p mackesd drain_placements_boots_via_the_seam_and_emits_the_placed_session --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed after a `.50` session_broker sweep hit a farm `disk I/O error` in one temp-bus test after `34/35` tests passed; `.170` slot `e12roam-preload-fmt5` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/session_roaming.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/mod.rs` passed. Remaining acceptance: live two-Workstation proof that the shared session + layout state persists and reappears on the second Workstation.
- [>] **E12-9: Bridges — clipboard, audio, mesh-share.** *(session=819545e6, cb03963)* **As** a user, **I want** bidirectional **clipboard** (host⇄guest, wired into mesh clipboard-sync), **audio** (virtio-sound local / protocol remote), and a **mesh-share folder** mounted into the guest, **so that** data and sound flow across host/guest/mesh. **Acceptance:** copy/paste crosses host⇄guest⇄a second peer; guest audio plays (virtio-sound local, RDP/VNC audio remote); a file dropped in the mesh-share folder appears inside the guest. **CLIPBOARD BRIDGE DONE (E12-9-clip `cb03963`):** the `mackesd` `clipboard_bridge` worker (1162 lines) — relays VDI clips over `action/vdi/clipboard` scoped to a broker `SessionId` (reused, no parallel type); `relay(event,policy)→Forward/Drop/Truncate`, 1 MiB `MAX_CLIP_BYTES` hard ceiling, echo-guard against loops, per-session (not leader-gated — every serving node applies its own clips). Injectable `ClipboardAccess`; `OsClipboardAccess` integration-gated (typed errors, the SPICE/RDP vdagent link isn't wired). 22 tests, 2401 mackesd total. **MESH-SHARE BRIDGE DONE (E12-9-meshfs `7089fc5`):** the mde-kvm virtio-fs path — a `SharedFolder` model on `VmSpec` (`spec.rs`), `build_ch_config` emits the CH `fs` entries (tag + virtiofsd socket, `config.rs`), an injectable `VirtiofsLauncher` (`virtiofs.rs`) whose live impl is integration-gated (needs the live virtiofsd binary + host mesh-share export — typed error, never fake); `SharedFolder::mesh_share()` for a Syncthing-replicated dir. Farm-green (mde-kvm). **REMAINING:** the **audio** bridge — **⚠️ PARKED [!] (E12-9-audio `9269b09`):** the remote path (RDP audio) needs an ironrdp **RDPSND** virtual-channel API the pinned ironrdp doesn't expose → needs an ironrdp bump or a custom SVC decoder (operator/design call, see `docs/NEEDS-OPERATOR.md`); the local path (cloud-hypervisor virtio-sound) is separately CH-support-uncertain. Plus the live guest clipboard vdagent + virtiofsd mount (integration-gated).
  - **QUASAR-CLOUD supersession 2026-07-10:** the mesh-share bridge's implementation moved, not the feature — the old `mde-kvm` virtio-fs path (`SharedFolder`/`virtiofs.rs`) is deleted with the rest of `mde-kvm` (QC-15); equivalent mesh-share now ships via the already-committed `crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/compute_provision.rs` (`share_meshfs` request flag + `MESHFS_VIRTIOFS_TAG = "mesh-storage"`, `meshfs_attach_decision`) on the new libvirt path. Clipboard (`clipboard_bridge`) is unaffected by the cutover and stays real. **Audio needs a genuine re-scope:** both this entry's own "the local path (cloud-hypervisor virtio-sound) is separately CH-support-uncertain" above and `docs/NEEDS-OPERATOR.md`'s E12-9-audio DESCOPED note ("Local CH virtio-sound stays in scope") are now stale — cloud-hypervisor is deleted outright, not merely uncertain, so there is no CH virtio-sound fallback left to fall back to. The remote-path ironrdp RDPSND blocker is unaffected, but the local-audio plan needs fresh design against the libvirt/QEMU-KVM stack (a different audio backend entirely) before E12-9's audio remainder can resume.
  - **Design doc landed 2026-07-10:** `docs/design/e12-9-10-libvirt-rescope.md` re-scopes the audio bridge against the live libvirt/QEMU-KVM stack (design/options only, nothing implemented). Recommended first slice: a `<sound model='virtio'/>` + `<audio type='pipewire'>` domain-XML addition (both `vm_lifecycle.rs`'s `build_domain_xml` and `compute_provision.rs`'s `build_virt_install_args` need it — two independent create paths today) feeding the **already-shipped** E12-16 PipeWire mixer via its `mde.vm.name`-tag classifier (`mde-seat/src/mixer.rs::classify_origin`, which has no producer yet) — closes the **local**-audio half of lock 5 with zero new client protocol code. **Remote** audio's best-aligned direction is a SPICE playback-channel decoder in `mde-vdi-spice` (matches quasar-cloud.md Q34's SPICE-primary console lock) — but the doc verified against the pinned `spice-client` 0.2.0 crate's own upstream source tree that it implements zero SPICE audio-channel code (only connection/cursor/display/inputs/main), plus its docs.rs build is currently broken (single-maintainer dependency-health flag) — so that's real new wire-protocol work, not a quick follow-on, and stays a separate future slice. RDP RDPSND stays the 2026-07-03 WON'T-DO (unchanged). `docs/NEEDS-OPERATOR.md`'s stale "Local CH virtio-sound stays in scope" line is corrected to point here.
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mackesd clipboard_bridge --features async-services` on `.50` slot `e12clip` passed `22/22`; `cargo test -p mde-kvm virtiofs` on `.90` slot `e12virtiofs` passed `4/4`.
  - **First slice landed 2026-07-10 (`13134d6c`):** implements the design doc's Option A recommendation for real. Both create paths now emit the local-audio device unconditionally: `vm_lifecycle.rs::build_domain_xml` adds `<sound model='virtio'/>` + `<audio id='1' type='pipewire'><output name='mde-vms' streamName='vm-{name}' latency='40'/></audio>` to every domain's `<devices>` block; `compute_provision.rs::build_virt_install_args` adds the matching `--sound model=virtio --audio id=1,type=pipewire` to every `virt-install` call (minimal suboptions only — virt-install's exact per-backend suboption set beyond `id`/`type`, e.g. a `streamName` equivalent, isn't confirmed, so this stays the honest minimal flag rather than guessed syntax). **Mixer-tag wiring deliberately NOT connected:** `streamName='vm-{name}'` is the doc-recommended connecting tissue toward `mde-seat/src/mixer.rs::classify_origin`'s `mde.vm.name` match, but actually stamping that tag needs either (a) empirically verifying which live `pw-dump` JSON property `streamName` lands under — unverifiable without a real running VM this environment can't spin up — or (b) a new mackesd-side PipeWire-metadata-stamping seam; `mackesd`'s `Cargo.toml` carries **zero** PipeWire dependency today (grep-verified) and `mde-seat` is a separate desktop-tier crate mackesd doesn't (and per the layered-tiers boundary, architecturally shouldn't) depend on — so this is real new cross-cutting integration work, not a small delta. Left as an explicit, named open follow-on rather than guessed at. 2 new tests (`domain_xml_includes_pipewire_audio_device` in `vm_lifecycle.rs`, `virt_install_args_always_include_pipewire_audio_device` in `compute_provision.rs`) + 2 existing tests extended with audio-specific assertions (`domain_xml_escapes_interpolated_values`'s streamName-escape check, `virt_install_args_baseline_shape`'s `--sound`/`--audio` presence check).
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-10:** `cargo test -p mackesd vm_lifecycle --features async-services` on `.130` (BigBoy) passed `43/43` (33 pre-existing + 10 new, zero regressions); `cargo test -p mackesd compute_provision --features async-services` on `.130` passed `31/31` (30 pre-existing + 1 new). `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` clean on both files; zero new compiler warnings (the pre-existing 29 `mackesd` warnings are all in unrelated files, grep-verified).
- [>] **E12-10: Advanced VDI (v1 — passthrough/USB/multi-mon/migration/adaptive codec).** *(CLAIMED 2026-07-01, session=26f9a93c wave-1 farm dispatch: adaptive-codec core → mde-vdi-rdp/vnc (.90); VFIO+USB+migration cores → mde-kvm (.170); per-monitor-VM mapping → mackesd session_roaming (.90). Buildable cores + injectable live seams; the runtime demos stay hardware/live-gated.)* **As** a power user, **I want** **GPU passthrough** (VFIO, operator-opt per host), **USB redirection**, **per-monitor-different-VM** mapping, **adaptive codec** (link-quality driven), and **live VM migration**, **so that** v1 covers the full desktop envelope (lock 48). **Acceptance:** each is runtime-demonstrated — a passthrough VM renders on the dGPU; a USB device redirects into a guest; two monitors show two different VMs; a weak link drops to lighter encoding; a running VM migrates to another host and reconnects. **CORES LANDED 2026-07-01 (outage-salvage + resume, this session):** VFIO passthrough / USB redirect / live-migration typed steps in mde-kvm (`ca22381`/`9230150`/`8f47087` + pedantic polish `d8566e5`; 65 kvm tests, clippy silent); **adaptive codec both protocols** (`888b1c9` VNC — protocol-neutral `LinkEstimator`→`QualityLadder` with asymmetric hysteresis, applied LIVE via queued SetPixelFormat/SetEncodings at the RFC 6143 drain point; `c485169` RDP — typed **OnReconnect** target-tier, the pinned ironrdp has no mid-session renegotiation; rdp 61 / vnc 91 tests, 0 new clippy). Per-monitor-VM mapping worker in flight. The five runtime demos stay hardware/live-gated (dGPU seat, real USB, two monitors, a real weak link, a live migrate).
  - **QUASAR-CLOUD supersession 2026-07-10 — partial reopen, not just a stale marker:** the adaptive codec (`LinkEstimator`/`QualityLadder`/`QualityTier` in `mde-vdi-vnc/src/link.rs` + `mde-vdi-rdp/src/link.rs`) and per-monitor-VM mapping (`session_roaming.rs`'s `MonitorLayout::is_per_monitor`) are real, intact, and unaffected by the cutover — those two runtime demos still stand, credit not lost. But VFIO/USB passthrough and live migration, which the "CORES LANDED" text above claims LANDED, lived entirely inside the now-deleted `mde-kvm` crate and are **gone**: `crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/compute_provision.rs`, `compute_migrate.rs`, and `vm_lifecycle.rs` (the new libvirt-backed replacements) carry zero vfio/usb code (grep-verified empty), and `compute_migrate.rs` implements a different, **cold/offline** `virsh shutdown` + `rsync` migration (VIRT-8's scope), not live migration. This is a genuinely reopened gap: VFIO/USB passthrough and live VM migration need fresh libvirt-native scoping/design from scratch, not a resume of the deleted mde-kvm code. The "CORES LANDED" language above overclaims until that redesign happens.
  - **Design doc landed 2026-07-10:** `docs/design/e12-9-10-libvirt-rescope.md` re-scopes VFIO/USB passthrough + live migration against libvirt (`<hostdev>` XML, `virsh migrate`), replacing the deleted cloud-hypervisor-era mde-kvm design (design/options only, nothing implemented). Findings: **(1) USB static passthrough** (`<hostdev type='usb'>`, admin-selected device, hot-attach via `virsh attach-device`) is realistically buildable + testable now — recommended first slice, as a new `LifecycleAction` op in `vm_lifecycle.rs` mirroring the existing `Pause`/`Resume` shape; dynamic SPICE `usbredir` (the click-to-redirect UX) shares the exact same thin-`spice-client`-dependency wall as the audio gap above (verified: no `usbredir` channel in the pinned crate either), so it's deferred. **(2) VFIO GPU passthrough** is a well-established, buildable-as-pure-XML mechanism (`<hostdev mode='subsystem' type='pci'>`), but this project's farm slots run nested atop Xen dom0s (no realistic IOMMU passthrough there) and no physical test seat has a recorded second GPU or confirmed IOMMU state — so the runtime demo may have **no reachable live-proof path today**, mirroring the pre-existing DATACENTER-22 finding on the old Xen/`xen-pciback` stack (`install-helpers/setup-workstation-passthrough.sh`, stays `[!]` hardware-gated). Recommendation: code the pure/unit-tested XML-building half (cheap, real progress), keep the runtime demo honestly hardware-gated instead of repeating the "CORES LANDED" overclaim. **(3) Live migration:** `vm_lifecycle.rs::build_domain_xml` hardcodes `<cpu mode='host-passthrough'>`, a verified concrete blocker for cross-host live migration on this project's heterogeneous farm hardware, independent of the already-known no-shared-storage gap (`mesh-virt-management.md`'s Risks section, pre-dates QC-15). Recommended first slice is a **pre-copy warm-migration extension of the existing, already-shipped VIRT-8 cold flow** (`compute_migrate.rs`, untouched) — rsync the disk once while the VM is still running, then the existing shutdown+delta-rsync+redefine sequence — not `virsh migrate --live`. Also flags a live product-scope tension for an operator call: `quasar-cloud.md` Q38 already locked live migration "not a goal" (roaming replaces it) for the overlapping Nova-instance case on the same libvirt/QEMU-KVM stack; not resolved here.
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mde-kvm vfio` on `.130` slot `e12vfio` passed `25/25` with `1` ignored live-IOMMU test; `cargo test -p mde-kvm usb` on `.170` slot `e12usb` passed `5/5`; `cargo test -p mde-kvm migrate` on `.50` slot `e12migrate` passed `9/9`.
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-08:** `cargo test -p mde-vdi-rdp link` on `.90` slot `e12rdplink` passed `18/18`; `cargo test -p mde-vdi-vnc link` on `.170` slot `e12vnclink` passed `18/18`; `cargo test -p mackesd per_monitor --features async-services` on `.130` slot `e12permon` passed `3/3`.
  - **First slice landed 2026-07-10 (`13134d6c`):** implements both of the design doc's recommended first slices for this gap, in `vm_lifecycle.rs` only (the doc's named "natural home" for both — no `compute_provision.rs` mirror attempted, consistent with the doc's own two-paths factoring-cost note). **(1) Static USB passthrough:** new `LifecycleAction::AttachUsb`/`DetachUsb` variants mirroring the exact `Pause`/`Resume` shape — parse → `plan_transition` precondition (running-only; a shut-off VM reads the same `NotRunning` refusal `Stop` already uses) → new `LibvirtBackend::attach_usb`/`detach_usb` methods → `build_usb_hostdev_xml` (a standalone `<hostdev mode='subsystem' type='usb'>` fragment addressed by vendor/product id) → `build_attach_device_argv`/`build_detach_device_argv` (`virsh attach-device`/`detach-device --live`) → unit tests, including an `apply_action`-level test against `FakeLibvirt` mirroring `apply_pause_resume_lifecycle`. This is the realistic path to E12-10's "a USB device redirects into a guest" acceptance bullet (the *static*, admin-selected half — dynamic SPICE `usbredir` click-to-redirect stays deferred, unchanged from the design doc's finding). **(2) VFIO PCI passthrough — pure XML only, explicitly NOT a runtime claim:** new `VmSpec.pci_passthrough: Vec<PciAddress>` field (empty by default — an unconfigured VM's domain XML is byte-for-byte unchanged, test-verified) + `build_pci_hostdev_xml` (a standalone `managed='yes'` `<hostdev mode='subsystem' type='pci'>` fragment), wired into `build_domain_xml`'s `<devices>` block (one stanza per configured address, test-verified for 0-, 1-, and 2-device cases). `build_pci_hostdev_xml`'s own doc comment states plainly that this project's farm build-VM slots (nested atop Xen dom0s) and physical test seats (no recorded second GPU/confirmed IOMMU state) can't validate a live IOMMU passthrough — mirroring DATACENTER-22's finding on the old Xen stack, matching this project's honest hardware-gating convention rather than repeating the "CORES LANDED" overclaim the now-deleted mde-kvm-era text made for this exact feature. **(3) Live migration: untouched, out of scope for this pass** — the design doc's recommended pre-copy warm-migration extension to `compute_migrate.rs` is explicitly a separate, bigger follow-on, not bundled here. 9 new tests in `vm_lifecycle.rs`: 6 for USB attach/detach (parse round-trip, `plan_transition` preconditions, hostdev-XML content + escaping, attach/detach argv shapes, the `apply_action`/`FakeLibvirt` dispatch test) + 3 for VFIO XML construction (hostdev-XML content, domain-XML wiring across 0/1/2 configured devices).
  - **Farm evidence 2026-07-10:** same run as the E12-9 bullet above (both slices landed together in `13134d6c`) — `cargo test -p mackesd vm_lifecycle --features async-services` on `.130` (BigBoy) passed `43/43`, zero regressions; `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` clean; zero new compiler warnings.

CARGO_PKG_VERSION asserts are tautological (env vs env) so no version-gated logic broke.)*
  **READINESS AUDIT 2026-07-01 (session=819545e6):** the cutover is a *surgical* multi-slice job, not a bulk delete — baseline consumers: **libcosmic ×13, iced ×7, mde-theme ×8, mde-card ×3** crates. Slices (in dependency order):
    - **E12-14a — CORRECTED 2026-07-01: there are NO trivially-removable iced crates.** The first-pass "2 dead crates" premise was wrong — the Cargo-*lib*-dep audit missed **binary / packaging / RPM** consumption. `mde-workbench` is a **5-bin crate** hosting live surfaces: `mde-workbench` + `mde-mesh-wallpaper` + `mde-notify-capture` + **`mde-notify-center`** + **`mde-notify-toast`** (the notification hub + wallpaper — shipped in the RPM via mackesd's `generate-rpm` assets + launched by `packaging/autostart/*.desktop`). `mde-music` hosts the live **MUSIC-DOCK-4** dock (shipped + autostarted). **Neither is removable** until its surfaces migrate to egui. (E12-14a's farm dispatch was cancelled before landing — invalid scope.)
    - **E12-14b — migrate the remaining iced surfaces to egui (the real bulk):** the notification hub (`mde-notify-center`/`-toast`/`-capture`), the mesh wallpaper (`mde-mesh-wallpaper`), and the music dock (MUSIC-DOCK-4) off `mde-workbench`/`mde-music`; PLUS the GUI/backend split for `mde-files`, `mde-voice-hud`, `mde-cosmic-applet` (their egui replacements reuse the *non-GUI backends* — mde-files-egui→mde-files, mde-voice-egui→mde-voice-hud, mde-shell-egui+mde-panel-egui→mde-cosmic-applet, §6 glue; strip the iced GUI, keep/fold the backend). Only once a surface's egui equivalent ships (incl. its packaging/RPM/autostart repoint) does its iced crate come out. **✅ GATE LIFTED 2026-07-01:** the two active iced epics E12-14 deferred behind are BOTH COMPLETE — **CTRLSURF 8/8 `[✓]` + NOTIFY-REDESIGN 3/3 `[✓]`** — so **E12-14b is now UNBLOCKED** (previously mislabelled "gated" here after those epics finished this session). **True nature (scoped 2026-07-01): NOT a mechanical iced→egui port.** The remaining iced surfaces are Cosmic-era **wlr-layer-shell** clients (`mde-mesh-wallpaper` = an 798-line `cosmic::iced` layer-shell Background rendering the peers-map; the notify hub = layer-shell toast/center surfaces) — and E12 has **no compositor** (the egui shell owns DRM directly, §5). So the migration = **re-implement each surface's *functionality* as an element of the one DRM-owned egui shell** (`mde-shell-egui`): the wallpaper → a shell background render-mode; the notify hub → an in-shell notification overlay + the toast/center as shell surfaces; then delete the iced clients (+ packaging/autostart repoint). This is the **E12 "Quasar" endgame** (decommission iced). **⚠️ PRODUCT-DECISION-GATED per the design doc:** `quasar-vdi-desktop.md` **lock 38 lists the surfaces to KEEP** as egui panels — **Workbench · Files · Music · Voice** — and the **mesh-wallpaper + notify hub are NOT on that list** (lock 37: no separate Wayland clients; E12 has no compositor for a layer-shell background/toast). So those two aren't a "migrate" — their E12 fate is a genuine **operator product decision: DROP them as obsolete Cosmic-era layer-shell surfaces, vs. re-conceive** (a mesh-map idle/background render in the shell? an in-shell notification overlay?). The design doc is silent → I can't decide it autonomously. **What IS executable now** (no product call): the kept surfaces (Workbench/Files/Music/Voice) are already egui panels in the shell (E12-3/5/7) — so E12-14b's remaining executable work is **stripping the migrated surfaces' OLD iced GUIs** (mde-files/mde-voice-hud/mde-cosmic-applet) once each egui panel is verified a complete replacement + its packaging/RPM/autostart is repointed (this dovetails with E12-14c). **Operator inputs needed:** (a) the drop-vs-reconceive call for wallpaper + notify hub; (b) a go-ahead to strip a kept surface's iced GUI (packaging-affecting). **✅ STRIP DONE 2026-07-01 (session=819545e6):** operator authorized ("strip the old iced GUIs"). Input (a) already resolved by the DROP in `026cca4` (wallpaper + notify hub gone); input (b) granted → the four migrated surfaces' iced/libcosmic GUIs are stripped, their non-GUI backends kept (§6): **`mde-music` crate DELETED whole** (purely iced; zero consumers — `mde-music-egui` drives `mde-musicd`); **`mde-files`** now lib-only (removed the 10 libcosmic GUI modules + the `mde-files` bin; kept the render-agnostic backend/model/send_to that `mde-files-egui` reuses); **`mde-voice-hud`** now lib-only (removed the wlr-layer-shell HUD bin + `theme`/`cosmic_compat`; kept the SIP core `sip`/`media`/`roster`/`resolve` incl. `sip::run_agent` that `mde-voice-egui` reuses — the standalone `--agent` autostart retires, the shell spawns the persistent agent); **`mde-cosmic-applet`** now lib-only (removed the libcosmic panel-shell bin; kept the LIGHTHOUSE-7 model that `mde-panel-egui`+`mde-shell-egui` reuse). Packaging repointed: the 4 `/usr/bin/*` RPM assets + the 4 `applications/` + 2 `autostart/` `.desktop` launchers removed; `mde-musicd`'s MPRIS `Raise`/`CanRaise`/`DesktopEntry` de-referenced from the deleted `mde-music` bin. All three panel replacements verified MOUNTED in `mde-shell-egui` (`Surface::Files/Music/Voice` ← `files_panel`/`music_panel`/`voice_panel` over the real backends) — none PARKED. **Remaining = E12-14c:** the last *real* `libcosmic` dep now lives ONLY in the retired `mde-workbench` (+ its own bins) — still gated behind OW-11 + the mde-workbench retirement.
    - **E12-14c — drop the shared iced stack** (`mde-theme`/`mde-card`/`libcosmic`/`iced`): ✅ **DONE 2026-07-01 (`063b0ec`..`a6637c6`, outage-salvaged chain; workspace build+test green on .170):** the iced `mde-workbench` retired (the egui shell IS the Workbench); `mde-card` folded into `mackesd_core::card` (one consumer, no crate); **`mde-theme` DELETED** — `mde_egui::Style` is the sole look-source (both couplings broken: mackesd's dep dropped, needed constants inlined); the retired toolkit's name purged from every crate + manifest; rustfmt pass over the diff. - [✓] **E12-21: the Storage plane — GParted-authentic UI.** *(integrated 49c7754 from farm agent afe2a6d; the plane was already built at b42173f — the agent closed the one real gap: walled-row deep-links (running VM → Instances) now emit `action/shell/goto` via the shell's single nav grammar, not inert prose. Re-gated on .50/e1221: mde-shell-egui 145 tests green, clippy 0 denials, fmt+layered-tiers clean.)* **As** an operator, **I want** a sixth Workbench plane rendering per-disk segment bars + partition tables + the pending-operations queue with reorder/undo and an Apply flow that always demands typed arming, locked rows showing wall reasons with deep-links (running VM → Instances), and a fleet rollup + peer picker staging remote queues through the same verbs, **so that** GParted lives in the Workbench for every node in the mesh. **Acceptance:** the USB-disk end-to-end (stage GPT+partitions+LUKS → typed-armed Apply → per-op progress → bar re-renders from signals); a remote peer's disk edited from this node with walls enforced by the remote worker; unavailable backend renders the typed state.
- [✓] **E12-22: virtual disks first-class (KVM images + Podman storage).** *(RECONCILED 2026-07-02: feature was ALREADY shipped at 6d09473 — `mde-kvm/qemu_img.rs` (QemuImgRunner/LiveQemuImg + argv/parse folds) + mackesd `workers/virtual_storage.rs` (2330 lines: staged VirtualStorageQueue, in-use walls, typed arming, LivePodman, apply_queue halt-on-failure) owned by the E12-23 StorageWorker. Marker was drift. Integrated clippy cleanup bbd726e (86db680): removed a dead InUseByVm arm + doc reflow. Gates: mde-kvm 74 + virtual_storage 22 tests, clippy clean, layered-tiers clean.)* **As** a workstation operator, **I want** KVM disk images (`~/Local` raw/qcow2: create/resize/snapshot/revert/convert/clone-from-golden/attach-detach reusing `mde-kvm` `VmSpec` types) and Podman storage (volume create/inspect/prune + image-store/per-container usage) staged in the SAME pending queue with the same in-use walls (CH broker + podman as truth), **so that** virtual and physical disks are peers in one interface. **Acceptance:** qcow2 snapshot→revert round-trip from the plane; golden-clone attaches and the VM boots; a staged prune lists exactly what dies before arming; a running VM's image is locked until shutdown.
- [✓] **E12-23: filesystem depth + packaging.** *(integrated b4538d5 from farm agent 051ae21; new mackesd `workers/fs_tools.rs` (capability matrix + argv builders + check→fs-shrink→part-shrink choreography state machine) + `storage.rs` LUKS/subvolume ops + `OpStatus::Unsupported` honest states; RPM requires + bootc Containerfile carry udisks2/e2fsprogs/xfsprogs/btrfs-progs/exfatprogs/dosfstools/ntfs-3g/ntfsprogs/cryptsetup/parted/qemu-img. Re-gated fully-integrated .170/e1223: mackesd 2684 tests green, clippy 0 on authored lines, layered-tiers clean. Honest deferrals (§7): live tool exec integration-gated (tools not on farm), partition-table geometry ops stay UDisks2/parted IntegrationGated, `rpm -qlp` image verify is release-gated.)* **As** the platform, **I want** the full fs set (ext4/xfs/vfat/exfat/btrfs incl. subvolume list-create-delete-snapshot/ntfs/swap) + LUKS (create/unlock/lock, format-inside-LUKS one staged op) + correct shrink/move choreography per fs (check→fs-shrink→part-shrink ordered steps), and udisks2 + e2fsprogs/xfsprogs/btrfs-progs/exfatprogs/ntfs-3g/cryptsetup/qemu-img in the RPM requires + bootc Containerfile, **so that** every locked format actually works on a shipped node. **Acceptance:** each fs formats/labels/resizes per its support matrix with honest per-fs capability states; the shrink choreography survives a mid-queue failure typed; a fresh bootc image carries every tool.
    - **E12-14d — identity cut:** workspace version → 12.0.0; About/greeter → `MCNF 12.0 "Quasar"`; `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` §4/5/6/8 → VDI model; strike the abandoned iced/cosmic-comp worklist tasks (I own this edit — keep it out of subagent scope to avoid WORKLIST.md churn). Do LAST (couples version+removal → no inconsistent 12.0-with-libcosmic state).

### E12-POLISH — egui surface polish (survey 2026-07-01: surfaces are §4-clean/§7-honest; consolidation is the open axis)
_Phase-0 survey of the egui surfaces found **0 ad-hoc `Color32`** across all 5 surface crates (they read `Style::*`) and honest empty-state copy (no `demo_data`/mockups). The open `/polish` axis is **7 — component reuse**: `mde-egui` lacked a shared component module (the E12 successor to the retired `mde-theme::components`), so surfaces hand-rolled the same idioms._
_**Sweep conclusion (2026-07-01):** an md5 audit of every `(ui, …)` helper across the 5 surfaces found **exactly three byte-identical duplications** — the empty-state idiom (E12-POLISH-1), `status_dot` (E12-POLISH-2), `field` (E12-POLISH-3) — all now consolidated onto `mde-egui`. The other same-name helpers are **per-plane/per-surface DISTINCT logic, not copy-paste** and are correctly left as-is: `presence_tone` (×4, four different md5s), `show_status` (×4, four different `*Status` types), `empty_state` (×2, different signatures — the files one is browser-specific), `form_field` (×2, different md5s). Merging those would force-combine distinct code — a §7/§4 regression, not polish. **So the widget-consolidation (axis-7) sweep of the egui surfaces is COMPLETE once E12-POLISH-3 lands.** Remaining polish axes (spacing rhythm, type tiers, motion, focus/a11y) are already `Style`-token-enforced (§4) or need a live visual pass (hardware-gated `/preview`); the larger remaining GUI work is E12-14b (iced→egui migration, plan-gated behind the active iced epics)._
- [✓] **E12-POLISH-1: shared `mde_egui::muted_note` + consolidate the empty-state idiom.** *(session=819545e6)* **As** a mesh operator, **I want** every honestly-empty / not-yet-reported panel state to render through one shared `mde-egui` widget, **so that** the empty/caption look is consistent and restyled in one place. **Acceptance** (runtime-observable): the shared `muted_note(ui, msg)` reads only `Style::TEXT_DIM`+`Style::SMALL` (no raw literals); the ~24 hand-rolled `colored_label(TEXT_DIM, RichText::new(_).size(SMALL))` sites across mde-shell/files/music/voice-egui call it instead; each crate builds+tests+clippy-clean. **DONE (helper `45f4055`, farm-gated .50: mde-egui 9/9, clippy 0; on master `04fe60e`):** `crates/shared/mde-egui/src/widgets.rs` adds `muted_note` (Style-driven + headless-render test). **SURFACE CONVERSIONS (2026-07-01):** the pure idiom `colored_label(TEXT_DIM, RichText::new(_).size(SMALL))` lives in **mde-shell-egui (~36 sites) + mde-files-egui** → converting via disjoint farm workers (shell→.130 · files→.90). **mde-music-egui + mde-voice-egui = correct NO-OP** — a normalized scan found **0** pure-idiom sites there; they use *variant* forms instead (a plain `colored_label(TEXT_DIM, "…")` at default BODY size, and `label(RichText::new(x).size(SMALL).color(TEXT_DIM))`). Folding those in is a **separate follow-up (E12-POLISH-2?)** that needs a call: the plain-`&str` ones are a deliberate BODY→SMALL size change (not a no-behavior consolidation). ⚠️ **Process lesson (this unit):** `isolation:"worktree"` workers branch from **pushed master**, NOT the coordinator's local unpushed HEAD — the first dispatch flailed because the helper commit was unpushed; fixed by pushing the helper first ([[serialize-same-file-units]] dependency, generalized → [[workers-branch-from-pushed-master]]). **✅ DONE 2026-07-01: 44 sites consolidated across all 4 surfaces** — shell 36 (`621a648`, 7 files, net −82 lines) · music 4 (`602c327`) · voice 2 (`aa4103d`) · files 2 (self). Combined integration gate on BigBoy green: mde-egui 9 · files 18 · music 15 · shell 79 · voice 11 tests, **0 failed, 0 new clippy warnings, no compile errors**. The plain-`&str` BODY-size loaders were deliberately left (folding them is a visible size change — a separate call, if ever).
- [✓] **E12-POLISH-2: shared `mde_egui::status_dot` — consolidate the duplicated status-dot primitive.** *(session=819545e6)* **As** a mesh operator, **I want** the inline health/presence dot to come from one shared widget, **so that** the drawing primitive isn't hand-rolled per surface. **Acceptance** (runtime-observable): `status_dot(ui, color)` lives in `mde-egui` (Style-driven `SP_S` circle + headless test); mde-files-egui + mde-voice-egui drop their **byte-identical** local copies and call the shared one; each crate builds+tests+clippy-clean. **DONE (this session, solo — the primitive was found byte-identical in both surfaces):** `mde_egui::status_dot` added to `widgets.rs`; files (3 call sites) + voice (1) repointed, both local `fn status_dot` deleted; `color` stays a `Style` palette token (no raw literal). **✅ Gate green on .90: mde-egui 10 (incl. the new headless test) / files 18 / voice 11 tests, 0 failed; clippy 0 new (mde-egui clean); net −12 lines.**
- [✓] **E12-POLISH-3: shared `mde_egui::field` — dedupe the labelled-value row across shell panels.** *(session=819545e6)* **As** a mesh operator, **I want** the "dim label + toned value" row to come from one shared widget, **so that** the shell's 4 panels don't each carry a copy. **Acceptance** (runtime-observable): `field(ui, label, value, tone)` lives in `mde-egui` (Style-driven + exercised in the headless test); mde-shell-egui's controller/provisioning/thisnode/network drop their **4 byte-identical** local `fn field` (confirmed same md5) and call the shared one (12 call sites); `tone` stays a `Style` palette token; mde-shell-egui builds+tests+clippy-clean. **✅ DONE 2026-07-01:** helper landed on master (`fb53c03`); the 4 byte-identical `fn field` defs deleted + **all 20 call sites repointed** to `mde_egui::field` across controller/provisioning/thisnode/network (12 single-line + 8 multi-line — the farm gate caught the 8 multi-line ones a local single-line sed missed, the whole point of gating). Re-gate green on BigBoy: mde-shell-egui **79 passed, 0 failed**; clippy 0 new (88 pre-existing baseline); fmt clean. `mde_egui::field` now has real consumers (closes the §7 dead-helper risk). *(The dispatched worker stalled at startup — 148-byte output, no build; taken over + done solo. [[workers-branch-from-pushed-master]] + a new failure mode: a worker can silently stall producing nothing.)*

## ONBOARD-WIZARD — first-time onboarding wizard (operator 2026-06-30; design: docs/design/onboarding-wizard.md)
One installer takes a bare box from first boot to a working mesh: role-branched (**Lighthouse / Workstation** — 2 roles), create-new-mesh or join, optional lighthouse spawn + back-office services. `mackesd` is the engine; egui (display) + ratatui (headless Workstation) front-ends. **⚠️ REVISED 2026-06-30 (operator "lock it"): 2-role model · Fedora+KVM (no XCP-NG role) · same-stack-everywhere (role = config) — see the design doc's REVISION block + `docs/design/mesh-virt-management.md`. OW-9 (XCP-NG provisioning) is DROPPED; the KVM service work is the MESH-VIRT epic below; OW-1 chooser is binary (Lighthouse/Workstation).** All 50 survey locks remain in the doc as the record.

- [✓] **OW-1: Role-chooser → wizard entry (role + new/join + DISCLAIMER).** **As** a new operator, **I want** the first-run role-chooser to capture role *and* create-new-mesh-vs-join after a DISCLAIMER ack, **so that** it launches exactly the right flow. **Acceptance:** no-role-pinned autostart shows `mde-disclaimer` (ack required) → role + intent; "Create New Mesh" is hard-absent on Lighthouse/XCP-NG; pins the role; launches the role+intent wizard (egui on WS, ratatui on headless).
- [✓] **OW-2: `mackesd` onboard engine.** **As** the wizard, **I want** `mackesd` to expose the onboard verbs (`mesh-create`/`invite-issue`/`enroll`/`role-provision`/`spawn-lighthouse`/`ca-migrate`/`service-add`/`mesh-dns`/`self-test`/`revoke`), **so that** both front-ends drive one engine, not scripts. **Acceptance:** each verb runs headless + is unit-tested; the egui/TUI wizards call them. *(landed `69cd793`: onboard dispatcher + self-test + role-provision, 14 tests; mesh-create=OW-3, invite=OW-4, enroll=OW-5, mesh-dns/network=OW-6)*
- [✓] **OW-3: Create-mesh — founding Workstation CA bootstrap.** **As** a Workstation operator, **I want** Create to mint the Nebula CA + auto mesh-id (optional friendly label) and bring up a LAN-only overlay, **so that** a lone WS is a working mesh-of-one with no internet. **Acceptance:** create → CA on the WS + overlay up + egui shell renders; `mde-mesh-view` shows one node; fully offline. *(mesh-create verb landed `932d5d4`: mints CA+mesh-id+LAN overlay, reuses mesh_init, idempotent, 10 tests; end-to-end shell-render is E12-3-gated)*
- [✓] **OW-4: Invite + Join — QR/short-code enrollment.** **As** an operator adding a box, **I want** any enrolled node to issue a short-TTL mesh-scoped invite (QR + typeable code) and a new box to scan/type it → CSR → bundle, **so that** joining is fast + decentralized. **Acceptance:** a member issues an invite (minutes TTL); a 2nd box joins via the code, gets an overlay IP, is reachable; expired/foreign codes refused. *(invite-issue verb landed `68a4b16`: signed short-TTL mesh-scoped code, ledger-recorded, 12 tests; join/enroll redemption = the completing half via nebula_enroll_client)* **LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 (11.3.1 fleet):** `mackesd onboard invite-issue` on LH1 mints a valid 15-min mesh-scoped invite (typeable `MDEINV1-…` code + `mde-invite:` QR) for `magic-mesh` — the ISSUE half is confirmed live; the JOIN redemption still needs a fresh node. **JOIN HALF LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 (operator lifted the test-node gate → I spun a throwaway DO droplet):** a fresh Fedora-43 droplet installed mackesd 11.3.1 from the channel and **joined magic-mesh headlessly via `mackesd join '<v3-token>' --role workstation`** (token minted on LH1 by `mackesd add-peer`) — got overlay **10.42.0.4**, nebula+mackesd+mesh-health boot-durable+running, convergence playbook written, and is **REACHABLE over the overlay from LH1 (ping ✓)**. That is OW-4's join acceptance, proven end-to-end. **⚠️ REAL GAP found via the live-verify ([[live-verify-deploys]]):** the OW-4 wizard's `onboard invite-issue` mints a self-contained `MDEINV1-{mesh_id,exp,secret}` code, but the headless `mackesd join` **rejects it** — it needs the v3 form `mesh:<id>@<ip>:<port>#<bearer>?fp=<sha256>` (which carries the lighthouse `/enroll` endpoint + the pinned cert fingerprint). So the wizard invite doesn't drive the headless join. **OW-4 completing work:** make `invite-issue` mint a full v3-shaped token (embed this mesh's lighthouse endpoint(s) + `/enroll` fp), OR teach `mackesd join`/a headless `onboard enroll` to accept the MDEINV1 code + resolve the endpoint from the local roster. (Also: the first enroll attempt timed out at 20s but a retry succeeded in 4ms — the `/enroll` endpoint is responsive; likely a transient cold-start, worth a longer initial enroll timeout.)
  - **OW-4 gap resolved 2026-07-09:** `onboard invite-issue` still prints the short `MDEINV1` code and `mde-invite:` QR, and now also prints an endpoint-bearing `mesh:<id>@<ip>:<port>#<bearer>?fp=<sha256>` `join-token` plus a ready `mackesd join '<token>'` line whenever the local `/enroll` endpoint certificate/address are available. The v3 token does not mint a second capability: its bearer is the same canonical invite payload recorded by `invite::issue`, so the short code and join-token spend the same ledger entry. `JOIN_TOKEN_MAX_LEN` is 512 to cover the canonical invite payload bearer plus endpoint fingerprint. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `ow4-invite` `cargo test -p mackesd invite --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 18/18 invite tests, including `to_join_token_maps_mdeinv1_onto_the_v3_enroll_inputs` round-tripping through `nebula_enroll::parse_join_token`. `git diff --check` clean. Package-level `cargo fmt --check -p mackesd` remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated unformatted `mackesd` dirty files; the rustfmt delta in this slice was fixed.
- [✓] **OW-5: Network bring-up (NM-keyfile + LAN detect).** **As** the wizard, **I want** onboarding to write the NM keyfile + detect DHCP-vs-static, **so that** a static-only no-DHCP LAN comes up before the overlay. **Acceptance:** a fresh box on a no-DHCP LAN reaches the LAN + overlay after onboarding (the cloud-init NM-keyfile fix applied). *(network verb landed `c927289`: DHCP-vs-static detect via router_discovery + NM keyfile render, 13 tests; live bring-up integration-gated)*
- [✓] **OW-6: Mesh DNS — `<host>.<mesh>`.** *(session=819545e6, d9db4cc)* **As** an operator, **I want** nodes resolvable by `<host>.<mesh>` over the overlay, **so that** I needn't memorize Nebula IPs. **Acceptance:** after two nodes join, each resolves+reaches the other by name over the overlay (served/synced by the CA holder). **DONE `d9db4cc`:** `onboard mesh-dns` verb — folds the replicated peer roster (`mackes_mesh_types::peers`, reusing `PeerRecord.overlay_ip` whose own doc names Mesh-DNS as consumer) into a `<host>.<mesh-id>`→overlay-IP zone + writes a managed `/etc/hosts` block (distinct markers from the runtime `workers/mesh_dns.rs` reconciler, `IpAddr`-typed, `mesh_id`-parameterized). Pure `build_zone`/`render_hosts` core + injectable `EtcHosts` apply seam; `--dry-run` prints zone+block. Farm-green on .90 (mackesd 2310 lib tests + 9 onboard, clippy/fmt 0); hand-merge with OW-5's `network` verb re-gated green.
- [>] **OW-7: Spawn Lighthouse (Workbench · cloud|local · pair · CA-migrate).** *(session=819545e6, 6cff8a2)* **As** a Workstation operator, **I want** a Workbench "Spawn Lighthouse" action that provisions a cloud (zone1-do IaC) or local (cloud-hypervisor) lighthouse — offering a pair — push-provisioned over SSH, then migrates the CA to it, **so that** off-LAN reach + always-on signing exist. **Acceptance:** the action stands up a reachable lighthouse (pair offered), push-provisioned (RPM+enroll); CA migrates; off-LAN join works; no-cloud-token → stays LAN-only + a retry action. **CORE DONE `6cff8a2`:** the `onboard spawn-lighthouse` verb — `plan_spawn` (cloud DO / local cloud-hypervisor, `--pair` HA quorum) → `SpawnPlan` with provision spec + enroll bootstrap + ordered `CaMigrationStep`s; injectable `Provisioner` seam (`LiveProvisioner` integration-gated); no-token → `LanOnly` + retry; `--dry-run`. Farm-green (mackesd 2324 lib tests, 14 new; fmt clean). **REMAINING (integration/GUI-gated, stays [>]):** the live cloud/SSH provision + real CA-move (needs the DO token + live SSH — classifier-gated), and the Workbench "Spawn Lighthouse" button.
  - **QUASAR-CLOUD supersession 2026-07-10:** QC-15 deleted the old local
    cloud-hypervisor lighthouse option. `spawn-lighthouse` is now cloud-only
    (DigitalOcean/zone1-do defaults) with the existing `--pair`, no-cloud-token
    LAN-only, push-enroll, and CA-migration semantics intact.
