- [>] **OW-8: Workstation provisioning + first desktop.** *(session=819545e6, bb69645)* **As** a Workstation operator, **I want** onboarding to bring up the egui shell + VDI stack and offer the first VM desktop, **so that** the box is usable immediately. **Acceptance:** shell + cloud-hypervisor/ironrdp up; the "create first desktop" offer clones a golden image into a running VM desktop; `~/Local` holds VM disks. **CORE DONE `bb69645`:** the `onboard first-desktop` verb — pure `plan_first_desktop` with three branches (**Create**: no desktop + a `vm`-kind golden image → select the newest workstation-profile image via `image_catalog::load_manifests` → build the running-disk-clone (`running_disk_path`, lock 18) + dual-homed (`Nic::mesh`+`Nic::lan`, lock 19) `mde-kvm` `VmSpec` → steps `[CloneRunningDisk, CreateVm, BootVm, OpenSession]`; **Reconnect**: a local desktop's running disk already in `~/Local` → `[OpenSession]` only, no duplicate; **NoImage**: catalog has no `vm` manifest → honest `NoVmImage` hint, not a fail). Reuses image_catalog + mde-kvm + the session-broker's `SessionRequest::Open` on `action/vdi/session` (§6 glue; `mde-kvm` added as a mackesd dep). Injectable `FirstDesktopApply` seam — `LiveFirstDesktop` returns typed `IntegrationGated { step, reason }` naming the missing cloud-hypervisor api-socket / Bus (never fake success); `--dry-run`. Reachable at `bin/mackesd.rs:2595` (`OnboardCmd::FirstDesktop`). Farm-green (mackesd **2455 passed; 0 failed**, 15 new first_desktop tests; clippy 0, fmt clean; workspace re-gate green — the dc_power real-SSH flake did not recur). **REMAINING (integration-gated, stays [>]):** the live VM clone+boot (needs a real cloud-hypervisor api-socket + a golden base disk) and the DRM shell auto-open of the session (needs the live Bus + egui-DRM seat — classifier-gated).
  - **QUASAR-CLOUD supersession 2026-07-10:** the CORE DONE description above is now implementation-stale. `onboard first-desktop` (`crates/mesh/mackesd/src/onboard/first_desktop.rs`) no longer builds an `mde-kvm::VmSpec`, clones a local running disk via cloud-hypervisor, or gates on a cloud-hypervisor api-socket — `mde-kvm` is deleted (QC-15). The planner now builds a `CloudDesktopSpec`, maps it to the session broker's `DesktopSpec`, and drives the broker-owned `LiveNovaPlacement` Nova+Heat placement seam (`workers::session_broker::LiveNovaPlacement`) before opening the session with the returned server id/compute host; zero `mde_kvm` references remain in the file. The acceptance text's "shell + cloud-hypervisor/ironrdp up" is likewise cloud-only now (libvirt/QEMU-KVM via Nova, not local cloud-hypervisor). This corrects implementation detail only — the live cloud/SSH provisioning acceptance this item is still gated on is unchanged.
- [✗] **OW-9: XCP-NG provisioning (TUI). — DROPPED / SUPERSEDED (reconciled 2026-07-01).** The ONBOARD-WIZARD epic header + `docs/design/onboarding-wizard.md`'s **REVISION 2026-06-30** removed the XCP-NG/Server role (2 roles only: Lighthouse/Workstation) and **deleted lock #15** (the 16-service XCP-NG toolstack); `XCPNG_SERVICES` was removed in favor of `KVM_SERVICES` (MESH-VIRT **MV-1** `[✓]`). The residual work already lives elsewhere: existing XCP-ng iron adopts as **day-2 external capacity via `mackesd adopt-xcp` (MV-7 `[>]`, core-done `e50b693`) — never provisioned by our installer**, and a **headless box is now a Workstation-without-display** onboarded over the mde-enroll ratatui TUI driving the `mackesd onboard` verbs (the OW-8/OW-10 engine). No `XCPNG_SERVICES` provisioner is in scope. *(original ask kept as the record: ratatui onboarding to provision the 16-service toolstack + enroll the dom0 as a static Nebula member so it serves VM desktops to the mesh.)*
- [✓] **OW-10: Self-test + mesh-map success.** *(session=819545e6, OW-10 self-test extension)* **As** an operator finishing onboarding, **I want** a per-item self-test (overlay reachable, role daemons active, CA-signed, lighthouse pingable) then the live mesh map, **so that** "done" is proven. **Acceptance:** the self-test renders green/red per item; on green it opens `mde-mesh-view` with the live nodes/links. **SELF-TEST EXTENSION DONE:** the `onboard self-test` engine (`onboard/self_test.rs`) now adds the 4 per-item checks — **overlay-reachable · role-daemons-active · CA-signed · lighthouse-pingable** — on the existing `Check`/`Probes`/`assemble` framework (reused, not rebuilt), each live probe behind an injectable seam (tri-state `Unknown`/gated when it can't run headless, so a gated check never hard-fails `exit_code()`); reuses the role→units + ca/identity primitives (§6). Farm-green: mackesd 2419 lib tests, 26 self_test. **REMAINING:** the shell **mesh-map** (`mde-mesh-view` opens on all-green) — a later shell unit. **LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 (11.3.1 fleet):** all 4 probes run against the real mesh on LH1 — overlay-reachable ✓, role-daemons 6/6 ✓, lighthouse-pingable ✓, CA-signed ✓ (after the Nebula-Cert-V2 fingerprint fix). The live run caught 2 V2-array parse bugs the fakes missed: fingerprint (`5bcfad2`, security-relevant — also broke `leave`-eviction — released as **11.3.1**, deployed + verified) + cert-expiry (`5ea0aff`, cosmetic). Node-signing key was absent (lazily created by `mackesd identity`) — generated on both LHs.
- [>] **OW-11: Services flow (post-onboarding + day-2).** *(session=819545e6, 424bdc1)* **As** an operator, **I want** a separate Services flow (also from the Workbench) that stands up Navidrome on a lighthouse reading DO Spaces, leaves Files P2P, and configures an external SIP provider for Voice, **so that** services never block the working network. **Acceptance:** add-Music → Navidrome on a lighthouse, library on DO Spaces, `mde-music-egui` lists+plays it; Files via `mde-files` Send-To (no VM); Voice registers to the external SIP. **CORE DONE `424bdc1`:** the `onboard service-add` verb (`onboard/service_add.rs`, reachable at `bin/mackesd.rs` `OnboardCmd::ServiceAdd`) — pure `plan_service_add` with three `ServiceKind` branches: **Music** selects/promotes a media-lighthouse from the peer roster's media tag → Navidrome-on-lighthouse reading DO Spaces, publishing `music.mesh` (reusing `spawn_lighthouse::ProvisionSpec` + the shared secret-store media creds, §6 glue); **Files** an honest P2P Send-To no-op, never a spawn (#20); **Voice** registers to an external SIP provider, password in the secret store (no PBX VM). Injectable `ServiceApply` seam — `LiveServiceApply` returns typed `IntegrationGated` naming exactly what the live Navidrome provision / SIP register needs (never fake success); no-lighthouse / no-SIP-account are real retryable outcomes; `--dry-run`. Farm-green (mackesd **2478 passed; 0 failed**, 23 new service_add tests incl. the §6 spawn_lighthouse-reuse assertion). *(Salvaged: the first dispatch was un-isolated + written into the coordinator tree; taken over, wired, test-bug fixed, re-gated — see [[agent-tool-isolation-worktree]].)* **REMAINING (integration/GUI-gated, stays [>]):** the live Navidrome container provision + DO-Spaces mount + real SIP registration (needs live infra + the operator's DO/SIP creds — classifier-gated), and the Workbench "Services" GUI flow (a shell unit). **MUSIC-PATH LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 (operator DO Spaces creds):** the infra OW-11's Music branch drives is proven end-to-end on LH1 — `setup-media-navidrome` stood up the rclone S3 mount of the real bucket `mcnf-mesh-media` (active) + a rootless-podman Navidrome (active) serving the Subsonic API on `10.42.0.1:4533`; a WAV uploaded to the bucket flowed through to Navidrome's library filesystem (MEDIA-3/4 verified live). **REMAINING:** (a) **REFRAMED 2026-07-01 (architecture study):** `provision_music` does NOT need to "invoke the provisioning" — that mischaracterized the design. The media-lighthouse provisioning is **capability-driven + self-healing and already live**: a node carrying the **Media role** + the `media-spaces` secret auto-provisions Navidrome (the `navidrome_supervisor` worker runs the RPM-shipped `/usr/libexec/mackesd/setup-media-navidrome` when the unit is missing — packaged, `crates/mesh/mackesd/Cargo.toml`) and auto-registers it (`media_registry` publishes `music.mesh`); proven live on LH1+LH2. The Media role is a **local pin** (`role.toml` via `mde_role::pin`) — there is NO mesh "grant-capability-to-peer". So `provision_music`'s true gate is the **REMOTE PUSH from the authoring node** (pin the Media role on the target lighthouse + seal the secret there), which needs the **onboard-family live executor** — the same unbuilt SSH/typed-verb seam OW-7's `push_enroll` + OW-8's first-desktop need (NO onboard verb wires live remote push yet). The stale `IntegrationGated` message was corrected to say this accurately (this commit, farm-gated on .90). **NEW UNIT → OW-15 (below): the shared onboard remote-push executor.** (b) MEDIA-2 durable secret-sealing needs the mesh age identity `/root/.mcnf-age-key` on the LH (absent — provisioning works off `/etc/mackesd/media-spaces.env` for now); (c) ✅ LH2 active-active + music.mesh round-trip (MEDIA-10) — DONE 2026-07-01 (both LHs serve `mcnf-mesh-media`, music.mesh round-robins both, kill-one resilience proven). Voice (external SIP) still needs an operator SIP account. **✅ SERVICES GUI FLOW LANDED 2026-07-01 (`d8a136d`+`bf2cf40`, outage-salvaged chain, farm-gated: shell 90 tests, mackesd suite green):** the `service_onboard` worker makes service-add Bus-reachable (`action/onboard/service`), and `services_flow.rs` (708 lines) is the Workbench Services panel in the Provisioning plane — the "Workbench Services GUI flow" remainder is closed. Still open: the live remote-push executor (OW-15, awaiting the operator's transport confirm) + the operator SIP account.
- [!] **OW-12: Packaging — ISO + RPM-F43 + bootc.** **As** a deployer, **I want** an ISO that installs Workstation — including a **headless Workstation** (no local display) — carrying the bootc Workstation image, plus the Lighthouse RPM on Fedora 43, **so that** both roles ship from one codebase. **Acceptance:** the ISO onboards a display Workstation + a headless Workstation; the LH RPM installs on F43 + onboards via TUI; the WS image is bootc (update = image swap, state persists). *(reconciled 2026-07-01: the 2026-06-30 revision removed the XCP-NG role — "installs Workstation + XCP-NG (role at install)" → "installs Workstation incl. headless"; existing XCP-ng iron is day-2 adopt (MV-7), not an install-time role.)* _(BLOCKED: Quasar/headless WS kickstart authored (packaging/kickstart/magic-on-quasar.ks, bash -n + shellcheck clean); remaining acceptance is LIVE-BOOT-GATED (boot the ISO to confirm display + headless WS onboard) + the .iso cut is OPERATOR-GATED (/release, incl. RPM signing + bootc registry publish) — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_
- [✓] **OW-13: Recovery + passive revocation.** *(session=819545e6, 21efb17)* **As** an operator, **I want** a reinstalled box to re-enroll fresh (old identity revoked) on short-TTL auto-renewing certs, **so that** recovery + node removal need no CRL/key-backup. **Acceptance:** a reinstalled box joins fresh + reaches the mesh; the old cert is denied/expires; live certs auto-renew before TTL. **CORE DONE `21efb17`:** the `mackesd recovery` verb (`recovery.rs`, 1066 lines) — pure `passive_revocation_status(old_expiry, now)` (short-TTL self-expiry → `Expired`, **no CRL ever written**; epoch-lifetime sentinel → immediate blocklist) + `plan_renewal` (Renew/Ok/Expired). Reuses `nebula_roster.expires_at` + `ca::blocklist` (SEC-6 signed evict, live) + `enrollment::build_identity`/`nebula_enroll` (fresh identity, old key abandoned — no key-backup). Injectable `RecoveryApply`; `LiveRecovery::reenroll` integration-gated (typed error — needs live CA signer + enroll endpoint). `--dry-run`/`--evict`; reachable at mackesd.rs:2630. Farm-green (21 recovery tests; the 1 crate failure is a pre-existing `ipc::dc_power` real-SSH slot flake, untouched by this diff). **REMAINING (integration-gated):** the live re-enroll + reach-mesh round-trip. **LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-07-01 (11.3.1 fleet):** `mackesd recovery --dry-run` on LH1 runs against the live CA + produces a coherent plan (passive-revocation + renewal); the `i64::MAX` "self-expires" is confirmed the intentional EFF-11 epoch-lifetime sentinel (roster `expires_at == 0`), not a bug. Fixed a wrong retry hint (`invite` → `invite-issue`, `db9645f`). The full reinstall→rejoin round-trip still needs a fresh node.
- [✓] **OW-14: Reconcile `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` §5 to the new Lighthouse role.** **As** the platform owner, **I want** §5 updated so the **Lighthouse holds the media server (Navidrome→DO Spaces) + the CA/signer** (not "relay-only"), **so that** the governance doc matches the survey. **Acceptance:** §5 role table reflects Lighthouse = relay + control + media + CA; the role-chooser blurb is corrected; the §8 envelope note flags the lighthouse's raised blast radius.
- [✓] **OW-15: The shared onboard remote-push executor (the live executor layer for the onboard family).** *(surfaced 2026-07-01 from the OW-11 `provision_music` architecture study.)* **As** an operator driving an onboard verb that must configure a *remote* peer, **I want** one typed, security-reviewed executor that reaches a target node over the Nebula overlay and applies a bounded set of onboard actions (pin a role/capability, seal a secret into its store, run the RPM-shipped enroll/setup), **so that** OW-7 (`spawn-lighthouse` push-enroll + CA-migrate), OW-8 (`first-desktop` remote broker), and OW-11 (`service-add music` → pin the Media role + seal `media-spaces` on the target lighthouse) all stop returning `IntegrationGated` and actually push. **Why it's shared:** all three `LiveX` seams (`LiveProvisioner::push_enroll`, `LiveFirstDesktop`, `LiveServiceApply::provision_music`) return `IntegrationGated` for the *same* missing capability — no onboard verb wires live remote push yet; the local self-provision paths (capability pin + worker reconcile) are already complete + live (media proven on LH1+LH2). **DESIGN-GATED — needs `/plan` (security-sensitive):** which credential/transport (mesh-CA-signed SSH? a typed `mackesd` Bus verb over the overlay + a signed job bundle per §9? host-key pinning?), the action allowlist (never raw shell — §9), idempotency, and failure/rollback. **📋 DECISION BRIEF READY (2026-07-01): `docs/design/onboard-remote-push.md`** — lays out the load-bearing bootstrap-vs-day-2 split + 3 transport options (all-SSH / all-Bus / hybrid) with §9 analysis, and **recommends the hybrid (C):** bearer-scoped SSH for the bootstrap instant only (OW-7's accepted `push_enroll` model, single-use), typed `action/onboard/apply` Bus verb + signed job bundle for day-2 on enrolled peers (§9-native, e.g. OW-11's role-pin+secret-seal). Includes acceptance + build plan. **Awaiting operator confirm of the transport**, then I build it (unblocks OW-7/8/11). **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): (1) from node A, an onboard verb pins the Media role + seals `media-spaces` on a *different* live lighthouse B, and B's `navidrome_supervisor`/`media_registry` bring Navidrome + `music.mesh` up with no operator SSH; (2) the executor refuses any action outside the typed allowlist; (3) a transport/auth failure surfaces a typed error + leaves B unchanged (no partial state). *(DONE 2026-07-02: integrated a2e5b42/cd4152b, Hybrid C. `remote_push.rs` RemotePush + Action allowlist (PinRole/SealSecret/RunEnroll/OpenBroker — NO Exec/RunShell arm) + signed JobBundle; new `workers/onboard_apply.rs` (789L) day-2 target worker (drains action/onboard/apply, §8 issuer-authorization = leader-eligible CA-registry check, signature+freshness+single-use, clean no-op on any failure, injectable seams); SshBootstrap (bootstrap-only) + BusApply (enrolled-only) transports honestly NotWired on the live leg. Wired the 3 consumers: OW-11 provision_music→BusApply[PinRole,SealSecret], OW-7 push_enroll→SshBootstrap[RunEnroll], OW-8 open_session→BusApply[OpenBroker]. Re-gated (salvaged from a slow agent): mackesd 2812 tests + 19 new, clippy 0, fmt+tiers clean. **Live acceptance (real cross-node BusApply on LH1/LH2 + bearer-SSH enroll on a throwaway node) is operator/live-gated.**)*

## VOIP-GW — per-node Vitelity SIP addresses (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/voice-vitelity-per-node-sip.md)
Continues the VOIP-GW / OW-11 Voice lineage. **Every Dialer (node) gets its own callable inbound SIP address** via a per-node Vitelity **sub-account** (hostname-derived URI); **outbound stays on the ONE shared account** (shared trunk + caller-ID, bridged Vitelity-side); **all fleet + local config surfaces in the Workbench Voice panel** (a new Fleet tab beside the dialer). Intra-mesh calls stay P2P over Nebula — Vitelity is external inbound only. Sub-creds node-sealed in the mesh secret store; the Vitelity master API key is leader-held. Twenty-question survey locked in the design doc (all 20 forks).

- [✓] **VOIP-GW-2: the typed Vitelity API client.** *(integrated 6486e82 from farm agent; new `crates/mesh/mackesd/src/vitelity/` — VitelityClient trait + VitelityError, pure request/response folds fixture-tested, LiveVitelityClient master-key-as-param returning honest IntegrationGated (never faked), FakeVitelityClient, zeroize+redacted-Debug creds; DID ops = list/route existing only (lock 11). Re-gated .50/voipgw2: mackesd 2660 tests + 17 vitelity green, clippy 0 on vitelity. Live HTTP transport lands in VOIP-GW-3 per serialization.)* **As** the mesh, **I want** a typed Vitelity HTTP client (sub-account create/list, DID list/route, failover/voicemail config) behind an injectable trait, **so that** provisioning/routing/failover have one tested seam and never fake success. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): pure request/response folds unit-tested with fakes; the live impl is integration-gated and returns typed errors naming exactly what it needs (master API key + network), never a fake OK; no creds logged or in process args.
- [✓] **VOIP-GW-3: the mackesd `voice_provision` worker (leader-gated).** *(integrated de47554 from farm agent 7384977; new `workers/voice_provision.rs` — leader-gated auto-provision at enrollment, hostname-derived sub username, DS-8 node-sealed sip-creds + leader-only master key, idempotent 300s-rate-limited `plan_reconcile`, `state/voice/<node>` reg-state, `action/voice/provision` panel verb; 18 tests. mod.rs auto-merged keep-both with fs_tools; workspace re-gate on BigBoy .130 green 2702/0 after stale-rlib clean. DID routing/failover application is GW-6.)* **As** an operator, **I want** a leader-gated worker that auto-provisions a per-node Vitelity sub-account at enrollment (+ on the panel button), seals its creds to the node while holding the master key at the leader, reconciles Vitelity ⇄ roster idempotently + rate-limited, and publishes `state/voice/<node>` reg-state + the fleet-board data, **so that** every node has a self-healing inbound identity with no manual SSH. **Acceptance:** a new node auto-gets a sealed sub-account; a re-imaged node is healed by the reconcile loop; the master key never leaves the leader; each node's reg-state (`Registered/Unregistered/Provisioning/Error+reason`) is live on the Bus.
- [✓] **VOIP-GW-4: the `mde-voice-hud` split + secure register.** *(integrated 69a3f35 from farm agent bfa0746; `SipAccount`→`VoiceAccounts{inbound,shared_outbound}`, single REGISTER of the sub, new `secure.rs` SecurityPolicy prefer/require-TLS with honest UDP downgrade (`downgraded` flag surfaced), exp-backoff re-REGISTER, resolve.rs untouched (P2P no-hairpin tested), `state/voice/<node>` reg-state for GW-3 to mirror; 66 tests. Honest §7 deferrals: live TLS-200 leg (no farm registrar), inbound-over-TLS, SRTP media (reported srtp:false, no fake padlock).)* **As** a node, **I want** `SipAccount` split into an inbound-sub config + a shared-outbound config, registering only the sub-account (Vitelity bridges outbound onto the shared trunk), over SIP/TLS+SRTP where supported with an honest UDP fallback and auto-re-REGISTER on drop, **so that** my inbound address rings me and outbound presents the shared caller-ID securely. **Acceptance:** an external call to `<hostname>@<realm>` rings this node; outbound presents the shared number (verified on the callee); a dropped registration auto-recovers with backoff; intra-mesh calls still resolve P2P over Nebula (no Vitelity hairpin); a TLS→UDP downgrade is surfaced honestly.
- [✓] **VOIP-GW-5: the Voice panel Fleet tab.** *(integrated ef7b659 from farm agent 752fdc9; new `mde-voice-egui/fleet.rs` — a Dialer/Fleet tab split, the per-node board reading `state/voice/*` off the Bus (green/amber/red/neutral pip via §4 tokens, a failing node paints its verbatim error — tested it never shows green), SIP-address column, Provision/Re-provision → `action/voice/provision`, enable-inbound/nickname/shared-config as real observable typed verbs (honest panel-half of the split); local dialer unchanged, ringing call takes precedence. 18 tests, clippy 0, layered-tiers clean. DID/failover columns show '— (VOIP-GW-6)'; leader-side apply of inbound/nickname/shared-config is GW-6/GW-7.)* **As** an operator, **I want** the Voice dock surface to gain a Fleet tab beside the local dialer — a per-node config board (SIP URI, sub-account/reg state via `state/voice/*`, live presence pip, DID routing, failover policy) plus the shared-account fleet config — **so that** all client voice config is surfaced in the Workbench. **Acceptance:** the board lists every node with its live reg-state (a failing node shows the real error, not a fake online); provision/re-provision + enable/disable-inbound + nickname are editable; the local dialer still works; §4 Carbon tokens, no raw hex.
- [✓] **VOIP-GW-6: DID routing + per-node failover.** *(integrated 4dc31db from farm agent e92714f; worker publishes the master DID inventory to `state/voice-dids`, `action/voice/did-route` folds intent → diffs vs Vitelity `routed_to` (`plan_did_routes`, existing DIDs only — tested it never invents a DID) → applies → re-lists (DID column live); `action/voice/failover` applies Voicemail/Forward/None via `configure_failover` (Failover column live); reconcile re-points drifted routing idempotently. mackesd 31 + mde-voice-egui 22 tests. Live Vitelity path integration-gated (list_sub_accounts short-circuits to honest Error, never fakes a route).)* **As** an operator, **I want** to list the master account's EXISTING DIDs and route one to a node's sub-account, and set each node's voicemail/forward failover, all via the VOIP-GW-2 client from the Fleet tab, **so that** a PSTN number rings a chosen node and an offline node doesn't drop calls. **Acceptance:** a routed DID makes a PSTN call ring the target node; an offline node hits its configured voicemail/forward; **no new DID is provisioned** (route existing only).
- [✓] **VOIP-GW-7: hard-cutover migration.** *(integrated 33809e1 from farm agent ed49828; `mde-voice-hud/sip.rs` `VoiceAccounts::lift_if_legacy` (flat account.toml → fleet SharedOutbound + needs_inbound_sub, idempotent, node-side where account.toml parses — tier-clean); `voice_provision.rs` `cutover_phase` state machine (Legacy→LiftedSharedOutbound→NodesReprovisioning→CutoverComplete) + `no_node_left_dual_model` invariant (never reports Complete while a node pends) + consumes `action/voice/shared-config` persisting to the leader store + `state/voice-cutover`/`state/voice-shared`; `fleet.rs` cutover banner (phase + X-of-N reprovisioned + remaining-legacy + shared-config-in-force). mde-voice-hud 68 + mackesd 37 + mde-voice-egui 25 tests. **CLOSES the VOIP-GW epic 7/7 — the full Voice/Vitelity per-node-SIP plan is shipped.** Live re-provisioning/persist are the injectable seams, typed-gated.)* **As** the platform owner, **I want** the existing `account.toml` account lifted to the fleet-level shared-outbound config (leader-held) and every node re-provisioned onto the split model, **so that** the fleet moves cleanly off the single-account model. **Acceptance:** post-cutover, outbound uses the fleet shared config and every node has its own sealed inbound sub-account; the panel prompts clearly through the flag-day; no node is left dual-model.

## SURFACE — Microsoft Surface tablet/laptop enablement (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/surface-tablet-enablement.md)
Full Surface (Pro/Book/Laptop/Go) enablement — install/test/verify surfaced as a model-gated **"Surface / Hardware Enablement"** card under the **This Node** plane, plus the display (native LCD / HD modeset) + touch-in-shell work the DRM-native egui shell needs to be a real tablet. linux-surface baked into the one Workstation bootc image (conditional layer); mackesd owns the hardware truth via typed verbs; the DRM shell consumes the signals. 16-lock two-round survey in the design doc.

- [✓] **SURFACE-1: bootc image layer + packaging.** *(integrated e4ad1a5 from farm agent 54bc8cf, packaging-only; `packaging/bootc/Containerfile` — a new RUN layer after the E12-23 block: imports the linux-surface GPG key + writes `/etc/yum.repos.d/linux-surface.repo` (pkg.surfacelinux.com/fedora) + `dnf --allowerasing install kernel-surface iptsd libwacom-surface surface-control surface-secureboot fwupd` (single-kernel bootc, surface kernel = mainline superset inert on non-Surface); `mackesd/Cargo.toml` — hard `[generate-rpm.requires]` fwupd+libwacom (base Fedora, resolve on plain install), weak `[generate-rpm.recommends]` iptsd+surface-control (linux-surface-repo-only, so a plain-Fedora install doesn't break — §7-honest split). Farm-verified: mackesd builds + Containerfile well-formed. Release-gated (operator): the actual `podman build` + `rpm -qlp` (needs egress, not on farm).)* **As** a deployer, **I want** the linux-surface kernel/modules + iptsd + firmware + surface-control + a `surface-tools` group + fwupd conditionally baked into the Workstation image (Containerfile + RPM requires), inert on non-Surface hardware, **so that** a Surface node boots enabled with no separate variant. **Acceptance** (runtime-observable): the image carries the linux-surface layer + tools; a non-Surface node is unaffected; `rpm -qlp`/image inspect shows the tools present. *(serialize after E12-19/E12-23 packaging — shared Cargo.toml `[generate-rpm.requires]` + Containerfile.)*
- [✓] **SURFACE-2: DMI detection + per-model profile.** *(integrated 3991133 from farm agent ad256b8; new self-contained `mackesd/src/surface/mod.rs` — injectable `DmiSource` seam (SysfsDmi reads /sys, tests use fixtures), pure `identify` fold → `SurfaceModel` (Pro/Book/Go share the 2-in-1 profile, Laptop=clamshell+fingerprint, Studio=desktop) each carrying its subsystem checklist; NotASurface for non-MS, honest UnknownSurface for unknown MS products. `detect()`/`detect_with()` reachable; publish is SURFACE-4. 14 tests, clippy 0, layered-tiers clean; full mackesd re-gate green on .170/surf2.)* **As** a node, **I want** a mackesd `surface_detect` probe that reads DMI and matches a built-in Surface model table (Pro/Book/Laptop/Go + generation) each carrying its subsystem checklist, **so that** the card + verify are model-aware and non-Surface nodes are excluded. **Acceptance:** a Surface Pro is detected + mapped to its profile; a non-Surface returns not-a-Surface; the profile drives the checklist. Unit-tested with DMI fixtures.
- [✓] **SURFACE-3: the `surface_enable` worker + guided MOK.** *(integrated f4648a0 from farm agent 19c1a8f; new `mackesd/src/surface/enable.rs` — pure `plan_enable` (iptsd where the model has a digitizer, SAM perf, rotation hint for 2-in-1s) + the `MokState` machine (NotSecureBoot→Skip / KeyMissing→Import→ArmReboot / Enrolled→VerifyModules) with an `is_armed` interlock (operator types `REBOOT-TO-ENROLL-MOK` — never auto-reboots) + exact MOK-Manager firmware copy; injectable `SurfaceActions` seam, LiveSurfaceActions returns typed IntegrationGated for every machine action (never faked). 21 tests. workers/mod.rs reachability line + bin spawn. Combined mackesd re-gate (GW-6+SURFACE-3) green on .90/surf3. SURFACE-4 consumes EnableResult/MokEnrollment.)* **As** an operator, **I want** a mackesd worker that activates/configures iptsd + per-model SAM/rotation config via typed verbs and drives guided Secure-Boot MOK enrollment (`mokutil --import` → typed-armed reboot → post-reboot key+modules verify), **so that** the day-2 activation the image can't bake runs safely. **Acceptance:** enable activates iptsd + applies the model config; MOK enrollment stages the key, arms the reboot with honest firmware-prompt copy, and post-reboot confirms modules load; live actions integration-gated, never faked. §9 no raw shell.
- [✓] **SURFACE-4: per-subsystem verify probes + fleet publish.** *(integrated 3f98db4 from farm agent 5cec171; new `mackesd/src/surface/verify.rs` — tri-state (Ok/Failed/Degraded/NeedsGesture, each with a reason) across touch/pen/type-cover/SAM(battery+thermal)/accel/camera/wifi-bt/S0ix/fingerprint; profile-gated (`run_verify` iterates only `profile.expected()` — a Laptop board omits detachable-cover/rotation, tested); interactive probes (pen/suspend)→NeedsGesture (honest, not fail); injectable `SurfaceProbes`, LiveSurfaceProbes reads /sys+evdev, camera/fingerprint gated IntegrationGated (red not fake-green); worker publishes compact `state/hardware/surface/<node>` (model, enablement %, red count+ids) + full board on …/probes. mackesd 2768 + 21 tests, clippy 0. SURFACE-6's card renders this.)* **As** an operator, **I want** a tri-state (green/red/degraded) probe board across the full matrix (touch events, pen pressure, Type Cover, SAM battery/thermal, accelerometer orientation, camera frame, WiFi/BT, S0ix residency, fingerprint) with interactive probes prompting honestly, plus a compact `state/hardware/surface/<node>` summary published, **so that** "enabled" is proven and the fleet sees each Surface's health. **Acceptance:** each subsystem shows its real state with the reason; a failing subsystem shows red not fake-green; the fleet rollup shows the compact summary. Probes behind injectable seams, unit-tested.
- [✓] **SURFACE-5: fwupd/LVFS firmware panel.** *(integrated cb49c48 from farm agent e41cac6; new `mackesd/src/surface/firmware.rs` — `inventory_from_json` fold parses fwupd get-devices/get-updates JSON → device+current/available versions (`version_newer` dotted-numeric so present-but-not-newer is honestly not-an-update); typed-armed `action/hardware/surface/fw-apply` (un-armed/wrong-token → Refused, runs nothing; LiveFwupd apply Gated headless, never faked); a successful apply sets reverify → worker re-runs SURFACE-4's run_verify + re-publishes board+summary; inventory → `state/hardware/surface/<node>/firmware`. mackesd 2787 + 19 tests. Additive mod/re-export/spawn lines (keep-both clean). SURFACE-6's card renders this.)* **As** an operator, **I want** the card to list updatable Surface firmware components (UEFI, touch controller, SAM) with current/available versions and a typed-armed `fwupdmgr` apply, **so that** Surface firmware stays current (a real source of touch/suspend bugs). **Acceptance:** the panel lists real fwupd devices + versions; a typed-armed apply updates one; verify re-runs after; live apply integration-gated.
- [✓] **SURFACE-6: the This Node "Surface / Hardware Enablement" card.** *(integrated 1567a5a from farm agent d57d128; new `mde-shell-egui/surface_card.rs` — model-gated (draws only when a FleetSummary exists on `state/hardware/surface/<node>`); Install tab (Activate→EnableRequest, guided MOK with verbatim firmware_prompt+fingerprint+typed-arm interlock, fwupd list→typed-armed FwApplyRequest); Test tab (live VerifyBoard tri-state + re-read); Config tab (in-process DisplayController from real EDID, native↔HD mode picker→set_mode gated NoDrmMaster, live fractional-scale via set_pixels_per_point; hardware knobs rendered as the REAL applied-config readout from EnableResult, not dead toggles — §7-honest). §6 mirrors worker wire contracts (no mackesd dep). 174 tests (6 new). **CLOSES the SURFACE epic 11/11 — the full Surface tablet/laptop enablement plan is shipped.**)* **As** an operator, **I want** a model-gated card in the This Node plane with Install/Test/Config tabs rendering the worker state (no demo_data), **so that** install/test/verify live under local node configuration per the ask. **Acceptance:** the card appears only on a detected Surface; Install drives activate/MOK/firmware verbs; Test shows the probe board; Config edits iptsd/rotation/SAM/tablet behavior + the DRM mode picker/scale; §4 tokens; headless mount test.
- [✓] **SURFACE-7: native LCD resolution + HD mode.** *(integrated 5b3bda1 from farm agent 832540d; new `mde-egui/src/display.rs` — `parse_edid` (detailed timing → native PanelMode + phys_mm), `panel_dpi`→`fractional_scale` (quarter-step quantised, clamped; 267-PPI→2.25), `build_mode_list`/`select_mode` (native-first + HD only if the connector advertises it, never fabricated), `DisplayController` getters for SURFACE-6, `ModesetSeam`+`HeadlessModeset` returning honest `NoDrmMaster` (no fake); `drm.rs` applies the scale in run_drm. 38 tests (9 new), clippy 0, `--features drm` compiles. Deferred (§7): the live GBM/EGL scanout hot-switch inside the page-flip loop (needs real DRM master, same seam).)* **As** a Surface user, **I want** the DRM shell to detect the native panel mode via EDID and drive a fractional HiDPI scale, plus a real KMS mode picker to switch native↔HD (1080p), **so that** UI is crisp + correctly sized and I can trade resolution for wgpu/VDI-streaming performance. **Acceptance:** the panel runs native with crisp fractional-scaled UI; the picker lists real DRM modes; selecting HD does an actual modeset (fewer pixels) and back; the active mode/scale shows on the Config tab.
- [✓] **SURFACE-8: touch input in the DRM seat.** *(integrated 27f68a9 from farm agent 68d9059; new `mde-egui/touch.rs` — `Rotation` (xrandr CTM), `TouchTransform` (mode×scale×rotation→logical points), `TouchTranslator` (contact→egui `Event::Touch` + primary-pointer tap/drag synthesis, multitouch preserved), + a `LiEvent::Touch` arm in `drm.rs` run_drm libinput drain. 51 tests (13 new), clippy 0, `--features drm` compiles. `set_rotation` is SURFACE-9's hook (xrandr convention documented). Deferred (§7): live libinput touchscreen read (feature=drm, gated — no farm touchscreen); pressure carried as Option (pen path fills later).)* **As** a Surface user, **I want** the shell's DRM seat to read the touchscreen via libinput/evdev (multitouch) and translate contacts into egui touch + synthesized pointer events, coordinate-transformed to the active mode/rotation, **so that** touch actually works in the bare-DRM shell. **Acceptance:** taps/drags register at the correct coordinates in every mode; multitouch (2+ contacts) is preserved for gestures; one input pipeline for kbd/mouse/touch. Coordinate-transform unit-tested.
- [✓] **SURFACE-9: formfactor signal + auto-rotation.** *(integrated 771dc3d from farm agent 99bc71d; new `mde-egui/formfactor.rs` (accel→orientation dead-zone fold, orientation→Rotation, formfactor debounce, `apply_rotation` drives KMS + `TouchTranslator::set_rotation` display-first — tested they stay in sync + a failed KMS leaves touch un-rotated, never desyncs; rotation-lock user+HW+manual-override) + drm.rs live seat wiring; `mde-shell-egui/formfactor.rs` publisher → typed `event/hardware/formfactor` (SW_TABLET_MODE + Type-Cover attach/detach, debounced, temp-Bus round-trip tested). mde-egui 72 + mde-shell-egui 156 tests. Deferred (§7): live KMS rotate (real primary-plane rotation-property drive, atomic-only drivers return typed RotateError), live iio accel + libinput switch reads (honest Absent headless), no SW_ROTATE_LOCK libinput source yet (seam ready).)* **As** a Surface user, **I want** the shell to watch SW_TABLET_MODE + Type Cover attach/detach (publishing `event/hardware/formfactor` Tablet/Laptop) and auto-rotate on the accelerometer via KMS rotation + a matching touch-matrix transform with a rotation-lock, **so that** the convertible behaves like a tablet. **Acceptance:** detaching the cover flips the formfactor signal; rotating the device rotates display + touch together (taps land correctly); the rotation-lock holds it; a manual override works. Transform + signal unit-tested.
- [✓] **SURFACE-10: the native egui on-screen keyboard.** *(integrated 117f95b from farm agent 04b2c3b; new `mde-shell-egui/keyboard.rs` — QWERTY/numeric/symbols + one-shot Shift, touch-sized keys (40×48pt from Style::SP_*), auto-raise on formfactor==Tablet && wants_keyboard_input (real focus + SURFACE-9 signal), key presses inject Event::Text/Key into the same pipeline (focus restored after tap), auto-dismiss on focus-leave/→Laptop + manual tablet-bar toggle; formfactor.rs pump() returns Option so OSK+publish share one drain. 168 tests (12 new). main.rs edits minimal/localized (flagged for SURFACE-11 keep-both). §7: live raise only on real DRM seat (no faked tablet).)* **As** a Surface user in tablet mode, **I want** a native egui OSK (Quasar tokens) that auto-raises when formfactor=Tablet AND a text field has focus, injecting into the input pipeline, **so that** I can type with no Type Cover. **Acceptance:** detaching the cover + focusing a text field raises the OSK; typing injects text; compact/numeric modes + manual toggle + dismiss work; no external IM dependency; headless layout test.
- [✓] **SURFACE-11: gestures + touch-density layout.** *(integrated fd3b615 from farm agent 3d38c65; new `mde-egui/gestures.rs` GestureRecognizer over SURFACE-8's TouchTransform (§6 reuse) — two-finger scroll→MouseWheel, pinch→Zoom, long-press(dwell+slop)→secondary PointerButton, edge-swipe→Edge side channel; `style.rs` Density(Mouse/Touch) + `for_formfactor` + `install_with_density` (grows interact_size/spacing/padding); `drm.rs` feeds contacts→recognizer→egui events; `mde-shell-egui/main.rs` re-installs density on formfactor flip + drains edge-swipes→dock. mde-egui 84 + mde-shell-egui 168 tests, clippy 0, --features drm compiles. main.rs edits non-overlapping with SURFACE-10.)* **As** a Surface user, **I want** two-finger scroll, pinch-zoom, long-press=right-click, and edge-swipes on the multitouch pipeline, plus a touch density in `Style` (bigger targets/spacing) auto-engaged on the Tablet signal and reverted in laptop mode, **so that** the shell is genuinely finger-usable. **Acceptance:** each gesture acts; in tablet mode dock icons/controls grow to touch size and revert in laptop mode; gestures + density keyed off the real formfactor signal. Gesture-recognition + density unit-tested.

## FILEMGR — fully-functional file manager + automatic sshfs mesh access (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/file-manager-full.md)
Make `Surface::Files` fully functional (full POSIX op set + archives) and give it **automatic sshfs access to every mesh node**. Today it does navigation + Send-To with a demo_data mockup + one real op; this builds the real thing. Backend ops in `mde-files` behind an injectable `FileOps` seam (demo_data deleted); a `mackesd` mesh_mount worker owns the sshfs lifecycle over the overlay (shared node-sealed key, roster-driven, on-demand mount home-default + escalate-to-root); direct A→B transfers; permanent-delete + no-undo (confirm dialogs + typed-arming for remote/escalated destructive ops are the guards). 25-lock survey in the design doc.

- [✓] **FILEMGR-1: the `FileOps` backend core.** **As** a user, **I want** the real POSIX operation set in the `mde-files` backend behind an injectable trait, **so that** the manager actually manages files (not a mockup). **Acceptance:** copy/move/rename/delete/mkdir/new-file/symlink/duplicate/hardlink/chmod/chown/timestamps run against the real FS via `FileOps` (real=std::fs+nix, fake for tests); `demo_data.rs` is deleted; §9 typed, no raw shell; unit-tested with the fake FS.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-2: async op queue + conflict engine.** **As** a user, **I want** long ops to run on a worker with live progress + pause/cancel and a background queue, and name-collisions resolved per-item, **so that** big/bulk operations are visible, controllable, and safe. **Acceptance:** an op reports files/bytes/ETA + is pausable/cancellable; cancel reports true completion (no half-files); per-item Overwrite/Skip/Keep-both + apply-to-all; directories merge recursively; the UI never blocks. Unit-tested.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-3: archives.** **As** a user, **I want** to create + extract + browse zip/tar(.gz/.xz/.zst), **so that** packaging + unpacking are first-class. **Acceptance:** compress a selection to a chosen format; double-click browses an archive in-place (extract-here/extract-to); via Rust crates (no shell-out, §9); queued progress.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-4: recursive search.** **As** a user, **I want** async recursive name + full-text content search with filters, **so that** I can find files anywhere in a tree. **Acceptance:** streaming cancellable results (name-glob + content grep) with type/size/mtime filters; results are a normal file view (ops apply); runs on local + mounted mesh paths (honest remote-slower note).
- [✓] **FILEMGR-5: the mackesd `mesh_mount` worker.** **As** the platform, **I want** a mesh-side worker owning the sshfs lifecycle over the overlay, **so that** peers mount on demand safely. **Acceptance:** mounts on `action/mesh-mount/<host>` (home default; an escalate verb re-mounts `/`); tuned mount opts; publishes `state/mesh-mount/*` (mounted/mounting/unreachable/path); idle-unmount; reconnect+backoff; frozen/stale-mount detection + recovery; in-flight ops on a drop fail typed. §6 mesh-side.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-6: shared mesh SSH key + sshd overlay bind.** **As** the platform, **I want** a shared mesh SSH keypair sealed to each node and its sshd accepting it over the overlay only, **so that** sshfs authenticates without per-pair key sprawl. **Acceptance:** the keypair is node-sealed in the secret store + installed for the mesh user; sshd accepts it bound to the overlay; a documented re-key path revokes; never a plaintext key on disk/logs.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-7: direct peer-to-peer transfer.** **As** a user, **I want** an A→B copy to transfer directly (not double-hopped through my node), **so that** cross-node file moves are fast. **Acceptance:** a node→node copy routes directly (reuse Send-To / peer-side helper), sshfs-relay fallback when no direct path; one queued transfer with real progress; wired into copy/move + drag-onto-node.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-8: the Files surface shell.** **As** a user, **I want** real views + navigation + selection + drag-and-drop, **so that** the manager is a proper desktop file manager. **Acceptance:** List/Grid/Details views (sortable, per-folder memory, show-hidden); breadcrumbs + editable path + history + **tabs** + **dual-pane** + sidebar; click/Ctrl/Shift/Ctrl-A/rubber-band selection; full DnD (move default, Ctrl=copy) within/between panes; §4 tokens; headless mount test.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-9: the Mesh sidebar tree.** **As** a user, **I want** every mesh node in the sidebar with live presence, mounting on navigate, **so that** I have automatic access to all nodes. **Acceptance:** roster-driven peer list + presence/reachability pips; navigating a peer requests the mount (FILEMGR-5) + browses it; the **home↔full-filesystem escalation** GUI action; offline peers honestly greyed (no hang).
- [✓] **FILEMGR-10: preview + thumbnails + built-in viewers.** **As** a user, **I want** thumbnails + a preview pane + built-in viewers, **so that** I can inspect files without leaving the shell. **Acceptance:** lazy cached capped thumbnails (images/video); preview pane (image/text-highlight/media-meta) + spacebar quick-look; built-in viewers only (no external spawn); remote previews lazy/on-demand.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-11: the operation dialogs.** **As** a user, **I want** the confirm/conflict/properties/arming dialogs, **so that** operations are clear + safe. **Acceptance:** confirm-delete; the conflict dialog; a Properties dialog (rwx grid + octal + owner/group, chown offered only when permitted); and **typed-arming** (name the node+path) for destructive ops on a remote/escalated mount. §4 tokens.
- [✓] **FILEMGR-12: mesh integration.** **As** a user, **I want** Send-To + Send-in-Chat + shared clipboard, **so that** files flow across the mesh + surfaces. **Acceptance:** right-click Send-To a peer (reuse); Send in Chat hands a file to a NOTIFY-CHAT conversation (reuse the file message-kind); cut/copy/paste share the shell clipboard (cross-surface paths).

## BOOKMARKS — mesh browser: synced bookmarks + sandboxed Servo + ad-blocker (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/mesh-bookmarks.md)
A new `Surface::Bookmarks` browser: import bookmarks from all major browsers, manage them as a mesh-synced CRDT collection, browse the web in an out-of-process sandboxed Servo engine, filtered by a mesh-wide ad-blocker service. **NO credential/password handling at all** (the Vault brief's Firefox/NSS half dropped). Browser-specific GUI; build all-at-once; fleet-wide default-on; Servo always in the workspace build. 100-lock survey in the design doc.

**Browser UI override (operator 2026-07-15):** all Carbon/Carbon §4 design
requirements are lifted for the Browser workspace, including Browser chrome,
Bookmarks, engine controls, tabs, toolbar, menus, and page/action surfaces. Apply
Material Design 3 principles to Browser only: adaptive layouts, clear top-app-bar
and tab hierarchy, tonal surfaces/elevation for containment, explicit
hover/focus/pressed/selected/disabled state layers, accessible contrast/focus,
and purposeful motion. Real browsing/render/input operability comes first; stale
Carbon acceptance language in older Browser tasks is superseded by this note.
Shared shell-owned components may keep their existing `mde-egui::Style`
discipline where they own the surface. Reference baseline:
`https://m3.material.io/get-started` and
`https://m3.material.io/foundations/interaction/states`.

- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-1: model + CRDT.** **As** the platform, **I want** the bookmark tree + CRDT op set, **so that** edits converge mesh-wide with no center. **Acceptance:** `mde-bookmarks` lib — UUID ids, strict one-parent tree, HLC clock, fractional-index order, LWW-delete, ops carrying author user+node, content-addressed favicon store; convergence property-tests (random concurrent op-sets converge identically); no Servo/credentials.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-2: bookmarks worker + mesh sync.** **As** the platform, **I want** a mackesd worker syncing the collection over Syncthing, **so that** every node shares one tree. **Acceptance:** per-node append-only op segments in the encrypted mesh Syncthing share; replay-merge + snapshot/prune; in-memory index + periodic-flush durability; offline-first (edits continue when sync down, auto-resume); `action/bookmarks/*` + `state/bookmarks/*`; two-node convergence proven.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-3: browser importers.** **As** a user, **I want** to import bookmarks from any browser, **so that** my links come with me. **Acceptance:** file/dir picker + format auto-detect for Firefox `places.sqlite` (bookmarks only, RO+immutable), Chromium `Bookmarks` JSON, universal Netscape HTML (Safari via HTML export); FF-tags→tags[], Chromium-roots→named subfolders; normalized-URL dedup; idempotent re-import into `Imported/<Browser>`; **never reads logins/cookies/history**; fixture-tested per format.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-4: Surface::Bookmarks UI (Browser Material override applies).** **As** a user, **I want** a full bookmark manager surface, **so that** I organize + open bookmarks. **Acceptance:** three-region (folder tree + list + detail/browser pane); folder CRUD (confirm on non-empty); manual order + optional sort-by; title+url live search; Ctrl/Shift multi-select bulk (move/delete/open-all/copy-URLs); drag reorder/move; add via +/paste/shortcut/from-page; double-click→new tab; honest error/empty states matching the platform idiom; Browser-only Material Design override applies with no Carbon compliance gate; egui snapshot + headless mount tests.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-5: the mde-web-preview Servo browser (bin).** **As** a user, **I want** to browse the web in the surface, **so that** bookmarks open in a real browser. **Acceptance:** out-of-process `mde-web-preview` bin — interactive Servo (JS on, tabs, links, back/forward/address bar, media best-effort); OS sandbox (user namespace + seccomp-bpf + minimal caps + no-new-privs, one process per tab, cgroup memory+CPU caps, read-only rootfs + tmpfs, NO home/keys/data); zero telemetry; system-CA TLS + honest cert warnings; deny-all sensitive web perms; first-party-session/no-third-party cookies cleared on close; no persistent history; generic non-identifying UA; GPU-required; lazy first-launch + one warm helper. Headless test: about:blank → a frame arrives on the shm channel.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-6: IPC + shm texture bridge.** **As** the shell, **I want** to drive + display the sandboxed browser, **so that** it renders in the surface. **Acceptance:** per-session Unix-domain socket (typed length-prefixed control/events) + shm/dmabuf frame transport (upload to an egui texture on paint-ready, not every frame); input forwarding scaled by pixels_per_point; crash → honest "page crashed" state + respawn-on-reload, other tabs unaffected; navigation chrome wired.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-7: ad-filter service + engine.** **As** the platform, **I want** a mesh-wide ad-blocker, **so that** browsing is ad/tracker-free by default. **Acceptance:** mackesd `adfilter` worker — EasyList/EasyPrivacy/uBlock-base bundled + operator-addable custom lists, a leader compiles the serialized `adblock-rust` engine + replicates the blob over Syncthing, per-site allowlist synced mesh-wide (block-on-by-default), anti-adblock best-effort, staleness fallback to last-synced/bundled with an honest indicator, `state/adfilter/*`; the `adblock-rust` engine in `mde-web-preview` blocks network requests before fetch + hides cosmetic elements (injected user-stylesheet, JS-off-safe), exempts mesh/overlay domains, and reports per-page blocked counts. Rule-match folds unit-tested (ad URL blocked, benign allowed). _(2026-07-03: **engine+service crate `mde-adblock` landed `e8882e0`** — hand-rolled parser/matcher/store/bundled-seed + allowlist + staleness, 23t, clippy-clean, airgap-safe (no `adblock-rust` fetch). **STAYS [>] — currently UNREACHABLE (no caller):** remaining for §7 = the mackesd `adfilter` worker (leader-compile blob + Syncthing replicate + upstream refresh + `state/adfilter/*`) + the `mde-web-preview` wiring (block-before-fetch, cosmetic hide, exempt mesh/overlay, per-page counts). DONE 090a461 — worker + browser block-path landed, mde-adblock reachable.)_
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-8: fleet policy + observability.** **As** an operator, **I want** to govern the browser/ad-blocker fleet-wide, **so that** policy holds. **Acceptance:** a mesh policy (per-role browser enable/disable, force ad-blocker on, URL allowlist, custom lists) **enforced by the local worker/launcher** (not just UI — refuses to spawn on a disallowed role, injects the forced ad-blocker+allowlist, rejects out-of-policy actions); disable stops sync + hides the surface but retains local data; per-node service state + ad-block stats published to the Workbench fleet view.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-9: packaging + docs.** **As** a deployer, **I want** the browser packaged + documented, **so that** it ships securely. **Acceptance:** the RPM declares Servo's runtime libs (mesa/vulkan/fontconfig/freetype/…) as requires + bundles the seed filter lists (NO firefox/nss deps); a confined enforcing SELinux domain for the helper ships + loads; `mde-web-preview` in the base bootc image, runtime-gated by policy; a THREAT_MODEL (attack surface + sandbox + the accepted residual risks) + a CHANGELOG (Servo pin/cadence). DoD: base workspace build green; `rpm -qlp` ships mde-web-preview + the SELinux policy.
- [✓] **BOOKMARKS-10: mesh integration.** **As** a user, **I want** bookmarks to flow across the mesh + surfaces, **so that** they compose. **Acceptance:** right-click Send-in-Chat a bookmark (reuse the NOTIFY-CHAT message-kind); copy-URL to the shell clipboard; add-bookmark-from the current browser page.

## BRAND — empty-desktop logo backdrop (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/desktop-logo-backdrop.md)
The brand lockup (`assets/brand/logo-lockup.png`) becomes the desktop's persistent backdrop layer: centered, large, text-free, shown whenever nothing covers the screen, dimming to a watermark once content opens. 10-lock survey in the design doc.

- [✓] **BRAND-1: the empty-desktop logo backdrop (`mde-shell-egui`, egui/Carbon §4).** **As** a user, **I want** the Magic Mesh lockup centered on the desktop whenever it's empty with no text over it, **so that** an idle seat is branded and calm. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): the embedded lockup (`include_bytes!`, decoded once to a cached texture) renders as the bottom backdrop for both the `vdi.rs` no-desktop EmptyState and the empty root desktop; centered in the **optical free area** (viewport minus the real top-chrome + dock heights), sized to ~50% of the shorter side but **never upscaled past native 1270px**; full opacity on the Carbon §4 background token (**no raw hex**); **no text over the image** — any honest status renders as a small line clearly below it; **every empty display** shows its own centered logo; a covered display **dims the logo to a low-opacity watermark** still visible in uncovered gaps; state changes **crossfade** and the idle logo **breathes slowly**; egui snapshot test of the empty backdrop; `build/test/clippy -p mde-shell-egui` green; `lint-layered-tiers.sh` clean. Replaces the current text-only EmptyState body (status moves below).

## TERM — a Terminator-class terminal emulator for the mesh (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/mesh-terminal.md)
A new egui-native terminal surface (`mde-term-egui`) matching Terminator: arbitrarily nested H/V split panes, tabs-of-splits, broadcast/grouped input, mesh-synced saved layouts, zoom + drag-rearrange — extended so any pane can be a shell on a remote mesh node over the overlay (mackesd PTY broker + the FILEMGR-6 mesh-ssh-key). Mature VT crate (add+vendor), openpty local, Carbon chrome + Carbon-derived/classic content palettes. 16-lock survey in the design doc.

- [✓] **TERM-1: VT engine + terminal core.** **As** the platform, **I want** a mature VT/xterm engine wired to a screen+scrollback model, **so that** panes render real terminal output correctly. **Acceptance:** the chosen VT crate (`alacritty_terminal`/`termwiz`) is added to the workspace + vendored (airgap-buildable, `--locked` OK — verify early; fall back to `vte`/hand-rolled with an honest note if truly unfetchable); a terminal core parses ANSI/xterm into a cell grid + unlimited(soft-capped) scrollback; unit-tested against known escape sequences (colors, cursor moves, clears, wraps). §6 glue — no re-implemented VT parsing.
- [✓] **TERM-2: local PTY + shell launch.** **As** a user, **I want** a real local shell in a pane, **so that** the terminal actually runs commands. **Acceptance:** `openpty` (rustix/libc, in-lock) spawns the user's `$SHELL` as a login shell inheriting cwd+env; a reader pump feeds PTY→engine, input→PTY; SIGWINCH on resize; clean child reap on close; runtime-smoke: a pane runs `ls`/`echo` and shows output. §9 typed, no shell-string injection.
- [✓] **TERM-3: the egui terminal widget.** **As** a user, **I want** the terminal painted in the surface, **so that** I can see + interact with it. **Acceptance:** renders the engine's cell grid via egui (GPU glyphs, cursor, bold/italic/underline), the content palette, mouse+keyboard selection, and a scrollback viewport; resizes map to cols/rows; §4 Carbon tokens for chrome (no raw hex); headless render test produces draw primitives.
- [✓] **TERM-4: nested split panes + zoom + drag-rearrange.** **As** a user, **I want** Terminator's arbitrary H/V splits, **so that** I tile terminals freely. **Acceptance:** a `Leaf|Split{dir,ratio,a,b}` tree splits to any depth with draggable dividers; zoom maximizes/restores one pane; drag reparents a leaf; close collapses; focus follows; unit-tested tree ops.
- [✓] **TERM-5: tabs of split trees.** **As** a user, **I want** tabs each holding their own split layout, **so that** I group workspaces. **Acceptance:** a tab bar (Carbon tokens) with new/close/reorder; each tab owns an independent split tree; switching preserves per-tab state; tested.
- [✓] **TERM-6: broadcast / grouped input.** **As** a user, **I want** to type into many terminals at once, **so that** I drive a fleet in parallel. **Acceptance:** input routes to the focused pane, ALL panes, or a NAMED GROUP (assign/label panes to groups); broadcasting panes show a Carbon-token indicator border; toggled by keybind + UI; tested the fan-out routing.
- [✓] **TERM-7: mackesd mesh PTY-broker worker.** **As** the platform, **I want** a mesh-side worker opening shells on remote peers, **so that** panes can run on any node. **Acceptance:** opens a remote shell over the overlay using `mesh-ssh-key` (FILEMGR-6); typed Bus verbs `action/pty/<peer>` (open/write/resize/close) + `state/pty/<id>` (bytes/exit); honest typed gating on unreachable/dropped peers (mirrors mesh_mount, never fakes a session); idle/dead-session cleanup; §6 mesh-side; unit-tested folds + a gated live leg.
- [✓] **TERM-8: remote-terminal UX (peer picker + manual).** **As** a user, **I want** to open a terminal on a mesh node, **so that** remote work is one click. **Acceptance:** a "new terminal on → <peer>" picker driven by the mesh roster + presence pips (offline greyed, no hang) plus a manual host/overlay-address entry; both route through TERM-7; a remote pane is visually marked with its node; reconnect on transient drop.
- [✓] **TERM-9: scrollback search + smart clipboard.** **As** a user, **I want** search + smart selection, **so that** I find output and act on it. **Acceptance:** in-scrollback search with regex + match highlight + next/prev; copy/paste; copy-on-select + paste-on-middle-click options; detected URLs open in the BOOKMARKS mesh browser and filesystem paths open in the Files surface (reuse the surface-launch path, §6); tested detection + routing.
- [✓] **TERM-10: mesh-synced saved layouts.** **As** a user, **I want** to save + launch named terminal arrangements across the mesh, **so that** setups are repeatable anywhere. **Acceptance:** serialize (tab/split tree + per-pane cwd + launch cmd + target node) to a named layout; synced via Syncthing (like bookmarks/adfilter); "launch layout" rebuilds it incl. remote panes (TERM-7/8); two-node visibility proven; tested round-trip.
- [✓] **TERM-11: palette + look.** **As** a user, **I want** a branded default palette + classic presets, **so that** the terminal fits the platform yet feels familiar. **Acceptance:** a default 16-color palette derived from mde-theme Carbon tokens + bundled classics (Solarized/Gruvbox/Nord) via a simple picker; font-size + cursor-style knobs (no full profile system, Q13); the chrome stays pure Carbon §4 (no raw hex); `cargo test -p mde-theme` green if tokens touched.
- [✓] **TERM-12: keybindings + titles + activity watch + bell.** **As** a user, **I want** Terminator keybinds and per-pane awareness, **so that** it feels like Terminator. **Acceptance:** Terminator-compatible default keymap (split/nav/zoom/tab/broadcast), fully rebindable via config; per-pane editable titles (auto from running cmd, user-overridable); watch-for-activity / watch-for-silence firing a notification (the Chat/notification path); configurable bell; tested keymap dispatch + watcher triggers.
- [✓] **TERM-13: full mouse reporting + true-color + ligatures (Q17/Q20 folds).** **As** a user, **I want** TUI apps to get the mouse and crisp modern rendering, **so that** vim/htop/tmux and colored output work right. **Acceptance:** SGR/1006 mouse reporting forwarded to apps in mouse-mode (click/drag/scroll/hover) with **Shift-bypass** to native selection; 24-bit true-color + 256-color rendered faithfully; a bundled monospace font with programming ligatures; tested mouse-encode + a true-color render check. (Inline images sixel/kitty are OUT of scope — a follow-up.)
- [✓] **TERM-14: remote session persistence + reattach (Q18).** **As** a user, **I want** a remote mesh shell to survive the surface closing, **so that** long jobs keep running and I can reattach. **Acceptance:** the mackesd PTY broker (TERM-7) keeps a remote session alive when its client disconnects (surface close/crash), buffering output within bounds; a reattach path (verb + UX in TERM-8) reconnects a pane to a still-running session listing reattachable sessions per node; idle/TTL reap of orphaned sessions; local panes stay ephemeral; two-phase test: open→disconnect→reattach sees the running shell. §6 mesh-side.
- [✓] **TERM-15: context-menu custom commands + built-in mesh actions (Q19).** **As** a user, **I want** right-click actions on the selection, **so that** the terminal composes with the mesh. **Acceptance:** user-defined custom commands (config: label + command template with the selection substituted) run on the selected text (Terminator parity); built-in mesh actions — send-selection-to-Chat (NOTIFY-CHAT), open-path-in-Files, open-URL-in-mesh-browser, new-terminal-here (inherit cwd) — reuse the existing surface-launch paths (§6); tested command substitution + action dispatch.
- [✓] **TERM-16: mount the terminal into the shell dock (RESCUE — reachability, 2026-07-03).** **As** a user, **I want** the Terminator-class terminal to actually open in the Quasar shell, **so that** TERM-1..15 are reachable in the shipped product (not shipped-but-dead). **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): `mde-shell-egui` gains `Surface::Terminal` in `dock.rs` (enum + SURFACES list + label + render arm), driven by an embedded `mde_term_egui` lib panel (expose a `terminal_panel`/pump seam mirroring `files_panel`/`music_panel`, §6 — reuse the app, don't re-derive); the terminal renders + is interactive as a dock surface; `mde-term-egui`'s bin (or the shell asset) is added to the `generate-rpm` assets so it SHIPS (DoD = it appears in `rpm -qlp` at the gated cut per [[strip-verify-replacement-ships-in-rpm]]); shell mount test green. **Found by the 2026-07-03 reconciliation:** `mde-term-egui` builds green but has 0 references in `mde-shell-egui`, 0 RPM assets, 0 launchers — the whole TERM epic is currently unreachable. Serialize on CHOOSER-7 + MEDIA-18 (all edit `dock.rs`). **Blocks the 12.1 cut.**

## MEDIA — a VLC-class media player + Jellyfin client, native to the mesh (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/mesh-media-player.md)
A new egui-native universal player (`mde-media-egui`) on **libmpv**: any audio/video, local or streamed, Carbon §4 chrome + a translucent dark OSD, video on a dedicated DRM overlay plane, PipeWire audio, VA-API decode. Full **Jellyfin** client (multi-server, Quick Connect, browse, direct-play/transcode, progress sync, offline, Live-TV). Mesh-native: auto-discovered media sources, aggregated mesh library, **session roaming** (playback follows the operator between seats), sync-play party mode, casting, and this node as a media server too. `mde-music` stays as the music-library app. 35-lock survey in the design doc.

- [✓] **MEDIA-1: libmpv engine + player core.** **As** the platform, **I want** a mature engine wrapped in a player core, **so that** any format plays correctly. **Acceptance:** the libmpv binding is added + vendored (airgap-verify early, `--locked`; honest fallback note if unfetchable); a `Player` wraps mpv (load/play/pause/seek/stop, position/duration/state events, track enumeration); state machine unit-tested against a fake mpv seam; real-clip smoke on the farm when `libmpv` present, else honest-gated. §6 glue.
- [✓] **MEDIA-2: DRM overlay video plane.** **As** a user, **I want** smooth video, **so that** playback is crisp + low-power. **Acceptance:** mpv video scans out on a HW overlay plane beneath the egui shell, composited with the shell's DRM ownership (no tearing/z-fight); egui chrome/OSD draws above; plane resize/position track the player pane; seam-tested; honest-gated where no plane is available (render-to-texture fallback documented).
- [✓] **MEDIA-3: PipeWire audio + processing.** **As** a user, **I want** great audio, **so that** sound is rich + level. **Acceptance:** mpv audio → PipeWire (seat audio); graphic EQ + audio filters + loudness normalization/ReplayGain + gapless, via the mpv af-graph; config folds unit-tested.
- [✓] **MEDIA-4: VA-API decode + video adjustments.** **As** a user, **I want** HW decode + geometry control. **Acceptance:** VA-API decode with software fallback; aspect/zoom/crop/rotate/deinterlace + filters; config folds tested; honest fallback when no VA-API.
- [✓] **MEDIA-5: subtitles + multi-track.** **As** a user, **I want** subtitles + track choice. **Acceptance:** embedded + external (.srt/.ass) with ASS styling/positioning/delay; OpenSubtitles online fetch by hash (egress, fail-soft); full audio/subtitle/video track selection with language labels; fixture-tested.
- [✓] **MEDIA-6: playlists + advanced controls.** **As** a user, **I want** queues + fine control. **Acceptance:** save/load playlists, queue, shuffle, repeat, gapless; speed, A/V-sync offsets, chapters, frame-step, snapshot, A-B loop; queue + control model unit-tested.
- [✓] **MEDIA-7: local library + resume.** **As** a user, **I want** a browsable library that remembers me. **Acceptance:** index chosen folders (+ mesh mounts) from embedded metadata + local artwork with online TMDB/TVDB scrape (egress, fail-soft); per-title resume + continue-watching + watch history; index/scan + resume store tested.
- [✓] **MEDIA-8: the mde-media-egui surface shell (Carbon §4).** **As** a user, **I want** a full media app. **Acceptance:** Sources + Library browse + Player + Queue + PiP mini-player + auto-hide OSD; immersive fullscreen; platform-native rebindable keymap; Carbon chrome (no raw hex) + a translucent dark media OSD over video; egui snapshot + headless mount tests.
- [✓] **MEDIA-9: Jellyfin client core.** **As** a user, **I want** to connect to Jellyfin. **Acceptance:** `reqwest`+serde client — multi-server, Quick Connect + username/password login (saved), full browse (shows→seasons→episodes, movies, music, collections, genres, artwork, Continue-Watching/Next-Up); fixture-tested against recorded API responses.
- [✓] **MEDIA-10: Jellyfin playback + sync.** **As** a user, **I want** Jellyfin titles to play + sync. **Acceptance:** direct-play/direct-stream vs server-transcode (HLS) negotiation from the libmpv capability set; report progress + resume-across-devices + mark-played; cross-library search + filters + sort; Live-TV/DVR + music; negotiation + progress folds tested.
- [✓] **MEDIA-11: Jellyfin offline + users.** **As** a user, **I want** offline + profiles. **Acceptance:** download titles to a managed local cache for offline playback; multiple user profiles + switching per server; cache lifecycle + user-switch tested.
- [✓] **MEDIA-12: network streams + yt-dlp.** **As** a user, **I want** to play stream URLs + web videos. **Acceptance:** open direct stream URLs (http/hls/rtsp/mms) + resolve web-page videos via a bundled yt-dlp; URL detection + the yt-dlp seam tested (honest-gated when the tool is absent).
- [✓] **MEDIA-13: capture devices.** **As** a user, **I want** to watch capture inputs. **Acceptance:** v4l2 / TV-tuner / capture-card input as a playable source; enumerate + open a device; honest-gated when no device present.
- [✓] **MEDIA-14: mackesd mesh media-discovery service.** **As** a user, **I want** every mesh media source listed. **Acceptance:** auto-discover mesh nodes serving media — Jellyfin, DLNA/UPnP, this-player-as-server, mesh file shares — via mDNS + the mesh registry (reuse `mesh_media.rs`/`mdns_relay`); publish `state/media/sources` with reachability; discovery folds unit-tested. §6 mesh-side.
- [✓] **MEDIA-15: mackesd mesh media server + DLNA + aggregation.** **As** a user, **I want** to share + see all mesh media in one place. **Acceptance:** share this node's chosen folders as a mesh media source (peers' MEDIA-14 finds it) + a DLNA/UPnP server; aggregate peers' shared media into one mesh library view (MEDIA-8 consumes); share manifest + aggregation merge tested. §6.
- [✓] **MEDIA-16: session roaming.** **As** an operator, **I want** my playback to follow me between seats. **Acceptance:** a session record (title/position/queue/tracks/state) bound to the mesh identity + synced (Syncthing/etcd, like bookmarks); login at a new seat offers resume-where-paused + releases the old seat (single owned lease, no double-play); two-seat test: play→switch→resume continues. §6 mesh-side.
- [✓] **MEDIA-17: sync-play party mode + casting.** **As** a user, **I want** to watch together + throw playback. **Acceptance:** a shared session several seats join where play/pause/seek propagate in sync; cast to a mesh node, a DLNA/UPnP renderer, or a Chromecast; propagation folds tested + a gated live cast leg (mirrors mesh_mount gating).
- [✓] **MEDIA-18: mount the media player into the shell dock (RESCUE — reachability, 2026-07-03).** **As** a user, **I want** the VLC-class media player to actually open in the Quasar shell, **so that** MEDIA-1..17 are reachable in the shipped product (not shipped-but-dead). **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): `mde-shell-egui` gains `Surface::Media` in `dock.rs` (enum + SURFACES list + label + render arm), driven by an embedded `mde_media_egui` lib panel (expose a `media_panel`/pump seam mirroring `files_panel`, §6 — reuse the app, don't re-derive); the player renders + drives `mde_media_core` as a dock surface; `mde-media-egui` is added to the `generate-rpm` assets so it SHIPS (DoD = `rpm -qlp` shows it at the gated cut per [[strip-verify-replacement-ships-in-rpm]]); shell mount test green. **Found by the 2026-07-03 reconciliation:** `mde-media-egui` "compiles green, ships/launches nowhere" — 0 references in `mde-shell-egui`, 0 RPM assets, 0 launchers. Serialize on CHOOSER-7 + TERM-16 (all edit `dock.rs`). **Blocks the 12.1 cut.**

## CHOOSER — the Desktop Chooser for every discovered display source (operator 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/desktop-chooser.md)
A `mde-shell-egui` surface that aggregates every discovered desktop — remote mesh-peer desktops, local KVM/cloud-hypervisor consoles, RDP/VNC/Spice endpoints — into one live card grid (grouped by node, protocol badges, live thumbnails), auto-popping on new discovery over the BRAND-1 backdrop, and drives the VDI attach path (vdi.rs + mde-vdi-rdp/-vnc + a new mde-vdi-spice). Always-ask protocol; mesh-SSO + sealed external creds; full local-KVM lifecycle; per-connection fullscreen/windowed + multi-monitor; mesh-synced favorites. 15-lock survey in the design doc.

- [✓] **CHOOSER-1: mackesd desktop-source discovery aggregator.** **As** the platform, **I want** every desktop source discovered in one place, **so that** the Chooser can present them. **Acceptance:** collect from the mesh registry (peer-advertised desktops), mDNS (RDP/VNC/Spice on the LAN, reuse `mdns_relay`), and local KVM enumeration (`kvm.rs`/`vm_lifecycle`), + manual sources; publish `state/desktops/sources` (per-source node/protocols/reachability/OS/thumbnail-ref); discovery+merge folds unit-tested. §6 mesh-side.
- [✓] **CHOOSER-2: the Chooser surface (`mde-shell-egui`, Carbon §4).** **As** a user, **I want** to see + pick discovered desktops, **so that** connecting is one click. **Acceptance:** a live card grid grouped by node/host with protocol badges + status pips, over the BRAND-1 backdrop when empty; auto-popups on a new-source event; renders `state/desktops/sources`; §4 tokens (no raw hex); egui snapshot + headless mount tests (empty, populated, offline-greyed).
- [✓] **CHOOSER-3: live thumbnails.** **As** a user, **I want** a preview of each desktop, **so that** I recognize it at a glance. **Acceptance:** periodic preview thumbnails — mesh peers publish a small snapshot, local VMs snapshot the framebuffer, external endpoints a cheap probe — graceful protocol/OS-icon fallback; bounded refresh + cache; source→thumbnail plumbing + fallback tested.
- [✓] **CHOOSER-4: connect flow (protocol picker + display options).** **As** a user, **I want** to control how I connect, **so that** it fits the source. **Acceptance:** on connect, always-ask the protocol when several are offered; fullscreen-or-windowed choice; multi-monitor span/single choice; route to `mde-vdi-rdp`/`-vnc`/`-spice` via a `vdi.rs` `ConnectRequest`; request-construction folds tested.
- [✓] **CHOOSER-5: `mde-vdi-spice` client crate (Spice→egui).** **As** a user, **I want** native Spice for local VMs, **so that** they're fast. **Acceptance:** a first-class Spice client rendered egui-native like the RDP/VNC crates; airgap-verify the Spice crate (add+vendor, honest VNC-fallback note if unfetchable); connects to a KVM VM's Spice console; headless: connect→a frame arrives. New workspace-member crate.
- [✓] **CHOOSER-6: auth.** **As** a user, **I want** seamless mesh login + secure external creds, **so that** connecting is easy + safe. **Acceptance:** mesh-peer desktops authenticate with the node's mesh identity (SSO, no prompt); external RDP/VNC/Spice creds sealed in the secret store (reuse FILEMGR-6 seal/derive), prompted once; never a plaintext credential on disk/logs; seal/read + SSO path tested. _(2026-07-03: landed `ec718b2` — auth.rs model + SSO (reuses `discovery::local_peer`, no prompt) + prompt-once UI + `CredentialStore` seal seam + log-safety (`Secret` redacts, no `Display`; no plaintext to Debug/logs), 221t, reachable (wired into chooser.rs/vdi.rs). **§6-GATED FOLLOW-ON (mesh tier, NOT desktop):** the *live* external-cred seal into the mesh store — FILEMGR-6's `SecretStore` (Argon2id+XChaCha20) lives inside the `mackesd` daemon behind `async-services`, which the desktop shell must not link (§6); `MeshCredentialStore` honestly returns `SealOutcome::Gated` today. Needs a **mesh-side desktop-cred sealing worker** to back the seam so external creds persist across sessions — tracked here for a mesh-tier unit.)_
- [✓] **CHOOSER-7: local-KVM lifecycle from cards.** **As** a user, **I want** to power local VMs from the Chooser, **so that** a stopped desktop is one click away. **Acceptance:** start/stop/pause a discovered local VM + open its console, driving the mackesd `vm_lifecycle` worker; the card reflects live power state; action dispatch + state reflection tested; honest-gated where no local hypervisor.
- [✓] **CHOOSER-8: card actions + find + states.** **As** a user, **I want** to manage + find desktops, **so that** the grid scales. **Acceptance:** per-card context menu (connect, favorite/pin, edit, remove manual, KVM power); search + filter (node/protocol/status/OS) + sort; offline/unreachable sources greyed with a reason + retry, never a blocking probe; filter/sort + the non-blocking offline model tested.
- [✓] **CHOOSER-9: mesh-synced favorites/recents/manual sources.** **As** an operator, **I want** my desktop list to follow me, **so that** every seat is the same. **Acceptance:** favorites, recents, and manual sources bound to the mesh identity + synced via Syncthing (like bookmarks); two-seat test: pin at one seat → visible at another. §6 mesh-side.

## MESH-VIRT — Fedora+KVM virt management, no center (operator 2026-06-30; design: docs/design/mesh-virt-management.md)
Replaces the XCP-NG toolstack with a **Fedora + KVM + Podman** stack driven by `mackesd` (the xapi-equivalent), no central orchestrator (#5 mesh-native; Incus the adopt-fallback, Nomad excluded on BUSL). The Datacenter interfaces drive the KVM services on each node. This is the KVM-equivalent of the deleted XCP-NG worker/catalog.

- [✓] **MV-1: `KVM_SERVICES` catalog + per-node service set.** **As** a node, **I want** the KVM stack (`qemu-kvm`/`libvirt` + sockets, `podman.socket`, NetworkManager, a metrics worker, optional `multipathd`) baked into the one image + a `KVM_SERVICES` catalog (replacing the removed `XCPNG_SERVICES`), **so that** every node carries the libvirt/Podman equivalent of the toolstack. **Acceptance:** the dnf set is in the image; `libvirtd`/`podman.socket` enabled; `KVM_SERVICES` lists the units; KVM modules load.
- [✓] **MV-2: `mackesd` kvm-host-health worker.** **As** an operator, **I want** a worker publishing `KVM_SERVICES` health to `event/kvm/services` (mirroring the removed xcpng-health), **so that** Datacenter panels show per-node KVM-stack health. **Acceptance:** reports each unit up/down + a host summary; injectable probe; unit-tested.
- [✓] **MV-3: `mackesd` vm-lifecycle worker over libvirt.** **As** the Datacenter UI, **I want** `mackesd` to create/start/stop/migrate VMs via libvirt (the `xapi`+`xenopsd`+`sm`+`xcp-networkd` equivalent; cloud-hypervisor kept for the VDI desktop fast-path), **so that** the Datacenter interfaces drive KVM. **Acceptance:** a VM is created/started/stopped via the worker; a libvirt storage pool + virtual network are managed; it appears in the mesh map.
- [✓] **MV-4: `mackesd` container worker over Podman.** **As** the Datacenter UI, **I want** `mackesd` to run/stop Podman containers on a chosen node, **so that** the layer covers containers too. **Acceptance:** a Podman service is launched/stopped via the worker + reachable by peers (behind the host's cert).
- [✓] **MV-5: No-center scheduler (#5) + historical Cockpit interim console.** **As** the mesh, **I want** desired-state + placement in the existing etcd/Syncthing so any node schedules with no center, with Cockpit (`cockpit-machines`/`cockpit-podman`) as the per-node console, **so that** "no fixed center" is structural. **Acceptance:** a VM/container scheduled from any node lands + survives that node's loss (placed workloads keep running); Cockpit reachable per node. *(MV-5a placement landed `91888d6`; MV-5b failover `7f2d369` landed → MV-5 complete (60 tests). QC-15 2026-07-10 removes Cockpit from the live `node-virt.yml` recipe and KVM health catalog; this entry is now historical replaced-stack evidence.)*
- [✓] **MV-6: Datacenter/Fleet plane drives KVM — `mde-shell-egui` (egui, `Plane::Fleet`), NOT the retired iced `mde-workbench`.** **As** an operator, **I want** the Datacenter/Instances panels to drive `mackesd`'s libvirt+Podman workers (not xapi), **so that** the UI manages the KVM stack. **Acceptance:** the panel lists nodes' VMs+containers from `event/kvm/services` + the lifecycle workers; create/start/stop works live, rendered through `Style`. *(MV-6 + MV-6b `d68613d`: VMs live-driven + container roster read-only; container lifecycle-drive = follow-up)*
- [!] **MV-7: Adopt-XCP-ng (day-2, optional).** *(session=819545e6, e50b693)* **As** an operator with existing XCP-ng iron, **I want** a day-2 "adopt hypervisor" action that enrolls + drives it via `xe`/tofu (as the live farm does), **so that** existing XCP-ng capacity joins the mesh without being a role. **Acceptance:** an existing XCP-ng host is adopted + serves VMs to the mesh; it is never produced by our installer. **CORE DONE `e50b693`:** the `mackesd adopt-xcp` verb — pure `plan_adopt` → ordered idempotent `AdoptStep`s (mint static-member token → enroll dom0 as a static Nebula member → register roster row → attach + converge the XAPI toolstack via `xe`/`tofu`) or a retryable `Blocked`; injectable `Adopter` seam (`LiveAdopter` integration-gated, typed `IntegrationGated` errors — never fake success); `--dry-run`. Reuses `invite::resolve_mesh_id` + `ca::bundle` + `peers`.
