Farm-green (11 tests, clippy-clean). **REMAINING (integration-gated, stays [>]):** the live enroll + xe/tofu drive against a real XCP-ng host (needs live SSH + the xen-xapi provider + host creds — classifier-gated).

## DRAIN-ENGINE — keep the build going until the worklist is clear (operator 2026-06-24; design: docs/design/autonomous-drain.md)
Methods so the autonomous drain can't be derailed by the mechanical failures that halted it (disk wedges 4×, sequential grinding, stalling on blockers). Locks: **Hybrid engine** (FARM-AUTO reconciler builds; AI supervises) · **Hard enforcement** (local builds impossible) · **Park + keep draining** (never stall on one item).
- [✓] **DRAIN-1: cargo-farm-guard — make local builds impossible.** `install-helpers/cargo-farm-guard.sh` installs AS `cargo`; `build/test/check/clippy/run` exit 97 with a farm redirect, `fmt/metadata` pass through. **Acceptance:** a dev-host `cargo build` exits non-zero with the redirect; `xcp-build.sh` (farm) unaffected. *(verified standalone 2026-06-24: build→97.)*
- [✓] **DRAIN-2: disk-watchdog — auto-heal disk pressure.** `install-helpers/disk-watchdog.sh` reclaims stray `target/` + aged logs when free `<` threshold; **active-safe** (never nukes a live agent's worktree). **Acceptance:** below-threshold free space triggers reclaim; a live agent's worktree survives.
- [✓] **DRAIN-3: install-drain-guardrails — operator-run activator.** `install-helpers/install-drain-guardrails.sh {--install|--uninstall}` installs the guard + a 5-min watchdog systemd timer; reversible (real toolchain preserved as `cargo-real`). **Operator runs it** (self-installing persistence is not agent-authorized). **Acceptance:** `--install` makes the guard live + the timer active; `--uninstall` restores.
- [✓] **DRAIN-4: FARM-AUTO reconciler — the no-AI mechanical build half.** A timer reads open units tagged `@farm:{crate,verify}`, builds the slice on the farm, opens a PR, flips the task to needs-review — no AI tokens in the build loop (§10 / build-platform.md). **Acceptance:** an `@farm`-tagged open unit produces a farm-green PR with zero AI tokens spent; red builds raise a triage task.
- [✓] **DRAIN-5: park-and-continue — never stall on a blocker.** A unit hitting a live-infra/artifact/gate blocker becomes `[!]` + a `docs/NEEDS-OPERATOR.md` entry; the loop continues with the next unblocked unit. **Acceptance:** a blocked unit appears in NEEDS-OPERATOR.md and the loop advances — zero whole-loop stalls attributable to one item. — DONE (2026-06-25): `install-helpers/park-blocker.sh <ID> "<reason>"` flips the first open bullet for `<ID>` to `[!]`, annotates it `_(BLOCKED: … — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_`, surfaces it under "Parked by the drain loop" in `docs/NEEDS-OPERATOR.md`, and exits 0 so the loop continues. Idempotent; word-boundary id match (FAKE-1 ≠ FAKE-10); not-found → exit 1; self-tested green. (The prior NEEDS-OPERATOR "blocked: §C.4 absent" note was itself stale — §C.4 exists — now removed.)
- [✓] **DRAIN-6: coordinator pattern in /ship.** Encode: pre-flight disk-watchdog → saturate N farm agents (disjoint crates, farm-only) → on each completion merge+reclaim+relaunch (no batch-wait) → park-don't-stall → no-flinch. **Acceptance:** with the loop running, N agents stay in flight until the open+unblocked queue is empty; a completed agent is merged+replaced within one tick. — DONE (2026-06-25): `install-helpers/drain-coordinator.sh {plan|slots|next|preflight}` encodes the tick (preflight `disk-watchdog.sh` → free-slot compute over the REAL `.50/.90/.130` topology, caps 2/2/3 → next N open-unblocked unit ids); the ship skill gained a "coordinator loop (DRAIN-5/6/7)" section wiring drain-coordinator + park-blocker + assert-own-worktree with the rearm-don't-batch-wait + park-don't-stall + no-flinch discipline, and its stale `.50/.51/.52` topology table is corrected to `.50/.90/.130`. `slots` verified live (free 2/1/3 → 6).
- [✓] **DRAIN-7: worktree-isolation discipline.** A subagent strayed into the shared `calm-ray-dcr8` worktree + its work was wiped (2026-06-24). Agents must operate ONLY in their assigned isolated worktree. **Acceptance:** an agent's STEP-0 verifies it is in its own worktree (not the shared one) and refuses to edit/reset the shared checkout. — DONE (2026-06-25): `install-helpers/assert-own-worktree.sh` is the agent STEP-0 guard — REFUSES (exit 3) the shared `calm-ray-dcr8`/primary checkout, PASSES (exit 0) only from an isolated worktree; `--coordinator` asserts the inverse. Verified: refuse-from-shared, pass-from-isolated (incl. a real `wf_*` worktree), coordinator-from-shared. The ship skill mandates it as every isolated subagent's STEP-0.

## INCIDENT — mde-bus broker resilience (BROKER-RESILIENCE-1, diagnosed live 2026-06-25)
A missing/skipped `ntfy` broker (`<overlay_ip>:8443`) was design-"non-fatal" but FATAL in practice: mackesd kept publishing to the unreachable `:8443` (every publish persisted to the `/run/mde-bus` spool, which grew unbounded — observed boot-readiness 25M + audit 19M + compute 13M ≈ 77M of a 190M tmpfs) and the broker-unreachable path starved the 60 s systemd watchdog → `status=6/ABRT` ~every 3 min (NRestarts climbing on both lighthouses). The broker client was `reqwest::Client::new()` with NO timeout.
- [✓] **BROKER-RESILIENCE-1: a skipped/unreachable broker is truly non-fatal.** Four-part fix: **(1)** the outbound-publish reqwest client now has a CONNECT (3 s) + overall REQUEST (5 s) timeout via a shared `hooks::publisher::broker_client()` used by both the webhook listener and the `mde-bus publish` CLI, so a publish to a dead broker fails FAST instead of hanging ~60 s. **(2)** when `broker::evaluate_prereqs` returns `Skip`, the daemon binds the webhook listener in a new persist-only mode (`ListenerConfig::persist_only_for_overlay_ip`) — it still ingests + persists + audits but skips the doomed outbound POST entirely (mirrors `--no-broker`), so a known-absent broker drives no outbound retries. **(3)** a conservative per-topic `/run` cap (`TOPIC_RUN_CAP_BYTES` = 8 MiB + 20k-entry backstop) now bounds EVERY non-audit topic to its most-recent window every retention pass (the analogue of the existing `audit/*` cap), so a down broker can never fill the tmpfs. **(4)** verified the sd_notify watchdog ping rides its own 250 ms timer in mackesd, sharing no task with any broker/spool call. **Acceptance:** a publish to a dead broker returns within the timeout (not ~60 s); a skipped-broker daemon persists without retrying outbound; the spool stays bounded under sustained no-broker publishes. *(build + clippy + new tests GREEN on the farm, slot `brokerfix`.)*
- [✓] **BROKER-RESILIENCE-2 / WATCHDOG-2: the watchdog ping was STILL being starved — real root cause + fix (11.0.11, 2026-06-27).** BR-1 part (4)'s claim ("the ping rides its own 250 ms timer, sharing no task with any broker/spool call → can't be starved") was **false in practice**: both production lighthouses kept watchdog-aborting every ~90 s for ~40 h (lh1 1355 / lh2 1348 `status=6/ABRT`), across 11.0.8→11.0.10, which blocked every enrollment (`:4243` only up between crashes). The ping rode `tokio::time::sleep` **on the runtime**, and on a **1-vCPU droplet** `new_multi_thread` defaults to a SINGLE worker that also owns the **tokio time driver**; when that worker reaches a blocking bridge (`substrate::peers::block_on` → `block_in_place` for an etcd round-trip) the time driver **freezes**, the sleep never fires, and systemd SIGABRTs a healthy daemon. A missing broker reliably triggered it via extra `block_in_place` churn. Fix: **(a)** floor the runtime at 4 worker threads even on 1 vCPU (`bin/mackesd.rs`), so a second worker keeps timers alive while another blocks; **(b)** move the heartbeat to a **dedicated OS thread** gated on an async liveness beacon the serve loop stamps every 250 ms (`sd_notify::watchdog_should_ping`) — kernel-scheduled (unstarvable) yet still reflects true liveness (a genuine wedge stops the beat → ping withheld → systemd restarts). Released as signed **11.0.11** (RSA-4096 d0921c73 verifies on F43) → GitHub Release + gh-pages dnf channel (fedora-43/44). **Acceptance:** lh1/lh2 stop crash-looping after upgrade (NRestarts stops climbing, zero new ABRT, `:4243` stays up) — the live deploy/verify is the remaining step (operator-gated channel `dnf upgrade`). *(farm: 7/7 lib tests incl. new beacon tests, clippy+fmt clean, `cargo check --bin` exit 0.)*
- [✓] **BROKER-RESILIENCE-3: the broker is now turn-key PRESENT on a fresh lighthouse, not just non-fatal when absent.** BR-1/2 made a missing `ntfy` broker survivable; this makes it actually get installed at provision time. The chain was already wired (RPM vendors the pinned `ntfy_2.24.0` tarball at `/usr/share/magic-mesh/vendor/`; `mesh-install-ntfy` is shipped + bundled-first/offline-capable + idempotent; post_install `enable`s `mesh-broker-setup.service`), but the oneshot is only `enable`d — NOT `--now` (deliberately, so the dnf transaction never blocks on `network-online.target`). So on a freshly provisioned lighthouse that never reboots, there was no broker until the first reboot. Fix: both lighthouse cloud-inits (`do-lighthouse-cloudinit.sh` found-path + `do-lighthouse-join-cloudinit.sh` join-path) now `systemctl start --no-block` the three first-boot fetch oneshots (`mesh-broker-setup` / `mesh-netdata-setup` / `mesh-shell-setup`) after the daemon comes up — post-boot, network up, idempotent, non-stalling. **Acceptance:** a just-provisioned lighthouse has `/usr/bin/ntfy` (and netdata + starship) without a reboot; re-running is a no-op. *(`bash -n` clean on both scripts; pure cloud-init template change, no compile.)*

## LIGHTHOUSE-MIGRATION — 3 new lighthouses, retire old 2, move Eagle (DONE 2026-06-27)
Goal: "Create 3 new Lighthouses, Delete the existing 2, move Eagle to the new Lighthouses." **COMPLETE + live-verified.** Live topology is now Eagle (10.42.0.2) + **nyc3 (10.42.0.4) / fra1 (10.42.0.5) / sfo3 (10.42.0.6)**; old lh1 (167.71.247.150) + mcnf-lh2 (68.183.55.253) droplets deleted; etcd = 4 healthy voters; Eagle's roster dials only .4/.5/.6; cross-mesh connectivity verified. Three turn-key code gaps were found+fixed live and shipped (signed, gh-pages dnf channel):
- [✓] **11.0.11 WATCHDOG-2** — the watchdog crash-loop (see INCIDENT above).
- [✓] **11.0.12 LIGHTHOUSE-ENROLL-SELF-CERT** — a joined lighthouse self-generates its `/enroll` endpoint cert at startup (CA key present + cert absent), so `:4243` binds and it can SERVE enrollment, not just sign. Proven: a new node enrolled against nyc3.
- [✓] **11.0.13 MULTI-LH-IP-ALLOC** — overlay-IP allocation now consults the global etcd directory (`allocate_overlay_ip_excluding`), not just the local cert-store, so a joined lighthouse no longer hands a new node a colliding `.1`. Proven: re-test node via nyc3 got 10.42.0.7 (unique).
### Follow-ups (turn-key sharp edges found during the migration)
- [✓] **MIG-1: `mackesd remove-peer` must delete the etcd `/mesh/peers/<host>` directory key.** It removes the etcd MEMBER + revokes + bans, but leaves the directory row, so the roster reconcile kept re-adding the dead node until the key was `etcdctl del`'d by hand. Decommission must drop the directory entry too.
- [✓] **MIG-2: atomic cross-lighthouse overlay-IP allocation.** _(shipped 11.0.14, d6e6f20)_ 11.0.13 reads the directory at sign time (closes the systematic collision), but two lighthouses signing in the SAME instant can still race (the parallel sfo3+fra1 provision hit a `(overlay_ip,epoch)` UNIQUE collision). Make allocation atomic via an etcd CAS/lease, or serialize signing per mesh.
- [✓] **MIG-3: provision the CA-backup passphrase on a joined lighthouse.** _(DONE, integrated a862854: `cmd_join`'s lighthouse path now calls `provision_ca_backup_passphrase_if_lighthouse` — a new `ca::backup_provision` module mints a fresh 256-bit CSPRNG passphrase, seals it host-bound via `systemd-creds encrypt --with-key=auto` (TPM/host-key, NOT mesh-replicated) to `/etc/mackesd/backup-passphrase.cred` 0600 + writes the `LoadCredentialEncrypted` drop-in, so the upcoming mackesd restart surfaces it at `$CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/backup-passphrase` where `nebula_ca_backup` reads → SEC-7/ENT-11 clears. Generated-on-joiner (secret never leaves the box, never logged), idempotent, no-op for Server/Workstation. 8 new tests; mackesd 1239 lib tests green. **Live-boot confirmation of the cleared warning is the remaining check (systemd-creds round-trip only exercises on a real host).** The off-fleet CA backup PUSH stays operator-run (classifier-blocked) — that's the separate DR line below.)_ A joined lighthouse holds the CA key but had no backup passphrase credential → `SEC-7/ENT-11: the CA is UNBACKED-UP` error each boot.
- [✓] **MIG-4: provision a sequential lighthouse-add (no parallel) or fix MIG-2 first.** _(resolved by MIG-2 — atomic alloc landed, so the sequential-only constraint is lifted)_ `do-lighthouse-join.sh` runs must be sequential until MIG-2 lands (parallel runs collide on IP allocation).

### MEDIA-LIGHTHOUSE — LIVE on one node (2026-06-27)
Brought up the first Lighthouse_Media node end-to-end: **DO Spaces** `mcnf-media-4533` + `mcnf-dr-4533` buckets + rotated full-access key (`/root/.mcnf-spaces-cred.json`); **secret CLI** `mackesd secret put|get [--local]` (11.0.15) seals the `media-spaces` secret into the Syncthing-replicated LocalAead store; **node** `lh-join-1782565832` (104.236.78.23, overlay **10.42.0.3**, s-2vcpu-4gb nyc3, 11.0.15) tagged `Lighthouse_Media`; **Navidrome 0.53.3** serving the Subsonic API on the overlay (ping.view→ok, reachable cross-node), rclone-mounted on the bucket; **music.mesh** DNS resolving (MEDIA-5); shared `admin` account created + sealed (MEDIA-6, via the `createAdmin` fix). The mesh age identity was distributed to the node so it decrypts the store.
- [✓] MEDIA-1 role/capability · MEDIA-2 bucket+keys · MEDIA-3 Navidrome Subsonic API · MEDIA-4 bucket mount · MEDIA-5 music.mesh DNS · MEDIA-6 shared account (createAdmin) · 11.0.15 secret CLI.
- [✓] **MEDIA-10: 2nd Lighthouse_Media node** for active-active redundancy — DONE: LH2 (10.42.0.2) runs Navidrome+music-store on `mcnf-mesh-media`, `music.mesh` round-robins LH1+LH2, kill-one resilience proven 2026-07-01 (see the MEDIA-10 acceptance entry below).
- [!] **MEDIA-9: content upload** — the bucket is empty; the operator uploads music (rclone to `mcnf-media-4533`), then a Navidrome rescan surfaces it.
- [✓] **MEDIA-pkg: ship `setup-media-navidrome.sh` in the RPM** — added to BOTH RPM asset arrays (full + headless `magic-mesh-server`) → `/usr/libexec/mackesd/setup-media-navidrome` (755); it self-installs rclone/podman/fuse3 at run, so the add-Media flow is turn-key (was scp'd by hand).
- [✓] **MEDIA-pkg-2: `mackesd` Navidrome supervisor worker** — `navidrome_supervisor` (Media-gated): each 30s tick adopts `mcnf-navidrome.service` — restart-if-down (past systemd's own), re-provision-if-missing via the RPM-shipped `setup-media-navidrome` when creds present, else log needs-setup. Pure `decide()` core unit-tested; farm lib-test + bin compile green.
- [✓] **MEDIA-pkg-3: deterministic `music-writer.mesh` alias.** DONE 2026-07-06. The media verifier exposed that `music-writer.mesh` was documented for playlist writes but `mesh_dns` only emitted the active-active `music.mesh` record set. `mesh_dns` now derives a single `music-writer.mesh` record from the same media-lighthouse directory snapshot, choosing the lowest overlay IPv4 address deterministically, while `music.mesh` remains the two-node read/browse set. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd mesh_dns` (18 passed). Live evidence after rebuilding and force-replacing the same-NEVRA `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1` on Eagle + both DO lighthouses: `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh media-verify` passed (`music.mesh=2`, `music-writer.mesh=1`, Subsonic ping ok), and `media-verify --mutate-playlist` passed the create/read/delete temporary playlist proof.
- [✗] **MIG-3 / DR shared-passphrase + off-fleet CA backup** → `mcnf-dr-4533` bucket is provisioned and ready; wire the DATACENTER-23 age-push + leader-managed backup passphrase to it (this also satisfies MIG-3).

### EPIC-DRIVE 2026-06-27 — block-lift status (after the migration)
Six block-areas, driven to their fully-in-my-control extent:
- [✓] **SVC-VIEW (#6)** — unified "All Services" Workbench panel (published ∪ probed ∪ VM-internal), source-tagged + deduped; 13 tests, farm-green (`2431b54`).
- [✓] **BUILD-PLATFORM / test-mesh (#3)** — the nightly L1/L2/L3 pyramid is ENABLED on the control host (`mcnf-nightly-tests.timer`, next 02:39 nightly) + a verification run is executing; harness viable (MDE-VM-golden template present on both dom0s); results publish to `event/test/nightly`.
- [✓] **MEDIA (#2)** — live on one node (above). Tail: 2nd node (redundancy) + operator music upload.
- [~] **DR (#4)** — off-fleet push + CA backup CODE done (`1b70f99`); **execution is operator-run** — the safety classifier HARD-blocks an agent exporting the CA key + secrets off-fleet (data-exfiltration; not clearable by authorization). Run: `MCNF_DR_REMOTE=mcnf-spaces:mcnf-dr-4533 bash automation/dr/dr-backup.sh` on a CA holder.
- [✓] **UniFi (#5)** — cred sealed this session (`unifi-cred` = ubnt) + `MCNF_UNIFI_HOST` drop-in committed (c1454cb); only the host-drop-in install on the LAN datacenter node remains. Generalized into the ROUTER-CONTROL epic (per-node, Vyatta-CLI).
- [✓] **MIG-1/2** shipped (11.0.14); MIG-3 folds into DR above.

## FARM-AUTOSCALE — demand-driven elastic build farm (operator 2026-06-24; design: docs/design/farm-autoscale.md)
Tofu auto-scaling + full lifecycle of VM **and** POD resources: each dom0 runs EITHER one hardware-maxing big build VM OR several small VMs/agent pods, created/destroyed by tofu to fit the live build queue — best on-demand hardware use. Locks: **demand-driven** (the @farm queue is the signal) · **FARM-AUTO reconciler orchestrates** (no AI in the loop) · **warm pool, scale-to-zero + snapshot-reset** · **mutually-exclusive shapes per dom0, demand wins**.
- [✓] **FA-1: tofu shape model.** **As** the farm, **I want** a per-dom0 `shape` var (`big`/`small`/`off`) + `small_count` driving `infra/tofu/build-vms.tf`, **so that** `tofu apply` provisions one hardware-maxing big VM, N standard small VMs, or nothing. **Acceptance:** `shape=big` on BigBoy provisions ~10vCPU/26GiB `mcnf-build-big-52`; `shape=small,count=2` provisions 2 standard VMs; `shape=off` destroys them (scale-to-zero); idempotent re-apply.
- [✓] **FA-2: golden `clean` snapshot + fast reset.** **As** the autoscaler, **I want** each toolchained build VM to carry a post-toolchain `clean` snapshot and a `farm-vm reset <vm>` that `xe snapshot-revert`s it, **so that** inter-job state returns to baseline without a full re-clone. **Acceptance:** a build dirties the VM, `reset` reverts to clean (toolchain + sccache priming intact, job state gone), verified on a reset drill.
- [✓] **FA-3: the farm-autoscaler reconcile step (no AI).** **As** the FARM-AUTO reconciler, **I want** a step that reads the `@farm` queue, classifies each pending job big/small, computes the desired per-dom0 shape, writes the tofu vars, and `tofu apply`s — scaling to zero when idle, **so that** the farm matches demand with zero AI tokens. **Acceptance:** a queued whole-workspace build → big VM provisioned + the build runs + scaled down after; many per-crate jobs → small×N provisioned + run in parallel + scaled to zero; no AI tokens spent.
- [✓] **FA-4: mutual-exclusion drain/preempt + hysteresis.** **As** a dom0, **I want** to run EITHER big OR small (never both), switching only after draining current jobs, with a min-dwell debounce, **so that** shape changes don't thrash against tofu's ~minutes apply latency. **Acceptance:** a big job queued while smalls run → smalls drain, then destroy+provision big; a dom0 never hosts big+small simultaneously; no flip within the dwell window.
- [✓] **FA-5: POD-level scaling.** **As** the autoscaler, **I want** to cap agent/build podman pods per VM to its vCPU/RAM budget and fold pod demand into the shape choice, **so that** many queued agent pods provision small×N rather than starving one big VM. **Acceptance:** pod count per VM never exceeds its core budget; a pod-heavy queue biases the dom0 toward the small shape.
- [✓] **FA-6: route builds/agents by shape.** **As** a build job, **I want** `xcp-build.sh` + the drain coordinator to target the autoscaler-provisioned VM matching my shape (big→BigBoy's big VM, small→the small pool), **so that** jobs land on the right elastic resource. **Acceptance:** a whole-workspace build routes to the big VM; per-crate builds spread across the small pool; routing degrades to BigBoy if the autoscaler is paused.
- [✓] **FA-7: lifecycle observability.** **As** an operator, **I want** the autoscaler to log every create/destroy + publish the live farm topology (per-dom0 shape + utilization) to a CLI/Workbench surface, **so that** on-demand use is auditable. **Acceptance:** each scale event is logged with the reason; `farm topology` (CLI + panel) shows current shapes, running VMs, and per-dom0 utilization.

## ENTERPRISE FIT-AND-FINISH (2026-06-11) — toward an enterprise-class deliverable

Lifted from a 5-pass parallel fit-and-finish evaluation (reliability · security · observability · packaging/release · quality/UX) for an enterprise deliverable **within the stated ≤8-peer flat-trust envelope** (§8 — NOT a zero-trust/hyperscale product). `EFF-#` ids. P1 = blocks a credible enterprise/production cut.

> **DRAIN STATUS (2026-06-12):** the tractable EFF backlog is drained — ~30 items closed this session (EFF-1..17, 19..23, 25..28, 32, 33, 35, 36, 39..44, 46..49) plus the AUD2/AUD3 audit-fix sets and 6 audit cycles (converged CLEAN). **ZERO EFF items remain open — the ENTERPRISE FIT-AND-FINISH program is fully drained** (49 items closed/resolved). The only remaining tracker content is the PEERS feature epic (feature backlog, not fit-and-finish). EFF-18 (resolved: operator chose to keep the correct serial CI) and EFF-31 (resolved: hard coverage gate at 84.84%/80) are now closed. The PEERS epic below is feature backlog, not fit-and-finish. Resuming the GUI items is a deliberate next step, not a quick drain.

### P1 — blockers for a production cut

- [✓] **EFF-1 · SEC · FileXfer Send-To path traversal (target).** `ipc/files.rs:265` joins the `peer:<target>` selector raw into `inbox_root(target)` — `peer:../../etc` escapes the QNM root (`create_dir_all` + write anywhere mackesd can). Reject `target` with `/`,`..`,`\0`,non-`[A-Za-z0-9._-]`; assert the resolved dest is under `<qnm>/inbox/`. *(Regression from AUD-1 this session.)*
- [✓] **EFF-2 · SEC · FileXfer Send-To arbitrary-file read (sources).** `ipc/files.rs:290` copies caller-supplied absolute `sources` verbatim → a Bus writer can exfil `/etc/shadow`/keys into a peer's replicated inbox. Confine sources to an allowlisted root (operator home / declared share); refuse symlinks + paths outside it. — DONE: `FileXfer` gained a `share_root` (defaults to the operator home, canonicalized; `with_share_root` override). `allowed_source()` canonicalizes each source (resolving symlinks + `..`), requires a regular file, and refuses anything not `starts_with(share_root)` — refused sources are skipped + warn-logged + counted in the reply's `refused` field. Two regression tests: a source outside the root is refused while an in-root one delivers, and a symlink-under-root → outside is refused.
- [✓] **EFF-3 · SEC · FileXfer rollback path traversal.** `ipc/files.rs:338` removes files from `inbox_root(target).join(host).join(f)` off forgeable outbox rows. Same target/basename validation + canonicalize-and-contain before `remove_file`.
- [✓] **EFF-4 · REL · Supervisor doesn't restart on worker PANIC.** `workers/mod.rs:481` matches only `Ok/Err`; a panicking `run()` becomes a logged `JoinError`, never restarted. Wrap each `worker.run()` so a panic → restartable `Err` (feed the back-off/breaker).
- [✓] **EFF-5 · REL · Worker-loop `Mutex.lock().expect()` poisons → permanently kills the worker** (compute_expose/firewall_monitor/compute_registry/peer_join). With EFF-4 this silently downs workers. Use poison-tolerant locks (`PoisonError::into_inner`) or `parking_lot`.
- [✓] **EFF-6 · REL · `mackesd healthz` GUI refresh has no timeout** (`panels/mesh_control.rs:606`) — a hung daemon spins the panel forever. Run with a timeout wrapper + render "healthz timed out".
- [✓] **EFF-7 · REL · Bus responder threads have no restart + reply closures no `catch_unwind`** (`bin/mackesd.rs:6146`, `ipc/files.rs:64`) — one panicking request downs Fleet.Files/Settings/file surfaces for the session. Wrap reply dispatch in `catch_unwind` → `err()` body.
- [✓] **EFF-8 · OPS · `healthz` returns a hardcoded `HealthReport::empty()`** (`bin/mackesd.rs:1500`, `ipc/shell.rs:102`) — never live state (leader/node-count/applied-revision all unset). Populate from the store via one shared fn used by CLI + IPC. *(Highest-value ops gap.)* — DONE: `HealthReport::from_store(conn)` populates real node_count + healthy/degraded/unreachable buckets + audit_chain_intact (Break→false, Empty/Intact→true); wired into CLI `Cmd::Healthz` + `ShellService::build_healthz` (both open the store per call, fall back to `empty()` if no store). Remainder: `is_leader`/`applied_revision` stay defaulted pending leader-lease + applied-revision query plumbing.
- [✓] **EFF-9 · OPS · Prometheus textfile exporter built but never written.** `metrics::write_textfile` has zero callers. Add a periodic worker snapshotting control-plane counters (revisions, drift, reconcile failures, breaker trips, peer health, cert-days). — DONE: new `metrics_exporter` worker (Lighthouse-tier, 30s cadence) snapshots store-derivable gauges (mesh node-health buckets, audit-chain-intact, applied-migration count) via `snapshot_counters(conn)` reusing `HealthReport::from_store` (single source of truth with healthz), atomically writes `<textfile_collector>/mackesd.prom`. Remainder (follow-ups appending to the same file): runtime in-process counters (drift/reconcile-failures/breaker-trips) need a shared process registry the workers increment; cert days-remaining is EFF-11.
- [✓] **EFF-10 · OPS · Structured-logging plane logs human text, not the documented JSON.** `bin/mackesd.rs:1469` uses `fmt()` not `.json()`; `LogContext`/correlation-id unused. Switch the subscriber to JSON (env/flag-gated for TTY) + a span layer for the fields.
- [✓] **EFF-11 · OPS · No certificate-expiry monitoring/alert.** Certs store `expire_at` but nothing computes days-remaining; a ≤8 mesh silently loses every cert at turnover. Add a periodic check → `healthz`/metrics/alert under a threshold. — DONE: new `ca::expiry` module probes the **CA cert** (the real cliff — peer certs are epoch-lifetime sentinel `0` by design, so per-peer expiry was a non-target) via `nebula-cert print -json` → `details.notAfter` → days-remaining (pure `parse_not_after_unix` is unit-tested). Wired into the `metrics_exporter` worker: each tick emits `mackesd_ca_cert_days_remaining` (clamped ≥0) + `mackesd_ca_cert_expiry_warning` (1 at/under the 30-day threshold) and logs a warn under threshold (the alert). Remainder: surfacing days-remaining on `healthz`/HealthReport would need `from_store` to take the CA path + run the subprocess (it's store-only today) — Prometheus alert-rule on the metric covers the operational need; the healthz field is a thin follow-up.
- [✓] **EFF-12 · OPS · systemd units have no sandboxing.** `packaging/systemd/*.service` set only `NoNewPrivileges`. Add `ProtectSystem=strict`, `ProtectHome`, `PrivateTmp`, `ReadWritePaths`/`StateDirectory`, `RestrictAddressFamilies`, `MemoryMax`/`CPUQuota`, `LockPersonality`.
- [✓] **EFF-13 · PKG · RPM license contradicts the workspace license + copyleft deps.** generate-rpm `license = "proprietary"` vs workspace `GPL-3.0-or-later` + MPL/GPL pop-os deps. Reconcile the license string; add root `LICENSE`/`COPYING` + a `NOTICE` for bundled deps.
- [✓] **EFF-14 · PKG · No RPM scriptlets** — daemon never enabled, no `systemctl daemon-reload`, no icon/desktop cache, no `/etc/mackesd` dir. Add `post_install`/`preun`/`post_uninstall` (daemon-reload + role-policy enable + `gtk-update-icon-cache` + `update-desktop-database`).
- [✓] **EFF-15 · PKG · `mackesd.service` won't start without the encrypted credential** (`LoadCredentialEncrypted=…/backup-passphrase.cred`, never created) — fresh install = dead unit. Make the credential optional (Condition/wrapper) + create `/etc/mackesd` (0700) in a scriptlet.
- [✓] **EFF-16 · PKG · No supply-chain gating.** — DONE (2026-06-12): `deny.toml` (advisories deny-level w/ unmaintained=workspace-only; license allow-list scoped to what the tree ships, per-crate exceptions for rsip's X11-swapped text, clarifies for the license-field-less pop-os git crates; bans deny openssl/native-tls per §3, multiple-versions warn pending EFF-34; sources locked to crates.io + the pinned pop-os tree). Validated GREEN locally (cargo-deny 0.19.8: advisories/bans/licenses/sources all ok) so the new `supply-chain` CI job is a HARD gate; it also emits a CycloneDX SBOM artifact (cargo-cyclonedx). Bonus hardening en route: migrated mackes-nebula-https-tunnel off unmaintained `rustls-pemfile` (RUSTSEC-2025-0134) onto rustls-pki-types' PemObject; added the missing `license` field to 5 workspace crates; RUSTSEC-2023-0071 (rsa Marvin) ignored with written justification (keygen-only usage, no remote timing oracle — ring does wire sign/verify).
- [✓] **EFF-17 · PKG · `magic-mesh-release` repo RPM referenced but never defined** (`packaging/repo/magic-mesh.repo:11`) — so `dnf install <url>` leaves no repo for upgrades + the `gpgkey=` URL has no key. Define the release sub-package shipping the `.repo` + committed `RPM-GPG-KEY-magic-mesh`. — DONE (2026-06-12): generated the project signing key (Ed25519, 2y expiry; private key in the operator's `~/.gnupg`, revocation cert in `openpgp-revocs.d`); committed the public key at `packaging/repo/RPM-GPG-KEY-magic-mesh`. One-RPM design (no sub-package): the main RPM now ships the `.repo` → `/etc/yum.repos.d/` + the key → `/etc/pki/rpm-gpg/`, so a one-shot install leaves a working gpgcheck'd upgrade channel. Signing the RPM itself stays operator-gated at /release; the gh-pages publish of the key matches the gpgkey URL when stood up.
- [✓] **EFF-18 · QA · `set_var` env-race forces serial mackesd CI.** — RESOLVED (2026-06-12, operator decision: option 3 — keep the serial flag). The `--test-threads=1` run is CORRECT and green; mackesd's ~61 `set_var`/`remove_var` test sites mutate process-global env, which races the parallel harness. This was a CI-SPEED optimization only, and both alternatives carry flakiness risk (inject-config = large production ripple; `serial_test` = must annotate every env *reader*, not just writers). Operator chose to accept the safe serial suite rather than trade correctness for speed. WON'T-DO unless mackesd test latency becomes a real CI pain point, at which point option 1 (inject config) is the path. The CI job comments already document the serial requirement + this rationale.
- [✓] **EFF-19 · CLI · `magic-fleet` is a hand-rolled CLI** — DONE (2026-06-12): full clap derive port (real `--help`/`-V`, typed flags, unknown-flag errors with usage; `--root=`/`--hostname=` syntax the fleet_reconcile worker shells stays accepted). `--json` added to apply/heal/converge/elect/reconcile emitting one stable outcome object ({verb, drift, ok, changed, failures, unreachable, converged}; error form {verb, error, converged:false}); exit codes unchanged. Smoked: help, bad-flag rejection, apply --json round-trip; 49 tests green.

### P2 — enterprise ratchet

- [✓] **EFF-20 · REL · ~8 daemon workers shell out blocking, no timeout** (firewall_preset/mesh_dns/mesh_firewall/cups_sync/netassess/peer_cap/netdata_aggregator/ssh_pubkey_gossip) — a hung child pins a runtime thread. Route through the existing `subprocess_tick` timeout helper. — DONE: new `workers/proc.rs` with `output_with_timeout`/`status_with_timeout` (blocking, dependency-free poll+kill) for the sync `tick_once` helpers and `status_with_timeout_async` (tokio) for the already-async workers, all killing the child at a 15s deadline. Routed every shell-out in all 8 workers (firewall_preset ×5, cups_sync central run_lpadmin/run_capture + systemctl/lpoptions/hostname, netassess run_stdout/binary_present/ping, mesh_firewall, mesh_dns, netdata_aggregator systemctl, peer_cap + ssh_pubkey_gossip async). A hung child is now bounded to the timeout instead of pinning forever. Helper has 5 tests (kills a `sleep 60` promptly, captures fast output, surfaces spawn failure, async variant). Minor follow-up hygiene: the sync `tick_once`es still run on the runtime thread (now bounded, not infinite) — wrapping them in `spawn_blocking` would also keep a slow-but-not-hung child off the scheduler.
- [✓] **EFF-21 · SEC · Secrets via env-var + argv.** — DONE (2026-06-12): (1) **daemon env scrub** — run_serve captures `MDE_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE` once at boot, hands it to the backup worker via `with_passphrase`, and `remove_var`s it immediately, so the dozens of worker subprocesses (nebula-cert, df, firewall-cmd…) never inherit the secret; the exporter's backup-posture gauge rides a boot-time flag OR the systemd-creds file instead of a per-tick env read. The creds path (ENT-11, never env-borne) is untouched and stays preferred. (2) **stdin intake** — `ca export`/`ca import` gained `--passphrase-stdin` (import requires `--input` since its default bundle source is stdin); `enroll` gained `--passcode-stdin`/`--token-stdin` with full clap conflict rules; shared `read_secret_line` helper, empty-secret rejected. Argv/env forms remain as documented fallbacks with prefer-stdin help text. Smoked: stdin passcode read+validation, help text, conflict rejection. 1448 serial green.
- [✓] **EFF-22 · SEC · Netdata `:19999` web UI not confined to the overlay** (aggregator rewrites only `[stream]`). Set `[web] bind to = <overlay-ip>`/127.0.0.1 and/or land the firewalld rule (the open NET-plane item). — DONE: generalized the conf rewriter to `rewrite_named_section` and added `build_web_block` — the aggregator now also writes a `[web] bind to = 127.0.0.1 <overlay-ip>` block each tick (loopback-only pre-enrolment), so the dashboard never binds the default `0.0.0.0`/underlay. Also fixed a latent non-idempotency in the rewriter (it grew a blank line per pass → would have churned a netdata reload every tick); the trailing blank run is now collapsed so an unchanged conf is a true no-op. Tests: web block confines to overlay+loopback / loopback-only, a stock `0.0.0.0` bind is rewritten away, and re-running is idempotent.
- [✓] **EFF-23 · SEC · No body-size cap on Bus responders** — `from_str` on unbounded bodies; cap inbound length + `sources`/row counts before parse. — DONE: shared `ipc::MAX_RPC_BODY_BYTES` (64 KiB) + `body_within_cap()` + `body_too_large_reply()`. Applied at every body-parsing responder loop (files generic `poll_once`, fleet, settings, jobs, directory lifecycle-result/lifecycle/wake) — an over-cap body is refused with an `{"error":…}` envelope + warn, never reaching `from_str`. send-to also caps `sources` at 1024 rows. Tests: over-cap body suppressed + within-cap dispatched.
- [✓] **EFF-24 · OPS · No readiness probe / per-worker liveness.** — DONE (2026-06-12): supervisor maintains a shared `WorkerStatusMap` (alive / restarts / breaker_tripped / last_exit_ok, updated at every lifecycle transition incl. ENT-6 trips); `HealthReport` gains serde-defaulted `workers_alive/workers_total/breaker_tripped/ready`; the Bus healthz (daemon in-process) folds live worker status into `ready` = audit-intact AND all-alive AND no-trip, while the CLI's store-only view reports the store half. Tests: lifecycle/restart tracking, alive-while-running, readiness fold (dead worker or trip ⇒ not ready). Honest remainder: 'leader known' stays out of `ready` until the EFF-8 leader-lease plumbing lands.
- [✓] **EFF-25 · OPS · Wire or remove the dead alert layer.** `events.rs`/`AlertHook`/`dispatch_alerts` have no live callers; only `Reconcile` of 5 `EventKind`s is emitted. Wire it + emit the missing kinds, or delete + document Netdata+firewall_monitor as the only sources. — DONE (wired, 2026-06-12): `MackesdConfig` gained `[[alert_hooks]]` (kind + command, fail-open TOML; typo'd kind drops the hook with a warn — never widened to fire-on-everything; empty command skipped) + `alert_hooks()` resolving to `events::AlertHook`. `apply_repair_rows` now returns its emitted `Event`s and the reconcile tick fires `dispatch_alerts` post-commit (a rolled-back event can never alert). Tests: TOML→hook resolution incl. drop/skip cases. Remainder (honest): only `Reconcile` events are emitted today; emitting `ConfigChange`/`Auth`/`Lifecycle`/`AdminAction` at their natural sites flows through the same dispatch automatically.
- [✓] **EFF-26 · OPS · Headless alert path + disk/breaker/backup alerts.** — DONE (2026-06-12): (1) alert_relay now journals EVERY alert at its severity level (`target: mackesd::alert`, error/warn/info) unconditionally and FIRST — on a headless Lighthouse/Server the journal is the alert surface; Bus-FDO/notify-send toasts are additive. (2) The metrics exporter gained the continuous checks, each a gauge + a `mackesd::alert`-target journal alert: worker liveness/breaker (`mackesd_workers_alive/total`, `mackesd_breaker_tripped`; trip = repeated crit ERROR), disk headroom per monitored fs via bounded `df` (`mackesd_disk_available_bytes{path}`; warn <10% free; QNM root + store fs wired), and backup posture (`mackesd_backup_passphrase_set`, `mackesd_backup_age_seconds`; warns on passphrase-unset, >48h staleness, and enabled-but-no-bundle). Operators alert off either the journal (`journalctl -t … mackesd::alert`) or the Prometheus series.
- [✓] **EFF-27 · OPS · No day-2 admin guide.** — DONE (2026-06-12): root `ADMIN.md` walks the full lifecycle (install/first-boot → mesh-init + enroll → backup provision incl. the unset-passphrase alert behavior → observability table (healthz/doctor/journal-alerts/metrics/hooks) → dnf + fleet upgrade → fleet rollback + node exceptions → state-restore/ca-import DR → cert lifecycle (days-remaining cliff, rotate, decommission, passcode) → troubleshoot), cross-linking every docs/help runbook. All cited commands verified against the live CLI. Ships in the RPM at /usr/share/magic-mesh/.
- [✓] **EFF-28 · OPS · DR path untested + manual.** — DONE (2026-06-12): (1) `mackesd state-restore --verify` — dry-run that dearmors + unseals + reports the bundle (mesh id, exported_at, CA/peer counts, meshfs presence) and stops before ANY mutation; exit 0 = restorable with this passphrase. (2) `tests/dr_drill.rs` walks the mesh-recovery runbook against the real library surfaces: surviving sealed+armored bundle → fresh empty store (the rebuilt box) → wrong-passphrase rejected → restore lands every CA/peer row (values asserted) → post-restore a new single-use enroll bearer mints (43-char base64url contract verified) → export-after-restore re-seals + round-trips (the backup chain re-establishes). Plus a tampered-ciphertext rejection test. Both green.
- [✓] **EFF-29 · PKG · Daemon runs as root, no service identity.** — DONE (2026-06-12, decided + documented + hardened): root is INTENTIONAL — mackesd is the node's system-management plane (writes /etc/nebula + /etc/hosts + voice configs, drives firewalld/systemctl/CUPS/sshd/dnf); DynamicUser or a %pre user breaks every one of those paths or re-grants them via a sudo/polkit matrix wider than root. The decision now lives in the unit itself, alongside the containment: `CapabilityBoundingSet=~` drops the never-needed cap classes (module/rawio/boot/time, audit, MAC, suspend/lease/immutable, pacct/tty/ipclock) + ProtectKernelModules/ProtectClock/SystemCallArchitectures=native on top of the EFF-12 set. `systemd-analyze verify` clean (also dropped the removed CPUAccounting option).
- [✓] **EFF-30 · PKG · RPM GPG signing + ISO checksum/signature undefined.** — DONE (2026-06-12): `install-helpers/sign-release.sh` (operator-gated, invoked from /release, never CI) — rpmsign-embeds the project signature into any .rpm argument, then writes `SHA256SUMS` + a detached armored `SHA256SUMS.asc` covering every artifact (the ISO path). Fails early without the secret key. **Smoked end-to-end with the real project key:** checksum validates, `gpg --verify` returns Good signature. Release skill updated to invoke it.
- [✓] **EFF-31 · CI · Coverage gate is advisory (`continue-on-error`).** — DONE (2026-06-12): flipped to a HARD gate (removed `continue-on-error`). The denominator is now the meaningful **library-logic** set — the 7 iced/libcosmic GUI crates `--exclude`d (render code, correctness via mde-theme tokens + the lifted visual gate, not headless line-coverage) and the binary entrypoints (`src/bin/*.rs`, `src/main.rs` — CLI dispatch + daemon bootstrap glue; mackesd.rs alone is ~5.7k integration-level lines) `--ignore-filename-regex`'d. Measured 84.84% lines on that denominator (cargo-llvm-cov 0.8.7) → an 80 floor with ~5% margin; mackesd runs `--test-threads=1` (EFF-18 env-race). Floor ratchets up as coverage grows; lowering needs a deliberate edit.
- [✓] **EFF-32 · CI · Build on the real target.** — DONE (2026-06-12, scoped): new `fedora` CI job builds+tests the workspace in a `fedora:44` container with the documented dnf dev libs AND the platform binaries (nebula, ansible-core) present, using Fedora's shipped rustc — exactly the mock/RPM build reality. Advisory until soaked on the runner fleet (the ubuntu gates stay required). MSRV check: already enforced (every required job pins 1.85 and builds the full workspace — EFF-33). Honest remainder: **aarch64** needs an arm64 runner or a full ALSA/GTK cross sysroot — tracked, deliberately not faked with a check-only cross job.
- [✓] **EFF-33 · DEPS · MSRV drift.** `rust-toolchain.toml` 1.94 vs `rust-version`/CI 1.85. Reconcile + enforce with the MSRV job. — DONE (2026-06-12): not a conflict — the two bound opposite ends, now documented at both sites: `rust-version = 1.85` is the MSRV **floor**, already enforced (every ci.yml job builds+tests the full workspace on pinned 1.85 — that IS the MSRV job); `rust-toolchain.toml` 1.94 is the dev/build **ceiling** (softbuffer 0.4.8 breaks on 1.95; drop when softbuffer 0.5 lands).
- [✓] **EFF-34 · DEPS · Duplicate iced 0.13 (mde-music) vs 0.14.** — DONE (2026-06-12): mde-music fully ported off iced 0.13 onto libcosmic's vendored iced (same pinned rev as the other three consumers, EFF-35 policy) mirroring the GUI-7 cutover: cosmic::Application impl (Core/APP_ID/init/update/view), cosmic_compat .colr() shim (only the shim it needs), ~8 fork-API-drift fixes (listen_with keyboard events, widget Ids, progress_bar girth, Space builders, Palette.warning, AbsoluteOffset Options), carbon-ok album-art annotations preserved. **iced 0.13 + its exclusive transitive subtree are GONE from the lock**; deny.toml hard-bans `iced <0.14` so it can't return. The remaining crates.io-0.14 vs fork-0.14 pair is deliberate until the operator-locked full-libcosmic cutover ports workbench/voice-hud/iced-components (multiple-versions stays warn, comment updated). Gates: mde-music 42 tests green, full workspace green, cargo deny bans ok, Carbon lint clean.
- [✓] **EFF-35 · DEPS · Floating git pins.** Pin every pop-os git dep by explicit `rev`; document the libcosmic update cadence. — DONE (2026-06-12): all three libcosmic consumers (applet, mde-files, role-chooser) already pin the same explicit rev `cca48bc…` (verified consistent — one resolved tree); the update-cadence policy is now documented in the root Cargo.toml header (bump deliberately, re-pin all three in one commit, gate on full workspace build + GUI tests, never track a branch).
- [✓] **EFF-36 · QA · No property/fuzz tests on untrusted parsers** — DONE (2026-06-12): (1) **KDC wire decoder** — proptest suite (mde-kdc-proto/tests/codec_properties.rs): arbitrary bytes never panic (one-shot + streamed in arbitrary chunkings with a bounded-buffer assertion), encode→decode round-trips over arbitrary type tokens + unicode bodies, split-anywhere streaming reassembly, deterministic oversize-junk error-and-recover. Runs in plain cargo test → already in CI. (2) **Archive extract** — adversarial tests: a raw-forged ../escape.txt tar member (byte-patched header + recomputed cksum — tar::Builder refuses to even write one) is refused while the legitimate member lands and nothing escapes dest; junk/truncated/fake-gzip inputs error, never panic. (3) **Stale claim fixed** — codec.rs + lib.rs no longer cite the never-existent cargo-fuzz target; they cite the real proptest harness. Bus bodies were already capped+tested (EFF-23). Honest remainder: a libFuzzer corpus (cargo-fuzz, nightly) would still add coverage depth beyond proptest — deliberate non-goal until a nightly toolchain lands in the dev tier.
- [✓] **EFF-37 · GUI/a11y · The iced Workbench has no a11y** — RESOLVED (2026-06-12, scoped to what the renderer permits): (1) **Workbench: upstream-impossible on the current renderer** — crates.io iced 0.14.0 ships NO a11y/accesskit feature at all (verified via cargo metadata feature list); the `a11y` + `iced_accessibility` features exist ONLY in libcosmic's vendored iced fork. Workbench a11y therefore arrives with the **operator-locked full-libcosmic cutover** (round-2 decision, 2026-06-10) — labels/focus order land as part of that port, where the hooks actually exist. Same closure pattern as GUI-9 (upstream-impossible, direction recorded). (2) **What was completable, done:** enabled `a11y` on mde-cosmic-applet (it was the one libcosmic surface missing it); mde-files and mde-role-chooser already enable it. All three libcosmic surfaces now expose accesskit trees.
- [✓] **EFF-38 · DOCS · No CONTRIBUTING / architecture doc / README doc-links.** — DONE (2026-06-12): `CONTRIBUTING.md` (Fedora prereqs, MSRV floor/ceiling, the serial-mackesd test rule, all 7 gates incl. cargo-deny, the bite-you conventions — tokens, Bus-not-D-Bus, body caps, proc timeouts, libcosmic pin policy, crypto floor — and the where-things-live table); `docs/architecture.md` (four-plane map, 22-crate table, the key mechanisms: Bus RPC, supervisor/breaker, router+scorer+encryption-floor, LizardFS-as-the-wire transfer, no-center fleet, observability, security posture, and the deliberate NOTs); README gained a Documentation table linking all of it + help/SUPPORT/governance.

### P3 — polish

- [✓] **EFF-39 · OPS · `eprintln!` in daemon/library paths** (telemetry/mdns_relay/events/mde-musicd) bypass the logging plane → `tracing::warn!/error!` with fields. (CLI `println` is fine.) — DONE (mackesd scope): converted the 8 daemon/library `eprintln!` in `telemetry/mod.rs` (2), `workers/mdns_relay.rs` (4), `events.rs` (2) to `tracing::warn!/info!` with structured fields — these run under mackesd's initialized subscriber. Split: mde-musicd is a separate binary with NO `tracing` subscriber (and is dual CLI/daemon), so its conversion is **EFF-39b** below.
- [✓] **EFF-39b · OPS · mde-musicd has no logging plane.** 18 `eprintln!` across `main.rs`/`engine.rs`/`mpris.rs`/`bus_responder.rs`; the binary inits no `tracing` subscriber. Add `tracing`+`tracing-subscriber` to it, init in `serve()` (the daemon path), and convert the daemon/library `eprintln!` (keep the CLI-subcommand `eprintln!`/`println!` as user output). — DONE (2026-06-12): subscriber initialized in `main()` (stderr writer + RUST_LOG env-filter, `info` default — journal captures it under systemd AND interactive CLI runs still see library warns); converted all 9 daemon/library sites (engine ×3, mpris ×3, bus_responder ×1, serve ×2) to `tracing::warn!/error!/info!` with structured fields. CLI result output (ping/cache/state/queue/play) stays println/eprintln by design. 77 tests green.
- [✓] **EFF-40 · PKG · No CHANGELOG / release tags / `release` NEVRA field.** — DONE (2026-06-12): root `CHANGELOG.md` (Keep-a-Changelog; [Unreleased] seeded with the packaged-release feature/security set; pre-release history stays in git/docs/design); `release = "1"` added to the generate-rpm block with the bump-alone-for-asset-changes / reset-on-version-bump rule; the `magic-mesh-v<ver>` tag convention already lives in the release skill (first tag lands at the operator-gated cut).
- [✓] **EFF-41 · PKG · Release skill stale** — says "no generate-rpm metadata / cut blocked" though it exists. Update `/release` to the live packaging + signing/publish steps. — DONE (2026-06-12): rewrote the "not yet wired" block to the live state (generate-rpm metadata location, asset-list-as-source-of-truth, build steps, 22-crate count), and the failure-modes section now gates a public release on the signing key while allowing a scoped unsigned test cut.
- [✓] **EFF-42 · PKG · RPM metadata gaps** — DONE (2026-06-12): verified `nebula` IS in stock Fedora (1.10.3 on F44) so the hard Requires resolves; `ansible-core` moved to **Recommends** (only Server-tier workers shell to it and they degrade gracefully; default dnf still pulls weak deps); added `Obsoletes: mde-core` for upgrade continuity from the E8-era package name. Provides is implicit (the package IS `magic-mesh`).
- [✓] **EFF-43 · PKG · Voice units referenced but not shipped** — `mackesd voice render-config` ExecStartPre cites kamailio/rtpengine units absent from `packaging/systemd/`. Ship them or fix the comments. — DONE (2026-06-12): shipped `kamailio-mde.service` + `rtpengine-mde.service` (disabled by default per the v4.1.0 spec; ExecStartPre regenerates the config set via `mackesd voice render-config`; EFF-12-style hardening; external kamailio/rtpengine packages deliberately not hard-Required) + added both to the generate-rpm assets.
- [✓] **EFF-44 · GUI · Workbench window controls render Unicode-glyph fallback** (`header.rs:11`) — DONE (2026-06-12): FALSE POSITIVE / stale comment. The UX-8.a SVG swap already shipped (BUG-13.c): `control_button` resolves WindowMinimize/Maximize/Close through `mde_icon(..).svg_bytes()` (→ remove/fullscreen/close Material Symbols, baked at 20/24/40px — verified by the mde-theme `optical_svg_sizes_map_to_bundled_assets` + `svg_bytes_constant_for_never_fill_icons` tests), so the SVG render path always wins; the Unicode glyphs survive only as the unreachable `fallback_glyph` branch. Fixed the stale module-doc comment that still claimed Unicode placeholders.
- [✓] **EFF-45 · GUI · ~18 workbench panels lack explicit error-state handling** — DONE (2026-06-12): new `panel_chrome::error_state` (the load-FAILED counterpart to `empty_state`: Icon::StatusError, fixed "Couldn't load this panel" heading, danger-tinted detail, Retry CTA). 11 offender panels converted to the pattern (load_error: Option<String>; Result-honest loaders where source-absent stays the legitimate empty state but real I/O/RPC/parse-of-source failures are errors; error render BEFORE the is-empty branch; Retry wired to each panel's refresh): hardware (exemplar), audit, connect, fleet_revisions, fleet_rollup, inventory, jobs, mesh_history, playbooks, run_history, snapshots. mesh_bus + mesh_federation verified ALREADY honest (per-tab error: Option<String> + Result messages) — no redundant panel-wide state added. 875 workbench tests green, clippy clean, §4 lint clean.
- [✓] **EFF-46 · QA · `#[ignore]`d tests** (systemd-creds, throughput-floor) never run in CI — add an `--include-ignored` nightly/pre-cut job. — DONE (2026-06-12): ci.yml gains `schedule` (04:00 nightly) + `workflow_dispatch` triggers and an `ignored-tests` job: the NF-1.6 throughput floor runs hard-fail (runner-capable), the systemd-creds round-trip runs `continue-on-error` (needs a real host's credential key; authoritative when dispatched on one). YAML validated.
- [✓] **EFF-47 · BUS · RPC reply/request topics persist at Default (7-day TTL)** — give `reply/*` + ephemeral `action/*` a short per-topic TTL so interactive RPCs don't accumulate. — DONE (2026-06-12): `RetentionPolicy.ttl_ephemeral_secs` (1 h default) + a dedicated victim query in `run_pass_at` reaping `reply/%` + `action/%` regardless of priority class (deduped against the per-priority pass; `audit/*` exclusion kept for defense in depth). Tests: 2h-old RPC topics reap while a same-age normal default-priority topic survives; a 30-min in-flight RPC survives; a both-passes victim removes exactly once. 328 mde-bus tests green.
- [✓] **EFF-48 · CLI · STUN encoder `panic!` on IPv6** (`stun.rs:104`) — return Result/skip-with-warn. — DONE: `encode_binding_success_with_xor_mapped` now returns `Option<Vec<u8>>` (`None` for IPv6, deferred per Q9) instead of panicking; the stun_gather responder skips a `None`. Test asserts IPv6→None, IPv4→Some round-trips.
- [✓] **EFF-49 · I18N · No localization provision** (all en-US). Likely out-of-envelope for a ≤8-peer workgroup — document the en-US-only scope in SUPPORT, or introduce `i18n-embed`+Fluent if in scope. — DONE (2026-06-12): scoped OUT deliberately — SUPPORT.md's supported-envelope table now carries a Language row (en-US only; revisit only if the envelope widens). No i18n framework introduced.

## AUDIT-2026-06-12 r3 — cycle-3 fresh sweep (all closed same day)

Cycle-3 sweep over the post-CLEAN tree (deeper dig: previously-skimmed crates, off-scale metrics, substrate gates, doc command accuracy). 12 findings, all resolved:

- [✓] **AUD3 S-1 · stale "Phase G stubs" comment** at the fleet responder spawn — rewritten to the real FPG-4 state.
- [✓] **AUD3 S-2 · KDC2-1.9 scorer never wired.** `tick_once` now runs `scorer::select` per tracked peer (Control class drives primary; fallback refreshed on quiet ticks; "nothing sendable" leaves the prior path); daemon loads policy.toml (system+user, fail-open) into `with_policy`. 3 new router tests.
- [✓] **AUD3 S-3 · `peer_join` REMOVED** — it spawned `mde-peer-card`, a binary deleted in the E11 pivot, so the PC-3.a "wire into enrollment" plan would have wired a dead modal. Module + `Cmd::PeerCard` deleted; peer-arrival UX is the Workbench PEERS surfaces.
- [✓] **AUD3 S-4 · FALSE POSITIVE** — mdns_relay's inbound half IS implemented (INBOUND block + tests); only the module doc still said "follow-up". Doc fixed.
- [✓] **AUD3 S-5 · KDC2-1.12 audit emission wired.** New `events::append_event` (hash-chained, verifier-intact test) + `append_and_alert`; every primary flip appends a `PathSwitchEvent` row (kind=lifecycle → `[[alert_hooks]]` fire on path switches too) via `with_audit_sink(db_path, node_id)`.
- [✓] **AUD3 CV-1 · encryption floor enforced.** `TransportSample.encryption` + `Policy.min_content_encryption` (default AES-256-class; `strength_rank`: None<128<{256,ChaCha20}); content classes (Clipboard/FileBulk/Notification) filter out weaker transports in `score()`; Control exempt so path negotiation keeps working. `EncryptionKind::for_transport` maps all four kinds (all ≥ floor — locked by test); `direct_udp_default` corrected from the stale WireGuard-AES-128 claim to Nebula AES-256. policy.toml gains `min_content_encryption` (typo = hard parse error, never silently weakened). 4 new scorer tests.
- [✓] **AUD3 CV-2 · off-scale `spacing(22)`** in mde-files banner → space.lg (20).
- [✓] **AUD3 CV-3 · density-blind `spacing(20)`** ×2 (workbench app shell, jobs panel) → new `panel_chrome::column_gap(density)`.
- [✓] **AUD3 F-1..F-3 · doc command accuracy:** `mackesd decommission` is positional (not `--node-id`); `enroll-token` examples gained the required `--mesh-id` (×3); `state-restore` example gained the required bundle path.
- [✓] **AUD3 F-4 · `mackesd --help` said "Mackes XFCE Workstation"** → MCNF / Fedora-Cosmic.
- [✓] **AUD3 F-5 = EFF-43** — closed above (units shipped).

## AUDIT-2026-06-12 — post-hardening integrity sweep (current actionable set)

Lifted from the 2026-06-12 `docs/COMPLIANCE.md` sweep (supersedes 2026-06-11; every prior headline item verified resolved + reachable). 5 new actionable + 3 REMOVE-or-FINISH; 3 cross-refs confirmed still open (EFF-17, EFF-25, EFF-41 — tracked above, not duplicated). Finding ids `AUD2-#` → COMPLIANCE.md row #.

- [✓] **AUD2-7 · PKG** — **FALSE POSITIVE, cleared 2026-06-12.** `mde-mesh-wallpaper` IS a real bin: `mde-workbench/src/bin/mde-mesh-wallpaper.rs` (PD-10 landed it), auto-discovered by Cargo (`autobins`) without an explicit `[[bin]]` block; `cargo build -p mde-workbench --bin mde-mesh-wallpaper` verified green. Asset line kept with a clarifying comment; the audit skill's safeguards now warn about the auto-discovery pitfall.
- [✓] **AUD2-1 · OPS · Router histogram observed but never exported.** — DONE (2026-06-12), and worse than reported: production never even attached the histogram (`with_metrics` had zero bin callers, so the router ran with `metrics: None`). run_serve now creates the shared `RouterMetrics` Arc, attaches it to `MeshRouterWorker` AND `MetricsExporterWorker::with_router_metrics`; the exporter snapshots it per tick (brief lock+clone) into `write_textfile`'s histogram section. Test: an attached histogram lands in mackesd.prom as a full `_bucket`/`_sum`/`_count` series.
- [✓] **AUD2-2 · REMOVE dead pre-FileXfer free fns.** — DONE (2026-06-12): deleted free `inbox_reply`/`outbox_reply`/`file_ops_reply` + the orphaned `SEND_TO_NOT_CONFIGURED` const; kept `downloads_reply` (still the wired downloads responder). Their tests were folded onto the live `FileXfer` methods.
- [✓] **AUD2-3 · THEME · `Elevation` enum unused.** — REMOVED (2026-06-12): the Q29/Q30 per-tier bundles targeted the deleted shell's OSD/greeter/menu surfaces, which Cosmic owns post-E11. `shadows::Shadow` stays (consumed via `Theme::modal_shadow`); 156 mde-theme tests green after removal.
- [✓] **AUD2-4 · THEME · `brand` module unused.** — WIRED (2026-06-12): mde-role-chooser now renders the MCNF wordmark in its header via `Brand::new().bytes(BrandSlot::Wordmark)` → cosmic svg (baked-fallback guarantees render); the RPM also ships the swappable brand pack to `/usr/share/mde/brand/` so the loader's documented system layer is real.
- [✓] **AUD2-5 · DOC · KDC crypto prose says "RSA-2048".** — DONE (2026-06-12): module header + section comment + struct doc now state own keys are always 4096 (mde-kdc-host keygen, §3 floor) and that `RSA_PKCS1_2048_8192_SHA256` is ring's verify-range constant accepting stock KDE Connect peers ≥2048 (interop acceptance of a foreign key, never our key size).
- [✓] **AUD2-6 · DOC · audit skill says "20 crates"** — DONE (2026-06-12): 20→22; also fixed the same skill's stale "mackesd is a library crate (no main.rs)" (it has the daemon + meshctl bins) and its "no generate-rpm metadata" packaging note, and added the Cargo `src/bin/*.rs` auto-discovery safeguard (the AUD2-7 false-positive lesson).

## AUDIT-2026-06-11 — fit-for-purpose follow-ups (superseded — all closed)

Lifted from the 2026-06-11 `docs/COMPLIANCE.md` sweep ("audit the platform for fit for purpose"). The mesh control plane is solid; these close the data-plane + distribution + guardrail gaps. Finding ids are `AUD-#` → COMPLIANCE.md row #. Ordered by impact.

- [✓] **AUD-1 · Mesh file transfer (Send-To) is a facade — wire it end-to-end (FINISH).** The SVC-5 headline capability is dead at every seam: GUI `Message::SendTo`/`DragDrop` reducers discard the request (`mde-files/app.rs:653,642`), no UI calls `Backend::send_to`, `RealBackend::send_to` → local → `DestinationUnreachable` (`backend.rs:736,583`), the `file-ops/send-to` verb returns `SEND_TO_NOT_CONFIGURED` (`mackesd/ipc/files.rs:185`), and the complete tested `path_safety→preflight→orchestrator` engine is orphaned (zero callers). Connect the GUI reducers → a real `BusBackend`/`MeshBackend` transfer method → the `send-to` verb → the orchestrator engine. **Biggest single fit-for-purpose win.**
- [✓] **AUD-4 · Package the cosmic-applet (FINISH).** The §5 Cosmic integration binary (`mde-cosmic-applet`) is in no RPM `assets`, no CI, and has no cosmic-panel `.desktop` registration — it builds into a void. Add it to `[package.metadata.generate-rpm]` + ship a panel-registration `.desktop` under `packaging/`.
- [✓] **AUD-5 · Runtime DISCLAIMER accept gate (FINISH).** §5 wants a pre-flight accept; today it's build-time non-empty enforcement + a display-only About panel. Add a first-run / install-time "I agree before use" consent path.
- [✓] **AUD-6 · Cosmic first-run role-chooser GUI (FINISH, = PKG-5 visual half).** Role-pin is real on CLI/kickstart, but the Cosmic first-run chooser the kickstart defers to doesn't exist. Build the libcosmic first-run RoleChooser (Lighthouse ⊂ Server ⊂ Workstation).
- [✓] **AUD-2 · KDC outbound drainer (FINISH).** `ring`/`sms`/`clipboard`/`share` enqueue to `PendingSends` but nothing drains it; `LanTransport::send_to` is called nowhere — so the PD-3 L6 Devices Ring/Send-file + phone hub never reach the device. Build the `kdc_outbound` worker that drains the queue over the live `LanTransport`.
- [✓] **AUD-3 · Rewire the Connected Devices panel (FINISH or REMOVE).** `connect.rs` `PeerAction` is a no-op waiting on the retired `dev.mackes.MDE.Connect` D-Bus; `load()` returns nothing. Rewire to the live `action/connect/devices` Bus roster (which PD-3 L6 already uses), or drop the nav entry. (Becomes fully real once AUD-2 lands.)
- [✓] **AUD-21 · Stand up the §4 + §2 lint gates (FINISH).** `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` claims both are lint-gated but only `lint-mesh-boundary.sh` (§6) exists. Add a §4 raw-hex/scattered-token lint + a §2 private-bus-name lint (mirroring the boundary lint), wire into CI. Prevents recurrence of AUD-8/9/10.
- [✓] **AUD-8 · §2 — `dev.mackes.MDE.Workbench` private D-Bus name (FINISH/decide).** Single-instance detection `request_name`s a reintroduced MDE-private bus name (`main.rs:134`, `single_instance.rs:25`). Move single-instance to the Bus / a lockfile, or sanction it as the one documented §2 exception.
- [✓] **AUD-9 · §4 — mde-files parallel token module (FINISH).** `mde-files/theme.rs:39-130` is a ~40-constant parallel palette claiming to be "the single source," contradicting §4 (single-sourced in `mde-theme`); evades the lint via `rgb_hex(0x..)`. Re-source from `mde-theme` (or have §4 explicitly sanction it).
- [✓] **AUD-10 · §4 — raw inline blue in `tx_row` (FINISH).** `mde-files/widgets.rs:825` `Color::from_rgb(0x6f.../255,…)` on the render path; replace with an `mde-theme` token.
- [✓] **AUD-7 · inbox/outbox/downloads producers (FINISH).** `mackesd/ipc/files.rs:121,139,158` serve empty `"[]"` — the AF-5 producer side is absent. Wire the producers (or fold into AUD-1's transfer pipeline).
- [✓] **AUD-11 · §3 — document the SIP MD5 interop exception (FINISH-doc).** RFC 3261 SIP digest uses MD5 (server-chosen, external-spec); add SIP to §3's documented MD5 exceptions (or prefer SHA-256 when offered).
- [✓] **AUD-12 · REMOVE dead mesh-types modules.** `tag_predicate.rs`, `window_rules.rs`, `workspace_overrides.rs` have zero workspace consumers (MackesWorkstation leftovers; the WM ones are Cosmic's concern now).
- [✓] **AUD-13 · REMOVE dead mde-iced-components widgets (= GUI-5).** `context_menu_surface` + the `ContextMenuItem` family + the `overlay_*`/`with_alpha` re-exports are unused. **Keep `object_card`** (still used by `mesh_bus.rs`).
- [✓] **AUD-14 · REMOVE the never-spawned `MeshShuntWorker` struct.** `mesh_shunt.rs:141` wrapper is never instantiated; its free fns stay (inlined into `kdc_host.rs`).
- [✓] **AUD-15..20 · Doc drift + dead retired-surface actions (FINISH-doc/retarget).** `app.rs:885` live `mde settings` exec (retired dispatcher); `repair.rs` `labwc --reconfigure` action (EOL shell → retarget Cosmic/drop); `displays/keyboard/mouse` docs naming labwc as current; `picker.rs:7` "warm-dark"; `mackes-transport` docs "Tailscale DERP" heritage prose; `home.rs:894` stale `dev.mackes.MDE.Connect` probe → use the Bus.

Faster direct paths rank higher; unmeasured peers neutral; over-budget/unreachable floor. 2 new tests; loss_pct doc corrected.

## AUDIT-2026-06-12 r5 — cycle-5 verify + convergence (all closed same day)

- [✓] **AUD5-1 · QA · stale insta snapshot.** EFF-24 added 4 HealthReport fields but committed only the `.snap.new`; `snapshot_health_report_shape` failed at workspace scope. Promoted the accepted snapshot (shape verified against `HealthReport::empty()`).
- [✓] **AUD5-2 · SEC/QA · audit-chain hash flake (latent).** `events::append_event` + `worker::apply_repair_rows` hashed each row with `now_ms()` but stored `created_at` from a separate `Utc::now()`; `load_audit_rows` reparses created_at to recompute the chain hash, so sub-ms drift spuriously failed `audit::verify` (false tamper alarm). Both now derive created_at from the single now_ms instant. Passed in isolation, failed under load.

- [✓] **AUD6-1 · SEC · passcode on argv (EFF-21 caller regression).** `mesh_join.rs:165–176` `run_mackesd_enroll` passes the 16-char passcode as `--passcode <value>` on argv (`/proc/<pid>/cmdline`-visible); the daemon's `--passcode-stdin` path exists and is ignored. - [✓] **AUD6-2 · DEAD · orchestrator.rs unreachable.** 522-line Send-To state machine in mackesd, zero production callers workspace-wide (only a "future epic" comment in `ipc/files.rs`); internal tests only. REMOVE (git history keeps it for the future epic). **DONE 2026-06-13:** module deleted; lib.rs + ipc/files.rs comments updated.
- [✓] **AUD6-3 · DEAD/DOC · ipc/mod.rs.** `MACKESD_BUS_NAME = "org.mackes.mackesd"` never referenced (delete); module doc table still lists retired `org.mackes.*` D-Bus services as active (rewrite for the mde-bus reality). **DONE 2026-06-13:** constant deleted, doc table rewritten.
- [✓] **AUD6-4 · VERIFY · ipc/notifications.rs.** zbus FDO-Notifications `#[interface]` with no apparent `serve_at` in `run_serve` — confirm wiring; REMOVE if dead scaffolding. **VERIFIED DEAD + REMOVED 2026-06-13:** `NotificationsService` had zero callers workspace-wide (Cosmic owns FDO Notifications under E11; mackesd's live path is the `bus_bridge` client side); 325 lines deleted.
- [✓] **AUD6-5 · VERIFY · SIP digest algorithm preference.** `sip.rs:1249` defaults to MD5 (RFC 3261 §3 interop exception) — confirm SHA-256 is preferred when the registrar offers it; fix if not. **VERIFIED + REAL BUG FOUND/FIXED 2026-06-13:** the client correctly echoes the registrar's challenged algorithm, BUT rsip 0.4 only parses the non-RFC `SHA256` spelling — an RFC 7616 `algorithm=SHA-256` challenge failed `typed()` wholesale and broke registration outright. Added a manual fallback challenge parser (quote-aware param split; accepts hyphenated + bare spellings; unknown algorithms skip the header so a sibling challenge can win) + a SHA-256-challenge→SHA-256-response test.
- [✓] **AUD6-6 · PKG · voice externals not Recommends.** `kamailio-mde.service`/`rtpengine-mde.service` ExecStart `/usr/sbin/kamailio`+`/usr/bin/rtpengine` but neither is in generate-rpm Requires/Recommends — enabling the units on a fresh install fails silently. Add weak Recommends (ansible-core pattern). **DONE 2026-06-13:** kamailio + rtpengine added.
- [✓] **AUD6-7 · POLISH · `role-workers` empty help text.** Add the clap doc comment in bin/mackesd.rs. **DONE 2026-06-13.**
- [✓] **AUD6-8 · GUI · compute panel Loading-forever.** First-paint "Loading instances…" has no timeout/error arm (probes local hypervisors directly; EFF-45 N/A) — add a failure arm. **DONE 2026-06-13:** 10 s `QUERY_TIMEOUT` on every hypervisor query — a wedged libvirtd degrades to the honest "No local hypervisor found" instead of Loading-forever.
- [✓] **AUD6-9 · DOC · topology/mod.rs:58.** `NebulaLighthouseRelay` doc says "Tailscale's relayed-via-DERP transport" — Nebula-native variant, fix the copy. **DONE 2026-06-13** (NebulaDirect WireGuard mention fixed too).
- [✓] **AUD6-10 · DOC · README 4 fixes.** Drop `gtk3-devel` (no GTK dep); "sshd overlay-bind" → additive (public listener kept); remove `mesh-wallpaper` from the crate diagram (bin lives in mde-workbench); "~30 workers" → ~50. **DONE 2026-06-13** (verified no GTK linkage in Cargo.lock).
- [✓] **AUD6-11 · DOC · pre-pivot crate headers (5 crates).** mackesd lib.rs ("linked into mackes-panel", `PROJECT_WORKLIST.md`); mde-workbench lib.rs+Cargo.toml ("GTK3 Python Workbench", "mded", `dev.mackes.MDE.Shell.*`); mackes-config lib.rs+Cargo.toml ("mackes-panel"); mackes-mesh-types lib.rs ("mackes-panel"). Rewrite for current reality. **DONE 2026-06-13:** all five headers + both Cargo descriptions rewritten.
- [✓] **AUD6-12 · DOC · COPR residue in design docs.** enterprise-readiness.md ("signed COPR"; "no unit exists to start any of them at boot" — units ship now), platform-survey-answers.md ×2, planes.md W61, platform-survey.md Q79 (annotate superseded → GitHub-hosted dnf repo, 2026-06-10 decision). **DONE 2026-06-13:** all four docs updated; the two stale enterprise-readiness FAIL verdicts annotated historical/resolved.
