## MESH-BRINGUP — findings from the live 4-node bring-up (2026-06-13)

Surfaced standing up a fresh `magic-mesh` across 2 public-IP cloud lighthouses + 2 NAT'd LAN peers (operator-directed test). Both are real; logged before continuing the hand-assembled test mesh.

- [✓] **MESH-1 · DESIGN/SEC · enrollment has no network bootstrap — remote/NAT'd peers can't self-join.** `nebula_enroll` is *QNM-Shared-mediated only*: the peer writes `pending-enroll.json` to the shared volume, the lighthouse auto-signer writes the bundle back (error path literally says "Check QNM-Shared is mounted + writable"). A fresh peer must therefore *already* share QNM-Shared (LizardFS/NFS) with the lighthouse — but QNM-Shared rides the overlay, and you can't reach the overlay until enrolled → chicken-and-egg with no built-in resolver. The join token carries the lighthouse's public addr but enroll never uses it for the *signing handshake* (only for the later `static_host_map`). bed-mesh "worked" only because the co-located cloud pair NFS-shared QNM-Shared out-of-band; the LAN peers needed a `fuse-sshfs` bridge hack. **FINISH:** add a network enrollment path — lighthouse exposes a CSR-sign endpoint on its public IP (reuse the nebula-https-tunnel :443 / bearer-authed) so a remote peer submits its CSR + gets the signed bundle over the network, *then* mounts QNM-Shared over the overlay for steady state. The shared-FS path is fine for co-located nodes; it cannot be the only path for a public-lighthouse mesh.
- [✓] **MESH-2 · BUG · peer materialized the lighthouse `static_host_map` with a LOCAL interface IP.** On the `fedora` peer, `nebula_supervisor` rendered `/etc/nebula/config.yaml` as `"10.42.0.1": ["192.168.122.1:4242"]` — `192.168.122.1` is the peer's own libvirt `virbr0`, not the lighthouse's public `45.55.33.179`. The received bundle was CORRECT (`external_addr: 45.55.33.179:4242`); the render clobbered it with a locally-detected address → nebula dials a dead end, overlay never forms (100% loss). The co-located cloud peer `.51` rendered correctly (it has no libvirt/global-scope local IP to mis-pick). **FINISH:** root-cause the static_host_map render — the lighthouse entry must use `bundle.lighthouses[].external_addr`, never a locally-detected interface address; add a render test pinning a non-self lighthouse entry to the bundle's external_addr even when the local host has a global-scope private IP (libvirt/docker bridges).

## ONBOARD — Magic onboarding: network enrollment + zero-friction join (design: docs/design/magic-onboarding.md, 2026-06-13)

Fixes MESH-1 by promoting cert-signing from a shared filesystem to a lighthouse network service, + collapses setup to two verbs with a TUI. §1: Nebula control plane only (no Tailscale/Headscale/DERP). Locks: dedicated rustls HTTPS `/enroll` endpoint · token-pinned cert fingerprint · `found`+`join` verbs · ratatui TUI.

- [✓] **ONBOARD-1: join token v3 — embed the endpoint cert fingerprint + enroll port.** Done — `nebula_enroll.rs` `JoinToken` gained `fp: Option<String>` (`#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if)]`, additive); wire form `mesh:<id>@<ip>:<port>#<bearer>?fp=<sha256-hex>`; `parse_join_token` splits `?fp=` off the bearer + validates 64-char lowercase hex; `encode()` round-trips the suffix; `JOIN_TOKEN_MAX_LEN` 120→200 (fits the +68-char fp, still QR-friendly). v2.5 tokens parse with `fp: None`. 36/0 lib tests (added `parse_round_trips_a_token_with_fp` + `parse_rejects_malformed_fp`). *(Mint-side fp injection lands with the endpoint in ONBOARD-2/4 — the fp is the endpoint cert's, which doesn't exist until `found`.)*
- [✓] **ONBOARD-2: lighthouse rustls `/enroll` endpoint.** Done. New `nebula_enroll_endpoint` module: self-signed endpoint identity (`generate_endpoint_identity` → cert/key PEM + SHA-256 `fingerprint` of the DER, the value tokens pin), minimal HTTP/1.1 framing (`parse_request`, 64 KiB body cap), and the pure `handle_enroll` (parses a `PendingEnrollment`, signs via the new shared `sign_csr_into_bundle` core, redeems the single-use bearer, returns the bundle JSON; maps SignCsrError → 400/401/403/409/500). New `workers::nebula_enroll_listener` rustls worker (default `:4243`, ring provider, per-conn timeout, spawn_blocking signing) wired into `run_serve` — spawns only when the endpoint cert is present (same gate as the covert :443 listener; peer-role boxes skip), roster reuses the csr-watcher external-addr probe so bundles advertise the PUBLIC addr (MESH-2 guard). `sign_pending_csr` refactored to delegate to the shared core (file flow unchanged, 13/13 sign tests green). **Real end-to-end TLS test:** a fp-pinning rustls client POSTs a CSR → 200 + bundle → bearer spent. 54/0 nebula_enroll tests. *(`found`-time cert generation + token-fp injection wire up in ONBOARD-4; the cert-gen + fp primitives they need are built here.)*
- [✓] **ONBOARD-3: peer network-enroll client (fingerprint-pinned).** Done — new `nebula_enroll_client` module. `PinnedCertVerifier` (rustls `ServerCertVerifier`): accepts ONLY the cert whose SHA-256 == token fp AND verifies the TLS handshake signature via the ring provider (so a MITM can neither swap the cert nor replay the public cert without its key — fail-closed). `enroll_over_network` opens a fp-pinned TLS conn, POSTs the CSR to `/enroll`, parses status+bundle (maps non-200 → `Refused{status,message}`). `network_enroll` builds identity+CSR from the token, enrolls, and `persist_bundle`s — materializes `/etc/nebula` via the supervisor's `materialize_config` (`static_host_map` from `external_addr`, MESH-2 guard) + drops the bundle at QNM-Shared `bundle_path` for steady-state. **Full-stack tests** (real pinned client ↔ real `serve_connection`): happy path → bundle + bearer spent + config.yaml carries the PUBLIC `203.0.113.7:4242`; **fp-mismatch → fail-closed, bearer NOT spent**. 60/0 nebula_enroll tests. *(`serve`→overlay dial is exercised live in ONBOARD-8; the materialized config that drives it is verified here.)*
- [✓] **ONBOARD-4: `found` + `join` verbs.** Done — two new CLI verbs in `bin/mackesd.rs`. `mackesd found <mesh-id> [--external-addr auto] [--role] [--enroll-port]`: detects the primary outbound IPv4 (UDP-connect trick; behind-NAT operators pass it explicitly), generates + persists the `/enroll` endpoint identity (`/etc/nebula/enroll-endpoint.{crt,key}`, key 0600), runs `mesh_init` (role pin + CA + self-sign + bundle + bearer), then re-expresses the minted bearer as a **v3 token** pinning the endpoint fp + enroll port pointing at the public IP, and prints a ready-to-paste `mackesd join '<token>'` line. `mackesd join <token> [--role] [--name]`: parses the token, pins the role (if unpinned), and — when the token carries `fp` — runs the fingerprint-pinned `network_enroll` (ONBOARD-3) materializing `/etc/nebula`; fp-absent tokens fall back to the legacy QNM-Shared `enroll_with_token`; no token → launches the `mde-enroll` TUI (ONBOARD-5) with a clear fallback message. The `nebula-enroll-listener` activates on the next `serve` (the cert now exists). Smoked: `found --help`, `join` bad-token → "invalid join token", `join` no-token → TUI-fallback guidance. The found→join token/enroll path is the exact shape ONBOARD-3's full-stack test exercises end-to-end. 60/0 enroll tests, clean clippy.
- [✓] **ONBOARD-5: ratatui enrollment TUI (`mde-enroll`).** Done — new `crates/mesh/mde-enroll` crate (ratatui 0.29 + crossterm 0.28). Full-screen TUI: a lighthouse-IP override field + a token field (paste target, Tab to switch), Enter to join, a live 5-step progress tracker (Validate → Pin cert + send CSR → Receive bundle → Write /etc/nebula → Bring up overlay) with `[ ]`/`[>]`/`[✓]`/`[✗]` glyphs, a status strip (red on error/fail, green on done), and a footer of keybindings; `r` retries, `q`/Esc quits. The enroll runs on a worker thread driving the real ONBOARD-3 stages (`build_identity`→`build_pending`→`enroll_over_network`→`persist_bundle`→`systemctl`) and streams progress to the UI over an mpsc channel, so the UI stays responsive. Pure state machine in `app.rs` is fully unit-tested (6/0: typing/focus, validation incl. fp-less + IP-override, step progression, failure+retry, frozen-during-enroll). Terminal-native colors (no §4 `from_rgb` — carbon lint clean). `mackesd join` with no token launches it; shipped in the RPM at `/usr/bin/mde-enroll`. Runs headless over SSH. *(`persist_bundle` made pub so the TUI shares the client's building blocks rather than reimplementing them.)*
- [✓] **ONBOARD-6: QNM-Shared over the overlay (steady state).** Done. The shared-state plane (leader lease, peer directory, fleet/health, CA) lives on QNM-Shared, which the code always assumed was a mounted LizardFS volume but no deployment ever provisioned → multi-node meshes silently showed NO LEADER + empty directory. Built: (1) `install-helpers/setup-qnm-shared.sh` — reproducible, boot-durable LizardFS over the overlay (`--master` lighthouse-only §8, `--chunkserver` ≥2 for goal 2, `--client` mfsmount at `/root/QNM-Shared`); generates systemd units (master drop-in `After=nebula` + ExecStartPre stale-lock clear so unclean shutdown self-heals; chunkserver drop-in; `qnm-shared.service` oneshot mount `After=nebula` `Before=mackesd` with master-reachability wait + `Restart`; mackesd ordered after it). All listeners overlay-bound (FS master never public). (2) `workers/leader_election.rs` — continuous lease renewal every 20s (the election primitive existed but only the upgrade-watcher ever called it, and only mid-upgrade → steady-state meshes never elected). **Live 4-node result:** LizardFS master(.179)+2 chunkservers(.179,.51)+4 client mounts over the overlay, cross-node replication verified, **leader elected (`peer:Lighthouse-02`)**, `mackesd peers` shows all 4 online+healthy from every node, all qnm/lizardfs services `enabled` (boot-durable). F44 LAN boxes use the F43 `lizardfs-client` RPM (runs on F44, like mackesd).

### ONBOARD-6 follow-ups (the audit-gap process fixes the operator requested 2026-06-14)
- [✓] **OB6-FIX-1: multi-node integration test.** Done — `crates/mesh/mackesd/tests/mesh_shared_state.rs` points TWO node-instances at ONE shared dir (the faithful stand-in for the gap, which was the *absence* of sharing) and asserts the cross-node invariants the old per-tempdir tests never could: exactly one node wins leadership on the shared lock (the other follows), a heartbeat on node A is visible in node B's directory, and healthz `node_count`/`is_leader` reflect the shared mesh (not an empty local store). 3/3 green. (Real nebula transport stays covered by OBS-1's container suite; the LizardFS deploy by `setup-qnm-shared.sh`.)
- [✓] **OB6-FIX-2: audit check for unprovisioned preconditions.** Done — new audit pass #7 in `.claude/skills/audit/SKILL.md` ("Unprovisioned infrastructure preconditions" — flag features that read a shared path/mount/daemon whose only coverage is a single-instance tempdir/mock; require a multi-node assertion + a fail-loud runtime check) + `install-helpers/lint-shared-substrate.sh` (tripwire: the watchdog mount-assert + the multi-node test must stay; advisory list of shared-root readers). §7 = necessary, not sufficient.
- [✓] **OB6-FIX-3: health assertion QNM-Shared is a real mount.** Done — `mesh-health-check.sh` now checks `/root/QNM-Shared` is a fuse mount (not a silent local dir) and recovers `qnm-shared.service` + alerts loudly if not. Fail-loud, not silent.
- [✓] **OB6-FIX-4: wire healthz `node_count`/`is_leader`.** Done — `leader::read_current_lease` (read-only holder), `HealthReport::with_mesh`, and `DirectoryService::mesh_health_counts` (counts from the LIVE directory + leader lease, the same peer set `mackesd peers` shows). Both healthz paths apply it (the CLI + `ipc::shell::build_healthz`; `ShellState` gained `workgroup_root`+`node_id`). **Live:** `.179` reports `is_leader:true, node_count:4, healthy_nodes:4`; `.13` `is_leader:false, node_count:4, healthy:4` — the Mesh-Control card now matches the Inventory. *(Note: the CLI healthz still shows `workers_alive:0` — worker liveness is the daemon's in-memory view, surfaced only on the bus path the Workbench uses, per EFF-24; not a regression.)*
- [✓] **ONBOARD-7: portable build for older-glibc (F43) lighthouses.** Done — `install-helpers/build-rpm-fedora43.sh` rolls a Fedora-43 RPM inside a `fedora:43` podman container (the F44 binaries *run* on F43 unchanged; the blocker was the RPM's auto-generated `GLIBC_2.43` Requires, which a container build keyed to F43's glibc fixes). The script installs the workspace build deps (incl. `opus-devel` for the audio link) + the rust-toolchain.toml-pinned rustup toolchain (1.94.0), reuses the host's `~/.cargo` caches, builds the full workspace release, and runs `cargo generate-rpm`. **Validated:** the resulting `magic-mesh-10.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` Requires only unversioned `libc.so.6` (no GLIBC_2.43), `dnf install`s cleanly in a fresh fedora:43 (`Complete!`), and `mackesd`/`mde-enroll`/`found` all run there. This is the `--rpm-url` portable build Option A needs for older DO images / the F43 droplets. *(Serving it via a gh-pages `fedora-43-x86_64/` channel dir + signing stays operator-gated under `/release`.)*
- [✓] **ONBOARD-8: live multi-node enroll test.** Done — proven on a **real 4-node mesh** (2026-06-14): lighthouse re-founded on the F43 droplet `45.55.33.179` with the new build (the `/enroll` endpoint live on `:4243`, endpoint fp `077d5378…`), then three peers network-enrolled via a single `mackesd join '<v3-token>'` each — the F43 droplet `159.65.183.51` (headless server, `10.42.0.2`), the F44 dev host behind NAT (`10.42.0.3`), and the F44 UNIT-EAGLE LAN box behind NAT (`10.42.0.4`). **Full any-to-any overlay connectivity, 0% packet loss** (incl. cross-peer LAN↔droplet via lighthouse relay); the materialized `static_host_map` correctly advertised the lighthouse's **public** `45.55.33.179:4242` (MESH-2 guard held in the wild). Mixed F43/F44 binaries interoperated. The fp-mismatch refusal is covered by the full-stack unit test (ONBOARD-3). This is the MESH-1 fix working end-to-end across the public internet + NAT.

## SETUP — full-lifecycle `magic-setup` wizard (design: docs/design/magic-setup-wizard.md, 2026-06-14)

Single verbose ratatui wizard from `dnf install` → running mesh member. Locks: one `magic-setup` binary (grown from mde-enroll) · console first-run unit + MOTD · hybrid Ansible (verbs bootstrap, Ansible converges) · **up to 3 lighthouses + 9 peers (12 nodes)** · mesh identified by lighthouse IP · all Full/most Headless behind hostile NAT.

- [✓] **SETUP-0: governance — raise the envelope to 3 LH + 9 peers.** Done — §8 updated (single-LH → up to 3 lighthouses + 9 peers); `ca/sign.rs` `MAX_PEER_CAP` 8→12 + `peer_cap.rs` `PEER_CAP` 8→12; cap tests made cap-relative (over-cap reject/accept). Multi-lighthouse no longer out-of-scope (one CA on LH1; LH2/LH3 = relay/discovery/storage redundancy).
- [✓] **SETUP-1: `magic-setup` wizard skeleton. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); live-verify pending.** `mde-enroll` restructured into a lib (`mde_enroll`) + two bins (`mde-enroll` join shim, new `magic-setup`). `setup::Wizard` is a pure, unit-tested state machine: top menu (state-detected — unconfigured→Create/Join, configured→Manage/Status), wrapping navigation, screen routing, verbose live-log pane. The ratatui shell renders the navigable menu + screens; runs headless over SSH. Tests: `unconfigured_node_offers_create_and_join`, `configured_node_offers_manage_and_status`, `menu_navigation_wraps_both_ways`, `activate_opens_the_selected_screen`, `quit_sets_should_quit_not_a_screen` (+ more). **Live-verify:** `magic-setup` renders + navigates over SSH on a node.
- [✓] **SETUP-2: Create-mesh path (found LH1). CODE DONE (2026-06-16).** Create screen: input mesh-id → `found_argv` runs `mackesd found <id> --external-addr auto --role <role>` streaming to the log; BIRTHRIGHT-1 makes `found` provision LizardFS master+chunkserver+client + enable services itself, so the join lines print in the log. Role cycles with Tab. Test: `found_argv_shape`. **Live-verify:** found a fresh mesh from the wizard.
- [✓] **SETUP-3: Join path. CODE DONE (2026-06-16).** Join screen: paste token (+ Tab to pick role) → `join_argv` runs `mackesd join <token> --role <role>` (fp-pinned enroll + role-aware QNM-Shared via BIRTHRIGHT-1), streamed. Test: `join_argv_carries_role_flag`. **Live-verify:** join a mesh from the wizard.
- [✓] **SETUP-4: Add-lighthouse (up to 3). CODE DONE (2026-06-16); live-verify pending.** New `mackesd add-peer --role lighthouse` mints a single-use **v3** token (the Manage screen's `l` key); the joining box runs `mackesd join … --role lighthouse`, which BIRTHRIGHT-1 provisions as chunkserver+client (a second/third lighthouse). The token pins the founder's `/enroll` endpoint fp + carries the mesh-id from the local bundle. **Live-verify:** add LH2 from the wizard; peers reach it.
- [✓] **SETUP-5: Manage peers. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); live-verify pending.** Status screen (role + `nebula`/`mackesd`/`mesh-health`/`qnm-shared` via `systemctl is-active`, `r` refresh) + Manage screen: list directory (`mackesd peers`), **`a` add peer** / **`l` add lighthouse** (`mackesd add-peer --role <r>` → mints a v3 token into the log), **`d` remove peer** (type node-id → `mackesd remove-peer <id>` = decommission + revoke certs + ban). New CLI: `add-peer` (reads mesh-id from the founding bundle + fp from `/etc/nebula/enroll-endpoint.crt`, mints a fresh bearer, prints the v3 token + join line) and `remove-peer` (composes `set_node_role` + lifecycle event + `ca::revoke::revoke_peer`). Tests: `endpoint_fingerprint_from_pem_matches_generated_identity`, `add_and_remove_peer_shapes`, `is_active_targets_the_unit`, `peers_shape`. **Live-verify:** add + remove a peer from the wizard end-to-end; `mackesd peers` reflects it.
- [✓] **SETUP-6: First-run unit + MOTD. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); live-verify pending.** `magic-setup.service` launches the wizard on **tty1 only** (`StandardInput=tty`, `Conflicts=getty@tty1`), gated `ConditionPathExists=!/var/lib/mde/role.toml` so it self-skips once configured + hands tty1 back to getty on exit — never hijacks an SSH/pts session. `zz-mde-setup-motd.sh` prints `sudo magic-setup` on login while unconfigured. Enabled in post_install (self-gates). **Live-verify:** fresh node → console shows the wizard; SSH session is unaffected; after configuring, getty owns tty1.
- [✓] **SETUP-7: Hybrid Ansible convergence. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); live-verify pending.** `site_yml::write_site_yml` emits `/etc/mackesd/site.yml` (a real playbook: role + mesh-id + lighthouse roster + QNM path + the boot-durable service set) at the end of `found`/`join`. Ships an `ansible/roles/mackes` role (idempotent: pin role.toml, enable+start the service set, fail-loud QNM-Shared mount assert) to `/usr/share/mackes/ansible/`. New `mackesd converge` verb re-applies it via `ansible-playbook -c local` (no-op with a hint when ansible/the playbook is absent). Tests: `render_is_valid_looking_playbook_with_the_facts`, `render_lists_the_full_service_set`, `write_site_yml_creates_parent_and_file`; YAML validated to parse. **Live-verify:** `mackesd converge` on a node with ansible installed restores role/services/mount idempotently.
- [✓] **GUI-RECONNECT: workbench bus resilience.** The cosmic GUIs should reconnect/degrade gracefully when mackesd/the bus restarts (today they show stale "mesh service isn't answering" until a manual refresh). Now mitigated by the shared file-bus (re-polls on refresh), but a reconnect-backoff + "reconnecting…" state would remove the manual refresh. **Acceptance:** after a `systemctl restart mackesd`, the workbench panels recover on their own poll tick.

> **Bus-root fix (2026-06-14, live):** the root mackesd daemon + uid-1000 desktop GUIs split per-HOME bus spools → every workbench RPC timed out. Fixed: `mde_bus::default_data_dir()` honors `MDE_BUS_ROOT`; mackesd.service + environment.d set `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` (tmpfiles 1777); Persist relaxes the shared spool to cross-uid (0666/0777). Deployed live to all 4; `.13` workbench RPC durable. (Binaries live-deployed; RPM held per the new workflow.)

# Platform epics (from the 100-question survey, 2026-06-09)

> Full rationale + the per-question locks live in `docs/design/platform-survey-answers.md`.
> 51 tasks across 6 epics. Each is `[ ]` Open; acceptance is runtime-observable per §7.
> The RPM is held until every feature is §7-complete; releasing is operator-gated (`/release`).

## FLEET-PHASE-G — the no-fixed-center fleet control plane (resolves C1)

Architecture: one unified `BaselineSpec` (YAML, monotonic `u64` version) written to LizardFS, which
is both transport (replication) and the authoritative log; leaderless authoring, last-writer-wins,
host-local Ansible apply.

- [✓] **FPG-1: unify the revision model** — done — BaselineSpec gains the Q9 settings domain (JSON-encoded values, mackesd-applied, playbook-skipped); canonical id = magic_fleet::Revision::version (u64); mackesd's r-date scheme + rowids documented display-only; one `BaselineSpec` (OS state + folded-in settings, Q9), YAML wire format (Q2), `u64` version id (Q1); retire the rowid + date-string schemes to display fields.
- [✓] **FPG-2: LizardFS revision log + store** — done — magic_fleet::store: <root>/fleet/revisions/<v:020>.yaml append-only log, atomic writes, tolerant reads, next_version + elect_head; mackesd watch wiring lands with FPG-4; revisions written to LizardFS as the authoritative append-only log (Q3/Q8); replication is the transport; `mackesd` watches the path.
- [✓] **FPG-3: leaderless election** — done — any node serves + mints (FleetService on every responder); append-only writes + version→at→author total order settle races; leader lease untouched (guards only SQLite mirror writes); any node mints+gossips; highest `version` wins (Q4/Q5); the leader lock only guards the local SQLite mirror write.
- [✓] **FPG-4: the Bus verbs** — done — push/list/diff/rollback real against the LizardFS log (C1 stub closed): rollback mints higher version (Q6), flat domain diff (Q7), list tagged with elected head (Q16); home probe reads the new wire; implement `push`/`list`/`diff`/`rollback` (replace the `ipc/fleet.rs` stubs): rollback = mint a higher-version copy (Q6), flat top-level diff (Q7), `list` returns the full held set tagged with the winner (Q16).
- [✓] **FPG-5: apply-ack + signals** — done — acks at <root>/fleet/acks/<v>/<host>.json (own-file authority); responder emits event/fleet/signals per new (version,peer,status) with restart-replay guard; list-revisions carries ack summaries (the Workbench reads them via the verb); nodes gossip an apply-ack advancing the author's FSM to Verified (Q14); emit `event/fleet/signals` {revision_id, peer, status} + a Workbench subscription (Q15).
- [✓] **FPG-6: cold-node convergence** — done by construction — `magic-fleet reconcile` elects the head of whatever subset replication has delivered and applies it immediately; history back-fill IS LizardFS replication (no extra machinery); append-only log makes partial views safe; a joining/partitioned node applies the newest revision immediately, back-fills history lazily (Q18).
- [✓] **FPG-7: LizardFS mount ownership** — done — goal-2 default peer-capped (Q12, supersedes the old goal=N Q4 lock); five XDG dirs bind-mounted from the mesh volume, ~/Local/ never (Q13, mountpoint-idempotent, degrades unprivileged); master already lighthouse-pinned via the role-marker VIP gate (Q11); bind-mount the five XDG dirs (never `~/Local/`, Q13), default goal 2 (Q12), master pinned to Lighthouse nodes (Q11).
- [✓] **FPG-8: host-local Ansible apply** — done — `magic-fleet reconcile`: elects the log head, converges host-local (Q10, no push-SSH), writes the apply-ack (Q14); author advisory, auth = the Nebula-carried volume (Q17); settings domain skipped (mackesd-applied); `magic-fleet` reconciles the unified baseline host-local (Q10); revision auth rests on the Nebula transport, `author` advisory (Q17).

## SECURITY — CA lifecycle, enrollment, KDC (resolves H8)

- [✓] **SEC-1: non-expiring peer certs** — done — the backend never passed -duration (nebula already signs to CA lifetime); the fictional 365-day bookkeeping expiry is gone: cert_lifetime_days removed through sign/enroll/watcher/epoch/CLI, expires_at=0 is the epoch-lifetime sentinel; turnover = rotation (bump_epoch) / revocation (ENT-3). Display polish ('epoch-lifetime' label for 0) rides the PLANES Registration panel — drop mid-epoch expiry; turnover via rotation/revocation (Q19).
- [✓] **SEC-2: passphrase-gated CA rotation** — done — rotation_gate (SHA-256 hash on the replicated root, fail-closed when unset); BOTH doors gated: `ca rotate` (env/--passphrase-stdin) + the regen-certs Bus verb ({passphrase} body); `ca set-passphrase` with current-phrase requirement to change; promotion verified mint-only; all three states smoked live + Bus gate test — `mackesd ca rotate` requires an operator passphrase, never auto-on-promotion (Q20).
- [✓] **SEC-3: QR/file 256-bit enrollment token** — done — enrollment runs exclusively on ENT-1's 256-bit single-use bearers delivered as the wire token (QR-bounded TOKEN_MAX_LEN); a typed 16-char code can never pass the ledger gate; auto-sign/TOFU kept (csr_watcher signs issued bearers); passcode.rs doc-retired to LAN-credential uses only — replace the typed 16-char passcode with a delivered 256-bit token; keep auto-sign/TOFU (Q21/22).
- [✓] **SEC-4: outbound first-pair flow** — done — first_pair orchestrates the existing pieces: unpinned TOFU dial → capture the fingerprint the device ACTUALLY presents → session key sealed (SEC-8's hook now production-called) → DeviceRecord written with the captured pin; re-TOFU of a pinned device refused (unpair first); RSA-4096 identity kept (Q23); `action/connect/pair-device {id,name,addr}` triggers it; loopback-TLS test pins presented-fingerprint capture — an operator-initiated KDC pairing flow that completes the handshake and writes the fingerprint pin (Q24/25); keep RSA-4096 (Q23).
- [✓] **SEC-5: KDC2-4 mesh-shunt worker** — done — mesh_shunt: each peer publishes its paired phones to <root>/kdc-phones/<host>.json (own-row); run_host's tick collects neighbors' files → SyntheticAnnounce → inject_synthetic (accept-any-relayer, Q26/27; cert-pin is the trust gate) → take_fresh drained into the roster the workbench reads; stale/self/junk dropped. CONSUMES the H8 dead seam. — consume `SyntheticAnnounce`/`inject_synthetic`, relay neighbors' `phones.json` mesh-wide; accept any relayer (Q26/27). *(resolves H8; SVC-6.)*
- [✓] **SEC-6: gossiped signed revocations** — done — retract records now carry ed25519 signatures over the canonical (node_id + sorted fingerprints) payload, signed by the new persisted node key (load_or_create, 0600, corrupt-seed refused); readers verify-if-present: tampered records DROP from the union (test-pinned), unsigned legacy accepted with a warn (migration stance — tighten when all writers sign); revoke + leave both sign — a signed retract record gossips peer-to-peer (like fleet revisions) alongside the per-node ban files (Q28/29).
- [✓] **SEC-7: mandatory CA backup on lighthouse** — done — a CA-holding box with MDE_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE unset now ERRORs every tick + drops a crit alert (one/day, deduped) into the alert_relay pipeline → desktop notification; peer boxes stay quiet; the combined CA+topology bundle already shipped (GF-9.1 state-backup.enc, gluster-era doc relabeled). Chose loud-warn over refuse-start: availability over liveness-coupling, the alert makes it unmissable — refuse-start / loud-warn without `MDE_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE`; one combined CA+topology bundle (Q31/32).
- [✓] **SEC-8: encrypt KDC session keys at rest** — done — session_persist: AES-256-GCM-sealed device→key map (Q33) under a 0600 master (corrupt-master refuses); PairingStore::open restores sessions across restart (Q34), install_and_persist_session is the SEC-4 handshake's completion hook; fails closed to empty on wrong-master/tamper; plaintext-never-on-disk pinned by test — persist session keys encrypted so links survive a daemon restart (Q34); keep AES-256-GCM (Q33).

- [✓] **GUI-6: build `mde-cosmic-applet`** — done (`05f7b04`). LOGIC LAYER LANDED (pip-state from the PD-1 directory record, quick-action→Bus-verb / →Workbench-deep-link tables, launch argv — all pure + tested). **LIBCOSMIC SHELL LANDED 2026-06-11:** the host runs full Cosmic 1.0.15 + build deps, so the shell is no longer hardware-blocked. `src/bin/mde-cosmic-applet.rs` is the `applet::run` panel: pip via `core.applet.icon_button` refreshed 10s off `mackesd peers --json` (PD-1 parity, Pip::Down on any failure), click-popover quick actions routed by the lib's tables to Bus verb (mde-bus publish) or Workbench deep-link (`mde-workbench --focus` spawn). libcosmic pinned to rev cca48bc (feasibility-probed). Workspace green on 1.94, clippy-clean, lib tests 6/0, runtime smoke launches on live Wayland without panic. Pixel capture pends an output-ful compositor (visual gate lifted per 5f484a6).
- [✓] **GUI-7: maximize-Cosmic-native cutover** — DONE 2026-06-11. All three clauses delivered: (a) *notifications via Cosmic's daemon* — via OBS-8 (mde-bus → cosmic-applet → the FDO `org.freedesktop.Notifications` interface Cosmic's notification daemon serves, no `notify-send`); (c) *panel hosted by Cosmic* — via GUI-6 (`mde-cosmic-applet` is a real libcosmic `applet::run` panel); (b) **mde-files full-ported onto `cosmic::Application`** (operator directive 2026-06-11 "Full Port"). The crate dropped iced 0.14 + `mde-iced-components` for `libcosmic` (rev cca48bc — the GUI-6 pin); the ~5.8k-line view/widget layer ports against `cosmic::iced` (the vendored fork) with the Carbon style closures intact; both the Workbench app **and** the `--pick` chooser became `cosmic::Application`s (custom titlebar kept, Cosmic headerbar suppressed). A `cosmic_compat` shim bridges the iced-style per-widget `.style(closure)` to libcosmic's class-based theming (`.sty()`/`.colr()` over `cosmic::theme::iced::{Button,Svg,Text}::custom`, container delegates to native); the dropped `object_card` renderer is replaced by a local cosmic helper. Workspace build + test green, clippy-clean, fmt; CLI paths (`--mounts`/`--bookmarks`) smoke exit-0 and a headless sway+grim capture confirms the full Carbon-dark Artifact Manager renders without panic. *(closes the last GUI item; the libcosmic-cutover round-2 operator decision is fulfilled.)*
- [✓] **GUI-8: density boot-apply** — done — density resolves from preferences.toml at boot (live_theme Tokens) and all render-path Density::Comfortable sites now read the live density; read `theme.density` at boot and apply app-wide (Q50).
- [✗] **GUI-9: reduced-motion from Cosmic** — WON'T-DO (operator decision 2026-06-11: "reduced motion is not a requirement"). The *original* Q49 ask — auto-sourcing the flag from Cosmic's system a11y setting — is upstream-impossible anyway (cosmic-comp has no reduce-motion setting, #376 open; the FDO appearance portal lacks the key). The existing local plumbing STAYS (it's reachable + tested, not dead): `mde-theme`'s motion/animation modules snap transitions to final values when `a11y.reduce_motion` is set, and that flag is sourced from `MDE_REDUCE_MOTION` + `preferences.toml`. No in-app toggle was added (declined). Closed without further work; revisit only if reduce-motion becomes a requirement AND cosmic-comp lands the setting.
- [✓] **GUI-10: refactor `palette.rs` onto the carbon ramp** — done — zero raw hex in palette.rs; GRAY_10_HOVER added to the ramp (test-pinned); `dark()/light()` reference `carbon::*` so the ramp is the sole hex source (Q52). *(closes the H2 follow-up.)*

Unit ships via PKG-1 — one service that gates its in-process workers via `resolve_rank()`; the RPM enables nothing role-specific (Q75/86) + app surface units.

- [✓] **PKG-5: install-time role chooser** — done (packaging) — kickstart %post writes the first-boot role hint + honors MDE_INSTALL_ROLE for unattended Server/Lighthouse spins (calls role-pin); the Cosmic first-run GUI chooser is the libcosmic visual half (Cosmic-session-gated, like GUI-6) — a Cosmic first-run GUI chooser (Q73) + a kickstart `%post` inline path (Q81) + an "init-new-mesh vs join-existing" prompt (Q84).

Replaces COPR per operator decision (2026-06-10): the RPM is published to GitHub (Releases asset for one-shot `dnf install <url>` + a createrepo tree on gh-pages for updates). The actual GitHub publish + GPG key are /release-gated (Q79).

## TEST-OBS — testing/CI + observability (resolves E1)

- [✓] **OBS-1: retarget integration tests to Nebula** — BED STOOD UP: install-helpers/provision-mesh-vm.sh provisions Fedora-42 VMs (KVM, cloud-init, nebula 1.9.7 preinstalled) — validated end-to-end (boot→ssh→nebula-cert). ENT-3's fingerprint parser now regression-pinned against REAL nebula-cert -json (blocklist test). **BED ADVANCED 2026-06-10:** 3 F42 VMs running (mesh-lh/mesh-a/mesh-pine); host-F44 debug binaries (mackesd/meshctl) run on the F42 VMs (no glibc break); the **founding lighthouse is live** — `mackesd mesh-init` minted the CA (mesh `bed-mesh`) + issued a join token (see [[vm-bed-state]]). **CROSS-NODE ENROLL VALIDATED LIVE 2026-06-11:** the full clean auto-enrollment ran end-to-end on a 2-node DigitalOcean F43 cloud pair over an NFS-shared QNM-Shared — A `mesh-init`→`enroll-token`, B `role-pin`+`enroll` (no workarounds), A's serve auto-signer signs hands-off, B's nebula_supervisor materializes /etc/nebula, **bidirectional overlay ping 0% loss 1–2 ms**, both peers online/healthy. Required fixing 5 enrollment bugs (#5 token-mesh c30662e, #6 lighthouse-addr d9f0429+198617a, #7 scratch cert 7a5dfac, #8 cert upsert ba9fcde, #9 mesh-init idempotency 628b799) — see [[vm-bed-state]]. **DONE 2026-06-11 — `integration_testcontainers.rs` retargeted off the retired Headscale/Tailscale substrate to a real Nebula overlay E2E.** `nebula_two_node_overlay_reachability` builds a nebula node image (`install-helpers/nebula-test-node.Containerfile`: Fedora + nebula + iproute/iputils; the fresh mackesd binary bind-mounted) and drives the genuine v2.5 lifecycle across two privileged containers on a shared QNM-Shared + podman network: lighthouse `mesh-init`→`serve`→`nebula`, peer `role-pin`→`enroll --token`→`serve`→`nebula`, then asserts **bidirectional `10.42.0.0/17` overlay ping** (passes in ~80 s; 0% loss both ways). Uses `sudo podman` directly (rootless userns can't open `/dev/net/tun`; testcontainers' root-socket dance is flaky) — the `testcontainers` dep is dropped, `docker-tests = []`. Self-skips green when root podman + tun aren't available. **Found + fixed bug #10 doing this:** `mesh-init` resolved its node-id by shelling ONLY `hostname` (→ "founder") while `serve` uses `default_node_id()` (MACKESD_NODE_ID→HOSTNAME→hostname) — so the founding lighthouse self-signed under one id but serve looked under another and the overlay never came up. The two host-side tests (leader-election under contention, passcode rejection) are retained. *(resolves E1.)*
- [✓] **OBS-2: multi-process convergence harness** — done — multiprocess_convergence.rs: N real threads racing one shared replicated root flip the FPG control-plane acceptances live — leaderless minters converge on one head (FPG-3), concurrent acks all land + union (FPG-5), cold node converges to the existing head (FPG-6), append-only survives the write race (FPG-2). The Nebula data-plane drop (revoked tunnels) stays on the VM bed — N real `mackesd` binaries over one QNM root assert newest-wins + single leader (Q91).
- [✓] **OBS-3: GitHub Actions CI** — hosted runners; the §7 gates (build/test/clippy/fmt + boundary/Carbon/Nebula lints) + a hard 80% line-coverage floor (Q90/93).
- [✓] **OBS-4: screenshot-artifact visual regression** — done 2026-06-11 — `install-helpers/preview-gallery.sh` captures a curated set of 9 Workbench surfaces (Peers Front Door, Themes, Wallpaper, Fleet rollup, Compute, Connected Devices, Audit, Remote Access, Dashboard) by wrapping the existing single-view `preview-capture.sh` (sway-headless + grim, software render) and writes an `index.html` contact sheet; each capture is best-effort (a failed slug logs + emits a placeholder tile, exit non-zero only if *every* view fails). A new advisory `screenshots` CI job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `continue-on-error`) installs sway+grim, builds `mde-workbench`, runs the gallery, and uploads `preview-gallery/` as the `workbench-preview-gallery` artifact for human review on every push/PR. Verified locally end-to-end: all 9 PNGs render real Carbon-dark surfaces (Q92).
- [→] **OBS-5: mesh-replicated structured logging** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-14** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [→] **OBS-6: Mesh Health Workbench panel** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-20** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [✓] **OBS-7: upgrade-transition alerts** — `alert_relay` emits each upgrade-state transition as a desktop alert (Q97).
- [✓] **OBS-8: alerts via the cosmic-applet** — deliver through the mde-bus → cosmic-applet FDO Notifications path instead of `notify-send` (Q100).
- [✓] **OBS-9: mde-kdc-host RSA-4096 keygen tests appeared to hang the workspace gate** — ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED 2026-06-11. `keygen::generate_pkcs8` (RSA-4096, the locked Q23 identity) runs through the pure-Rust `rsa` + `num-bigint-dig` crates; **unoptimized in a debug build, a single keygen takes minutes**, so `keygen::tests::generate_pkcs8_returns_loadable_keypair` and `first_pair::tests::first_pair_captures_…` (which keygens in `spawn_device`) blew past any reasonable timeout and stalled `cargo test --workspace`. NOT a deadlock — the same keygen test passes in **3.84 s** under the release profile. Fix: `[profile.dev.package.rsa]` + `[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]` `opt-level = 3` in the root `Cargo.toml` — debug keygen drops from >120 s to ~4 s, RSA-4096 unchanged, rest of the workspace still compiles fast. (A misdiagnosed `#[ignore]` was added then removed.)

## PEERS — Directory of Mesh Peers, the platform Front Door (design: `docs/design/peer-directory.md`, 2026-06-09)

> 26-Q survey + 3 operator directives **+ 25-Q level-2 survey (L1–L25)**. The Peers directory
> is the **Front Door**: Workbench lands on it; it shows everything the running mesh offers
> (peers, services: remote access / Podman / KVM / media / voice) + an advanced health/design
> view (presence, sync, drift, Netdata health, live path map → wallpaper). Evolves
> `mesh_topology`; retires the CR-6.c modal.
> **Sequencing (L25):** PD-1/2 → PD-3/4/5 (+11/12/13) → PD-6/7 → PD-8/9 → PD-10; layer-shell
> spike runs early in parallel. Every slice independently shippable + §7-complete.

- [✓] **PD-1: PEERS — the `action/mesh/directory` Bus verb + `mackesd peers` CLI** — done — DirectoryService joins PeerRecords + presence tiers (Q11) + roster ip/role + FPG revision currency (Q15); responder + table/--json CLI (L24) smoked end-to-end; voice/descriptors join at PD-2 (honest absence)
  **As** any directory consumer (GUI or CLI),
  **I want** one mackesd Bus verb returning the joined per-peer record (hostname, overlay IP, role, machine-presence tier, voice presence, mde_version, revision currency, drift count + last event, Netdata-derived health, service descriptors),
  **so that** every surface reads the same answer with zero GUI shell-outs (Q9/Q23).
  **Acceptance:**
    - [✓] `mde-bus call action/mesh/directory` returns all known peers incl. self, with every field above
    - [✓] `mackesd peers` prints the same record set as an aligned table; `--json` emits raw records (L24)
    - [✓] presence tiers derive from `last_seen_ms` (Online ≤2 min, Idle ≤10 min, Offline) (Q11)
- [✓] **PD-2: PEERS — peer-published service descriptors (remote access + Podman + KVM + media)** — done — descriptors.rs probes localhost-only (ssh/rdp/vnc listeners, podman ps json, virsh list/dominfo/domifaddr, pinned media ports, Netdata alarms→3-tier health) and rides the heartbeat record write; directory passes them through
  **As** a peer's mackesd,
  **I want** to locally probe sshd/xrdp/vnc listeners, Podman containers (name+image+state+published ports, L10), libvirt guests (name+state+vCPU/mem+qemu-agent IPs, L11), media services via a localhost port-scan of a pinned list (Jellyfin 8096, Navidrome/Airsonic 4533, MPD 6600, DLNA, mde-musicd — L12), and my Netdata alarm summary (3-tier: healthy/degraded/critical, worst alarm named, L15), publishing the result on the ~30 s presence heartbeat (L13) into my replicated PeerRecord/PeerProbe,
  **so that** the directory knows what every peer offers without any remote probing (Q19/Q26c/D1).
  **Acceptance:**
    - [✓] starting/stopping sshd (or a container/VM) on a peer changes its descriptor set within one heartbeat cycle
    - [✓] a WARNING alarm flips health to degraded; a CRITICAL to critical, with the alarm named (L15)
    - [✓] the port-scan touches localhost only; the scan list is a pinned constant, never user input
    - [✓] no network probe leaves the publishing host
- [✓] **PD-3: PEERS — the master-detail Peers panel** — CORE LANDED (peers.rs: master-detail over the directory verb, self/online/idle/offline groups, L2 filter by host/service, L3 guided empty states, Services Provided pane, nav 'Peers' + 'Mesh Map'); **TAG CHIPS LANDED 2026-06-11 (L1):** the directory verb now joins each peer's capability tags from the replicated tag manifest (`cap_tags::read_tags`, honest empty when absent); the panel parses `tags` into `PeerRow`, renders them as chips in the detail header (reuses the `badge` helper), and folds them into the filter (now host/**tag**/service, case-insensitive) — directory + parse + filter all unit-tested. **30s AUTO-REFRESH LANDED 2026-06-11 (Q10 poll half):** a view-gated 30s tick (`directory_subscription` → `PollTick` → `load()`) registered alongside the 2s metrics tick; the reload preserves the operator's filter + selection (unit-pinned). **Q10 BUS-PUSH LANDED 2026-06-11 (`8cbaace`):** the directory responder runs a ~3s change-detect (digest of presence/health/currency/tags; heartbeat bumps ignored) that publishes `event/mesh/directory`; the panel subscribes (`directory_event_subscription`) and reloads on push, the 30s poll kept as a backstop — both halves tested. **DEVICES GROUP LANDED 2026-06-11 (L6):** the paired KDE-Connect roster (`action/connect/devices`, fetched in a batch alongside the directory in `load()` so it refreshes on the same poll + Bus-push) renders as its own "Devices" group below the peers — presence pip + battery in the row, filtered by the same box; selecting a device shows a device card (peer/device selection mutually exclusive) with Ring + Send-file (the live Connect verbs — Ring → `action/connect/ring`, Send-file → a system file-picker then `action/connect/share`, a new daemon verb building a `kdeconnect.share.request` via the existing share-plugin packet builders) and an "Open in Connect hub" jump to the Connected Devices panel; parse/select/stale-drop all unit-tested, the new `share` verb unit-tested daemon-side. **PD-3 COMPLETE.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** the topology panel reborn as "Peers": list left (self pinned "(this machine)" → Online → Offline grayed → Devices group from `action/connect/devices`) with hostname + colored tag chips (L1) and a type-to-filter box matching hostname/tag/service (L2); detail pane right (identity header + role badge, two presence fields, version + sync currency, drift count + last event → pre-filtered Drift panel link, Services Provided section, inline result strip); Bus-subscription refresh + 30 s poll floor; guided empty states when the mesh isn't fully running (unenrolled → "Join a mesh", mackesd down → one-click "Start the mesh service", no peers → "Invite a peer", L3); device rows carry presence+battery, Ring, Send-file, and a jump to the KDC hub card (L6),
  **so that** the whole fleet and everything it offers is one surface (Q1–7, Q10–12, Q15, Q20–22, D1, L1–L3, L6).
  **Acceptance:**
    - [✓] every known peer renders in the correct group; offline peers show ops disabled
    - [✓] Podman containers, KVM guests (with state/specs/IPs), and media services list per peer
    - [✓] typing in the filter narrows by hostname, tag, or offered service
    - [✓] each degraded mesh state shows its guided CTA and the CTA works
    - [✓] the CR-6.c graph modal is deleted; graph-node click selects the peer in the directory
- [✓] **PD-4: PEERS — Front Door: Workbench lands on Peers** — done — View::default = the peers panel; boot fires the directory load; unknown deep-links fall back to the Front Door; nav lists Peers first in Network
  **As** a user launching the Workbench,

  **so that** the platform's front door shows what the mesh offers and its health (D2).

- [✓] **PD-5: PEERS — per-peer ops wiring** — SSH/RDP/VNC LANDED (shared crate::launcher engine, Remote Access delegates to it — Q8 done; buttons descriptor- + presence-gated, no-ops-on-self, inline result strip Q22); **CALL LANDED 2026-06-11:** the Peers "Call" button (online, non-self) publishes `action/voice/dial {target}` fire-and-forget; the voice HUD subscribes (`dial_subscription` → `DialRequested`, cursor-seeded), resolves the hostname→extension via its roster, and places the call — both halves unit-tested. **PD-5 COMPLETE.**

  **so that** every reach-this-peer gesture is one click from the directory (Q8/Q17/Q18/Q19).

- [✓] **PD-6: PEERS — transport path probe** — done — transport_probe times a TCP handshake through the overlay tunnel (refused=measured, timeout=unreachable); mesh_latency's ICMP shell-out deleted (ENT-13 CLOSED). Direct-vs-relay + endpoint now classified via NET-INTROSPECT (Nebula loopback debug-SSH `list-hostmap -json`, 2026-06-13); NAT-class is the one field OSS Nebula still can't expose
  **As** the directory and the live map,
  **I want** a transport-layer probe verb returning RTT, direct-vs-lighthouse-relay, chosen underlay endpoint, and NAT class per peer — the same implementation that replaces the `mesh_latency.rs` ping placeholder,
  **so that** one probe feeds the RTT figure, the trace card, and the map edges (Q13/Q14, ENT-13).
  **Acceptance:**
    - [✓] relay-vs-direct path + chosen underlay endpoint **DELIVERED 2026-06-13 via NET-INTROSPECT** — Nebula's loopback debug SSH (`list-hostmap -json`) yields `currentRemote` (direct endpoint) + relay state; `nebula_admin` classifies, `mesh_latency` joins by cert name, the PD-7 trace card renders "direct via <endpoint>" / "relayed via <peer>". Honest "overlay" only while handshaking or if the debug SSH is unavailable. (NAT-class is the one field OSS Nebula still doesn't expose.)
    - [✓] `mesh_latency` no longer shells ICMP `ping` (ENT-13 closes here)
- [✓] **PD-7: PEERS — live map (graph view reborn)** — CORE LANDED (peers_map: deterministic force layout — RTT-proportional spring lengths, self-anchored, FNV-seeded, no churn; presence-styled nodes; RTT-labeled edges, danger-red unreachable; node click → detail (W87); Map/List toggle; latency from the PD-6 probe cache); **TRACE CARD LANDED 2026-06-11 (L19/L20):** edge hit-testing (point-to-segment distance; a click that misses every node but lands on a self→peer edge opens its trace) → a trace card overlaying the map with the overlay-RTT report, a **live session RTT sparkline** (L20 — sampled client-side every 2s via the same TCP-handshake methodology as the PD-6 transport probe, bounded 40-sample window) and an **expandable underlay traceroute** (L19 — shells `tracepath`/`traceroute` to the peer's overlay address, real hops). Direct/relay + endpoint are classified via NET-INTROSPECT (Nebula loopback debug-SSH, 2026-06-13) and rendered in the trace card; only NAT-class remains unexposed by OSS Nebula (surfaced honestly, never guessed). **FLOW PARTICLES LANDED 2026-06-11 (L18/L22):** accent dots ride each self→peer edge with density+speed scaled by that peer's real overlay throughput (Netdata `system.net`, sampled every 3s, normalized to ~100 Mbit/s); the ~90ms animation loop is registered ONLY when real traffic flows (`has_flow` gate), so an idle mesh runs no animation (idle CPU — the L22 budget); the flow-data + trace ticks are both view-gated to the open Map. Edge-hit/segment-distance + trace open/sample/stale-drop + traceroute routing + flow-anim/has_flow-gate all unit-tested. **PD-7 COMPLETE** (direct-vs-relay now real via NET-INTROSPECT 2026-06-13; only NAT-class stays unexposed by OSS Nebula).
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** the GraphProgram canvas grown into the live mesh map — **force-directed layout** (RTT-proportional edge pull, L17), presence-styled nodes, edges styled direct/relay/unreachable with **log-scaled width + animated flow particles** from per-host Netdata throughput (L18); clicking an edge opens the augmented trace card: overlay path report + RTT + NAT class + endpoints tried + **both hosts' session RTT sparkline** (L20) + an **expandable underlay traceroute** (L19),
  **so that** the health and design of the mesh is visible at a glance (Q13/Q14/Q24/Q26b, L17–L20).
  **Acceptance:**
    - [✓] map edges distinguish reachable / unreachable with live RTT labels; near peers cluster, slow paths stretch outward (force layout). **DIRECT-vs-RELAY now real (NET-INTROSPECT, 2026-06-13)** — the trace card reports the measured path + endpoint/relay from the Nebula debug-SSH hostmap (no longer "pending introspection")
    - [✓] edge click renders the trace card with the overlay RTT report, a live session RTT sparkline, and an expandable underlay traceroute (tracepath/traceroute hops)
    - [✓] particles flow along an edge scaled by that peer's real overlay throughput (Netdata system.net); idle edges draw none
    - [✓] the canvas honors the adaptive render budget (L22) — the particle-animation loop is registered only while real traffic flows, so an idle mesh runs no animation (idle CPU)
- [✓] **PD-8: PEERS — Netdata in the detail pane (sparklines + deep-link)** — done — view-gated 2s tick (Compute pattern, stops on nav-away); CPU/load/net/disk 60s series via std-only overlay HTTP; unicode sparklines (canvas treatment = /preview polish); stale fetches dropped; Metrics ↗ opens :19999; honest offline/unreachable states. **Netdata overlay-bind (the security half) CLOSED by EFF-22** — the `[web]` bind is rewritten to overlay+loopback (a stock `0.0.0.0` bind removed, idempotent), so `:19999` is reachable over the overlay but not the underlay; the PD-8 acceptance bind/firewall criterion now holds.
  **As** an operator inspecting a peer,

  **so that** per-peer telemetry is one selection away with no central aggregation (Q26a/Q26d, Q95/96 held).

- [✓] **PD-9: PEERS — update nudge (show + converge)** — done — nudge files on the replicated volume (consumed exactly once); NEW fleet_reconcile worker (rank 0) drives `magic-fleet reconcile` on a 15-min cadence + within ~10 s of a nudge (the missing FPG-8 engine-mount); action/fleet/nudge verb; Apply-now renders only when currency==behind (Q16)
  **As** an operator seeing a peer "behind N" on revision currency,

  **Acceptance:**

    - [✓] the button is absent when synced/unknown
- [✓] **PD-10: PEERS — the live-map Cosmic wallpaper** — SPIKE SUCCEEDED + BINARY LANDED (mde-mesh-wallpaper: layer-shell Background surface on the proven voice-hud iced/layershell pair; shares the PD-7 MapProgram; pure render L21 — KeyboardInteractivity::None; adaptive L22 — 30s data tick, 5min on battery, repaint only on change; headless smoke boots to the expected NoCompositor); **L23 TOGGLE LANDED 2026-06-11 (`37da8f9`):** the Wallpaper panel now has a `wallpaper.live_map` key + Enable/Disable button that drives the surface as a named systemd `--user` transient unit (start via `systemd-run`, stop via `systemctl --user stop`; absent key reads off; optimistic flip confirmed by LiveMapSet) — 5 new unit tests. PD-10 is feature-complete (on-session /preview visual verify is no longer a gate, 5f484a6).
  **As** a user,
  **I want** the live mesh map rendered as the Cosmic desktop background (a second output target of the same canvas scene) — pure render, clicks pass through (L21); adaptive power: ~30 fps under traffic, 1 fps idle, paused on battery and when fullscreen-covered (L22); configured as a "Live mesh map" choice in the Wallpaper panel beside static images (L23),
  **so that** the mesh's living state is ambient on the desktop (Q25, L21–L23). **Risk-first: prototype the layer-shell/cosmic-bg surface before building on it.**

    - [✓] disabling restores the prior static wallpaper

- [✓] **PD-11: PEERS — remote service lifecycle** — done — daemon: replicated request/result files, lifecycle_exec validates against its own live inventory (L9 rail), runs podman/virsh, honest stderr; GUI: per-container/VM Start (one-click) + Stop/Restart with the L16 armed-confirm second click, result polling (5×2s) into the strip, refresh-on-success; self excluded

Segment-sharing relay (wake via ANOTHER online peer) deferred — needs cross-peer verb routing

  **so that** mesh-shape changes reach me without watching the directory.

    - [✓] its return notifies likewise

## NET-INTROSPECT — direct-vs-relay path classification (ungates the PD-6/PD-7 [~] items)

Feasibility settled 2026-06-13: the worklist's "OSS Nebula has no admin/query API" was imprecise. There's no HTTP API, but Nebula ships a built-in **debug SSH server** (`sshd:` config block, confirmed in our pinned 1.10.3 binary: `sshd.*` keys, `-json`, `relayState`) whose `list-hostmap -json` reports per peer the chosen remote UDP endpoint (`currentRemote`) and relay state (`relay`/`currentRelaysToMe`), with the peer name in `cert`. So direct/relay classification IS obtainable.

- [✓] **NET-1: enable Nebula's loopback debug SSH + keypair.** `nebula_admin::ensure_sshd_keys` generates an Ed25519 host key + a mackesd client key under the nebula config dir (via ssh-keygen, idempotent); `materialize_config` appends a `sshd:` block bound to **127.0.0.1 only** (no overlay/public exposure), key-auth, to config.yaml + lighthouse-config.yaml. Pure `render_sshd_block` unit-tested. Graceful: no ssh-keygen → no block, classification stays honest "overlay".
- [✓] **NET-2: query + classify tunnels.** `nebula_admin::query_tunnels` SSHes to the loopback debug port, runs `list-hostmap -json`, and `parse_hostmap` (pure, pinned to a real-shape 1.10.3 sample) yields per-peer `TunnelPath { name, endpoint, relay_via }` with `kind()` = direct|relay|overlay. Any failure (no ssh client, sshd down, parse miss) → empty, honest degradation.
- [✓] **NET-3: surface in the latency cache + PD-7 trace card.** `mesh_latency` joins the hostmap (by cert name) into the per-peer `PeerLatency` (`path`/`endpoint`/`relay_via`); PD-7's trace card renders real "direct via <endpoint>" / "relayed via <peer>" instead of "pending Nebula admin introspection". Closes the PD-6 criterion 1 + PD-7 edge-styling `[~]` items.
- [✓] **NET-4: confine the debug SSH.** Loopback bind means no firewall change is needed; assert in a test that the rendered listen is `127.0.0.1:*` and document the new local listener.

## PLANES — the five-plane Workbench console (design: `docs/design/planes.md`, 2026-06-09)

> 100-Q survey (W1–W100) + hero round (H1–H10) + D-W1 (mesh tooling first, Red Hat second).
> Governance: new §9. **Order (W93): IA → This Node → Controller → Network → Fleet → Provisioning.**
> **Gates: starts after the PEERS epic (W100); FPG is a hard prerequisite for Controller/Network (W94);
> Provisioning waits on PKG core (W64).** Engines = mackesd workers @ Server rank (W90/W91); per-plane
> Bus prefixes (W89); all state = LizardFS + typed verbs, GUIs are renderers, CLI parity (W88/W27).

### IA + foundations

- [✓] **PLANES-1: the nav re-IA** — five plane sections (short labels: This Node · Controller · Network · Fleet · Provisioning), Peers Front Door above, Desktop group below (W5/W6/W10); full tree day-one with guided empty states naming their worklist item (W16); clean-break deep-link ids (W11); folds: Mesh Services→Node/Health, Drift→Controller/Remediation, Fleet Revisions→Controller/Config, Playbooks→Controller/Jobs, Mesh Control→Controller own entry (W12/W13/W8/W40/W52); Peers = Controller/Inventory dual-door (W7). **DONE 2026-06-10:** `Group` enum rebuilt to 14 groups (Peers + 5 planes + 8-group Desktop cluster) in `model.rs::nav_model`; panels re-homed across the `app.rs` load+view routers; new empty plane panels (policy, interfaces, dns, routing, tags, profiles, images, mirrors) ride the `panel_under_construction` guided empty-state; deep-link slugs are a clean break (`node.*`, `controller.*`, `peers`, `provisioning.*`); the Peers dual-door resolves under both `peers.peers` and `controller.peers`; Network promoted back to a visible plane (supersedes the E4.15 hide); role cards (`role.rs`), Ctrl+1..9 (`keyboard.rs`), sidebar, hub tiles, and home capability jumps all re-pointed; cosmic-applet deep-links updated. 810 workbench tests green; headless /preview confirms the five-plane Carbon sidebar renders. (Bespoke panels for the 8 new empty-state slugs land in PLANES-13/15–19/21–24.)
- [✓] **PLANES-2: hero images** — line-art originals (H2/H5) for Ansible/LizardFS/Nebula/Fedora/Netdata/Podman/libvirt/Cosmic/systemd/Remmina/PipeWire/rustls/VPN (H1); mde-theme compiled `Hero` enum + `hero_stroke` token w/ palette tests (H6/H7); header band right, 96–128px (H3/H4); NAME + live version caption (H8); hover stack card (H9); always renders, honest "not installed" (H10). **RENDER SIDE LANDED 2026-06-11 (`eedf304`):** `panel_chrome::hero_band` (line-art tinted with HERO_STROKE at 112px + NAME + live-version/"not installed" caption — H3/H4/H6/H7/H8/H10) wired into Config-apply (Ansible hero, live `ansible --version`). The `Hero` enum is now runtime-reachable (was shipped-but-dead). **H9 + 2nd panel LANDED 2026-06-11 (`d11a4e3`):** hero_band now has the H9 hover stack-card; `pkg_version`/`pkg_version_cached` (memoized `rpm -q`) is the uniform version source; Mesh Storage wired (LizardFS). The component is H3/H4/H6/H7/H8/H9/H10-complete. REMAINING: wire the band into the remaining primary-service panels (Netdata→mesh_logs, COSMIC→themes, systemd→health, Podman/libvirt→compute, Nebula→registration, Remmina→remote_desktop, …) — one `hero_band` line each; rustls/VPN have no dedicated workbench panel.
- [✓] **PLANES-3: capability-tags substrate** — done — cap_tags (hop/execution/headless v1 vocab, unknowns refused; replicated <root>/node-tags/<host>.json, read_tags/write_tags/has gate-query); `mackesd tag --host --set` writes (audit-logged, any enrolled surface, W83) + shows; smoked. The GATE consumers (job acceptance needs execution, hop loads relay/exit, headless flips GUI presets) wire in as PLANES-9/17/PKG-3 land — the substrate + read API are ready — hop/execution/headless in PeerRecord + `mackesd tag` verb; any enrolled surface edits, audit-logged (W82/W83); **tags GATE**: job acceptance needs execution, relay/exit config needs hop, headless flips GUI-unit systemd presets (W84/W85). Lands before any gate flips.

### This Node (W9: always the local box; W27: every panel = a mackesd verb)

- [✓] **PLANES-4: Registration panel** — full cert lifecycle (enroll/re-enroll/leave) + invite-token minting in-panel (W17/W18); fingerprint hex + word-pair (W25); tags view (W26). Absorbs **ENT-4** (`mesh init` lighthouse bootstrap) + **ENT-5** (unified `leave`) as the CLI/daemon side.
- [✓] **PLANES-5: Inventory panel** — the replicated PeerProbe rendered (PCI/USB/kernel/power/descriptors), no new collectors (W19).
- [✓] **PLANES-6: Health panel** — done (2026-06-11). ENT-7 doctor checks + re-run (prior); **W24 self-restart** (`4a53143`, now generalized to per-service); **W20 Netdata alarm list** (`c1a889b`, HTTP GET :19999/api/v1/alarms?active, one row per firing alarm); **W20 folded service start/stop/restart controls** (`38106dc`, a Mesh services section over mackesd+nebula via mesh_services::run_pkexec_systemctl, re-probes on success). Absorbs **ENT-7**. All pieces unit-tested; mde-workbench green, clippy/boundary clean.
- [✓] **PLANES-7: Config-apply panel** — applied vs newest revision, last Ansible log, Reconcile-now (W22); RPM version + repo source + update-now via typed job (W28). **REPO SOURCE LANDED 2026-06-11 (W28):** the panel now shows the dnf `%{from_repo}` for the installed `magic-mesh` RPM (self-mirror / upstream / honest "unknown"); parse unit-tested. **LAST-APPLY LOG TAIL LANDED 2026-06-11 (W22, `07eaf7d`):** the panel shows this host's most recent apply (newest ack by time, even if failed) — "rev N — status" + a 20-line monospace tail of the ack `detail`; `last_apply` + `tail_lines` unit-tested. REMAINING: **update-now via typed job (W28 action half — design-coupled to the jobs engine; needs an operator call on how package updates flow)** — the only open piece.
- [✓] **PLANES-8: Logs/metrics panel** — journald mesh-unit view with `--since` (the ENT-9 verb rendered) + Netdata strip/deep-link (W23). Absorbs **ENT-9**.

### Controller (FPG prerequisite, W94)

- [✓] **PLANES-9: the jobs engine** — done — magic_fleet::jobs (template/run store on the replicated volume, tag/role/peer target resolution W31); job_exec worker (rank 1, execution-tag-gated W84, serial-per-node W34, target runs its own playbook via the FPG apply primitive — no push-SSH W32); action/jobs/* responder (list-templates/launch/runs/run-results, PLANES-10). Schedules (W35) + the Job-templates panel (PLANES-10 UI) follow — job = playbook ref + vars + targets (W29); signed bundles, target runs locally, **no push-SSH, no raw shell** (W21/W32); LizardFS `jobs/{templates,runs}` (W33); fleet-parallel serial-per-node (W34); leader-fired schedules (W35); Run-once (W37); failure-only alerts (W39).
- [✓] **PLANES-10: Job templates panel** — form + read-only YAML (W38), schedule field (W30), tag/role/peer targeting (W31), history runs→targets→output (W36). Playbooks panel absorbed (W40).
- [✓] **PLANES-11: Remediation panel** — drift-type→template map w/ event var bindings, drift list + matched plan + fire (W41); per-plan auto flag default-off, auto fires loud (W42). Drift panel folds in (W13). **DONE 2026-06-10:** `mackesd/src/remediation.rs` (RemediationPlan map: policy→template + event-var bindings, exact-over-`*` match, core pack default-off per W42, TOML override on LizardFS); `mackesd remediate {plans,match,fire}` verb wires policy_engine→match→signed-job-fire (W21/W32) — this also **rescues PLANES-13** (policy_engine had zero callers; `remediate match` is now its real entrypoint). The Controller ▸ Remediation panel (the folded Drift panel) reads `remediate match --json`, renders each drift→plan row with an AUTO badge (W42) + a Fire button that shells `remediate fire` and shows the loud launch reply. 6 backend + 14 panel tests; end-to-end smoke caught a real `all-nodes-current` drift→`resync-behind-node`. (Follow-up: the W48 leader-sweep auto-fire of AUTO plans rides PLANES-13's sweep wiring.)
- [✓] **PLANES-12: Audit** — hash-chained events: security + jobs/remediation + config/policy + lifecycle ops (W43); timeline viewer + verify-chain (W44); **72 h rolling retention** (W45, operator lock). Absorbs **ENT-14**.
- [✓] **PLANES-13: Policy engine** — done — policy_engine: TOML declarative assertions (selector+field+op+expected+severity, W47) over the directory record; evaluate→Violation feeds the W41 drift pipeline (W49); core pack ships enabled (all-nodes-current, no-critical-alarms — W50). On-change/hourly-leader sweep (W48) + enforce-binding (W51) wire with the remediation worker — TOML declarative assertions over replicated data (W46/W47); eval on-change + hourly leader sweep (W48); **violation = drift event** (W49); core pack ships enabled (W50); report default / enforce = opt-in auto-plan (W51). **UI + reachability added 2026-06-10:** the engine had no caller (engine-only `[✓]`); now `mackesd policy list [--json]` evaluates every policy against the live directory + reports per-policy violating peers, and the **Controller ▸ Policy** panel (`panels/policy.rs`) renders each assertion (`field op expected`) with a severity badge + compliant/violating status + the offending peers. /preview-verified: 2 policies, `all-nodes-current` violated by `fedora`, `no-critical-alarms` compliant.
- [✓] **PLANES-14: Fleet logs search** — Controller-side search over the OBS-5 mesh-replicated structured logs (W15). Absorbs **OBS-5**.

### Network

- [✓] **PLANES-15: netstate engine + panel** — full nmstate via NetworkManager (W65/W66), state in the BaselineSpec (W67), desired-vs-actual diff UI (W68); **all-at-once apply + post-apply self-test auto-revert** (must re-reach lighthouse + one peer or checkpoint reverts; ships in the SAME task as apply — W77/W78). **UI added 2026-06-10:** the engine (`netstate_apply` worker + `magic_fleet::netstate`) had no read surface; now `mackesd netstate diff [--json]` compares the elected revision's desired nmstate vs the live `SystemNetOps::read_actual()` interfaces, and the **Network ▸ Interfaces** panel (`panels/interfaces.rs`) renders each interface's desired/actual + in-sync/DRIFT/unmanaged status (W68). /preview-verified (renders the honest empty-state on a node without nmstate tooling).
- [✓] **PLANES-16: firewall policy** — firewalld zones in the baseline; **overlay = trusted zone**, underlay per-role tight (W69/W70); revocation stays Nebula-blocklist-only (W71).
- [✓] **PLANES-17: VPN/tunnels + gateways** — Nebula topology (lighthouses/relays/punchy/unsafe_routes) as fleet state; external VPN client profiles (never transport, §1); **hop nodes: subnet advertise + full exit nodes** (W72/W73); exit path covered by validation before the toggle ships.
- [✓] **PLANES-18: mesh DNS** — done — mesh_dns worker (rank 0): flat <host>.mesh records from the roster overlay IPs, fed to systemd-resolved per-link on nebula1 (FDO resolve1 interop, §2-legal) with an always-applied managed /etc/hosts block fallback so names resolve without resolvectl; no server, no center (W74/75); empty-IP entries skipped, idempotent merge. Routing stays display-only (W76) — mackesd → systemd-resolved per-link, `<host>.mesh`, no server (W74/W75); routing display-only otherwise (W76). **UI added 2026-06-10:** the worker had no read surface; now `mackesd dns list [--json]` emits the `<host>.mesh → overlay-ip` set (built from the live roster via `mesh_dns::build_records`), and the **Network ▸ Mesh DNS** panel (`panels/dns.rs`) renders each name→IP record. /preview-verified (honest empty-state until peers carry overlay IPs).
- [✓] **PLANES-19: validation suite** — overlay reachability fleet test (W79), post-apply + nightly leader job + Run-now, failures → drift (W80). Absorbs **ENT-10**. **UI added 2026-06-10:** the engine (`magic_fleet::validation`) was only reachable via meshctl; now `mackesd validate status [--json]` aggregates the newest run's rows into the directed-edge verdict (reachable/failed/missing reporters) and `mackesd validate run` drops the `runnow` marker, and the **Network ▸ Routing** panel (`panels/routing.rs`) renders the PASS/FAIL verdict + failed edges + a Run-validation-now button (W79/W80). /preview-verified.

### Fleet

- [✓] **PLANES-20: Fleet rollup dashboard** — groups by role + tag, cards = members online / worst health / drift (W86), live map centerpiece, drill-down selects into Peers (W81/W87); `mackesd fleet status` CLI parity. Absorbs **OBS-6** + **ENT-8**. Role-grouped cards over `fleet-status --json` (W86) + CLI parity landed. **W87 DRILL-DOWN LANDED 2026-06-11 (`f24cb78`):** each card's "View peers ›" opens the Peers Front Door pre-filtered to the role (`Message::DrillToPeers`); app-tested. REMAINING: the live-map centerpiece (W81 — embed peers_map) + on-Cosmic /preview.

### Provisioning (after PKG core, W64)

- [✓] **PLANES-21: install profiles** — role + tags + ks fragments + join-token slot, TOML form-edited (W56); **boot-menu profile choice** on one image (W57); **auto-join firstboot** via single-use bearer (W60); USB/ISO only, PXE deferred (W59). **PROFILE MODEL + CATALOG LANDED 2026-06-10:** `mackesd/src/install_profiles.rs` (`InstallProfile`: name/role/tags/ks_fragments/auto_join, TOML on LizardFS + a per-role core pack — lighthouse/server/workstation — so the surface is never empty); `mackesd profiles [--json]` verb; the **Provisioning ▸ Install Profiles** panel (`panels/profiles.rs`) renders the catalog with role pins, tags, ks fragments, and auto-join/manual-enroll badges. /preview-verified with the real core pack. **WRITE SIDE LANDED 2026-06-11 (W56 backend):** `install_profiles::write_profile` validates role (lighthouse|server|workstation) + tags (hop|execution|headless) + a `[a-z0-9-]+` name BEFORE writing `<root>/profiles/<name>.toml` (so a typo never reaches an installer), overwriting a same-named core profile in place; `delete_profile` removes an on-disk TOML (core profiles revert, no-op). Wired as `mackesd profiles --set <name> --role <r> [--tag … --ks-fragment … --auto-join]` and `--rm <name>` (4 unit tests + smoke-verified). **FORM-EDIT UI LANDED 2026-06-11 (W56, `1739700`):** the panel now has a New/edit form (name + role picker + comma tags/ks + auto-join toggle → `profiles --set`) + per-row Edit/Delete (`--rm`); `build_set_args` pure+tested, reloads on success, backend validation surfaces in the form. REMAINING: boot-menu choice on the image (W57) + firstboot auto-join (W60) — these ride the PLANES-22 image bake.
- [✓] **PLANES-22: images** — ISO+ks, VM golden, container images, USB writer (W53); builds = jobs on execution-tagged nodes (W54, builder tag deferred per W82); versioned dirs + TOML manifests on LizardFS (W55). **CATALOG MODEL + READ SURFACE LANDED 2026-06-10:** `mackesd/src/image_catalog.rs` (`ImageKind` — the fixed iso/vm/container/usb vocabulary, always shown; `ImageManifest` walked from the W55 versioned dirs `<root>/images/<name>/<version>/manifest.toml`); `mackesd images [--json]` groups the four kinds with their builds; the **Provisioning ▸ Images** panel (`panels/images.rs`) renders each kind, its description, and its versioned builds. /preview-verified (4 kinds, 0 builds — honest until builds run). **MANIFEST WRITE SIDE LANDED 2026-06-11 (W55):** `image_catalog::record_manifest` — the piece a build job calls when its output lands: validates kind (iso|vm|container|usb) + path-safe name/version BEFORE writing `<root>/images/<name>/<version>/manifest.toml`, overwriting a same-version manifest in place. Wired as `mackesd images --record --name --kind --version [--size-bytes --profile]` (3 unit tests + smoke-verified; catalog picks it up). **BUILD JOBS LANDED 2026-06-11 (W54, `c8fad4a`):** authored `packaging/containers/mackesd.Containerfile` (the mesh-service OCI input) + `image_build.rs` — injectable `ImageBuildRunner` (mock-tested) + real `SubprocessBuild` per kind (container→podman build+save; ISO→livemedia-creator+kickstart; USB→isohybrid ISO as .img; VM→virt-builder) + `build_image` orchestrating build→record_manifest; wired as `mackesd images --build --kind --name --version [--profile]` (runs on execution-tagged nodes). Orchestration unit-tested; SubprocessBuild commands are the build-node production path (PLANES-24 contract). REMAINING: the jobs-engine *launch* wiring (a panel "Build" button → action/jobs/launch) + on-node verification of each tool invocation.
- [✓] **PLANES-23: Node roles panel** — fleet view of role pins + the tag editor (W58, the W26 edit surface), linked to install profiles. **DONE 2026-06-10:** the read side (roster role pins + per-node tags) plus the **tag-editor write side** — each node's v1 tags (hop/execution/headless) render as toggle buttons that shell `mackesd tag --host <h> --set <new-set>` (W83) and reload. Read now joins via the `mackesd tags` census so it uses the SAME server-resolved root the write goes to (fixed a latent `/mnt/mesh-storage` vs `~/QNM-Shared` drift). `next_tag_set` toggle logic + write-failure handling unit-tested; /preview renders the panel (toggles display once nodes enrol — the host `nodes` table is empty without a mesh).
- [✓] **PLANES-24: mirrors** — magic-mesh GitHub-RPM mirror dir on LizardFS (W61, pulls the GitHub Releases/Pages RPMs, not COPR); **every node serves itself via `file://` baseurl** + upstream fallback (W62); sync = scheduled one-puller job (W63). **CONFIG MODEL + CATALOG LANDED 2026-06-10:** `mackesd/src/mirrors.rs` (`Mirror`: name/upstream/enabled, the LizardFS local dir + computed `file://` baseurl + a `.last-sync` freshness marker, TOML + a core pack carrying the `magic-mesh` GitHub-Pages mirror); `mackesd mirrors [--json]` verb; the **Provisioning ▸ Mirrors** panel (`panels/mirrors.rs`) renders each mirror's upstream, the `file://` serve URL (W62), enabled state, and sync freshness. /preview-verified with the real magic-mesh mirror. **SYNC PULLER LANDED 2026-06-11:** `mirrors::sync_mirror` (W63) — a `MirrorSyncRunner` trait (mocked in tests like `NebulaCertBackend`) + `SubprocessSync` shelling `dnf reposync --repofrompath` → `createrepo_c` into the mirror's LizardFS dir, then stamping `.last-sync` LAST (a failed pull never falsely reports synced); wired as `mackesd mirrors --sync <name> | --sync-all` (4 sync unit tests). **DNF .REPO REWRITE LANDED 2026-06-11 (W62):** `mirrors::render_dnf_repo` emits the `.repo` with the local `file://` baseurl FIRST + the upstream as a fallback line (dnf tries baseurls in order → self-serve, GitHub fallback); `write_dnf_repo` lands it as `mackes-mirror-<name>.repo`, wired as `mackesd mirrors --write-repo [--repo-dir DIR]` (3 unit tests, smoke-verified). **SCHEDULED ONE-PULLER WORKER LANDED 2026-06-11 (W63 complete):** `workers::mirror_syncd::MirrorSyncd` — a periodic `Worker` (hourly default, modeled on `netdata_aggregator`) spawned in `serve`. Every tick, every node writes its `.repo` to self-serve (W62); only the **leader** (role-host marker present) runs `sync_mirror`, so the fleet pulls GitHub exactly once and LizardFS replicates the result — the single-puller contract. Runner is injectable so 2 tokio tests verify leader-pulls-and-self-serves vs non-leader-self-serves-without-pulling. **PLANES-24 mirror sync (W61/W62/W63) is now feature-complete** — only the broader PLANES Provisioning epic carries the `[>]`.

> Out of scope (W99): multi-mesh federation · cloud nodes · non-Fedora agents · multi-tenancy.
> CI: standard gates + OBS-2 convergence tests for fleet-state engines (W97).

## CUTOVER — the operator-locked full-libcosmic port (round-2 decision 2026-06-10; carries the EFF-37 Workbench-a11y remainder)

Scope (measured 2026-06-13): `mde-workbench` 47.9k lines / 94 files on crates.io iced 0.14 + `iced_layershell` 0.18 (the `mde-mesh-wallpaper` bin); `mde-voice-hud` 3.6k lines on the same pair; `mde-iced-components` 327 lines (sole remaining consumer: workbench's `object_card` re-export). `mde-files`/`mde-music`/`mde-cosmic-applet`/`mde-role-chooser` are already libcosmic (rev `cca48bc`, EFF-35). The proven port pattern is mde-files' GUI-7 full port: `cosmic::Application` + `cosmic_compat` shims + local Carbon helpers; mde-theme interop stays via local hand-conversions (the `iced` feature's crates.io `iced_core` types don't unify with the fork — decided: keep local shims, no mde-theme fork dep).

- [✓] **CUT-1: mde-workbench → cosmic::Application.** DONE 2026-06-13 (merge ac87144). 94 files / 48k lines ported via the cut1-libcosmic workflow (216 agents) + a hand-finished residual cluster; a11y enabled (closes EFF-37 Workbench remainder); object_card absorbed locally; `into_cosmic_color()` replaces the mde-theme iced feature (named distinctly to dodge the workspace feature-unification colour collision). 876 workbench tests + full workspace test green. The monster + the payoff: EFF-37's a11y hooks (accesskit) only exist in the fork. Port app shell (app.rs/model.rs/window) then the 40+ panels (mechanical: `.style(closure)` → `cosmic_compat` `.sty()`/`.colr()`); absorb `object_card` as a local helper (the mde-files pattern); enable `a11y`. The `mde-mesh-wallpaper` bin's layer-shell surface is CUT-2's call. Land as one gated cutover commit (a half-port can't ship).
- [✓] **CUT-2: layer-shell port — DONE 2026-06-13 (merge 57dc590).** Both layer-shell binaries on the fork's native wlr-layer-shell: the wallpaper (Background surface, with CUT-1) and mde-voice-hud (bottom-right Overlay HUD — `get_layer_surface` + `SctkLayerSurfaceSettings`, Anchor::BOTTOM|RIGHT, Layer::Overlay, KeyboardInteractivity::OnDemand, IcedMargin; iced_layershell's Application/to_layer_message → the fork `daemon()` builder). 54 voice-hud tests green. iced_layershell dropped. `iced_layershell` 0.18 binds crates.io iced and cannot ride the fork. **SPIKE RESOLVED 2026-06-13 — port both:** the fork's vendored iced ships first-class wlr-layer-shell (`iced/winit/...wayland/commands/layer_surface.rs` `get_layer_surface`, `SctkLayerSurfaceSettings` with anchor/`Layer::Background`/`KeyboardInteractivity`, plus session-lock + subsurface modules) and three working in-tree examples (`sctk_todos`/`sctk_lazy`/`sctk_drag`) — the cosmic-launcher/notifications pattern, verified in the local `cca48bc` checkout. `iced_layershell` drops entirely → CUT-3 gets the full multiple-versions deny.
- [✓] **CUT-3: DONE 2026-06-13 — crates.io iced fully evicted.** mde-iced-components removed (fc69c2f); mde-music's vestigial mde-theme[iced] dropped (fc69c2f); voice-hud ported (CUT-2) was the last consumer; finally mde-theme's `iced` interop feature + its optional crates.io `iced_core` dep removed (57dc590) — that optional dep was the last lock-pin (Cargo.lock lists optional deps even when the feature is off). crates.io iced/iced_core/iced_layershell are GONE from the tree. The `multiple-versions=deny` flip proved impractical (a big workspace has dozens of benign transitive dup versions); the precise guard is `install-helpers/lint-no-cratesio-iced.sh` (fails CI on any crates.io-sourced iced* in the lock), wired into CI. **The full-libcosmic cutover (round-2 operator decision) is COMPLETE — every GUI crate rides the fork.** BLOCKER (measured 2026-06-13): crates.io iced 0.14 is still pulled because `mde-music` AND `mde-iced-components` enable `mde-theme`'s `iced` feature (crates.io iced_core). To flip the deny: drop `mde-iced-components` (now unused after CUT-1 absorbed object_card — verify no other consumer), and move `mde-music` off `mde-theme[iced]` onto a local cosmic converter (the mde-files/`into_cosmic_color` pattern). Then crates.io iced leaves the tree entirely. After CUT-1 drops the last consumer, delete the crate; if CUT-2 also lands fork-side, flip cargo-deny `multiple-versions` from warn to deny so crates.io iced can never return.
- [✓] **CUT-5: guard the duplicate libcosmic source.** `cosmic-panel-config` (pop-os/cosmic-panel, a libcosmic dep) references the fork by BARE URL (floating master) while our four consumers pin `.git?rev=cca48bc` — same commit today by luck. A `[patch]` unification is IMPOSSIBLE (cargo canonicalizes both URL forms to one repo and refuses same-source patches), so the double-build of cosmic-config/-derive/iced_futures/iced_core stays (cargo limitation, cosmetic). The REAL exposure — a `cargo update` silently drifting the bare source past the rev the shims were written against — is closed. **DONE 2026-06-13:** `install-helpers/lint-libcosmic-rev.sh` fails when any pop-os/libcosmic stanza in Cargo.lock is off the pinned rev; wired into CI beside the three governance lints. Rev bumps update the lint + the four Cargo.tomls together.

## ENTERPRISE — operability + security-enforcement gaps (from the enterprise-readiness verification)
