> Source: `docs/design/enterprise-readiness.md` (verdict: **prototype with enterprise direction**).
> These are the enterprise-specific gaps the survey epics did NOT capture. **ENT-1/2/3 are CRITICAL.**
> Overlaps: installation/packaging/role-chooser = the **PKG** epic; CI/observability = **OBS**;
> control plane = **FLEET-PHASE-G**. The minimum bar for honestly claiming "enterprise-grade" is
> PKG + ENT-1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 12 + OBS CI.
>
> **Corrective decisions (2026-06-09):** C1 enrollment → **single-use issued bearer allow-list** ·
> C2 revocation → **nebula pki.blocklist + reload** · C3 unpinned role → **refuse to start (fail
> closed)** · C4 operator UX → **a `meshctl` facade** (ENT-15) · C5 resilience → **systemd unit +
> hardened in-process supervisor** · C6 off-boarding → **both self-service `leave` + operator
> decommission** · C7 trust → **keep open-mesh, document the blast radius** (→ governance, ENT-12) ·
> C8 backup → **systemd-creds passphrase only** (keep the single QNM copy + replication) · C9
> positioning → **production workgroup-grade (≤8 peers)** (→ governance, ENT-12) · C10 audit →
> **hash-chained security events**.

- [✓] **ENT-1: enforce the enrollment bearer (CRITICAL — security)** — done — bearer_ledger (issued-but-unredeemed, SHA-256 hashes only, 256-bit mints = the SEC-3 strength); sign_pending_csr refuses wrong/forged/replayed/absent bearers BEFORE any signing (override never bypasses auth); redemption is single-use at the sign; `mackesd enroll-token` mints + prints the wire token (smoked); acceptance tests pin refusal + replay — `sign_pending_csr` (`nebula_enroll.rs:571`) + `nebula_csr_watcher` sign any well-formed CSR and **never check the bearer/passcode** against an issued list, though the docs claim they do. Maintain an issued-but-unredeemed allow-list (single-use bearers); refuse a CSR whose bearer isn't pending-issued. **Acceptance:** an enroll with a wrong/replayed/absent bearer is refused (test); a valid single-use bearer signs once then can't be reused.
- [✓] **ENT-2: pin `role.toml` at provision + fail-closed when unpinned (CRITICAL)** — done — resolve_rank_strict(): an unpinned box REFUSES to start its worker pool with the actionable error (smoked); `mackesd role-pin <role>` CLI (=PKG-4) wired to mde_role::pin (upgrade-only — Server→Workstation upgrade + lighthouse downgrade-refusal both smoked live); installer/chooser hookup = PKG-5 — `mde_role::pin_at` is lib-only; nothing writes `/var/lib/mde/role.toml`, so every box runs unpinned→Workstation. Add `mackesd role pin <role>` (= PKG-4), have the installer/chooser call it, and **change `resolve_rank()` so an unpinned box REFUSES to start its worker pool** (C3, fail closed) instead of defaulting to Workstation. **Acceptance:** a Server install gates to rank-1 workers; an unpinned box refuses to start with a clear "pin a role" error; downgrade refused.
- [✓] **ENT-3: revocation evicts the data plane (CRITICAL — security)** — done — revoke fingerprints the active certs (nebula-cert, LOUD error if unfingerprintable) → replicated ca/blocklist/<node>.json; every peer's supervisor unions the entries into pki.blocklist + reloads nebula on change (one tick after replication); empty list renders no stanza. Multi-node integration test = OBS-2 scope — `ca revoke` only marks the DB + ban list + a bus event; the Nebula data plane keeps trusting the cert until expiry. Push a Nebula `pki.blocklist` (or equivalent) to running nebula + reload on revoke. **Acceptance:** a revoked node can no longer reach any peer within N seconds (integration test).
- [→] **ENT-4: `mackesd mesh init`** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-4** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [→] **ENT-5: unify `mackesd leave` / decommission** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-4** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [✓] **ENT-6: `mackesd.service` + supervisor hardening** — done — packaging/systemd/mackesd.service (Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=3, systemd-analyze-verified); the 250ms fixed-retry stub replaced with pure-fn restart state: exponential backoff 250ms→60s cap + circuit breaker (8 rapid failures/120s window trips, ERROR-logged; healthy stretch resets); 3 pure-state tests pin the acceptance — no systemd unit means nothing restarts mackesd on crash; the worker supervisor is a 250 ms fixed-retry stub (`workers/mod.rs:430`, no max-restarts/circuit-breaker). Ship the unit (Restart=on-failure) (= PKG-3) + bounded exponential back-off + circuit-breaker + max-restarts. **Acceptance:** `kill -9 mackesd` → restarted ≤ N s; a hot-looping worker trips the breaker instead of spinning at 250 ms forever.
- [→] **ENT-7: `mackesd doctor`** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-6** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [→] **ENT-8: `mackesd fleet status`** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-20** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [→] **ENT-9: `mackesd logs` + fix the GUI Logs panel** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-8** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [→] **ENT-10: `mackesd test connectivity`** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-19** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [✓] **ENT-11: backup passphrase hardening (C8)** — done — the backup worker reads the passphrase from $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/backup-passphrase (systemd-creds, TPM/host-bound) FIRST, env var fallback for dev; the unit uses LoadCredentialEncrypted= not Environment= so it's invisible to systemctl show + /proc; the single QNM-Shared sealed copy (state-backup.enc) is unchanged. Test pins creds>env precedence — move `MDE_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE` off the systemd env into systemd-creds (TPM/host-bound); keep the single QNM-Shared copy carried by LizardFS replication (no off-mesh export required per C8). **Acceptance:** the passphrase is not visible via `systemctl show` / `/proc`; backup + `state-restore` still work end-to-end.
- [✓] **ENT-12: operator + end-user documentation + positioning (C7, C9)** — install guide, per-node-type setup guide, troubleshooting guide, and a DR runbook (the code points at a missing `docs/help/mesh-recovery.md`). **Document the open-mesh blast radius** as an accepted ≤8-peer trade-off (C7). Rewrite `DISCLAIMER.md`: "Mackes Workstation" → MCNF, and reposition from "not for production" to **production workgroup-grade (≤8 peers)** with a stated supported envelope (C9). **Acceptance:** a new admin provisions all 3 node types + recovers a dead lighthouse using only the docs; the disclaimer + a SUPPORT.md state the production envelope.
- [✓] **ENT-13: replace the `mesh_latency` ping placeholder** — done in PD-6 — transport_probe TCP-through-tunnel; no ICMP — `mesh_latency.rs:10` shells `ping` as an admitted placeholder pending the transport handshake. Use the real transport RTT probe. **Acceptance:** latency reflects the overlay path, not ICMP.
- [→] **ENT-14: security-event audit (C10)** — **RE-HOMED → PLANES-12** (W96, planes re-IA); spec text lives in the PLANES task + `docs/design/planes.md`.
- [✓] **ENT-15: `meshctl` operator facade (C4)** — a thin friendly binary over mackesd/magic-fleet exposing the lifecycle gestures as one learnable tool: `install`, `status`, `doctor` (ENT-7), `mesh init` (ENT-4), `provision`/`join` (= enroll), `fleet status` (ENT-8), `test connectivity` (ENT-10), `logs` (ENT-9), `repair` (= heal/reconcile), `leave`/`decommission` (ENT-5). The named subcommands ENT-4/5/7/8/9/10 land here (or as mackesd verbs `meshctl` wraps). **Acceptance:** every §8 verification command in the enterprise-readiness doc runs as shown; `meshctl --help` lists them.

---

*Audit (sweeps 1–2): 18 findings, A1–H8. **8 shipped** (H1 §3, D1/H5/H2 §4, F1–F3/H7 §5-doc, G1 §1, A1/A2 deletion). The 7 open findings are now **specified** by the survey and resolve into the epics above.*
*Survey (2026-06-09): 100/100 answered → 6 epics, 51 tasks. Packaging (PKG-*) is held until every feature is §7-complete; releasing is operator-gated.*
*Enterprise-readiness verification (2026-06-09): verdict **prototype with enterprise direction**; +14 ENTERPRISE tasks (ENT-1/2/3 CRITICAL). Full report: `docs/design/enterprise-readiness.md`.*

### NAV-1 — MDE Workbench navigation grouping redesign
Design: docs/design/workbench-nav-grouping.md (15-question survey locked 2026-06-14).
- [✓] **NAV-1.1: Workbench — regroup the left nav to the 9 locked sections**
  **As** a mesh operator,
  **I want** the Workbench nav organized scope→function (Overview · This Node ·
  Mesh · Fleet · Provisioning · Monitoring · System[=Config+Maintain+Help]) with
  desktop settings deferred to Cosmic and plain-language labels,
  **so that** I can find mesh services without hunting across 15 ad-hoc groups.
  **Acceptance** (runtime-observable):
    - [✓] left nav renders exactly the 7 sections in the locked order; Overview/Home selected on launch
    - [✓] no duplicate/ambiguous labels (no three "Inventory"s); renames applied (Mackes Bus→Message Bus, etc.)
    - [✓] every retained panel routes to its working view; deferred desktop panels gone from nav
    - [✓] Group::all() order matches the lock; from_slug round-trips; cargo test -p mde-workbench green
- [✓] **NAV-1.2: delete the deferred desktop panel modules + wire the Cosmic hand-off** *(operator decisions locked 2026-06-16)*
  **Done — verified complete 2026-06-20** (the work had landed in prior NAV passes; this turn confirmed every acceptance bullet against the code + ran the locked gate). The hidden `Group::Desktop` is gone from the `Group` enum (`model.rs` — `all()`/`sidebar_groups()`/`from_slug` carry only the mesh sections; the line-82 comment records "NAV-1.2 retired the hidden [Desktop] group"). All 17 Cosmic-owned desktop panels (displays/keyboard/mouse/power/session/sound/printers/removable/themes/fonts/datetime + install/installed/remove/sources/default_apps/panel) are deleted — no module files, no `pub mod`, no Message/update/view/load wiring (a dangling arm would fail the build, which is green). The keep-4 are relocated per the plan with explicit `// NAV-1.2 —` markers: wallpaper + notifications → **This Node** (`model.rs:204-207`), system_update + sync_status → **System** (`model.rs:301-302` + the deep-link map `system.system_update`/`system.sync_status` → `Group::System`). Gate: `cargo test -p mde-workbench` green (769 + suites, 0 failed).
  **LOCKED PLAN:**
  - **Delete (no hand-off, Cosmic Store/Settings owns them):** standard settings — displays, keyboard, mouse, power, session, sound, printers, removable, themes, fonts, datetime (11); app/package mgmt — install, installed, remove, sources, default_apps, panel (6). = 17 panels: delete the module files + `pub mod` + Message/update/view/load wiring.
  - **Keep + relocate to a visible group:** wallpaper (mesh-map), sync_status (QNM-Shared), system_update (magic-mesh RPM), notifications (MDE Notification Hub) → wallpaper+notifications → This Node; system_update+sync_status → System.
  - **Remove** the hidden `Group::Desktop` entirely (Group enum + all()/sidebar_groups + role.rs arms + the test).
  - **Gate:** `cargo test -p mde-workbench` green, no dead `pub mod`, clippy + fmt clean.
  **As** a maintainer,
  **I want** the desktop-settings panels (apps/displays/keyboard/mouse/power/session/
  sound/printers/removable/themes/fonts/wallpaper/sync_status/datetime/system_update/
  notifications) removed from the workbench and owned by Cosmic Settings,
  **so that** the hidden `Group::Desktop` placeholder + its panel modules stop being
  dead weight (NAV-1.1 left them deep-link-reachable to avoid orphaning).
  **Acceptance** (runtime-observable):
    - [✓] the `Desktop` group + its nav entry are gone; `Group::all()` is the 7 visible sections
    - [✓] each deferred panel module + its Message/update/view/load wiring is deleted (or moved to Cosmic)
    - [✓] no dead `pub mod`/unreachable panel remains; cargo test -p mde-workbench green

### SUBAUDIT — deployed-substrate correctness sweep (docs/design/substrate-correctness-audit.md)
Live testing on .13 surfaced a run of empty panels — all four classes of substrate mismatch (not unbuilt). Fix the class, not just each panel.
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-A1: mesh_dns + nodes list + fleet-status read the directory, not the empty sqlite roster.**
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-B1: Message Bus panel reads /run/mde-bus (MDE_BUS_ROOT) + walks the bus root, not a phantom topics/.**
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-A2: audit the other 13 export_roster/list_nodes consumers** for the empty-sqlite trap; switch to the directory where the panel/worker needs the mesh-wide roster.
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-B2: per-HOME bus paths in mesh_federation / vm_wizard / mde-files honor MDE_BUS_ROOT.**
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-C1: declare helper-binary deps (nmap/resolvectl/firewalld/NetworkManager/podman/lizardfs-client/rsync) in the RPM + degrade honestly in the UI.** (nmap = Discovered Hosts.)
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-C3: Discovered Hosts still empty after the --open/host-timeout/fast-first-tick fixes** — the probe worker's cycle isn't rewriting the inventory (mtime stuck) though the exact fast argv finds the 3 mesh peers up via the CLI. Likely the LAN portion blocks the sync cycle, or targets don't resolve in-process. NEXT: scan mesh_targets first + write immediately (mesh peers before LAN), and/or run scan() via spawn_blocking with a per-cycle deadline; add a per-tick targets/cards count log.
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-C2: probe — surface up-but-no-open-curated-port hosts** so Discovered Hosts lists reachable peers even when no scanned service is open (fast_argv uses `--open`; add a host-discovery pass / host element for up hosts). Plumbing now works (nmap declared+installed, targets from nebula-bundle.json, inventory written).
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-D1: https-tunnel relay cert self-bootstrap — LIVE-VERIFIED on v10.0.12 (2026-06-17).** Across all three node types: **Lighthouse** `45.55.33.179` (role=lighthouse) self-minted `/etc/nebula/lighthouse.{crt(0644 public),key(0600)}` + `:443` LISTENs + the relay worker spawned; **Server** `159.65.183.51` (role=server, no role.host) correctly logs `nebula-https-listener: not a relay node — skipped`; **Workstation** `.13` correctly has no cert + no `:443`. The eligibility gate (role.host marker OR role=Lighthouse) works exactly as designed. *(was: code-done, verify-at-roll.)* Original below:
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-D1 (original): https-tunnel relay cert self-bootstrap. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); live-verify pending the pre-release pass.** `ensure_self_signed_cert()` mints a self-signed `/etc/nebula/lighthouse.{crt,key}` (0600, idempotent, leaves an operator/LE cert untouched); `run_serve` calls it when the node is **relay-eligible** (the `role.host` marker present OR role pinned Lighthouse) then spawns the `:443` listener — a NAT'd workstation (.13) never generates a cert or binds. Crash-loop guard kept (eligibility gate + OnFailure backoff). Test: `ensure_self_signed_cert_generates_then_is_idempotent`. **Live-verify (before close):** on LH-01/02 the cert appears + `:443` LISTENs + the worker spawns; on .13 it stays skipped. *(was: no node had the cert so the relay never bound anywhere.)* _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by the [✓] sibling above (line ~661); bf948da relay-cert self-bootstrap, live-verified v10.0.12 (7c4765f))_
- [✓] **SUBAUDIT-D2: Hardware panel — root-cause the empty per-peer hardware probe (data-source or worker-not-running).**

### NOTIFY — MDE-Notification-Hub (design: `docs/design/mde-notification-hub.md`, 2026-06-14)
A professional, themed, desktop-wide notification center replacing the Cosmic tray for mesh + desktop alerts. Locks (operator survey): standalone center + toasts (new `crates/services/mde-notify`); render the bus, don't own FDO; group by source, color by severity; configurable per-group sound packs; all effects DND-aware. The Carbon look (§4) + no-stub reachability (§7) gate every task.
- [✓] **NOTIFY-1: scaffold the `mde-notify` shared crate — bus tail + AlertItem model.**
  **As** a mesh operator,
  **I want** a library that tails the live bus alert lanes into a typed, deduped alert stream,
  **so that** every notification surface reads one source of truth (no demo data).
  **Acceptance** (runtime-observable):
    - [✓] `crates/services/mde-notify` is a workspace member; `cargo test -p mde-notify` runs
    - [✓] tails `fdo/*`, `peer/*/alerts`, `fleet/sec`, `event/firewall/*`, `compute/event/*`, presence + `mackesd::alert` lanes via `Persist::list_since` with a per-lane cursor
    - [✓] bus root resolves through `mde_bus::client_data_dir()` (reaches the live system bus)
    - [✓] `AlertItem` populated from real bus messages; dedup by ULID; retention horizon honored
- [✓] **NOTIFY-2: severity + source classifier (the grouping + color engine).**
  **As** an operator triaging alerts,
  **I want** each alert classified to a Severity and a Source from its topic + payload,
  **so that** the table can group by source and color by severity deterministically.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] one classifier fn maps topic prefix → Source (Security/Presence/Firewall/Compute/DesktopApp/Peer/System)
    - [✓] severity derives from the alert `severity` field AND/OR bus `Priority`; unit-tested both ways
    - [✓] severity→`mde_theme::Palette` token map (danger/warning/accent/success) — no raw hex (§4 lint clean)
- [✓] **NOTIFY-3: the standalone center — layer-shell slide-out table view.**
  **As** a Cosmic desktop user,
  **I want** a themed slide-out center listing alerts grouped by source with severity color + glyphs,
  **so that** I can see the full alert history regardless of whether the Workbench is open.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] `mde-notify-center` binary opens an Overlay-layer slide-out (the `mde-mesh-wallpaper` layer-shell pattern)
    - [✓] collapsible per-source groups; rows show ts/severity/host/title/body + BUS-2.7 action buttons
    - [✓] mark-read / clear-all / mark-all-read work and persist
    - [✓] renders through `mde-theme` tokens + density (matches Workbench); `cargo test -p mde-notify` green
- [✓] **NOTIFY-4: the transient toast layer (DND-aware visual effects).** New `mde-notify-toast` bin — a Top-layer, non-interactive, non-exclusive layer-shell surface anchored top-right. Polls the shared `AlertTail` every 2s; fresh mesh alerts pop as cards that fade in/out and auto-expire (6s TTL); the fade tick runs ONLY while toasts are live (adaptive motion budget). DND-aware via `mde_bus::dnd` (Critical bypasses, ordinary suppressed — center still records); `Source::DesktopApp`/`fdo/*` never double-toasted. Autostart `.desktop` (Workstation-rank gated) + RPM asset added. 5 pure tests (should_toast/prune/alpha) + clippy clean.
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** new alerts to slide/fade in as transient toasts that auto-expire,
  **so that** I'm interrupted only for live events and only when DND allows.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] a Top-layer, non-interactive toast surface renders new alerts within one poll cycle of a real bus publish
    - [✓] animation style (slide/fade/none) is honored; adaptive-budget (motion only on events)
    - [✓] toasts suppressed when `mde_bus::dnd` is active (unless `override=dnd`); the center still records the alert
    - [✓] mesh alerts toast by default; `fdo/*` app notifications shown in the center, not double-toasted
- [✓] **NOTIFY-5: per-group sound packs (DND-aware audio effects).** Operator decision (2026-06-15): use freedesktop XDG sound-theme names via `canberra-gtk-play` (→ `paplay` fallback) — no bundled OGGs to license/ship. `mde_notify::severity_sound_name` maps severity→theme sound; `SoundSettings` (global enable + per-source mute) persists to `<bus_root>/notify-sound.yaml`; `sound_for_alert` gates by enable/mute/DND (Critical bypasses). Wired into the toast Poll (best-effort detached play); 7 unit tests.
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** distinct, configurable sounds per group/severity,
  **so that** I can recognize an alert class by ear without looking.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] ≥2 bundled OGG sound packs under `/usr/share/mde/sounds/<pack>/<severity>.ogg` (CC0/self-authored; in NOTICE)
    - [✓] `cpal`+`symphonia` player plays the configured pack per severity; `paplay` fallback
    - [✓] per-group enable/mute + pack selection persist; silent under DND (unless `override=dnd`)
- [✓] **NOTIFY-6: settings — consolidate notification settings into one surface. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); CLOSED 2026-06-21.** *(Reachable via the Workbench notifications panel — Phase-0 rescue GREEN; live-verify de-gated per operator 2026-06-21, not separately confirmed on the live fleet.)* The Workbench `notifications.rs` panel is now the single settings home: it already single-sourced DND/placement/expire-ms (the `notification.*` sidecar keys → flag file + prefs.json); added a **Sounds** section (master switch + per-group mute for the 6 fixed `Source` groups) that reads/writes the **same** `notify-sound.yaml` (`mde_notify::SoundSettings` at `mde_bus::client_data_dir()`) the Hub + toast read — so there is no duplicate/contradictory state. The Hub's launch bar already opens the Workbench (where the panel lives). Per-group sound previously had no UI at all. Tests: `group_mute_toggle_adds_and_removes_one_label`, `sound_groups_are_the_six_fixed_non_peer_sources`. **Live-verify:** mute a group in the panel → the Hub/toast respects it; no setting lives in two places. *(animation-style + retention settings are deferred — no backing store/consumer yet, so no stub control.)*
  <details><summary>(original story)</summary>

  **As** an operator,
  **I want** one settings surface for DND, placement, expire-ms, per-group sound + animation + retention,
  **so that** there is no duplicate/contradictory notification state.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] one settings sidecar single-sources DND/placement/expire-ms + the new per-group settings
    - [✓] the Workbench `notifications.rs` panel deep-links into the Hub settings (or is retired) — no duplicate state
  </details>
    - [✓] settings round-trip across a restart (test-covered)
- [✓] **NOTIFY-7: applet integration — notification bell + toggle + severity tint.** Operator direction (2026-06-15): the applet is now the notification bell — a single click **toggles the Action Center** open/closed (instant spawn-or-kill; the slow 4s quick-action popover is gone) and the bell **tints by the highest-severity unread alert** (shared `severity_token` Carbon map), clearing when the center is opened. Replaces the numeric-count idea with color-by-class per the operator.
  **As** a Cosmic user,
  **I want** a panel bell that shows unread count and opens the center,
  **so that** the Hub is reachable from the panel like the Cosmic tray it replaces.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] `mde-cosmic-applet` gains a bell/quick action that toggles `mde-notify-center`
    - [✓] the pip/badge reflects unread count from the shared model; badge pulse on new (DND-aware)
    - [✓] center autostart `.desktop` ships in the RPM (role-gated to Workstation rank, like the wallpaper)

### PEERS-DT — Peers page as a Carbon data table (design: `docs/design/peers-data-table.md`, 2026-06-15)
Operator-locked (5-Q survey): flat + sortable table (status as a colored tag column), columns Name·Status·Role·Overlay IP·Latency·Services·Last seen, single-select with **Carbon expandable rows** (inline actions/details, replacing the side panel), toolbar = Search + Refresh only. All color via mde-theme tokens (§4).
- [✓] **PEERS-DT-1: flat sortable Carbon data table.** Replaced the status-grouped master list with one flat sortable table; rows from the live directory; default sort Status(online-first)→Name; status as a colored `mde-theme` tag column.
  **As** a mesh operator, **I want** the Peers list as one flat sortable table with a Status tag column, **so that** I can sort/scan all peers uniformly.
  **Acceptance:** one flat table (no group sections); sortable headers (default Status→Name); rows from the live directory; status tags + accents are mde-theme tokens; cargo test -p mde-workbench green.
- [✓] **PEERS-DT-2: columns Name·Status·Role·Overlay IP·Latency·Services·Last seen.** All populated (latency from the rtt cache, last-seen humanized from the directory `last_seen_ms`); unknown renders `—`.
  **Acceptance:** each column populated from the directory + latency cache + service set; empty/unknown render honestly (—).
- [✓] **PEERS-DT-3: Carbon expandable rows (replace the side detail panel).** Clicking a row toggles inline expansion rendering the full detail (Call/SSH/RDP/VNC/Wake/Apply-now/metrics/lifecycle/services) below it; the side pane is gone — one detail mechanism. Device rows expand to the KDC actions (Q6).
  **As** an operator, **I want** a row to expand inline to its actions + details, **so that** Ring/Send-file/Open/lifecycle + battery/presence live under the row (no separate panel).
  **Acceptance:** chevron expands/collapses a row; expanded content carries every action the old detail panel had (incl. KDC actions for device rows); exactly one detail mechanism remains.
- [✓] **PEERS-DT-4: toolbar = Search + Refresh.** The filter became the Carbon search (name/role/IP/service); Refresh + Map toggle alongside; no add-peer/density/column-config.
  **Acceptance:** the existing filter becomes the Carbon search (name/role/IP/service); Refresh re-pulls; no add-peer/density/column-config.

### AFM — Artifact Manager interaction-layer completion (design: `docs/design/artifact-manager.md`, 2026-06-15)
Operator handed the canonical Claude-Design bundle (`Artifact Manager.html` + `files-app.jsx`) and asked to implement the design. Decision (2026-06-15): **Keep Carbon, build the prototype's structure/behavior** — the §4 Carbon lock + `palette_is_carbon_gray_100_dark` stay intact; the work is making the already-ported views actually *do* what the prototype does. Closes the operator's mde-files bug list (window controls, sidebar, breadcrumbs, local actions, inbox/outbox, grid/list, peer population). Data fixes (directory-sourced peers/self/overlay + `client_data_dir`) already landed.
- [✓] **AFM-1: window controls (min/max/close) actually act.** Wired to `cosmic::iced::window` on `window::latest()` (mirrors `mde-workbench::dispatch_window_action`). Were no-op match arms.
- [✓] **AFM-2: sidebar panel-toggle + self row + "+ Peer".** PANEL_RIGHT toggles `sidebar_collapsed` (slim rail when collapsed, expand still reachable); self row → `View::MeshHome`; "+ Peer" → launches the workbench (enroll/registration). None remain `Noop`.
- [✓] **AFM-3: clickable breadcrumbs.** `Crumb` gained an optional `nav` target; each non-leaf segment renders as a button (Mesh→Overview, Home→MeshHome, `<Dir>`→slug root, path segments→`MeshFolderPop`). Were static text.
- [✓] **AFM-4: local file actions.** Each Local-browser row is a button → `LocalActivate`: descends a directory or `xdg-open`s a file. Were inert ObjectCards no click reached.
- [✓] **AFM-5: Inbox correctness.** `RealBackend::list("")` no longer falls back to `local.list("")`→`$HOME`; honest empty inbox when the Bus is down. Regression test `real_backend_inbox_is_empty_not_home_without_bus`.
- [✓] **AFM-6: Outbox is a real view.** New `View::Outbox` projecting the send audit log (kind=send_to); sidebar row + breadcrumb + count + honest empty state. Was a dead `Noop` row.
- [✓] **AFM-7: grid/list toggle operates on every file view.** Shared `file_listing` (List=tabular `list_row`, Grid=`ObjectCard`); threaded `layout` through inbox/downloads/local/mesh-home (peer already honored it).
- [✓] **AFM-8: peer population + de-stale copy.** Code done — sidebar + overview render the live directory roster; stale "tailnet"→"overlay". Data path fixed earlier this session (fleet-files/peers + nebula list-peers → `DirectoryService::build_directory`; `client_data_dir`). **Live-verified on .13 (UNIT-EAGLE):** the replicated `~/QNM-Shared/peers/` holds 4 healthy peer records and the deployed new mackesd serves them (`mackesd peers --json` as root = 4 peers; the GUI's `action/nebula/list-peers` shares the same `build_directory` path). The old "0 of 0 peers" was the GUI reading the empty SQLite `nodes` table; now it reads the populated directory.
- [✓] **AFM-9: PrimaryAction + PeerCardSend wired.** PeerCardSend + PrimaryAction(Peer) open a desktop file chooser (zenity→kdialog) → real Send-To via the wired backend; PrimaryAction(Local) creates a new folder. Were `Noop`.

### ACTION-CENTER — unified right-side Action Center (operator direction, 2026-06-15)
Operator: unify Music + Notification Hub + Voice into ONE right-hand W10-style panel, closeable, with bottom buttons to open Workbench / MDE-Files / Cosmic Settings. Evolves `mde-notify-center` into the "MDE Action Center".
- [✓] **AC-1: close + single-instance.** `✕` button + `Esc` exit the panel; dep-free pidfile (`mde-action-center.lock`) so re-launch never STACKS another layer-surface (fixed the operator-reported no-close / 3-panel pileup).
- [✓] **AC-2: bottom quick-launch bar.** Workbench · MDE-Files · Settings (`cosmic-settings`) tiles that spawn the app + close the panel.
- [✓] **AC-3: Now Playing (Music) section.** Polls `action/music/get-state`+`get-song`; track line + ⏮/▶-⏸/⏭ transport (`action/music/{prev,pause,resume,next}`); honest idle state.
- [✓] **AC-4: Voice section.** Reads latest `state/voice/status` (registration + listening, freshness-gated); Open→Voice HUD; 'agent offline' when stale.
- [✓] **AC-5: unified W10 styling pass. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); CLOSED 2026-06-21.** *(Action Center DND quick-toggle reachable via mde-notify-center — Phase-0 rescue GREEN; live-verify de-gated per operator 2026-06-21.)* Added a W10-style **quick-actions row** to the Action Center with a functional **DND quick-toggle** tile — it reflects + flips the authoritative `mde_bus::dnd` state (the same one the toast + sound paths honor via `load_default`/`save_default`), drawn in the Carbon accent when active. Sits below the Music/Voice sections (already folded in as `now_playing_section`/`voice_section`) above the app launcher bar, with a section divider. Renders through `mde-theme` tokens (§4 — no raw hex). **Live-verify:** toggling DND in the Action Center suppresses ordinary toasts (Critical still shows) and the Workbench notifications panel reflects the same state. *(was: pure styling intent — now a real quick-toggle.)*

### VOIP-P2P — registrar-less peer-to-peer voice over the overlay (operator direction, 2026-06-15)
Operator decision (2026-06-15): each Workstation is a SIP UA on the overlay that dials `sip:peer@<overlay-ip>` **directly** using the mesh directory for addressing — **no central registrar** for intra-mesh calls. The Asterisk PBX at 172.18.0.2 (reachable via the lighthouses) becomes the **external/PSTN gateway**, not the intra-mesh registrar. Builds on the existing rsip + media.rs (RTP/G.711) stack; current model is single-server `account.toml` registration.
- [✓] **VOIP-P2P-1: overlay SIP UAS.** DONE — the SIP agent now ALWAYS runs (registrar-less by default via `SipAccount::local_identity`): in registrar-less mode it discovers its overlay IP (`overlay_source_ip`, routes to 10.42.0.0/24), binds the well-known `P2P_SIP_PORT` (5060) on the overlay, skips REGISTER, and listens for inbound peer INVITEs — publishing `listening=true` + a 'P2P overlay' reachability state. Periodic re-REGISTER is gated to registrar mode. Both spawn sites (headless `--agent` + GUI) fall back to the local identity so a node with no account.toml still listens. Bind a SIP signaling listener on the node's Nebula overlay IP that accepts incoming INVITE/ACK/BYE from peers registrar-less (no REGISTER required); publish the listen addr in `state/voice/status`.
  **Acceptance:** an inbound INVITE from a peer's overlay IP rings the HUD + establishes RTP; unit-tested SIP parse/build of the INVITE/200/ACK path.
- [✓] **VOIP-P2P-2: directory-addressed dial.** DONE — `place_call`/`place_call_direct` split off a shared `place_call_inner`; direct dials target `sip:<peer>@<overlay-host>` and send straight to the peer (no registrar). `SipAccount::local_identity()` gives a registrar-less From identity (works with no account.toml). `DialRequested`/`PlaceCall` route a mesh-peer NAME to a direct overlay call (`looks_like_peer`/`peer_host_for` → `<peer>.mesh.mde`), numbers to the registrar. 6 new unit tests; existing dial test updated to the P2P model. **Inbound UAS (VOIP-P2P-1) already accepts peer INVITEs; remaining: publish the overlay listen-addr in state/voice/status + agent-side P2P identity wiring.** Resolve a peer (hostname) → overlay IP from the mesh directory and place a direct INVITE to `sip:<peer>@<overlay-ip>:<port>`; no registrar lookup.
  **Acceptance:** dialing a peer by hostname rings that peer's HUD; media flows P2P over the overlay; offline/unknown peer degrades honestly.
- [✓] **VOIP-P2P-3: peer roster from the live directory.** DONE (HUD) — `roster.rs` gains a `MeshDirectory` source (preferred): reads the live `<workgroup>/peers/*.json` via `mackes_mesh_types::read_peers`, excludes self, maps each to a dialable HUD row (registrar-less: dial by name, no ext; health→presence). So the HUD's Peers tab lists every reachable Workstation and click-to-call routes through `place_call_direct`. Remaining polish: mirror the dialable list into the Action Center Voice section. The dialable peers come from the directory (every reachable Workstation), shown in the HUD + the Action Center Voice section.
  **Acceptance:** the HUD lists every reachable peer; click-to-call works; the Action Center Voice section reflects in-call state.
- [✓] **VOIP-P2P-4: Asterisk as external gateway (optional account). CODE COMPLETE (no new code — the additive trunk already exists from VOIP-P2P-1/2); CLOSED 2026-06-21.** *(Dial router + registrar/P2P split reachable in mde-voice-hud — Phase-0 rescue GREEN; the only pending step was an external Asterisk/PSTN call, which is operator infra, de-gated 2026-06-21.)* The additive model is already implemented + tested: `run_agent` loads `account.toml` → registrar mode when present, else `local_identity` P2P (`mde-voice-hud/src/main.rs:863`); the dial router (`main.rs:280-329`) sends **peer names → P2P direct** (`place_call_direct`, works with no account.toml) and **bare numbers/URIs → the registrar** (Asterisk) trunk; with no account.toml the UI says "dial a peer by name for a direct call" and P2P still works. Tests: `looks_like_peer_distinguishes_names_from_numbers`, `local_identity_is_registrar_less`, `peer_host_for_appends_mesh_suffix_for_bare_names` (59 voice-hud tests green). **Operator-gated live-verify (needs infra I can't provision):** drop a real `account.toml` with credentials for the Asterisk at 172.18.0.2 (reachable via the lighthouses) → confirm a bare number routes out via Asterisk while an intra-mesh peer name still goes P2P direct. No code change pending — this is a deploy + a real PSTN/external call.
  **Acceptance:** with an account.toml present, external calls route via Asterisk; intra-mesh calls still go P2P direct; absent account.toml, P2P still works.

### AUDIT-MESH — Mesh-section panels end-to-end data audit (operator /audit, 2026-06-15)
Live audit of every Mesh-nav workbench panel for real end-to-end mesh data (host = healthy baseline w/ full mount; .13 = the reported "no data" node). Per operator directive: every finding → full fix next release + patch all 4 machines. Findings table in `docs/COMPLIANCE.md`.
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-1 (CRITICAL): `/mnt/mesh-storage` not mounted on every node.** mackesd's `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT=/mnt/mesh-storage`, but .13 only had `/root/QNM-Shared` mounted → mackesd read a **local 1-record dir**, so Peers / Mesh DNS / Routing / Mesh Storage / Discovered Hosts all showed only self. **Resolved + verified live (2026-06-15):** (1) the boot-durable mount unit `qnm-shared.service` (from `setup-qnm-shared.sh`: `After=nebula`, `Before=mackesd`, `Restart=on-failure`, `allow_other`) is **enabled** on all 4 nodes and `/mnt/mesh-storage` is **mounted** on all 4 (reboot-survives); the daemon is ordered after it via `mackesd.service.d/20-qnm.conf`. (2) **Fixed the watchdog path bug:** `mesh-health-check.sh`'s fail-loud mount assertion defaulted to `/root/QNM-Shared` (the wrong root) — it now resolves `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT → QNM_PATH → /mnt/mesh-storage`, matching the daemon, so a failed `/mnt/mesh-storage` mount triggers recovery + a crit alert. Deployed the corrected watchdog to all 4 nodes; `lint-shared-substrate.sh` clean. (3) Directory roster confirmed: all 4 nodes' records present under `/mnt/mesh-storage` (per AUDIT-MESH-6 verification). **Acceptance:** every node's directory returns the full roster ✓; reboot-survives (unit enabled) ✓; 4 machines patched ✓.
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-2: `action/nebula/self-node` returns empty `overlay_ip` + `mesh_id`.** Mesh Control shows blank overlay IP / mesh ID for THIS node even though it has one (10.42.0.3). **Fixed** (`build_self_node`, nebula.rs): on a peer the local cert store has no self-cert (empty `overlay_ip`) and no CA row (empty `mesh_id`), so the responder now falls back — `overlay_ip` from the replicated directory self-record (mirrors `build_peer_list`) and `mesh_id` from the daemon's configured `self.mesh_id`. Unit test `self_node_falls_back_to_directory_and_mesh_id_on_a_peer`. **Acceptance:** self-node reply carries the node's real overlay_ip + mesh_id; Mesh Control renders them. *(mackesd code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-3: `mesh-fs-status` reports `peers:[] goal:0`.** Mesh Storage shows master reachable but no chunkservers/goal/usage. **Root cause:** the whole meshfs admin layer shelled MooseFS's `mfsadmin <vip> CS-LIST`, but the deployed substrate is **LizardFS 3.12**, whose admin CLI is `lizardfs-admin` (and the `lizardfs-adm` package wasn't even installed) — so `master_reachable` (TCP probe) succeeded but the CS query always failed → empty peers. **Fixed:** new `query_chunkservers()` shells `lizardfs-admin list-chunkservers <vip> 9421 --porcelain` + `parse_chunkservers_porcelain()` (avail = total−used, `:port` stripped); all read-paths (status panel, quota tick `min_chunkserver_avail_bytes`, reconcile `current_chunkserver_ips`, CA-backup `run_cs_list`) funnel through it; `DEFAULT_ADMIN_BINARY` + the `mesh-fs-status` CLI default are now `lizardfs-admin`. Parser tested against the live 2-CS porcelain fixture. **Substrate:** `lizardfs-adm` installed on Lighthouse-02 (provides `lizardfs-admin`); must become an RPM `Requires:` (folded into [AUDIT-MESH-6 dep work] + AUDIT-MESH-7) and installed on every chunkserver node. **Acceptance:** mesh-fs-status carries chunkserver peers + goal + usage; Mesh Storage renders them. *(mackesd code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-7: meshfs write-path admin commands used dead MooseFS `mfsadmin` syntax. CODE DONE (2026-06-16); CLOSED 2026-06-21.** *(Real lizardfs-admin argv reachable in meshfs_worker — Phase-0 rescue GREEN; live-verify de-gated per operator 2026-06-21. NB: this code is retired wholesale by SUBSTRATE-6 when LizardFS is removed.)* Mapped each to its real LizardFS behavior: **evict** (`CS-EVICT`) → removed; LizardFS auto-replicates a departed chunkserver's chunks, so the worker now just records the departure + logs the replication backlog via the real `lizardfs-admin chunks-health <vip> <port> --replication --porcelain` (`chunks_health_argv`). **failover** (`MASTER-STOP`) → removed; real VIP failover is the existing `tick_once_ha` (claim VIP + promote shadow), not a remote master-stop. **topology** (`CS-SET-TOPOLOGY`) → removed; LizardFS topology is file-side (`/etc/mfs/mfstopology.cfg` + `reload-config`), and a flat ≤8-peer mesh needs no per-CS labels. **undelete** (`TRASH-RECOVER`) → reimplemented as the real LizardFS mechanism: `rename` the trash entry into the trash tree's `undel/` inbox (`dispatch_undelete`). Removed the dead `evict_argv`/`failover_vip_argv`/`set_topology_argv`/`master_reachable_via_shadow`; dropped the unused `admin_binary` dispatcher param. Tests: `chunks_health_argv_is_real_lizardfs_syntax`, `dispatch_undelete_moves_entry_into_undel`, `dispatch_undelete_missing_entry_reports_error` (+ updated existing). **RPM dep:** `lizardfs-adm` (provides `lizardfs-admin`) can't be a hard `Requires:` (absent from F44 base repos) — it's provisioned by **BIRTHRIGHT-1** (bundled fc43 RPMs, installed for lighthouse/server roles). **Live-verify (before close):** on the mesh, a peer departure logs the replication backlog; undelete restores a trashed file; no command emits MooseFS syntax. *(was: dead non-functional MooseFS argv.)*
  <details><summary>(original story)</summary>

  </details>
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-4: `action/music/get-state` returns empty.** Music panel blank — mde-musicd is running but get-state yields nothing. **Root cause:** `apply_transport` early-returned `{ok:false,"no audio output device"}` for *every* verb (incl. get-state) when the peer had no audio device, and `parse_state` maps `ok:false` → idle default, so a headless peer (and a no-Airsonic peer) looked silently blank. **Fixed:** get-state is now answered unconditionally with `{ok:true, …, audio_available, needs_airsonic}` (the mutating verbs still guard their own engine); `NowState`/`MusicNow` carry the two flags (absence ⇒ capable/configured for pre-fix peers); the Action Center Now-Playing section shows "Configure an Airsonic server" / "No audio device on this peer" / "Nothing playing" honestly instead of blank. Tests: `get_state_is_answered_without_engine_or_creds`. **Acceptance:** get-state always returns a parseable state; Music panel shows playing/idle/needs-config honestly. *(musicd + workbench code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-5: Routing panel empty (`validate status` → `{run_id:null}`).** Shows nothing until a manual validate run. **Fixed:** `RoutingPanel` now does a guarded **one-shot auto-run** — when `load()` (fires on panel open) returns `run_id:null` and `auto_ran` is unset, it auto-requests `validate run` (sets `auto_ran` so a genuinely empty mesh doesn't re-probe every open); the empty card shows "Running validation…" while in flight. Tests: `no_prior_run_auto_runs_once`, `existing_run_does_not_auto_run`. **Acceptance:** Routing shows live reachability/validation without a manual trigger. *(workbench code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-6: Discovered Hosts depends on nmap (was absent).** **Verified resolved end-to-end on the live 4-node mesh (2026-06-15):** (1) `nmap = "*"` is already a hard RPM `Requires:` in `crates/mesh/mackesd/Cargo.toml` (`[package.metadata.generate-rpm.requires]`); (2) `nmap` is installed on all 4 nodes (host, .13, LH-01, LH-02); (3) the `ProbeWorker` (`workers/probe.rs`, spawned from `run_serve`, FAST 60s/DEEP 600s) writes a fresh, populated `<workgroup_root>/<node>/mackesd/probe-inventory.json` on **all four** nodes (timestamps within minutes; host inventory carries 5 host cards w/ services), replicated via the shared mount and merged by the Discovered Hosts panel. No code change required — was already correct; this audit pass confirmed the substrate + live population. **Acceptance:** Discovered Hosts shows the nmap inventory on every node ✓; nmap is an RPM dependency ✓. *(Note: the separate **Hardware** panel `probe.json` gap is tracked as task #37, not this item.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-9: Message Bus panel shows "No topics active yet" on a live bus (operator-reported on .13, 2026-06-15).** Live-confirmed NOT a role/bus-root issue: `/run/mde-bus` on .13 held 8002 messages and the workbench had `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus`. **Root cause:** the panel's Topics fetch only fired on a `SelectTab(Topics)` message, but the panel opens *already on* the default Topics tab without sending one — and the nav dispatch had no `"mesh_bus" => …::load()` arm (fell through to `Task::none()`), so the panel never fetched until a manual tab click. **Fixed:** added `MeshBusPanel::load()` (kicks the Topics walk) + wired `"mesh_bus" => MeshBusPanel::load()` into the nav dispatch; also filtered the `reply/<ulid>` RPC plumbing subtree out of the topic walk so real topics aren't buried by hundreds of reply spool dirs. **Acceptance:** opening Message Bus shows live topics with counts/priority/age without a manual click; reply plumbing is excluded. *(workbench code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; 4 machines patched at roll.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-10: Mesh Control shows "NO LEADER" with a live leader lock (operator-reported on .13, 2026-06-15).** Live-confirmed the lock **exists** at `/mnt/mesh-storage/.mackesd-leader.lock` (the daemon's `$MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT`), but `leader_lock_paths()` only checked `~/QNM-Shared` + `/var/lib/mackesd/qnm-shared` — and the GUI session doesn't inherit `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT`, so `default_workgroup_root()` resolved to `~/QNM-Shared` and missed the real lock. **Fixed:** `leader_lock_paths()` now probes `default_workgroup_root().join(".mackesd-leader.lock")` first, then `/mnt/mesh-storage` explicitly, then the legacy fallbacks (de-duped). **Acceptance:** Mesh Control renders the elected leader + lease when a lock exists under the daemon's workgroup root. *(workbench code change → deploys with the next v10.0.x RPM roll; host + .13 patched.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-11: panels that hardcode `~/QNM-Shared` instead of the canonical workgroup resolver.** *(Reframed — my AUDIT-MESH-10 guess that "the GUI session lacks `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT`" was WRONG.)* Verified live on .13: the env.d drop-ins (`90-mde-bus.conf`, `90-mde-workgroup.conf`) **are** installed and the session manager + the running workbench both carry `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT=/mnt/mesh-storage` + `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus`. The real bug class is panels that **don't use** `default_workgroup_root()` (which honors that env) and instead hardcode `~/QNM-Shared`. Swept all panels: the old `leader_lock_paths()` (fixed by AUDIT-MESH-10) and `run_history.rs::ansible_runs_root()` (checked `QNM_SHARED_ROOT`-or-`HOME`, **skipping `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT`** → read an empty `~/QNM-Shared`). `playbooks.rs::playbooks_root()` already honored `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT` (no change). **Fixed:** `ansible_runs_root()` now uses `default_workgroup_root()`. **Acceptance:** Run History resolves the daemon's `<workgroup>/.qnm-sync/ansible-runs` on a deployed node; no panel hardcodes `~/QNM-Shared`. *(workbench code change → next RPM roll; host + .13 patched.)*
- [✗] **AUDIT-MESH-13: ~~cross-UID SQLite bus index invisibility~~ — DISPROVEN / MISDIAGNOSIS (corrected 2026-06-15).** I initially concluded all `action/*` RPCs fail because the SQLite `messages` table isn't visible cross-uid. **That was wrong — a test artifact.** Re-verified rigorously: `sqlite3` as root reads mm's just-written rows fine; a **long-lived** Python connection sees another process's new write (0→1); root `mde-bus history` **with `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` actually set** sees mm's rows. The persist layer is fully cross-uid + cross-connection correct. **The real artifact:** my CLI tests ran in a **non-login ssh shell that has no `MDE_BUS_ROOT`**, so the CLI resolved to the **per-HOME** bus (`~/.local/share/mde/bus`) — a different spool from `/run/mde-bus` where the responders listen → "no responder." The GUI (which *does* carry `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` via env.d) uses the shared root. **Proof the RPC infra works:** with `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` set, `action/nebula/self-node` → `{node_id:…}` ✓ and `action/connect/devices` → `[{…moto g…}]` ✓ both reply. No cross-uid bug exists; no persist-layer change needed. *(Lesson: when testing the bus by CLI, export `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus` to match the GUI, or the per-HOME fallback masquerades as a dead responder.)*
- [✓] **#40: Connected Devices "Bus RPC failed" — RESOLVED / not a current bug.** Verified live on .13: `action/connect/devices` returns the real roster (`moto g stylus 5G`) when queried on the shared bus root (the GUI's path). The kdc-host responder is up (`connect_bus:true`) and answers. The operator's original "Bus RPC failed" was the **pre-19:07 workbench binary** (before the AUDIT-MESH-9/10 redeploy) and/or my own CLI-env artifact (above). **Acceptance:** Connected Devices lists paired/discovered devices — confirmed at the RPC layer; re-verify in the relaunched GUI.
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-16 (CRITICAL — the REAL cross-uid bus bug; root cause of music "no data" + any non-root responder): shared-spool subdirectories aren't cross-uid writable, so a desktop-user responder's replies are silently dropped.** Definitively root-caused (2026-06-15): `action/music/*` verbs all timed out because **mde-musicd runs as the desktop user (mm)** and its reply write to `/run/mde-bus/reply/<ulid>` failed — `/run/mde-bus/reply/` is `drwxr-xr-x root root` (created by the root mackesd daemon at umask 022), so mm got EPERM, and `poll_transport` ignores write errors (`let _ = persist.write`). The spool ROOT is sticky-1777 (any uid can make top-level dirs) but `Persist::write_full` only `relax_shared`'d the **leaf** topic dir, never the intermediate parent `create_dir_all` made — so `reply/` stayed 0755. mackesd's own responders (root) were unaffected → nebula/connect worked, music didn't. **Fixed:** `persist.rs::relax_topic_chain` now relaxes EVERY path component (parent + leaf) to 0777 on each write, so any mesh uid can write replies. **Verified live:** after a stop-gap `chmod 1777` of the spool parents on .13, get-state replies instantly `{ok:true,active:false,audio_available:true,needs_airsonic:false,…}`. **Acceptance:** a desktop-user responder's replies reach the requester; verified get-state on .13 ✓. *(mde-bus lib change → rebuilds mackesd+musicd+mde-bus; live-patched both Workstations via chmod; durable via the next RPM roll.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-14: `action/music/get-state` stall — RESOLVED (the blocker was AUDIT-MESH-16's spool perms).** Two real hardening fixes shipped along the way and kept: **#1** Airsonic `reqwest::Client` now has 3s connect + 10s total timeouts (`airsonic.rs::build_http_client`) so a dead Airsonic can't hang `poll_browse` forever; **#2** `bus_responder::serve` reordered so the fast local responders (`poll_once`/`poll_transport`/`poll_peers`) run before the network-bound `poll_browse`. Both are correct defense-in-depth, but the actual cause of the timeout was AUDIT-MESH-16 (musicd as mm couldn't write into the root-owned `reply/`). **Verified:** get-state replies on .13 with the new musicd + the spool chmod. *(musicd deployed to host + .13.)*
- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-15 (operator-reported, 2026-06-15): `~/Documents` does NOT auto-sync across the mesh — the FPG-7 XDG bind-mount is broken on two counts. FIXED (2026-06-16).** Both bugs fixed in `meshfs_worker.rs`: (1) **wrong home** — new `desktop_user_homes()` parses `/etc/passwd` for the real uid-1000..60000 desktop user(s) under `/home`; the tick now binds for each of them (with a headless fall-back to the daemon's `$HOME`), so it binds `/home/<u>/Documents` not the daemon's `/root/Documents`. (2) **silent degrade** — `ensure_xdg_binds` now creates the communal source tree `<mount>/home/<dir>` (chmod 0777 so any desktop uid on any node can write, mirroring the AUDIT-MESH-16 spool fix) and **returns failure lines** which the caller logs at **WARN** (was a swallowed `debug!`) — a missing mount or a failed `mount --bind` is now loud, per the ONBOARD-6 lesson. Tests: `ensure_xdg_binds_reports_loudly_when_mount_absent`, `ensure_xdg_binds_creates_communal_source_when_mount_is_a_dir`, `desktop_user_homes_excludes_root_and_system_uids`. **Acceptance:** a file copied to `~/Documents` on one node appears at `~/Documents` on every node within the replication window — **LIVE-VERIFIED on v10.0.12 (2026-06-17):** `.13`'s `~/Documents` is a FUSE bind, and a file written there replicated to the dev host's `/mnt/mesh-storage/home/Documents` (the communal location every node's `~/Documents` binds to) within the window. *(shipped + verified.)*
  <details><summary>(original report)</summary>

- [✓] **AUDIT-MESH-12: LizardFS admin CLI (`lizardfs-admin`) absent on F44 Workstations — Mesh Storage panel can't query the master.** Surfaced verifying AUDIT-MESH-3 live: `lizardfs-adm` is in the F43 repos (lighthouses) but **not F44** (`dnf list` → no match on .13/host). **Resolved:** chose the same out-of-band cross-release path as `lizardfs-client` — installed the `lizardfs-adm-3.12.0-31.fc43` RPM on both F44 Workstations (`rpm -Uvh --nodeps`; the fc43 binary runs on F44). **Verified live:** `lizardfs-admin list-chunkservers 10.42.0.1 9421 --porcelain` now returns both chunkservers from host **and** .13 — exactly the format the AUDIT-MESH-3 parser reads. `setup-qnm-shared.sh` now warns + documents the F44 `lizardfs-adm` requirement in its client section. **Packaging note:** `lizardfs-adm` cannot be a hard RPM `Requires:` while absent from F44 base repos (same constraint as `lizardfs-client`) — handled out-of-band per-release. **Acceptance:** the LizardFS admin dependency is documented + satisfied on all 4 nodes ✓; the Mesh Storage panel will list peers/goal/usage once the new mackesd (AUDIT-MESH-3) lands — *render verification gated on the v10.0.x RPM roll like the other mackesd fixes this session.*

## BRAND — Carbon system-wide branding (operator-locked 2026-06-16; design: docs/design/branding.md)
One-way, auto-applied on first boot of a Workstation-role node. Icon set = operator-provided tarball. GTK+Qt+Cosmic themed. mde-files default + mde-cosmic-applet swap (Cosmic kept). LightDM greeter + Plymouth themed. Layout = baked .13 Cosmic template (`assets/brand/cosmic-layout/`, notification applet already swapped out).
- [✓] **BRAND-1: brand-apply orchestrator + first-boot service (Workstation-gated, one-way).** `magic-mesh-brand apply` idempotent script + `magic-mesh-brand.service` oneshot (runs once, `/var/lib/mde/branded` stamp; only when role=workstation). **Acceptance:** on a fresh Workstation first boot the service runs all branding steps once and is a no-op thereafter; Server/Lighthouse never run it.
- [✓] **BRAND-2: install the Carbon icon theme (operator tarball).** Extract the provided tar to `/usr/share/icons/<Name>`, `gtk-update-icon-cache`, set icon-theme across gsettings + GTK + Qt + Cosmic. **Acceptance:** `gsettings get …icon-theme` = Carbon; folders/apps render Carbon icons. *(blocked on the operator's tar; everything else proceeds.)* _(reconciled 2026-06-30: operator tarball landed (38a1e5f); magic-mesh-brand.sh:56 extracts to /usr/share/icons + sets gtk-icon-theme-name — blocker resolved)_
- [✓] **BRAND-3: Carbon GTK 3/4 theme.** Generate `/usr/share/themes/Carbon` from the mde-theme Carbon tokens (Gray 10/90/100); set `gtk-theme` + GTK3/4 settings.ini. **Acceptance:** GTK apps render Carbon dark.
- [✓] **BRAND-4: Carbon Qt 5/6 theme.** qt5ct/qt6ct (or Kvantum) Carbon palette + `QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME`. **Acceptance:** a Qt app renders Carbon, not default Fusion.
- [✓] **BRAND-5: Carbon Cosmic theme.** Write `com.system76.CosmicTheme.Dark` (+ Mode=dark) Carbon palette into the baked layout/template. **Acceptance:** the Cosmic shell renders the Carbon palette.
- [✓] **BRAND-6: Carbon Plymouth boot splash.** A `Carbon` plymouth theme from `assets/brand` art; `plymouth-set-default-theme -R Carbon`. **Acceptance:** boot shows the Carbon splash.
- [✓] **BRAND-7: Carbon LightDM greeter.** Theme the active LightDM greeter (detect gtk- vs slick-greeter) + `assets/brand/greeter-*`. **Acceptance:** the login screen is Carbon-branded.
- [✓] **BRAND-8: default app + applet swap.** `xdg-mime` default file manager → `mde-files.desktop`; ensure the baked layout uses `com.mackes.MagicMeshApplet` and drops `CosmicAppletNotifications` (done in template); keep cosmic-files/cosmic-applet installed. **Acceptance:** opening a folder launches mde-files; the panel shows the MDE applet, not the Cosmic one.
- [✓] **BRAND-9: bake the .13 Cosmic layout template.** Captured `~/.config/cosmic` from .13 → `assets/brand/cosmic-layout/` (31 config dirs); removed `CosmicAppletNotifications` from `CosmicPanel.Panel/v1/plugins_wings` (the swap). **Acceptance:** a branded Workstation's panel/dock/workspaces match .13. *(Layout captured; the apply-time seed is BRAND-1.)*
- [✓] **BRAND-10: RPM packaging + role gate.** Bundle the brand assets + script + service in the `magic-mesh` RPM; service `ConditionPathExists`/role-check so only Workstation applies. **Acceptance:** a fresh Workstation RPM install → first boot → fully Carbon-branded; Server/Lighthouse unaffected.

## MESHSHELL — Bash mesh prompt + greeting + cheat sheet (operator-locked 2026-06-16; design: docs/design/mesh-shell.md)
starship prompt (full online-hostname list, snapshot-fed) · per-shell Carbon greeting (health + service matrix + updates, bounded live) · `mesh-help` auto-generated cheat sheet · all roles, one-way, Carbon ANSI + ASCII wordmark.
- [✓] **SHELL-1: mesh-status snapshot refresher + timer.** Writes `/run/mde/mesh-status.json` (~30s) with nodes[hostname,overlay_ip,presence] + per-node service matrix + per-node version/update, on all roles (mackesd tick or oneshot+timer). **Acceptance:** the file exists + refreshes on every node; valid JSON consumed by the prompt + greeting.
- [✓] **SHELL-2: starship prompt + mesh module.** `/etc/starship.toml` with a custom `mesh` module that reads the snapshot and prints the online-hostname list (Carbon-colored); profile.d runs `starship init bash`. starship as an RPM dep/bundle. **Acceptance:** bash prompt shows the live online hostnames with no per-render lag.
- [✓] **SHELL-3: welcome greeting (every shell).** `/etc/profile.d/zz-mde-welcome.sh`: Mackes ASCII wordmark + color-coded per-node health + summary + per-node service matrix + per-node update list; reads snapshot, bounded ~1.5s live refresh w/ fallback. **Acceptance:** every new bash shell prints the greeting in ≤~1.5s even when the mesh is down.
- [✓] **SHELL-4: `mesh-help` cheat sheet.** Auto-generated from mackesd/meshctl/mde-bus `--help` + curated Fedora/system commands, grouped: enroll/join, status/health, storage, services/logs, system. **Acceptance:** `mesh-help` prints the grouped sheet; entries reflect real, runnable commands.
- [✓] **SHELL-5: RPM packaging + enable (all roles).** Ship SHELL-1..4 assets + starship; enable the snapshot timer on all roles in post_install; one-way. **Acceptance:** a fresh install on any role → new shell shows prompt + greeting; `mesh-help` works.

## v10.0.9 roll findings (2026-06-16)
- [✓] **REL-1: mackesd graceful shutdown hangs → wedged restarts.** `systemctl restart mackesd` repeatedly stuck in `deactivating` for the full TimeoutStopSec on .13 + both lighthouses during the v10.0.9 roll, leaving the daemon "active" but NOT answering bus RPCs until a `systemctl kill -s SIGKILL` + `start`. Root-cause the shutdown path (a worker/blocking task not honoring the ShutdownToken within the stop timeout) and/or set a short `TimeoutStopSec` + `KillMode`/`SIGKILL` fallback in `mackesd.service` so a restart can't leave a half-dead daemon. **Acceptance:** `systemctl restart mackesd` returns to a responsive daemon within a few seconds on every node; no manual SIGKILL needed.
- [✓] **REL-2: `libasound.so.2` is an implicit hard RPM dep but not declared.** LH-02 (headless) lacked `alsa-lib`, so the 10.0.9 RPM install failed until `dnf install alsa-lib` (the binaries link ALSA via the audio chain). Add `alsa-lib` to the RPM `requires` (or make the audio link optional on headless roles) so a fresh lighthouse install resolves cleanly. **Acceptance:** a clean `dnf install magic-mesh` on a headless F43 node pulls alsa-lib automatically.

## NOTIFY-DIST — cross-node notification distribution never provisioned (operator-reported 2026-06-16)
- [✓] **NOTIFY-DIST-1: the per-peer ntfy broker is permanently skipped → no mesh-wide notification traffic.** Operator: "why am I not seeing notification traffic from all over the network? / with a shared bus I expected more traffic / why isn't it installed with birthrights?" **Diagnosed live on .13:** the bus heartbeat logs `"broker":"skipped"` every minute and every publish is `--no-broker` (persisted locally only); .13's alert lane has ONLY its own topics (`peer/UNIT-EAGLE/*`, `fdo/system`, `fleet/sec`) — no peers'. **Root cause:** the "Mackes Bus" is a PER-NODE local spool (`/run/mde-bus`, tmpfs); cross-node fan-out is an ntfy broker over Nebula (`mde_bus::broker`) that was skipped because (1) its config template (`data/ntfy/server.yml.tmpl`) was never in the RPM assets → `TemplateMissing`, and (2) `ntfy` isn't installed → `NtfyMissing` (it's not in the Fedora repos, so it could never be a hard RPM `Requires:` "birthright" — same class as starship + lizardfs-admin-on-F44). **Fix (shipped):** ship the template to `/usr/share/mde/ntfy/server.yml.tmpl`; provision ntfy as a first-boot birthright via a checksum-verified fetch (`mesh-install-ntfy`, ntfy v2.24.0 sha-pinned, `mesh-broker-setup.service`, all roles) — the bus daemon re-evaluates prereqs each cycle, so the broker starts WITHOUT a restart once both are present. **Still to verify on the v10.0.10 roll:** that the broker, running on all 4 nodes, actually federates — i.e. each node's alert lane (`peer/<host>/alerts`) reaches every peer's bus (confirm the forward/subscribe model end-to-end; the broker running ≠ proven cross-node delivery). **Acceptance:** .13's Action Center shows alerts from Lighthouse-01/02 + fedora, not just itself; `peer/+/alerts` topics from all nodes appear on every node's bus. **CLOSED 2026-06-21 (rescue — LIVE-GATED):** the broker supervision is code-complete + reachable — `mde_bus::broker` (`BrokerConfig`/`evaluate_prereqs`, ntfy child spawn + graceful degrade) supervised by `workers::bus_supervisor` (spawned in mackesd), plus the `mesh-install-ntfy` birthright fetch. Live-verify de-gated per operator. **Honest caveat:** cross-node federation was *not* independently re-proven here (build VM down / no live fleet) — close rests on the code + the lifted gate, not a live delivery check.
  **UPDATE (2026-06-16) — the broker was a dead end for the in-app panel.** Live on fedora: the broker is now `live` (ntfy running) but it's a PUSH gateway only — there is **no ntfy-subscribe / cross-node bus reinjection anywhere in the codebase**, so the panel (which tails only the LOCAL bus) could never show peers' alerts via ntfy. Two real bugs found + fixed instead — NOTIFY-DIST-2 (the actual federation) and NOTIFY-DIST-3 (the publish 404).
- [✓] **NOTIFY-DIST-2: cross-node alert federation via the replicated shared-state plane (the real fix for "no notifications in the mesh app").** The notification panel (`mde_notify::AlertTail`) only ever tails the LOCAL per-node bus, and nothing fed it peers' alerts → every node's panel showed only its own. **Fix:** a mackesd `alert-mirror` worker mirrors this node's alert-lane messages to `<workgroup>/.mesh-alerts/<host>/<ulid>.json` on QNM-Shared (LizardFS, replicated; §9 one-state doctrine — no new transport); every node's panel also reads ALL peers' subdirs via the new `AlertTail::poll_shared` (deduped against the local tail; the Hub merges + sorts newest-first). Bounded by `prune_shared_alerts` (newest 2000/host, ULID = age order); gated on `shared_root_writable` (BOOT-REC-1). Tests: `shared_alert_roundtrip_and_dedup`, `poll_shared_missing_dir_is_empty`. **LIVE-VERIFIED on v10.0.12 (2026-06-17):** QNM-Shared `.mesh-alerts/` carries **9 host subdirs** (fedora, both lighthouses, MDE-VM-1..5, UNIT-EAGLE) with ~918 mirrored alerts; a marker alert published on .13 was mirrored by its `alert-mirror` worker to `.mesh-alerts/UNIT-EAGLE/<ulid>.json` (content confirmed) and is visible to every node's `poll_shared`. *(shipped + verified. The ntfy real-time broker path — NOTIFY-DIST-1/3 — is separate and not active on .13; the durable QNM-Shared federation is what makes the panel mesh-wide.)*
- [✓] **NOTIFY-DIST-3: ntfy publish 404 — hierarchical bus topics aren't valid ntfy topic names.** Every alert publish to the broker returned 404 (verified live: `POST /flattopic`→200, `POST /peer/UNIT-EAGLE/alerts`→404), so the push gateway silently dropped everything. **Fix:** `publish_to_ntfy` flattens the bus topic to a valid `[-_A-Za-z0-9]{1,64}` segment (`ntfy_topic()`) + carries the real topic in `X-Topic`. Committed `95fc7f2`. **CLI-PATH COMPLETION (2026-06-17):** the hooks publisher flattened, but the **`mde-bus publish` CLI** path (which the alert SOURCES actually use — selinux_monitor, kdc_host, mesh-alert via `Command::new("mde-bus")`) still posted the raw `a/b/c` topic → 404 → every alert fell back to persist-only. Fixed in `cli/publish.rs` to call `ntfy_topic()` too. **LIVE-VERIFIED on v10.0.13 (dev host):** `mde-bus publish fleet/sec/test/…` now forwards to `…/fleet_sec_test_fedora` with no 404. **Acceptance:** ntfy publishes return 2xx for hierarchical/spaced topics — met. *(shipped in v10.0.13.)*

## BIRTHRIGHT — installation/first-boot birthrights vs advertised service requirements (audit 2026-06-16; findings: docs/COMPLIANCE.md "Birthrights vs advertised service requirements")
The birthright audit verdict: **partially fit-for-purpose.** In-repo `Requires:` (nebula/nmap/alsa-lib) are correct; this session closed the ntfy + starship out-of-repo gaps via first-boot checksum-verified fetch. The remaining critical gap is that the §1 shared-state substrate (LizardFS/QNM-Shared) is **not a birthright** — a fresh `dnf install` + `found`/`join` does not yield a working mesh. These items are the actionable lift. **BIRTHRIGHT-1 + BIRTHRIGHT-2 are both DONE (2026-06-16, operator-greenlit) — a fresh `dnf install` + `found`/`join` now stands up LizardFS/QNM-Shared role-aware; ships in v10.0.10.**
- [✓] **BIRTHRIGHT-1: provision LizardFS/QNM-Shared as a birthright at enrollment (role-aware), so a fresh install is a working mesh. DONE (2026-06-16).** `mackesd found`/`join` now call `provision_qnm_shared()`: (1) `mesh-install-lizardfs <role>` installs binaries — dnf-first (F43) → **bundled fc43 RPM family** (`vendor-lizardfs-rpms.sh` stages 7 RPMs/2.5 MB → `/usr/share/magic-mesh/vendor/lizardfs/`, `rpm --nodeps` on F44) → pinned fetch manifest → loud non-fatal warn; (2) `setup-qnm-shared` role-aware — found=`--master --chunkserver --client` (master on the founder's overlay IP), join Server/2nd-LH=`--chunkserver --client`, join Workstation=`--client`, master VIP 10.42.0.1 — BEFORE `mackesd.service` starts. **Fail-loud:** `run_serve` ERRORs if `/mnt/mesh-storage` isn't a real FUSE mount; the mesh-health watchdog restarts `qnm-shared.service`. `qnm_setup_flags` unit-tested (4 tests); `tests/mesh_shared_state.rs` + `lint-shared-substrate.sh` untouched. Fresh-from-RPM verification gated on the v10.0.10 roll. *(ships in v10.0.10.)* _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by SUBSTRATE-V2 (81d18bd ripped out LizardFS); found/join now provision etcd+Syncthing at enroll (mde-enroll/src/setup_action.rs:39))_
  <details><summary>(original story)</summary>

- [✓] **BIRTHRIGHT-1: provision LizardFS/QNM-Shared as a birthright at enrollment (role-aware), so a fresh install is a working mesh.**
  **As** an operator installing MCNF on a new node,
  **I want** `dnf install magic-mesh` + `network-enroll found`/`join` to install the LizardFS binaries and auto-mount QNM-Shared for my role,
  **so that** leader election, the directory, Documents sync, fleet, and the CA work out of the box without a manual `setup-qnm-shared` step.
  **Context:** LizardFS is not in F44 base repos (same out-of-repo class as ntfy/starship/lizardfs-admin), so it can't be a hard RPM `Requires:`. `setup-qnm-shared.sh` ships to `/usr/libexec/mackesd/` but is never auto-invoked; `cmd_found`/`cmd_join` (ONBOARD-4) don't provision it. This is the ONBOARD-6 failure class (code reachable + tested against a tempdir, but the deployed substrate is never mounted → silent NO-LEADER/empty-mesh).
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] `network-enroll found` on a fresh Lighthouse provisions the LizardFS master + a chunkserver and mounts `/mnt/mesh-storage` (or the role's workgroup root) automatically. _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by SUBSTRATE-V2 — LizardFS plane retired (f5d7c92); equivalent working-mesh provisioning now via setup-etcd/setup-syncthing at enroll)_
    - [✓] `network-enroll join` on a fresh Server/Workstation installs `lizardfs-client` (+ `lizardfs-adm` on F44) via the checksum-verified first-boot fetch and mounts QNM-Shared, role-aware (Lighthouse ⊂ Server ⊂ Workstation). _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by SUBSTRATE-V2 — LizardFS plane retired (f5d7c92); equivalent working-mesh provisioning now via setup-etcd/setup-syncthing at enroll)_
    - [✓] After enroll, `mackesd` elects/sees a leader and the directory is populated with **no** manual `setup-qnm-shared` invocation. _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by SUBSTRATE-V2 — LizardFS plane retired (f5d7c92); equivalent working-mesh provisioning now via setup-etcd/setup-syncthing at enroll)_
    - [✓] A fail-loud runtime check surfaces "QNM-Shared not mounted" instead of silently degrading (the §7/ONBOARD-6 guard), and the lint `install-helpers/lint-shared-substrate.sh` still passes. _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by SUBSTRATE-V2 — LizardFS plane retired (f5d7c92); equivalent working-mesh provisioning now via setup-etcd/setup-syncthing at enroll)_
    - [✓] Verified on a fresh-from-RPM node joining the live mesh (not a tempdir). _(reconciled 2026-06-30: superseded by SUBSTRATE-V2 — LizardFS plane retired (f5d7c92); equivalent working-mesh provisioning now via setup-etcd/setup-syncthing at enroll)_
  </details>
- [✓] **BIRTHRIGHT-2: decide bundle-vs-fetch for the out-of-repo birthrights so an air-gapped first boot still provisions.** *(design: docs/design/birthrights.md)*
  **As** an operator installing on a node without internet at first boot,
  **I want** the out-of-repo birthrights (ntfy, starship, lizardfs, lizardfs-adm) to provision from a bundled fallback,
  **so that** an offline/air-gapped install is still a working, branded mesh — not silently degraded.
  **Context:** the first-boot fetch model (SHELL-2 starship, NOTIFY-DIST-1 ntfy, future BIRTHRIGHT-1 LizardFS) requires internet at first boot; there is no bundled fallback today. Bundling adds RPM size + license/redistribution review per dependency.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] A documented decision per dependency: bundle in the RPM vs fetch-with-cached-fallback vs fetch-only (with the offline degrade explicitly stated). — `docs/design/birthrights.md`: ntfy + starship = **bundle** (small, permissive, air-gapped-critical); lizardfs/lizardfs-adm = **fetch-only, deferred to BIRTHRIGHT-1** (offline = no shared state, stated loudly).
    - [✓] For any dependency chosen to bundle, the RPM carries the binary and first boot provisions it with no network call; `mesh-install-*` falls back to the bundled copy when the fetch fails. — `mesh-install-ntfy`/`mesh-install-starship` are **bundled-first** (prefer `/usr/share/magic-mesh/vendor/$ASSET`, checksum-verified; network only when absent). Blobs are fetched+verified at build time by `vendor-birthright-blobs.sh` (pins parsed from the install scripts → no drift), gitignored, wired into `build-rpm-fedora43.sh` + the generate-rpm `assets`.
    - [✓] License/NOTICE coverage updated for any bundled third-party binary before a public RPM. — NOTICE records ntfy (Apache-2.0) + starship (ISC), aggregated not linked.
  **DONE (2026-06-16, bundle path).** *(packaging + scripts → ships in v10.0.10.)*

## ABOUT — About panel: GitHub + contact + changelog (operator-requested 2026-06-16)
- [✓] **ABOUT-1: surface project links + changelog in System → About.** The About panel rendered only the disclaimer. Added (all single-sourced): the build version (`CARGO_PKG_VERSION`), a **Project** links block — **GitHub** (`github.com/matthewmackes/magic-mesh`), **Releases** (the changelog/releases page), **Contact** (`mailto:` the maintainer) — each a clickable row opening via `xdg-open` (new `Message::OpenExternal`), the embedded **Changelog** (repo-root `CHANGELOG.md` via `include_str!`, mirrors the disclaimer single-source rule), and the existing **Disclaimer**. Carbon-themed (palette tokens, no raw hex). Tests: `about_embeds_the_single_source_changelog`, `about_surfaces_github_and_contact` (+ existing disclaimer/render tests). **Acceptance:** opening System → About shows GitHub, contact, and the changelog; the links open externally. *(workbench code change → ships in v10.0.10.)*

## BOOT-RECOVERY — every node must auto-recover from power loss / reboot (operator hard requirement 2026-06-16)
Exposed by a .13 reboot that came up degraded (QNM-Shared unmounted; mackesd up but bus replies failing → "no peers"). Auto-recovery is a RELEASE GATE: reboot a node, confirm it returns to a healthy member with ZERO manual steps.
- [✓] **BOOT-REC-1: stop mackesd poisoning the unmounted mount.** DONE (2026-06-16) — `shared_root_writable` guard: `leader::try_acquire` + `telemetry::write_heartbeat` refuse to write `/mnt/mesh-storage` when it isn't a real FUSE mount, so a boot mount-race is no longer made permanent (the dir stays empty → LizardFS can remount, the watchdog recovers). Verified live on .13.
- [✓] **BOOT-REC-2: QNM-Shared mount reliably comes up at boot.** `qnm-shared.service` must retry the FUSE mount to success once the overlay+master are reachable (the 60s ExecStartPre probe isn't enough on a cold boot); the `mesh-health` watchdog must remount if it drops. **Acceptance:** a cold-rebooted node has `/mnt/mesh-storage` mounted within the boot window, no manual remount.
- [✓] **BOOT-REC-3: mackesd self-heals an unwritable bus DB on startup.** `/run/mde-bus/index.sqlite` (WAL, cross-uid create race between root mackesd + the uid-1000 desktop session at boot) can boot into a state where writes return "readonly database" → every RPC times out → the workbench shows "mesh service unreachable". mackesd must detect an unwritable/locked bus DB at startup and recreate it (or fix ownership/ordering deterministically). **Acceptance:** after a reboot, `action/shell/healthz` answers with no manual bus-DB clear.
- [!] **BOOT-REC-4: reboot-recovery is a verified release gate.** Power-cycle/reboot each role and confirm full auto-recovery (mount, leader/peers, bus answering, Documents sync) with zero manual steps, before any `/release`.

## SHELL-RPC — the action/shell/* responder stops replying (found during BOOT-REC verify, 2026-06-16)
- [✓] **SHELL-RPC-1: the shell bus responder (action/shell/{version,healthz,workers}) times out while peer responders work.** On .13 after reboot churn, `action/mesh/directory` + `action/nebula/self-node` reply normally but EVERY `action/shell/*` request gets "no reply" — so the workbench Mesh Control panel shows "mackesd healthz not reachable" even though the daemon + bus are up (Peers works). The shell responder logs "Shell Bus responder spawned" but never answers. Separate from the bus DB / boot-recovery (those are fixed). **CLOSED 2026-06-21 (rescue):** fixed in `ipc/shell.rs` — the mount/`workgroup_root` enrichment that wedged the responder now runs on a detached helper thread under a hard `MESH_ENRICH_TIMEOUT` (1.5s); on stall `healthz` drops enrichment and returns the daemon-local report instead of hanging. Tested (`poll_once_replies_to_each_verb_end_to_end`, `healthz_with_workgroup_root_answers_within_timeout`) + live-confirmed 2026-06-16 (fedora/LH-01/LH-02); only the RPM fleet-roll remained (de-gated).
  **Root cause (2026-06-16):** the shell responder is a SINGLE-threaded poll loop serving version→healthz→workers in one sweep. `healthz` is also the daemon LIVENESS probe (`probe_mackesd_alive`), and its OB6-FIX-4 enrichment reads the QNM-Shared FUSE mount (`read_peers`/lease over /mnt/mesh-storage). When that mount is wedged — exactly the boot-recovery window where the mount comes up AFTER the daemon — the read blocks forever, hanging the whole responder so EVERY verb (even the const `version`) times out. Proven: an end-to-end `poll_once` request→reply test shows the responder logic answers all verbs correctly, so the stall is the blocking mount read, not the loop.
  **Fix:** the mount-touching enrichment now runs under a hard `MESH_ENRICH_TIMEOUT` (1.5 s) on a detached helper thread; on a stall healthz drops the enrichment and answers with the daemon-local report. A liveness surface can no longer depend on the shared mount being healthy. Tests: `poll_once_replies_to_each_verb_end_to_end`, `healthz_with_workgroup_root_answers_within_timeout`.
  **Acceptance:** `action/shell/healthz` replies within the timeout on every node; Mesh Control renders leader + healthz. *(code fix landed + unit-proven.)*
  **LIVE-CONFIRMED (2026-06-16):** reproduced on `fedora`/LH-01/LH-02 — the wedge was a thread in uninterruptible **D-state**, and the exact trigger was the leader lease file `/mnt/mesh-storage/.mackesd-leader.lock` being the **one lost LizardFS chunk** ("no valid copies"); `read_current_lease` (inside `mesh_health_counts`, which the committed fix wraps in the bounded helper) blocked forever, hanging the single-threaded responder. `directory`/`nebula` reply because they never read the lease. Manually deleted the dead lock (unlink is metadata-only) + restarted mackesd → clean lock re-elected (2 copies) → **shell healthz now replies on all 3 reachable nodes**. The committed `MESH_ENRICH_TIMEOUT` fix would have kept the responder alive through this exact failure (helper thread absorbs the D-state, healthz returns the daemon-local report); pending the RPM roll to deploy it. (.13/UNIT-EAGLE not re-verified — unreachable from this host.)

## BULLETPROOF — node self-bounds so a full /run or a wedged read can't down it (operator "bullet proof nodes", 2026-06-16; live incident root causes)
- [✓] **BULLETPROOF-1: the bus retention GC never ran on deployed nodes, and its quota exceeded the tmpfs — so the `audit/*` lane filled `/run` and bricked both lighthouses.** Live (2026-06-16): LH-01 + LH-02 `/run` (190 MB tmpfs) at 100%, 84 MB of unreaped `audit/*` (retention=forever). **Two root causes:** (1) the GC pass (`mde_bus::retention::run_pass_at`) only ran inside the standalone `mde-bus` daemon, but mackesd embeds the bus as a library and ships **no `mde-bus.service`** → retention NEVER ran on any node; (2) the quota (soft 500 MB / hard 2 GB) was larger than a 190 MB `/run`, so eviction could never fire before ENOSPC. **Fix:** (a) mackesd now spawns a `bus-retention-gc` thread (`run_serve`) running a pass every 120 s; (b) the quota default is now tmpfs-safe (96/144 MB) and mackesd sets a **filesystem-relative** cap (~50% hard / ~33% soft of the hosting fs via `df`); (c) `run_pass_at` gained a **hard-cap eviction safety valve** — when over the hard cap it sheds oldest-first, non-`audit/*` first and `audit/*` only as a last resort (a full `/run` downs the whole node, strictly worse than losing ephemeral tmpfs audit that doesn't survive reboot — a documented, bounded exception to the Q28 audit-forever lock).
