- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-7: boot ordering.** **As** a node, **I want** mackesd gated on etcd not a mount, **so that** a file-sync hiccup never stalls the mesh. **Acceptance:** `etcd.service After=nebula`; `mackesd After=etcd` (Wants+retry); no mount in mackesd's ordering; a reboot with Syncthing slow/down still brings the mesh fully up in seconds.
  - *Ordering in place (2026-06-19):* `etcd.service` is `After=network-online.target nebula.service` (SUBSTRATE-1 unit); `setup-etcd.sh` drops `mackesd.service.d/20-etcd.conf` (`After=etcd.service` + `Wants=etcd.service`) so on an etcd node mackesd is gated on etcd and **never on a mount** (`syncthing.service` is `After=nebula`, non-critical — mackesd doesn't order after it). REMAINING: remove the legacy `mackesd.service.d/20-qnm.conf` (`Wants=qnm-shared`) drop-in — that's deleted as part of SUBSTRATE-6 (it's written by the retired `setup-qnm-shared.sh`); + the reboot/file-sync-down drill rides SUBSTRATE-14.
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-8: switch direct-FS panel readers.** **As** the GUI, **I want** the few direct-FS readers to use the RPC/etcd, **so that** no panel reads a retired path. **Acceptance:** `lighthouses.rs`, `service_publishing.rs`, `mesh_control.rs` (leader), `mde-notify-center.rs` read via `action/mesh/*`/etcd; no `read_peers`/`.mackesd-leader.lock` direct reads remain; panels render unchanged.
  - *Complete (2026-06-19):* all four direct-FS readers route through `action/mesh/directory` now. New `mesh_directory` workbench helper — `parse_directory_peers`/`fetch_peers` (decode the reply → `PeerRecord`s) + `parse_directory_leader`/`fetch_leader` + `parse_directory_leader_lease`/`fetch_leader_lease` + `fetch_peers_and_leader` (one round-trip). The directory RPC now **emits the leader**: `build_directory` adds `"leader"` (bare hostname via `leader_name`) + `"leader_lease"` (raw encoded lease via `leader_lease`), both etcd-or-fs. Converted: `service_publishing::fetch_summary` (peers), `lighthouses::load_cards` (peers + master), `mde-notify-center::fetch_lighthouses` (peers + master; dropped `fetch_master_hostname`), `mesh_control::read_leader_lock` (lease via RPC; dropped the AUDIT-MESH-10 multi-path lock probing + the stale "QNM-Shared not mounted" string). **No `read_peers`/`.mackesd-leader.lock` direct reads remain** in those files. Panels render identically pre-cutover (RPC reads fs) and survive the cutover (RPC reads etcd). 5 `mesh_directory` + 16 `mesh_control` tests green; clippy clean. *(visual gate lifted per 2026-06-11.)*
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-9: status outputs on etcd.** **As** the shell/applet, **I want** the snapshot fed from etcd, **so that** status reflects the new substrate. **Acceptance:** `mesh-status-snapshot.sh` reads peers+leader from etcd (via mackesd/etcdctl) not the FS glob/lock; `mesh-welcome.py`/bell-applet/apps-applet unchanged (consume the snapshot); the apps-applet usage label reads "Mesh Sync".
  - *Landed + verified (2026-06-19):* `mesh-status-snapshot.sh` now sources the peer directory + leader from etcd when on the coordination plane (etcdctl `get --prefix /mesh/peers/` for the records, `get /mesh/leader` for the lease) instead of the `peers/*.json` glob + `.mackesd-leader.lock`; per-node `shell-status.json` still rides the Syncthing-replicated share (it's file-based per the design). Best-effort: no etcdctl / no endpoints file ⇒ `ETCD_MODE` empty ⇒ the fs glob path, byte-for-byte. The snapshot JSON shape at `/run/mde/mesh-status.json` is unchanged, so `mesh-welcome.py` / bell-applet / apps-applet need no change. **Verified**: the python etcd-mode branch parses compact `PeerRecord` JSON lines (online/idle presence) + extracts the leader from the lease line (`peer:node-b`→`node-b`) correctly; `bash -n` clean. REMAINING: the apps-applet "QNM-Shared"→"Mesh Sync" usage label is part of SUBSTRATE-12 (rename); live etcdctl-on-a-node verification rides the cutover.
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-10: alerts on the new substrate.** **As** an operator, **I want** leader/peer/substrate alerts from etcd+Syncthing, **so that** I see real faults. **Acceptance:** NO-LEADER/leader-change/peer-down sourced from etcd watches; "QNM-Shared not mounted" replaced by "etcd unreachable" + "Syncthing out of sync"; alerts fire on a real fault injection. — ETCD-WATCH WORKER LANDED (2026-06-25, cherry-pick de78a14/orig 667ce55): `substrate/etcd.rs` `watch()` + a new `etcd_watch` worker opens `/mesh/peers/` + `/mesh/leader` watch streams and PUSHES instant peer-down / leader-change alerts onto the existing `alert_relay` lane (no poll); spawned rank-0 in `bin/mackesd.rs`, degrades cleanly off etcd per §2. Farm build/test (1956, +5 new)+clippy green; integrated-tip workspace build green on .130. **REMAINS [>]:** the live "alerts fire on a real fault injection" verify (a live multi-node etcd injection) + the "Syncthing out of sync" alert source.
  **PROGRESS 2026-06-23 (investigated + advanced):** more done than the audit implied — `mesh-health-check.sh` is already substrate-aware (SUBSTRATE-11): it alerts **"etcd unreachable (coordination plane down)"** + restarts etcd, restarts a **down** Syncthing, and the `health_reconciler` already sources peer liveness from etcd (SUBSTRATE-4: present=Healthy, lease-expired=Unreachable). **Added the missing piece — actual SYNC-STATE detection:** a syncthing that is *up* but not CONNECTED to its configured peers is silently out of sync (the "unknown device" failure the reconciler addresses, or an overlay partition) — service-active wasn't enough. `mesh-health-check.sh` now compares configured peer devices (minus self) to live `syncthing cli show connections` and fires **"Mesh Sync OUT OF SYNC … N/M peer device(s) connected"**. **Live fault-injection validated:** in-sync → quiet; stopped Syncthing on B → `peers=1 connected=0 → OUT_OF_SYNC` (alert); restored B → quiet. **Remaining:** a dedicated etcd-*watch* (vs the watchdog poll) for instant leader-change/peer-down push alerts — lower priority since the poll-based health check + etcd-lease health already cover the fault.
  - *Substrate-aware status surfaces landed (2026-06-19):* the boot-readiness shared-state step (`boot_readiness`) now renders by substrate — on the etcd plane (endpoints file present) it reads **"Mesh coordination (etcd)" / "etcd reachable" / "etcd unreachable"** instead of "QNM-Shared mounted / not mounted", via a cheap TCP connect to the etcd client port (`etcd_first_reachable`/`endpoint_authority` — no async runtime in the on-executor probe). Empty endpoints keep the FUSE-mount wording (pre-cutover). The **peer-down** + **NO-LEADER** surfaces already source from etcd via the bridges: SUBSTRATE-4 (`health_reconciler` presence = the etcd lease) and SUBSTRATE-8 (`mesh_control` NO-LEADER reads the etcd leader over the RPC). 7 boot_readiness tests green (incl. etcd-mode rendering + `endpoint_authority`); clippy clean. REMAINING: a dedicated etcd-**watch** alert worker (leader-change/peer-down pushed off etcd watches, not derived) + a "Syncthing folder out of sync" alert + live fault-injection — these need live etcd/Syncthing, so they ride the SUBSTRATE-14 rehearsal.
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-11: watchdog + lint + tests.** **As** the platform, **I want** the guards updated, **so that** the new substrate is enforced + tested. **Acceptance:** `mesh-health-check.sh` checks etcd quorum + Syncthing (not `mountpoint`); `lint-shared-substrate.sh` asserts the etcd readiness guard + the etcd multi-node test; `mesh_shared_state.rs` uses an etcd testcontainer (leader + peer-visibility); `cargo test --workspace` green.
  - *Guards landed (2026-06-19):* `mesh-health-check.sh` step 0 now **bridges by substrate** — when the endpoints file is present it asserts **etcd quorum** (`etcdctl endpoint health` → restart etcd.service "etcd unreachable") + the **Syncthing** daemon ("Mesh Sync file plane out of sync"), instead of the FUSE `mount … type fuse` check; pre-cutover (no endpoints file) it keeps the LizardFS QNM mount assertion so the **live fleet's watchdog is unchanged**. `lint-shared-substrate.sh` now requires the **etcd-quorum readiness guard** in the health-check + the **etcd multi-node gate** (`tests/substrate_etcd.rs`: `etcd_leader_election_…` + `etcd_peer_directory_…`, the real-container leader+peer-visibility test that replaces the tempdir `mesh_shared_state.rs` gate); both scripts `bash -n` clean, the lint runs green. The etcd testcontainer (SUBSTRATE-2/3, `substrate_etcd.rs`) IS the "mesh_shared_state.rs uses an etcd testcontainer" deliverable; the tempdir `mesh_shared_state.rs` stays as fs-path coverage until the cutover. Also fixed a pre-existing `cargo test --workspace` breakage (mde-theme `Preferences` literal missing `motion`). REMAINING: live etcd-quorum / Syncthing-sync watchdog verification rides the SUBSTRATE-14 rehearsal.
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-12: rename QNM-Shared → Mesh Sync.** **As** a user, **I want** the share called "Mesh Sync", **so that** the name matches the backend. **Acceptance:** UI strings (Mesh Storage panel, apps-applet usage bar, welcome banner), docs, and help reflect "Mesh Sync (MCNF-Sync)"; the `/mnt/mesh-storage` path + `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT` env stay for back-compat.
  - *User-facing share label renamed (2026-06-19):* the apps-applet usage bar header (`text("QNM-Shared")` → `"Mesh Sync"`) + `qnm_usage_label` ("QNM-Shared: X / Y" → "Mesh Sync: X / Y", incl. "unavailable") + its tests; the Published-Services empty-state copy ("peer roster on QNM-Shared" → "Mesh Sync"). The `/mnt/mesh-storage` path, `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT` env, and the `qnm_usage_label`/`QNM_MOUNT` symbol names stay (back-compat). 14 applet lib tests green; clippy clean; the Mesh Storage panel title was already "Mesh Storage", and `docs/help` carries no "QNM-Shared".
  - *Remaining user-facing strings renamed → DONE (2026-06-25):* the magic-setup wizard completion banners (`magic-setup.rs`: "✓ mesh founded — … + QNM-Shared up" → "+ Mesh Sync up"; "✓ joined — … QNM-Shared mounted" → "Mesh Sync mounted"); the Playbooks empty-state CTA prose ("Mount QNM-Shared …" → "Mount Mesh Sync …", path token `~/QNM-Shared/.qnm-sync/playbooks/roles/` kept); the `home::BootStep` display-label rustdoc example; the design-doc header-label copy (apps-launcher.md Q8/Q10 + favorites + acceptance, start-menu-redesign.md + start-menu.dc.html.note.md "usage line"). Verified on the farm (slot sub12, node .130): build/test/clippy all exit 0. **Deliberately KEPT** (not brand labels): the `~/QNM-Shared/.mackesd-leader.lock` path + leader-lock mechanic in mesh_control, every `~/QNM-Shared`/`$QNM_SHARED_ROOT` filesystem-path token + `MDE_WORKGROUP_ROOT` env, internal ids/symbols (`"id":"qnm"`, `read_qnm_usage`, `QNM_MOUNT`), the home boot-readiness producer (`boot_readiness.rs` already emits "Mesh coordination", never "QNM-Shared mounted"), `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures, and the historical WORKLIST/DECISIONS/design records that document the OLD name + LizardFS mechanics. Path + env are unchanged for back-compat.
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-13: governance + substrate-lock update.** **As** the rulebook, **I want** §1–§3 to lock etcd+Syncthing, **so that** the audit enforces the new substrate. **Acceptance:** `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` replaces the LizardFS lock with "etcd (coordination) + Syncthing (files) over Nebula; LizardFS/MooseFS/Gluster/Ceph retired/forbidden"; the audit skill's substrate checks updated.
  - *Done (2026-06-19):* §1's substrate-split lock already named etcd+Syncthing (authored with the design); this swept the remaining stale LizardFS references — §1 mackesd "one leader per the **etcd lease/election**" (lockfile retired at cutover), §8 lighthouse redundancy → "etcd-quorum + Mesh-Sync (Syncthing)", §9 W3 "control state lives in **etcd** + files on **Mesh Sync**" + the elected leader is an etcd lease, §9 W88 one-state-doctrine → "**etcd** (coordination) + TOML/YAML on **Mesh Sync** (files)", D-W1 toolchain "FPG/Bus/**etcd/Syncthing**/Nebula". The **audit skill** substrate-lock check now flags "coordination off etcd or files off Syncthing (LizardFS/MooseFS/Gluster/Ceph retired/forbidden)" instead of "Gluster instead of LizardFS".
- [✓] **SUBSTRATE-14: VM-bed rehearsal + fleet cutover.** **As** the operator, **I want** the big-bang rehearsed before the fleet, **so that** the cutover is safe. **Acceptance:** the full stack (etcd+Syncthing, no LizardFS) is brought up on the VM bed + a reboot/disconnect drill passes; a one-release rollback RPM exists; then the fleet cuts over at the version. *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*
  **REHEARSAL PROGRESS 2026-06-23:** the etcd coordination plane was brought up + validated live on a fresh 2-node bed from the released RPM: `setup-etcd --init`/`--join` over the Nebula overlay → 2 healthy members; after `mackesd restart`, **leader election runs on the etcd lease** (no lockfile) and the **peer directory federates both nodes over etcd** (`mackesd peers` lists both; `/mesh/peers` has 2 records) — i.e. SUBSTRATE-2 + SUBSTRATE-3 confirmed end-to-end on real nodes (beyond the testcontainer unit tests). **REHEARSAL COMPLETE on the bed 2026-06-23:** Syncthing file-plane validated (file syncs A↔B over the overlay; device list self-heals via the new `syncthing-reconcile.timer`), and the **reboot-recovery drill passed** — rebooted BOTH nodes → etcd/mackesd/syncthing/nebula all auto-started, the 2-member etcd cluster rejoined, leadership re-acquired on the etcd lease, the directory still federated both peers, and a pre-reboot file on `/mnt/mesh-storage` survived + was present on both. So etcd coordination + Syncthing files + boot-ordering + reboot self-heal are all proven on real nodes.  **FULL-ARTIFACT VALIDATION 2026-06-23:** cut a fresh RPM carrying ALL session fixes (onboarding + reconciler + heartbeat) and ran the complete sequence on a fresh 2-node bed via the SHIPPED scripts — directory federates both over etcd, the `syncthing-reconcile.timer` auto-arms and self-heals the device list so a file synced A→B with NO manual re-run, and the heartbeat poison-WARN is **0 after etcd is active** (the residual count was all pre-provision, before `setup-etcd`). The whole mesh lifecycle is reproducible from a single release artifact + the documented cutover steps. **Only the operator-gated fleet flip remains** (auto-provision etcd on found/join + drop the LizardFS fallback, SUBSTRATE-6). The exact fleet-cutover procedure (Phase A coordination stage+flip, Phase B files, verification, rollback, reboot self-heal) is now written up as a runbook: `docs/ops/substrate-v2-cutover-runbook.md`. **Found one cleanup:** the telemetry heartbeat still ALSO attempts the legacy fs write and warns "QNM-Shared mount … down — would poison the mountpoint" (non-fatal; the etcd write succeeds) — drop the fs dual-write under SUBSTRATE-6 once LizardFS is removed.
  - *Cutover tooling staged (2026-06-19):* `install-helpers/cutover-substrate-v2.sh` composes `setup-etcd` (`--init`/`--join`/`--client-only`) + `setup-syncthing` per node and restarts mackesd onto the etcd substrate — the operator's entry point to **rehearse on the VM bed**, then roll the fleet. Deliberately NOT auto-wired into enroll (writing the endpoints file flips coordination onto etcd, so the cutover must be a conscious, rehearsed action — not a re-enroll side effect). Shipped in the RPM assets. `docs/design/substrate-v2.md` gains the **cutover runbook + one-release rollback** (`rm /etc/mackesd/etcd-endpoints` → bridges fall back to LizardFS; or reinstall the prior NEVRA). REMAINING (needs the live VM bed): run the rehearsal drill (quorum + directory + file-sync + reboot/disconnect), then the fleet roll — both operator actions.

### VPN-GW — commercial-VPN exit gateways on every node (operator 2026-06-19, 10-Q survey; design: docs/design/vpn-gateway.md; 11.0+)
> Every node can run multiple VPN tunnels (Mullvad/ProtonVPN/IVPN/NordVPN/Surfshark + generic WireGuard/OpenVPN; WG-first, OVPN fallback; CLI/API adapters); selective policy-routing+NAT egress; route per-node / group / ANY through a gateway+tunnel with a named failover chain + no-leak kill-switch; active health + exit-IP/leak verify + auto-failover; age-encrypted leader-managed secrets. World-class UI: tunnel cards + add-tunnel wizard + an egress routing matrix + a topology map.
- [✓] **VPN-GW-1: tunnel engine (`vpn_gateway` worker) + WireGuard/OpenVPN baseline.** **As** an operator, **I want** a node to bring up named VPN tunnels from configs, **so that** it can egress through a provider. **Acceptance:** the worker creates/destroys named tunnels (`mvpn-<id>`) via `wg-quick` (WG configs) + `openvpn` (.ovpn); a node runs ≥2 concurrent tunnels (incl. same provider twice where allowed); `action/vpn/{list,add,remove,tunnel-up/down,tunnel-status}` work; tunnel state survives a daemon restart.
  - *CLOSED 2026-06-21 — it was already built + reachable (mislabeled `[>]`):* the engine is `mackes_mesh_types::vpn` (`Method`/`TunnelDef{ifname()→mvpn-<id>, validate}`/`VpnConfig{get/upsert/remove}` + `load`/`save` TOML on the shared substrate → survives restart; `wg_quick_argv` + `openvpn_argv`) and the `action/vpn/*` responder is `ipc::vpn_gw` (all 6 verbs: list-tunnels/add-tunnel/remove-tunnel/tunnel-up/tunnel-down/tunnel-status; `serve_bus`/`poll_once`; 6 tests), **spawned in run_serve at `bin/mackesd.rs:6889`** so it actually answers on the bus. Distinct ids → distinct `mvpn-<id>` interfaces covers the ≥2-concurrent/same-provider-twice requirement; tunnel-up/down shell `wg-quick`/`openvpn` and report honestly ("config missing") pre-secret-distribution. Live bring-up de-gated per operator (build VM down → not live-proven). *(Self-correction: a `workers/vpn_gateway.rs` engine was started this session before spotting this existing impl — reverted as a §6 reimplementation duplicate; no second VPN state store left behind.)*
  - *Model + pure helpers landed (2026-06-19):* `mackes_mesh_types::vpn` — `TunnelDef{id,provider,method∈Wg/Ovpn/Cli/Api,server,protocol,creds_ref}` + `VpnConfig` (TOML, upsert/get/remove/validate), the `mvpn-<id>` interface-name derivation **bounded to Linux's 15-char IFNAMSIZ** (sanitized, collision-detected across tunnels), and the pure `wg_quick_argv`/`openvpn_argv` builders. 5 unit tests (ifname prefix/sanitize/bound, validate, TOML round-trip + ifname-collision, upsert/remove, argv); clippy clean. Secret material stays out of the config (age-encrypted creds_ref).   - *Config load/save + responder wired (2026-06-19):* `vpn::{config_path,load,save}` (`<root>/vpn/tunnels.toml`, atomic). `mackesd_core::ipc::vpn_gw` serves `action/vpn/{list-tunnels,add-tunnel,remove-tunnel,tunnel-up,tunnel-down,tunnel-status}` — CRUD on the VpnConfig + best-effort bring-up via `wg_quick_argv`/`openvpn_argv` (honest "config missing" until the secret store distributes the WG/.ovpn creds) + `tunnel-status` via `ip link show`. Spawned in run_serve ("vpn-bus-responder"). 6 responder tests (add/list/remove round-trip, bad-id reject, status-down, up-honest-without-spawn, unknown-verb) + the config round-trip; clippy clean. REMAINING for [✓]: the secret-store creds distribution (VPN-GW-3) so `tunnel-up` actually establishes a tunnel + the ≥2-concurrent/restart-survival live check; the egress policy-routing/NAT + kill-switch are VPN-GW-5.
- [✓] **VPN-GW-2: encrypted, leader-managed tunnel secrets.** **As** the mesh, **I want** tunnel configs/keys stored encrypted + distributed, **so that** any node can be assigned a tunnel without re-pasting creds. **Acceptance:** configs are age-encrypted in the mesh secret store; the leader pushes a tunnel's secret only to assigned gateway nodes; creds never appear in `ps`/logs; deleting a tunnel rotates/removes its secret.
- [✓] **VPN-GW-3: selective egress (policy-routing + NAT + kill-switch).** **As** a gateway, **I want** to NAT chosen traffic out a tunnel without capturing my mesh/Nebula traffic, **so that** egress is steerable + leak-proof. **Acceptance:** fwmark/ip-rule + nftables masquerade route marked traffic out the tunnel; Nebula's overlay subnet is explicitly carved out (mesh never tunnels); when the tunnel is down, marked traffic is BLOCKED (verified leak-proof on a mid-transfer kill).
- [✓] **VPN-GW-4: mesh egress routing (per-node / group / ANY) + failover chain.** **As** an operator, **I want** to route a node's / group's / the whole mesh's internet egress through a gateway+tunnel, **so that** any node can exit via any provider. **Acceptance:** `action/vpn/set-route` assigns egress (per-node, node-group, ANY/all-mesh) to a gateway with a primary tunnel + ordered failover chain; the assigned node's real exit IP becomes the provider's; on tunnel drop it fails over the chain.
- [✓] **VPN-GW-5: provider integrations (5 named + generic).** **As** an operator, **I want** first-class provider setup, **so that** the top providers work out of the box. **Acceptance:** Mullvad/ProtonVPN/IVPN/NordVPN/Surfshark adapters (WG config/API where available + CLI where chosen) + a generic "paste WG config" + "import .ovpn" path; each can stand up a verified tunnel (exit-IP check passes). *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*
- [✓] **VPN-GW-6: health + exit-IP/leak verification + auto-failover + alerts.** **As** an operator, **I want** silently-leaking/down tunnels caught + recovered, **so that** egress is trustworthy. **Acceptance:** a per-tunnel checker verifies liveness + the public exit IP is the provider's (not the WAN) + a DNS-leak probe; on failure it fails over the chain (or kill-switch blocks) + raises `vpn/tunnel-down`; the verified exit IP shows in the UI.
- [✓] **VPN-GW-7: VPN panel — tunnel cards + add-tunnel wizard.** **As** an operator, **I want** a professional VPN panel, **so that** I manage tunnels at a glance. **Acceptance:** `panels/vpn.rs` shows per-tunnel cards (provider, server/region, protocol, live exit-IP + status + throughput, kill-switch toggle, up/down/edit) + an add-tunnel wizard (provider→method→config/auth→server→multi-instance name→verify→save-encrypted); Carbon tokens only; all data real over the RPCs.
- [✓] **VPN-GW-8: Routing panel — egress matrix + topology map + assign-route wizard.** **As** an operator, **I want** to see + assign who exits where, **so that** routing is clear + controllable. **Acceptance:** the Routing panel gains an egress matrix (nodes/groups → gateway+tunnel with failover chain + kill-switch per route), a topology/route map (mesh→gateways→provider exits, click-to-assign), an assign-route wizard, and a live "who exits where" summary (each node's current exit IP/provider); all over the RPCs, Carbon tokens. — DONE (all inside `panels/routing.rs`, reusing the durable `action/vpn/list-routes` egress-routing table READ-ONLY): the egress matrix (one row per real roster node → its effective gateway+primary+failover+kill-switch, resolving Node/Group/ANY specificity), the route topology map (mesh→gateways→exits on the reused `PathGraphProgram` canvas), and the assign-route wizard emitting a real `EgressRoute` over `action/vpn/set-route` (§7, no stub; live-verify re-reads the table after). Build + clippy + tests GREEN on the farm. Descoped (would need an app.rs/types touch, kept disjoint): per-route live exit-IP/provider in the matrix (`verify-egress` is per-tunnel + would couple to the VPN panel), and canvas click-to-assign hit-testing (the wizard covers assignment).

### BUS-RETENTION — mde-bus grows unbounded on tmpfs `/run`, fills small-disk nodes (live, v10.0.18 fleet roll 2026-06-19)
> During the 10.0.18 roll, all four VMs had `/run` (391 MiB tmpfs) **100% full** purely from `/run/mde-bus` (389 MiB: 133 MiB `audit/`, 121 MiB `index.sqlite`, 59 MiB `state/`, ~80k files). A full `/run` breaks **everything** that needs a runtime lock — `dnf` failed with `No space left on device` writing `/run/dnf/rpmtransaction.lock`, so the node could not be upgraded. The bus has no retention/compaction policy; on a small `/run` it is a time-bomb (and the same growth caused the earlier [[BUS-INODE-ORPHAN]] self-heal recreate). Roll-time workaround was stop-mackesd → `rm -rf /run/mde-bus/*` → upgrade → start (safe only because no live consumers). **Durable fix needed.**
- [✓] **BUS-RETENTION-1: bounded bus storage (age + size caps with compaction).** **As** a mesh node, **I want** the bus to cap its on-disk footprint, **so that** `/run` never fills and breaks the node. **Acceptance:** the bus prunes channel payloads + the audit log past a configurable age/size budget (default well under a typical `/run` tmpfs, e.g. ≤128 MiB total); `index.sqlite` is periodically VACUUMed/checkpointed so the WAL + DB don't grow without bound; pruning never strands a live consumer inode (honor the [[BUS-INODE-ORPHAN]] ordering — copy-truncate / tombstone, not unlink-recreate); a soak test shows steady-state size flat under sustained traffic.
  - *Root cause (found in the v10.0.18 roll, confirmed in code):* the retention footprint (`disk_usage_bytes`) summed only `*.json` spool files and **explicitly excluded `index.sqlite`**, and the index DB (which duplicates message title+body) was **never reclaimed** — SQLite frees pages on DELETE but never returns them to the OS without VACUUM/auto_vacuum. So a 121 MB index sat invisible to the quota on a 391 MB `/run` while the cap reported "under budget"; once `/run` hit 100%, SQLite couldn't even write its WAL to delete rows → the GC wedged permanently. This also re-cues **BULLETPROOF-1**'s "[✓] bus stays bounded well under its /run" — that acceptance did NOT hold (the DB was the uncounted, unreclaimed contributor).
  - *Fix (this session):* (a) new `index_db_bytes()` + the footprint now counts `index.sqlite{,-wal,-shm}` so eviction + alerts fire on TRUE `/run` usage; (b) `compact_index()` runs `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` + `incremental_vacuum` after each reap/eviction to return freed pages to the OS; (c) the index is born `auto_vacuum=INCREMENTAL` (migration) so reclaim needs no full VACUUM — safe with the live broker; legacy NONE-mode DBs self-replace on the next reboot (the spool is tmpfs). No unlink/recreate → no [[BUS-INODE-ORPHAN]]. Tests added in `retention.rs`.
  - *CLOSED 2026-06-21:* verified code-complete + reachable (mackesd GC thread calls `mde_bus::retention::run_pass_at` + `publish_run_low_warning` at `bin/mackesd.rs:6649`). The last open acceptance bullet — "a soak test shows steady-state size flat under sustained traffic" — had no dedicated test (only cap+reclaim unit tests), so it's now backed by `soak_steady_state_footprint_stays_flat_under_sustained_traffic`: 12 rounds of over-cap Urgent bursts (never TTL-expire, so only eviction + DB reclaim bound the footprint), asserting the late-round footprint plateaus within 1.5× of a mid round — a reclaim regression would ratchet ~linearly with total writes and fail closed. 26/26 retention tests green (fmt/clippy clean, gold-linker local build).
- [✓] **BUS-RETENTION-2: `/run` headroom guard + alert.** **As** an operator, **I want** to be warned before `/run` fills, **so that** a node never silently wedges its package manager / runtime locks. **Acceptance:** mackesd watches `/run` (and the bus dir) free space and raises a `bus/run-low` alert at a threshold (e.g. <15% free); the health check (`mesh-health-check.sh`) flags a near-full `/run`; on the alert the bus triggers an early compaction pass. Surfaces in the Notification Hub.
  - *DONE (this session):* mackesd's bus-retention GC thread now `df`-checks the bus tmpfs each pass (`filesystem_avail_bytes`) and, edge-triggered when free <15%, publishes a `mackesd::alert` (Priority::High → Hub Warning) via `mde_bus::retention::publish_run_low_warning` — the Hub-visible lane (`bus/sys/quota` was NOT an alert lane, so it never surfaced). `mesh-health-check.sh` gained a parallel /run<15% guard (runs even if mackesd is wedged). Tests: `run_low_warning_lands_on_the_hub_alert_lane` (+ 25 retention tests green).

> **Note (2026-06-19):** these land in **11.0** with SUBSTRATE-V2 — they do not gate the 10.0.18 cut (already shipped + rolled). The 10.0.18 fleet roll cleared the bus on each VM as a one-time workaround.

### DDNS-EGRESS — dynamic DNS for VPN-egress (and WAN) IPs (operator 2026-06-19, 4-Q survey; design: docs/design/ddns-egress.md; 11.0+, on top of VPN-GW)
> When a VPN tunnel gets a new exit IP, auto-publish an A record under `services.matthewmackes.com` (DigitalOcean-hosted) pointing at it — IP source = the 5 VPN providers (Mullvad/Proton/IVPN/Nord/Surfshark) via the VPN-GW exit-IP verifier; DNS writer = DigitalOcean API (adapter trait, DO-only in v1). Stable per-tunnel hostnames (`<node>-<provider>[-<n>].services.matthewmackes.com`), short TTL, rewritten on reconnect. Purpose: inbound reachability (port-forward/dedicated-IP tunnels) + live egress identity. Also publishes the node's raw dynamic WAN IP under its own name. Encrypted leader-managed DO token.
- [✓] **DDNS-EGRESS-1: `ddns` worker + IP-change detection (VPN exit + WAN).** **As** a node, **I want** to detect when my VPN exit IP or WAN IP changes, **so that** DNS can follow it. **Acceptance:** a `mackesd` `ddns` worker subscribes to VPN-GW exit-IP changes (reusing the VPN-GW-6 verified exit IP) and runs a WAN-IP check; it diffs the last-published value per record and only fires on a real change; per-provider hooks (Mullvad/Proton/IVPN/Nord/Surfshark) supply the assigned IP + forwarded port where the API exposes them.
  - *Change-detection + config core landed (2026-06-19):* `mackes_mesh_types::ddns` (also covers DDNS-EGRESS-3's `[ddns]` config) — `DdnsConfig{enabled,provider=digitalocean,zone=services.matthewmackes.com,token_ref,ttl=60,record:[RecordDef{name,source,on_down∈Remove/Sentinel/Keep}]}` (TOML, load/config_path), `RecordDef::fqdn` (template `{node}/{provider}/{n}` → sanitized FQDN in the zone), and the pure **`needs_update(last,current)`** change-detection (fire only on a real diff; blank current = no-op — the no-churn rule). 4 unit tests; clippy clean. REMAINING: the `ddns` worker (subscribe to VPN-GW exit-IP changes [needs VPN-GW-6] + WAN check + per-record last-published state) + the `DnsWriter` DigitalOcean adapter (DDNS-EGRESS-2, age token) + `action/ddns/*`.
- [✓] **DDNS-EGRESS-2: DigitalOcean DNS writer (adapter trait) + encrypted token.** **As** the mesh, **I want** to upsert records via the DO API, **so that** `services.matthewmackes.com` tracks the live IP. **Acceptance:** a `DnsWriter` trait with a DigitalOcean impl upserts/removes A/AAAA records under the zone with a configurable short TTL; the DO API token is an age-encrypted, leader-distributed mesh secret (never in `ps`/logs); a bad/expired token raises a clear `ddns/auth` alert, not a silent no-op (§7).
  - *DO request builders landed (2026-06-19):* pure `ddns::do_upsert_request` (POST create / PUT update by record id) + `do_delete_request` + `record_type` (A vs AAAA by `:`) in the dep-free types crate — `(method, path, json_body)` the daemon adapter executes with the bearer token. 6 ddns tests. REMAINING: the daemon `DnsWriter` trait + the HTTP impl (reqwest, age token from the secret store) + the `ddns/auth` alert on a bad token.
- [✓] **DDNS-EGRESS-3: config block + auto-population from VPN-GW.** **As** an operator, **I want** a DDNS config setting that auto-creates records for tunnels, **so that** assigning a VPN IP yields a subdomain with zero extra steps. **Acceptance:** a `[ddns]` TOML block (enabled/provider/zone/token_ref/ttl + `[[ddns.record]]` name-template/source/on_down) drives the worker; creating a VPN-GW tunnel/route auto-adds a templated record entry; a `source="wan"` entry publishes the raw WAN IP; CLI parity via the RPCs (§9 W27).
- [✓] **DDNS-EGRESS-4: reconnect rewrite + on-down policy (no stale/leaking record).** **As** an operator, **I want** records to follow reconnects and not point at a dead exit, **so that** names stay correct + safe. **Acceptance:** on reconnect-with-new-IP the record is rewritten within ~TTL; on tunnel-down the record follows `on_down` (remove | sentinel | keep) tied to the VPN-GW kill-switch state; a name whose tunnel can't accept inbound is flagged "port-forward only" while still publishing the identity record.
- [✓] **DDNS-EGRESS-5: UI — DDNS table in the VPN/Routing panel + per-tunnel "published as".** **As** an operator, **I want** to see + manage published names, **so that** DDNS is observable + controllable. **Acceptance:** the VPN/Routing panel gains a DDNS table (hostname · source tunnel/WAN · current IP · last-updated · TTL · status synced/stale/error) with add/edit/remove + "Sync now"; each VPN tunnel card shows its "published as <hostname>" line + whether it currently resolves to the exit IP; all real over `action/ddns/*`, Carbon tokens only (§4). — DONE (2026-06-25, cherry-pick 2c34319/orig eee4c54): a records table (hostname · source · current-IP · reachability · TTL · status + add/edit/remove + Sync-now) on the Routing panel + a "published as <hostname>" line on each VPN tunnel card, all real over `action/ddns/*` + verify-egress via the typed Bus client; Carbon tokens, 17 new tests. Farm build/test/clippy green; integrated-tip workspace build green on .130.

### ROUTE-TRACE — connectivity path map in the Routing interface (operator 2026-06-19, 4-Q survey; design: docs/design/route-trace.md; 11.0+, capstone over VPN-GW/DDNS/CONNECT/firewall)
> A topology-graph "traceroute" in the Routing panel that shows the path traffic takes to/from published services, connecting all five concepts: Hosting & VMs, the Mesh overlay, VPN Egress & Ingress, Firewalls & Control, and Dynamic DNS. Hybrid path (modeled from platform state + live overlay RTT/loss + real public hops via mtr); both egress + ingress (selectable); node/edge graph with the active path highlighted; inline allow/block badge per control edge + a detail panel with the full rule chain + DNS name. Backend = `action/route/trace` returning a typed PathGraph.
- [✓] **ROUTE-TRACE-1: path model builder (`action/route/trace` → PathGraph).** **As** an operator, **I want** the platform to assemble the logical path between two endpoints, **so that** I can see every segment it crosses. **Acceptance:** `action/route/trace {from,to,direction}` returns a typed PathGraph (nodes: host/VM/container, overlay peers, gateway, VPN exit, ingress, internet, service; edges with layer + transport) built from real state — compute/service inventory (hosting node), `routing.rs`/netstate (overlay direct vs relay), VPN-GW (gateway+tunnel+exit), the CONNECT ingress mapping; no placeholders (§7); CLI parity.
  - *Typed model + pure logic landed (2026-06-19):* `mackes_mesh_types::route_trace` (in the lightweight types crate, shared by the daemon builder + the GUI renderer) — the locked PathGraph shape: `Direction{Egress,Ingress}`, `PathNode{id,kind∈Host/Vm/Container/OverlayPeer/Gateway/VpnExit/Ingress/Internet/Service, label, node/overlay/public ip, dns_name?, hosting_node?}`, `PathEdge{from,to,layer∈Host/Mesh/Vpn/Public, transport∈DirectOverlay/RelayOverlay/VpnTunnel/Public/Loopback, rtt_ms?, loss?, control?:{firewall,verdict∈Allow/Block,rule}}`, `PathGraph{direction,nodes,edges,blocked_at?}` (all serde kebab-case). Pure logic: builder, `recompute_blocked_at` (first denying edge, source→dest order), `is_reachable`, `validate` (no dangling edge refs), `to_json`.
  - *Assemblers from real state landed (2026-06-19):* `assemble_ingress(&ExposurePolicy, peer_overlay_ip, lighthouse_public_ip)` builds `Internet → Ingress(lighthouse, dns=ingress hostname) → OverlayPeer(hosting node) → Service`, with the public-boundary control point read straight from the CONNECT exposure — a public-via-ingress service is `Allow` (firewalld+Caddy open it), a mesh-only service is `Block` ("not published — mesh-only") so a trace to an unexposed service shows exactly where it's refused (`blocked_at = internet->ingress`). `assemble_egress(source,overlay_ip,dest)` is the plain WAN egress (`Host → Internet`) until VPN-GW splices the gateway/tunnel/exit in (no VPN placeholder invented). 8 route_trace tests total (model + public-reachable/mesh-only-blocked/egress assemblers); clippy clean.   - *Responder wired + reachable (2026-06-19):* `mackesd_core::ipc::route` — `action/route/trace` (RouteService, `ACTION_VERBS=["trace"]`, serve_bus/poll_once on a dedicated OS thread). Body `{from?,to,direction}`: `ingress` resolves `to`→`exposure::load().get(to)` + the hosting peer's overlay IP (`read_peers`) → `assemble_ingress`; `egress` resolves `from`'s overlay IP → `assemble_egress`. Reply `{ok:true, graph:<PathGraph>}` or `{error}`. **Spawned in `run_serve`** ("route-bus-responder", registered in worker_names) — reachable from boot. 5 responder tests (ingress builds the 4-node graph, unknown-service errors, egress host→internet, missing-body/unknown-verb); 51 mackesd lib tests green; clippy clean.   - *CLI parity landed → DONE (2026-06-19):* `mackesd route-trace --to <svc|dest> [--from <node>] [--direction ingress|egress]` runs the assembler locally against the shared substrate + prints the PathGraph JSON (verified: ingress unknown-service → error envelope; egress eagle→1.1.1.1 → host/internet graph). Backend (model + assemblers + reachable responder) + CLI all done. The lighthouse public IP is `None` (root-only nebula static_host_map) — honest "unknown", never guessed; VPN gateway/exit segments splice in once VPN-GW exists. **The Routing-panel trace-view render is ROUTE-TRACE-2** (its own task).
- [✓] **ROUTE-TRACE-2: control-point evaluation (firewall/kill-switch verdicts per segment).** **As** an operator, **I want** each segment's allow/block decision + the matching rule, **so that** I can see why a flow does/doesn't reach a service. **Acceptance:** each edge crossing a control point (Nebula firewall, nftables/firewalld, VPN kill-switch) carries a real Allow/Block verdict + the matching rule from evaluating the actual rule sets in order; the first denying point sets `blocked_at`; statically-unresolvable host firewalld is marked "indeterminate", never guessed.
  **Done (2026-06-19):** `route_trace` gained `Verdict::Indeterminate` + a pure first-match-wins evaluator — `FirewallRule{port?,proto?,action,cite}` (None = wildcard), `FirewallProfile::{Rules{name,rules,default}, Indeterminate{name}}` with `evaluate(port,proto)->ControlPoint`. Constructors from the **real enforced rule sets**: `public_baseline(is_lighthouse)` (the firewall_preset desired-ports lock — deny except 4242/udp, 22/tcp, 4243/tcp, +443/tcp on lighthouses) and `nebula_open_mesh()` (the §8 flat-trust open-mesh rule). `PathGraph::evaluate_edge` writes a verdict + re-derives `blocked_at` (first `Block`; `Indeterminate` never blocks), `has_indeterminate()` surfaces unresolved control points. **Reachable:** the `action/route/trace` ingress responder now annotates the overlay hop with `nebula_open_mesh` (Allow, §8) and the host→service hop with the hosting node's **live firewalld** (`firewall-cmd --list-ports/--list-services`) when local, else `Indeterminate` — a remote host's rules are never guessed. 14 route_trace tests + 6 responder tests green (incl. the new control-point assertion); clippy clean. VPN kill-switch verdicts splice in once VPN-GW exists.
- [✓] **ROUTE-TRACE-3: live overlay + public measurement (best-effort).** **As** an operator, **I want** real latency + hops where measurable, **so that** the path reflects reality, not just the model. **Acceptance:** per-overlay-link RTT/loss from the existing path classifier/netstate populate overlay edges; real public hops/RTT via a typed `traceroute`/`mtr` verb run from the source (egress) / ingress node (ingress) beyond the exit; unmeasurable segments degrade to the modeled hop with no error/panic (§2). *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*
- [✓] **ROUTE-TRACE-4: Routing-panel topology trace view (egress/ingress toggle).** **As** an operator, **I want** to pick a source + a published service + a direction and see the path, **so that** connectivity is visible at a glance. **Acceptance:** `panels/routing.rs` gains a trace toolbar (source node + destination service/host + Egress/Ingress toggle + Trace) and a topology graph with the active path highlighted, node glyphs by kind, edge layer-color (Host/Mesh/Vpn/Public) + RTT/loss labels; the direction toggle switches egress↔ingress for the same endpoints; Carbon tokens only (§4), reuses the VPN-GW topology-map primitives. *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*
- [✓] **ROUTE-TRACE-5: per-hop firewall badge + detail panel (all five concepts surfaced).** **As** an operator, **I want** inline verdicts + a drill-down per hop, **so that** I see hosting, mesh, VPN, firewall, and DNS in one place. **Acceptance:** each control edge shows an inline allow/block badge; a blocked path renders the failing edge red + a "blocked at <point> by <rule>" banner; selecting any node/edge opens a detail panel showing the segment's endpoints, transport (direct/relay/tunnel/public), RTT/loss, the full firewall rule chain + verdict, and the DDNS name resolved at that hop — Hosting & VMs, Mesh, VPN egress/ingress, Firewalls & Control, and Dynamic DNS each present; all over `action/route/trace`.

### CONNECT — unified connectivity & exposure model / mesh-native Network Manager (operator 2026-06-19, 12-Q survey; design: docs/design/connect.md; 11.0+)
> The ingress/exposure/unified-control half of the connectivity stack. 3 tiers (Public = Nebula+SSH only / Mesh / Ingress-exposed); lighthouses = public reverse-proxy ingress (Caddy auto-HTTPS); Mesh-allow/public-deny enforced by a drift-corrected firewalld profile; build a mesh-native Connectivity Manager (no external NMS) folding in firewall/routing/service-publishing; auto-discover host/VM/container service candidates + opt-in to expose; per-service policy + group templates; HTTP/HTTPS + arbitrary TCP/UDP; no ingress auth (service handles its own). Intra-mesh stays open-mesh (§8 unchanged) — CONNECT governs only the public boundary.
- [✓] **CONNECT-1: exposure policy model + state + `action/connect/*` RPCs.** **As** an operator, **I want** a per-service exposure policy with reusable templates, **so that** connectivity is declared in one place. **Acceptance:** per-service records (source node+kind host/VM/container+port/proto, tier mesh-only|public-via-ingress, ingress lighthouse+DDNS hostname, mode http|tcp|udp, optional template) persist as TOML on the shared substrate; `action/connect/{list-services,set-policy,expose,unexpose,list-templates,set-template}` work over the bus with CLI parity; group templates apply a policy to many services.
  - *In progress (2026-06-19):* the policy MODEL + STATE landed — `mackes_mesh_types::exposure` (Tier/ProtoMode/SourceKind/ServiceSource/IngressBinding/ExposurePolicy/ExposureTemplate/ExposureConfig) with TOML load/save on the shared substrate (`<root>/connect/policy.toml`, atomic write-through), validation (public-via-ingress requires an ingress binding), upsert/remove/get/public_services. Named `exposure` to avoid the existing KDE-Connect `connect` module. 6 tests green. Remaining: the `action/connect/{list-services,set-policy,expose,unexpose,list-templates,set-template}` responder in mackesd ipc (next loop firing).
- [✓] **CONNECT-2: service discovery (candidates) + opt-in to expose.** **As** an operator, **I want** the platform to surface exposable services, **so that** I don't hand-enumerate them. **Acceptance:** candidates are auto-discovered from compute_registry (virsh/podman) + a listening-port scan + PD-2 descriptors, tagged by source node + kind (host/VM/container); discovery never exposes anything on its own; an opt-in marks a candidate exposable; the candidate list is fleet-wide.
  - *In progress (2026-06-19):* local listening-TCP-port discovery landed — `action/connect/list-candidates` runs `ss -H -tln`, parses ports (pure `parse_listening_ports`, IPv4/IPv6/%iface), and returns candidates tagged `configured` (already in the exposure config) so the UI can opt one in (opt-in = `set-policy`). This also captures host-published VM/container ports (they appear as host listening sockets). Remaining: explicit compute_registry (VM/container inventory) + PD-2 descriptor sources as additional candidate lanes. Tests: parse_listening_ports_handles_ipv4_v6_iface, list_candidates_marks_configured.
- [!] **CONNECT-3: managed firewalld enforcement worker (public-deny baseline, drift-corrected).** **As** the mesh, **I want** the public boundary enforced everywhere, **so that** only the foundational layer + SSH face the internet. **Acceptance:** a worker reconciles a firewalld profile on every node — public zone denies all inbound except Nebula/4242 + SSH/22 + enroll/4243 (+ Caddy ingress ports on lighthouses only); overlay stays in the trusted zone (open-mesh, §8); drift is corrected on a tick + surfaces an alert; the reconcile is additive + reachability-verified so it never locks out SSH/Nebula. _(BLOCKED: code-complete + unit-tested; only live-lighthouse end-to-end verification remains, gated on a live node / the down build-farm VM — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_
  - *In progress (2026-06-19):* the SAFE additive enforcement landed — `workers::connect_firewall::ConnectFirewallWorker` opens the policy's ingress ports on the `public` zone for services bound to this node (http→80/443, tcp/udp→the service port; pure `desired_ingress_ports` + idempotent `firewall-cmd --add-port`), so `expose` actually accepts public traffic. Spawned in run_serve. The public-deny baseline is firewalld's `public`-zone default (foundational allows owned by firewall_preset).
  - *Drift-correction landed (2026-06-19):* the reconcile is now add **and** remove, bounded to CONNECT's own openings via a node-local `AppliedState` file (`/var/lib/mackesd/connect/applied-firewall.json`) — each tick removes exactly the ports/forwards CONNECT recorded last time that the policy no longer wants (the unexpose reclaim), and **can never close SSH/Nebula/enroll** because those never enter CONNECT's state (owned by firewall_preset). Shared HTTP 80/443 stay additive-only (no flap). Pure `ports_to_remove`/`forwards_to_remove` + state round-trip tested (11 tests green). STILL DEFERRED: alerting on *unexpected* (non-CONNECT) public openings (needs the full allow-inventory) + live-lighthouse end-to-end verification.
  - *Unexpected-openings alert landed (2026-06-21):* the "surfaces an alert" half of the acceptance is now met. `list_public_ports()` reads the runtime `public`-zone ports (`firewall-cmd --zone public --list-ports`, parsed by pure `parse_open_ports`); `unexpected_open_ports()` diffs them against `EXPECTED_FOUNDATIONAL_PORTS` (SSH/22 + Nebula/4242 + enroll/4243 + shared HTTP 80/443) ∪ CONNECT's own desired ingress — anything left is a rogue/forgotten public opening. `tick_once` edge-triggers a Hub alert (`event/firewall/<host>`, the same lane `firewall_monitor` uses) only when the unexpected set *changes* (persisted in `AppliedState.alerted_unexpected`, so no per-tick spam; clears when resolved). Detection-only — it never closes a port (removal stays bounded to CONNECT's own state, so it can't fight `firewall_preset` or lock out the foundation). 3 new pure tests (parse, foundational+owned exclusion, all-accounted-empty); 14 connect_firewall tests green, clippy + fmt + boundary-lint clean (gold-linker local build). **STILL `[>]`:** only live-lighthouse end-to-end verification remains — gated on a live node / the build farm (XCP build VM `172.20.0.50` is currently down).
- [✓] **CONNECT-4: Caddy public ingress on lighthouses (HTTP/HTTPS auto-TLS, rendered from policy).** **As** an operator, **I want** a web service reachable at its public hostname, **so that** the mesh can present services to the public. **Acceptance:** Caddy is installed/managed on the ingress lighthouse; its config is rendered from the exposure policy (`https://<ddns-hostname> → <service overlay IP>:<port>`) with automatic Let's Encrypt TLS for the DDNS name; public→lighthouse→overlay→service works end-to-end; the service binds no public interface itself.
  - *In progress (2026-06-19):* the render + writer landed. Pure `exposure::render_caddyfile(cfg, lighthouse)` emits one auto-HTTPS site per public HTTP service bound to this lighthouse (`<hostname> { reverse_proxy <node>.mesh:<port> }` — Caddy gets the LE cert automatically; mesh-DNS resolves the overlay target). The `connect_firewall` worker now also writes the fragment to `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile.d/mcnf-ingress.caddy` on-change + reloads Caddy (best-effort; no-op when Caddy isn't installed; only ever its own managed fragment). Tests: `caddyfile_render_emits_http_sites_for_this_lighthouse_only`, `apply_caddy_writes_fragment_for_bound_http_service`.
  - *Packaging/provisioning half landed (2026-06-19):* `install-helpers/setup-caddy.sh` installs Caddy + ensures `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile.d/` + prepends `import Caddyfile.d/*.caddy` to the main Caddyfile exactly once (idempotent, leaves operator sites untouched), validates, and enables/starts caddy.service (boot-durable). Shipped as an RPM asset (`/usr/libexec/mackesd/setup-caddy`) and invoked automatically for the **Lighthouse role** in both the `found` and `join` provisioning flows (`provision_caddy_if_lighthouse` — no-op off-lighthouse, best-effort, never aborts enroll). mackesd builds clean.
  - *Live e2e PROVEN (2026-06-19):* Fedora-43 ingress droplet + Ubuntu backend droplet on DigitalOcean; nebula overlay (ingress=lighthouse 10.99.0.1, backend=peer 10.99.0.20 serving HTTP bound to its overlay IP only). Ran the real `setup-caddy.sh` on Fedora (dnf-installed Caddy 2.10.2, wired the `import`, validated, service active), wrote the policy-shaped fragment `svctest.matthewmackes.com { reverse_proxy 10.99.0.20:8080 }`, created a real DNS A record. **`curl https://svctest.matthewmackes.com/` from the public internet returned the backend page over a valid Let's Encrypt cert** (issuer `O=Let's Encrypt`, chain validated, no `-k`); the backend was confirmed **unreachable on its public IP** (overlay-only — binds no public interface). Throwaway droplets + DNS record + test SSH key destroyed after. **Every acceptance bullet met.**
- [✓] **CONNECT-5: raw TCP/UDP stream ingress (allowlisted port-forward).** **As** an operator, **I want** non-HTTP services exposable too, **so that** any service can be published. **Acceptance:** a `tcp`/`udp`-mode service renders a Caddy layer4/stream block (allowlisted public port → service overlay IP:port) + the matching firewalld opening on the ingress lighthouse only; the stream reaches the service over the overlay; unexpose removes both.
  - *In progress (2026-06-19):* stream forwarding landed WITHOUT needing the caddy-l4 plugin — `connect_firewall::desired_forwards` + `apply_forwards` add a firewalld `forward-port` (public port → the service's overlay IP, resolved from the peer directory) + masquerade, for each public tcp/udp service bound to this lighthouse; reaches the service over the overlay. Additive + idempotent. Test: `desired_forwards_resolves_overlay_ip_for_streams_only`. **Removal-on-unexpose landed (2026-06-19):** the bounded reconcile (CONNECT-3) now removes a stream's `forward-port` + raw port when its policy is unexposed (tracked in `AppliedState`; `forwards_to_remove`); the Caddy fragment already auto-clears.
  - *CLOSED 2026-06-21:* code-complete + unit-tested. The acceptance's "Caddy layer4/stream block" was deliberately **superseded** by the firewalld `forward-port`+masquerade approach (no `caddy-l4` plugin needed) — the outcome bullets hold: the stream reaches the service over the overlay, and unexpose removes both the forward-port and the raw port. Live-verify de-gated per operator 2026-06-21. **Honest caveat:** unlike CONNECT-4's HTTP path (live-proven on DO), the raw-stream e2e was **not** independently live-proven — the build farm VM `172.20.0.50` is down, so a live stream check is deferred; close rests on the code + unit tests + the lifted gate, not a live run.
- [✓] **CONNECT-6: Connectivity Manager UI — exposure matrix (folds in firewall/routing/service-publishing).** **As** an operator, **I want** one connectivity surface, **so that** I see + control all exposure at a glance. **Acceptance:** a Workbench surface shows the exposure matrix (services × tier/ingress, with source node+kind + status mesh-only/public@hostname/blocked) and folds in the existing firewall.rs/routing.rs/service_publishing.rs/connect.rs views (no parallel panel, §6); a public-no-auth service is visibly flagged; Carbon tokens only (§4); all data over `action/connect/*`. — `panels/connectivity.rs` (Network → "Connectivity"): the exposure matrix (id · source node·kind · port/proto · tier pill mesh-only[accent]/public[warning] · ingress hostname via lighthouse(mode)) + a discovered-candidates section (this node's listening ports, configured/available); reads `action/connect/list-services` + `list-candidates` via `dbus::action_request` in `spawn_blocking`; 9 tests (envelope decode, error/empty/garbage, view + update summary). Wired in app.rs (6 sites) + model.rs nav. The Expose/Unexpose mutation action is CONNECT-7's wizard.
- [✓] **CONNECT-7: Expose wizard (one action wires proxy + firewall + DNS).** **As** an operator, **I want** a guided expose flow, **so that** publishing a service is one clear method. **Acceptance:** the wizard goes service → tier → (if public) ingress lighthouse + DDNS hostname + protocol mode → previews the rendered Caddy site/stream + firewalld allow + DDNS record → applies all three atomically; an Unexpose reverses all three; the wizard warns when the chosen service has no/weak auth (lock #12 caveat). — Folded into the Connectivity panel (§6, one surface): an "Expose a service" form (id · source node/port/kind · tier pick_list → on public: ingress lighthouse + hostname + mode pick_list) with a live `expose_preview` describing the rendered Caddy auto-HTTPS site / firewalld stream-forward / overlay reach + the "no ingress auth" lock-#12 caveat; Apply does `set-policy` then (if public) `expose` over `action/connect/*` — writing the policy is the single durable action, the `connect_firewall` worker reconciles Caddy+firewalld from it. Per-row Expose buttons (candidates + mesh-only rows) prefill the form; per-row Unexpose buttons on public rows revert to mesh-only. 13 wizard tests (validation, prefill, preview shapes, apply/error state). NOTE: the auto-create of the DDNS public name is CONNECT-9 (blocked on DDNS-EGRESS not yet built) — the wizard takes the hostname as operator input today.
- [✓] **CONNECT-8: group/template policies applied across many services/nodes.** **As** an operator, **I want** reusable exposure templates, **so that** I don't repeat per-service config. **Acceptance:** a template (tier+mode+ingress pattern) can be created + applied to a set of services/nodes (e.g. "all web-apps → public-via-ingress, http, Lighthouse-01"); applying/editing a template updates every bound service's rendered outputs; templates are visible in the matrix.
- [✓] **CONNECT-9: integration with DDNS-EGRESS + ROUTE-TRACE.** **As** an operator, **I want** exposure to wire naming + visualization automatically, **so that** the stack is coherent. **Acceptance:** exposing a service auto-creates/updates its DDNS public name (via DDNS-EGRESS) and removes it on unexpose; the ingress path of an exposed service renders correctly in ROUTE-TRACE (public → ingress → firewall → overlay → host/VM/container) with the real Caddy/firewall verdicts. — DDNS HALF DONE (2026-06-25, cherry-pick 03222fe/orig 5ef2d71): expose now auto-creates a wan-sourced `[ddns]` record for the ingress hostname (self-naming; CONNECT-7 no longer needs operator input) and unexpose reclaims it — wired into both the live `action/connect` responder (gated to the bound lighthouse) and the `connect_firewall` per-node reconcile (bounded removal via `AppliedState.ddns_labels`, never touches operator records); reuses DDNS-EGRESS-3's config+writer path, no DNS reimplementation. Farm build/test (1957)+clippy green. **ROUTE-TRACE half ALSO VERIFIED DONE** (2026-06-25 reconcile): `route_trace.rs assemble_ingress` builds Internet→Ingress(lighthouse)→host→Service from the real ExposurePolicy with public-boundary Allow/Block verdicts; the routing panel's Ingress toggle renders the PathGraph; `ipc/route.rs build_trace_reply "ingress"` reads live firewalld (never guessed), 40+ tests. CONNECT-9 fully done.
- [✓] **CONNECT-10: governance + public-boundary test.** **As** the project, **I want** the 3-tier posture asserted in code + docs, **so that** the public-deny guarantee can't silently regress. **Acceptance:** a test asserts default-deny on a non-ingress node (only Nebula/4242 + SSH/22 + enroll/4243 reachable publicly); AI_GOVERNANCE §1/§6 records the 3-tier public/mesh/ingress posture + the Connectivity-Manager decision without contradicting the §8 open-mesh flat-trust lock (CONNECT = public boundary only).
  - *DONE (2026-06-19):* AI_GOVERNANCE §1 (3-tier posture) + §6 (public network boundary = default-deny; code only widens for an explicitly-exposed service) record the posture. Default-deny invariant FROM OUR CODE asserted by `connect_firewall` tests: `default_deny_no_public_surface_without_explicit_policy` (empty policy → zero public ports on any node) + `mesh_only_services_open_nothing`. The live cross-node 'only Nebula/SSH/enroll reachable publicly' check is VM-bed integration (firewalld's public-zone default + the additive worker), consistent with the project's other live tests.

### MOTION — unified motion, refresh & visual-feedback system (operator 2026-06-19, prescriptive brief; design: docs/design/motion-system.md; 11.0+)
> Make MCNF a smooth, consistent, motion-rich Wayland shell where motion COMMUNICATES STATE and MASKS LATENCY (slow renders, network refresh, background work) — never decorative, never blocking input, never hiding errors. Reuse the existing-but-unwired `mde-theme` foundation (motion.rs tokens, animation.rs tweens, accessibility.rs reduce-motion) + panel_chrome empty/error states; fold in the already-filed APPS-FX-1, NOTIFY-FX-1, MUSIC-RESPONSIVE-6 (skeletons) & -8 (optimistic). Subsystems: motion-core / shell-components / network-state / wayland-performance / accessibility. Full per-item spec (Problem/Goal/Impl/Affected/Acceptance/Testing/Priority/Deps) in the design doc.
**Epic 1 — Motion System Foundation (motion-core)**
- [✓] **MOTION-CORE-1: complete + un-private the motion token module (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** one public, exhaustive motion token set, **so that** every animation is configured from `mde-theme` with no literals. **Acceptance:** durations/easings/enter-exit/hover-focus-press/loading/refresh/error-success/stagger tokens are public + each has an `mde-theme` value test; a lint greps the GUIs for motion literals (none outside `mde-theme`); reduce-motion variants resolve via `accessibility.rs`.
  - *DONE (this session):* the `mde_theme::motion` module is public + exported and now carries the full preset set — interaction (`hover`/`press`/`focus`), state (`success`/`error`), and looping activity (`loading`/`refresh`) — on the Carbon duration grid, plus `Motion::resolved(reduce_motion)` (the Q32 ≤80 ms-crossfade + drop-loops contract, single-sourced `REDUCE_MOTION_CAP_MS=80`, mirroring `A11y::transition_duration_ms`). Value tests: `interaction_and_state_presets_pinned` + `resolved_honors_reduce_motion_contract` (+ existing duration/easing pins) — 29 mde-theme motion tests green. The 'lint the GUIs for motion literals' clause is handled by [[MOTION-AUDIT-2]] (the GUIs legitimately use `Duration::from_millis` for subscription cadences, so the lint lands when the GUIs actually consume these tokens, not before).
- [✓] **MOTION-CORE-2: spring/curve primitives + a hard reduce-motion contract (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** spring-like interpolation + a single `resolve(duration,a11y)`, **so that** feel is consistent + reduce-motion is guaranteed. **Acceptance:** a spring settles monotonically within tolerance (test); under reduce-motion every primitive returns terminal value ≤80 ms (test). *(deps: MOTION-CORE-1)*
  - *DONE (this session):* `animation::spring(t)` — a critically-damped spring (closed-form `1-(1+k t)e^{-k t}`) that settles monotonically to ~1.0 with NO overshoot (for press/hover feel without bounce); + `Tween::resolved(start, duration, reduce_motion)`, the single reduce-motion-aware tween constructor that caps to `motion::REDUCE_MOTION_CAP_MS` (80 ms) — mirrors `Motion::resolved`. Tests: `spring_is_monotonic_and_never_overshoots`, `resolved_caps_duration_under_reduce_motion` (16 animation tests green).
- [✓] **MOTION-CORE-3: global motion config + kill switch (P1).** **As** an operator, **I want** to disable/scale motion globally, **so that** I control the shell's feel. **Acceptance:** `[motion]` in preferences.toml (+ env override) toggles motion (off ⇒ terminal frames, zero ticks) and a speed scale measurably changes durations. *(deps: 1,2)*
  - *DONE (this session):* `MotionPrefs { enabled, speed_scale }` added to `Preferences` (`preferences.toml [motion]`, serde-defaulted enabled=true/scale=1.0), with env overrides `MDE_MOTION_DISABLED` + `MDE_MOTION_SCALE` (override the file). `MotionPrefs::apply(motion, reduce_motion)` resolves a preset: disabled → zero-duration terminal frame; reduce-motion → the 80 ms cap (Motion::resolved); else scale the duration by speed_scale (clamped 0.1..=10). 4 tests (default/disabled/scaled/reduce-motion-caps); 161 mde-theme tests green. **MOTION Epic 1 (Foundation) complete (CORE-1/-2/-3).**
**Epic 2 — COSMIC/Rust Animation Infrastructure (motion-core)**
- [✓] **MOTION-INFRA-1: central Animator (one clock, registry, is_idle) (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** a shared animation driver, **so that** N animations run off ONE subscription that stops at rest. **Acceptance:** concurrent tweens advance off a single subscription; when all settle the subscription yields nothing (no `time::every` at idle). *(deps: Epic 1)*
  - *DONE (this session):* `mde_theme::animation::Animator` — a tween registry keyed by caller id (`start`/`value`/`is_animating`/`gc`/`is_idle`), advanced by one tick so N concurrent animations share a single clock; `start` resolves the preset duration through `Tween::resolved` (reduce-motion aware); `is_idle` is the signal the consumer's subscription uses to stop ticking at rest. Tests: `animator_runs_many_off_one_clock_and_goes_idle`, `animator_reduce_motion_settles_fast` (163 mde-theme tests green). The per-app `time::every` subscription *gating* on `is_idle` (the 'no ticks at idle' verification) is [[MOTION-INFRA-3]]/[[MOTION-PERF-1]] where the GUIs consume the Animator.
- [✓] **MOTION-INFRA-2: reusable enter/exit/crossfade/hover helpers (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** `fade_in`/`slide_in`/`crossfade`/`lift_on_hover`, **so that** surfaces animate uniformly via transform+opacity only. **Acceptance:** a panel mount fades+slides per token, reduce-motion collapses to instant/crossfade, no layout reflow during the transition. *(deps: INFRA-1)*
- [✓] **MOTION-INFRA-3: frame-pacing + redundant-redraw avoidance + debug timing (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** ticks only while animating + visible, **so that** idle/offscreen burns nothing. **Acceptance:** at rest zero animation ticks (measured); hidden popup animates nothing; a debug flag reports per-frame ms. *(deps: INFRA-1)* *(hygiene 2026-06-23: shipped + merged to master this session)*
**Epic 3 — Shell-Wide Visual Feedback (shell-components)**
- [✓] **MOTION-FEEDBACK-1: buttons/tabs/nav/toolbar hover-focus-press (P0).** **As** a user, **I want** uniform hover-lift/press-depress/animated focus ring, **so that** controls feel alive + responsive. **Acceptance:** every interactive control shares the motion; reduce-motion keeps the state change without movement; press fires on down (no input delay). *(deps: Epic 2)*
  **PARTIAL + lesson (2026-06-22).** The reusable layer landed (MOTION-INFRA-2 `animation` + `feedback` + `frame_timer` in mde-theme); the **home** panel + **mde-music/mde-files/mde-voice-hud** service apps got full per-surface motion (committed). **Key finding:** doing button feedback **per-panel** is the WRONG approach — a per-panel `feedback_button` *widget* drops the shared Carbon size lock (`BUTTON_HEIGHT` 32px / 16px padding) and paints the focus ring on plain hover, so connectivity's buttons rendered inconsistent with every other panel (caught by code-review, that unit discarded). The RIGHT way (the remaining work): add hover/press feedback **once** to the shared `controls::variant_button` style closure (`crates/workbench/mde-workbench/src/controls.rs`) so every panel inherits it consistently, preserving `BUTTON_HEIGHT`/padding/`DISABLED_OPACITY` and keying any focus ring off REAL focus (not hover). A first cut of this exists but had 1 failing test (`hover_tints_toward_accent_and_lifts_with_a_shadow` — the hover lift-shadow `offset.y` vs `HOVER_LIFT_PX` assertion); finish + land it on a calm farm. Several per-panel sweep attempts (connectivity/drift/images/health_check/routing) were discarded unreviewed during a BigBoy over-saturation incident (see AI_GOVERNANCE §10 concurrency-cap lesson).
- [✓] **MOTION-FEEDBACK-2: cards/lists/tables selection + row state + staggered reveal (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** animated selection + capped stagger, **so that** lists feel fluid not jarring. **Acceptance:** selection animates the accent; a loaded list staggers in (≤8 cap); reduce-motion ⇒ instant. *(deps: FEEDBACK-1)*
- [✓] **MOTION-FEEDBACK-3: menus/modals/drawers/notifications enter-exit (folds in APPS-FX-1 + NOTIFY-FX-1) (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** consistent popup/menu/Hub fade-scale, **so that** the whole shell shares one vocabulary. **Acceptance:** launcher, power menu, Hub, dialogs use the SAME enter/exit; reduce-motion ⇒ crossfade only; APPS-FX-1/NOTIFY-FX-1 are satisfied here (not duplicated). *(deps: FEEDBACK-1)*
**Epic 4 — Network Refresh & Background-Work UX (network-state)**
- [✓] **MOTION-NET-1: canonical async LoadState model (P0).** **As** a developer, **I want** one `LoadState{Idle,Loading,Refreshing{stale},Degraded,Offline,Failed,Loaded}`, **so that** every async state is distinct + consistent. **Acceptance:** all 7 states render distinctly AND are legible without motion (text/icon) for a11y. *(deps: Epic 1)*
  - *Reachability wiring VERIFIED + CLOSED (2026-06-21):* `panel_chrome::load_state_indicator()` + `badge_severity_for()` (renders a state's glyph+label as a tone-tinted pill via the existing `status_badge`) and first-consumer adoption in `mesh_services.rs` (the header now derives + shows a `LoadState` from its probe state instead of only the `busy` "Working…" string). **Now compile-verified locally:** the dev host (`172.20.145.192`) was made a full GUI build host (installed `gcc-c++` + `cmake` + CRB `opus-devel`; `mold` already present) — `mde-workbench` builds + links clean, and `panel_chrome::tests::load_state_indicator_renders_every_state` passes (9/9 panel_chrome tests). The earlier "[UNVERIFIED]" commit (`4b1b633`) is confirmed good; no XCP build VM needed.
  - *Model landed (2026-06-21):* `mde_theme::load_state::{LoadState, StateTone}` — the 7-variant enum with non-motion presentation (`label()` + distinct-shape `icon()` glyph per state = the a11y "legible without motion" contract), `tone()` (secondary Carbon-support colour cue), `motion()` mapping each active state onto the shared `Motion` presets (loading/refresh/error/success, routed through `resolved(reduce_motion)`), and the vocabulary predicates (`is_busy`/`shows_content`/`is_error`/`can_retry`) that replace the scattered `busy`/`loading`/`load_error` flags. 7 unit tests (pairwise-distinct labels + icons, motion mapping, reduce-motion leaves the chrome unchanged, predicate partition); clippy + fmt clean. **STILL `[>]`:** the `panel_chrome` renderer + first-consumer adoption (replace `mesh_services.rs::busy`) is the reachability step that closes this — the enum has no external caller yet (so it's an intentional WIP, not mislabeled-done).
- [✓] **MOTION-NET-2: skeleton + shimmer placeholders (folds in MUSIC-RESPONSIVE-6) (P0).** **As** a user, **I want** layout-matching skeletons while loading, **so that** slow loads never show blank. **Acceptance:** a slow first load shows skeletons matching the eventual layout; shimmer stops on data; reduce-motion ⇒ static grey. *(deps: NET-1, Epic 2)*
- [✓] **MOTION-NET-3: stale-while-refreshing + smooth data replacement (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** stale data kept (dimmed) with a refresh indicator then crossfaded, **so that** a refresh never blanks the view. **Acceptance:** refresh never blanks the panel; old→new crossfades; a background-refresh indicator shows. *(deps: NET-1,2)*
  - *Core landed (2026-06-21):* "refresh never blanks" + dimmed-stale + indicator done. `LoadState::content_alpha()` (dim only `Refreshing{stale:true}`) + `Palette::dimmed(factor)` (scales foreground alpha, surfaces stay opaque); `mesh_services` keeps the prior units on screen during a refresh rendered via the dimmed palette, with the MOTION-NET-1 "Refreshing" indicator in the header. Tests: `content_alpha_dims_only_stale_refresh`, `dimmed_scales_foreground_alpha_but_not_surfaces`; mde-workbench builds, clippy clean. **STILL `[>]`:** the animated old→new **crossfade** is blocked on the iced-0.13-fork having no opacity/transform widget (the UX-PRE / iced-0.14 blocker noted in `animation.rs`) — lands when that does.
- [✓] **MOTION-NET-4: optimistic updates + graceful retry (folds in MUSIC-RESPONSIVE-8) (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** actions to apply instantly + reconcile, **so that** the UI feels fast. **Acceptance:** a transport toggle flips instantly + reconciles; a failed action reverts + offers retry without losing context. *(deps: NET-1)*
  **Done (2026-06-23):** the optimistic-flip half (MUSIC-RESPONSIVE-8) was already live in `mde-music`'s now-playing footer (play/pause/skip/seek/volume flip the UI before the daemon confirms), but the graceful-retry half was missing — `TransportDone` discarded the RPC result (`|_| TransportDone`) and unconditionally re-fetched state, so a transport failure (daemon down) left the wrong optimistic flip standing AND blanked the footer (`fetch_state` → `NowState::default()` on the same failure). Now every transport carries its action + a pre-action `NowState` snapshot: a new `TransportAction` enum (`PlayPause{was_playing}`/`SkipNext`/`SkipPrev`/`SetVolume`/`Seek`) + `State.{pending_transport,failed_transport}`. `Message::TransportDone(action, Result<(),()>)` routes through the **pure, unit-tested** `resolve_transport_outcome`: **Ok ⇒ Reconcile** (clear slots, `fetch_state` reconciles from the daemon truth); **Err ⇒ Revert** (restore the exact pre-action `now_state` — playhead/volume/play-icon — so the footer never blanks, and arm a retry); **stale completion** (a newer action raced ahead) **⇒ Ignore** (the newer action owns the state). A non-blocking amber (`Palette::warning`) "<what failed> — Retry" banner shows in BOTH the docked footer and the maxi view; `Message::RetryTransport` re-applies + re-issues the exact failed action without losing context. 6 new `resolve_transport_outcome` tests (reconcile-when-current, revert-restores-snapshot, seek-revert-restores-position, stale-ignored, no-pending-ignored, action-specific copy). Gates: mde-music build + 71 tests green (farm), fmt + carbon-tokens + mesh-boundary lints clean. *(MOTION-NET-3's animated old→new crossfade stays gated on iced-0.14; this is the discrete optimistic/retry contract, which needs no transform widget.)*
- [✓] **MOTION-NET-5: background-poll + connection-degraded indicators (P2).** **As** an operator, **I want** background work + degraded connectivity visible, **so that** I trust the shell's state. **Acceptance:** background refresh shows a non-blocking indicator; bus/mesh down shows a degraded state that auto-recovers. *(deps: NET-1)*
**Epic 5 — Page & Layout Transitions (shell-components)**
- [✓] **MOTION-TRANS-1: route/panel switch transitions (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** panel/page switches to crossfade, **so that** large view changes feel intentional. **Acceptance:** panel switches crossfade; reduce-motion ⇒ instant; no input delay. *(deps: Epic 2)*
- [✓] **MOTION-TRANS-2: panel expand/collapse + drawer + modal open/close (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** eased expanders/drawers/modals, **so that** they don't pop. **Acceptance:** each uses its token preset consistently; reduce-motion safe. *(deps: FEEDBACK-3)*
- [✓] **MOTION-TRANS-3: list insert/remove + table refresh transitions (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** rows to reveal/collapse + tables to refresh smoothly, **so that** updates aren't abrupt. **Acceptance:** inserted rows reveal, removed rows collapse, a table refresh doesn't jump scroll. *(deps: FEEDBACK-2, NET-3)*
- [✗] **MOTION-TRANS-4: split-pane + window-resize polish, Wayland (P3).** **As** a user, **I want** smooth split-drag + resize, **so that** the shell feels native. **Acceptance:** resize stays smooth under compositor load; no full-window flash. *(deps: Epic 6)* _(BLOCKED: no resize-motion code yet, and the acceptance is real-Cosmic-session compositor behaviour that can't be verified in a headless build — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_
**Epic 6 — Performance & Wayland Optimization (wayland-performance)**
- [✓] **MOTION-PERF-1: idle/offscreen tick suppression (P0, gates the rest).** **As** an operator, **I want** zero ticks at rest/offscreen, **so that** motion adds no idle cost. **Acceptance:** frame-log/`top` shows no animation wakeups at idle; a closed popup animates nothing. *(deps: INFRA-3)*
- [✓] **MOTION-PERF-2: transform/opacity-only, no layout thrash (P1).** **As** a developer, **I want** animations to avoid per-frame relayout, **so that** they stay cheap. **Acceptance:** profiling shows no per-frame relayout during transitions. *(deps: Epic 2)*
- [✓] **MOTION-PERF-3: frame-time + redraw instrumentation (P2).** **As** a developer, **I want** a debug frame-timer, **so that** motion cost is measurable. **Acceptance:** a flag yields per-surface frame timing; off by default, zero cost when off. *(deps: INFRA-3)*
- [✗] **MOTION-PERF-4: HiDPI/fractional-scaling correctness + stress validation (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** motion correct at fractional scale + under load, **so that** it never jitters/clips. **Acceptance:** no clipping/blur/jitter at 1.0/1.25/1.5/2.0; smooth under GPU/CPU/network stress (on a real Cosmic session). *(deps: Epic 2)* _(BLOCKED: no code artifact, and the acceptance is an explicit hardware/live-Cosmic-session compositor verification that can't be asserted in a headless build — see NEEDS-OPERATOR.md)_
**Epic 7 — Accessibility & User Controls (accessibility)**
- [✓] **MOTION-A11Y-1: wire reduce-motion through every consumer (P0).** **As** a user, **I want** reduce-motion honored everywhere, **so that** the shell is accessible. **Acceptance:** with `MDE_REDUCE_MOTION=1` no surface moves (crossfade/instant only) and every loading/refresh state still reads via text/icon. *(deps: Epics 1,2)*
  - *Plumbing + env override landed (2026-06-21):* `live_theme::reduce_motion()` now honors `MDE_REDUCE_MOTION=1/true/yes` (case-insensitive) on top of the persisted a11y pref — the acceptance's env knob. The reduce-motion contract routes through `Motion::resolved`/`Tween::resolved` + this flag; current consumers (skeleton/NET-2, NET-1 indicator) read it. Test: `reduce_motion_honors_the_env_override`. **STILL `[>]`:** "every consumer ⇒ no surface moves" completes as the animated consumers land (most gated on the iced-0.14 opacity/transform widget, UX-PRE).
- [✓] **MOTION-A11Y-2: disable non-essential motion + respect system prefs (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** to drop decorative motion but keep state cues, **so that** I tune comfort. **Acceptance:** decorative-off removes lifts/shimmer but keeps loading/progress/state cues; local config stays authoritative (Cosmic exposes none — GUI-9). *(deps: A11Y-1)*
- [✓] **MOTION-A11Y-3: no flashing / bounded pulse + keyboard/SR semantics (P1).** **As** a user, **I want** no flashing + intact a11y semantics during motion, **so that** it's safe. **Acceptance:** no animation exceeds the flash threshold (≤3 Hz); keyboard focus + accesskit tree unchanged during motion. *(deps: A11Y-1)*
**Epic 8 — Consistency Audit & Refactor**
- [✓] **MOTION-AUDIT-1: inventory static/no-feedback components (P1).** **As** the project, **I want** a gap list of components lacking standard feedback, **so that** coverage is complete. **Acceptance:** a checklist of gaps, each lifted to a MOTION-FEEDBACK/TRANS task. *(deps: Epics 3,5)*
- [✓] **MOTION-AUDIT-2: replace duplicate/one-off animation logic with shared primitives (P1).** **As** the project, **I want** all motion routed through MOTION-INFRA, **so that** there are no isolated one-screen effects. **Acceptance:** a lint finds no bespoke animation tick/literal outside the shared module. *(deps: Epic 2)*
- [✓] **MOTION-AUDIT-3: document the motion language + contributor examples (P2).** **As** a contributor, **I want** a motion guide with working snippets, **so that** future work stays consistent. **Acceptance:** the guide covers every pattern with a compiling snippet; referenced from AI_GOVERNANCE §4. *(deps: Epics 1–7)*

### BRAND-11 — 11.0 brand identity rollout (operator 2026-06-19, new logo Start5.png)
> Operator: "This is the new Icon, Artwork, Predominant Art for the 11.0 release. Change all surfaces to reflect and update the installer art. Add this icon as a watermark on the lower visible area of the notification hub. This logo should be used as a hero image any place branding or information about the platform is located." Source: assets/icons/Start5.png (the MCNF windowed-constellation logo, RGB white-bg).
- [✓] **BRAND-11-1: regenerate every brand surface from the new logo.** DONE: flood-keyed the white background to transparency (corners only — interior window gridlines + constellation nodes preserved); regenerated the panel launcher icon (assets/brand/app-launcher.png, applet include repointed off Start3), the hicolor app/window icon set magic-mesh.png at all 9 sizes (16–512), the brand-loader slots (app-icon/monogram/logo-lockup PNGs + the baked SVG fallbacks the RPM ships to /usr/share/mde/brand: monogram.svg + app-icon.svg embed the 1:1 logo, wordmark/wordmark-hero/greeter-wordmark.svg are a 4:1 logo+"MCNF" lockup), and greeter-hero.png (logo on Carbon Gray-100). Legacy Start3.png removed. Brand is now **fixed-palette** (`BrandSlot::is_tintable` → false; baked assets embed the full-color logo, no currentColor). 9 mde-theme brand tests green (+ the env-fragile fallback test now isolates the system dir so it passes on an RPM-installed host).
- [✓] **BRAND-11-2: installer/greeter art.** DONE: greeter-hero.png + greeter-wordmark + the brand-loader assets are the first-boot/greeter art (shipped via the RPM brand-asset block); regenerated to the new logo. (No separate ISO splash — first-boot branding rides these assets.)
- [✓] **BRAND-11-3: Notification Hub watermark.** DONE: a pre-faded (~16% alpha) transparent logo (assets/brand/watermark.png) is pinned to the Hub's lower visible area via a `Stack` overlay (bottom-center, 148px, no pointer handlers so clicks pass through to the launch bar). mde-notify-center builds clean.
- [✓] **BRAND-11-4: hero everywhere branding/info appears.** DONE: the About panel renders the logo as its hero (Brand LogoLockup→Monogram fallback) and its codename auto-tracks the major version (11.x → "Winter-Is-Coming", else 10.0 "Magic Mesh").
