- [✓] **KIRON-2: `event/toast/show` Bus lane + shell drain + sound + suppression.** **As** any node/worker/surface, **I want** a typed `event/toast/show` Bus message (severity, source-host, flag, headline, optional action-verb) that the shell's `ToastHost` subscribes + paints once per frame, with **DND + a per-VM-session focus/gaming mute** suppressing Info/Warning (a **Critical always breaks through**), the **single notification sound** fired here (severity-scaled via the E12-16 mixer, DND-aware), and a click-through that runs the action-verb (`action/shell/goto`), **so that** fleet-wide alerts + in-shell OSD share one authority. **Acceptance:** a remote worker's `event/toast/show` appears on the shell chyron with the source hostname + a working action; a volume hotkey flashes the OSD level bar via a direct call independent of the queue; DND/focus-mute suppresses Info/Warning + sound; a Critical breaks through with sound; no double-beep.
- [✓] **KIRON-3: adopt across surfaces (prove ≥2 real emitters).** *(RECONCILED 2026-07-02: the ≥2-real-emitters + no-bespoke-overlay acceptance is met in-tree. Emitter 1 = the OSD tier — `mde-shell-egui/main.rs:244` `toasts.flash_osd(level)` routes the E12-19 host-controls hotkey OSD through the ONE KIRON `ToastHost` (no bespoke overlay left — E12-19 removed it). Emitter 2 = the chyron/alert tier — `mackesd/workers/chat.rs` `toast_for_alert`→`emit_toast` publishes `event/toast/show` on the Bus (NOTIFY-CHAT-5's Warning+ severity alerts → the shell drains to a lower-third chyron end-to-end). Two independent real producers (direct OSD call + Bus lane), pattern runtime-reachable, no second overlay path. Marker was drift.)* **As** the platform, **I want** E12-19's rich-OSD to emit the OSD tier into the KIRON `ToastHost` and a security/folded-alert path wired as the first real `event/toast/show` producer, **so that** the pattern is runtime-reachable with no second overlay path (§7). **Acceptance:** the host-controls hotkey OSD renders through the one ToastHost; a real security alert raises a lower-third chyron end-to-end; `grep` finds no bespoke overlay render left in host-controls; NOTIFY-CHAT-5 emits here (retarget note below).

### NOTIFY-CHAT — ICQ-style mesh chat = the unified messaging + notification interface (25-Q survey 2026-07-02; design: docs/design/mesh-chat-icq.md)

- [✓] **NOTIFY-CHAT-1: `mde-chat` core crate.** **As** the platform, **I want** a shared `mde-chat` crate — `Message` (kinds: Text, Clipboard, Alert{severity,action}, File, CallAction, RemoteAction), `Conversation`/`Room` **ring-buffer** log, `Roster`/`Contact`/`Presence`, **Ed25519 sign/verify**, and the pure **alert-fold** (a Bus alert payload → a Message from a host, hostname=username) — all pure + headless-tested, no I/O (the mde-kvm/mde-seat pattern), **so that** every downstream unit shares one model. **Acceptance:** message serde round-trips per kind; sign→verify rejects a tampered sender; the ring buffer evicts oldest past cap; the alert-fold maps a real `event/security/alert` payload to an Alert message from the origin host; zero I/O in the crate; layered-tiers gate green.
- [✓] **NOTIFY-CHAT-2: mackesd `chat` worker.** **As** every node (incl. headless), **I want** a `chat` worker that does live Bus send/recv (`action/chat/send` · `event/chat/message`), owns the Syncthing per-conversation + per-room ring logs, queues to offline peers and backfills on reconnect, **subscribes EVERY existing alert/event Bus lane and folds each into a message from its origin host** (Lock 11, no emitter changes), and gossips presence (auto-from-mesh-health + manual Away/DND/Invisible), **so that** messages + fleet-wide notifications flow with no fixed center. **Acceptance:** a 1:1 message delivers live between two nodes (signed, acked); an offline peer's message queues + backfills on return; a live `event/security/alert` appears as a folded message on every node; presence transitions on heartbeat loss; runs headless (emit+relay, no UI).
- [✓] **NOTIFY-CHAT-3: `Surface::Chat` — the ICQ UI.** *(integrated 2f2ad89 from farm agent 7798f67; roster/panes/composer/delivery/message-kinds already existed — the gap was rooms: a Rooms group (system+ad-hoc) + unread badges + a room pane (Join/Dissolve/timeline/composer scope:room) over `state/chat/rooms`+`state/chat/conversation/room:*`; 149 mde-shell-egui tests, §4 tokens, chat.rs only. Per-member room delivery is worker fan-out, gated.)* **As** an operator, **I want** the authentic ICQ surface — the contact roster (Online/Offline groups, per-contact status icon + presence, unread bold+count), conversation panes opened per contact, a composer, and Sent/Delivered/Queued-offline delivery checkmarks — rendered in Quasar `Style` over the real worker (no demo_data), **so that** the whole fleet's people + machines are one roster. **Acceptance:** the roster lists every mesh peer + self + VMs with live presence; selecting a contact opens its ring-log timeline; a sent message shows the correct delivery state; headless mount-tessellate test; §4 tokens only.
- [✓] **NOTIFY-CHAT-4: message kinds + per-contact actions.** *(RECONCILED 2026-07-02: already implemented in `mde-shell-egui/chat.rs` — `MessageKind` Text/Emoji/Clipboard(one-click re-copy)/File(send_to via `bus_backend::send_to`, §6)/alert cards (severity + inline `action/shell/goto`), plus per-contact Call (hands to `mde-voice` dial verb) + Remote-Control (VDI) conversation rows. Marker was drift; verified present + compiled in the 149-test mde-shell-egui suite. Live SIP/VDI session-open is the honest integration-gated edge, consistent with §7.)* **As** a user, **I want** each message kind to render + act — text/emoji, clipboard items (one-click re-copy), alert cards (severity color + inline `action/shell/goto`), file Send-To (reuse `mde-files`), and the per-contact **Call** (SIP via `mde-voice`) + **Remote Control** (VDI into that host via `mde-vdi`/Instances) conversation actions — each reusing its owning crate (§6 glue), **so that** chat is the launch point for data, voice, and remote desktop. **Acceptance:** a clipboard message re-copies locally; an alert card's action navigates the shell; a file transfers over the mesh and downloads; Call opens a live SIP session and Remote Control opens that host's VDI desktop, both from the conversation.
- [✓] **NOTIFY-CHAT-5: rooms · muting · sound · toast.** *(integrated a004f57 from farm agent 9d19f45; new mde-chat `notify.rs` NotifyPrefs + `conversation.rs` RoomKind/creator/dissolve/system-rooms + mackesd `workers/chat.rs` wiring. Rooms live+offline-backfill, open-join/creator-dissolve, muted-but-logged, Warning+ toasts/DND via the ONE KIRON `event/toast/show` lane (no 2nd overlay; §6 — the mesh worker emits severity, KIRON owns sound via the mixer), per-severity system rooms. Re-gated .50/chat5rg: mde-chat 36 + mackesd suite green, clippy 0, layered-tiers clean. Honest: cross-node room-descriptor gossip via Syncthing union at bootstrap (messages fan out live regardless).)* **As** an operator, **I want** ad-hoc named rooms + auto system rooms (All Fleet + per-severity) with **open self-join** and a shared Syncthing room ring-log fan-out (sender → Bus to online members + append to the room log; offline backfill), per-contact/room mute + a global per-severity threshold, per-kind DND-aware **sounds via the seat mixer** (E12-16), and severity-colored **toasts emitted via the KIRON `event/toast/show` lane** (the one lower-third chyron ToastHost — KIRON-1/2 own the sound + suppression; this unit just emits), **so that** groups work and the machine-alert firehose is tamed. **Acceptance:** a room message reaches all online members live + an offline member on reconnect; anyone can join, creator can dissolve; a muted contact is silent but still logged; a Warning+ alert sounds/toasts while Info stays silent; DND suppresses both.
- [✓] **NOTIFY-CHAT-6: hard cutover — subsume Notifications + Clipboard.** *(integrated 4eb2462 from farm agent d7ba086; DELETED Surface::Notifications+Clipboard (enum/ALL 11→9/arms/test), `notifications.rs`+`clipboard.rs`, the WHOLE `mde-notify` crate (+ workspace member + deps in mde-shell-egui/mackesd), and mackesd's dead alert-mirror worker + prune_shared_alerts (its only reader was the deleted panel). Straight delete — no fold needed: the chat worker (NOTIFY-CHAT-2) already folds every alert lane into per-host `alert:<host>` conversations over the replicated Syncthing log. Added the chrome **unread indicator** (`ChatState::total_unread` over the roster watermarks) routing to Surface::Chat via a new `apply_nav` seam (KIRON chyron refactored onto it too; retired notifications/clipboard verbs aliased to Chat). Sound = XDG named-theme via canberra (SystemChime, no bundled assets — nothing to re-home). grep Surface::Notifications/Clipboard empty; mde-notify gone. Workspace build green + mde-shell-egui 171 + mde-chat 36 tests; re-gated on BigBoy.)* **As** the platform owner, **I want** `Surface::Notifications` + `Surface::Clipboard` (and their modules) deleted and `mde-notify` folded into `mde-chat` + removed once the last consumer moves, plus the chrome bar's presence/unread indicator that expands into `Surface::Chat`, **so that** there is ONE notification interface and no dead parallel surfaces (E12-14 decommission discipline). **Acceptance:** `grep Surface::Notifications` / `Surface::Clipboard` empty; `mde-notify` gone from the workspace; the chrome unread indicator opens Chat; the RPM/packaging carries the sound assets; workspace builds + tests green.

### MESHFS — Mesh-Sync storage status (restore the removed verb)

_The `mackesd mesh-fs-status` verb was deleted with the LizardFS plane (SUBSTRATE-6) but the Workbench Mesh Storage panel + `mde-files` still shell it — restored Syncthing-native. Operator: "All" (build local + per-peer + Syncthing-native)._

- [✓] **MESHFS-1: `mesh-fs-status` subcommand — local mount report** *(farm-verified, pushed)*
  As an operator, I want the Mesh Storage panel to show real storage instead of "returned no output", so it stops lying on every node.
  - [✓] `mackesd mesh-fs-status` emits JSON — the union shape both GUIs read (peers[].{addr,used_bytes,avail_bytes} + goal + quota_cap/limiting + master_reachable/undergoal/offline); local mount `df` as one peer; absent mount → valid non-empty JSON (no false error)
- [✓] **MESHFS-2: per-peer aggregation from the replicated probes** *(farm-verified, pushed)*
  As an operator, I want every mesh node's storage usage in the report, so I see the whole share, not just this node.
  - [✓] a serde-defaulted `MeshFsUsage` field on `ServiceDescriptors` (`mackes-mesh-types/src/peers.rs`), populated in `descriptors.rs::probe_mesh_fs()` via `df`; `mesh_fs_report` aggregates every peer's from `read_peers()` over the replicated directory (skipping un-probed peers); falls back to the local mount on a fresh mesh; goal = present-peer count
- [✓] **MESHFS-3: Syncthing-native completion** *(farm-verified, pushed)* — operator greenlit building the REST client (2026-06-30).
  As an operator, I want real replication state, so an out-of-sync share surfaces honestly.
  - [✓] new `mackesd::syncthing` reads the API key from the provisioned `config.xml` (string-scan, no XML/HTTP dep) + shells `curl` to `/rest/db/completion?folder=mcnf-mesh`; `mesh_fs_report` adds `sync_completion_pct` (`None` when Syncthing unreachable — degrades honestly, never a faked 100%); the Mesh Storage panel renders "replication N%". Pure parsers unit-tested; the live curl is gated on a real Syncthing node. Per-device `undergoal`/`offline_peers` granularity = a future refinement.

---

### QBRAND — Quasar platform branding (professional icons + version placement)
Design: `docs/design/quasar-branding.md` (operator-locked 2026-07-03; **round-2 artwork placement locks added same day** — official art `assets/brand/MDE-QUAZAR-*.png` landed `35fef34`; codename spelling = **Quazar** (Z); product name = **MDE Quazar — Mackes Display Environment**; splash=W1 (animate its bar), default wallpaper=W4 + all 5 ship, About=MAIN, README banner=W2, product icon = crop+vector-trace the round mesh mark; QBRAND-10's mark/wordmark placeholders to be retraced from the official art). Branding for the DRM-native egui platform; supersedes the retired Cosmic-era `branding.md` (BRAND-1..10) for the egui world. Mechanism: a `brand` submodule inside `mde-theme` (single source of truth). Icons = inline SVG → egui vectors, monochrome Carbon line-art. Version = `12.0.0 "Quasar"`, baked via build.rs.

- [✓] **QBRAND-1: build-info stamped via build.rs (`mde-theme::brand::build`).** **As** an operator, **I want** the exact version+provenance baked at compile time, **so that** every surface + `--version` report one authoritative build identity. **Acceptance:** a `build.rs` stamps `BuildInfo { version, codename, git_hash, build_date, channel }` (env→`env!`); `version_line()`→`12.0.0 "Quasar"` + `full()`→the complete build-info string; `mde-shell-egui --version` and `mackesd --version` print `full()`; a no-git build yields a valid line with a sentinel hash; tests assert the format.
- [✓] **QBRAND-2: SVG icon loader + set (`mde-theme::brand::icons`).** **As** the shell, **I want** monochrome Carbon SVG glyphs rasterized at DPI + token-tinted, **so that** every surface icon is crisp + consistent. **Acceptance:** `icon(id,size,tint)` rasterizes an inline-SVG const to an egui texture tinted from a `Style` token; renders crisp at HiDPI/200% zoom; ids exist for the product mark + ~12 surface glyphs + 3 role badges + node glyph; tests cover load + tint. *(needs QBRAND-10 art + QBRAND-1)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-3: product mark + wordmark lockup (`mde-theme::brand::logo`).** **As** a user, **I want** the platform's logo lockup available to the shell, **so that** the boot-splash + About render the real mark. **Acceptance:** `brand::logo` exposes the mark + wordmark lockup via the same SVG→egui path, token-tinted, sized for splash + About; test renders non-empty. *(needs QBRAND-10)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-4: DRM boot-splash (`mde-shell-egui`).** **As** a Workstation user, **I want** a branded startup screen, **so that** boot looks professional. **Acceptance:** before the dock mounts, the shell paints `brand::logo` lockup + `version_line()` centered on the Carbon background; dismisses when the shell is ready; a shell test asserts the splash state renders the lockup + version. *(needs QBRAND-1/3)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-5: chrome version tag + role badge (`mde-shell-egui`).** **As** an operator, **I want** the running version + node role always visible, **so that** I know what I'm on. **Acceptance:** the shell chrome/status bar shows a subtle `version_line()` tag + this node's role badge (`brand::icons`); Carbon tokens only; test asserts the tag + badge render. *(needs QBRAND-1/2)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-6: About / System surface (`mde-shell-egui`).** **As** a user, **I want** a canonical "about this platform" screen, **so that** provenance + license are one click away. **Acceptance:** an About surface renders the logo lockup + `brand::build::full()` (version · hash · date · channel) + codename + license/NOTICE links; reachable from the dock/nav; test asserts build-info + lockup render. *(needs QBRAND-1/3)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-7: dock + surface glyphs from `brand::icons` (`mde-shell-egui`).** **As** a user, **I want** every dock surface to use the consistent Carbon glyph set, **so that** the shell looks unified. **Acceptance:** each `Surface` pulls its glyph from `brand::icons` (replacing ad-hoc glyphs), tinted active/inactive by token; renders crisp at HiDPI; test asserts every surface resolves a glyph. *(needs QBRAND-2)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-8: Mesh Map per-node role badge + version (`mde-mesh-view`/`mde-shell-egui`).** **As** an operator, **I want** each node's role + running version on the Mesh Map, **so that** fleet version-skew is visible. **Acceptance:** the Mesh Map renders each node's role badge (`brand::icons`) + its reported version; a node on an older build is visibly distinct; test asserts per-node badge + version render from `MeshState`. *(needs QBRAND-2; reuses the peer version field)*
- [✓] **QBRAND-9: single-source product icon into packaging.** **As** a deployer, **I want** the RPM `.desktop` launcher + favicon + app icon to all use the one product mark, **so that** the brand is identical everywhere. **Acceptance:** the generate-rpm assets + `.desktop` + favicon derive from the single brand product-icon source (`regen-app-icon.sh` / `assets/brand`); `rpm -qlp` (gated build) shows the icon shipped; programmatic asset-list check passes on the farm.
- [✓] **QBRAND-10: draw the monochrome Carbon glyph set (art).** **As** a designer, **I want** the actual Carbon line-art glyphs authored, **so that** QBRAND-2/3 have real assets. **Acceptance:** SVG line-art (single-weight, Carbon grid) for the product mark + wordmark + ~12 surface glyphs + 3 role badges + node glyph land under `assets/brand/quasar/`; each validates as clean SVG + renders through the QBRAND-2 loader; starting point = `assets/icons/carbon/` + `assets/brand/*`.
- [✓] **QBRAND-11: wallpaper set shipped + shell backdrop.** **As** a user, **I want** the official Quazar wallpapers on my desktop, **so that** the platform looks branded out of the box. **Acceptance:** all five `MDE-QUAZAR-WALLPAPER*.png` ship in the RPM (programmatic asset-list verify); the shell's desktop backdrop renders WALLPAPER4 by default (scaled/cropped correctly at any resolution); a settings affordance selects among the five (persisted); honest fallback to the Carbon field if the asset is missing. *(needs the artwork, landed 35fef34)*

---

### EDITOR — native Zed-style code editor (`mde-editor-egui`)
Design: `docs/design/editor.md` (operator-locked 2026-07-03, 2-round survey). Rope-backed egui widget · Tree-sitter highlighting · standard keymap · tabs+splits · fuzzy finder/palette/project-search · own project tree + reuse mde-files/mde-term (§6). LSP = Phase 2, mesh CRDT collab = Phase 3.

#### Phase 1 — solo code editor
- [✓] **EDITOR-1: surface scaffold + dock mount.** **As** a user, **I want** an Editor in the dock, **so that** I can open it. **Acceptance:** new crate `crates/desktop/mde-editor-egui` exposing `editor_panel`/`EditorSurface` (mirror `mde_files_egui::files_panel`); `Surface::Editor` added to `dock.rs` (enum + ALL/SURFACES + label + hint + render arm) + `IconId::Editor` glyph (`assets/brand/quasar/surface-editor.svg` + registry); opens from the rail + renders an honest empty state; shell tests green. *(done: 64d8d3a — new `mde-editor-egui` crate + `Surface::Editor` + `IconId::Editor`; farm-gated build/test/clippy green, shell 319/319.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-2: rope buffer core.** **As** a user, **I want** to open/edit/save real files, **so that** the editor is functional. **Acceptance:** a `ropey`-backed `Buffer` opens a file (encoding-aware), tracks a line index, applies inserts/deletes, undo/redo (grouped), and saves (bytes on disk change); handles a 100k-line file without stalling; tested. *(done: ba11481 — `mde_editor_egui::Buffer` (open/from_text/scratch, insert/remove, char↔line index, grouped undo/redo w/ cursor hints + `commit_group`, save/save_as writes real bytes, is_dirty/is_lossy); ropey 1.6.1 vendored on the farm; 15 tests incl. 100k-line tolerance (~0.13s). panel.rs untouched for EDITOR-3.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-3: text widget — render + cursor + selection.** **As** a user, **I want** to see + edit text, **so that** I can write code. **Acceptance:** a custom egui widget paints visible lines + a gutter (line numbers), a cursor, click/drag/double/triple selection, keyboard editing (insert/delete/nav/home-end/page), vertical+horizontal scroll, soft-wrap toggle; mono font reused from the shell; Carbon tokens; tested. *(done: 2cde627 — `widget.rs` (viewport-culled render + pinned gutter, blinking caret + anchor selection, click/drag/word/line select, full keyboard incl. Ctrl-word/Home-End/Page/Shift-extend/goal-column + undo-redo via buffer hints + copy/cut/paste, ScrollArea + caret-reveal, soft-wrap WrapMap); `EditorSurface` owns `Option<Doc>` via open_path/open_text/open_scratch/close, empty-state has a scratch button. 32 editor + 339 shell tests; re-gated green against the DroidSansMono mde-egui. Deferred to EDITOR-4/5+: multi-cursor, tree-sitter, tabs/splits, palette.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-4: multi-cursor + column selection.** **As** a power user, **I want** multiple cursors, **so that** I edit in parallel. **Acceptance:** add-cursor-above/below + add-at-next-match + box/column selection; every cursor applies the same edit; tested fan-out. *(done: f88f20f + clippy-fix follow-up — N-caret `EditorView` (add above/below, add-at-next-match, Alt-click toggle, Alt-drag column select), highest-index-first fan-out edits, overlap-merge, Esc-collapse; single-caret path + EDITOR-3 tests intact. Combined editor re-gated green: 71 tests, clippy clean. **Salvaged by hand** after the authoring agent hit a weekly usage limit mid-gate — built + tested green, I finished its 4 residual clippy warnings + the lib.rs doc-merge with EDITOR-7.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-5: Tree-sitter syntax highlighting.** **As** a developer, **I want** accurate highlighting, **so that** code is readable. **Acceptance:** tree-sitter incremental parse per buffer; a core grammar set (rust, python, js/ts, toml, json, markdown, bash) vendored + building on the farm; highlights map to a `mde-theme` code-theme token module (no raw hex); re-highlights incrementally on edit; tested per language. *(done: 2e03331 — ALL 8 grammars vendored + built on the farm (ts 0.25.10); `highlight.rs` engine: buffer edit-deltas → `Tree::edit` + old-tree chunk-wise reparse (never materializes the doc; `full_parses()` instrumented — stays 1 across typing/undo), visible-window `spans_in` querying matching the viewport cull; `mde_egui::code` Carbon token module (KEYWORD/FUNCTION/TYPE/STRING/… + CodeToken map — the shared Style crate is where §4 tokens live); unknown ext honestly plain. editor 96/96 + mde-egui 105/105, clippy 0.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-6: tabs + splittable panes.** **As** a user, **I want** side-by-side buffers, **so that** I compare/reference. **Acceptance:** tabbed open buffers per pane + horizontal/vertical splits (reuse/generalize the `mde-term-egui` split machinery); focus/close/move-tab; a split shows two buffers at once; tested.
- [✓] **EDITOR-7: fuzzy file finder + command palette.** **As** a user, **I want** keyboard nav, **so that** I move fast. **Acceptance:** Cmd-P fuzzy-opens a file by name from the project; Cmd-Shift-P runs a named editor command; both reuse the shell's fuzzy/ladder store; tested match+dispatch. *(done: 58786df — panel-level Cmd-P finder (bounded project walk, skips target/.git → open_path) + Cmd-Shift-P palette (6 real commands: Save/Scratch/Toggle-Tree/Toggle-Wrap/Close/Open-Folder) over a self-contained `fuzzy.rs` matcher; chords consumed before the widget (widget.rs untouched); 63 tests, clippy clean. Farm-gated; agent finished just before its account hit a weekly usage limit.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-8: project search + find/replace.** **As** a user, **I want** to search across files + in-buffer, **so that** I find code. **Acceptance:** project-wide search shells `ripgrep` (honest fallback if absent) with jump-to-hit; in-buffer find/replace (regex opt); tested.
- [✓] **EDITOR-9: project tree + mde-files Send-to-Editor.** **As** a user, **I want** to browse + open files, **so that** the editor is a workspace. **Acceptance:** a lightweight project-tree panel lists the open folder (expand/collapse, open-on-click); `mde-files` gains a "Send-to-Editor" action (reuse the existing surface-launch/Bus path) that opens the file in the editor; tested both open paths. *(done: 3f553fa — `project_tree.rs` (lazy dirs-first tree, real read_dir, honest unreadable-dir note) as a toggleable ☰ SidePanel → `EditorSurface::open_path`; `mde-files/editor_open.rs` verb `action/editor/open` + `EditorOpenRequest`, Files "Open in Editor" menu → Bus → shell `EditorLaunchWatch` drains → `editor.open_path` + switch to Surface::Editor. editor 39 + mde-files 148 tests; combined re-gated green 351 shell. main.rs auto-merged clean with POWER-4.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-10: embedded integrated terminal.** **As** a developer, **I want** a terminal in the editor, **so that** I run commands in context. **Acceptance:** `mde-term-egui`'s `TabbedTerminal` embedded as a toggleable editor panel (reuse, like TERM-16 mounted it) running a real PTY in the project cwd; tested reachable.
- [✓] **EDITOR-11: save/autosave + dirty + external-reload.** **As** a user, **I want** my work safe, **so that** I don't lose edits. **Acceptance:** dirty indicator per tab, manual save + optional autosave, external-change file-watch prompts reload; tested.
- [✓] **EDITOR-12: code folding + symbol outline.** **As** a developer, **I want** structure, **so that** I navigate big files. **Acceptance:** tree-sitter-driven fold regions (fold/unfold) + a symbol outline (functions/types) with jump; tested.

#### Phase 2 — language intelligence (LSP) *(follow-on)*
#### EDTB — the Word-97 toolbar (operator 2026-07-04; 10-Q survey; design: docs/design/editor-toolbar.md)

Serialize behind EDITOR-5 (panel.rs/widget.rs contention); EDTB-1 establishes menu_bar.rs/toolbar.rs/md_actions.rs, then 2/3 extend.

- [✓] **EDTB-1: menu bar + Standard toolbar core.** **As** a user, **I want** Word's chrome on the editor, **so that** it feels like a full app. **Acceptance:** `menu_bar.rs` (File/Edit/View/Insert/Format/Tools/Table/Help → real seams: file ops, clipboard, undo/redo, finder/palette, view toggles; no dead items — unbacked entries absent per lock #4) + `toolbar.rs` Standard strip (New/Open/Save/SaveAs/Cut/Copy/Paste/Undo/Redo/Find/Zoom, Word order, Carbon glyphs); zoom = editor-view scale; mounted above the editor in `panel.rs`; tested dispatch per control. *(done: e7fa386 — data-driven menu_bar.rs + toolbar.rs; Find/Insert/Format/Table honestly omitted per lock #4 (EDTB-2/3/8 backfill them, asserted by test); zoom 25–400% scales the whole Metrics chain; every item drives the palette/widget/buffer seams, no duplication; 126/126, clippy 0.)*
- [✓] **EDTB-2: Formatting toolbar + markdown actions.** **As** a writer, **I want** B/I/U/styles/lists, **so that** formatting is one click. **Acceptance:** `md_actions.rs` pure edit engine (wrap-selection `**`/`*`/`<u>`, Style dropdown → `#` heading levels, `- `/`1. ` line-prefix toggles, indent shift) — multi-cursor aware, sane undo groups, unit-tested; the Formatting strip drives it (Word order). *(engine done: 1745f43 — md_actions.rs pure engine over &mut Buffer + char-range carets: toggle_wrap (** / * with real star-run arithmetic, ~~, `, <u>), set_heading 0-6, list toggle (bullet↔numbered convert + renumber per run), 2-space indent shift floored at 0, insert_table; multi-caret highest-first fan-out, one commit_group per op, 38/38. The Formatting STRIP that calls this lands once EDTB-1's panel settled — followup UI unit.)*
- [✓] **EDTB-3: Insert Table grid-picker + Font/Size global setting.** **As** a user, **I want** Word's table grid + font control, **so that** structure is easy. **Acceptance:** `table_picker.rs` hover rows×cols grid → md-table skeleton insert; Font+Size dropdowns write the persisted **platform** font/size (shell config + live apply via the mde-egui font install) — honest cross-crate wiring, not per-buffer.
- [✓] **EDTB-4: compact-aware bars.** **As** a user, **I want** lean compact mode, **so that** space survives. **Acceptance:** expanded = menu + both toolbars; compact = menu bar only; follows the shell mode; tested.
- [✓] **EDTB-5: Print via CUPS (P2).** **As** a user, **I want** to print, **so that** paper exists. **Acceptance:** Print pipes the buffer as formatted mono text to `lp` (honest error when no CUPS/printer); Print Preview paginates; the buttons appear only with this phase (lock #4).
- [✓] **EDTB-6: Spelling via hunspell (P3).** **As** a writer, **I want** squiggles + F7, **so that** typos die. **Acceptance:** system hunspell (RPM `Requires`; shell-out `hunspell -a` acceptable) drives red squiggly underlines in the widget + an F7 walk dialog (suggest/replace/ignore); honest absent-state when hunspell is missing; md/text files first. **DONE:** new `spell` module (egui-free engine + per-doc `SpellChecker`) shells to `hunspell -a` off the paint thread (`SpellWorker`, debounced on the buffer revision); parses `*`/`+`/`-`/`&`/`?`/`#` → miss spans + suggestions; the widget paints a `Style::SPELL` red squiggle (new mde-egui token) under each miss with a suggestion hover; `F7` / Tools → Spelling / a toolbar button open the walk (Suggest / Replace [undoable rope edit] / Ignore / Ignore-All / Add-to-dictionary); honest `SpellState::Unavailable` greys the control + a "hunspell not installed" note when hunspell is absent (probed once); md/text buffers only. RPM should `Requires: hunspell` + a default dictionary (noted in `spell.rs`).
- [✓] **EDTB-7: split markdown preview (P4).** **As** a writer, **I want** rendered output, **so that** markup reads as a document. **Acceptance:** View→Preview + toolbar toggle opens a side-by-side rendered pane (headings/bold/italic/lists/tables via Carbon text styles), live as you type; token-styled; tested render mapping.

- [✓] **EDITOR-LSP-1: LSP client subsystem** — per-language server lifecycle, initialize/shutdown, document sync (glue over `lsp-types`/a tower-lsp client); reachable, honest gated state when no server. *(done: 4a55c75 — lsp.rs, stdio JSON-RPC framing hand-rolled, initialize/shutdown/exit, didOpen/Change/Close, publishDiagnostics→typed Vec<Diagnostic>; honest LspUnavailable when the server binary is absent; fake-sh-server tests (real framing) 13/13; lsp-types 0.97 vendored. UI consumption = EDITOR-LSP-2.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-LSP-2: diagnostics + completion + hover** — gutter/inline diagnostics, completion popup, hover docs, signature help.
- [✓] **EDITOR-LSP-3: goto-definition + find-references + rename + format** — cross-file navigation + workspace edits.

#### Phase 3 — mesh-native collaborative editing *(follow-on, the Zed differentiator)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-COLLAB-1: CRDT buffer** — a conflict-free replicated buffer model (glue over an existing Rust CRDT, e.g. `yrs`/`diamond-types`), local-first, deterministic merge; tested convergence. *(done: 84a8d04 — crdt.rs, yrs 0.23 CollabDoc: insert/remove→binary updates, apply_remote order-tolerant merge, encode_state_vector/diff sync seam, EditSink Buffer-bridge contract; convergence tests (both orders, 3-way, offline-reconnect) 12/12. Bus session = EDITOR-COLLAB-2.)*
- [✓] **EDITOR-COLLAB-2: mesh share-session** — publish/subscribe an editing session over mde-bus/Nebula (P2P, no cloud), scoped to a mesh SessionId; remote cursors/selections + presence; permissions (host/guest RW/RO).
- [✓] **EDITOR-COLLAB-3: follow mode + Mesh-Map presence** — follow a collaborator's viewport; show active co-editing sessions on the Mesh Map (reuse the QBRAND-8 per-node presence). *(done: 10ffdb7 — Presence.viewport (wire-compat) + follow/unfollow + local-input break at the session layer; follow.rs apply_follow + banner; EditorView::place_selection seam; mesh_view.rs CoEditWatch drains collab/session/* lanes → QBRAND-8 badge pip (live end-to-end); editor-side live mount honestly rides the future panel.rs share-session unit (same gate as COLLAB-2). Farm .170: 398+634 tests, clippy 0 touched-file warnings.)*

---

### BUGS — live demo QA (2026-07-03, Eagle/.13 on 12.0.0 Quazar)
- [✓] **BUG-STORAGE-1: Storage surface has no local disk info on a Workstation.** **As** a workstation user, **I want** my own disks/partitions in the Storage surface, **so that** it's useful on a single node. **Root cause:** `crates/mesh/mackesd/src/bin/mackesd.rs:6539` gates the `StorageWorker` behind `worker_role::runs("storage", role_rank)`, which excludes the **workstation** rank — so a workstation never publishes `state/storage/<node>`, and the surface shows "No peer has published a storage mirror yet." **Acceptance:** the storage worker runs on the workstation role too (a workstation publishes its own `state/storage/<node>` from UDisks2); the Storage surface on a solo workstation shows its local disks/partitions; unit test that `worker_role::runs("storage", workstation_rank)` is true; verified live on Eagle (Storage shows local disks). *(alt/also acceptable per §7: the surface reads local disks directly as a fallback — but the worker-on-workstation fix is cleaner + matches the mesh-mirror architecture.)* *(code fix landed 5f2f3d2 — the real defect was that `"storage"` was **missing from the `WORKER_TIERS` census**, so the `mackesd role-workers` diagnostic omitted it (read as "excluded from Workstation"); now an explicit `("storage", 0)` entry + tested 13/13. ⚠️ NOTE: runtime behavior was already correct (2-role model, rank is a floor → the worker already spawned on Workstation), so this alone won't change the live `.13` symptom. Root cause of the demo symptom is almost certainly that **`.13`'s deployed mackesd predates the E12-20 storage worker** — STAYS `[>]` pending a 12.x mackesd redeploy to `.13` + live-verify (`mackesd role-workers` lists `storage` AND `state/storage/<node>` present on the Bus).)* *(✅ CONFIRMED via live-verify 2026-07-03: `.13` runs **11.4.5**; `strings $(command -v mackesd)` finds NO `state/storage`/`StorageWorker` — the storage worker is absent from the deployed binary. `role-workers` on 11.4.5 lists 30 workstation workers, none `storage`. FIX = deploy a **12.x** mackesd/RPM to `.13` (also lands BUG-BRIGHTNESS-1 properly), then re-verify. Tracked as the `.13` 12.x redeploy follow-up.)*
  - 2026-07-07 storage-publish fix slice: live Eagle on 12.0.0-1 listed the `storage` worker but still had no `state/storage/*` topics. The worker now writes mirrors/progress directly through the configured `Persist` bus root before falling back to the best-effort `mde-bus` subprocess path. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::storage::tests::unavailable_backend_publishes_typed_state --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::storage --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 43/43; `.170` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role::tests::storage_mirror_publishes_on_every_role_including_workstation --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed. Still `[>]` until a rebuilt RPM is promoted to Eagle/seats and `state/storage/<node>` plus the GUI local-disk rendering are live-verified.
  - 2026-07-07 closure: the live defect had two additional runtime legs after the 12.x redeploy: storage defaulted to `/root/.local/share/mde/bus` instead of the service `MDE_BUS_ROOT=/run/mde-bus`, and UDisks2 reported `Block.Device`/`PreferredDevice` as NUL-terminated byte arrays (`ay`), which the parser treated as empty. Fixed both. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `default_bus_root_prefers_service_environment` and `udisks_byte_array_device_paths_decode_as_strings` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::storage --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 45/45. Built final candidate `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` (`112289467` bytes, 2026-07-07 10:21 EDT) and promoted it to Eagle; `mackesd`, `nebula`, `syncthing` active. Live Eagle mirror `/run/mde-bus/state/storage/peer:UNIT-EAGLE/...` publishes `backend=available`, `device_count=4`: `/dev/zram0`, `/dev/sda` with `/dev/sda1`, `/dev/nvme0n1` with `/boot/efi`, `/boot`, `crypto_LUKS`, and `/dev/dm-0`. The Storage surface folds `state/storage/*` and renders `topology.devices`, so the local-disk data path is live ✓.
- [✓] **BUG-BRIGHTNESS-1: backlight brightness fails for the non-root session shell (`Permission denied os error 13`).** **As** a user, **I want** the brightness slider to work, **so that** I can dim my panel. **Root cause:** `crates/desktop/mde-seat/src/backlight.rs:129` does a raw `std::fs::write` to `/sys/class/backlight/<name>/brightness` (root:root 0644); the DRM shell runs as the non-root session user → EACCES. **Acceptance:** brightness set goes through **logind `org.freedesktop.login1.Session.SetBrightness(subsystem,name,value)`** (the privileged session API — no root needed), with the direct sysfs write as a root fallback; PLUS the RPM ships a `video`-group udev rule (`ACTION=="add", SUBSYSTEM=="backlight", chgrp video + chmod g+w brightness`) as belt-and-suspenders; the shell user is in `video`. Verified: the brightness slider changes the panel on Eagle without a permission error. *(Eagle got an immediate udev+chmod workaround 2026-07-03.)* *(done: 1427cbd — logind `SetBrightness` via the existing zbus SysBus seam + sysfs fallback + `packaging/udev/90-mde-backlight.rules` shipped in the RPM; farm gates green (mde-seat 76/76); operator confirmed the slider works live.)*
- [>] **BUG-VIDEO-1: Media/video player won't play a local video on Eagle.** **As** a user, **I want** to open a video file from my home dir and have it play, **so that** the Media surface is usable locally. **Root cause (live-verified 2026-07-03):** `.13` runs magic-mesh **11.4.5**, whose RPM ships **no `mde-media-egui` binary** (the MEDIA player epic is a 12.x feature; `command -v mde-media-egui` = absent). The local files are `~/Downloads/*.mkv` = **AV1 video (libdav1d) + Opus audio in Matroska**; `libmpv.so.2` + gstreamer + ffmpeg are all present on the box. **Acceptance:** deploy a **12.x** build to `.13` so the Media surface/player exists; the player opens a local `~/Downloads/*.mkv` and plays it (video + audio); confirm the player's decode path handles **AV1+Opus/Matroska** (libmpv-backed → uses system dav1d) — if the 12.x player can't decode AV1, that's a follow-on codec task. Verified live on Eagle: a local .mkv plays. *(Same old-build root cause as BUG-STORAGE-1 — folded into the `.13` 12.x redeploy; also recon `crates/desktop/mde-media-egui` decode backend to confirm AV1 before deploying.)* *(⚠️ RECON VERDICT 2026-07-03 — a straight 12.x deploy will NOT play it; the codec is a red herring (mpv+dav1d on `.13` decodes AV1+Opus/mkv fine). TWO build/wiring blockers: **(A) shipped player is `FakeMpv`** — the `mpv` feature (`mde-media-core/Cargo.toml` `mpv=["dep:libmpv2"]`, default off) is never enabled in any build path (`xcp-build.sh:448` builds `--features drm`; `build-rpm-fedora43.sh:100` default features), so the embedded player simulates playback (flips to Playing, 0:00 frozen, no A/V). FIX: add `media-mpv=["mde-media-egui/mpv"]` to `mde-shell-egui/Cargo.toml`, build `--features drm,media-mpv`, and provision **`mpv-libs-devel`** on the farm build VMs (links system libmpv at build — farm lacks it, honest-gated). **(B) video stage is a placeholder** — `mde-media-egui/src/app.rs:1324` `player_stage` paints a dark rect; no mpv-render-API→egui-texture upload and no DRM overlay-plane scanout (MEDIA-2 shipped only as a seam). FIX: implement the frame→display path. ⚠️ **DESIGN FORK (operator): render via mpv render-API → egui `TextureHandle` upload, vs a DRM overlay plane beneath the egui shell.** Local-file open + surface mount are FINE (MEDIA-18 done; Open-URL/Index-folder accept `~/Downloads/*.mkv`). Storage+brightness are unaffected by this — the `.13` 12.x deploy still fixes those two.)* **L0+L1 VALIDATION SLICE 2026-07-10 (`docs/gpu_encoder.md`):** the design fork is resolved as **texture upload, not the render API** — `mde-media-core`/`mde-media-egui` are workspace `unsafe_code = "forbid"`, and libmpv2 6.0's render API is OpenGL-only (verified against the upstream `kohsine/libmpv2-rs` source), so driving it would need an off-screen EGL/GL context built from `unsafe` FFI these crates cannot carry; the DRM-overlay path is explicitly phase 2 and stays out of scope. Instead `MediaEngine` grew `latest_frame(&mut self) -> Option<VideoFrame>` (`mde-media-core/src/engine.rs`): `MpvEngine::latest_frame` (`src/mpv.rs`) captures via mpv's `screenshot-to-file "video"` command (throttled ~150 ms) and decodes the PNG with the same `png = "0.18"` path `mde-shell-egui`'s `chooser::decode_png_rgba` already uses — no GPU/EGL context needed at all (confirmed against mpv's own `video/out/vo.c` + `player/client.c` sources: frame retention for screenshots is generic core-VO behaviour, not per-driver, so it works under `vo=null`; the synchronous `mpv_command` client API blocks until the spawned screenshot-writer thread finishes, so there is no read-after-write race). `MpvEngine::new` now also sets `vo=null` (the shell owns the one DRM/KMS master; mpv must never grab its own display, extending the existing "don't fight the seat for a resource" comment already covering terminal/input) and `audio-fallback-to-null=yes` (a missing/broken audio device degrades to silent video instead of aborting the file). `FakeMpv::latest_frame` stays an honest `None` unless a test scripts one via new `with_frame()`. **Release wiring:** `media-mpv = ["mde-media-egui/mpv"]` landed on `mde-shell-egui/Cargo.toml`; `xcp-build.sh`'s `rpm` recipe and `build-rpm-fedora43.sh` both now build the shell `--features drm,live-helper,live-vdi,media-mpv` (previously `drm`-only / default-features — the exact gap the recon named) and `dnf install mpv-libs-devel` (resolves via Fedora 42's `mpv-devel`/`mpv-libs` 0.40.0) alongside the other native build deps; `setup-build-vm-toolchain.sh` provisions it on the base toolchain too. **L0 production-feature-detection test** (the doc calls this out explicitly): `release_shell_configuration_enables_the_real_media_engine` (`mde-shell-egui/src/main.rs`) asserts `!cfg!(feature="drm") || cfg!(feature="media-mpv")` — always compiled, only a real constraint once `drm` is on. **L1 fixture-decode test**: `mde-media-core/tests/mpv_fixture_decode.rs` (`#![cfg(feature = "mpv")]`, mirrors the `media_smoke` real-clip honest-gate shape) drives a real `MpvEngine` (`TypeId`-checked, never `FakeMpv`) against a checked-in ~14 KB synthetic fixture (`tests/fixtures/tiny_clip.mkv` — VP8 video + Opus audio in Matroska, `ffmpeg` `testsrc2`/`sine` lavfi sources, chosen for decoder portability over codec coverage — AV1 stays the standing follow-on codec task the original recon already named); asserts `Playing`/`Ended` is reached, `current-ao` resolves (a real device or the honest `null` fallback), and `latest_frame()` returns a nonblank frame (pixel-chunk comparison, not raw-byte — a fixed bug the farm run itself caught) with a nonzero, stable FNV-1a checksum. **Frame sink**: `player_stage` (`mde-media-egui/src/app.rs`) now uploads `latest_frame()` output through a new `VideoTextureCache` (an `egui::TextureHandle`, mirroring `mde-shell-egui/src/vdi.rs`'s `VdiState.texture` allocate-once/`set`-after pattern) and paints it in place of the placeholder rect once loaded, with the Carbon OSD scrim/title unchanged on top; `media_panel`/`player_view`/`MediaApp`/the shell's `Shell` struct all thread the cache through. **Farm evidence (`.130` BigBoy, `libmpv` freshly provisioned live via the sanctioned `xcp-build.sh rpm`-style remote `sudo dnf install` pattern, then landed in the provisioning scripts above):** `cargo build -p mde-media-core -p mde-media-egui -p mde-shell-egui` (default features) clean; `cargo test` same scope → 231+85+959 passed/0 failed (incl. the new `VideoFrame`/`FakeMpv::latest_frame`/`render_with_video` unit tests and the detection test trivially passing with `drm` off); `cargo build -p mde-media-core --features mpv` clean (confirms the real link once `mpv-libs-devel` is present); `cargo test -p mde-media-core --features mpv` → `fixture_decodes_through_real_mpv_with_a_nonblank_frame` passed (0.29s) + 231 existing passed; `cargo build -p mde-media-egui --features mpv` clean; `cargo build -p mde-shell-egui --features drm,live-helper,live-vdi,media-mpv` clean (the exact RPM release combination); `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm,media-mpv` → `release_shell_configuration_enables_the_real_media_engine` + `shell_mounts_and_renders_the_media_surface` both passed (959 total); **negative proof** `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm` (no `media-mpv`) → the detection test FAILS with the intended diagnostic, proving the gate is real, not tautological; `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean on every touched file across all four feature combinations (a handful of pre-existing `mpv.rs` pedantic/nursery warnings — never clippy-checked before, since `mpv-libs-devel` had never been on the farm — fixed in passing: doc-markdown backticks, the track-count cast, `end_reason` as `const fn`, one documented `#[allow(clippy::match_same_arms)]` in `poll()`); `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` clean on every touched file. **Remaining:** **L2** — a full `xcp-build.sh rpm` cut proving the generated RPM actually ships `media-mpv` + `rpm -qlp` evidence (not attempted here; the doc's own heavier gate) — note the shared default farm slot showed unrelated concurrent drift in `Cargo.lock` from other work, so re-sync before that cut. **L3** — live-seat validation on Eagle/`.2`: open a real `~/Downloads/*.mkv`, confirm frames visibly change over the whole clip (not just one capture), audio through PipeWire, pause/seek/OSD controls, and specifically **AV1** decode (the L1 fixture used VP8 for portability, so AV1 coverage is still the standing follow-on codec task). The DRM overlay plane (MEDIA-2 phase 2 performance path) remains explicitly unattempted, per the doc's own recommended path. *(branch `worktree-agent-a101e274e40e25d46`, not pushed — operator review pending)*
- [✓] **BUG-BLUETOOTH-1: Bluetooth had no controls in the Workbench.** **As** a user, **I want** to scan/pair/connect/manage Bluetooth from the shell, **so that** the System surface is usable for BT. **Root cause:** the `mde-seat` BlueZ backend (`bluez.rs`) is complete, but the shell's `system.rs` BT section was **read-only** — pair/connect/disconnect/trust/scan/forget were never wired to buttons. **Acceptance:** the BT panel drives the real backend verbs + a pairing dialog; no mockups. *(done: dd16142 (worker 461c23c) — adapter Power/Discoverable/Pairable/Scan toggles + per-device Connect/Disconnect/Pair/Forget/Trust; a `PairingBridge`/`bt_pairing.rs` modal (PIN/passkey/confirm/authorize) blocking off the egui thread; errors→BLUETOOTH toasts; + 3 backend gaps folded (BtDevice.address/rssi, BtAdapter.discoverable/pairable). Farm-gated: mde-seat 76/76, shell 330/330, clippy clean. Live pair/connect needs a real adapter (headless farm has none) — D-Bus round-trips tested as typed-error paths.)*
- [✓] **BUG-CHAT-1: Chat surface looked incomplete.** **As** a user, **I want** a finished chat, **so that** it feels complete. **Root cause:** the panel was real + live-wired but had four visible edge holes (no message timestamps, no self presence-picker, no status editor, no mute). **Acceptance:** the four holes closed with real worker round-trips (not local-only UI). *(done: f297611 (worker 66656bc) — `HH:MM`+hover timestamps with day separators; ICQ presence ComboBox (Free-for-Chat/Available/Away/DND/Invisible) + inline status editor via new `ACTION_CHAT_PRESENCE`; per-contact/room Mute via new `ACTION_CHAT_MUTE` + `STATE_CHAT_NOTIFY` mirror. Round-trips proven: presence/status republish `state/chat/roster` + reload from `presence.json`; mute mutates `NotifyPrefs`, persists `notify.json`, silences a muted Critical alert. Combined chat+BT+nav shell re-gated green (336 tests). Dead Clipboard/File/Call/Remote message-cards left as a follow-on.)*

## POWER — battery indicator + full power settings (operator 2026-07-03; recon+3-fork survey; design: docs/design/battery-power.md)

Live "battery does not show" + "include power settings". Backend (UPower/logind) is real; gaps = no chrome battery slot + missing settings. Operator locked FULL scope: native mde-seat power-profiles client, DRM-native idle+lid honorer, charge thresholds + rich telemetry. mde-seat backend adds serialize on snapshot.rs/upower.rs; push backend before the panel.

- [✓] **POWER-1: battery indicator in the chrome status bar.** **As** a laptop/Surface user, **I want** battery % at a glance, **so that** I don't open System to check charge. **Acceptance:** a `battery_view` `SlotView` in `chrome.rs` over the polled `SeatSnapshot.batteries` — system-pack %, charging bolt, severity dot (OK/WARN under ~20% / DANGER critical), honest "No battery"/"unavailable"; tooltip = kind+state; token-styled; unit-tested. *(done: fc6cfc5 — `battery_view` slot (Signal·BT·Vol·Batt), system-pack %, ⚡ bolt, OK/WARN(<20%)/DANGER(≤5%/Empty) dots, "No battery"/"unavailable"/pre-poll dash, tooltip=kind·state·%; token consts BATTERY_LOW/CRITICAL; standalone SlotView (folds cleanly into NAVBAR-4 tray). Farm-gated; combined shell re-gated green 339 tests.)*
- [✓] **POWER-2: mde-seat Hibernate verb + on-AC source.** **As** a user, **I want** Hibernate + to know if I'm on AC, **so that** power management is complete. **Acceptance:** `PowerVerb::Hibernate` + `PowerCaps.hibernate` (CanHibernate probe); `SeatSnapshot` carries on-AC/on-battery folded from the UPower LinePower `Online` prop (currently dropped); real D-Bus, honest None, unit-tested. *(done: 45b4412 — `PowerVerb::Hibernate` (Manager.Hibernate, CanHibernate) + `PowerCaps.hibernate`; `SeatSnapshot.on_ac: Probe<Option<bool>>` folded from LinePower `Online` (honest None when absent). mde-seat 79 tests. Integration: shell SeatSnapshot literals got `on_ac: absent()` — combined shell re-gated green.)*
- [✓] **POWER-3: mde-seat power backend v2 (profiles + thresholds + telemetry + lid).** **As** the shell, **I want** local power hardware access, **so that** the settings panel drives real hardware. **Acceptance:** a native `net.hadess.PowerProfiles` zbus client (get active + list available + set), folded to `SeatSnapshot.power_profile: Probe<..>` (honest "unavailable" when the daemon is absent); charge thresholds via sysfs `charge_control_end_threshold` (read+write); rich `Battery` telemetry (time-to-empty/full, energy-rate draw W) folded from UPower; an evdev **`SW_LID`** lid-switch watch → `SeatSnapshot` lid state. All real, honest None, unit-tested. *(done: 6d05ec3 — `powerprofiles.rs` (net.hadess.PowerProfiles get/list/set → `SeatSnapshot.power_profile: Probe<ProfileState>`), `charge_threshold.rs` (sysfs read/write → `charge_limit: Probe<Option<u8>>` + `set_charge_threshold`, EACCES surfaced honestly), `Battery` telemetry (time_to_empty/full + energy_rate W), `lid.rs` → `lid: Probe<LidState{Open/Closed/Unknown}>`. Drive: `set_power_profile`/`set_charge_threshold`. ⚠️ lid uses the `/proc/acpi/button/lid` read (evdev not farm-vendored + crate forbids unsafe → no raw SW_LID ioctl); revisit if a richer lid signal is needed. mde-seat 100 tests, shell 339; combined re-gated green. Charge-write + lid Open/Closed are HW-gated (need a real laptop).)*
- [✓] **POWER-4: Power Settings panel (System surface).** **As** a user, **I want** power settings in one place, **so that** I can tune power. **Acceptance:** extend `system.rs` "Power & Battery" + a new `power_settings.rs`: a profile segmented control (Performance/Balanced/Power-saver, honest "unavailable" when absent), a caps-gated Hibernate row, an on-AC/on-battery line, a charge-threshold slider (writes sysfs), the time-to-empty/draw telemetry, and idle-timeout + lid-action settings (persisted); each drives the real backend/honorer — no inert controls (§7); §4 tokens. *(done: 69b4f53 — `power_settings.rs` + extended `power_section`: profile segmented control (SetPowerProfile→Seat), caps-gated Hibernate, on-AC line, 50–100% charge-threshold slider (SetChargeThreshold→sysfs), time-to-empty/full + draw-W telemetry. Honest Absent/unsupported/EACCES states, no inert controls. shell 351 tests; combined re-gated green. Idle-timeout + lid-action UI moved to POWER-5 with the honorer, so they're not inert.)*
- [✓] **POWER-5: DRM-native idle + lid honorer (shell).** **As** a user, **I want** idle-suspend + lid-close to actually work, **so that** the settings aren't inert. **Acceptance:** a shell-owned idle timer (last-input → configured timeout → `seat.power(Suspend)`) + a lid-close handler reading POWER-3's `SW_LID` watch → the configured action, both driven by POWER-4's persisted settings; works on the DRM shell (no Wayland/swayidle); live-verify suspend-on-idle + lid-close on real hardware. *(done: 18a7b32 — `power_honor.rs`: idle timer (last-input reset from egui events → Suspend once, no re-fire w/o activity, default Never) + lid Open→Closed edge handler (default Suspend, debounced, unarmed-if-closed-at-start, desktop-no-lid=no-op); config `power-honor.json` via `client_data_dir` (atomic write), edited by the POWER-4 panel's new idle-timeout + lid-action pickers; tiny 2-line main.rs tick hook (auto-merged clean with EDITOR-9). shell 364 tests; live suspend/lid HW-gated. **POWER epic COMPLETE (1..5).**)*

## PLATFORM changes (operator directives)

- [✓] **PLAT-FONT-1: DroidSansMono is the default platform font.** **As** the operator, **I want** DroidSansMono as the default, **so that** the platform reads in it. **Acceptance:** the shared `mde-egui` font install embeds DroidSansMono as the default for BOTH Proportional + Monospace; every surface + the terminal render it; no stale Fira Code. *(done: f84b47e vendored `DroidSansMono.ttf` + Apache NOTICE (from Fedora google-droid-sans-mono-fonts); 2d6d09c swapped `mde-egui/fonts.rs` + `mde-term-egui/fonts.rs` + all 14 refs, deleted the dead FiraCode TTFs+OFL — `rg FiraCode` = 0 code hits; mde-egui 102 + mde-term-egui 248 tests green. ⚠️ trade-off: DroidSansMono has NO programming ligatures — the terminal loses the Fira Code ligatures (TERM-13); revert the term-only swap if ligatures are wanted.)*

## NAVBAR — bottom nav taskbar (relocate the shell dock, Carbon icon-first) (operator 2026-07-03; 15-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/workbench-navbar.md)

Moves the surface launcher from the left vertical rail to a **full-width bottom taskbar**, redrawn Carbon icon-first, folding the separate status chrome into the same bar. Tasks NAVBAR-1..3 are the visible relocation + look (dispatch first); NAVBAR-4..8 largely **serialise on `dock.rs`/`chrome.rs`/`main.rs`** (same-file — follow the core in sequence, don't parallel-edit).

- [✓] **NAVBAR-1: relocate the rail to a full-width bottom taskbar.** **As** a user, **I want** the nav along the bottom, **so that** it reads like a desktop taskbar. **Acceptance:** `dock.rs` renders via `TopBottomPanel::bottom("shell-taskbar")` (48px) with a **horizontal** entry layout; the left `SidePanel` rail is removed; the surface body fills the `CentralPanel` above it; flat Carbon `layer` fill + hairline top divider (§4); shell tests green + renders.
- [✓] **NAVBAR-2: grouping, dividers + tray-style order.** **As** a user, **I want** the surfaces sectioned, **so that** the bar is scannable. **Acceptance:** entries partition into **mesh-control ∣ apps ∣ system** with thin Carbon dividers; apps pack left, the **system** group (System/Storage/About) is pushed hard-right beside the tray; a declarative `group_of(Surface)` classifier drives it; `Surface::Editor` is in the apps group.
- [✓] **NAVBAR-3: icon-first glyph cell (label, two-tone tint, active mark, hover).** **As** a user, **I want** clear per-surface glyphs, **so that** I can tell them apart at a glance. **Acceptance:** each cell = `brand::icons` Carbon glyph + a tiny always-on label; **active glyph filled / idle outline** (two-tone, tokens); **accent top-border + brighter tint** on the active cell; hover shows the `hint` tooltip + a cell highlight; all via `mde-egui::Style` (no raw hex). *(done: NAVBAR-1/2/3 in 303cc78 (worker f43d4a5) — 48px `TopBottomPanel::bottom`, grouped mesh∣apps∣system w/ system tray-ordered hard-right, icon-first cells, accent top-border active mark, left rail deleted, body fills above. Farm-gated build+test (merged BT+nav tree re-gated on .130: 332 tests pass). NOTE lock #9 approximated: the `brand::icons` set has no separate outline artwork, so "filled vs outline" is done via tint intensity (active=solid ACCENT / idle=dim) — a true outline variant is a follow-on if wanted.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-4: unified status tray (fold `chrome.rs` in).** **As** a user, **I want** one bottom bar, **so that** status + nav aren't split. **Acceptance:** the status widgets (clock, peer count, health, quick-toggles, role badge, version tag) render in a right-aligned tray segment of the taskbar; the separate status strip is retired; no regression in what `chrome.rs` showed. *(done 2026-07-07: marker drift closed with a focused regression. `chrome.rs` is now the pure mesh/status data projection only; `main.rs::mount_dock_chrome` feeds that projection plus seat/status rollups into `DockState::set_status_inputs`; `dock::notification_rail_with_sources` renders Start/Advanced, Desktop, session entries, the bottom status detail toggle, `status::notification_rail` pips, Pin, and Clock in the one full-width bottom rail. The old separate `status_bar` local-grade pip is not mounted. Added `dock::tests::navbar4_status_tray_is_folded_into_the_bottom_rail` to pin that Advanced/status/clock share the bottom rail, the retired `status_bar` pip is absent, and the folded status detail panel still exposes the old chrome health/device content. Farm evidence on `.50`: focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::navbar4_status_tray_is_folded_into_the_bottom_rail -- --nocapture` passed; full `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` passed `41/41`.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-5: live badges (unread / peers / health).** **As** a user, **I want** at-a-glance status on glyphs, **so that** I see activity without opening a surface. **Acceptance:** `badge_for(Surface,&ShellState)` paints a Chat unread badge (from `chat::total_unread`), a Mesh Map peer count, and a health-tinted node/System dot; badges are token-styled + only shown when meaningful. *(done 2026-07-07: `DockState::set_status_inputs` now stores the live `MeshSummary` + Chat unread count instead of ignoring them, and `badge_for(Surface, &StatusInputs, transfer_count)` projects token-styled badges: Files active transfers, Chat unread count, Mesh Map online-peer count once the mesh snapshot is seen, and a health-tinted System/Settings dot from the lighthouse verdict. Zero/unseen inputs paint no badge. The renderer now uses one badge model for count pills and health dots while preserving the existing Files transfer badge seam. Farm evidence on `.50`: focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::navbar5_live_badges_project_unread_peers_and_system_health -- --nocapture` passed; full `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` passed `42/42`.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-6: keyboard + right-click context menu.** **As** a keyboard user, **I want** to drive the bar without the mouse, **so that** nav is fast. **Acceptance:** `Super`+1…9/0 jump to the Nth visible surface (reuse `hotkeys.rs`); arrow keys traverse focus; right-click a glyph opens a context menu (pin, info, close-if-closable). *(done 2026-07-07: `hotkeys.rs` now latches shell-local `NavSlot`s for `Super+1…9/0` without expanding the closed host-action table; the shell drains that latch and jumps through the dock's canonical `Surface::ALL` order, with `Super+0` selecting the tenth slot. Dock glyphs now support arrow-key focus traversal across the addressable picker order, and right-click opens a token-styled context menu with stable Pin / Info / Close rows; anchor/system surfaces omit Close honestly, while closable app/panel glyphs close back to Workbench. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui navbar6 -- --nocapture` passed `2/2`; `.50` hotkey router `super_numbers_latch_taskbar_navigation_slots_without_toggling_the_dock` passed; `.90` full `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` passed `44/44`.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-7: overflow 'More' (⋯) tray.** **As** a user on a narrow panel, **I want** every surface reachable, **so that** none is lost. **Acceptance:** when grouped glyphs + tray overflow the width, trailing/low-priority surfaces collapse into a `⋯` popup (`egui::menu`); nothing is silently dropped; measured before painting. *(done 2026-07-07: the bottom rail no longer silently drops session/task glyphs when the fixed tray cluster leaves insufficient width. `notification_rail_with_sources` now snapshots the session run, measures desired entry widths before painting, reserves a `⋯` More cell when overflow exists, renders only the entries that fit, and mounts a token-styled popup for hidden sessions. Hidden entries reuse the same `session_entry` renderer and latch `desktop_session_focus` exactly like inline entries; More has a same-click open guard so it does not immediately close on its opening click. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::navbar7_bottom_rail_more_popup_keeps_overflow_sessions_reachable -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` full `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` passed `45/45`.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-8: tie the bar to the compact↔expand mode.** **As** a user, **I want** the bar to match the shell density, **so that** compact mode reclaims space. **Acceptance:** the locked compact/expand mode selects the bar variant — expand = 48px + labels; compact = a shorter icon-only bar; one mode drives the whole shell (reuse the control-surface enum). *(done 2026-07-07: the bottom rail now consumes the same `mde_egui::Density` that `main.rs` derives from the SURFACE-9 formfactor/control-surface path and passes through `Style::install_with_density`; there is no second dock toggle. `Density::Mouse` keeps the 20px compact icon rail, while `Density::Touch` selects the 48px labelled rail variant for Advanced/Desktop/Clock/Pin/Status and makes the session overflow popup and left-dock bottom clearance use the same rail height. Added `dock::tests::navbar8_shell_density_selects_compact_or_expanded_bottom_rail` to prove compact vs expanded geometry and shared rail ids. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui navbar8_shell_density_selects_compact_or_expanded_bottom_rail -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` full `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` passed `46/46`.)*

### NAVBAR-W10 — pixel-perfect Windows-10 taskbar (operator 2026-07-04; 20-Q survey; design: workbench-navbar.md "NAVBAR-W10")

Remove the top status bar; the bottom bar becomes a Win10-pixel taskbar: icon-only 24px app row (no labels/dividers, bottom-underline active mark), a right-justified 16px icon-only tray (all chrome slots as Carbon glyphs + dots + ^ chevron + stacked clock), and a Win10-activation-style brand watermark on the backdrop. Supersedes NAVBAR locks 5/6/7 + NAVBAR-4/8's bar variants. Serialization: W10-2..4 share `tray.rs`/`dock.rs`/`main.rs` — glyphs (W10-1) land+push FIRST, then the core (W10-2), then followers.

- [✓] **NAVBAR-W10-1: tray glyph set (brand::icons).** *(done: bf9335f — 12 new tray glyphs incl. the 5-step battery fill ladder + overlay bolt, `IconId::TRAY` subset, 16/24px raster tests; mde-theme 18/18.)* **As** the tray, **I want** 16px-legible Carbon glyphs, **so that** every status is an icon. **Acceptance:** new `IconId`s + SVGs: Signal, Sessions, Chevron-up, Volume (+muted variant ok), Bluetooth-small, and a **Battery fill set** (~empty/quarter/half/threequarter/full + a charge-bolt overlay); registry + `ALL` + tests updated; crisp at 16px (2px-grid strokes); reuse node/health glyphs for Peers/Status; farm-gated mde-theme.
- [✓] **NAVBAR-W10-2: the core relayout — kill the top bar, Win10 row + tray.** **As** a user, **I want** one Win10-style bar, **so that** the shell reads like a desktop OS. **Acceptance:** top chrome strip removed (`main.rs` no longer mounts it; body fills to the top); `dock.rs` → 40px bar, 24px icon-only flat app row (no labels/dividers), bottom-edge accent underline + fill wash on active, hover fill only (no tooltips); new `tray.rs` right-justified after flexible space: 16px icons (Chat+badge, BT, Vol, Batt fill-glyph, Status; Signal+Peers behind a **^ chevron flyout**; Sessions only-when-active), tiny corner status dots, **stacked time/date clock** (click→System); tray icon click routes to the owning surface; reuses chrome.rs's fold helpers (retire its strip); shell tests updated + green. *(done: d4cb422 — tray.rs new (980 lines, 16 tests); chrome strip retired (MeshSummary/poll folds survive, badge+Expand/Collapse deleted); 40px bar, chevron flyout, stacked clock→System; battery fill ladder + bolt + dot rules; combined-tree re-gate 365/365. W10-4 note: Surface::label()/hint() were deleted — reintroduce from design copy if flyouts need names.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-W10-3: brand watermark on the backdrop.** *(done: 5848a4b — 3 ghost lines (MDE Quazar / version_line / hostname·role) bottom-right, TEXT_DIM ghosted, anchored via `dock::TASKBAR_H` so it auto-tracks the W10-2 relayout; paints on every backdrop surface; 16/16 backdrop tests. W10-6 (click→About) extends this next.)* **As** the operator, **I want** the Win10-activation-style watermark, **so that** the brand rides every screen. **Acceptance:** `backdrop.rs` paints 3 ghost lines bottom-right above the bar — "MDE Quazar" · `brand::build::version_line()` · hostname/role — dim token text, on every surface's backdrop layer; token-styled; tested.
- [✓] **NAVBAR-W10-4: micro-flyouts (Chat recent, Vol mute, BT power).** **As** a user, **I want** quick glances/actions, **so that** I don't open a surface for one toggle. **Acceptance:** Chat icon click opens a recent-messages flyout (header jumps to Chat) instead of routing; Volume flyout = status + mute toggle; BT flyout = status + power toggle; all real backend calls (Seat/mirrors); token-styled anchored popups. *(serialise after W10-2)* *(done: f4ea66a — tray.rs only; Volume flyout drives a narrow TrayVerbs seam (wpctl master vol/mute) + Bluetooth Adapter1.Powered toggle + connected count, both real backends with confirmed-write echo cache (8s TTL); Chat honestly scoped to the folded unread tally + Open Chat (recents need main.rs plumbing); one-at-a-time shared OpenFlyout, click-away close; 375/376 shell tests.)*
- [✓] **NAVBAR-W10-6: watermark links to About (W12b).** **As** a user, **I want** the watermark clickable, **so that** About is one click from anywhere. **Acceptance:** clicking any of the watermark's ghost lines switches to `Surface::About`; hover brightens the text one token step (the affordance — no tooltip per W6); the click region matches the text rect (not a dead zone); routing plumbed from the backdrop paint to the nav state; tested (hover tint + click→About). *(serialise after W10-3 lands — extends its backdrop.rs watermark.)* *(STAGED: backdrop.rs done in agent commit 5c8f19d1 — hover-brighten works, click latches a Surface::About nav-request via take_nav_request(ctx); INERT until a 1-line main.rs drain lands. Held to land bundled with the wire AFTER the KDC-9 main.rs work, to avoid a dead-code interim + a main.rs collision.)* *(done: f48eff2 backdrop + the main.rs drain — watermark hover-brightens one token step + click routes to Surface::About (curtain-guarded, one-shot take_nav_request idiom); farm shell build green.)*
- [✗] **NAVBAR-W10-5: compact-mode tray collapse.** **As** a user, **I want** compact mode leaner, **so that** the tray yields space. **Acceptance:** in compact mode the tray renders only ^ + clock (all icons into the flyout); expand restores the strip; the old Expand/Collapse chrome button is gone (the mode rides the shell). *(serialise after W10-2)* *(WON'T-DO 2026-07-05 — OBSOLETE: VDOCK-6 ripped out the horizontal bottom bar + VDOCK-3 removed the chevron flyout (route-on-click quads); there is no control-surface mode enum in mde-shell-egui to consume. Implementing as written = reverting the landed VDOCK cutover. Superseded by VDOCK-1's Super-toggle auto-hide, which already satisfies "compact yields space".)*

### NAVBAR-UNION — merge the Nav Bar + Desktop Chooser into one picker (operator 2026-07-03 follow-up survey; design: docs/design/workbench-navbar.md "one picker")

The bar and `chooser.rs` (the Picker) become one picker system sharing one `ChooserState`. Rides **after NAVBAR-1..3**; touches `dock.rs`/`chooser.rs`/`main.rs` — serialise on those files.

- [✓] **NAVBAR-U1: Desktop entry as a split button.** DONE 2026-07-07. The bottom-rail Desktop cell is now a split button: the main half latches a Desktop reconnect/focus request while routing to `Surface::Desktop`, and the caret/source flyout is landed under NAVBAR-U2. `Shell::mount_dock_chrome` first checks the local broker-log rail projection; if this seat already has a visible desktop session, the Desktop click focuses the Desktop face without querying chooser recents or fabricating a duplicate reconnect. With no broker-visible session, it asks the existing `ChooserState` to reconnect the newest recent source through the same `activate` + `confirm_connect` path the expanded chooser uses; if no recent source can connect, or a credential prompt is needed, the shell stays on the Desktop/chooser face. Focused rail controls activate on Enter/Space: the Desktop half latches reconnect/focus, the caret opens the source flyout, and a focused source row latches the chooser source id. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui visible_desktop_sessions_focus_instead_of_reconnecting_recents -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chooser::tests::compact_reconnect_uses_the_newest_recent_source -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::the_desktop_rail_cell_latches_a_reconnect_request -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::the_desktop_split_button_is_keyboard_reachable -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` (39 passed). **Acceptance:** the Desktop cell renders as a split button; main click reconnects the last/active remote desktop, or opens the picker if none; caret opens the source flyout; token-styled; keyboard-reachable.
- [✓] **NAVBAR-U2: source flyout in the bar.** DONE 2026-07-07. Added the compact Desktop source flyout to the bottom rail split control: the Desktop icon remains the main reconnect action, and the adjacent micro chevron opens a foreground popup above the rail listing discovered + pinned sources from the same `ChooserState`. The shell passes `ChooserState::rail_sources()` into `dock::notification_rail_with_sources`; rows are bounded UI summaries only, sorted with favorites/recents first, and selecting one drains a source id back to the shell, which calls `ChooserState::connect_source_id()` through the same `activate` + `confirm_connect` path as the full chooser. If auth is needed, the chooser's pending prompt remains authoritative; the dock does not fabricate a session or keep a second source store. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chooser::tests::compact_source_rows_reuse_chooser_state_and_selected_source_connects -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::the_desktop_rail_caret_opens_sources_and_latches_a_pick -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` (38 passed). **Acceptance:** the caret opens a bar popup listing discovered + pinned sources from shared chooser state; selecting one connects through the chooser path; popup uses the existing foreground flyout idiom and is tested.
- [>] **NAVBAR-U3: active sessions as temporary bar entries.** **As** a user, **I want** my open desktops in the bar, **so that** I can switch like a taskbar. **Acceptance:** each connected remote desktop shows a live glyph in the bar (a 'desktops' run), appearing on connect + removed on disconnect; click focuses that session; a session/count badge (reuse NAVBAR-5). **PARTIAL SLICE 2026-07-07:** the bottom rail first rendered the current Desktop request from the existing `VdiState::requested_summary()` as a named taskbar-style session entry (`SessionRailEntry`, `IconId::Sessions`, target label + protocol), immediately after Desktop and before the right tray; clicking it focuses `Surface::Desktop`. With no concrete target it preserves the old dim generic Sessions micro-icon. **BROKER-LOG SLICE 2026-07-07:** added `session_rail.rs`, a shell-local projection of the broker's public `action/vdi/session` JSON wire. The shell now folds Open/Active/Disconnect/Close actions from the Bus log, filters to this client peer, and feeds non-closed entries into the bottom rail before falling back to the pending `VdiState` request. This consumes the broker wire shape without depending on `mackesd` or creating a parallel session type; `SessionStore`/etcd remains the live multi-seat authority when it lands. **FOCUS SLICE 2026-07-07:** broker-backed rail entries now carry their `SessionId`; clicking one latches that id through `DockState::take_desktop_session_focus()`, routes to `Surface::Desktop`, and marks the local rail projection live for that session without publishing a fake broker lifecycle transition. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui session_rail::tests::broker_session_actions_fold_into_local_rail_entries -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui session_rail::tests::focused_session_entry_marks_the_local_rail_entry_live -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::a_requested_desktop_session_renders_as_a_named_bottom_rail_entry -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi::tests::requested_summary_names_the_pending_desktop_and_protocol -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` (38 passed). **Remaining:** true cross-seat/multi-session truth requires the broker/roaming `SessionStore` live etcd/Syncthing session directory; this slice reads the local Bus action log and does not claim the shared plane is live.
- [✓] **NAVBAR-U4: one state, two faces.** DONE 2026-07-07. The shell owns one `ChooserState` and uses it for both faces: `chooser_panel(ui, &mut self.chooser)` is the expanded Desktop chooser, while `self.chooser.rail_sources()` and `self.chooser.connect_source_id()` feed and mutate the compact bottom-rail flyout. The compact face returns only a source id through `DockState`; the shell hands that id back to `ChooserState`, so protocol choice, credentials, favorites, recents, manual sources, pending prompts, and connect handoff all stay in the one chooser model. Added a regression proving a compact selection of an external endpoint without a sealed credential raises the pending credential prompt in the same `ChooserState` that the expanded `chooser_panel` renders. `session_rail.rs` remains separate on purpose: it mirrors the broker's public VDI session log for NAVBAR-U3 entries and does not duplicate chooser source state. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chooser::tests::compact_selection_and_expanded_panel_share_the_same_pending_picker -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chooser::tests::compact -- --nocapture` (3 passed). **Acceptance:** compact and expanded chooser faces read/mutate the same `ChooserState`; no second source store; tested pending-state continuity across compact→expanded.

## CURTAIN — the secure login/lock curtain (operator 2026-07-04; 10-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/lock-curtain.md)

The missing login gate for the DM-less DRM shell: a full-screen Carbon lock/login curtain (slide+settle-bounce), giant clock, PAM auth, lock-screen media transport. All in `mde-shell-egui` — CURTAIN-1 establishes `curtain.rs`; 2/3/4 serialize behind it (and behind the in-flight W10-2 on main.rs/hotkeys).

- [✓] **CURTAIN-1: the curtain surface + motion + clock.** **As** a user, **I want** a lock screen, **so that** the seat is coverable. **Acceptance:** `curtain.rs` full-screen top layer with the state machine (Unlocked/Dropping/Locked/Revealing/Verifying/Lifting/Backoff); slide-down + settle-bounce drop, lift-up unlock (Motion tokens); giant digital HH:MM + date face; idle-dim to faint; while not Unlocked ALL input routes to the curtain only; Super+L drops it; tested state transitions + input-exclusivity. *(done: e88f31a — curtain.rs (1310) + main.rs; state machine + settle-bounce motion + giant clock + idle-dim; 4-layer input exclusivity (pointer whole-screen Foreground area, per-frame focus-steal, hotkey/edge/chyron gates, and closed the vdi::forward_input raw-drain leak that would reach a live guest under lock); injectable Verifier seam (default NotWired CANNOT grant — CURTAIN-2 fills PAM) + face_extras seam for CURTAIN-4; 388 combined shell tests, 0 new warnings.)*
- [✓] **CURTAIN-2: PAM auth + two-stage reveal + backoff.** **As** the operator, **I want** real auth, **so that** the lock is secure. **Acceptance:** `pam_auth.rs` runs a real PAM conversation for the seat user OFF the render thread (channel bridge); any key reveals the password field (Esc returns to the face); wrong password → honest error; 5 fails → 30s cooldown with countdown (field disabled); farm-probe libpam bindings EARLY; unit-test the state/backoff logic via an injectable auth seam (the live PAM leg verified on real hardware).
- [✓] **CURTAIN-3: triggers — boot-gate, idle/lid Lock, logind signal.** **As** a user, **I want** locking everywhere it should happen, **so that** coverage is complete. **Acceptance:** persisted `require_login_at_boot` (default ON once shipped) starts the shell Locked before any surface; POWER-5's honorer gains a **Lock** idle action + lid-Lock routes to the curtain in-process; a logind Lock/Unlock listener drops/lifts it; `loginctl lock-session` works live; tested trigger routing. *(touches power_honor.rs/main.rs/hotkeys.rs — serialize)*
- [✓] **CURTAIN-4: lock-screen media transport + status row.** **As** a listener, **I want** playback on the curtain, **so that** music doesn't need an unlock. **Acceptance:** the unified now-playing strip (active player's title/artist, play/pause/next/prev, volume) drives the REAL music/media transport seams while locked — the ONLY interactive elements besides unlock; a status glanceables row (battery/mesh/date) reuses the tray folds; no message content anywhere; tested transport-while-locked + content-privacy.

## VDOCK — the left vertical auto-hide dock (operator 2026-07-04; 25-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/vertical-dock.md)

REPLACE the horizontal Win10 bottom bar with a left-edge, full-height, auto-hide vertical dock. Clock removed → Timers&Alarms; status icons → stacked 2×2 quads; Settings/Show-Desktop → a 2×2 system quad. Super toggles + pin, slide-from-left, ~48px solid dark panel. A re-layout of the grouped-picker/tray work, not a rebuild. Serialize the dock.rs units.
