- [✓] **VDOCK-1: the vertical dock frame + auto-hide.** **As** a user, **I want** a left dock I summon, **so that** the screen is uncluttered. **Acceptance:** a left `SidePanel`/`Area` ~48px, full-height, solid Carbon-dark + hairline right divider; hidden by default, **Super toggles** it (hotkeys path) with a **pin** to hold open (#9/#13), **slide-in-from-left** motion (#14); central content fills the freed width when hidden; no focus/input steal while hidden (DRM-seat safe); tested toggle/pin/slide + hidden-passthrough. *(reworks main.rs + dock.rs frame — serialize all VDOCK)* *(reconciled done 2026-07-05, session 759c1f91: the frame+auto-hide is ALREADY LIVE in dock.rs — Super-tap reveal latch + pin + DOCK_SLIDE_KEY slide Motion + hidden-passthrough (dock.rs VDOCK-1 block ~383-475), landed with the VDOCK-2..6 wave; marker was stale. No new code needed.)*
- [✓] **VDOCK-2: the vertical app picker.** **As** a user, **I want** my apps grouped vertically, **so that** I launch by section. **Acceptance:** Workbench lead pinned top; the 6 groups stacked top→bottom, **single-column** icon-only cells (#2/#11), each group with a **horizontal accent label** above + a **left-rail accent stripe** + divider (#4/#21, PICKER-2 tokens); **left-edge accent bar** on the active surface (#10); ~24px icons (#12/#23); **'…' overflow** popup when groups exceed height (#22); all 13 app surfaces present once (Settings/Desktop moved to the system quad); routing unchanged; tested membership + active-mark + overflow. *(after VDOCK-1)*
- [✓] **VDOCK-3: the status 2×2 quads.** **As** a user, **I want** compact status, **so that** glances are quick. **Acceptance:** the tray reworks into **stacked 2×2 quads** anchored bottom (#6/#8): Chat[badge]/BT/Vol/Batt, then Status/Signal/Peers/Sessions; ~18px icons; each cell **routes to its owning surface** (#15, no flyouts); Chat's badge counts CHAT-FIX-2 unread + peer messages (#19); battery-fill/dot logic carried over; tested quad layout + route + badge. *(after VDOCK-1; reworks tray.rs)*
- [✓] **VDOCK-4: the system 2×2 quad + Power menu.** **As** a user, **I want** system actions gridded, **so that** they match the status size. **Acceptance:** a 2×2 quad (#7/#17): Settings→System, Show-Desktop→existing route, Lock→curtain, Power→a **Lock/Suspend/Reboot/Shutdown menu** (#18, reboot/shutdown typed-armed); sized to the status icons; tested each cell dispatches + the armed power menu. *(after VDOCK-1)*
- [✓] **VDOCK-5: Timers & Alarms surface (clock replacement).** **As** a user, **I want** timers/alarms, **so that** the removed clock earns its place. **Acceptance:** a clock-glyph status cell showing the time as its glyph (#20) opens a new `Surface::Timers` — create/run countdown **timers** + set **alarms** that fire notifications via the CHAT-FIX-2 producer even when the surface is closed (a shell-side or daemon timer that survives surface switches, risk); Carbon tokens; tested a timer fires an alarm/notification. *(after VDOCK-1; new surface)* *(done: 6e2906a — timers.rs (1063) + dock clock strip + per-frame tick (POWER-5 idiom, fires with surface closed); alarms ride the CHAT-FIX-2 event/notify/timer lane (prefix-folded, no mackesd change); absolute-deadline persistence; excluded from Surface::ALL per lock #20 (clock strip is its only home, test-pinned); farm 636 tests/clippy-0 on .130.)*
- [✓] **VDOCK-6: rip out the horizontal bottom bar.** **As** the shell, **I want** one dock, **so that** the layout is coherent. **Acceptance:** the bottom `TopBottomPanel` taskbar is removed (#25); the shell body fills top-to-bottom with the vertical dock on the left; the old horizontal taskbar()/tray-flyout code retired or dormant; every surface still reachable exactly once; shell layout tests updated + green; the PICKER-3 layout harness extended to pin the vertical geometry. *(the final cutover — after VDOCK-1..5 land)*

## KDC-MESH — KDE Connect over the Nebula mesh (operator 2026-07-04; 16-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/kdc-mesh.md)

Move the existing KDE Connect subsystem (`crates/kdc/` + `kdc_host` worker + the SEC-5 mesh-shunt) onto the Nebula overlay so a phone (Android Nebula client + KDE Connect app) is a full mesh member with all features across all nodes. Overlay-only transport, one mesh-wide pairing, kdc_host on every node. Android apps are STOCK — "one Mesh device" is a host-side fanout (design note).

- [✓] **KDC-MESH-1: overlay transport (Nebula-only).** **As** a phone, **I want** KDC over the overlay, **so that** it works anywhere I have mesh. **Acceptance:** an `OverlayTransport` in `crates/kdc/mde-kdc-host` binds 1716 on the node's Nebula overlay IP + dials peers/phones by overlay IP; overlay-only (no LAN direct, #3); `kdc_outbound` sends over it; nothing binds the public NIC (tested — bind addr = overlay); LAN transport retired/gated off. §7 + proto tests green.
- [✓] **KDC-MESH-2: directed discovery over the mesh roster.** **As** a host, **I want** to find the phone without broadcast, **so that** discovery crosses Nebula. **Acceptance:** the `kdc_host` worker consumes the mesh-shunt roster + peer directory to learn the phone's overlay IP + every host's; hosts directed-announce to the phone (no UDP broadcast, #2); the phone's overlay IP flows from the enroll/roster; tested roster→announce.
- [✓] **KDC-MESH-3: mesh-wide pairing + universal role.** **As** the mesh, **I want** one pairing recognized everywhere, **so that** the phone isn't tied to a node. **Acceptance:** the `PairingStore` record replicates on the substrate so every node's `kdc_host` recognizes the phone (#5); `kdc_host` → **rank-0** in `WORKER_TIERS` (every node incl. lighthouses/headless, #15) with the census count-tests updated; the firewall preset opens 1716 on the **overlay iface only**, never public (tested default-deny public); a node that hasn't synced the pairing honest-gates.
- [>] **KDC-MESH-4: QR pair-to-mesh onboarding.** **As** an operator, **I want** one scan to enroll+pair, **so that** onboarding is trivial. **QR PAYLOAD/RENDER SLICE 2026-07-07:** the Phones hub Pair tab now renders a real in-shell QR matrix using a compact `mde-kdc-mesh-pair:<base64url-json>` payload with versioned `enroll` and `pair` fields. The `pair` field carries the existing overlay KDC target (`mde-kdc-pair:<device-id>@<overlay-ip>:1716`); the `enroll` field carries a pasted short-TTL token from `mackesd found` or `mackesd onboard invite-issue` when the operator provides one, an automatically daemon-minted `mde-invite:` token when available, and is honestly `null` for pair-only bring-up until a fresh token is published. The raw payload is visible/copyable for scanner bring-up, and the QR is painted directly from the encoder matrix through egui rectangles (no fake bitmap asset). Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo check -p mde-shell-egui`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui phones_hub::tests::pair_qr_payload_carries_enroll_and_pair_fields -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui phones_hub::tests::pair_qr_payload_honestly_allows_pending_enroll_token -- --nocapture`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui phones_hub::tests::normalized_enroll_token_trims_manual_token_for_the_qr -- --nocapture`. **AUTO TOKEN SLICE 2026-07-10:** `kdc_host` now serves `action/connect/mesh-enroll-token`, mints a short-TTL onboarding invite through the existing onboard invite issuer, records it in the bearer ledger, publishes latest state to `state/connect/mesh-enroll-token`, and replies with the same token; the Phones hub automatically requests a token when the manual override is empty and no fresh daemon token is present or the token is close to expiry, then folds the reply into the Pair QR. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-kdchost` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `57/57`; `.50` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd mesh_enroll_token --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `2/2`; `.90` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui enroll_token -- --nocapture` passed `8/8`; `.170` slot `kdc-auto-enroll-fmt` farm-side touched-file `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check` passed. **PAIRING RECORD SLICE 2026-07-10:** `kdc_host` now has daemon-boundary coverage for `action/connect/pair-device`: the regression spins up a loopback TLS phone, drives the same sync Bus verb handler the shell uses, proves TOFU captures the presented fingerprint, writes the canonical pairing record, persists the sealed session, and survives reopening `PairingStore`. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `kdc-pair-record-kdchost2` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `58/58`; `.50` slot `kdc-pair-record-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd connect_verb_pair_device_tofu_pins_and_persists_the_pairing_record --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `kdc-pair-record-firstpair` `cargo test -p mde-kdc-host first_pair -- --nocapture` passed `3/3`; `.170` slot `kdc-pair-record-fmt3` farm-side touched-file `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check` passed. **Remaining:** Android-side scan redemption. **Acceptance:** the Phones hub renders a QR encoding the mesh enroll token + a KDC pairing token; scanning it (Android Nebula client + KDE Connect) enrolls the phone onto Nebula AND pairs it mesh-wide (#14); the RSA-4096 TOFU + fingerprint-pin completes over the overlay (#4); tested token gen + the pairing-record write.
- [✓] **KDC-MESH-5: bidirectional mesh notifications.** **As** a user, **I want** notifications both ways, **so that** phone + mesh talk. **Acceptance:** phone notifications appear on every desktop (all-nodes, #6) AND the CHAT-FIX-2 local-event feed pushes to the phone (#9); reply-from-desktop where supported; a de-dup/coalesce so one phone notification isn't N unpleasant desktop toasts (risk); over the overlay transport; tested both directions + de-dup. *(composes with CHAT-FIX-2)*
- [>] **KDC-MESH-6: remote input + media control.** **As** a user, **I want** phone-as-touchpad + media keys, **so that** I control the desktop. **MEDIA-CONTROL SLICE 2026-07-07:** `kdc_host` now advertises `kdeconnect.mpris` only after adding an inbound handler; paired phone MPRIS command bodies are parsed through `mde-kdc-proto`, mapped through a fixed allowlist (`play`, `pause`, `play-pause`, `next`, `previous`, `stop`), dispatched to the host's active MPRIS player via `playerctl`, and audited as `kdc_media_control`. At that point state-pull (`kdeconnect.mpris.request`) stayed unadvertised until player state reports existed, so the host did not overpromise. The full RPM weak-recommends `playerctl`; the headless server variant does not. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services` (48 passed, including the MPRIS capability + allowlist regressions) and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision::tests::base_rpm_recommends_workstation_media_helpers --features async-services` (1 passed). **REMOTE-INPUT HANDOFF SLICE 2026-07-09:** `mde-kdc-proto` now parses `kdeconnect.mousepad.request` bodies for bounded motion, scroll, click, text-key, and special-key events (including KDE's lowercase click aliases); `kdc_host` advertises the mousepad request capability only after adding the handler, gates it on paired phones, publishes sanitized `action/seat/remote-input` Bus events with phone/source/timestamp metadata, and audits successful batches as `kdc_remote_input`. This is still a handoff seam, not direct evdev/uinput injection. Farm evidence: `.130 kdc-remote-input-check` `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services`; `.50 kdc-remote-input-proto2` `cargo test -p mde-kdc-proto mousepad -- --nocapture` (4 passed); `.170 kdc-remote-input-loopback2` `cargo test -p mde-kdc-proto every_plugin_kind_dispatches_unambiguously_on_packet_kind -- --nocapture` (1 passed); `.90 kdc-remote-input-kdc` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` (50 passed); `.90 direct rustfmt` on the touched files passed. **SEAT-CONSUMER SLICE 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now has a Workstation-tier `seat_remote_input` worker that drains KDC's `action/seat/remote-input` Bus handoff, validates `kdc_host` source/op/phone/timestamp plus bounded move/scroll/button/text/special-key payloads, calls `MDE_SEAT_REMOTE_INPUT_COMMAND` or `/usr/libexec/mackesd/seat-remote-input` when present, and publishes honest retained `state/seat-remote-input/<node>` plus per-event `event/seat-remote-input/<node>` outcomes (`injected`, `unavailable`, `error`) instead of faking injection. Farm evidence: `.130 kdc-seat-check` `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services`; `.50 kdc-seat-tests` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::seat_remote_input --features async-services -- --nocapture` (7 passed); `.90 kdc-seat-role` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` (19 passed); `.170 kdc-seat-producer` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host::tests::kdc_remote_input_body_maps_motion_click_and_text --features async-services -- --nocapture` (1 passed); `.90 direct rustfmt` on `seat_remote_input.rs` and `worker_role.rs` passed; local `git diff --check` on touched files passed. **PACKAGED UINPUT HELPER SLICE 2026-07-09:** the full Workstation RPM now ships `/usr/libexec/mackesd/seat-remote-input` from `install-helpers/seat-remote-input.py`; the helper validates the worker's JSON event shape and injects move/scroll/button/text/special-key operations through `/dev/uinput`, exiting 69/78 as unavailable when the live seat is inaccessible so the daemon reports honest `unavailable` instead of fake success. `CommandSeatInputInjector` now maps those unavailable helper exits to the worker's unavailable status, and the server RPM variant deliberately does not ship the desktop seat helper. Farm evidence: `.130 kdc-seat-helper-check` `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services`; `.50 kdc-seat-helper-tests` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::seat_remote_input --features async-services -- --nocapture` (7 passed); `.90 kdc-seat-helper-pkg` `cargo test -p mackesd full_rpm_ships_seat_remote_input_helper_but_server_variant_does_not --features async-services -- --nocapture` (1 passed); `.170 kdc-seat-helper-script` Python `py_compile`, `--self-test`, and `--dry-run` passed; `.90` scoped rustfmt on `seat_remote_input.rs` and `role_provision.rs` passed; local Python probes and `git diff --check` passed. **Remaining:** live `/dev/uinput` injection validation on a Workstation seat and live Android validation. **Acceptance:** the phone drives touchpad + keyboard input on the active desktop via the shell's evdev/input-injection seam (#10) + media play/pause/next/vol + presenter on the playing node (media surface transport seam); real events (§7); tested input inject + media dispatch. *(touches mde-shell-egui input + media seams — coordinate)*
  - **Media volume slice 2026-07-10:** paired-phone `kdeconnect.mpris` commands now cover bounded player volume steps as well as transport controls: `VolumeUp`/`volume-up` aliases map to `playerctl volume 0.05+`, `VolumeDown`/`volume-down` aliases map to `playerctl volume 0.05-`, and raw phone action strings still never execute. The `MDE_HOME`-redirecting KDC audit tests are now serialized with a test-only mutex so the default parallel harness remains hermetic. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `kdc-media-volume-kdchost2` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `51/51`; `.50` slot `kdc-media-volume-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd mpris_media_command_maps_volume_to_bounded_playerctl_steps --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `kdc-media-volume-audit2` `cargo test -p mackesd kdc_mesh8_a_phone_action_appends_a_hash_chained_audit_row --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `kdc-media-volume-proto` `cargo test -p mde-kdc-proto mpris -- --nocapture` passed `8/8`; `.170` slot `kdc-media-volume-fmt2` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/kdc_host.rs` passed; local `git diff --check` passed. Remaining: live `/dev/uinput` injection validation on a Workstation seat and live Android validation.
  - **Presenter command slice 2026-07-10:** the default paired-phone runcommand catalog now includes Presenter next/previous/start/exit commands backed by the packaged `/usr/libexec/mackesd/seat-remote-input` helper and fixed KDE special keys (PageDown/PageUp/F5/Esc), so presentation control is a first-class paired-phone action without arbitrary phone shell. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `kdc-presenter-kdchost` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `52/52`; `.50` slot `kdc-presenter-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd presenter --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `1/1`; `.90` slot `kdc-presenter-runcommands` `cargo test -p mackesd runcommands --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `4/4`; `.170` slot `kdc-presenter-fmt` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/kdc_host.rs` passed. Remaining: live `/dev/uinput` injection validation on a Workstation seat and live Android validation.
  - **MPRIS state-report slice 2026-07-10:** `mde-kdc-proto` now models `kdeconnect.mpris.request` bodies plus upstream `kdeconnect.mpris` player-list/state fields; `kdc_host` advertises `.mpris.request`, gates request/control packets on an existing pairing, answers paired-phone player-list / now-playing / volume pulls with `kdeconnect.mpris` reports built from `playerctl` (`playerList`, play state, position, length, artist/title/album/art URL, volume percent, and control capability flags), and still routes request-form transport actions through the fixed allowlist. Album-art payload transfer remains deliberately unsupported (`supportAlbumArtPayload=false`). Farm evidence: `.130` slot `kdc-mpris-state-kdchost3` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `55/55`; `.50` slot `kdc-mpris-state-proto` `cargo test -p mde-kdc-proto mpris -- --nocapture` passed `10/10`; `.50` slot `kdc-mpris-state-focused3` `cargo test -p mackesd mpris_request --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `3/3`; `.90` slot `kdc-mpris-command3` `cargo test -p mackesd mpris_media_command --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `2/2`; `.170` slot `kdc-mpris-state-fmt4` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check` on the touched KDC files passed. Remaining: live `/dev/uinput` injection validation on a Workstation seat and live Android validation.
- [✓] **KDC-MESH-7: two-way any-node files + mesh service directory.** **As** a user, **I want** files across the mesh, **so that** I reach any node. **Acceptance:** send/receive (done) + browse the phone's FS from a desktop (SFTP mount) + browse ANY node's shared files from the phone (#11); a mesh-wide **service directory** (each node publishes its KDC services to the substrate) the phone/hub browse to target any node over the overlay (#7); tested the directory + a node-targeted file browse. *(done: 41d7653 — mesh service_directory (each node publishes its KDC services+shared-roots to <root>/kdc-services/<host>.json; phone selects any node), node-targeted file_browse over action/connect/browse (canonicalized containment gate, audited), phone-FS SFTP via injectable SshfsMount (ToolAbsent/NotMountable honest-gates). mde-kdc-proto/host + kdc_host 38 tests.)*
- [✓] **KDC-MESH-8: run-commands incl. OpenStack lifecycle (all nodes) + battery/telephony/find.** **As** an operator, **I want** to drive the fleet from my phone, **so that** ops are pocket-sized. **Acceptance:** run-commands targetable per-node via the directory, INCLUDING a series of **OpenStack lifecycle commands** (instance start/stop/reboot/etc.) driving the QC `state/openstack` verbs across all nodes (#12); battery + connectivity report on desktops; telephony (call/SMS alerts, SMS reply done); find-my-devices both ways; **pairing is the auth** (#16, no per-command confirm) but every action appends to the KDC hash-chained audit log (`events::append_event`); tested each command → its real seam + the audit append. *(done: dfd91a7 — 5 OpenStack lifecycle run-commands driving the QC action/cloud/* PUBLIC verbs (bulk start/stop/reboot-all/list/status; targeted delete deliberately NOT phone-exposed — safety); battery+connectivity report (real /sys+overlay), telephony ring/missed alerts, find-my-device both ways; EVERY action → the KDC hash-chain audit. kdc_host 45 tests --test-threads=1.)*
- [✓] **KDC-MESH-9: the Phones hub surface + mesh-fanout endpoint.** **As** a user, **I want** to manage my phone, **so that** the relationship is visible. **Acceptance:** a `mde-shell-egui` Phones hub: paired phone(s) with mesh identity + battery/signal, per-feature toggles, the file browser, the run-command editor (incl. the OpenStack set), unpair (fast + mesh-wide), pairing QR; a mackesd **mesh-fanout** so a designated endpoint advertises as "Quasar Mesh" (one device to stock KDE Connect, #8) relaying follow-everywhere actions + aggregating responses; Carbon tokens (§4); folds in the existing phone-hub/kdc pieces (§6); tested hub render + fanout. *(done: ff8f89e — phones_hub.rs (4 tabs: Phones roster+toggles+ring/clip/browse/unpair, Files node-picker over the service directory, Commands catalog incl. the OpenStack lifecycle set, Pair identity+code) + fanout.rs (deterministic stable-primary 'Quasar Mesh' endpoint relaying follow-everywhere actions over the replicated substrate → audit); Surface::Phones wired (dock ALL 16→17, Comms group, main.rs), new IconId::Phones. Farm .170: kdc-host 8, mde-theme 18, mackesd kdc_host 47, shell phones_hub 10/dock 32/menubar 59, all clippy clean. QR bitmap = KDC-MESH-4's job (honest manual-pair meanwhile). KDC-MESH epic 7/9 done.)*

## CONSOLE — the Terminal's Start-Menu front door (operator 2026-07-04; 50-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/console-frontdoor.md)

A Carbon Win10-style Start Menu that IS the Terminal's front door: the bottom rail's far-left Start/Advanced icon opens a panel of operational entries, each launching a real TUI/CLI op as a named tab in `mde-term-egui`. Grounded in Eagle's real toolset (btop/systemctl/journalctl, nmcli/ss/mtr, dnf/rpm/flatpak, lsblk/df/smartctl, podman/virsh, mesh stack). MVP = everything at once.

- [✓] **CONSOLE-1: the Start button + panel shell + nav.** **As** a user, **I want** a terminal front door, **so that** ops are one click. **Acceptance:** the far-left bottom-rail Start/Advanced icon in `dock.rs` toggles a new `console.rs` panel; Win10 two-pane (left rail = category jump-index + power/session + footer user@host·version; right = pinned Terminal+Monitor + grouped list); slide-up Motion from the button; Esc/click-away/re-click close; full arrow-key nav + focus ring (EXPLORER-18 posture); Carbon tokens (§4); tested open/close + nav + jump-scroll. *(serialise after PICKER-2 — dock.rs)* *(done: 8945ace + 2026-07-07 rail update — console.rs Start-Menu panel; Start/Advanced moved from the old top-left rail reconciliation into the bottom rail ahead of Desktop, using the repo tray Start glyph; panel keeps Win10 bottom-left slide-up; arrow-ring nav, tool-presence greying, Esc/click-away. Farm-gated dock tests.)*
- [✓] **CONSOLE-2: the terminal spawn-tab seam + entry launch.** **As** a user, **I want** entries to open in terminal tabs, **so that** ops run where I can see them. **Acceptance:** a `spawn_tab(name, argv)` seam on `mde-term-egui` (reuse `TabbedTerminal`/`new_tab`, add the thin entrypoint if absent — glue §6); selecting a Console entry closes the menu, switches to `Surface::Terminal`, opens a NAMED tab running the real command (stays-open-on-exit with a prompt); root-needing ops run under `sudo` in the tab (interactive password); tested a real command opens a named tab + sudo path. *(after CONSOLE-1)* *(done: ae19bf4 — TerminalSurface::spawn_tab(name,argv)→bool + sudo_argv (sudo -- <argv>, interactive PTY password) + new_command held pane (stays-open-on-exit w/ status-toned prompt, key/click ack); alacritty EventedPty SIGCHLD reap (§6). UNBLOCKS the CONSOLE-1/4 SpawnTabNotWired gate → a shell-side wire-up follow-on.)*
- [✓] **CONSOLE-3: the entry table + all groups.** **As** an operator, **I want** the real ops, **so that** the front door is useful. **Acceptance:** a const `ConsoleEntry` table for System (btop/systemctl/journalctl-f/live-dashboard), Network (mesh-status/ss/mtr/nmcli/firewall-cmd), Packages (check+apply dnf/list-installed/platform-update/flatpak), Storage (df+lsblk+ncdu/smartctl/mesh-share), Mesh (meshctl-status/peers/openstack-mirror/etcdctl), Containers&VMs (podman ps/virsh list/openstack — **combined, with a surface link to the Cloud/Instances plane**, Q41/Q50), Shells (user/root/tmux); each row = icon + label + one-line desc + Fedora/Quasar provenance tag; a missing tool → entry honestly hidden/greyed (§7, no dead entries); tested every entry maps to a present command + the surface link routes. *(after CONSOLE-2)* *(done: 9b4786c — the 7-group ConsoleEntry table was already landed by CONSOLE-1 (marker drift, not redriven); this closes the real gap: a per-entry domain `icon: IconId` (11 distinct glyphs, no new SVG) replacing the blanket Terminal glyph, + fixture-$PATH tests proving EVERY entry maps to a real command + the Containers- [✓] **CONSOLE-3: the entry table + all groups.** **As** an operator, **I want** the real ops, **so that** the front door is useful. **Acceptance:** a const `ConsoleEntry` table for System (btop/systemctl/journalctl-f/live-dashboard), Network (mesh-status/ss/mtr/nmcli/firewall-cmd), Packages (check+apply dnf/list-installed/platform-update/flatpak), Storage (df+lsblk+ncdu/smartctl/mesh-share), Mesh (meshctl-status/peers/openstack-mirror/etcdctl), Containers&VMs (podman ps/virsh list/openstack — **combined, with a surface link to the Cloud/Instances plane**, Q41/Q50), Shells (user/root/tmux); each row = icon + label + one-line desc + Fedora/Quasar provenance tag; a missing tool → entry honestly hidden/greyed (§7, no dead entries); tested every entry maps to a present command + the surface link routes. *(after CONSOLE-2)*VMs Cloud-plane link routes to Surface::Instances (Q41/Q50). 770 tests, clippy 0. CONSOLE epic complete.)*
- [✓] **CONSOLE-4: Power group (armed) + custom entries.** **As** an operator, **I want** power + my own ops, **so that** the front door is complete. **Acceptance:** a Power section (Lock→curtain, Suspend, Reboot, Shut Down) via real loginctl/systemctl, destructive ones behind the platform **typed-arming** confirm; a Custom group where the operator registers their own named command entries (in-UI add + config file); tested power actions dispatch (armed) + a custom entry opens its tab. *(after CONSOLE-1)* *(done: 27f7ebb — Power (Lock→curtain, Suspend/Reboot/PowerOff→honor_power, host-down typed-armed) + operator Custom group (config round-trip). Tab-launch entries honest-gated SpawnTabNotWired until CONSOLE-2 lands the mde-term-egui spawn_tab seam.)*
- [✓] **CONSOLE-5: wire the spawn-tab launch (close the SpawnTabNotWired gate).** **As** a user, **I want** Console entries to actually open their terminal tab, **so that** the front door launches ops. **Acceptance:** every Console `Tab` entry + operator Custom entry drives the now-landed `TerminalSurface::spawn_tab(name,argv)` (CONSOLE-2 `ae19bf4`) — closing the menu, switching to `Surface::Terminal`, opening the named tab (root ops via `sudo_argv`); the `SpawnTabNotWired` typed gate is removed; a still-missing tool stays honestly greyed (ToolMissing); tested a real entry opens its named tab + the sudo path. *(the CONSOLE-1/4 completion; touches console.rs + main.rs to thread the term seam)* *(done: a6db44e — SpawnTabNotWired gate removed; every Tab/Custom entry → launch() → ConsoleRequest::SpawnTab drained in main.rs (switch to Surface::Terminal + terminal.spawn_tab); commands ride bash -lc (globs/vars/sbin PATH), root ops via mde_term_egui::sudo_argv (interactive PTY). ToolMissing stays the only honest gate. 755 tests.)*

## WIN7-DESKTOP — Windows 7 desktop layout + live-tile Start Menu (operator 2026-07-07)

- [>] **WIN7-DESKTOP-1: refactor the left sidebar and top status bar into a Windows 7 Desktop Layout.** **As** the operator, **I want** the shell chrome reshaped around a Windows 7 desktop idiom, **so that** the desktop feels familiar while retaining Quasar's live operational surfaces. **Acceptance:** the current left sidebar and top status bar are replaced or upgraded into a Windows 7-style desktop layout; all existing color tiles remain and become live tiles on the Start Menu; the Advanced menu content fills the rest of the upgraded Start Menu; Carbon theme tokens remain authoritative; the work begins with a 150-question one-at-a-time multiple-choice survey to lock layout, density, live-tile behavior, menu grouping, status placement, accessibility, and migration constraints before implementation. **SURVEY LOCKED 2026-07-10:** worked a 23-question one-at-a-time survey (the operator opted for a targeted subset over the full 150 once every required axis had a real answer) — full locks + architecture + an 11-unit (WIN7-1..11) plus surface-complete-gate (WIN7-12) draining plan in `docs/design/win7-desktop-survey.md`. Headline locks: a true bottom taskbar (Start · sessions · tray · clock) with the Start Menu opening upward as a fixed-size two-pane panel (live tiles left, the existing Console panel's content migrated + redesigned right, Power section anchored bottom); real Win8-style rotating live tiles in function-based groups; denser-than-baseline chrome; full accesskit parity from the first slice; the NOTIF-6 critical edge-cue now also auto-closes the Start Menu when it fires. One unanticipated addition the survey surfaced: a CEF-rendered desktop/wallpaper layer (not native egui) — primary source is an operator-configured local URL for a separate project (`MACKESCODE-AI-PROXY`, installed independently of this repo), falling back to Bing's daily picture (on-by-default network call — a deliberate, called-out exception to this project's usual airgap-default convention for external-network features, kept individually toggleable). Two things intentionally left open per the design doc: the hotkey scheme itself (locked as "clean break," not yet surveyed in detail) and where Console currently persists its Custom-group entries (needs verifying before the multi-seat-sync unit can be scoped). **WIN7-1 DONE 2026-07-10:** relocated the bottom-taskbar chrome per lock #3 — but investigation found `dock.rs`'s own top-of-file doc comment (and 3 other in-file comments) were stale: the Start cell (`start_cell`), the NAVBAR-U1/U2/U3 session rail, the NOTIF-3 tray, and VDOCK-5's clock had ALREADY been consolidated into one full-width bottom rail (`notification_rail_with_sources`) by prior NAVBAR/NOTIF/CONSOLE-1 work, contradicting the doc comments that still described the clock as living in the left dock — the design doc's "top-bar+left-sidebar" framing inherited that staleness rather than dock.rs's real current state. So this slice's actual delta, verified against the code (not the doc): (1) reordered the auto-hide pin — previously wedged between the tray and the clock — to trail AFTER the clock, so the rail reads exactly "Start · sessions · tray · clock" contiguously (the pin isn't a Win7 taskbar concept, it's this shell's own dock-hold-open control); (2) relabelled the touch-density rail text "Advanced" → "Start" (label only, `dock.rs:1652` — the Start cell's click behavior is unchanged, still opens Console; WIN7-2 is the real two-pane Start Menu, not this unit); (3) applied lock #12's density pass — `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_H` (the default `Density::Mouse` rail) trimmed 20px → 18px via `Style::SP_M + Style::SP_XS / 2.0` (the same token-composition idiom `SYS_QUAD_ICON` already uses, not a new one-off constant), while `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_EXPANDED_H` (`Density::Touch`, 48px) is deliberately untouched since touch density exists specifically for bigger targets; (4) fixed the 4 stale doc comments (module header, VDOCK-1 banner, `dock()`, `paint_dock_frame()`) that mis-described clock/pin as left-dock content. Two new tests lock the contract end to end — `win7_1_the_taskbar_reads_start_sessions_tray_clock_left_to_right` (the full order incl. a real session entry) and `win7_1_the_default_taskbar_is_denser_than_the_shells_other_carbon_chrome` (pins `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_H` at 18px, denser than `DOCK_W`/`APP_CELL_H`, `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_EXPANDED_H` unchanged) — plus `navbar4_status_tray_is_folded_into_the_bottom_rail` extended with a pin-position assertion; all 54 `mde-shell-egui` `dock` tests pass on farm host 172.20.0.90 (`xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock`, 3m38s build, 0 failures) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean. Judgment call flagged for the operator: the new 18px compact height sits under `Density::min_hit_target`'s general 24pt pointer-target convention in `mde-egui`'s `style.rs` — a deliberate continuation of a pre-existing exception this rail already made (it was already 20px, below that convention, before this slice) rather than a newly-introduced one; picked 18px over a larger cut specifically because of that convention, and flagged rather than guessed further. NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat — confirmed no screenshot/pixel-diff harness exists for `mde-shell-egui` today (the `/polish` skill's own notes call headless capture a not-yet-built "high-value early polish unit"; the one gate that shipped, OBS-4, targets the retired iced `mde-workbench`), matching the NOTIF-13 precedent of layout-assertion tests + honest flagging over a guess. Left untouched (out of this unit's scope): the left-dock app picker (Workbench + 6 groups), the grade band, and VDOCK-4's system quad — WIN7-2/3 are what give the picker's tile content a new Start-Menu home; Console's internals, the critical edge-cue, and the CEF desktop layer are also untouched per the unit's own exclusion list. **WIN7-2 DONE 2026-07-10:** built the real fixed-size two-pane Start Menu shell, a new `start_menu.rs` module (`start_menu::StartMenuState` + `start_menu::start_menu_panel`) mounted from `main.rs`'s renamed `mount_start_menu` (was `mount_console`). Geometry reuses Console's own proven floating-`egui::Area` + `Motion`-tweened slide pattern verbatim (fixed size, `x = DOCK_W`, opening upward from the screen bottom, lock #2/#4) rather than inventing a new mechanism, exactly as the design doc pointed at. Resolved the Console-vs-Start-Menu question the design doc left open: the Start Menu now opens INSTEAD of Console on a Start-cell click (`DockState`'s `console_open`/`console_toggle` fields + `take_console_toggle`/`set_console_open` methods renamed to `start_menu_open`/`start_menu_toggle`/`take_start_menu_toggle`/`set_start_menu_open`), and its right pane mounts Console's REAL, fully-working content (not a placeholder label) via a new `console::console_content(ui, rect, state)` extracted from the old standalone `console_panel` (which owned its own `Area`/slide/click-away — deleted; that machinery now lives in `start_menu.rs`, generalized to the whole two-pane panel). `ConsoleState::open` is no longer self-toggled; it's mirrored in from the Start Menu each frame via a new `ConsoleState::set_open` (the `set_active` mirror idiom, edge-triggered so a steady "still open" mirror never re-resets the focus ring), and every existing Console action that used to close the whole front door (a routed link, a spawned tab, a fired power verb, Esc) still calls the SAME unchanged `ConsoleState::close` — `start_menu_panel` detects that self-closure (`console.is_open()` having gone false while the Start Menu itself is still open) and propagates it, so launching anything from the embedded Console still closes the whole menu, proven end to end by a real click on the embedded pinned-Terminal row. The left pane is a labelled placeholder ("Tiles — coming in WIN7-3", lock's own "empty panes to start" scope) sized `SP_XL · 9` (288pt, a token-composed placeholder WIN7-3 will very likely resize once real tiles exist). Wired the Super key (lock #13's "both, not either/or") by reusing — not duplicating — the vertical dock's OWN pre-existing clean-Super-tap latch (`HotkeyRouter::take_dock_toggle`, VDOCK-1): `main.rs`'s hotkey-dispatch block now applies that one drain to both `vdock.toggle()` and `start_menu.toggle()`. Accesskit (lock #14) from this slice on: the panel exports `Role::Menu` ("Start Menu") and each pane a `Role::Group` landmark ("Start Menu tiles" / "Console"), the `status.rs` `install_status_accessibility` idiom restated (dock.rs/console.rs carry none yet). Density needed no new tuning — the panel reuses Console's already-established, already-dense token-composed dimensions as-is. Tests: 6 new in `start_menu.rs` (toggle open/close; closed-mounts-no-layer + open-claims-its-fixed-bottom-left-footprint; open-does-not-cover-the-rest-of-the-screen; Esc + click-away-but-never-on-the-opening-frame; an embedded Console row activation closing the WHOLE panel; the accesskit role/label export), `dock.rs`'s Start-cell test renamed+adapted to the new toggle name, and `console.rs`'s panel-level Area/click-away tests (2) removed (their contract moved to `start_menu.rs`, over the real embedding Area) while its content-level Esc test (1) stays, adapted to a lighter test-local content-only `drive` helper in place of the deleted `console_panel`. All 968 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass on farm host 172.20.0.90 (`xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, the broader whole-crate run since `console.rs`/`main.rs`/`dock.rs` all changed — 38.94s build, 26.50s run, 0 failures, 0 warnings from `mde-shell-egui` itself) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean. Judgment call flagged for the operator: reusing the vertical dock's Super-tap latch means one Super tap now reveals BOTH the vertical dock AND the Start Menu together — the survey's lock #13 never anticipated VDOCK-1's pre-existing claim on Super, and stranding the dock behind an unreachable hotkey would break the shell's only real surface launcher until WIN7-3 lands tiles; revealing both together reads as one "show the nav chrome" gesture since the Start Menu already mounts flush beside the dock column, but this is a deliberate resolution of a gap the design doc left open, not a surveyed answer, and is worth a confirm — it's also likely moot once WIN7-3's tiles let the vertical dock retire. Second judgment call: embedding Console's real content (rather than a bare label) made this unit's `console.rs` diff considerably larger than a literal "empty panes to start" reading would need — done because the operator's own stated inclination was to avoid a real UX regression (losing Power/Lock/Custom-entry/spawn-tab access from the Start button until WIN7-5 lands) and to avoid throwaway work when WIN7-5 redesigns this same embedded seam. NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat, matching WIN7-1's own precedent (still no screenshot/pixel-diff harness for this crate). **WIN7-3 DONE 2026-07-10:** built the real live-tile grid in the Start Menu's left pane — all 17 `Surface::ALL` entries as uniform-size tiles (lock #6), grouped into lock #8's 7 function-based groups (Mesh Control: Workbench/MeshView/InfraCode; Desktop & Session: Desktop; Media: Music/Media/Voice; Files & Data: Files/Bookmarks/Storage; Web & Tools: Browser/Terminal/Editor; Comms: Chat/Phones; System: System/About), static content only (no rotation — WIN7-4's job). Investigation found the task's own framing assumed a reusable "icon+label" mapping the app picker already rendered — false: `dock.rs`'s own PICKER-1 module doc locks "no per-icon captions, no tooltips anywhere," so only the icon half (`Surface::icon_id`) was actually reusable; added a new `Surface::label()` (mirroring `icon_id`'s exact match-arm shape, in the same `impl Surface` block) since tiles need real captions the picker itself has never had. Sizing (item 7): replaced WIN7-2's arbitrary 288pt `LEFT_PANE_W` placeholder with a real, content-derived value — uniform tiles at `SP_XL · 2.5` × `(SP_XL + SP_M)` (80×48pt, wider-than-tall so long labels like "Infra as Code" have room, still ONE size for all 17 per lock #6), 3 columns (the widest locked group's width), `LEFT_PANE_W` = `264pt` from `PANE_PAD·2 + TILE_W·3 + TILE_GAP·2`; the render loop wraps generally (`usize::div_ceil`, not hardcoded to 1 row) so a future group past 3 members degrades to a second row instead of overlapping. A new `TILE_GROUPS` table + a `dock.rs`-style compile-time completeness guard (`const _: () = { ... }`) place every one of the 17 `Surface::ALL` entries in exactly one group, checked at build time AND by a runtime test (the dock.rs belt-and-suspenders convention). Click routing (item 5) reuses — not reinvents — the picker's own click/Enter/Space activation predicate (`dock::response_activated`, widened `fn` → `pub(crate) fn` for cross-module reuse) through a new `StartMenuState::tile_activation` slot, drained by `main.rs`'s `mount_start_menu` exactly like an embedded Console `Goto` request (`self.nav.expanded = true; self.nav.surface = surface;`) — both panes now end in the identical "route + close the whole menu" outcome. Accesskit (lock #14, item 6): every tile exports its own `Role::Button` node (label + bounds + `Action::Click`), the `status.rs` `install_segment_accessibility` per-item idiom restated, landing real per-tile parity beyond WIN7-2's panel-level-only export. Density (item 6) needed no new tuning — the whole grid derives from existing `Style` tokens already used elsewhere in this shell. Live-tile status tint (item 4, "maybe a simple status-color tint"): evaluated reusing `dock.rs`'s existing `badge_for`/`StatusInputs` (the same read-only source the dock's own pips already read) but judged the cross-module plumbing (threading `StatusInputs`/`DockState` into `start_menu_panel`'s own signature) NOT "trivial" in the way the task's conditional implied, especially since WIN7-4 has to build the real per-surface "tile fact" plumbing anyway (the design doc's own architecture note) — so this unit ships a real, documented `tile_status_tint(surface) -> Option<Color32>` seam that always returns `None` today, wired into `tile()`'s paint path but inert, rather than a half-built live path WIN7-4 would likely replace. Tests: 6 new (5 in `start_menu.rs` — the 7-group taxonomy/order/coverage; the tile-grid content height fits `PANEL_H` without overflow; all 17 tiles render at one uniform size and stay within the left pane; a tile click activates its surface AND closes the whole Start Menu; every tile exports a labelled `Button` accesskit node — plus 1 in `dock.rs`, `Surface::label`'s own exhaustive/injective map, the `every_surface_maps_to_a_named_brand_glyph` precedent restated). All 974 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass on farm host 172.20.0.90 (`xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, the broader whole-crate run since `dock.rs`/`start_menu.rs`/`main.rs` all changed — 974 passed, 0 failed, ~21s test run measured after iterative same-session farm builds on that host, 0 warnings from `mde-shell-egui` itself) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean. A bonus `cargo clippy -p mde-shell-egui --all-targets` diligence pass (not in this unit's stated verification bar, run anyway since the farm was idle) found the crate already carries ~350-400 pre-existing pedantic-clippy warnings untouched by either WIN7-1 or WIN7-2 (confirmed several by exact line content, e.g. WIN7-2's own `PANEL_W < SZ.x` / `PANEL_H < SZ.y` assertions already trip `assertions_on_constants`) — left that ambient debt alone (out of scope, matching the file's own existing tolerance) but fixed the two clippy findings that were genuinely new from this unit's own code: `TILE_GRID_CONTENT_H` is `#[cfg(test)]`-only now (nothing in the render path reads a pre-summed total; it was dead code in a non-test build, the `status.rs` `local_grade_pip_id` `#[cfg(test)]`-top-level-item idiom restated) and `StartMenuState::take_tile_activation` is `const fn` (matching `ConsoleState::take_request`'s identical `self.pending.take()` shape exactly). NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat, matching WIN7-1/WIN7-2's own precedent (still no screenshot/pixel-diff harness for this crate). Judgment calls flagged for the operator: (1) a tile click closes the WHOLE Start Menu — lock #23 only says "single click activates," it doesn't explicitly say the click also dismisses the panel, but this mirrors WIN7-2's own embedded-Console-row precedent exactly (launching anything closes the menu, the real Windows Start Menu's own behavior), so it was implemented rather than left ambiguous — worth a confirm; (2) tiles are 80×48pt (wider than tall) rather than square — lock #6 says "one uniform size, no small/wide/large variants," which a non-square-but-still-uniform rectangle satisfies literally, chosen so labels have real room, but a stricter "square tile" reading of the Win8 idiom is a defensible alternative the survey didn't pin down; (3) the status-tint seam (above) ships deliberately inert — a live tint was judged out of this unit's honest "trivially available" bar, not silently dropped. **WIN7-4 DONE 2026-07-10:** wired the live-tile rotation itself (lock #5) — the seam WIN7-3 shipped deliberately inert. A new `TileFactInputs` bundle (`start_menu::TileFactInputs`, `StartMenuState::set_tile_inputs` mirroring `dock.rs`'s `DockState::set_status_inputs`/`StatusInputs` idiom exactly: owned clones/copies refreshed each frame by `main.rs`'s `mount_start_menu`, the same call-order idiom `mount_dock_chrome` already uses) carries ~20 fields, every one traced to a REAL already-published source before being wired — investigated first rather than assumed from the design doc's own illustrative examples, several of which turned out to be aspirational: most notably, System's "CPU/temp" (the design doc's own lock #5 example) does not exist ANYWHERE in the codebase — confirmed by direct investigation of both `mde-seat` and `mde-shell-egui` (the closest real thing, NODE-GRADE-2's composite score, is CPU-weighted 3/11 but not isolated, and carries no temperature factor at all) — so System's tile instead reuses the Device/Power `StatusSegments` rollups (the SAME already-Bus-published `state/notify/segment/{device,power}` topics whose pips already route clicks to `Surface::System`; `status.rs`'s `severity_color`/`severity_label` widened `fn` → `pub(crate) fn` for this reuse, the `dock::response_activated` cross-module-widening precedent restated). Real sources actually wired, surface by surface: **Chat** — `ChatState::total_unread()`, the EXACT source `dock.rs`'s own Chat badge already reads, plus a NEW `ChatState::most_recent_sender()` folding the SAME already-loaded `convos` store the unread sum reads (not a second read) — giving Chat its own literal lock #5 example, "recent senders." **MeshView** — `chrome.summary()`, the EXACT source `dock.rs`'s own MeshView badge reads. **Files** — `transfers_counts().active`, the EXACT source `dock.rs`'s own Files badge reads. **Media** — `mde_media_egui::model::now_playing_title`/`MediaController::is_playing()`, the EXACT accessor the lock-screen curtain already reuses for its own now-playing readout. **Bookmarks** — `Manager::total()`, already `pub`, already Bus-fed via `state/bookmarks/collection`, simply never called from the shell chrome before this unit (zero new plumbing). **Storage / Phones / Infra as Code / Browser** — four new same-crate `pub(crate)` accessors (`StorageState::local_summary()`, `PhonesHubState::device_counts()`, `InfraCodeState::service_summary()`, `WebState::tab_count()`), each a thin fold over state the surface's own panel or status-chip cluster already renders. **Workbench / Desktop** — `ControllerState::peer_count()`/`leader()`/`seen()`, `ChooserState::source_count()`, `VdiState::requested_summary()`: all pre-existing `pub(crate)` accessors already driving Workbench's/Desktop's own status-chip clusters, just never threaded to the Start Menu before. **Music / Voice / Terminal** — three new cross-crate `pub` accessors (`MusicApp::now_playing()`, `VoiceApp::call_state()`, `TerminalSurface::tab_count()`), each mirroring `MediaController::player()`'s already-proven shape and delegating to state the standalone binary's own transport/dialer/tab-strip already renders — Voice in particular reuses `mde_voice_hud::sip::CallState::label()` verbatim (the dialer's own status-row formatter) rather than reformatting the call state a second time. **Editor** and **About** were investigated and confirmed to have NO genuinely live fact anywhere in the codebase (Editor: only per-buffer dirty flags exist, no aggregate open/unsaved count anywhere in `mde-editor-egui`; About: 100% compile-time `env!()` build info, one static path-exists check) — both stay on their static WIN7-3 icon+label, the honest §7 answer over inventing one. A 17th/18th-surface gap surfaced mid-build: `Surface` actually has 18 variants — `Surface::Timers` sits deliberately OUTSIDE `Surface::ALL`/`TILE_GROUPS` (lock #20, the clock strip is its one home) — so `tile_facts`'s exhaustive match needed its own explicit (never-reached-by-the-render-loop) arm, a real compile-time catch neither WIN7-3's own completeness guard nor this unit's design pass had surfaced since that guard only ever walked `Surface::ALL`. `tile_facts(surface, &TileFactInputs) -> Vec<String>` folds the bundle per-surface — the SAME "read already-published state, fold to a short display string" idiom `dock::badge_for` already uses, restated at the tile-grid level; zero/absent facts are honest silence (Files shows nothing while idle, never "0 transferring"), matching `dock.rs`'s own "zero paints no badge" convention. Rotation timing: a new named `TILE_FACT_ROTATE_INTERVAL` constant (5s — the design doc's own "~4-5s per fact, not surveyed precisely" estimate, picked at the high end for glanceability given an 80×48pt tile's 10pt label text) drives a pure `rotating_fact_index(len, time_secs)` derived from egui's own frame clock (`ui.input(|i| i.time)`, the SAME time source `explorer.rs`'s `hero_card`/`tick_ambient` already read for their own time-driven visuals — not a new mechanism) — no per-tile stored state, so every tile in the grid advances in lockstep off ONE shared clock and the shown value never drifts/accumulates error the way a stored "last rotated at" counter could; `start_menu_panel` requests a coarser 1s repaint heartbeat while settled-open so a rotation step is never more than ~1s stale without needing a full per-frame repaint. `tile_status_tint` (WIN7-3's inert seam) is now real: a severity-colour dot for System (worst-of Device/Power segment severity via a new `severity_rank` ordinal over `status::severity_label`'s vocabulary), MeshView (mesh health), and Chat/Files (the SAME `Style::ACCENT` their own count badges already paint, shown exactly when the count is nonzero) — `None` everywhere else, since most of the 17 tileable surfaces are plain counts with no health concept to tint (never an invented severity). Accesskit (lock #14): every tile's existing `Button` node now also carries `.set_value(current_display_text)` (the `status.rs` `install_segment_accessibility` label-stays-identity/value-carries-live-reading split, restated); the whole tile grid additionally exports ONE aggregate `Role::Status`/`Live::Polite` node (`install_tiles_live_summary`) — the NOTIF-11 `status_live_region_id` precedent restated at the tile-grid level, deliberately NOT per-tile (mirroring `status.rs`'s own shape of one live summary node plus per-item value-bearing-but-not-live pips, not up to 17 independent announcers all firing on the same clock, which would be an accessibility regression, not a win) — and installs NO node at all while nothing is actually rotating, the same honest-silence posture `tile_facts` uses. Tests: 12 new in `start_menu.rs` — a 2-fact surface reports both for rotation (Chat); a 1-fact surface shows it without ever rotating (Bookmarks, checked across 4 points on the clock); a currently-zero count shows no fact, not a fake zero (Files); the two verified-no-live-fact surfaces (About/Editor) never rotate even given a fully defaulted bundle; a spread check across the remaining tuple/Option-shaped and cross-crate sources (Storage/Media/Music/Voice/Workbench/Browser/Terminal); `rotating_fact_index`'s cadence + wraparound + the 0/1-fact no-op case; `tile_display_text`'s end-to-end 2-fact rotation; `tile_status_tint`'s worst-of/accent/none cases; the aggregate live region's presence + `Role::Status`/`Live::Polite`/value while rotating AND its deliberate absence while nothing is; a tile's accesskit node carrying its current display text as `.value()` for both a rotating and a fully-static tile. All 986 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass (974 + these 12, an exact match confirming every new test actually ran, none silently skipped) on farm host 172.20.0.130 (BigBoy, per the operator's standing "route the longest build to BigBoy" rule — `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui -p mde-music-egui -p mde-voice-egui -p mde-term-egui`, the broader multi-crate run since this unit's new cross-crate accessors touch `mde-music-egui`/`mde-voice-egui`/`mde-term-egui` too — 28/373/35 passed there respectively, 0 failed anywhere, 0 warnings from any of the four touched crates, the true `cargo test` exit code independently re-verified after an initial `| tail` pipe masked it) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean across every touched file. A first farm attempt caught 3 real compile errors before any of this shipped (the `Surface::Timers` exhaustiveness gap above, plus a missed `StatusSegments` test import) — fixed and re-verified green, not silently patched over. Judgment call flagged for the operator: a tile with EXACTLY ONE live fact shows that fact INSTEAD OF the static label, never both at once — the task's own framing offered "icon+label+one static fact" as one acceptable reading, but the locked 48pt-tall tile has no pixel room for a genuine third line (verified against the real `Style` spacing tokens: the icon and label already sit only ~2px apart at the current geometry), so this unit swaps the label slot's content rather than stacking a line that would visually collide; the surface's real name is never actually lost even then, since its accesskit `Button` node still carries `surface.label()` as `.label()` unconditionally. NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat, matching WIN7-1/2/3's own precedent (still no screenshot/pixel-diff harness for this crate). Left untouched (out of this unit's scope): WIN7-5's Console → right-pane migration, the CEF desktop layer, and the critical edge-cue auto-close. **WIN7-5 DONE 2026-07-10:** redesigned the Console content's presentation for its Start Menu right-pane home (lock #10), over the completely UNCHANGED `ConsoleState` data/actions/CONSOLE-2 seam (same 7 groups, same Power semantics/typed-arming, same Custom persistence — this unit changed how it draws, not what it does). Investigation of WIN7-2's straight embed (not assumed) found two real problems: (1) the rail positioned its Power section by subtracting purely from the bottom (`footer_top - POWER_H`) with nothing accounting for the space ABOVE it, leaving an unaccounted ~168pt dead gap between the category jump-index (ending ~240pt down the rail) and Power (starting ~408pt down) — a genuinely broken-looking void directly contradicting lock #12's density mandate; (2) the identity block (`user@host` + version) sat in a bottom FOOTER underneath Power, backwards from the authentic Win7/Win10 Start Menu shape where the signed-in user leads the rail and Power alone anchors the true bottom (lock #11). Fixed both: the identity block now leads the rail (top), `user@host` reading one rung brighter via `Style::TEXT_STRONG` since it now opens the rail instead of trailing it; the old "Console"/"Operations" title block it displaces is dropped outright (not relocated) since the tile pane beside it (`start_menu.rs`) carries no equivalent self-title either — a screen reader still gets this pane's identity from `start_menu.rs`'s own "Console" `Role::Group` landmark, a deliberate call flagged below. The jump-index (lock #49) is now 8 icon+label+"N entries" mini-rows at a new `JUMP_ROW_H` (48pt) — deliberately the SAME height as `start_menu::TILE_H` (restated, since `console.rs` sits lower in the module graph and can't import it directly without a cycle), so the rail's nav rows literally line up in height with the tile grid beside it; each row reuses its group's own first entry's icon as a representative glyph (verified by inspection that every group's first entry already carries a fitting domain icon — System→System, Storage→Storage, Mesh→MeshView, Containers&VMs→Instances, Shells→Terminal, etc., no mismatches — so `ConsoleGroup` itself needed no new field). The Custom row wears the SAME `Provenance::Quasar` accent every operator-owned entry already tags itself with, at rest, flagging "this one's yours." Power now anchors via the SAME bottom-relative math shape as before (`rail.bottom() - POWER_H`, just no longer offset by a footer that moved away) — proven flush with the pane's TRUE bottom edge to within 0.5pt (a new test), and the gap above it is now exactly one named, tested `RAIL_SECTION_GAP` (`SP_XL`, 32pt) — a byproduct of `IDENTITY_H`(40) + 8×`JUMP_ROW_H`(384) + `POWER_H`(120) summing to 544 of the rail's 576pt `PANEL_H`, not a hand-picked number. Investigated and explicitly REJECTED two tempting extensions: reusing `dock.rs`'s own per-group categorical-accent heading treatment (a THIRD, materially different heading recipe confirmed by a dedicated research pass — centred, non-uppercased, shrink-to-fit font, a per-group accent box-stroke — whose 6 named hues, Comms/Workloads/Terminals/Mesh/System/Media, are already claimed by a DIFFERENT taxonomy than this module's 7 differently-named operational groups; reusing the SAME hues here would blur what a categorical accent means everywhere else it appears, and minting 7 new ones would violate the shell's one-categorical-palette convention `Style`'s own tests already assert); and threading `nav.surface` into `ConsoleState` so a row could show dock.rs's "currently active surface" fill+bar+tint ladder (also confirmed as its own established-but-distinct idiom from explorer.rs's ring-based "selected" convention) — new cross-module plumbing this module doesn't have today, well beyond a presentation redesign of data already on hand. The list pane's own group headings (`heading()`) were investigated and found to ALREADY match `start_menu.rs`'s `tile_group_heading()` byte-for-byte (uppercased SMALL/TEXT_DIM/`SP_XS`-left-inset, confirmed via the same research pass) — left deliberately untouched, already coherent with the tile pane. Entry-row/custom-row/custom-add-form visual language similarly left untouched beyond accesskit (already dense, no genuine problem found there) — the redesign budget went entirely to the rail, which is where the real incoherence/dead-space problems actually were. Accesskit (lock #14): every RAW-PAINTED interactive row this unit touches now exports its own `Button` node via a new shared `install_row_accessibility` helper (role+label+value+bounds+`Click`, the `status.rs`/`start_menu.rs` established idiom) — the 8 jump rows, every static + Custom entry row (reusing the SAME flat-index id space `console_entry_id` already unifies), the Custom remove cross, the Custom add-form's Add row, the 4 Power action rows, and the arming stage's Confirm/Cancel (each carrying a real armed-state hint in its `.value()`, e.g. "Requires a typed confirmation before it fires" vs. "Fires immediately"). WIN7-2 shipped this whole module's embedding with only the Start Menu's PANEL-level landmarks covering it; individual rows were explicitly flagged as not-yet-covered — this is that coverage. A NEW `Live::Polite` region also announces the honest-gate notice (§7) the moment it fires — previously visual-only, so a screen-reader user pressing a greyed command heard nothing explaining why; it exports no node at all while there's nothing to say (the `install_tiles_live_summary` honesty convention restated). Real egui `TextEdit` widgets already in this module (the arming echo, the Custom add-form's two drafts) get accesskit for free from egui's own widget machinery and needed no manual call. WIN7-7 remains the later crate-wide full sweep — this unit does not claim to close every accesskit gap in the shell, only the rows it rewrote the rendering of. Tests: 10 new in `console.rs` — `jump_caption`'s singular/plural wording (pure); Power's last row proven flush with the pane's true bottom edge (lock #11, within 0.5pt); the jump-index-to-Power gap proven small and bounded by the named `RAIL_SECTION_GAP` rather than the old ~168pt void (a direct regression test for the bug found and fixed); the click-to-scroll-target mapping re-proven for a spread of 3 groups (generalizing the pre-existing Shells-only test, since this rewrite touches every jump row's paint path); every jump row's accesskit value proven to report the group's REAL entry count, including Custom updating LIVE after an operator add (not a number baked in at open time); a comprehensive Button-role accesskit sweep across every touched row type (jump rows, the pinned Terminal entry, all 4 Power rows); a Custom row's accesskit value proven to carry its real command, plus its remove cross; the gate notice's `Live::Polite` region proven present only while showing and absent otherwise; the arming stage's Confirm/Cancel accesskit values proven to flip between "disabled" and "ready" as the echo is typed. All pre-existing `console.rs`/`start_menu.rs` tests were re-read individually to confirm none assert on anything this redesign changed (row positions/content, never ids/semantics) before the farm run, not just trusted; 996 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass (986 + these 10, an exact match) on farm host 172.20.0.130 (BigBoy — `farm-topology.sh table` checked first, all 4 dom0s idle, `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, 15.15s build + 15.05s test run, 0 failures, 0 warnings from `mde-shell-egui` itself — every warning in the log traced to `mde-bus`/`mde-musicd`, pre-existing and untouched by this unit) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean on both touched files (`console.rs`, and `start_menu.rs`'s own doc-comment-only update superseding a now-stale WIN7-2-era claim that this content "renders exactly as it always has"). The `tee`-piped exit code was independently re-verified with `set -o pipefail` on a second, filtered `console`-only run (32 tests, 0 failed, real exit 0) — the WIN7-4 masked-pipe caution restated rather than assumed clean. Judgment calls flagged for the operator: (1) dropping the visual "Console"/"Operations" title block entirely (not relocating it) is a real, user-visible text removal — reasoned as matching the tile pane's own headerless convention (Windows' own Start Menu doesn't self-title either pane), but it's a defensible-not-surveyed call, easy to revert if the operator prefers a visible pane label; (2) `user@host` now painted in `Style::TEXT_STRONG` (previously plain `TEXT`) — a minor, cheap, easily-reverted emphasis choice, not a surveyed answer; (3) the jump-index's per-group entry count ("N entries") is genuinely new information not present in WIN7-2's straight embed — reasoned as filling the reclaimed vertical space with real, honest, already-derivable data (`group.entries.len()`) rather than arbitrary padding, but it IS new visible content, worth a confirm; (4) did NOT add scroll-position-aware "currently viewed group" highlighting to the jump rail (no such tracking exists in `ConsoleState` today; adding one would be new interactive behavior beyond a presentation redesign, flagged as a possible future nicety rather than implemented). **NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat** — confirmed no screenshot/pixel-diff harness exists for `mde-shell-egui` still (matching WIN7-1/2/3/4's own precedent). This is now the FIFTH consecutive WIN7-chrome unit to land honest-but-visually-unverified — an ACCUMULATING gap, not a fresh one each time: five units of real layout/visual changes (the bottom taskbar, the Start Menu shell, the tile grid, tile rotation, and now this unit's rail redesign) have shipped entirely on layout-assertion tests + accesskit output, never actually seen rendered on a real DRM seat. Flagging explicitly, again, that this is worth a dedicated live-seat smoke pass before the epic is considered presentable — individual per-unit honesty about the gap does not substitute for an actual look, and the gap only gets more expensive to discover-and-fix the longer it accumulates across units. **WIN7-6 DONE 2026-07-10:** wired the NOTIF-6 critical edge-cue's auto-close of the Start Menu (lock #9) — investigated the actual `CriticalEdgeCue` machinery in `status.rs` before trusting the design doc's own architecture note ("this needs one new call in whatever code path raises the cue... to also clear console_open/the new Start-Menu-open state"), and found that note half-stale: `ConsoleState::open` is no longer an independent field to clear — WIN7-2 already made it a same-frame mirror of `StartMenuState::open` (`start_menu_panel`'s `console.set_open(state.open)`), so closing the ONE real source of truth is sufficient; the design doc's "also clear console_open" phrasing pre-dates that unification. The real design question this unit had to resolve itself: `CriticalEdgeCue` already carries `acknowledged`/ack semantics (`CriticalEdgeCue::acknowledge`, driven by a click on the cue's own edges) the design doc never mentions, and naively closing the Start Menu every frame `CriticalEdgeCue::visible()` reads true would have created exactly the fight the unit's own brief warned against — it would ALSO block reopening the Start Menu at all while a still-unacknowledged critical is up, a much stronger, unsurveyed behavior lock #9 never asked for, not just a reaction to the firing. So the fix is edge-triggered, not level-triggered: a new `CriticalEdgeCue::became_visible` field, set inside `CriticalEdgeCue::update` by comparing `visible()` immediately before vs. after that call's own state fold (captured as literally the first line of `update`, before `self.muted` is overwritten, so the comparison uses last frame's effective muted state against this frame's — load-bearing for the unmute case below), drained once via a new `CriticalEdgeCue::take_became_visible()` (the `StartMenuState::take_tile_activation`/`HotkeyRouter::take_dock_toggle` one-shot `take_*` idiom restated). `main.rs`'s `render()` calls it once per frame, right after the existing `self.critical_edge.update(...)`/`.show(ctx)` pair (left in place — still drawn after the curtain, still curtain-independent, matching NOTIF-6's own "impossible to miss" posture): `if self.critical_edge.take_became_visible() { self.start_menu.close(); }`. `StartMenuState::close` (previously private, already exactly the right "close if open, no-op otherwise" semantics Esc/click-away/tile-activation/embedded-Console-self-closure already used) is widened `pub(crate)` for this cross-module call — the `dock::response_activated`/`status::severity_color` cross-module-widening idiom this epic already established, not a new wrapper. `became_visible` genuinely latches on THREE distinct real transitions, not just "a fresh critical": a brand-new own-seat critical, a genuinely different one re-raised after an older one was acknowledged, AND an unmute revealing a still-live one (the pre-existing `critical_edge_cue_respects_push_mute_without_clearing_state` test already proved this third case is a real `visible()` transition today) — all three are honest "the cue just started showing" moments and lock #9's "auto-closes... if open" reads as covering all three, not just the first. Verified precisely why edge-triggering (not level-triggering) clears the task's own "don't fight the user" bar: once acknowledged, `visible()` goes false and STAYS false for that same rollup (confirmed by the pre-existing ack tests), so no further edge ever fires for it — an operator can reopen the Start Menu afterward, whether that's after acknowledging (cue silent) or even while the SAME critical is still blazing unacknowledged (no NEW edge without a real change), and it is never immediately slammed shut again. Tests: 5 new — 2 pure `CriticalEdgeCue` unit tests in `status.rs` (`critical_edge_cue_take_became_visible_latches_once_per_real_edge`, walking fresh→drain→steady→acked→re-raised→resolved and asserting the drain fires ONLY on the two genuine edges; `critical_edge_cue_take_became_visible_latches_on_unmute_too`, isolating the mute/unmute case) using the file's own existing `rollup()`/`StatusSegments` fixture helpers, no egui context needed; 3 full-shell integration tests in `main.rs` restating the `shell_mounts_the_critical_edge_cue_from_own_seat_rollups` fixture pattern — `win7_6_a_critical_firing_closes_an_open_start_menu` (two real frames: opens the Start Menu with no critical live, confirms it stays open, then fires an own-seat Critical rollup and confirms both `critical_edge.visible()` and the menu now closed), `win7_6_a_warning_alert_does_not_close_the_start_menu` (the SAME fixture at "warning" severity, confirming neither the cue nor the Start Menu react — the strengthening is Critical-only, matching `is_critical_severity`'s own existing gate), and `win7_6_reopening_after_acknowledging_the_critical_is_not_re_closed` (three frames: open → critical fires and auto-closes it → the operator acknowledges → the operator reopens the Start Menu → still open, proving the no-fighting bar end to end, not just asserted in a comment). All 1001 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass (996 + these 5, an exact match) on farm host 172.20.0.90 (`farm-topology.sh table` checked first — and re-checked before the final run — both times all 4 dom0s idle; `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.90 xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, 9.42s build + 33.16s run on the final post-tidy-up pass, 0 failures, 0 warnings from `mde-shell-egui` itself) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean on all three touched files; run with `set -o pipefail` throughout so the `tee` never masked a real failure (the WIN7-4 caution restated) — real exit 0, plus two earlier scoped reruns (the three `win7_6`-prefixed tests; the two `status.rs` `became_visible` tests) independently confirmed 0 failures each before the doc tidy-ups that preceded the final full run. A bonus `cargo clippy -p mde-shell-egui --all-targets` diligence pass (idle farm, not this unit's stated verification bar) found every hit landing near this unit's own edited lines was pre-existing ambient debt, not a regression — confirmed directly rather than assumed: the one hit that looked plausibly new (`missing_const_for_fn` on the newly-`pub(crate)` `StartMenuState::close`) turned out to be the SAME lint already firing on the completely untouched sibling `StartMenuState::toggle` a few lines above it, proving the file's existing style just doesn't mark every const-eligible method `const` consistently — left both alone rather than arbitrarily "fixing" one sibling and not the other. Judgment calls flagged for the operator: (1) closing on every hidden→visible edge, including a bare unmute of an already-live critical, is a literal reading of lock #9's "auto-closes... if open" rather than a narrower "only on a brand-new firing" reading — the design doc doesn't disambiguate, and this unit picked the broader reading since an unmuted-into-view critical is just as much "the cue clearing its field" as a fresh one; (2) the auto-close reaches the Start Menu even behind the lock curtain (curtain-independent, like the cue itself) — a pure, harmless state mutation with no rendering/navigation effect while locked, so unlocking never resurfaces a stale-open Start Menu over a critical that fired while curtained, but this exact scenario wasn't separately unit-tested (out of this unit's stated test bar, flagged rather than silently assumed). **NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat** — re-checked rather than assumed: confirmed no screenshot/pixel-diff harness exists for `mde-shell-egui` today (`install-helpers/preview-capture.sh`/`preview-gallery.sh`, OBS-4's own harness, still hardcode `MDE_WORKBENCH_BIN`/`mde-workbench` and haven't been touched since 2026-06-17, well before this epic). This is the SIXTH consecutive WIN7-chrome-adjacent unit to land honest-but-visually-unverified — the accumulating gap WIN7-5 already flagged is unchanged by this unit, though the interaction here is a state-machine correctness question tests can and do fully pin down (unlike a pure layout/visual redesign), so the visual-verification gap matters less to THIS unit's own correctness than it did for WIN7-1..5's chrome work. **WIN7-7 DONE 2026-07-10:** the accesskit pass (lock #14) — investigated the ACTUAL current coverage across `dock.rs`/`start_menu.rs`/`console.rs`/`status.rs` before touching anything, per this unit's own brief, rather than assuming either "already done" or "build from scratch." Found the epic's own incremental accesskit work (WIN7-2/3/4/5 plus `status.rs`'s pre-existing NOTIF-11 work) had already covered the Start Menu panel/pane landmarks, every live-tile's `Button` role + rotating value + one aggregate live region, every Console row WIN7-5 rewrote (cross-checked all 8 of that file's raw `ui.interact` call sites against an `install_row_accessibility` call — 8/8 covered, confirming its own report's claim, not just trusting it), and — confirmed by direct inspection, not assumed — the tray's individual segment pips (`status.rs`'s `install_segment_accessibility`, called per-`StatusSegment` from `notification_rail`, already reused unchanged inside `dock.rs`'s taskbar). The one genuinely large gap: `dock.rs` itself exported ZERO accesskit nodes anywhere — verified by grep (0 hits for `accesskit`/`Role::`/`widget_info` in the whole file before this unit) and by reading egui 0.31.1's own source (`Response::widget_info` is the ONLY path that feeds a raw `ui.interact`-based response into the accessibility tree; this file never called it — the crate's one production call is `status.rs`'s own `#[cfg(test)]`-only retired `status_bar`) — meaning the ENTIRE bottom taskbar, this epic's own centerpiece chrome, was invisible to a screen reader beyond its tray pips: the Start cell, the Desktop rail cell, the Desktop-source caret + its flyout rows, every running-session entry + the overflow "more" popup (which reuses `session_entry`, so one fix covers both), the notification/status toggle, the clock, and the auto-hide pin. Fixed with a new `dock.rs`-local accesskit section (`accesskit_rect` restated + a new `install_cell_accessibility`, the exact `status.rs`/`console.rs`/`start_menu.rs` per-module-restated shape, named for this module's own "cell" vocabulary) plus a new `install_taskbar_accessibility` landmark answering the task's own "does the taskbar itself have a role" question — `Role::Toolbar`, accesskit's ARIA-toolbar-equivalent "a container grouping a set of controls," mirroring how `start_menu.rs`'s `install_accessibility` already landmarks the Start Menu panel ahead of its own content. Also closed: the clock's accessible VALUE is now the SAME live `HH:MM` reading its glyph paints (deliberately NOT `Live::Polite` — narrating the time out loud on every unattended minute rollover would be a spam regression, the same reasoning WIN7-4's tile live-region doc already used to reject per-tile announcements); the session-overflow "More" cell's value now carries the REAL hidden count (`rail_more_cell` gained a `hidden_count: usize` parameter, one call site updated) instead of a bare "more" with no number; a small bonus fix to the shared `context_menu_row` helper (reachable from the taskbar's Desktop cell's right-click menu, and incidentally also benefiting the out-of-scope app picker's identical menu) since it was a 3-line addition reusing the same helper. A second, independent gap: the Start Menu's own OPEN/CLOSE transition had a `Role::Menu` landmark (WIN7-2) but nothing ANNOUNCED it — a screen reader user would only discover the menu by independently exploring the tree, never by an actual event, unlike every other state change in this epic (NOTIF-6's critical alert, WIN7-4's tile rotation, WIN7-5's honest-gate notice). Fixed with `start_menu.rs`'s new `install_start_menu_state_announcement` — a `Role::Status`/`Live::Polite` region (label "Start Menu status," deliberately distinct from the landmark's own "Start Menu" label so a label-keyed test lookup can never confuse the two) whose value flips "Start Menu opened"/"Start Menu closed," called every frame the panel's content closure runs (opening, settled open, or still mid-close-tween) — the value's OWN stability while steadily in one state is what keeps a real AT from re-announcing every frame (the same reasoning `install_status_accessibility`/`install_tiles_live_summary` already rely on), verified deterministically off egui's `predicted_dt` (1/60s against `Motion::BASE`'s 0.18s, confirmed from `RawInput::time`'s own doc comment — NOT real wall-clock time) rather than a timing-fragile guess. Investigated and confirmed NOT a gap, despite how the task's own audit questions were phrased: tray icons already have individual accessible names (not just an aggregate row) via `status.rs`'s pre-existing per-segment accesskit; reading/tab order across taskbar → Start Menu tiles → Console pane is not structurally trapped (no focus-locking code exists anywhere in this crate — confirmed by grep for `with_accessibility_parent`, zero hits, so every accesskit node in this whole crate is a flat child of the pass root and reading order is just call order; `mount_dock_chrome` runs before `mount_start_menu` every frame per `main.rs`, so the tree already reads taskbar-then-Start-Menu) — the taskbar's prior total absence of accesskit nodes WAS the reading-order problem, not a separate defect, so fixing that gap resolves this concern too. Tests: 10 new — 7 in `dock.rs` (the taskbar's own `Role::Toolbar` landmark; a sweep across Start/Desktop/Sessions-fallback/Notification-panel/Clock/Pin/Desktop-sources all exporting labelled `Button`s; a real session entry's own label+value; the clock's value checked against an independently-recomputed `hhmm`; the pin + notification-toggle values flipping on the SAME node across two frames, not frozen at first paint; Desktop-source rows including one unavailable source still named AND flagged; the overflow More cell's value matched against the REAL count of sessions folded out of the inline rail, derived from what's actually registered rather than a guessed literal), 3 in `start_menu.rs` (opening announces via `Live::Polite`; closing announces mid-tween before the panel fully unmounts; a never-opened settled-closed panel exports no announcement at all).
