All 1013 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass (1003 + these 10, an exact match) on farm host 172.20.0.170 (`farm-topology.sh table` checked first — all 4 dom0s idle, 0/9 heavy slots active — `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.170 xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, a cold 3m26s build (no cached target on that host) + 33.26s test run, 0 failures, 0 warnings from `mde-shell-egui` itself) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean on all three touched files. An earlier same-session farm attempt caught 2 real bugs in this unit's OWN new tests before anything shipped — not implementation bugs: `win7_7_a_real_session_entry_exports_its_own_labelled_button` assumed `session_active` (a `set_status_inputs` field) drives a session row's selected tint, when the real predicate is `state.active == Surface::Desktop` (fixed by setting it explicitly); `win7_7_the_session_overflow_more_cell_reports_the_real_hidden_count` assumed the `navbar7_bottom_rail_more_popup_keeps_overflow_sessions_reachable` fixture hides exactly 1 of 4 sessions, when that precedent only ever proved "at least the last one" — the real narrow-380px fixture folds 3 of 4 (fixed by deriving the expected count from `ctx.read_response` on each entry, the same technique the reused precedent itself relies on, rather than a second guess). Both fixed and re-verified green, not silently patched over. Judgment calls flagged for the operator: (1) `Role::Toolbar` for the taskbar landmark is a defensible-but-unsurveyed pick — lock #14 doesn't specify a role, and this is accesskit's own ARIA-toolbar-equivalent, matching how a real Windows taskbar exposes itself to UI Automation; (2) the clock is `Role::Button` (clickable, routes to Timers) rather than accesskit's dedicated `Role::Time` — chosen for consistency with every OTHER informational-but-clickable cell in this shell (the segment pips are `Role::Button` too, despite also carrying a value), not because `Role::Time` would be wrong; (3) the Start Menu open/close announcement's exact wording is this unit's own phrasing, not surveyed. Left untouched, confirmed correct rather than silently assumed: `console.rs`'s own accesskit (its module doc updated with a one-line WIN7-7 confirmation in place of its old "WIN7-7 remains open" claim); `status.rs` (already complete pre-epic); the left vertical dock/app picker and VDOCK-4's system quad (confirmed out of this epic's scope by every prior WIN7-1..6 unit's own explicit exclusion list — a separate, not-yet-scheduled accesskit concern, not part of the Win7 chrome epic). WIN7-8 (multi-seat sync — pin/Custom-group layout onto the mesh-synced settings path, lock #21) is now DONE — see the WIN7-8 slice below. **WIN7-DESKTOP-1 REGRESSION FIX 2026-07-10 (post-WIN7-SHOT-1):** WIN7-SHOT-1 (`9bc65a81`, a headless CPU screenshot-capture test harness built this session precisely because WIN7-1..6 shipped "visually unverified") took the crate's first-ever real screenshot and immediately caught what all six prior "green" units missed: the bottom taskbar (Start · sessions · tray · clock) was rendering at the TOP of the screen instead of the bottom — a thin icon strip + clock at y≈[2,16] on an 800px screen with a large dead gap below it, while the Start Menu (correctly) sat toward the bottom-left. Root cause, confirmed by reading `notification_rail_with_sources` directly rather than trusting the prior trace: `dock.rs`'s `local` rect — the ABSOLUTE coordinate every rail cell paints through (`ui.painter()`) AND hit-tests through (`ui.interact()`, since egui Areas are always screen-space, never a re-offset local frame — contrast `dock()`'s own `paint_dock_frame`, which correctly hands back the rect `ui.allocate_exact_size` already returns absolute, never re-deriving one) — was computed as `Rect::from_min_size(pos2(0.0, rail_rect.top() - area_top), rail_rect.size())`. In the rail's default (no status-panel) state `area_top == rail_rect.top()` exactly, so that subtraction was always `0.0`, pinning the WHOLE taskbar — paint and click hit-rects alike — at literal screen y=0. `git blame` + `git show 41314016` trace this to `e0b368e6` ("shell: move status into bottom rail", 2026-07-08, the commit that FIRST wrote `notification_rail_with_sources`) — two days BEFORE WIN7-1 (`41314016`, 2026-07-10), whose own diff never touches this code (verified: it only reorders the pin past the clock and trims a density constant) — so WIN7-1 INHERITED this bug rather than introducing it. The identical root cause also mispositioned NOTIF-4's status-detail slide-out panel (`notification_panel_rect` derives from the same `local`), invisible for the same reason. **Fix:** `local = rail_rect` — `rail_rect` was ALREADY the correct absolute rect the whole time (computed two lines above from `screen.bottom()`); the bug was re-deriving a second, wrong one instead of reusing it. Investigating the fix's fallout (per this unit's own mandate: sanity-check whether fixing the taskbar's position reveals other layout problems built on the wrong position) surfaced TWO further real, pre-existing bugs, both invisible for the identical reason (the taskbar's own wrong position happened to mask them) and both fixed alongside the primary bug: (1) **the Start Menu overlapped the taskbar** — `start_menu.rs`'s `start_menu_panel` anchored its slide-up panel at `screen.bottom() - t*panel_h`, flush with the screen's LITERAL bottom edge, reserving nothing for the taskbar; once the taskbar renders where it belongs, the Start·session·tray·clock strip would sit exactly on top of the Start Menu's Power-anchored bottom (lock #11), directly violating lock #1's "true Win7 bottom taskbar" (an authentic Win7 Start Menu never overlaps the taskbar). Fixed in the same spirit as the primary fix (matching the already-locked design, not a new design call): `start_menu_panel` gained a `rail_h: f32` parameter (the live `DockState::rail_height()`, widened `pub(crate)`) and now anchors at `screen.bottom() - rail_h - t*panel_h`, with the existing `panel_h` safety clamp extended to also reserve `rail_h`; on the standard 1280×800 fixture at default `Density::Mouse` this only shifts the panel's Y position by 18px (`panel_h` itself stays the constant 576px, so the tile grid's/Console pane's own internal layout is untouched — verified: `PANEL_H.min(800-18-32) == 576`, unchanged). Both of `main.rs`'s call sites (`mount_start_menu`, and the WIN7-SHOT-2 test fixture `paint_taskbar_and_start_menu`) now thread `vdock.rail_height()` through; `start_menu.rs`'s own ~40 pre-existing tests pass `rail_h=0.0` (an isolated fixture with no taskbar modeled), preserving their exact prior semantics untouched. (2) **a single ArrowDown/ArrowUp press could cascade focus through the WHOLE app-picker + bottom-rail in one frame**, landing on the wrong cell — `navbar6_arrow_keys_traverse_picker_glyph_focus` started failing (`got` the Desktop cell's id, wanted Voice's) the moment the taskbar's Desktop cell moved to its correct position. Confirmed via a controlled revert-and-compare that this cascade is 100% pre-existing and position-independent in its root cause (`dock()`'s own `request_focus` chain is byte-for-byte identical with or without the taskbar-position fix) — it only ever "passed" because the pre-fix Desktop rail cell sat exactly where egui's OWN built-in spatial arrow-key search (vendored egui 0.31.1, `memory/mod.rs`'s `Focus::begin_pass`/`end_pass` — `end_pass` unconditionally overwrites focus with whatever its `find_widget_in_direction` spatial search finds, if anything, whenever the focused widget's `EventFilter` doesn't claim the arrow key) would ALSO land, purely by positional coincidence. Two independent, compounding mechanisms, both fixed in `apply_picker_arrow_focus`: claim vertical+horizontal arrows via `Memory::set_focus_lock_filter` (egui's own documented seam for "I handle these keys myself") every frame a picker cell holds focus, so egui's spatial search never fires; AND consume every arrow-key event the moment this function's OWN `request_focus` call fires, so the SAME un-consumed key press can't ALSO be seen by whichever cell focus was just moved to, later in the same frame — without this second half, the app's own `PICKER_FOCUS_ORDER` chain cascades exactly as much as egui's spatial one did. Both mechanisms verified necessary independently (each alone left the test red) against the actual vendored egui 0.31.1 source in `~/.cargo/registry`, not assumed from behavior alone. A THIRD, test-only issue surfaced investigating a related failure in `the_desktop_rail_caret_opens_sources_and_latches_a_pick`: the Desktop rail's compact source flyout (`desktop_source_flyout`) uses a non-default `egui::Area` pivot (`LEFT_BOTTOM`, opening upward from the Desktop cell) — egui doesn't know an Area's real content size until it's been laid out once, so the FIRST-ever frame an Area id is shown is a "sizing pass" positioned using a hardcoded `(600, 400)` placeholder (`Style::spacing.default_area_size`), self-correcting the frame after (confirmed directly: `AreaState::load` goes `None` → `Some(size: [240,36])` between those two frames). Pre-fix this was invisible because the flyout's anchor sat at literal screen y≈0, where the wrong sizing-pass estimate and the correct position happened to land within a couple of points of each other; post-fix they differ by ~360pt. Compounding this, `Context::read_response`'s own doc ("widget interaction happens at the start of the pass, using the widget rects from the PREVIOUS pass") means a rect read right after the frame that first computes it correctly STILL returns the prior (wrong) one — a third settle frame is what actually makes the correct rect visible to `read_response`. Fixed by driving three settle frames (not one) before reading the flyout row's rect, with the exact mechanism documented inline rather than a guessed frame count. Both position fixes verified with a full render via WIN7-SHOT-1's `screenshot.rs` harness (`win7_shot_2_start_menu_screenshot_shows_the_accumulated_win7_chrome`, `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/target/screenshots/win7-start-menu.png`, pulled to `~/mcnf-release-artifacts/`): BEFORE, the taskbar strip (icons + clock) sits in a thin band at the literal top of the screen with a large dead gap below it, while the Start Menu (unaffected by the taskbar bug) already sat correctly at the bottom-left; AFTER, Start·Desktop·tray·"23:54" clock sit flush along the screen's TRUE bottom edge and the Start Menu's two panes (tile grid left, System/Network/… list + Pinned column right) stop cleanly above it with a small clean gap — no overlap, no dead space. New regression tests — the class layout-assertion tests structurally cannot write, since none of WIN7-1..6's own tests ever read the driven `screen_rect`: `dock.rs`'s `win7_desktop_1_regression_the_taskbar_anchors_to_the_screens_true_bottom_edge` (Start/clock/pin cells within `Style::SP_S` of the screen's true bottom; confirmed to fail pre-fix at exactly `got 16` on an 800px screen — the EXACT y the WIN7-SHOT-1 trace predicted) and `win7_desktop_1_regression_the_status_panel_opens_above_the_rail_not_the_screen_top` (confirmed to fail pre-fix at `got top=0`); `start_menu.rs`'s `win7_desktop_1_regression_a_nonzero_rail_height_reserves_room_above_the_taskbar` (a nonzero `rail_h` measurably shrinks the panel's hit-testable footprint, proven via `ctx.layer_id_at`). All 1006 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass (1003 pre-existing + these 3 new, an exact accounting — confirmed via a genuine red-then-green farm cycle: a first farm run with only the 2 new dock.rs tests added showed `1003 passed; 2 failed` against the UNFIXED code, proving they actually catch the bug, not just assert something already true) on farm host 172.20.0.90 (`farm-topology.sh table` checked first, all 4 dom0s idle; `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.90 xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, ~35s build+test) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean on all three touched files (`dock.rs`, `start_menu.rs`, `main.rs`). The arrow-key-cascade and flyout-settle-frame fixes each independently needed a live revert-and-diagnose cycle against the vendored egui 0.31.1 source (`~/.cargo/registry/src/…/egui-0.31.1/src/{memory/mod.rs,containers/area.rs,context.rs}`) to pin the exact mechanism rather than a plausible-looking guess. Judgment calls flagged for the operator: (1) fixing the arrow-key-focus cascade and the flyout-pivot settle-frame issue went beyond this unit's own primary mandate (the taskbar position bug) — both are real, independently pre-existing bugs this fix's position change merely made OBSERVABLE (not introduced), and leaving them broken would have left `mde-shell-egui`'s test count net-negative, so they were fixed rather than deferred; both are called out as distinct from the primary fix in this note and are easy to isolate/revert if the operator disagrees with the scope call. (2) `apply_picker_arrow_focus`'s fix changes a REAL behavioral contract (arrow-key nav now reliably moves exactly one step, where before it was accidentally-sometimes-correct depending on unrelated screen geometry) — a strict correctness improvement with no plausible downside, but the biggest surface-area change in this note and the one most worth a second look. (3) Did not backfill a WIN7-SHOT-1 entry into this WORKLIST (that unit's own commit, `9bc65a81`, never updated this file) — out of this regression-fix note's own scope; flagging the gap rather than silently expanding to cover it. **REGRESSION INVESTIGATED, NOT REPRODUCIBLE — 2026-07-10:** this session's full-workspace verification pass flagged 2 pre-existing `dock.rs` tests as regressed by WIN7-1's lock #12 density pass (`NOTIFICATION_RAIL_H` 20px → 18px, `Style::SP_M + Style::SP_XS / 2.0`): `navbar6_arrow_keys_traverse_picker_glyph_focus` (dock.rs:5183 — ArrowDown from Workbench expected to land on `Surface::Voice`) and `the_desktop_rail_caret_opens_sources_and_latches_a_pick` (dock.rs:5088 — a simulated flyout-row click expected to latch `Some("peer:oak")`), bisected to `41314016`. Traced both code paths before trusting the report: `navbar6_...`'s ArrowDown/ArrowUp handling (`apply_picker_arrow_focus` → `picker_focus_neighbor`, dock.rs:2233-2259) walks the static `PICKER_FOCUS_ORDER` array (dock.rs:335-352) by plain index — no pixel/geometry input at all, so no rail-height change can move which surface it lands on. `the_desktop_rail_caret_...` never hardcodes a pixel literal — it reads the caret's and the flyout row's rects fresh at runtime (`ctx.read_response(id).rect.center()`) and clicks there, and the flyout's own row geometry (`DESKTOP_SOURCE_ROW_H`=28.0, `DESKTOP_SOURCE_FLYOUT_W`, `DESKTOP_SOURCE_MAX_ROWS`=8, dock.rs:1864-1866) is untouched by WIN7-1 — only the whole rail (and the flyout's `Align2::LEFT_BOTTOM`-pivoted anchor) shifts by the same uniform ~2px every other rail cell shifts by. Neither test has a structural dependency on the literal `NOTIFICATION_RAIL_H` value. Ran both directly rather than trusting the summary (this task's own instruction): individually, as the `dock::`-scoped module (49 tests), as the literal bare `-p mde-shell-egui dock` substring filter (55 tests — the extra 6 are non-dock-module tests whose names merely contain "dock"), and as the full unfiltered `-p mde-shell-egui` suite (1003 tests) — all green, 0 failures, from a clean isolated worktree on farm host 172.20.0.130. Went further and checked `41314016` itself (WIN7-1, the commit the original bisection named) out into its own dedicated, uncontaminated worktree + build slot: the `dock::` module (48 tests, the smaller pre-WIN7-2..6 count) and the full crate suite (964 tests) both pass there too, 0 failures — the exact commit named as the regression's origin shows no regression under a clean run. Likely explanation for the false report: mid-investigation this session's own tooling briefly pointed a farm build at the repo's shared primary checkout instead of an isolated worktree, and directly caught that shared checkout's `master` branch advancing under a concurrent agent's cherry-picks (83d1ba3f → 79d354cb, the WIN7-7 commit landing mid-read) while carrying uncommitted debug `eprintln!`-style instrumentation on this exact test — live, direct proof of the failure mode (a shared, concurrently-mutating checkout mistaken for a stable target) that most plausibly explains the original report too, rather than a real code defect. **Determination: neither side was wrong** — the layout code and both tests' expectations are already mutually consistent; no code or test change applied here. `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` stays clean (dock.rs untouched by this note). Residual honesty flag: this rules out the specific failure as reported and reproduces cleanly across 4 independent runs spanning 2 commits and 2 farm hosts with zero flakiness, but isn't proof no ordering/feature-flag combination could ever trip these two tests — if either resurfaces, re-verify from a freshly isolated worktree (never the shared primary checkout) before re-bisecting. **WIN7-8 DONE 2026-07-10:** multi-seat sync (lock #21) — resolved the design doc's own flagged open item first (`docs/design/win7-desktop-survey.md`'s "Open items": "Where Console currently persists Custom entries"): read `console.rs`'s `CustomFile`/`persist_custom` in full and confirmed Custom entries (lock #35) were persisted ONLY to a local JSON file (`<client-data-dir>/console-custom.json`) — no mesh-sync mechanism at all. Also verified, rather than assumed, the design doc's other flagged half of lock #21 (pin layout on the TILE side): `start_menu.rs`'s `TILE_GROUPS` (WIN7-3) is a compile-time `const [TileGroup; 7]`, not per-operator state — there is nothing to sync there; lock #21's "pin layout / Custom-group arrangement" refers entirely to Console's Custom group. Investigated this project's own established mesh-sync mechanisms before building anything new (per the unit's own "reuse, don't invent" mandate): the `mackesd` workers (`browser_session_sync`, `browser_passkeys`, the bookmarks worker's full CRDT op-log) all route through Bus action-topic RPC + a running daemon, a heavier mechanism than a same-crate GUI-side list needs; `crate::chooser::chooser_prefs` (CHOOSER-9) — "the operator's Desktop Chooser preferences that FOLLOW the mesh identity between seats: pinned favorites... manual (operator-added) sources" — turned out to be the closest structural AND textual match: a per-seat JSON file under the Syncthing-replicated workgroup root, LWW-per-id merge with tombstones, honest-offline gating, read in full as the template. Built a new `console/custom_sync.rs` submodule (mirroring `chooser/chooser_prefs.rs`'s own shape) that reuses the SAME mechanism: one `SeatCustomFile` JSON file per seat at `<workgroup_root>/console-custom-sync/<identity>/<seat>.json` (atomic temp+rename), folded across every seat's file on read; `chooser.rs`'s `mod chooser_prefs;` widened `pub(crate)` (the `dock::response_activated`/`status::severity_color` cross-module-widening idiom already established this epic) so `resolve_identity`/`resolve_seat`/`unix_millis` are reused verbatim rather than reimplemented, keeping every identity-bound record in this crate on the same resolution precedence. A Custom entry's LWW register is keyed by its own CONTENT (`name`+`command`, not a synthetic id) — this domain only ever adds/removes a whole entry, never edits one in place, so the content pair IS the stable identity; a remove converges mesh-wide even for an entry the removing seat never itself added, by writing a fresh tombstone into that seat's own file (the exact `ChooserPrefs::remove_manual` idiom). `console.rs`'s pre-existing `CustomFile`/`CUSTOM_FILE` local-file persistence is completely UNCHANGED (same shape, same round-trip behavior, same tests) — the sync layer is a strict ADDITION, never a replacement, so an unmeshed seat keeps working exactly as before this unit. Wired conservatively: `ConsoleState::with_store` (used by 30+ pre-existing tests in this file and `start_menu.rs`) leaves the new `custom_sync` field `None` — a new `with_store_and_sync` constructor is the injectable seam WIN7-8's own tests use, and a new `ConsoleState::for_shell` (now `main.rs`'s real production call site, replacing bare `default()`) is the ONLY path that resolves the real workgroup root. This split exists because `custom_sync::CustomSync::open_default()` resolves `mackes_mesh_types::peers::default_workgroup_root()` (`/mnt/mesh-storage` by default) — wiring that into `Default`/`with_store` themselves would have made `cargo test` read/write REAL shared mesh state on any farm host where that mount is genuinely live, since this file alone calls `ConsoleState::default()` 13 times across its own test suite (verified by grep before deciding, not assumed) — a hazard `chooser.rs`'s own test suite avoids by NEVER calling `ChooserState::default()` in a test (confirmed by grep: only `main.rs`'s production wiring calls it), a discipline this unit's split preserves without having to rewrite Console's already-large pre-existing test suite. The merge refreshes on open (`ConsoleState::set_open`'s closed→open edge, the SAME "refreshed on each open" cadence `refresh_presence`'s `$PATH` table already uses) and after every `add_custom`/`remove_custom`, and the merged view is cached back to the local file too (the BROWSER-DD-7 "local snapshot is both the offline fallback and the mirror source" shape) so a later fully-offline run still shows the last-known-good merged set. Confirmed, rather than assumed, that transient UI state never leaks into the synced record: this module has no persisted "scroll position" at all (owned entirely by egui's own internal `ScrollArea` memory, never read or serialized by this crate) and no "which group is expanded" concept either (the rail's `jump` field is a one-shot scroll TRIGGER drained by the next render, not a persisted expand/collapse state — the list pane always shows every group, it's not an accordion) — both are local-only purely by construction, with nothing in this crate that could even attempt to publish them; `open`/`focus`/`jump`/`gate`/`arming` have no field anywhere in `SeatCustomFile`/`SyncedCustomEntry`'s shape. Tests: 8 new in `custom_sync.rs` (the LWW merge — a remove beats an older add, distinct entries from different seats union, oldest-add-first ordering, exact-content-duplicates collapse to one register; the store round-trip across seats with a corrupt-file skip; an unprovisioned root is a silent no-op; an inert session still folds its own edit in-memory but persists nothing; the crux two-seat test — an add on seat A roams to seat B, and seat B's remove of an entry it never itself added roams back to seat A) plus 6 new in `console.rs` (`win7_8_*`-prefixed: a `ConsoleState`-level add roaming seat-to-seat over real `ConsoleState::with_store_and_sync` sessions; a `ConsoleState`-level remove converging the same way; `with_store` alone stays purely local, unchanged; and the explicit negative proof lock #21 calls for — opening/closing the panel 5× against a real ready sync session never creates a file under the store at all, moving `jump`/`focus`/`focus_moved` around never does either, and a synced record that DOES exist (from a real add) is read back as raw JSON and grepped to confirm it names only the Custom entry's own fields, never `open`/`jump`/`focus`/`scroll`/`expanded`/`gate`/`arming`). All 1030 `mde-shell-egui` tests pass (1016 pre-existing + these 14, an exact accounting — independently re-verified after a first farm attempt accidentally built the WRONG tree, `/root/magic-mesh`'s own non-worktree checkout rather than this isolated worktree, from a stray `cd` in the invocation; caught because that first run reported 1016 passed with ZERO of the 14 new tests present in the log, a re-run from the correct worktree root confirmed the real result) on farm host 172.20.0.130 (BigBoy, per the operator's standing longest-build rule — `farm-topology.sh table` checked first, all 4 dom0s idle — `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui`, 18.53s test run, 0 failures, 0 warnings from any file this unit touched — the sole warning in the log, an unused `std::time::Duration` import, traces to `mde-bus/src/hooks/server.rs`, pre-existing and untouched) and `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` is clean on all four touched files (`console.rs`, `console/custom_sync.rs`, `chooser.rs`, `main.rs`). A bonus `cargo clippy -p mde-shell-egui --all-targets` diligence pass (idle farm, not this unit's stated verification bar) on farm host 172.20.0.90 found the crate's own pre-existing ~350-400-warning pedantic debt (the WIN7-3 finding, unchanged) plus exactly 6 NEW hits, all in `custom_sync.rs`, all the SAME `redundant_pub_crate` family ("pub(crate) inside a private module") already firing 100+ times elsewhere in this crate for the identical `pub(crate) mod X;`-inside-`main.rs` shape (`console`/`chooser` themselves are private `mod` declarations at the crate root) — left alone, matching WIN7-3/4/7's own explicit "ambient pre-existing style, not a regression" calls; zero clippy hits traced to any of `console.rs`'s own new lines (`for_shell`/`with_store_and_sync`/`refresh_custom_sync`/the two `add_custom`/`remove_custom` call sites), confirmed by grepping the clippy log for those names directly rather than assumed clean. Judgment calls flagged for the operator: (1) an entry's LWW-merge identity is its own (name, command) content pair, not a synthetic id — the one honestly-flagged edge case: two entries sharing BOTH the identical name AND the identical command collapse to one register once meshed (nothing stopped registering the literal same pair twice locally before this unit; a random/synthetic id was considered and rejected since it would need a durable id threaded through the plain local file/UI/accesskit code that never needed one before, to guard a near-zero-stakes case); (2) the merge orders entries oldest-add-first by `updated_ms` across seats (the closest honest analogue to the pre-sync local behavior's plain append order) rather than, say, grouping by which seat added each one — an inference, not a surveyed answer, easy to change if the operator wants a different arrangement; (3) production wiring is a NEW `ConsoleState::for_shell()` constructor rather than widening `Default` itself — a deliberate, safety-motivated split (see above) rather than the more surface-minimal "just teach `Default` to sync," worth a confirm if the operator would rather every constructor eventually resolve real mesh state. **NOT visually smoke-tested on a live seat** — no rendering/layout changed in this unit at all (the merge only changes what populates the existing `custom.entries: Vec<CustomEntry>`, and every render/accesskit consumer of it is untouched), so this unit doesn't extend the WIN7-1..7 visual-verification gap already flagged, though it also doesn't close it. WIN7-9 (hotkey scheme redesign, lock #22) is the next open slice — the design doc's own "Open items" section flags it as needing its own short survey pass first ("clean break" was locked; the actual bindings were not), a slice this unit's own work did not touch or resolve.

## CHAT-FIX — the empty-Chat bug + local notifications (operator 2026-07-04, found live on Eagle; design: docs/design/console-frontdoor.md)

Eagle's Chat is empty. Two real defects: the chat worker never runs (census gap), and nothing produces local system notifications.

- [✓] **CHAT-FIX-1: register the chat worker in the census (reporting hygiene).** **As** an operator, **I want** `role-workers` to list chat, **so that** the census is honest. *(CORRECTED: live-verified on Eagle the chat worker ALREADY runs — boot log `starting worker: chat` (rank-0 unknown-worker default). This is a REPORTING gap (`role-workers`/census omits it), NOT the empty-Chat cause — the BUG-STORAGE-1 REPORTING half. The empty Chat is CHAT-FIX-2.)* **Acceptance:** `bin/mackesd.rs` spawns ChatWorker only `if runs("chat", role_rank)`, but "chat" is absent from `WORKER_TIERS` → the unknown-worker default keeps it off (the BUG-STORAGE-1 class); add `("chat", 0)` (universal/rank-0) to `WORKER_TIERS` so chat runs on every node incl. Eagle; census count tests updated; live-verify the chat worker appears in Eagle's roster after deploy.
- [✓] **CHAT-FIX-2: the local-notification producer worker.** **As** a user, **I want** the node to tell me what's happening, **so that** Chat isn't empty absent peer messages. *(THE real empty-Chat fix — operator reported it live on Eagle: chat worker runs but there are no peer messages + nothing produces local system notifications. Note: an ntfy broker already runs (overlay :8443) — the producer can ride it.)* **Acceptance:** a new mackesd `notify` worker watches: mesh peer join/leave (directory), dnf/platform update availability, `systemctl --failed` + mesh/cloud health, disk-usage/SMART thresholds, and **journal WARN-or-above**; emits typed notifications on the bus (bounded/rate-limited polls); the Chat surface renders them as a scrollable timestamped feed (newest first) + the tray badge counts unread (Q46/47); census entry (rank-0); tested each event source → a notification (fixture-driven) + the Chat feed render. *(the notify producer half of Q34; composes with CONSOLE)*

## PICKER-GROUPS — grouping the bottom picker's surface icons (operator 2026-07-04; 10-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/picker-groups.md)

Organize NAVBAR-W10-2's flat app row into Workbench-lead + 6 vertically-labeled accent groups (Comms·Workloads·Terminals·Mesh·System·Media), each label rotated bottom-to-top to the left of its icons beside a Carbon-blue hairline; overflow chevron when narrow. All in `dock.rs`.

- [✓] **PICKER-1: the group table + rotated labels + hairline dividers.** **As** a user, **I want** the picker organized into named groups, **so that** I can find surfaces by purpose. **Acceptance:** `dock.rs` renders Workbench as a standalone lead, then a const `Group` table (Comms:Chat,Voice · Workloads:Instances · Terminals:Editor,Terminal,Browser · Mesh:MeshView,Desktop · System:Files,System,Storage,About · Media:Music,Media) in that order (L5/L7); each group preceded by a bottom-to-top rotated label (L1) to its left (L2) beside a **Carbon-blue hairline** rule (L3); all 15 surfaces appear once, click-routing + active-underline + hover unchanged; Carbon tokens (§4, no raw hex incl. the blue); shell tests updated + green.
- [✓] **PICKER-2: per-group accent color tokens (shared with EXPLORER-15).** **As** a user, **I want** each group color-keyed, **so that** the picker + explorer speak one color language. **Acceptance:** six accent tokens (Comms/Workloads/Terminals/Mesh/System/Media) defined ONCE in `mde-egui::Style`/`mde-theme` (§4, no raw hex); the picker labels take their group accent (L4); the SAME tokens are what EXPLORER-15 consumes for category identity (coordinate — define once, both consume); tested token application per group. *(coordinate with EXPLORER-15)*
- [✓] **PICKER-3: compact overflow chevron.** **As** a user on a narrow bar, **I want** graceful overflow, **so that** nothing is silently clipped. **Acceptance:** the grouped run measures against available width (total − Workbench lead − pinned tray reserve); overflowing groups (last/least-used first) collapse into a trailing `»` chevron whose anchored popup lists the hidden groups' labels + icons (reuse the tray popup idiom, L6); the tray stays pinned right; tested overflow threshold + popup contents + no silent drop. *(reconciled 2026-07-07 after VDOCK-6: the horizontal grouped-width bar this wording targeted is retired, and VDOCK-6 explicitly moved the picker into the vertical dock while extending the PICKER-3 harness to pin vertical geometry. The live equivalent is implemented in `dock.rs`: `visible_group_count` measures the available vertical app zone, trailing/low-priority groups fold into the `…` overflow cell, and `pick_overflow` mounts the anchored popup listing hidden group labels + icons with normal surface routing. Farm evidence from the current `.90` dock sweep: `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests:: -- --nocapture` passed `46/46`, including `the_app_zone_fits_all_groups_when_tall_and_overflows_when_short` and `the_overflow_more_popup_routes_a_hidden_group_surface`. No horizontal `»` chevron is rebuilt because that would revive the retired bar.)*

## EXPLORER — the Hero unit explorer (operator 2026-07-04; 25-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/unit-explorer.md)

A cinematic full-bleed "Hero" view of every discovered unit — mesh peers, off-mesh LAN hosts, AND every OpenStack object on any node — folded into the Discovery surface. Daemon aggregator (§6) does the scanning; shell renders. MVP = all three sources at once (lock #25). EXPLORER-1 (aggregator worker) is the spine; the shell hero fold + scan submodule serialize behind it.

- [✓] **EXPLORER-1: the `unit_aggregator` mackesd worker + `Unit` model.** **As** the mesh, **I want** one unit stream, **so that** the shell renders without scanning. **Acceptance:** new `workers/unit_aggregator/` publishing `state/units*` on the bus; typed `Unit { id, kind(Peer/LanHost/Instance/Volume/Image/Network), name, reachability(InMesh/OnLan/CloudObject{node}), address?, health?, telemetry?, actions[] }`; folds the mesh mirror (peers/health) + unions every `state/openstack/<node>` (QC-2) dedup-by-id host-tagged (#20); unprobed fields explicit `unknown` (#12/§7); publish-on-change + rescan tick (#19); census entry (rank per the BUG-STORAGE-1 lesson); tested folds + union dedup. *(scan submodule = EXPLORER-2)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-2: the active LAN scan submodule.** **As** the aggregator, **I want** off-mesh discovery, **so that** LAN units appear. **Acceptance:** mDNS/DNS-SD listen + ARP/neighbor read + a bounded ping-sweep & light port fingerprint over the local subnet (nmap-style, #3) via the bounded-proc path; runs ONLY while the scan-active flag is set (#24) with a warm cache of the last result; emits `LanHost` units with type guesses + explicit unknowns; tested against a controlled fixture (no live-net assertion in unit tests; a live smoke on the farm subnet documented). *(serialise after EXPLORER-1)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-3: the Discovery-surface hero fold + navigation.** **As** a user, **I want** the hero view, **so that** each unit gets a focused presentation. **Acceptance:** the existing Discovery surface grows the full-bleed hero card + bottom filmstrip + top category chips + `/` search (#5–8); Carbon slide+fade paging (#21); adaptive cinematic↔list-detail by shell size (#22); subscribes to `state/units*`; renders self as the first unit at empty then streams (#23); sets the aggregator scan-active flag only while visible (#24); enrollment/pair purpose preserved (folds into the #16 invite action); shell tests green. *(serialise after EXPLORER-1; touches the shell — coordinate with W10/CURTAIN)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-4: the hero card content + live telemetry.** **As** a user, **I want** a rich card, **so that** I know each unit at a glance. **Acceptance:** large type glyph + live status ring (#9), name/type/reachability headline (#10), rich telemetry sparklines (load/mem/net/uptime) when reachable else dimmed-minimal card with explicit unknowns (#11/12); all Carbon tokens (§4); tested reachable-vs-dimmed rendering. *(after EXPLORER-3)* *(done: `explorer.rs` — a rolling per-unit `UnitHistory` (60-pt ring ≈ 5 min at the 5 s poll) sampled each `refresh` from the REAL polled load/mem scalars (§7 — every point observed, never synthesised; departed units pruned); the rich card now paints a load·mem·net·uptime metric grid where load/mem draw a live sparkline (axis expands to a real peak), net stays honestly dimmed "no source" (no wire seam yet), uptime shows its value on a neutral baseline (a scalar counter, no fake trend), and a still-filling series reads "collecting…"; dimmed-minimal path unchanged; all Carbon `Style` tokens; +4 tests: bounded-real-sample accrual, prune-on-depart, no-series-no-history, reachable-sparkline-vs-dimmed render.)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-5: per-type action bars (armed destructive).** **As** an operator, **I want** real verbs on the hero, **so that** it's a launchpad. **Acceptance:** peer → open-in-Fleet + live health-check + evict(typed-armed) (#14); cloud instance → console(SPICE/VNC) + start/stop/reboot/delete (every mutating verb typed-armed) + volume/image/network inspect+delete(armed) (#15); LAN host → invite-to-mesh(armed) kicking the existing pairing flow (#16); each drives the REAL seam (no dead buttons, §7); arming reuses the platform typed-confirm; tested each verb dispatches + arming gates the destructive ones. *(after EXPLORER-3/4)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-7: edge derivation in the aggregator + bus edge stream.** **As** the mesh, **I want** relationships as data, **so that** any client can render connectivity. **Acceptance:** the `unit_aggregator` derives a typed `Edge { kind, from, to, detail }` set covering all five kinds (E2: mesh tunnels incl. via-lighthouse, cloud attachments instance→network/volume/image + network→subnet→router, L2/L3 adjacency same-subnet/one-ARP-hop/same-gateway, host placement object→node, storage volume→instance + pool/share consumption); published alongside units on `state/units*`; a typed bus read API exposes the unit+edge stream (E9); edges derived from the SAME unioned sources (no new probes); tested per edge kind + dedup. *(after EXPLORER-1)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-8: hero-card edge chips (grouped by kind) + jump.** **As** a user, **I want** to see how a unit connects, **so that** I can traverse the topology. **Acceptance:** the hero card renders a grouped edge-chip region (E1/E6: Tunnels / Networks / Volumes / Same subnet / Runs on \<node\> / Storage sections) from the edge stream; each chip is a clickable related-unit that jumps the hero to that neighbor; Carbon tokens; absent-edge sections omitted (no empty headers); tested chip render + jump. *(after EXPLORER-3 + EXPLORER-7)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-9: full object detail + enrichment sources.** **As** a user, **I want** deep, named facts, **so that** each unit is fully legible. **Acceptance:** instances carry full Nova/Neutron/Cinder detail (E4: flavor/state/all IPs+ports+nets/volumes+sizes/image/host/keypair/created/secgroups); enrichment folds into every unit (E5: reverse-DNS+mDNS names, offline MAC-OUI vendor, service/port fingerprint→type guess+openable actions, mesh cert/role/groups/uptime); first-seen/last-seen timestamps (E10); all honestly `unknown` where unprobed (§7); tested field folds + the offline OUI lookup + fingerprint→type mapping. *(EXPLORER-1 model extension; enrichment probes ride EXPLORER-2's scan)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-10: the IPAM prefix/IP table mode.** **As** an operator, **I want** a NetBox-style address view, **so that** I can see what's on my networks. **Acceptance:** a third surface mode (beside Hero) — a live table of every discovered subnet/prefix (mesh 10.42/…, LAN 172.20/…, OpenStack tenant nets) with each address' occupant unit, free/used count, gateway (E7); rendered live from discovery (no manual allocation); rows jump to the occupant's hero unit; Carbon productive-density table; tested prefix aggregation + occupancy from the unit set. *(after EXPLORER-3 + EXPLORER-7)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-11: the mosaic overview + summary strip + zoom transition + D-pad grid.** **As** a user, **I want** a whole-fleet landing, **so that** I see everything before focusing one. **Acceptance:** the Discovery surface lands on a category-clustered mosaic of mini hero tiles (O1); a slim summary/filter strip on top shows per-category counts + health rollup (green/warn/down) + total addresses, doubling as the category chips (O2/#8); picking a tile shared-element **zooms into** its full hero, Back/Esc zooms out (O3); three-mode switch Mosaic⇄Hero⇄IPAM; full arrow/D-pad grid nav + focus ring (O6); Carbon tokens; tested mode switch + tile→hero zoom + grid nav + rollup counts. *(supersedes part of EXPLORER-3's scope — EXPLORER-3 narrows to the hero-card mode; serialise both after EXPLORER-1)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-12: ambient idle auto-cycle.** **As** an operator, **I want** a living wall display, **so that** an unattended screen still informs. **Acceptance:** after an idle interval the hero slowly auto-advances through units, pausing on ANY input; a toggle enables it (OFF by default), persisted; never fights user input; tested idle-advance + input-pause. *(after EXPLORER-11)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-13: view/selection/filter persistence.** **As** a user, **I want** my place kept, **so that** re-opening is continuous. **Acceptance:** the last mode (Mosaic/Hero/IPAM), last-selected unit id, and active filter/search persist in the shell config + restore on open (O5); survives lock/unlock + restart; tested round-trip restore + graceful fallback when the remembered unit is gone. *(after EXPLORER-11)* *(done: 9292856 — explorer-prefs.json snapshot-diff persistence, pending-focus restore w/ graceful fallback, headless prefs seam.)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-14: universal search + jump.** **As** a user, **I want** to find any unit by any field, **so that** scale doesn't slow me. **Acceptance:** `/` opens a search matching across name/IP/MAC/type/node/service (O7) over a self-contained fuzzy matcher (reuse the editor's `fuzzy` idiom if shareable, else local); results jump the hero/mosaic focus; tested field-spanning matches ("5900"→VNC host, "nova"→instance, MAC prefix, node name). *(after EXPLORER-11)* *(done: 8ae3b34 — / search over name/IP/MAC/type/node/service, local fuzzy scorer, E5 extras block wire-pinned, one jump path; side-fix: surface hotkeys gate off while any text box has focus (also fixed the EXPLORER-5 arming-echo Backspace zoom bug).)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-15: per-category visual identity (accent + glyph family).** **As** a user, **I want** instant category recognition, **so that** I know each unit's world at a glance. **Acceptance:** Mesh/LAN/Cloud each get a distinct Carbon accent token + coherent glyph family applied to tiles/chips/status-rings (O8); tokens live in `mde-theme`/`mde-egui::Style` (§4, no raw hex); tested token application per category. *(mde-theme token add + shell usage; can land early/parallel to EXPLORER-11)* *(done: 6b72482 — reuses PICKER-2 accents (no dup tokens), Category::accent() the one authority, chips wear accent at rest, tessellation vertex-colour test.)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-16: pinning + Pinned cluster.** **As** a user, **I want** my key units up front, **so that** they're always one glance away. **Acceptance:** pin any unit (key/right-click); pinned sort to the front of mosaic + filmstrip + get a Pinned filter chip (O9); persisted in shell config; tested pin/unpin + ordering + chip filter. *(after EXPLORER-11)* *(done: 59862ba — pin via button/P/right-click, one sort_units comparator fronts pinned everywhere, Pinned chip as independent axis, IPAM pinned scope; farm 651 tests, explorer.rs clippy 0.)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-17: multi-select + armed bulk actions.** **As** an operator, **I want** fleet operations, **so that** I act on many units at once. **Acceptance:** mark N units in the mosaic (shift/ctrl/D-pad-mark); the action bar offers verbs the selection SHARES (reboot instances, health-check peers) executed per-unit with a progress rollup; destructive verbs keep the typed-arming (O10, reuses EXPLORER-5's arming); no dead bulk verb (only shared+real, §7); tested selection→shared-verb set + armed dispatch + rollup. *(after EXPLORER-5 + EXPLORER-11)* *(done: 4a03ca6 — Ctrl/Shift/Space marking, shared_bulk_verbs = intersection ∩ bulk-capable ∩ seam-Ok (no dead verb §7), typed-armed destructive re-gating on count change, per-unit dispatch + BulkRollup.)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-18: accessibility — large-type + focus ring + text-scale.** **As** any user, **I want** legibility + keyboard clarity, **so that** it works across the room + by keyboard. **Acceptance:** generous display type, a high-contrast focus ring that always shows the selection (D-pad essential, O11), and honoring the platform text-scale setting throughout; Carbon tokens; tested focus-ring presence on every navigable element + text-scale response. *(folds into EXPLORER-11's nav; verified as its own acceptance)* *(done: 9d7f4d2 — shared focus_ring on all 3 navigable renderers, generous-type contract, text-scale composes with SETTINGS-5 zoom (no double-scale). Farm .170: mde-egui 129 + mde-shell-egui 727 tests, clippy 0.)*
- [✓] **EXPLORER-6: live smoke + wiring verify on the test bed.** **As** the operator, **I want** proof it works live, **so that** it's not just green tests. **Acceptance:** on a live node, opening Discovery shows self + real mesh peers + real LAN hosts (incl. testvm-lin 172.20.146.144) + real OpenStack objects once QC compute is up; a cloud instance console opens; the scan is surface-gated (no probe traffic when closed, verified). *(integration cap; after 1–5 + QC live)* *(done: SEAT-2 live on .2 — Discovery shows self + real LAN hosts (gateway .1, dev host .145.192 REACHABLE via ip neigh), mDNS lane ok 3 types; scan is surface-gated. Specific .146.x peers sparse until enrolled/trafficked.)*

## TESTVM — Spice/VNC/RDP test endpoints for the VDI bed (operator 2026-07-03; `/plan`; design: docs/design/vdi-test-endpoints.md)

Throwaway XCP-ng VMs on a dom0 (LAN-bridged, `172.20.x`) giving the shell's Desktop/VDI surface real targets. Basic connection only. AUTHORIZED (live VDI test bed + blanket op-authorize) — provisioning is live-infra; probe the classifier before exec.

- [✓] **TESTVM-1: provisioning scaffold + local images.** **As** a tester, **I want** a repeatable bringup, **so that** the test VMs are easy to (re)create + tear down. **Acceptance:** a dom0 with headroom chosen by live capacity; a locally-reachable **Alpine** image mirrored (airgap) + a check for a local **Windows** ISO/template (record present/absent); an `xe`/tofu bringup path (reuse `infra/tofu/xen-xapi` where it fits) + a documented teardown (`vm-shutdown`/`vm-destroy`). *(done: eea31e0 — install-helpers/testvm-up.sh (live capacity probe → dom0 .9 chosen 9299MiB free; airgap sha512-verified Alpine mirror; NoCloud seed; xe assemble; IP discovery; endpoint verify; --probe mode) + testvm-down.sh (vm-shutdown --force + vm-uninstall + orphan-VDI sweep). No Windows ISO on either dom0 — recorded for TESTVM-3 (Alpine+xrdp fallback).)*
- [✓] **TESTVM-2: `testvm-lin` (Alpine) — Spice + VNC endpoints.** **As** a tester, **I want** a Linux guest serving Spice + VNC on the LAN, **so that** I can test those viewers. **Acceptance:** Alpine VM boots on the dom0, gets a `172.20.x` address, runs a Spice server (:5930) + `x11vnc` (:5900); both ports reachable from the shell host; a viewer shows its framebuffer. Basic (no/trivial auth). *(done: eea31e0 — testvm-lin uuid 9ce6b326… on dom0 172.20.0.9, DHCP 172.20.146.144. VNC :5900 (x11vnc/Xvfb) — full RFB 003.008 handshake + 270KB framebuffer pulled; Spice :5930 — real SpiceLinkMess/REDQ link (Xspice segfaults on Alpine 3.24 → honest nested qemu -spice fallback, baked into script). Reboot-persistent via /etc/local.d. TESTVM-4 = point the Desktop chooser at :5900/:5930.)*
- [✓] **TESTVM-3: `testvm-win` — RDP endpoint (fallback Alpine+xrdp).** **As** a tester, **I want** an RDP target, **so that** I can test the ironrdp path. **Acceptance:** a Windows VM boots with Remote Desktop enabled (:3389) reachable on the LAN; OR — if no local Windows image — an **Alpine+`xrdp`** guest on :3389 (documented degradation, operator-flagged). `mde-vdi-rdp` connects and shows the login/desktop. *(done: ae69805 testvm-up.sh --rdp — testvm-win a337fe91 on dom0 .9, IP 172.20.146.54, xrdp :3389 LIVE-verified (X.224 CR→CC, PROTOCOL_SSL selected), reboot-persistent via /etc/local.d; DEGRADATION: no Windows ISO on either dom0 → Alpine+xrdp fallback per the unit's documented path — a real Windows target stays an operator gap. TESTVM-4 unblocked: VNC .144:5900 · Spice .144:5930 · RDP .54:3389.)*
- [✓] **TESTVM-4: shell Desktop-chooser connects to each endpoint.** **As** a user, **I want** the Desktop surface to connect to the test endpoints, **so that** the VDI path is proven end-to-end. **Acceptance:** the shell's Desktop chooser is pointed at each endpoint and establishes a basic session over VNC, Spice, and RDP respectively; each viewer renders the guest. (Live-verify; the DRM seat part may need real display hardware per the VDI-bed note.) *(done: d9062d8 + 1a6302b8 — chooser "Pin a desktop endpoint" ADD form over the CHOOSER-9 store (host:port+proto+optional cred), selectable with NO mesh discovery, sealed-cred straight-connect. LIVE-VERIFIED: VNC .144:5900 (RFB 3.8, framebuffer pulled, input echoed) + RDP .54:3389 (Full 1024x768, input echoed, tier-reconnect) — the RDP run caught+fixed a real "no reactor running" panic (tokio timeout built outside the runtime; green-tests≠works-live). Spice: mde-vdi-spice crate exists but not shell-Session-wired → CHOOSER-5 follow-on; its live_spice hung (no connect timeout) — Spice follow-up noted.)*

### QUASAR-CLOUD — the mesh becomes an OpenStack cloud (design: `docs/design/quasar-cloud.md`, 90-Q `/plan` survey 2026-07-03)

**Replacement epic** (operator: "replace the methods in the platform today when possible"). Nova/libvirt replace the mesh-native VM stack; Glance+DIB replace the golden-image script; Cinder adds volumes; Neutron/OVN puts instances on one flat mesh-bridged net; Designate replaces naming; the Controller plane becomes the Cloud plane. Nebula, etcd, Syncthing, Podman, mde-bus, netdata, chat, ISO installer all KEPT. Hard per-node cutover; replaced code deleted same-epic (§7). Parallel to E12; dev cloud on farm VMs.

- [✓] **QC-1: host virt foundation in the image.**
  **As** a mesh operator, **I want** libvirt/QEMU-KVM + OVN host bits baked into the one bootc image, **so that** any node can carry cloud duties without breaking immutability.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] the image ships libvirt/qemu-kvm + OVN; `virsh version` + `ovs-vsctl show` work on a stock node
    - [✓] cloud-hypervisor is absent from the image (Q32); the virtio-gpu→egui path re-validated on QEMU or a blocker filed
  **Host virt bootc image lane slice 2026-07-10:** the bootc image lane now
  installs `podman`, libvirt/QEMU-KVM, `virt-install`, OVMF, Open vSwitch,
  `ovn-host`, `cloud-init`, and `qemu-guest-agent` directly into the immutable
  image and removes the retired pinned cloud-hypervisor static-binary bake.
  `verify-image.sh` now checks `virsh`, `ovs-vsctl`, `cloud-init`, `qemu-ga`,
  the required QEMU/libvirt/OVN RPM payloads, NoCloud/None datasource gating,
  `openvswitch.service` enablement, the bootc install XFS rootfs default, the
  single-kernel `/usr/lib/modules` requirement, and explicitly fails if
  `/usr/bin/cloud-hypervisor` is present. Packaging/kickstart docs were updated
  to name the QC-1 host-virt foundation. Built-image proof is captured: BigBoy
  built `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm`, composed
  `localhost/magic-mesh-bootc:qc1-ovs-20260710`, fixed the linux-surface layer
  for Fedora 42 `dnf5` (`dnf -y install --allowerasing`), and
  `verify-image.sh localhost/magic-mesh-bootc:qc1-ovs-20260710` passed all
  static checks (`virsh` 11.0.0, `ovs-vsctl` 3.4.5, required RPM payloads,
  cloud-hypervisor absent, OVS/cloud-init services enabled). Disk-image proof
  is captured too: BigBoy root podman built the same tag, fixed relative
  `--out` mounting, added `/usr/lib/bootc/install/50-magic-mesh.toml`
  (`type = "xfs"`), removed the duplicate Fedora base kernel after installing
  `kernel-surface`, and `bootc-image-builder` produced
  `packaging/bootc/out-qc1-ovs/qcow2/disk.qcow2` (2,080,833,536 bytes).
  Generated-qcow2 boot proof is now live: BigBoy XCP imported the qcow2 as
  throwaway `testvm-qc1-cloudinit`, reported the guest `running` with BIOS boot
  + Xen PV drivers detected, and a stock-preserving NoCloud seed provided only
  temporary SSH/static-IP access. Inside the booted image, `cloud-init-*`,
  `NetworkManager`, `openvswitch`, `ovsdb-server`, and `ovs-vswitchd` were
  active; `virsh version` reported libvirt 11.0.0 / QEMU 9.2.4;
  `sudo ovs-vsctl show` returned the OVS UUID and
  `ovs_version: "3.4.5-3.fc42"`; required RPMs were installed; and
  `/usr/bin/cloud-hypervisor` was absent. Plain `ovs-vsctl show` as the seeded
  non-root user is denied by Fedora's OVS socket permissions, so the runtime
  proof is root/sudo scoped. The throwaway VM/VDIs were destroyed after
  evidence capture. The QEMU virtio-gpu→egui fast-path acceptance is honestly
  filed as **QC-23**: current code has no QEMU/libvirt dmabuf importer or
  vhost-user-gpu producer to validate; the old zero-copy path is still
  cloud-hypervisor-shaped in `mde-kvm`, while QEMU/libvirt console access is
  currently SPICE/VNC/RDP fallback territory. Farm evidence: `.50
  qc1-ovs-focused` passed the focused regression; `.90 qc1-ovs-role-suite`
  passed 26 `role_provision` tests; `.130` built/statically verified
  `qc1-ovs-20260710` and produced the qcow2; `.130` + BigBoy dom0 booted and
  probed the generated qcow2; `.170 qc1-ovs-static` passed bootc script syntax,
  rustfmt, and whitespace checks.
- [✓] **QC-2: mackesd `openstack` worker.** *(done: ad1bef8 — workers/openstack/{mod,catalog,fleet,podman,reconcile,testkit}; 14 ServiceKinds (Q24 MVP + foundation trio); FleetStateSource + PodmanRunner seams (reuses MV-4 argv builders §6); pure plan_converge + converge_cycle on spawn_blocking 15s tick; mirror state/openstack/<node> publish-on-change + 60s heartbeat; killed-container restart unit-tested; honest IntegrationGates → QC-3 (kolla archives), QC-4 (doctrine record + config render); census rank-0 deliberate; mackesd 30/30+14/14, clippy 0.)*
  **As** the platform, **I want** a mackesd worker that renders Kolla config from fleet state and supervises the service containers, **so that** the cloud obeys the one-state doctrine with no deploy tool.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] worker reads etcd/Syncthing fleet state and materializes per-service Podman units + Kolla config on its node
    - [ ] per-service health published on the Bus; a killed container restarts; `[!]`-grade failures surface in chat
- [✓] **QC-3: Kolla image transport over Syncthing.** *(done: 734d41f — images.rs: archive layout `<share>/kolla/<release>/<basename>-<release>.tar` + SHA256SUMS; on absent image → check_archive → typed ArchiveStatus; ONLY Verified (sha256 matches) → podman load (600s); ChecksumMismatch/SumsUnavailable/ShareAbsent/ArchiveMissing/ReadFailed → sharper Gated reason (+alert on mismatch/IO), load NEVER called on unverified (unit-asserted); share root = existing workgroup_root; openstack 44/44, clippy 0. No registry pull — the airgap trust boundary.)*
  **As** an operator, **I want** service images distributed as archives on the mesh share and `podman load`ed locally, **so that** deploys need no registry and work offline.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] a pinned Kolla release lands on `/mnt/mesh-storage`; nodes load images from it; checksums verified; no `quay.io` pull at deploy time
- [✓] **QC-4: foundation services — DB, MQ, coordination.**
  **As** the platform, **I want** leader-hosted MariaDB, clustered RabbitMQ (quorum queues), tooz on the mesh etcd, and per-node memcached, **so that** the control plane has no permanently-special node.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] MariaDB rides the etcd leader and re-places on failover; APIs recover (design acceptance #3)
    - [ ] RabbitMQ cluster survives one node down; tooz locks work against the mesh etcd
- [✓] **QC-5: Keystone + the identity bridge.**
  **As** a mesh member, **I want** enrollment to provision my Keystone identity and nodes to mint tokens invisibly, **so that** the mesh account IS the cloud account (Q21/62/87).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] a newly-enrolled member exists in Keystone with no manual step; the Cloud plane opens with no login
    - [ ] the CA/KDC no longer holds human cloud credentials (machine certs only)
- [✓] **QC-6: core APIs on every node.**
  **As** the platform, **I want** Keystone/Glance/Nova/Placement/Neutron/Cinder API containers on every OpenStack-carrying node, bound plaintext to the Nebula interface, **so that** the control plane is distributed and overlay-secured (Q5/22/23).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `openstack endpoint list` resolves to a mesh name reaching any healthy node; APIs unreachable from non-Nebula interfaces
- [✓] **QC-7: Neutron/OVN flat mesh network.**
  **As** a member, **I want** instances on one flat provider network bridged into the mesh with default-open security groups and LAN floating IPs, **so that** an instance is "inside" and reachable from any peer with no per-instance certs (Q43/44/45/48/49).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] an instance is reachable from a different mesh peer with no floating IP; tenant MTU set for Geneve-over-Nebula; a LAN FIP pool exists per site
- [✓] **QC-8: Cinder LVM + backups.**
  **As** a member, **I want** volumes carved from each node's writable partition with cinder-backup to the object tiers, **so that** data survives instances (Q51/56/57/59).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] create/attach/detach/snapshot round-trip green; a backup lands in the object tier and restores
- [✓] **QC-9: Glance + DIB image pipeline.**
  **As** an operator, **I want** Glance (local store + replication) fed by a diskimage-builder pipeline, **so that** the golden-image script retires (Q36/53).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] DIB builds the platform's standard images into Glance; `build-mde-vm-golden.sh` deleted; images replicate across API nodes
- [✓] **QC-10: capacity-derived flavors + hard per-user quotas.**
  **As** the platform, **I want** flavors generated from real node shapes and quotas derived from real capacity divided into enforced per-user limits, **so that** guardrails are honest (Q29/39/89).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] flavor set regenerates when a node joins/leaves; exceeding a per-user quota is rejected by Keystone/Nova and surfaced in the UI
- [✓] **QC-11: typed verbs wrap the cloud.**
  **As** the platform, **I want** the mackesd Bus verbs (boot/stop/rebuild/volume/net/image) reimplemented over the OpenStack APIs, **so that** §9's contract holds and GUIs never speak raw OpenStack (Q40).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] every Cloud-plane action round-trips through a typed verb; verb-level integration tests (QC-16) exercise them
- [✓] **QC-12: the Cloud plane (Controller plane replaced).** *(done: f4c0fb3 — cloud_plane.rs (2875) renderer-only over state/openstack mirrors + QC-11 verbs; all 5 kinds; creation = stacks-as-code via audited heat-create; Q84 presets = fleet-state records; typed-armed destructive; honest "not configured" gates; 653 tests. MENU-1 7f6d8c3 folded: "State of the Mesh" bar.)*
  **As** a member, **I want** the Workbench Controller plane to become the Cloud plane — instances, volumes+snapshots, images, networks+stacks, full launch picker, per-user usage view, **so that** every member self-serves from the shell (Q70/82/83/85).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] full picker launches an instance end-to-end; all five resource kinds manageable; fleet-state template records render as launch presets (Q84)
- [>] **QC-13: instance access — console + keys.**
  **As** a member, **I want** a one-click SPICE console (mde-vdi-spice viewer) and automatic SSH key injection, **so that** getting into my instance is trivial (Q34/88).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] console opens from the Cloud plane row; a launched instance accepts the member's mesh-derived SSH key
  **QC-13 typed-access slice 2026-07-10:** the typed cloud verb seam now owns
  the key/console contract instead of leaving it as UI prose. `heat-create`
  launch templates generated by the Cloud plane include `key_name: mcnf-mesh`
  plus `mcnf:ssh-key-source: mesh-ssh-key`; the daemon preflights that keypair
  by publishing the existing mesh SSH public key
  (`/etc/ssh/mackes-mesh/mesh_authorized_keys`) as Nova keypair `mcnf-mesh`
  before Heat creates a keyed server. New typed verbs:
  `action/cloud/ensure-mesh-keypair` (audited mutation) and
  `action/cloud/get-instance-console` (audit-exempt read returning Nova's
  SPICE console descriptor). The Cloud plane instance row now exposes Console
  and Ensure SSH key actions and reports the returned descriptor honestly.
  Remaining for full close: live-prove the Nova console descriptor path against a
  launched instance and prove that instance accepts the injected mesh key. Farm
  evidence: `.50 qc13-openstack-verbs`
  `cargo test -p mackesd openstack::verbs --features async-services` passed
  39/39; `.90 qc13-cloud-plane`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui cloud_plane --features live-vdi` passed 24/24
  after fixing the unrelated Browser offline-cache test's `live-helper`-gated
  `gate_notice` access; `.170 qc13-rustfmt` farm-side rustfmt passed for the
  touched Rust files, and `.170 web-gate-fix-static` passed scoped `web.rs`
  rustfmt/whitespace.
  **QC-13 SPICE Desktop transport slice 2026-07-10:** the shell now links
  `mde-vdi-spice`, treats SPICE as a renderable VDI protocol, starts an off-UI
  live SPICE worker under `live-vdi`, maps optional sealed/guest SPICE tickets
  into `SpiceConfig`, pumps frames into the same Desktop texture upload path as
  RDP/VNC, forwards input, and publishes the same broker active/disconnect
  lifecycle. `BlockingSpiceTransport` now owns the `spice-client` event loop so
  the synchronous shell worker drives the same live message pump proven by the
  async crate test. The Chooser no longer shows the stale CHOOSER-5 gate for
  SPICE-only sources. Remaining for full close: live Nova SPICE render proof and
  live proof the launched instance accepts `mcnf-mesh`. Farm evidence: `.50 qc13-spice-crate`
  `cargo test -p mde-vdi-spice` passed 40/40 with the env-gated live SPICE
  proof ignored; `.90 qc13-spice-shell`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi` passed 22/22 with the
  env-gated live VNC proof ignored; `.90 qc13-spice-chooser`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui spice_picker --features live-vdi` passed 1/1.
  Workspace `cargo fmt --check` remains blocked
  by unrelated pre-existing formatting drift; after applying the touched-file
  rustfmt deltas, the farm fmt output no longer listed this slice's touched
  files.
  **QC-13 Cloud-row console attach slice 2026-07-10:** Cloud console replies now
  fold into the Desktop attach path when Nova returns a directly dialable native
  `spice://host:port` descriptor: the Cloud plane queues a one-shot
  `ConnectRequest`, the shell drains it after Workbench renders, switches to the
  Desktop surface, and starts the native SPICE VDI transport. The fold remains
  honest for Nova `--spice-html5` HTTP proxy URLs: those descriptors are displayed
  and gated because an HTML5 proxy page is not a raw SPICE socket. Remaining for
  full close: live-prove what the deployed Nova console verb returns, render a
  launched instance through the native path when the descriptor is direct (or add
  the proper proxy/websocket bridge if the live cloud only returns HTML5), and
  prove SSH login with `mcnf-mesh`. Farm evidence: `.90 qc13-console-attach`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui cloud_plane --features live-vdi` passed 27/27;
  `.50 qc13-console-parser`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui console_descriptor --features live-vdi` passed
  2/2. Local `git diff --check` passed for the touched shell files.
- [✓] **QC-14: VDI broker overlay on Nova.** *(done: adf754f — session_broker learns action/vdi/place: DesktopSpec→select_flavor (capacity m1.* ladder) + vdi_metadata + build_vdi_template (OS::Nova::Server HOT) → NovaPlacementRequest carrying the QC-11 heat-create/update HeatRequest (consumes the PUBLIC verb only, openstack worker untouched); placed desktop folds into the SAME SessionRequest::Open the chooser renders (mde-vdi-rdp/vnc, no new render code); roaming=rebuild via heat-update (Q38, session_roaming untouched); LiveNovaPlacement IntegrationGated until a live Nova+Heat cloud. 34 session_broker + 3382 mackesd lib tests, clippy 0, no bin/mackesd.rs change (leader-gated topic drain).)*
  **As** a desktop user, **I want** the session-broker to place VDI desktops as Nova instances (flavor+metadata) while owning the display path/roaming/seat binding, **so that** one VM plane serves both worlds (Q33).
  **Acceptance**:

  the old `mde-kvm`/cloud-hypervisor shell surfaces and Cockpit VM-console

  **Cockpit/cloud-hypervisor node-virt deletion slice 2026-07-10:** the live

  **Shell Instances/mde-kvm deletion slice 2026-07-10:** the retired

  **First-desktop onboarding cutover slice 2026-07-10:** `mackesd onboard
  first-desktop` no longer builds an `mde-kvm::VmSpec`, clones a local running

  `CloudDesktopSpec`, maps it to the session broker's `DesktopSpec` in

  `.170 qc15-firstdesktop-static` rustfmt passed for the touched onboarding Rust

  diff/whitespace audit because a full-file rustfmt would churn unrelated dirty
  imports.
  **Spawn-lighthouse local-CH deletion slice 2026-07-10:** the OW-7 daemon core,
  Bus worker, shell flow, and CLI are now cloud-only. `SpawnTarget::Local`,
  `ProvisionSpec::CloudHypervisor`, `LanOnlyReason::NoLocalVirt`, the

  rustfmt passed for the directly touched non-recursive implementation files.
  **Virtual-storage mde-kvm dependency deletion slice 2026-07-10:**
  `workers/virtual_storage.rs` now owns its generic `qemu-img` seam locally
  (`ImageFormat`, `ImageInfo`, `LiveQemuImg`, `QemuImgRunner`, and snapshot
  helpers), so `mackesd` no longer depends on the `mde-kvm` crate for image

  virtual_storage -- --nocapture` passed 22/22 without an explicit feature flag;
  `.170 qc15-vstorage-static` rustfmt and exact retired-symbol audit passed.
  **mde-kvm workspace crate deletion slice 2026-07-10:** after the daemon and
  shell no longer referenced the crate, `crates/services/mde-kvm` was removed
  from the workspace and deleted. `Cargo.lock` was scoped to remove the stale
  package/dependency edges; live crate comments that cited `mde-kvm` as a
  current pattern were retargeted to typed seams, Workstation role text now names
  libvirt/QEMU-KVM, and the old PipeWire `application.name=cloud-hypervisor`
  VM-audio fallback was removed so only the explicit `mde.vm.name` tag classifies
  a stream as a VM. Farm evidence: `.170 qc15-kvm-delete-static` passed workspace
  metadata plus exact retired-symbol audit; `.90 qc15-kvm-delete-mackesd` passed
  `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.50

  `cargo check -p mde-role`.
  **Cutover `/audit` proof surface slice 2026-07-10:** `mackesd
  cutover-audit` is now a real QC-15 audit verb. It inspects a repo root for the
  retired VM-stack artifacts (`crates/services/mde-kvm`, the deleted local
  Instances source, and `build-mde-vm-golden.sh`), verifies the node-virt recipe
  does not install old Cockpit/cloud-hypervisor packages or enable
  `cockpit.socket`, scans live source/manifests for exact old-stack symbols, and
  requires an operator VM-rebuild ledger at
  `/var/lib/mackesd/quasar-vm-rebuild-ledger.json` (or `--vm-rebuild-ledger`) to
  prove either no pre-cutover VMs existed or every legacy VM was rebuilt fresh as
  a Nova server/Heat stack without importing its old disk. The command emits JSON
  with `--json` and exits nonzero on any failed check, so QC-15's `/audit` line
  now has a repeatable gate instead of ad hoc grep. Farm evidence: `.90
  qc15-cutover-audit-test` `cargo test -p mackesd cutover_audit --features
  async-services -- --nocapture` passed 4/4; `.50 qc15-cutover-audit-check`
  `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --bin mackesd` passed; `.170
  qc15-cutover-audit-static` rustfmt passed for `cutover_audit.rs`/`lib.rs` and
  exact-symbol audit passed on the live manifests/source that should not contain
  retired implementation symbols.
  **QC-15 status roll-up 2026-07-10:** nine deletion/cutover slices are complete
  and farm-verified — golden-script deletion, Cockpit/cloud-hypervisor removal
  from `node-virt.yml`, shell Instances/`mde-kvm` deletion, first-desktop
  onboarding cutover to `CloudDesktopSpec`/`LiveNovaPlacement`, spawn-lighthouse
  local-CH deletion, `virtual_storage` mde-kvm-dependency deletion, the
  `crates/services/mde-kvm` workspace-crate deletion (workspace `Cargo.toml` +
  `Cargo.lock` both cleaned, zero dangling references outside gitignored paths),
  the `mackesd cutover-audit` verb itself, and `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` updated in
  lockstep to drop stale mde-kvm/cloud-hypervisor language. A future drainer
  should not redo any of this. **Genuinely still open:** the item's actual
  acceptance — a live `/audit` clean pass plus a VM-rebuild-ledger proof — has
  not run on a real cutover node. Next step: run `mackesd cutover-audit
  --vm-rebuild-ledger <path>` there. Marker moves `[ ]` → `[>]` to reflect the
  completed deletion work while that live audit remains outstanding.
- [>] **QC-16: verb-level CI on the farm dev cloud.**
  **As** the platform, **I want** Rust integration tests driving boot/net/volume round-trips through the verbs against a farm-VM dev cloud, **so that** the cloud is continuously proven (Q74/75).
  **Acceptance**:

  Progress (2026-07-10): added the first verb-level QC-16 CI contract in
  `openstack::verbs`: `qc16_dev_cloud_boot_net_volume_roundtrip_rides_typed_verbs`
  drives a disposable Heat stack template containing `OS::Nova::Server`,
  `OS::Neutron::Net`, and `OS::Cinder::Volume` through the public
  `action/cloud/*` responder, proves Heat detail/preview/update/delete,
  lists the booted Nova instance, and fetches its console through typed replies.
  This pins the boot/net/volume verb contract the live farm-dev-cloud GitOps lane
  must run; the live IaC bring-up remains open.

  qc16_dev_cloud --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 1/1; `.90
  qc16-openstack-verbs` `cargo test -p mackesd openstack::verbs --features
  async-services -- --nocapture` passed 40/40; `.170 qc16-devcloud-static`
  synced the same tree and ran scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check
  crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/openstack/verbs.rs --config skip_children=true`
  successfully.

- [>] **QC-18: object tiers + media re-platform.**
  **As** the workgroup, **I want** Swift as the hot object tier with DO Spaces off-site, and the media stack (Navidrome) re-platformed as instances, **so that** a real platform feature lives on the cloud (Q54/55/60).
  **Acceptance**:

  Progress (2026-07-10): made Swift first-class in the OpenStack service
  catalog/render path instead of leaving it as only the `swift.mesh` string in
  Cinder backup config. Added Kolla service entries for `swift_proxy_server`,
  `swift_account_server`, `swift_container_server`, and `swift_object_server`;
  the proxy advertises the Keystone-catalog endpoint `http://swift.mesh:8080`,
  and the three storage agents remain every-node non-API ring members. The
  config renderer now emits overlay-bound Swift proxy/storage configs plus the
  sealed `swift` service-user/hash secret; `cinder-backup` continues to target
  `http://swift.mesh:8080/v1/AUTH_`, now backed by a supervised service. This is
  not a live object-store claim yet: ring bootstrap/off-site DO Spaces
  replication and the Navidrome-as-Nova-instance proof remain open.
  Farm evidence: `.130 qc18-swift-render` `cargo test -p mackesd
  openstack::config_render --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed
  37/37; `.50 qc18-swift-catalog2` `cargo test -p mackesd openstack::catalog
  --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 10/10; `.90
  qc18-swift-secrets` `cargo test -p mackesd openstack::secrets --features
  async-services -- --nocapture` passed 8/8; `.170 qc18-swift-static` synced the
  same tree and ran scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` for
  `openstack/catalog.rs`, `openstack/config_render.rs`, and
  `openstack/secrets.rs` successfully. A prior `.90 qc18-swift-catalog` attempt
  used an unescaped shell `|` in the test filter and failed with code 127; it was
  discarded and replaced by the passing `.50`/`.90` gates above.
  Progress (2026-07-10, bootstrap slice): added the next QC-18 object-tier
  bootstrap contract. The leader now renders `bootstrap/swift-bootstrap.sh`
  beside the QC-10 cloud seed from the peer directory. The cloud seed runs that
  artifact when present; the artifact builds deterministic account/container/
  object rings with `swift-ring-builder`, copies the resulting rings into each
  Swift Kolla config directory, and idempotently creates the Keystone-auth
  `volumebackups` container that `cinder-backup` targets. This moves the hot
  Swift tier past "rendered service names" toward an applyable ring/container
  bootstrap, but it is still not a live object-store/off-site claim: DO Spaces
  replication and the Navidrome-as-Nova-instance proof remain open.
  Farm evidence: `.130 qc18-swift-bootstrap-render` `cargo test -p mackesd
  openstack::config_render --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed
  39/39; `.90 qc18-swift-bootstrap-worker2` `cargo test -p mackesd
  the_leader_renders_the_swift_object_tier_bootstrap --features async-services
  -- --nocapture` passed 1/1; `.170 qc18-swift-bootstrap-static2` synced the
  same tree and a direct farm-host scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check
  crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/openstack/config_render.rs
  crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/openstack/mod.rs --config
  skip_children=true` passed. A broad `.170 qc18-swift-bootstrap-static2`
  `cargo fmt --check` attempt was intentionally not counted because it checked
  unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice.
  Progress (2026-07-10, Navidrome Heat slice): added the first QC-18
  media-replatform contract for Navidrome as a Nova workload. The leader now
  renders `bootstrap/navidrome-stack.yaml` beside the cloud seed. The HOT
  declares one `OS::Nova::Server` on the flat mesh network, a Neutron security
  group exposing the Subsonic API on port 4533 to mesh addresses only, hidden
  Heat parameters for the existing DO Spaces/admin secrets, cloud-init that
  writes `/etc/mackesd/media-spaces.env`, and the packaged
  `/usr/libexec/mackesd/setup-media-navidrome` invocation using the pinned
  Navidrome `0.53.3` container image. The cloud seed creates the stack
  idempotently only when `/etc/mackesd/media-spaces.env` (or
  `MCNF_MEDIA_SPACES_ENV`) exists, writing a root-only temporary Heat
  environment file so S3/admin secrets are not placed directly on the
  `openstack` CLI argv. This is still a CI/render contract, not a live media VM
  proof: a real Nova boot, service reachability, and DO Spaces off-site Swift
  replication remain open.

- [>] **QC-22: SELinux tightening.**
  **As** the platform, **I want** the initially-permissive OpenStack domains moved to enforcing with the proper policy modules, **so that** the RH-conventions claim becomes fully true (Q14).
  **Progress (2026-07-10 bootstrap/static contract):** `install-helpers/setup-selinux-policy.sh` was restored from the stale global-disable helper to the Quasar-cloud enforcing posture: it persists `SELINUX=enforcing`, loads `magicmesh-base.cil` as required plus the Podman/libvirt subsystem modules best-effort, and only attempts `setenforce 1` after policy load on a currently Permissive boot. The RPM path remains safe: `%post` enables the bounded `magic-mesh-selinux-policy.service` oneshot instead of running `semodule` synchronously from `dnf`. Static checks now assert the helper contains no `SELINUX=disabled` or `setenforce 0` path and still ships the CIL modules through the mackesd manifest.
