- [>] **QC-23: QEMU virtio-gpu→egui fast path.**
  **As** a Workstation user, **I want** the local QEMU/libvirt display fast path
  to deliver the guest virtio-gpu framebuffer directly into the egui Desktop
  surface, **so that** QUASAR-CLOUD preserves the local low-latency VM desktop
  experience after retiring cloud-hypervisor.
  **Progress (2026-07-10 QEMU display contract):** Nova/libvirt config now makes
  the QEMU console path explicit instead of relying on defaults: `[libvirt]
  virt_type = kvm`, `[vnc] enabled = False`, and `[spice] enabled = True` with
  SPICE bound to this node's overlay IP for the `mde-vdi-spice` in-shell fallback.
  The DIB→Glance standard image upload now pins Nova-consumed libvirt metadata
  with `--property hw_video_model=virtio` and `--property hw_qemu_guest_agent=yes`,
  so launched MCNF images request the QEMU virtio video model needed by the future
  importer instead of silently landing on cirrus/qxl defaults. Farm evidence:
  `.170` slot `qc23-nova-spice-focused2` `cargo test -p mackesd
  nova_selects_libvirt_spice_and_disables_vnc_for_qemu_consoles --features
  async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `qc23-image-virtio-props2`
  `cargo test -p mackesd glance_create_pins_the_qemu_virtio_video_contract
  --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot
  `qc23-config-render2` `cargo test -p mackesd openstack::config_render
  --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 44/44; `.170` scoped
  `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` on the two touched OpenStack files passed.
  **Progress (2026-07-10 native SPICE descriptor slice):** the typed
  `get-instance-console` cloud verb now prefers a native libvirt/QEMU SPICE
  descriptor before falling back to Nova's `--spice-html5` proxy descriptor. The
  production seam runs bounded `virsh --connect qemu:///system domdisplay
  <instance> --type spice --include-password`, parses only direct `spice://...`
  output as protocol `spice`, and leaves non-SPICE/empty/native failures to the
  existing Nova HTML5 fallback instead of pretending an HTTP proxy URL is a native
  framebuffer. This closes the daemon half of the Cloud-plane attach path that
  already converts native `spice://host:port` descriptors into `VdiProtocol::Spice`
  Desktop attaches for `mde-vdi-spice`; it is still not a live framebuffer proof.
  Farm evidence: BigBoy `.130` slot `qc23-native-console-verbs` `cargo test -p
  mackesd openstack::verbs --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed
  42/42; `.90` slot `qc23-native-console-focused` `cargo test -p mackesd
  native_spice_display --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed 2/2;
  `.50` slot `qc23-native-console-check` `cargo check -p mackesd --features
  async-services --lib` passed; `.170` slot `qc23-native-console-fmt` scoped
  `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/openstack/verbs.rs`
  passed.
  **Progress (2026-07-10 SPICE live-proof harness slice):** the env-gated
  `mde-vdi-spice` live proof now records the QC-23 evidence shape the VNC/RDP
  proofs already use: it pumps a real SPICE primary surface into `SpiceSession`,
  prints `FRAME OK` with geometry, FNV-1a pixel checksum, and distinct-color
  count, settles to a baseline checksum, sends `Key::M` plus Enter through
  `SpiceTransport::flush_input`, then records either `INPUT ECHOED` with
  before/after checksums or an honest unchanged/no-repaint line. The parser for
  `MDE_SPICE_LIVE_TARGET=host:port[,ticket]` is now covered by a non-live test.
  This gives the live QEMU/SPICE acceptance a repeatable checksum/input-echo gate
  to run once a real libvirt guest is available; it is not itself a live run.
  Farm evidence: BigBoy `.130` slot `qc23-spice-liveproof-crate` `cargo test -p
  mde-vdi-spice -- --nocapture` passed 41 runnable tests with the live SPICE
  proof ignored; `.90` slot `qc23-spice-liveproof-test` `cargo test -p
  mde-vdi-spice --test live_spice -- --nocapture` passed the parser helper and
  compiled the ignored live proof; `.50` slot `qc23-spice-liveproof-focused2`
  passed the current-tree focused parser helper; `.170` slot
  `qc23-spice-liveproof-fmt2` scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check
  crates/desktop/mde-vdi-spice/tests/live_spice.rs` passed.
  **Progress (2026-07-10 shell SPICE live-proof harness slice):** the shell
  `live-vdi` tests now have an env-gated
  `live_spice_worker_renders_real_console_and_accepts_input` proof for
  `MDE_SPICE_LIVE_TARGET=host:port[,ticket]`. The proof drives
  `VdiState::request_connect` through the normal `VdiProtocol::Spice` attach
  path, starts the `LiveSpiceHandle`, polls `state.poll_live_spice`, drains the
  imported `state.incoming` frame that feeds the Desktop texture path, prints
  `live-shell-spice: FRAME OK` with geometry and FNV-1a checksum, then sends
  `Key::M` plus Enter through the shell live handle and records either
  `INPUT ECHOED` with before/after checksums or an honest unchanged-frame line.
  The shell parser for `MDE_SPICE_LIVE_TARGET=host:port[,ticket]` is covered by
  a non-live test.
  Farm evidence: BigBoy `.130` slot `qc23-shell-spice-liveproof-vdi` current-tree
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi -- --nocapture` passed
  23/23 runnable tests with the live VNC/SPICE proofs ignored; `.90` slot
  `qc23-shell-spice-liveproof-focused` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui
  live_spice_target_parser --features live-vdi -- --nocapture` passed 1/1;
  `.170` slot `qc23-shell-spice-liveproof-fmt2` scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021
  --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/vdi.rs` passed.
  **Progress (2026-07-10 shell SPICE fallback/input proof):** the shell now has a
  non-live `input_forwards_to_a_spice_session_and_esc_returns_to_chrome` proof
  matching the existing VNC fallback assertion. A synthetic `SpiceSession` is
  mounted through the Desktop surface, the headless egui panel forwards pointer
  and `Key::M` input into `SpiceSession::pending_input`, and the reserved Esc
  chord raises `return_to_chrome` without leaking the Esc scancode to the guest.
  This does not replace the live QEMU/libvirt framebuffer proof, but it does
  tighten the fallback contract: RDP/VNC/SPICE all stay real shell-mounted
  decoder/input paths, not placeholder protocols.
  Farm evidence: BigBoy `.130` slot `qc23-shell-spice-input-vdi` current-tree
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui vdi --features live-vdi -- --nocapture` passed
  24/24 runnable tests with the live VNC/SPICE proofs ignored; `.170` slot
  `qc23-shell-spice-input-focused2` current-tree `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui
  input_forwards_to_a_spice_session_and_esc_returns_to_chrome --features live-vdi
  -- --nocapture` passed 1/1; `.50` slot `qc23-shell-spice-input-crate` `cargo
  test -p mde-vdi-spice input -- --nocapture` passed 15/15 input mapper tests
  with the live SPICE proof compiled and ignored; `.170` slot
  `qc23-shell-spice-input-fmt2` scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check
  crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/vdi.rs` passed.
  **Still open:** this is not the live dmabuf/virgl/venus importer, and — per
  the design doc below — none of the SPICE work in the Progress notes above
  advances it: SPICE is a wire protocol and cannot become zero-copy by
  definition, and it answers a different question (`quasar-cloud.md` Q34, the
  *remote*/Nova-brokered console) than this unit's own "As a Workstation
  user" acceptance criteria, which target the *local* same-host VM case
  (`quasar-vdi-desktop.md` lock 12). The QC-23 acceptance still needs a real
  libvirt/QEMU VM producing framebuffer evidence, shell import, input echo,
  and fallback proof — none of that has moved.
  **Design doc landed 2026-07-10:**
  `docs/design/qc23-virtio-gpu-zerocopy-rescope.md` re-scopes the remainder
  against the live libvirt/QEMU-KVM stack and this crate's actual DRM code
  (design/options only, nothing implemented). **Current-path cost, made
  concrete:** tracing a real SPICE frame through `mde-vdi-spice` finds ≥3
  full-framebuffer CPU copies (decode → `Framebuffer` → `ColorImage` → GPU
  upload), a hard 50ms/~20Hz poll ceiling in
  `BlockingSpiceTransport::pump_frame`, and whole-surface (not damage-rect)
  updates per the crate's own doc comments. **Correction:** this project's
  production DRM renderer is verified to be `egui_glow`/EGL/GLES2
  (`mde-egui/src/drm.rs`), **not wgpu** — lock 12's "dmabuf → wgpu texture"
  wording (and its own R1 risk) predate or were never reconciled with that
  shipped choice; a real design targets EGL/GLES external-texture APIs
  instead. **The actual bottleneck:** sourcing a dmabuf handle out of QEMU's
  process at all (a vhost-user-gpu backend, or QEMU's `-display
  dbus,gl=on` + a new D-Bus client) — both substantial new infrastructure;
  the natural pure-Rust building block (`vhost-device-gpu`) does not yet
  support dmabuf display sharing upstream. Once a handle exists, shell-side
  import is cheaper than this entry's "existing Desktop texture path"
  wording implies: a KMS-plane import (`prime_fd_to_buffer` +
  `add_planar_framebuffer` + `set_plane`, all already in the pinned `drm`
  0.14 crate, reusing MEDIA-2's exact `PlaneSet`/`VideoScanout` seam) needs
  zero new dependencies, versus a GL-texture import needing new unsafe
  EGL/GLES FFI, an EGL 1.4→1.5 bump, and possibly a shader fork.
  **Testability:** unlike the E12-10 VFIO finding, virgl/venus need no
  IOMMU/dedicated-device passthrough — but the farm's build VMs still cannot
  run any part of the DRM shell at all (the pre-existing `drm`-feature
  constraint), and no physical seat has a recorded
  libvirt/QEMU-plus-accelerated-render-node setup to test against — an
  unconfirmed combination, not a confirmed absence. **Recommendation:** no
  full first slice exists for the live importer itself (the
  delivery-mechanism choice is a real infrastructure decision, not made
  here); two small, decoupled pieces of real progress do: (1)
  `accel3d='yes'` on the virtio-gpu video model in both
  `vm_lifecycle.rs`/`compute_provision.rs` domain builders — cheap, works
  with the existing SPICE graphics stanza unchanged; (2) a self-contained
  PRIME-import liveness check (round-trip a locally-allocated GBM buffer
  through the same fd-import primitives, no QEMU involved), mirroring how
  MEDIA-2's `probe_primary_video_plane` proves its plane path with a
  harmless `clear()`, not a real frame. Also flags (not fixed here):
  `docs/WORKLIST.md` **E12-7** is a zombie entry describing the
  QC-15-deleted `mde-kvm`/cloud-hypervisor stack and should be marked
  superseded-by-QC-23 in a future reconciliation pass.
  **Progress (2026-07-10 accel3d + PRIME-import liveness slice):**
  implements the design doc's two Tier 0/Tier 1 recommendations above —
  both real, both still decoupled from the blocked importer. **(1) Tier 0
  `accel3d='yes'`:** `vm_lifecycle.rs::build_domain_xml`'s `<video>` stanza
  now nests `<model type='virtio'><acceleration accel3d='yes'/></model>`,
  unconditionally, alongside the unchanged `<graphics type='spice'>`
  stanza. `compute_provision.rs::build_virt_install_args` — which per §1.4
  had **no `--video` flag at all** — now always adds `--video
  model.type=virtio,model.acceleration.accel3d=yes`; that exact suboption
  spelling was verified (not guessed) against the real `virt-install`
  binary present in this dev environment via `virt-install --video=?`
  (confirms `model.acceleration.accel3d` is a real suboption path) and a
  `--dry-run --print-xml` probe, which emitted byte-identical
  `<video><model type="virtio"><acceleration accel3d="yes"/></model></video>`
  XML to the domain-XML side, then was discarded (no domain left defined).
  **(2) Tier 1 PRIME-import liveness:** `mde-egui/src/drm.rs` adds
  `probe_prime_import_liveness` (mirrors `probe_primary_video_plane`'s own
  shape/posture exactly): allocates a 64×64 scanout-capable GBM buffer,
  exports its dmabuf fd via `drm::control::Device::buffer_to_prime_fd`,
  re-imports that fd as a fresh GEM handle via `prime_fd_to_buffer` — the
  same call a real QEMU-delivered dmabuf fd would go through — wraps it in
  a new `ReimportedGemBuffer` (a small `drm::buffer::PlanarBuffer` impl;
  `prime_fd_to_buffer` returns a bare handle with no size/format/pitch of
  its own, unlike a live `gbm::BufferObject`, so the metadata a real
  handoff would carry out-of-band is supplied from the original local
  allocation here), and builds + immediately tears down a KMS framebuffer
  via `add_planar_framebuffer`. Never touches a plane/CRTC — plane
  selection is already MEDIA-2's job; this exercises only the import
  primitives Option B (design doc §3.5) still needed exercising. Per
  `AI_GOVERNANCE.md` §7, wired to a real caller: re-exported from
  `mde-egui`'s crate root behind `feature = "drm"` and driven by a new
  `hello_prime_import` example (the `hello_video_plane` mold). **Both
  pieces matched the design doc's own size estimate — neither grew beyond
  "small, decoupled, real progress"; no new crate dependencies were added.**
  Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd --lib vm_lifecycle` passed
  44/44 (incl. new `domain_xml_includes_virtio_gpu_accel3d`); `.90` `cargo
  test -p mackesd --lib compute_provision` passed 32/32 (incl. new
  `virt_install_args_always_include_accel3d_video`); `.90` `cargo test -p
  mde-egui --lib --features drm` passed 136/136 (incl. new
  `reimported_gem_buffer_maps_a_single_plane`,
  `explicit_modifier_filters_the_invalid_sentinel`,
  `prime_import_liveness_degrades_cleanly` — a `cargo clean -p mde-egui`
  was needed first to clear a stale rlib left by an earlier session sharing
  this build slot, the same class of issue as the project's known
  stale-rlib gotcha); `.90` `cargo build -p mde-egui --example
  hello_prime_import --features drm` compiled, and run directly on that
  (headless) build VM printed the same honest `no usable DRM master:
  /dev/dri/card0: Permission denied` fallback as the existing
  `hello_video_plane`/`hello_drm` examples, byte-for-byte the same failure
  mode — the farm can compile this path, never live-run it (unchanged
  constraint). Local `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check`
  passed on all five touched files. `cargo clippy` on both crates showed
  zero new warnings anywhere in the touched line ranges (checked by exact
  line-number grep against each diff — the only warnings present are
  pre-existing, in code neither piece touched).
  **Still blocked, unchanged by this slice:** getting a dmabuf handle out
  of QEMU's process at all (§3.3) — neither piece touches delivery; SPICE
  is still the only working console path, `accel3d` only speeds up guest
  rendering SPICE then re-encodes, and the liveness check proves the
  shell-side import primitives work on real hardware without ever
  producing or consuming a QEMU-sourced handle. The Acceptance list below
  is unchanged — nothing here checks off a box.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] a libvirt/QEMU VM is launched with the selected local display backend
      (virtio-gpu/virgl/venus or the chosen replacement) and produces a
      framebuffer without relying on RDP/VNC
    - [ ] `mde-shell-egui` imports that framebuffer into the existing Desktop
      texture path (or a KMS plane-scanout path — see the design doc) and
      forwards input
    - [ ] the same live proof records frame checksum/pixel evidence plus input
      echo, comparable to the existing VNC/RDP live proofs
    - [ ] RDP/VNC/SPICE remain honest fallbacks when the fast path is unavailable
  **Blocker filed from QC-1 (2026-07-10):** Q32 moved the image and local VMM
  target to libvirt/QEMU-KVM, but the existing zero-copy code is not
  revalidatable as-is: `mde-kvm` still models the fast path as a
  cloud-hypervisor vhost-user-gpu socket (`VmSpec::virtio_gpu` →
  `build_ch_config` `gpu`), and repo search found no QEMU/libvirt dmabuf
  importer or shell-side QEMU vhost-user-gpu producer. QC-1 therefore closes on
  the image/host-virt foundation plus this explicit blocker; QC-23 owns the
  real QEMU display implementation and live validation.


## SETTINGS — world-class Settings redesign: master-detail, tinted, Carbon (operator 2026-07-04; 5-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/settings-redesign.md)

Locks: master-detail rail + full-width detail pane; categorical accent tint + Carbon elevation layers; grouped by domain (Devices / Personalization / Mesh & System); Carbon expressive density; rail nav only (no search). Replaces the single-column stack in `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/system.rs`. Reuse every existing section body + its `apply()`/`SysAction` seam verbatim (§6) — a presentation/routing pass, not a rewrite.

- [✓] **SETTINGS-1: the master-detail shell.** **As** a user, **I want** Settings to use the whole page, **so that** it stops being a cramped column. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): a left `SidePanel` rail lists the 3 domain groups (Devices / Personalization / Mesh & System) each with its section rows; a right detail pane fills the remaining width and renders ONLY the selected section's body (reusing the existing per-section fns); the active section is highlighted; every existing section (Mixer/Bluetooth/Displays/Power/Wallpaper/Hotkeys) is reachable exactly once and its actions still dispatch; a `SettingsNav {group,section}` selection persists in the shell config across surface switches + restart; Carbon expressive spacing; tested rail render + section route + all-sections-reachable + nav persistence round-trip. *(touches system.rs + main.rs Surface::System arm; serialize vs the browser live-helper main.rs edit)*
- [✓] **SETTINGS-2: categorical accent + Carbon layer elevation.** **As** a user, **I want** areas visually separated by color, **so that** I read structure at a glance. **Acceptance**: three domain accent tokens REUSED from the existing `Style::ACCENT_*` set (coordinated with PICKER-2 + EXPLORER-15 — one shared token language, defined once, §4); rail group headers + the active detail header take their group accent; page = layer-01, cards = layer-02 with hairline borders; NO raw hex / scattered metric literals (grep-clean); tested accent-per-group application + the layer/border rendering. *(mde-egui::Style/mde-theme token use + system.rs)*
- [✓] **SETTINGS-3: expressive detail layouts that use the width.** **As** a user, **I want** wide sections laid across, **so that** the space isn't wasted. **Acceptance**: each section body reflows to the wide pane — Displays lays outputs in a row of cards, Bluetooth puts adapters + devices in columns, Mixer spreads channels across, Power shows battery + profiles side by side; the section LOGIC (`apply()`/`SysAction`) is unchanged (§6/§7 — presentation only); tested each section renders its controls in the wide layout + the actions still fire. *(system.rs section bodies)*
- [✓] **SETTINGS-4: the Mesh & System group.** **As** an operator, **I want** identity/role/pairing/network in Settings, **so that** the node's mesh facts are one place. **Acceptance**: the Mesh & System group surfaces Identity (mesh cert/name), Role (the pinned role), Pairing (folds in the existing `sync_pairing_agent` KDC pairing flow), and Network (overlay/underlay facts) from REAL node state, honest-`unknown` where unprobed (§7); Pairing drives the existing agent; tested each section renders live data + the pairing action dispatches. *(system.rs + reuse the pairing/role/mesh seams the shell already reads)*
- [✓] **SETTINGS-5: the Personalization Theme section.** **As** a user, **I want** an appearance section, **so that** I can tune the look. **Acceptance**: a new Theme section under Personalization exposing accent choice + text-scale (reusing the EXPLORER-18 accessibility posture) + any light/dark the shell supports, all honest to what the runtime actually applies (no dead toggles, §7); persists in shell config; tested the controls apply + persist. *(system.rs + Style/mde-theme)* *(done: new `theme_section` under Personalization — **accent choice** re-tints the live interactive `Style` accent across every surface via a new `mde_egui::Style::set_accent` (7 curated choices, each an existing `ACCENT*` token, no new hex); **text-scale** = the EXPLORER-18 whole-UI zoom in 5 steps (0.9–1.5×), applied as `zoom_factor` atop the seat's DPI base so it composes with hi-DPI, not clobbers it. NO light/dark switch (platform is dark-only — no dead toggle, §7). Both apply GLOBALLY every frame from `SystemState::poll` (both runners, restored on start) + persist as `settings-appearance.json` (the SettingsNav idiom). mde-shell-egui 500 + mde-egui 116 tests green (7 new SETTINGS-5 + refactored accent test), clippy clean on the touched files, style-leak + tier lints clean.)*
- [✓] **SETTINGS-6: live smoke on the seat.** **As** the operator, **I want** the redesign verified on a real seat, **so that** it's truly done. **Acceptance**: on Eagle / the test bed the redesigned Settings renders on the DRM seat — rail + wide detail, accent tint + layers visible, every section reachable, a real action fires (Bluetooth toggle or display mode change); captured/confirmed. *(runtime verify; §7 live-verify)* *(done: SEAT-2 live on .2 — state/host/local/seat reads live vol/muted/bluetooth/displays/battery matching hardware; actions route via the bus.)*


## NODE-GRADE — per-node A–F capability grade in the vertical dock (operator 2026-07-04; 20-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/node-grade.md)

Locks: a stacked A–F grade per mesh node above the left dock's system row and visually adjacent to the bottom notification rail; green→red; blink at D/F; four weighted factors (resources heaviest); classic 90/80/70/60 bands; each node self-grades + publishes; smoothed ~15–30s; blend health+headroom; local pinned first, peers worst-first; trend arrow; tap→Explorer hero; worst-N overflow; notify feed on drop to D/F.

- [✓] **NODE-GRADE-1: the node self-grade worker (mackesd).** **As** an operator, **I want** each node to grade its own capability, **so that** the fleet's health is legible at a glance. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): a rank-0 `node_grade` worker computes four 0–100 factor scores (CPU load/headroom, RAM+disk free, role/worker health + no failed services, mesh reachability), each blending health + spare headroom (#17); a weighted average (resources heaviest, #13) → an A–F band (90/80/70/60), smoothed ~15–30s, plus a recent-slope trend; publishes `{grade,score,factors,trend}` to `<root>/node-grade/<hostname>.json` on the substrate (SEC-5 mesh-shunt pattern); on a transition into D/F emits a debounced `event/notify/*` alert (CHAT-FIX-2). Reuses the existing telemetry (§6, no new probes). Census `("node_grade",0)`; tested: factor scoring, band mapping, smoothing/trend, the drop-to-D/F alert, honest-absent.
- [✓] **NODE-GRADE-2: the dock grade mini-list (mde-shell-egui).** **As** a user, **I want** the grades near the notification rail, **so that** a struggling node is obvious. **Acceptance:** `dock.rs` renders a stacked grade list above the left dock's system row and clear of the full-width bottom notification rail — the local node pinned first with a "you are here" marker (#18), peers worst-grade-first (#19); each row = the A–F letter in the green→red `Style` token (#4) + a tiny load bar (#5) + a ↑/→/↓ trend arrow (#14); D/F blinks hard on/off (always, #6/#16); tapping a node routes to its Explorer hero (#7); overflow shows worst-N + a "…" popup (#8); no header (#15); honest dim pre-poll. Reads the published grades via the dock's status-input seam (extend `set_status_inputs`). All `Style`/`Motion` tokens (§4). Tested: layout above the bottom rail/system row, sort/pin, blink at D/F, tap-route, overflow. *(serialize on dock.rs vs the VDOCK churn — land on the settled vertical-dock base)*
- [✓] **NODE-GRADE-3: the A–F color-ramp tokens (mde-egui Style).** **As** the shell, **I want** one grade palette, **so that** every grade UI is consistent. **Acceptance:** `GRADE_A..GRADE_F` green→red tokens in `mde-egui::Style` (reuse the status palette where it already covers the ramp; add only what's missing), no raw hex (§4), with a backing `mde-egui` test; consumed by NODE-GRADE-2. *(shared-kit; coordinate with the status/accent palette)* *(done: 6b7ccce — GRADE_A..F resolve onto existing OK/ACCENT/WARN/DANGER tokens (no raw hex) + GradeBand + Style::grade_fill, 6 mde-egui tests; NODE-GRADE-2 dock already consumes them.)*
- [>] **NODE-GRADE-4: live smoke on the test bed.** **As** the operator, **I want** the grades verified live, **so that** it's truly done. **Acceptance:** on Eagle/the test bed the dock shows real per-node grades from live telemetry, a stressed node's grade drops + blinks + posts a feed notification, tapping opens the Explorer hero. *(runtime verify; §7 live-verify)* *(SEAT-2 2026-07-05: honest-gated on .2 — the grade band folds from netdata (absent/first-boot-pending on .2) + needs mesh peers; band honestly empty pre-poll by design. Re-verify after netdata up + .2 enrolled.)*


## BROWSER-DD — the daily-driver browser (operator 2026-07-04; 100-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/browser-daily-driver.md)

A dual-engine (keep sandboxed Servo + ADD CEF/Chromium for compat) mesh-native, privacy-forward, Quasar-styled browser. Phased; Phase-1 pillars first. Reuses the BOOKMARKS-5/6 offscreen-render-into-egui helper architecture. Each task's full spec is in the design doc.

### Phase 1 — the daily-driver MVP
- [✓] **BROWSER-DD-0: BUGFIX — the browser never rendered any page (`run_tab` socket-protocol stub).** **As** a user, **I want** pages to actually render instead of hanging on "Loading the page…", **so that** the browser works at all. **Root cause:** `mde-web-preview`'s `tab` serve loop (`main.rs::run_tab`) was a STUB — it created the shm `FrameChannel`, `println!`'d `SHM_FD` to stdout, and looped `pump_step`+sleep with ZERO socket I/O, so it never sent `AttachFrame`/`PaintReady` over the session socket. The client (`WebSession`) only reaches `Live` (clears the "Loading…" note) on a `PaintReady` after an `AttachFrame` maps the shm fd, so EVERY url hung — not google-specific; the interactive browser had never worked. **Fix (landed):** (1) `run_tab` now speaks the BOOKMARKS-6 protocol in the confined child — wraps the session socket (the shell hands it as stdin/fd 0), sends `EventMsg::AttachFrame` + the shm fd via `SCM_RIGHTS`, then loops applying `ControlMsg` nav (Load/Reload/Back/Forward → the engine's methods; Resize/Input/verdict decoded-but-not-yet-wired), pumping a step, and signalling `PaintReady{seq}` on any delivered frame; a ~750ms first-frame watchdog force-emits so a slow/heavy SPA never hangs the shell — delivery is keyed on a delivered frame, NEVER on `LoadStatus::Complete`. (2) new `Engine::force_emit` (the watchdog path: paint+present+read-back+emit without a fresh frame-ready). (3) client Layer-A shm fallback in `WebSession::poll` — promotes to Live straight from the seqlock sequence, self-healing a `PaintReady` that arrived before `AttachFrame`. **Protocol sharing:** the excluded helper can't depend on the client, so it `#[path]`-includes the client's ONE `wire.rs` (single byte-exact source) + a minimal local `sock.rs` (SCM mechanism only); pinned by golden byte-compat tests on both ends. Tests: client Layer-A + early-PaintReady self-heal + golden (green, 40/40); helper wire golden + `sock` round-trips; `render-once` prints `FRAME_OK … distinct= mean_luma=` + `FORCE_OK` (force_emit live-exercise, separate process since Servo is one-instance-per-process). *(the release/RPM story unchanged: [[rpm-cut-needs-servo-build]] — the excluded helper still builds via `--manifest-path`)*
- [✓] **BROWSER-DD-1: CEF engine integration.** **As** a user, **I want** a real Chromium engine, **so that** every site works. **Acceptance:** vendor prebuilt CEF binaries on the farm (workspace-EXCLUDED like mde-web-preview); a new `mde-web-cef` helper renders offscreen into the BOOKMARKS-6 shm/texture seam; the `mde-web-preview-client` live-helper spawn launches either engine (Servo|CEF, user-selectable); a real site (e.g. Gmail) renders. *(heavy; the release/RPM story like [[rpm-cut-needs-servo-build]])* **Started 2026-07-07:** added workspace-excluded `crates/desktop/mde-web-cef` as the Chromium helper lane. It is intentionally an honest-gated scaffold until the farm has a pinned CEF bundle: binary modes `probe`/`render-once`/`tab`/`warm`, runtime discovery via `MDE_CEF_ROOT` or `/opt/mde/cef`, typed `CEF_MISSING` status when `libcef.so` is absent, and the same BOOKMARKS-6 `wire.rs` included from `mde-web-preview-client` so the Chromium helper cannot drift from the Servo/shell protocol. Farm verification: `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` on .50 passed **12/12** (11 lib/wire tests + 1 protocol golden). **Shell selection slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser Engine menu now has real Servo/CEF selection rows instead of captions; the selected engine is the helper used for future live-helper tabs, each tab records the engine that spawned it, the status chip reads the active tab's actual engine, and CEF launch is honestly gated before spawn unless both `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef` (or `MDE_WEB_CEF_BIN`) and the pinned CEF runtime (`MDE_CEF_ROOT` or `/opt/mde/cef/libcef.so`) exist. Existing Servo tabs are not relabeled when the default changes; CEF remains a named missing-runtime gate until the bundle is vendored. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-shell ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui web:: -- --nocapture` passed **48/48**; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-live-helper ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **54/54**, including selected-engine menu/state, helper path overrides, live open, absent helper, no-seat, spawn-failure, and CEF missing-runtime regressions. **Packaging slice 2026-07-07:** the full Workstation RPM now ships the CEF helper scaffold at `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef` alongside `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview`, while the `magic-mesh-server` headless variant ships neither browser helper. `install-helpers/build-rpm-fedora43.sh` now builds the workspace-excluded `mde-web-cef` helper into the active release target before `cargo generate-rpm`, so the asset is present for full RPM cuts without pulling CEF into the main workspace lock. Farm verification: `bash -n install-helpers/build-rpm-fedora43.sh` passed; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-pkg ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **17/17**, including the new full-vs-server helper asset assertion and excluded-helper build-script assertion; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **12/12**. **CEF runtime pin/install slice 2026-07-07:** added `packaging/browser/cef-linux64-minimal.env`, pinning the official CEF Linux64 minimal stable tarball `cef_binary_149.0.6+g0d0eeb6+chromium-149.0.7827.201_linux64_minimal.tar.bz2` (Chromium `149.0.7827.201`, 310966119 bytes, SHA-256 `f90dec4c5c42a7bbd4f2bd80a7a77e0ac6aacfc6627bb43572d803e77f26dfbc`) from `cef-builds.spotifycdn.com`. Added `install-helpers/install-cef-runtime.sh`, an idempotent installer that downloads/verifies the archive, extracts it under `/opt/mde/cef-runtimes/<version>-linux64-minimal`, writes `mde-cef-runtime.manifest`, and activates `/opt/mde/cef`; the shell and helper runtime gates now accept the real upstream layout `Release/libcef.so` while preserving the flat test override. Verification: `bash -n install-helpers/install-cef-runtime.sh` passed; a local fake-archive smoke proved SHA-256 verify -> extract -> symlink activation; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **15/15** including manifest/installer/layout regressions; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-live-helper ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **55/55** including `cef_runtime_gate_accepts_the_upstream_bundle_layout`. **RPM provisioning slice 2026-07-07:** the full RPM now ships the CEF runtime installer at `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`, the pin manifest at `/usr/share/magic-mesh/browser/cef-linux64-minimal.env`, and a deferred `mde-cef-runtime-setup.service` that waits for network, requires `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, skips once `/opt/mde/cef/Release/libcef.so` exists, and role-gates through `mackesd role-gate --min-rank 1` so headless roles do not fetch the browser runtime. The server variant ships none of those CEF runtime provisioning assets. Verification: `bash -n install-helpers/install-cef-runtime.sh` passed; `systemd-analyze verify packaging/systemd/mde-cef-runtime-setup.service` parsed the unit and only warned that installed commands are absent on the dev host; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-pkg ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **18/18**, including the full-vs-server CEF runtime provisioning assertion; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **15/15**. **Farm runtime fetch proof 2026-07-07:** on farm node `.50`, the installer fetched the real pinned CEF tarball, verified SHA-256 `f90dec4c5c42a7bbd4f2bd80a7a77e0ac6aacfc6627bb43572d803e77f26dfbc`, extracted `Release/libcef.so`, activated `.cef-active`, and `mde-web-cef probe` returned `CEF_OK` against that runtime. The archive cache measured `297M`; the extracted runtime measured `1.5G`. The first farm extraction attempt exposed a missing Fedora-minimal prerequisite (`bzip2`); the full RPM now hard-requires `bzip2`, and `install-cef-runtime.sh` fails early with `need_cmd bzip2` outside the RPM contract. **Spaces mirror slice 2026-07-07:** streamed the verified farm cache into DO Spaces at `mcnf-spaces:mcnf-mesh-media/browser/cef/cef_binary_149.0.6+g0d0eeb6+chromium-149.0.7827.201_linux64_minimal.tar.bz2`; `rclone lsl` reports `310966119` bytes and `rclone cat ... | sha256sum` returns the pinned `f90dec4c5c42a7bbd4f2bd80a7a77e0ac6aacfc6627bb43572d803e77f26dfbc`. Added `CEF_SPACES_REMOTE` to the pin manifest and `install-helpers/mirror-cef-runtime-to-spaces.sh` as a repeatable rclone mirror helper; its dry-run is non-mutating and `--push` verifies the remote stream hash after upload. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **16/16** including the mirror helper guard. **Renderer launch-contract slice 2026-07-07:** `mde-web-cef` now validates the full CEF runtime layout (`Release/libcef.so` plus `Resources/icudtl.dat` and `Resources/resources.pak`), builds a `CEF_LAUNCH` plan carrying the release dir/resources dir/`LD_LIBRARY_PATH`, and gates render/tab/warm modes on a named native bridge binary (`MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_BIN` or `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`) with `CEF_RENDERER_MISSING` instead of a generic scaffold message. The shell CEF gate now also requires the resources files, so a partial runtime cannot reach a later helper failure. Farm proof uses out-of-tree cache paths (`~/mde-cef-cache`, `~/mde-cef-runtimes`, `~/mde-cef-active`) because `xcp-build sync --delete` intentionally wipes untracked files inside the synced repo; against that runtime, `render-once` prints `CEF_OK`, `CEF_LAUNCH ... resources=... ld_library_path=...`, then exits 78 with `CEF_RENDERER_MISSING bridge=/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **18/18** including runtime-resource and launch-plan regressions; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-live-helper ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **55/55**. **Chrome bridge handoff slice 2026-07-07:** added the packaged `mde-web-cef-renderer` bridge binary, shipped by the full Workstation RPM at `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer` and omitted from the headless server variant. `mde-web-cef` now spawns that bridge for `render-once`/`tab`/`warm`, passes the validated CEF root/lib/release/resources env contract, prepends the CEF `Release/` dir to `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`, and propagates the bridge exit code. The bridge validates the runtime contract and exits with typed `CEF_OFFSCREEN_PENDING` until the callback/render loop lands, so the missing-binary blocker is removed without faking rendered pages. Role provisioning now classifies `mde-cef-runtime-setup.service` as Workstation-rank so Lighthouse nodes mask it. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **20/20** including the process-level handoff regression; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-pkg ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **18/18**. **CEF shm frame-sink slice 2026-07-07:** added the Chromium bridge's helper-side `MWP1` shared-memory writer, `SCM_RIGHTS` socket transport, and `OffscreenFrameSink` that attaches the frame fd once, publishes BGRA/RGBA offscreen buffers, and emits `PaintReady` with the stable seqlock sequence. The crate now carries its own `Cargo.lock` because the workspace-excluded CEF helper has a real native syscall dependency (`nix`) and the RPM path builds it with `--locked`. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **28/28** including shm/socket/offscreen-sink regressions; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-release ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo build --release --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml` passed. **CEF ABI probe slice 2026-07-07:** added a dynamic `libcef.so` ABI loader that resolves the metadata and lifecycle symbols required by the native loop (`cef_api_hash`, `cef_api_version`, `cef_version_info`, `cef_execute_process`, `cef_initialize`, `cef_get_exit_code`, message-loop functions, and `cef_shutdown`). `mde-web-cef-renderer` now loads the real runtime library and prints `CEF_ABI_OK` with CEF/Chromium versions, platform API hash, and commit hash before the remaining offscreen gate; missing libraries or missing symbols fail as typed `CEF_ABI_MISSING` exits. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **31/31**; a real farm runtime probe on `.50` loaded `/home/mm/mde-cef-active/Release/libcef.so` and printed `CEF_ABI_OK api=999999 cef=149.0.6 chrome=149.7827.201 platform_hash=bb387d66714b12d14cf27f2a6b50cb785166b83e commit=0d0eeb61160536e447c79335c1ee963f57eb6d60`, then exited 78 at `CEF_OFFSCREEN_PENDING`. **CEF init-layout slice 2026-07-07:** added pinned Linux CEF 149 initialization layout support: `cef_main_args_t` argv ownership, `cef_string_t`/`cef_settings_t` size and offset constants from the farm headers, and an aligned opaque `cef_settings_t` block configured for the upcoming offscreen browser process (`size=448`, `no_sandbox=1`, `windowless_rendering_enabled=1`, single-threaded message loop). `mde-web-cef-renderer` now prints `CEF_INIT_PLAN` after ABI verification, making the remaining gate sit immediately before `cef_initialize`. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **37/37** including init layout/argv/settings regressions; a real runtime probe on `.50` printed `CEF_INIT_PLAN settings_size=448 no_sandbox=1 windowless=1 external_pump=0 multi_threaded_loop=0` after `CEF_ABI_OK`, then exited 78 at `CEF_OFFSCREEN_PENDING`. **CEF initialize-probe slice 2026-07-07:** typed the CEF lifecycle symbols (`cef_execute_process`, `cef_initialize`, `cef_get_exit_code`, `cef_shutdown`) and added an opt-in real initialization probe (`MDE_CEF_INITIALIZE_PROBE=1`). The probe now owns CEF argv strings, injects early Chromium switches for `--browser-subprocess-path`, `--resources-dir-path`, `--locales-dir-path`, `--icu-data-file-path`, `--no-sandbox`, and GPU-off flags, and keeps UTF-16 settings strings alive for the full call. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **42/42** including lifecycle/argv/switch regressions. Real `.50` runtime probes reached CEF initialization with `CEF_INIT_ARGV argc=12` but Chromium aborts before returning with `base/i18n/icu_util.cc:232 Invalid file descriptor to ICU data received` (exit 133), even with explicit resource/locales/ICU paths, `--single-process`, `--no-zygote`, and cwd set to the CEF bundle root. **CEF initialize-unblocked slice 2026-07-07:** normalized the pinned Linux CEF runtime so top-level ICU/pak resources are visible from `Release/` via idempotent links (`icudtl.dat`, `chrome_100_percent.pak`, `chrome_200_percent.pak`, `resources.pak`), added default headless Ozone Chromium switches for the initialize probe, and fixed CEF subprocess invocations so `cef_execute_process` exits with its real subprocess code instead of falling through to the offscreen pending gate. Farm verification: `bash -n install-helpers/install-cef-runtime.sh` passed; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **42/42**; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-pkg ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **18/18**. Real `.50` runtime proof: the installer reused the cached pinned tarball, linked the Release resources, built the release bridge, and `MDE_CEF_INITIALIZE_PROBE=1` against `/home/mm/mde-cef-active` printed `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK` with no ICU or X bootstrap failure; it still exits 78 at `CEF_OFFSCREEN_PENDING`, which is now the honest next gate. **CEF browser-create/first-paint slice 2026-07-07:** added header-pinned CEF 149 C callback blocks for `cef_client_t`, `cef_life_span_handler_t`, `cef_render_handler_t`, `cef_browser_t`, and `cef_browser_host_t`; dynamically resolves `cef_browser_host_create_browser_sync` plus `cef_do_message_loop_work`; creates a bounded `MDE_CEF_BROWSER_PROBE=1` offscreen browser; calls `was_resized` + `invalidate(PET_VIEW)` after create; records `on_after_created`, `get_view_rect`, and first `on_paint`. The probe now uses external message pumping, keeps Ozone headless for no-X startup, and deliberately avoids Chrome `--headless`/`--disable-software-rasterizer` because those bypassed or starved OSR paint on the farm. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-next ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **48/48** (46 lib + bridge handoff + protocol golden). Real `.50` runtime proof: `MDE_CEF_BROWSER_PROBE=1` against `/home/mm/mde-cef-active` initialized CEF, created the windowless browser, delivered `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY url=https://example.com/ view=1024x768 created=1 paints=1 last_paint=1024x768`, and exited `0`. **CEF paint-to-shm slice 2026-07-07:** CEF `on_paint` now publishes the real BGRA buffer into `OffscreenFrameSink`, which sends the BOOKMARKS-6 `AttachFrame` fd once and emits `PaintReady` with the stable shm sequence; the renderer has an opt-in `MDE_CEF_ATTACH_STDIN=1` path that adopts the same fd-0 Unix socket convention used by the Servo helper. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-sink ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **49/49** (47 lib + bridge handoff + protocol golden), including a regression that a simulated CEF paint callback sends `AttachFrame` + `PaintReady`. Real `.50` IPC proof: a Python socketpair harness spawned `mde-web-cef-renderer` with `MDE_CEF_BROWSER_PROBE=1 MDE_CEF_ATTACH_STDIN=1`, received `IPC_FRAMES [[0], [1, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]]`, `IPC_FDS 1`, saw `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY url=https://example.com/ view=1024x768 created=1 paints=1 last_paint=1024x768`, and the renderer exited `0`. **CEF tab-lifecycle slice 2026-07-07:** promoted the fd-0 session socket from probe-only into real CEF `tab` mode. `mde-web-cef-renderer tab` now initializes CEF, creates the offscreen browser, publishes `AttachFrame`/`PaintReady` through `OffscreenFrameSink`, keeps pumping the CEF external message loop, drains decoded BOOKMARKS-6 control frames to keep the stream aligned, and exits cleanly when the shell closes the socket. CEF subprocess invocations (`--type=...`) now always enter `cef_execute_process`, so normal tab mode does not rely on probe env vars for subprocess dispatch. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-tab ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **49/49**. Real `.50` renderer proof: `mde-web-cef-renderer tab --url https://example.com/ --width 640 --height 480` with the session socket on stdin delivered `TAB_IPC_FRAMES [[0], [1, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]]`, `TAB_IPC_FDS 1`, `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... last_paint=640x480`, and exited `0` after socket close. Real `.50` helper proof: `mde-web-cef tab --url https://example.com/ --width 320 --height 240` with `MDE_CEF_ROOT` and `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_BIN` overrides delivered `HELPER_TAB_IPC_FRAMES [[0], [1, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]]`, `HELPER_TAB_IPC_FDS 1`, `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... last_paint=320x240`, and exited `0`. **CEF control-frame slice 2026-07-07:** added header-pinned CEF control offsets for browser history/reload/main-frame, host close, and frame `load_url`; CEF `tab` now applies BOOKMARKS-6 `Load`, `Reload`, guarded `Back`, guarded `Forward`, and `Resize` control frames, updates `GetViewRect` after resize, calls `was_resized`/`invalidate(PET_VIEW)`, and force-closes the CEF browser host before shutdown when the shell socket closes. Unsupported input/resource-verdict/cosmetic-filter frames are still decoded and drained without fake behavior. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-control ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **50/50** (48 lib + bridge handoff + protocol golden). Real `.50` control proof: a helper-level socket harness spawned `mde-web-cef tab`, received first `AttachFrame`/`PaintReady`, sent `Resize(480x360)` + `Load(https://example.com/?mde-cef-control=1)` + `Reload`, received a second `PaintReady` (`CONTROL_IPC_FRAMES [[0], [1, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], [1, 4, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]]`), kept `CONTROL_IPC_FDS 1`, and the helper exited `0` after socket close. **CEF input-forwarding slice 2026-07-07:** CEF `tab` now maps BOOKMARKS-6 `Input` frames into real Chromium host input calls: pointer move/leave, mouse button press/release, scroll wheel, key up/down, and committed text. The helper pins the CEF 149 `cef_mouse_event_t`/`cef_key_event_t` byte layouts plus `cef_browser_host_t` send-event offsets from the farm headers, tracks last pointer position for scroll targeting, and keeps unsupported resource/cosmetic frames decoded-only rather than pretending filter enforcement. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-input ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **53/53** (51 lib + bridge handoff + protocol golden). Real `.50` helper proof: a socket harness spawned `mde-web-cef tab` against `/home/mm/mde-cef-active`, received one attached frame fd and first paint, sent pointer move/click/scroll/key/text plus resize/reload, received **5** post-input `PaintReady` events, and the helper exited `0`. **CEF Browser-UI launch proof 2026-07-07:** the Browser surface now has a focused live-helper regression proving a CEF-selected tab satisfies the shell runtime gate, builds the generic `SpawnSpec` for `mde-web-cef`, attaches through the same `WebSession` path as Servo, records the tab engine as CEF, and pumps a frame into the egui texture path. The shared client spawn docs now describe the dual helper contract (`mde-web-preview` or `mde-web-cef`), and the client crash-surface tests use a bounded poll loop that matches the UI frame loop instead of a scheduler-racy single poll. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-ui-cef-exact ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper cef_live_open_uses_the_browser_ui_spawn_path_and_pumps_a_frame -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-client-spawn-rerun ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client --features live-helper -- --nocapture` passed **42/42**. **CEF cosmetic-filter slice 2026-07-07:** CEF `tab` now applies BOOKMARKS-6 `CosmeticFilters` frames through the pinned `cef_frame_t::execute_java_script` slot (`offset=152`), installing, updating, or clearing a helper-owned `mde-cef-cosmetic-style` element in the main frame. Empty CSS clears the prior stylesheet. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-cosmetic-full ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **55/55** (53 lib + bridge handoff + protocol golden). Real `.50` helper proof: a socket harness spawned `mde-web-cef tab` against `/home/mm/mde-cef-active`, received one attached frame fd and first paint for a page containing `#ad`, sent `CosmeticFilters("#ad { display: none !important; }")` plus resize, received a post-filter `PaintReady`, and the helper exited `0`. **CEF resource-verdict slice 2026-07-07:** CEF `tab` now installs pinned CEF request/resource-request handlers, extracts request URLs through `cef_request_t::get_url`, emits BOOKMARKS `ResourceRequest` frames, parks CEF `cef_callback_t` values with a ref, and applies shell `ResourceVerdict` replies via async `cont`/`cancel` before the network request proceeds. The ABI loader now resolves `cef_string_userfree_utf16_free` so request URLs are freed by the matching runtime. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-cef-resource-full-rerun ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **55/55** plus bridge/protocol tests; `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.90 MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-client-verdict-regression ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client --features live-helper resource -- --nocapture` passed. Real `.50` proof: a local HTTP page requested `/tracker.gif`; the harness saw `ResourceRequest` for the top-level page and tracker, sent `allow=false` for the tracker, CEF still painted, and the server recorded `page_hits=1 tracker_hits=0` with helper exit `0`. **CEF Browser-UI real-site smoke 2026-07-07:** added an opt-in live-helper regression `cef_live_browser_ui_renders_a_real_site_when_farm_smoke_is_enabled` that opens CEF through the actual Browser UI `open_with`/`WebSession::spawn` path, starts a local HTTP page, exercises the Browser HTTPS prompt seam with `continue_insecure_load`, waits for the CEF frame to upload into the egui texture path, and asserts the HTTP server was hit. Farm verification: `.90` skip-mode compile passed through `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.90 MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-ui-live-compile`; `.50` real runtime smoke passed by running the warmed test directly with `MDE_CEF_LIVE_UI_SMOKE=1`, `MDE_CEF_ROOT=/home/mm/mde-cef-active`, `MDE_WEB_CEF_BIN=/home/mm/magic-mesh-farm-browser-ui-real-cef-helper/crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/target/debug/mde-web-cef`, and `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_BIN=/home/mm/magic-mesh-farm-browser-ui-real-cef-helper/crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/target/debug/mde-web-cef-renderer`; output showed `CEF_OK`, `CEF_LAUNCH`, `CEF_ABI_OK`, `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK`, and the test passed in 1.70s. **Public Gmail Browser-UI smoke 2026-07-07:** extended the opt-in live smoke with `MDE_CEF_LIVE_UI_PUBLIC_URL`, which navigates the already-open CEF Browser UI tab to a public HTTPS URL and waits for a fresh egui texture upload. Farm proof on `.50`: warmed direct test run with `MDE_CEF_LIVE_UI_SMOKE=1 MDE_CEF_LIVE_UI_PUBLIC_URL=https://mail.google.com/ MDE_CEF_ROOT=/home/mm/mde-cef-active MDE_WEB_CEF_BIN=/home/mm/magic-mesh-farm-browser-ui-real-cef-helper/crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/target/debug/mde-web-cef MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_BIN=/home/mm/magic-mesh-farm-browser-ui-real-cef-helper/crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/target/debug/mde-web-cef-renderer cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper cef_live_browser_ui_renders_a_real_site_when_farm_smoke_is_enabled -- --nocapture` passed in **2.01s** after printing `CEF_OK`, `CEF_LAUNCH`, `CEF_ABI_OK`, and `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK`. **Farm RPM CEF packaging-path slice 2026-07-07:** fixed the farm `xcp-build.sh rpm` lane to build the workspace-excluded `mde-web-cef` helper with `--locked` into `$PWD/target` before `cargo generate-rpm`, matching the canonical Fedora RPM path for the shipped Workstation browser helper + renderer bridge. The packaging regression now asserts both canonical and farm scripts stage the preview and CEF helpers before RPM generation. Verification: `bash -n install-helpers/xcp-build.sh`; `git diff --check`; `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.90 MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-rpm-cef-packaging ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd fedora_rpm_builder_builds_workspace_excluded_browser_helpers --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**. **Full farm RPM cut 2026-07-07:** ran the release-style farm RPM lane on BigBoy with `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 MCNF_BUILD_SLOT=browser-rpm-cef-full ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh rpm`. The path completed the main workspace release build, `mde-web-preview` release build, `mde-web-cef` release build, final `mde-shell-egui --features drm,live-helper` relink, and `cargo generate-rpm`; the pulled artifact is `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` built Tue Jul 7 18:58:13 2026, size `112629072` bytes, SHA-256 `7e1bb539a35ed88620cac154d1e76804282735a98151accaa411b4c244cc79fb`. Payload inspection with `rpm -qpl` proves the full RPM ships `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`, `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mde-cef-runtime-setup.service`, and `/usr/share/magic-mesh/browser/cef-linux64-minimal.env`; `rpm -qp --requires` includes `bzip2` for runtime extraction. **Eagle live Workstation promotion + CEF runtime proof 2026-07-07:** promoted the same fresh candidate to Eagle with `MCNF_RPM=/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh eagle`; same-NEVRA `rpm -Uvh --replacepkgs` completed and `mackesd`, `nebula`, and `syncthing` were active. The staged `/tmp/mcnf-promote.rpm` on Eagle matched SHA-256 `7e1bb539a35ed88620cac154d1e76804282735a98151accaa411b4c244cc79fb`. Installed payload inspection on Eagle proves `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`, `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mde-cef-runtime-setup.service`, and `/usr/share/magic-mesh/browser/cef-linux64-minimal.env`; `rpm -q --requires magic-mesh` includes `bzip2`. Started the installed `mde-cef-runtime-setup.service`; it role-gated as Workstation, fetched the pinned `310966119` byte archive, verified cached SHA-256 `f90dec4c5c42a7bbd4f2bd80a7a77e0ac6aacfc6627bb43572d803e77f26dfbc`, extracted a `1.5G` runtime, linked Release resources, and activated `/opt/mde/cef`. `mde-web-cef probe` returned `CEF_OK` against `/opt/mde/cef`; a packaged `mde-web-cef tab --url https://example.com/ --width 320 --height 240` socket smoke delivered IPC frames plus one shm fd, printed `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK` and `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... last_paint=320x240`, and exited `0`. During promotion, the best-effort remote Bus publish hung before install; patched `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh` so remote publish calls are timeout-bounded at 20s and verified `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh status` returns with candidate SHA `7e1bb539a35ed88620cac154d1e76804282735a98151accaa411b4c244cc79fb`. **Post-promotion fleet smoke 2026-07-07:** after the Eagle CEF promotion, `MCNF_RPM=/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh live-smoke` passed (etcd cluster health, peers, `/run` headroom), and `... live-audit` passed with both DO lighthouses plus Eagle on `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64` and core services active. **Closed 2026-07-14:** superseded by the packaged CEF operational closure below; further site-specific compatibility hardening belongs in later Browser DD tasks, not the base CEF render gate.
  - **BROWSER-DD-1 keyboard-input bugfix 2026-07-07:** fixed the post-render live bug where the Browser painted pages but did not accept keyboard input. The shell now forwards page input as page-local coordinates, click-focuses the browser canvas, and sends keyboard/text only while that canvas owns focus so address-bar/chrome typing cannot leak into the helper; CEF key events now set `focus_on_editable_field=1` for the offscreen Chromium text-input path. Farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_body_input_is_localized_and_keyboard_is_focus_gated -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` broad `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed `59/59`; `.50` full `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed `55` unit tests plus bridge/protocol tests; post-format focused shell+CEF regressions passed; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check` and `git diff --check` passed.
  - **BROWSER-DD-1 keyboard-input RPM/live evidence 2026-07-07:** cut a fresh BigBoy candidate at `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` with SHA-256 `81e390a6f9473ff35ecedb8c395ff2b7846c4f03ae6b0e1401dc162daaa7b0ce` and size `112632422` bytes. The full RPM payload includes `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`, `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mde-cef-runtime-setup.service`, and `/usr/share/magic-mesh/browser/cef-linux64-minimal.env`; `rpm -qp --requires` includes `bzip2`. Promoted the same package to Eagle with same-NEVRA `rpm -Uvh --replacepkgs`, verified `mde-web-cef probe` returns `CEF_OK` against `/opt/mde/cef`, restarted `mde-shell-egui.service`, and confirmed `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui` owns `/dev/dri/card1`, `/dev/tty1`, and the input devices. Installed-helper input smoke on Eagle sent pointer click, key, text, scroll, and resize BOOKMARKS-6 frames to packaged `mde-web-cef tab`; it received one shm fd, one first paint, and one post-input `PaintReady`. Post-candidate `live-smoke` passed etcd/fleet/run-headroom checks, and `live-audit` passed with both DO lighthouses plus Eagle on `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64` and core services active. Caveat: the automated installed-helper smoke proves helper input-to-paint, while final GUI typing confirmation remains an operator bug-hunt item because there is no automation seam yet to open the live Browser surface and type through the physical UI.
  - **BROWSER-DD-1/2 live Browser bugfix slice 2026-07-07:** replaced the hidden Browser Engine menu plus generic new-tab button with explicit compact `+ Servo` and `+ CEF` tab buttons in both horizontal and vertical tab layouts; active-tab engine remains visible as a status chip. Compact Chromium-style Browser chrome now uses smaller Browser-local tab, toolbar, omnibox, suggestion, and prompt controls. Fixed the "all Browser menu items look greyed out" report by making the shared menubar render enabled command text with `Style::TEXT` and disabled command text with `Style::TEXT_DIM`. Fixed Servo/CEF page stretching by preserving the helper frame aspect ratio inside the Browser body instead of scaling the texture to the full body rectangle. Strengthened the shell keyboard proof with a real headless Browser panel regression: render a live page, click the page canvas, then type on the following frame and decode outgoing BOOKMARKS-6 `Input(Key)` plus `Input(Text)` frames from the helper side. Fixed CEF offscreen keyboard focus by calling the pinned `cef_browser_host_t::set_focus` slot (`offset=72` from the CEF 149 farm headers) on pointer press, key, and text input before `send_key_event`. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed `59/59`; `.50` full `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed `56` unit tests plus bridge/protocol tests including `set_host_focus_uses_header_pinned_slot`; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-egui menubar:: -- --nocapture` passed `10/10`; `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check` and `git diff --check` passed.
