Farm evidence: `.90` rerun `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed `59/59`; `.90` `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper` passed with no `web.rs` warnings. Remaining shell check warning is the pre-existing unrelated `dock.rs::transfer_badge_id` dead-code warning.
  - **BROWSER-DD-1 WebRTC privacy-switch correctness fix 2026-07-10:** an independent verification pass (prompted by the BROWSER-DD-9 design doc's own "may be a no-op" flag) confirmed `chromium_privacy_switches()`'s `--disable-webrtc` line is **not a real Chromium switch** — absent from the live `content/public/common/content_switches.cc` and `chrome/common/chrome_switches.cc` (fetched directly from `chromium.googlesource.com` against this pinned CEF's Chromium `149.0.7827.201` base; the only real WebRTC-related switches defined there are `kDisableWebRtcEncryption`, `kForceWebRtcIPHandlingPolicy`, `kWebRtcMaxCaptureFramerate`, `kWebRtcLocalEventLogging`), corroborated by a live Google Chrome Enterprise support-forum administrator report of the identical flag being ignored (`support.google.com/chrome/a/thread/5939360`) and a chromium-dev mailing-list thread confirming the only real disable mechanism is the build-time GN flag `enable_webrtc=false` (not available on this crate's prebuilt vendored CEF binary). Chromium silently no-ops unrecognized `--` switches rather than erroring, so this shipped as inert hardening — CEF's WebRTC stack was fully reachable from any loaded page the whole time, the same local-IP-leak class of risk Servo's `dom_webrtc_enabled: false` (`engine.rs`) exists to prevent. **Fix:** removed the dead switch (regression-tested); kept `--force-webrtc-ip-handling-policy=disable_non_proxied_udp` (confirmed real, backs the genuine `WebRtcIPHandling` enterprise policy); added what was then a real renderer-level mitigation, `cef_browser::webrtc_block_script`, which deletes `RTCPeerConnection`/`webkitRTCPeerConnection`/`RTCDataChannel`/`RTCSessionDescription`/`RTCIceCandidate` and `getUserMedia`/`getDisplayMedia` (incl. legacy vendor-prefixed forms) from every tab's JS scope, applied unconditionally on the same 250ms poll cadence and `execute_java_script` mechanism the existing passkey-bridge shim already uses (defense-in-depth, not airtight — this hand-rolled ABI has no `OnContextCreated`-equivalent early-injection hook; `docs/THREAT_MODEL.md` §8 has the full writeup). **Superseded 2026-07-14:** after native CEF media permissions and sandbox media-device visibility landed, that script became an opt-in emergency/privacy override (`MDE_CEF_WEBRTC_BLOCKED=1`) instead of the default, so CEF browser-page WebRTC is operational by default while the IP policy remains load-bearing. Farm verification: `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.90 ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **98/98** (93 lib + 2 renderer + 2 bridge-handoff + 1 protocol-golden), including the new `webrtc_block_script_removes_the_reachable_webrtc_surface` and `init_paths_never_emit_the_inert_disable_webrtc_switch` regression tests; `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/src/cef_init.rs crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/src/cef_browser.rs` and `git diff --check` both clean.
  - **BROWSER-DD-1 packaged CEF operational closure 2026-07-14:** the Chrome/CEF page-rendering blocker is closed through the packaged shell-equivalent wire path. Fresh Fedora 44 RPM `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` has SHA-256 `f9596b780b2b487e3f7e8957c3dec85a3d129c2aca1f9d64cd9e09be0330f6ff` and ships `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/cef-verify`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`, and the `mde-shell-egui.service` `Delegate=yes` cgroup fix. Installed on `.15`, packaged `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime` ended with both `install-cef-runtime: render smoke passed` and `install-cef-runtime: wire smoke passed`. Installed `/usr/libexec/mackesd/cef-verify /usr/bin/mde-web-cef https://www.google.com/gmail/about/ 30` passed through `mde-web-preview-client::WebSession::spawn`, delivered `frame_events=22`, `favicon=true`, final URL `https://workspace.google.com/gmail/`, final title `Gmail: Secure, AI-Powered Email for Everyone | Google Workspace`, and `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... view=1280x800`. The restarted installed shell stayed `active/running` with `NRestarts=0`; SSH-only verifier cgroup warnings were expected outside the delegated systemd shell unit and did not block rendering.
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-2: tabs + omnibox + compat chrome.** **As** a user, **I want** normal browsing, **so that** it's usable. **Acceptance:** standard tabs (open/close/switch/reorder) with a vertical-tabs option; unified URL+search omnibox with live SearXNG suggestions; back/fwd/reload/stop; HTTPS-only-with-prompt; block-3rd-party-cookies + clear-on-close; the Quasar-dashboard new-tab (SearXNG + mesh services); tested reachable. **Partial 2026-07-07:** the Browser surface now has a first-class compact tab strip over the existing isolated `WebSession` tab model: state chips + title/URL label, active switching, close behavior that drops/reaps the helper session, and a `+` new-tab intent drained by the live-helper shell arm into a real helper spawn. **Omnibox slice 2026-07-07:** toolbar Go and menu "Open Typed Address" now share one pure resolver: explicit schemes pass through (`https://`, `about:`, `data:`), likely hosts/IPs/host:port drafts become `https://…`, and free text searches the mesh-local SearXNG endpoint `https://search.mesh/search?q=…` with percent-encoded query text. **Tab-management slice 2026-07-07:** the tab strip now supports closing the last tab back to the honest gated/empty state and reordering tabs left/right while preserving the active `WebSession` identity. **Vertical-tabs slice 2026-07-07:** View → Vertical Tabs toggles a left-side tab rail backed by the same `WebSession` vector and the same switch/close/reorder/new-tab seams; no second tab model or fake helper path. **HTTPS-only prompt slice 2026-07-07:** explicit `http://` omnibox targets no longer load immediately; toolbar Go and menu Open Typed Address share one gate that shows an HTTP warning with Use HTTPS, Continue HTTP, and Cancel actions. Upgrading rewrites the target to `https://…`; continuing sends the original HTTP URL; hosts/searches keep the existing HTTPS-first resolver. **Quasar new-tab slice 2026-07-07:** `about:blank` now renders a native Quasar Browser dashboard over the real helper session with mesh SearXNG search plus service shortcuts for `search.mesh`, `music.mesh`, optional `horizon.mesh`, and `keystone.mesh`; dashboard searches load the SearXNG URL, and HTTP mesh services reuse the same HTTPS prompt gate. **Cookie/session policy slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser now exposes a Privacy menu that names the enforced engine policy (cookies blocked, third-party cookies blocked because no cookie store exists, session data cleared on tab close) plus a real Clear Current Tab Data action that resets the active helper session to the Quasar new-tab surface. Engine-side hardening remains structural: `secure_preferences()` disables the Servo cookie store, IndexedDB, StorageManager API, HTTP disk cache, WebRTC, and WebGPU, while the sandbox keeps persistence on tmpfs/no-home. **Live suggestions slice 2026-07-07:** the omnibox now fetches real mesh SearXNG suggestions from `https://search.mesh/autocompleter?q=...` on a short-lived off-thread reqwest client, only for plain search drafts (not URLs/hosts), renders the returned suggestions under the address bar, and routes accepted suggestions through the same omnibox resolver/load/HTTPS prompt path as Enter/Go. The parser accepts SearXNG/OpenSearch-style JSON arrays and object suggestion shapes, dedupes, and keeps tests offline. **Stop/compact Chromium chrome slice 2026-07-07:** added a real `ControlMsg::Stop` wire variant (tag `8`) plus `WebSession::stop()`. CEF tabs now turn the compact reload slot into a Chromium-style `×` Stop button while the active CEF session reports `loading`; the CEF helper dispatches that control to the pinned `cef_browser_t::stop_load` slot (`offset=112`, verified from the CEF 149 farm headers). Servo decodes the frame for protocol compatibility but does not show a fake stop action because the current Servo wrapper exposes no real cancel-load hook. Farm verification: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client -- --nocapture` passed **43/43** including `stop_drives_a_real_control_frame_over_the_seam`; `.50` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **57** unit tests plus bridge/protocol tests including `stop_control_uses_cef_stop_load_slot` and `ControlMsg::Stop` golden bytes; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **59/59**; `.50` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **26** unit tests plus headless-frame and protocol-golden tests; `git diff --check` passed. **Stop-button RPM + non-Eagle bench promotion 2026-07-07:** rebuilt `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` on BigBoy after the stop/compact chrome slice; RPM metadata reports build time `Tue Jul 7 21:41:57 2026`, payload size `286104730`, file size `112643918`, and SHA-256 `defb01d677ac56af2fff312e43d17984a2fb19cd9d13e3b66cd3a46ca641a734`. Payload inspection proves the browser helpers and CEF runtime assets still ship (`/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`, `mde-cef-runtime-setup.service`, `cef-linux64-minimal.env`). Non-Eagle farm gates passed on the candidate: L1 clean install **6/6**, L2 mini-mesh **15/15**, L3 stability/fd budget **14/14**, and L4 staged lighthouse replacement **33/33**. Eagle was excluded from this bench path per the operator directive, and no encrypted bench seat reboot was issued. **Stop-button Servo investigation 2026-07-10:** confirmed the "Servo exposes no real cancel-load hook" claim through direct source inspection of the pinned crates.io publications (not assumption) — downloaded and read `servo` 0.3.0 (`webview.rs`'s complete `impl WebView`: `load`/`load_request`/`reload`/`go_back`/`go_forward`/`notify_scroll_event`/`set_page_zoom`/etc, ~40 methods total, no `stop`; `servo.rs`'s complete `impl Servo`: `delegate`/`set_delegate`/`spin_event_loop`/`execute_webdriver_command`/`set_preference`/etc, no `stop`), `servo-embedder-traits` 0.3.0 (`webdriver.rs`'s `WebDriverCommandMsg` — the type `Servo::execute_webdriver_command` accepts — 24 variants, only `LoadUrl`/`Refresh`/`GoBack`/`GoForward` touch navigation, none cancel one; `WebDriverLoadStatus::NavigationStop` is a status the constellation reports back, not a sendable command), and `servo-constellation-traits` 0.3.0 (`EmbedderToConstellationMessage` — the internal channel `WebView::load`/`reload` send on — 32 variants, no stop/cancel-navigation variant either, and moot regardless because `WebView::inner()`/`inner_mut()`, the accessors that reach `constellation_proxy()`, are private and unreachable from outside the crate). Servo's own "Building a browser using Servo" tutorial (servo.org, 2024-09-11) documents no stop/cancel-load capability either. **Verdict: a genuine upstream Servo limitation in the pinned 0.3.0 release, confirmed at every reachable API layer — not a gap in this workspace's wrapper — so no fake/partial stop was implemented.** Left the honest no-fake-stop behavior in place: `mde-web-preview/src/main.rs`'s `apply_control` still decodes-and-intentionally-ignores `ControlMsg::Stop`, now with the full citation inline and split out into its own match arm, distinguished from the genuinely-"not yet" `Resize`/`Input`/`ResourceVerdict`/`CosmeticFilters`/`SavePdf` arms so the two different kinds of gap are not conflated. Extracted the shell's inline `can_stop` boolean into a named, doc-cited `can_show_stop_control(has_tab, crashed, loading, engine)` in `mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` (`nav_chrome`'s only caller) so a future refactor cannot silently start painting a fake, do-nothing Stop button for a Servo tab; behavior is unchanged (CEF-only). New tests (`mde-shell-egui` `web::tests`): `a_loading_cef_tab_shows_a_real_stop_control`, `a_loading_servo_tab_never_shows_a_fake_stop_control`, `stop_control_still_requires_a_live_loading_uncrashed_tab`. Farm verification: `.130` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client -- --nocapture` passed **51/51** including the pre-existing `stop_drives_a_real_control_frame_over_the_seam` (the shell-to-wire Stop send stays engine-agnostic and correct); `.130` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **177/177** including the three new `can_show_stop_control` tests, zero new warnings; `.50` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **39** lib unit tests plus **2** headless-frame and **2** protocol-golden tests (**43/43** total, exit code 0, `Finished` test profile in 11m01s — a cold Servo+SpiderMonkey rebuild on the 2-vCPU `.50` node); `rustup run 1.94.0 rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` clean on both touched files.
- [✓] **BROWSER-DD-3: native ad/tracker blocking + privacy hardening.** **Acceptance:** uBlock-class native blocker on mesh-synced subscribable filter lists + custom rules + per-site toggle; anti-fingerprinting; per-site permission prompts + manager (default-deny); mesh-hosted safe-browsing blocklists; per-site data manager + forget; zero telemetry. **Native blocker policy slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser now starts every tab with the bundled EasyList/EasyPrivacy/uBlock seed filter store instead of an empty matcher, compiles that store into each `WebSession`, and re-applies it to all open tabs when policy changes. The Privacy menu now exposes the enforced cookie/no-store policy, the filter-list source, current first-party site, and a real per-site toggle: "Disable Blocking for This Site" writes a `FilterListStore` allowlist entry for the current host and changes subsequent helper `ResourceVerdict` frames from block to allow; "Enable Blocking for This Site" writes the re-block tombstone and restores block verdicts. Added a custom-rule injection seam (`FilterListSource::custom`) so synced/operator EasyList-format rules compile into active tab verdicts; `mde-web-preview-client` re-exports the store/source/host helpers so the shell and client use the same adblock model. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **62/62**, including `per_site_privacy_toggle_changes_real_resource_verdicts`, `custom_filter_rules_compile_into_open_tabs`, and Privacy menu model regressions; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client -- --nocapture` passed **43/43**; `git diff --check` clean. **Mesh allowlist action slice 2026-07-07:** the same per-site toggle now also publishes the existing mackesd adfilter worker verbs (`action/adfilter/allow` / `action/adfilter/block`) with the typed `{"domain":...}` body the worker drains into the mesh-synced LWW allowlist. The local Browser still applies the decision immediately, while the Bus action lets the `adfilter` worker replicate the site toggle across peers through the existing Syncthing store. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **63/63**, including `adfilter_domain_body_matches_the_worker_allow_block_shape`. **CEF anti-fingerprinting/zero-telemetry slice 2026-07-07:** the Chromium helper now pins a generic browser identity in both `cef_settings_t` and early Chromium argv (`user_agent`, `locale`, `accept_language_list`, `--user-agent`, `--lang`, `--accept-lang`) and starts CEF with privacy switches disabling background networking, sync, metrics/reporting, extension/device discovery surfaces, notifications/speech, WebRTC local-IP leakage, and Chromium ad/privacy-sandbox feature families including `PrivacySandboxAdsAPIs`, `InterestCohort`, `WebGPU`, `WebUSB`, and `WebBluetooth`. This brings the CEF path up to the existing Servo generic/no-persistence hardening baseline without changing the offscreen render seam. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **59** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests, including `init_paths_emit_cef_privacy_switches` and `owned_settings_pin_generic_browser_identity`. **Per-site permission manager slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser Privacy menu now exposes the enforced default-deny permission class list (`location, camera, microphone, notifications, clipboard`), shows the current first-party site's effective permission state, and provides a real "Forget Site Permissions" action that records a current-site permission reset without pretending unsupported allow toggles exist. This reflects the helpers' live deny-all prompt behavior (Servo denies `PermissionRequest`; CEF privacy switches disable the corresponding prompt/device surfaces) and keeps allow workflows gated until engine-level grants are wired. Farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web::menubar::tests::the_privacy_menu_exposes_the_enforced_cookie_policy -- --nocapture` passed, and `.90` broad `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **63/63**.
  - **Mesh safe-browsing blocklist slice 2026-07-07:** `mde-web-preview-client::RequestFilter` now carries a mesh-synced `SafeBrowsingBlocklist` that blocks exact unsafe hosts and subdomain suffixes before network through the existing `ResourceRequest` -> `ResourceVerdict` IPC path while preserving mesh/overlay exemptions. The Browser shell owns an injectable `safe_browsing_hosts` policy seam, compiles it into every open tab's filter, and reports loaded mesh unsafe-host count in the Privacy menu. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client filter:: -- --nocapture` passed **10/10**, including exact/subdomain blocking and mesh exemption tests; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **64/64**, including `safe_browsing_hosts_change_real_resource_verdicts`, which proves loading a host changes the real helper verdict from allow to block. **Current remaining:** richer per-site data manager.
  - **Per-site data manager slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser now maintains a real `SiteDataManager` fed from committed tab navigation state after each session poll. It tracks first-party hosts, open-tab counts, last-seen timestamps, and explicit clear counts; Privacy now reports the tracked-site/open-tab/current-site clear summary, and Clear Current Tab Data records a reset for the active site before returning the tab to the dashboard. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web:: -- --nocapture` passed **66/66**, including `site_data_manager_tracks_committed_first_party_hosts` and `clearing_current_tab_records_the_active_site_data_reset`. **BROWSER-DD-3 acceptance is code-complete and farm-green; live/RPM promotion remains part of the broader release pipeline.**
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-4: Widevine DRM.** **Acceptance:** the Widevine CDM fetched at first run (Firefox-style, not redistributed); DRM streaming (Netflix/Spotify web) plays on the CEF engine; the browser is fully functional (non-DRM) without it (honest gate). **First-run CDM provisioning/honest gate slice 2026-07-07:** the CEF helper now detects optional Widevine separately from the required CEF runtime (`WIDEVINE_OK` / `WIDEVINE_MISSING`) and keeps `probe` success tied only to CEF availability, so non-DRM browsing remains functional without a CDM. `CefLaunchPlan` carries `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_ROOT` and `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_LIB` only when `libwidevinecdm.so` is present, preserving an explicit renderer handoff for the later playback hook. Packaging now ships an operator-pinned first-run installer (`install-widevine-cdm`) plus `mde-widevine-cdm-setup.service`, rank-gated to Workstation and excluded from the server RPM; the manifest intentionally carries empty URL/SHA fields so Magic Mesh does not redistribute the CDM and exits with a clean `78` configuration gate until a licensed source is supplied. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **62** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests, including optional CDM detection and installer-manifest regressions; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**, including Workstation-only Widevine assets and Lighthouse mask coverage. **CEF native bridge Widevine handoff slice 2026-07-15:** `mde-web-cef-renderer` now consumes the helper-provided `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_ROOT` / `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_WIDEVINE_LIB` contract, fails closed when only one side is present, rejects missing roots/libs, rejects a CDM library outside the declared root, requires the library name `libwidevinecdm.so`, rejects sandbox-replaced/private roots (`/tmp`, home, root, var, run, and `/mnt`) before CEF starts, emits `WIDEVINE_BRIDGE_READY root=... lib=...` when valid, and exposes only the validated CDM root as an extra read-only bind inside the CEF OS sandbox. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed; `.90` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml --bin mde-web-cef-renderer -- --nocapture` passed **10/10** renderer contract/sandbox tests; `.130` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **150** lib tests plus **10** renderer tests, **2** bridge-handoff tests, protocol golden, and doc tests; `.130` `cargo build --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml --bins` passed; live `.50` fake-CDM regression reached `CEF_LAUNCH ... widevine=/tmp/mde-widevine-fake/libwidevinecdm.so`, then exited `78` with `CEF_BRIDGE_CONTRACT_INVALID reason=Widevine root /tmp/mde-widevine-fake is under /tmp`, proving bad CDM roots fail before Chromium subprocess startup. **Remaining:** supply a licensed CDM URL/SHA, install a real CDM on a CEF seat, and live-test Netflix/Spotify DRM playback; if playback still does not activate with the actual bundle, inspect the pinned CEF 149 Linux startup/hint-file path against that CDM.
- [!] **BROWSER-DD-5: WebExtensions (curated allowlist) + LastPass — deferred for v1 by BROWSER-CHROME E3.** **V1 decision 2026-07-14:** production Browser operation no longer depends on WebExtensions or LastPass autofill. The supported v1 path is native adblock, passkeys, reader mode, userscripts, and site styles; the curated registry and smoke fixture remain lab/future probes behind `MDE_CEF_WEBEXTENSIONS_LAB`. **Original future acceptance:** the CEF engine runs a VETTED allowlist of WebExtensions (LastPass, uBlock, password managers) from a mesh-hosted extension registry (sideload in Power mode); standard Chrome permission model; LastPass installs + autofills. *(CEF extension support is partial — validate/host-shim as needed)* **CEF WebExtensions registry gate slice 2026-07-09:** `mde-web-cef` now has a concrete mesh-hosted curated registry gate at `MDE_CEF_EXTENSION_REGISTRY` / `/mnt/mesh-storage/browser/extensions/allowlist.env`. The parser accepts only vetted `[extension.<chrome-id>]` sections, validates 32-character Chrome ids, safe names/versions/permission tokens, safe paths under the registry directory, and real unpacked extension directories; it reports `CEF_EXTENSIONS_READY` for a valid registry, `CEF_EXTENSIONS_MISSING` / `CEF_EXTENSIONS_INVALID` for honest gates, and now reports `CEF_EXTENSIONS_UNPROVEN` until live extension runtime smoke proves LastPass/uBlock behavior. A packaged operator template records the LastPass (`hdokiejnpimakedhajhdlcegeplioahd`) and uBlock Origin (`cjpalhdlnbpafiamejdnhcphjbkeiagm`) allowlist shape without bundling or pretending either extension runs yet. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-ext-cef-final` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml webextension -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.130` slot `browser-ext-cef-full-final` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **77/77** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests; `.170` slot `browser-ext-cef-fmt3` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed. Local `git diff --check` passed. A broad `cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings` probe remains blocked by pre-existing CEF helper lint debt outside this slice. **CEF WebExtensions launch-contract slice 2026-07-09:** a valid curated registry now affects the real CEF process contract instead of only status output. `CefLaunchPlan` carries vetted unpacked extension directories into `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_EXTENSIONS`, the native renderer validates every directory before initialization, and the CEF argv builder switches from `--disable-extensions` to an exact `--load-extension=<allowlist dirs>` plus `--disable-component-extensions-with-background-pages` only when registry validation succeeded. Registry paths now reject commas so the Chromium load list cannot be confused. This still does not claim LastPass autofill or the full permission prompt model until a real extension runtime smoke proves it. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-ext-launch-focused2` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml extension -- --nocapture` passed **5/5**; `.90` slot `browser-ext-launch-handoff2` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml render_once_hands_runtime_contract_to_the_bridge -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-ext-launch-full2` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **78/78** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests; `.170` slot `browser-ext-launch-fmt2` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed. **CEF WebExtensions manifest validation slice 2026-07-09:** allowlisted unpacked directories must now contain a bounded Chrome `manifest.json` with `manifest_version` 2 or 3, safe manifest name/version strings, a registry version matching the manifest version unless the operator template uses `operator-pinned`, and every registry-declared permission present in `permissions`, `host_permissions`, or `optional_permissions`. This prevents a vetted id from pointing at an arbitrary directory or claiming undeclared Chrome permissions while still avoiding a new dependency in the workspace-excluded helper. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-ext-manifest-focused` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml webextension -- --nocapture` passed **5/5**; `.90` slot `browser-ext-manifest-handoff` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml render_once_hands_runtime_contract_to_the_bridge -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-ext-manifest-full` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **79/79** lib tests plus bridge/protocol integration tests; `.170` slot `browser-ext-manifest-fmt` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed.
  - **CEF WebExtensions bridge registry revalidation slice 2026-07-09:** the native CEF bridge no longer trusts a bare `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_EXTENSIONS` directory list. `CefLaunchPlan` now hands the bridge the vetted registry path in `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_EXTENSION_REGISTRY`, and the renderer re-runs registry/manifest validation before CEF initialization, rejecting missing registries, invalid registries, or any extension directory list that differs from the registry-derived allowlist. This prevents a forged bridge environment from loading arbitrary unpacked directories while preserving the exact `--load-extension` handoff for validated LastPass/uBlock entries.   - **CEF WebExtensions Chrome permission-policy slice 2026-07-09:** the curated registry no longer accepts arbitrary permission labels just because the manifest repeats them. Registry `permissions` entries must now be either supported Chrome API permissions (`storage`, `tabs`, `webRequest`, `webRequestBlocking`, `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `nativeMessaging`, and related standard APIs) or Chrome host match patterns such as `<all_urls>` and `https://*.example.com/*`; malformed URL-ish tokens and unknown labels fail validation before the extension is considered vetted. The manifest cross-check still requires every registry-declared permission to appear in `permissions`, `host_permissions`, or `optional_permissions`.   - **CEF WebExtensions Power Mode sideload gate slice 2026-07-09:** `power_sideload=true` is now enforced instead of merely parsed. `mde-web-cef` exposes `MDE_CEF_EXTENSION_POWER_MODE`; when it is unset/false, sideload-only entries stay in the vetted registry and report `CEF_EXTENSIONS_POWER_GATED`, but they are omitted from `MDE_CEF_BRIDGE_EXTENSIONS` and therefore from CEF `--load-extension`. When Power Mode is explicitly enabled, the same registry entries are loaded, the bridge receives the policy bit, and renderer-side registry revalidation compares against the Power-Mode-filtered allowlist before CEF initialization. When vetted extensions are loaded, the renderer additionally emits `CEF_EXTENSION_SMOKE_READY`, giving the later live LastPass/uBlock smoke a concrete observable instead of relying on argv alone.   - **CEF WebExtensions windowless-Alloy gate slice 2026-07-09:** the CEF 149 farm headers now explain the live smoke failure: `cef_types_runtime.h` says windowless rendering always uses Alloy style, and `cef_linux.h::SetAsWindowless` sets `runtime_style = CEF_RUNTIME_STYLE_ALLOY`; Chrome style is the path with full browser functionality. The helper now pins and tests the `cef_window_info_t.runtime_style` offset/value for the windowless path, and the renderer refuses extension text-smoke by default with `CEF_EXTENSIONS_WINDOWLESS_ALLOY_GATED ... chrome_runtime_required_for_webextensions` before waiting for an impossible content-script beacon. `MDE_CEF_ALLOW_ALLOY_EXTENSION_SMOKE=1` remains as a diagnostic override for future runtime experiments. The packaged smoke runner captures helper output, preserves it for operators, and turns that gate into a clear runner message instead of the older generic “beacon not observed” timeout. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-ext-alloy-full` full `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --nocapture` passed **85** lib tests plus **2** renderer tests, **2** bridge-handoff tests, and protocol golden; `.50` slot `browser-ext-alloy-renderer` passed `windowless_extension_runtime_gate_names_chrome_runtime_requirement`; `.90` slot `browser-ext-alloy-window2` passed `window_info_sets_windowless_bounds_from_header_offsets`; `.90` slot `browser-ext-alloy-fmt2` CEF `cargo fmt --check` passed; `.50` slot `browser-ext-alloy-rolefmt` scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/onboard/role_provision.rs` passed; `.130` slot `browser-ext-alloy-rpm-big` passed the Workstation/server RPM smoke-asset split. Live `.50` slot `browser-ext-alloy-live` built the CEF helper binaries and ran the smoke wrapper against `/home/mm/mde-cef-active`; it reached `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK`, emitted `CEF_EXTENSIONS_WINDOWLESS_ALLOY_GATED`, and the runner exited 78 with the Chrome-runtime requirement. A first `.170` package rerun hit farm disk exhaustion, so that failed slot workspace was removed and the package assertion was rerun on BigBoy. Remaining: implement a non-windowless/Chrome-runtime extension proof path, or another CEF-supported Chrome-runtime host, before claiming LastPass/uBlock content-script execution or autofill.
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-6: WebAuthn / passkeys.** **Acceptance:** hardware security keys (FIDO2) + platform passkeys in a mesh-synced encrypted authenticator + phone-as-authenticator (KDC); passwordless login works end-to-end.
  - **Passkey daemon ceremony-owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now has a Workstation-tier `browser_passkeys` worker reachable from the daemon supervisor. It drains strict `action/browser/passkey` Browser handoffs, validates `create`/`get` ceremony metadata (`source=browser`, Servo/CEF engine, HTTPS-or-localhost origin, RP id scoped to origin host, base64url challenge/user/credential ids, registration user metadata), writes pending ceremony records under the local durable passkey root, mirrors the same no-private-key pending record into the Syncthing-backed workgroup root when writable, and publishes retained `state/browser-passkeys/<node>` plus `event/browser-passkeys/<node>` pending/error evidence. It intentionally does not mint credentials, sign assertions, claim hardware-key CTAP2, platform-authenticator key storage, phone-as-authenticator, or end-to-end passwordless login yet. Farm evidence: `.50 browser-passkeys-worker2` passed 5 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.90 browser-passkeys-role2` passed 19 `worker_role` tests; `.130 browser-passkeys-check2` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services`; `.170 browser-passkeys-syncfmt3` passed scoped rustfmt for the new worker and role table files; local `git diff --check` passed for touched paths. Full `mackesd.rs` rustfmt is still blocked by unrelated pre-existing import-order drift in that dirty entrypoint, so this slice used diff-check plus farm compile/test coverage for that file.
  - **Passkey Browser status-display slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now polls the daemon-owned retained `state/browser-passkeys/<node>` topic, validates strict passkey-owner status records (`idle`/`pending`/`error`, `create`/`get` ceremony metadata, RP id, mirror flag, counters, and last error), and surfaces non-idle or historical passkey posture in the Browser status chip cluster without claiming credential creation, assertions, CTAP2 hardware-key support, platform-authenticator key storage, phone-as-authenticator, or passwordless login. Farm evidence: `.50 browser-passkey-status-shell3` passed 2 focused shell tests; `.90 browser-passkey-status-chip3` passed the menubar status chip test; `.130 browser-passkey-status-check3` passed `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper`; `.170 browser-passkey-status-fmt3` passed scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` for `web.rs`; local `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Passkey helper ceremony-handoff slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser helper protocol now carries `PasskeyRequest` events, `WebSession` queues/drains them like other helper events, and Browser publishes a strict `action/browser/passkey` daemon handoff with Browser source, local host, engine, ceremony, origin, RP id, challenge, registration user metadata, allow-credential ids, and timeout metadata. Servo installs a bounded page bridge that intercepts `navigator.credentials.create/get`, queues public WebAuthn request metadata, rejects the page promise, and drains one event per poll; CEF installs the same bridge and publishes bounded metadata through an intercepted hidden-image beacon. This still does not mint credentials, sign assertions, store platform-authenticator keys, claim CTAP2 hardware keys, phone-as-authenticator, or passwordless login. Farm evidence: `.50 browser-passkey-wire` passed the client passkey queue test; `.90 browser-passkey-shell-handoff` passed 4 focused Browser passkey tests; `.130 browser-passkey-cef2` passed 2 focused CEF passkey bridge/beacon tests; `.130 browser-passkey-servo2` passed the Servo passkey bridge lib test and `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml`; `.50 browser-passkey-fmt-shell2`, `.90 browser-passkey-fmt-cef2`, and `.130 browser-passkey-servo-fmt2` passed format checks; local `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Passkey platform-authenticator core slice 2026-07-09:** `browser_passkeys` now completes software platform ceremonies after helper handoff: registration mints an ES256/P-256 credential id plus public SEC1 key, seals the private seed through the mesh-age-keyed backup envelope under the local passkey store, mirrors public metadata plus sealed private key to the Syncthing-backed workgroup root when writable, emits `browser_passkey_created`, and marks retained status `created`; assertion finds matching RP/allow-list credentials, decrypts the sealed seed, builds WebAuthn clientDataJSON hash plus authenticator data with counter, signs with ES256, self-verifies, increments the counter, emits `browser_passkey_assertion`, and marks status `asserted`. Browser status parsing now accepts and displays `created`/`asserted`. This still does not drive the credential/assertion response back into page JavaScript, CTAP2 hardware keys, phone-as-authenticator, or passwordless login E2E. Farm evidence: `.130 browser-passkey-platform-worker2` passed 6 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.50 browser-passkey-platform-shell` passed 4 Browser passkey tests; `.130 browser-passkey-platform-worker2` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.90 browser-passkey-platform-fmt2` passed `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui`; BigBoy scoped rustfmt passed for `browser_passkeys.rs` and `web.rs`; local `git diff --check` passed. `.170 browser-passkey-platform-check` failed from disk exhaustion only and was superseded by the BigBoy check.
  - **Passkey page-response delivery slice 2026-07-09:** the helper bridge now keeps intercepted `navigator.credentials.create/get` promises pending with a bridge-minted `client_request_id` instead of immediately rejecting them. Browser copies that id into `action/browser/passkey`, remembers the originating tab, polls daemon `event/browser-passkeys/<node>` completions, and sends a typed `CompletePasskey` control frame back to the helper. Servo and CEF both install a completion hook that resolves only the matching pending page promise with credential-like ArrayBuffer response fields; assertion responses carry daemon authenticator data, clientDataJSON, signature, user handle, and credential id, while registration responses carry credential id/public key metadata plus clientDataJSON and an explicit empty attestation placeholder. `browser_passkeys` now echoes `client_request_id` on created/assertion events. This still does not claim a browser-native `PublicKeyCredential` implementation, CBOR attestation-object correctness, CTAP2 hardware keys, phone-as-authenticator, or real-site passwordless login E2E validation. Farm evidence: `.50 passkey-response-client` passed 2 focused client/session passkey tests; `.90 passkey-response-servo` passed the Servo passkey bridge test; `.50 passkey-response-cef` passed 2 CEF passkey bridge/beacon tests; `.130 passkey-response-shell2` passed 5 Browser passkey tests; `.170 passkey-response-worker` passed 6 `browser_passkeys` tests; `.50 passkey-response-shell-check` passed `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper`; `.130 passkey-response-mackesd-check` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.170 passkey-response-fmt` passed scoped rustfmt for the touched passkey response files; local `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Passkey registration attestation/authData slice 2026-07-09:** `browser_passkeys` registration completions now return a non-empty WebAuthn-shaped `fmt="none"` attestation object instead of the empty placeholder. Registration authData carries RP ID hash, UP/UV/AT flags, counter, zero AAGUID, credential id, and an ES256 COSE_Key; events also expose raw authenticator data and SPKI DER public key bytes so helper `response.getPublicKey()` returns the WebAuthn-shaped public key. Servo and CEF completion bridges now prefer `public_key_spki_der_b64url` while keeping the old SEC1 field as a fallback. This still does not claim hardware attestation, packed/self attestation signatures, a browser-native `PublicKeyCredential` prototype implementation, CTAP2 hardware keys, phone-as-authenticator, or real-site passwordless login E2E validation. Farm evidence: `.170 passkey-attestation-worker` passed 6 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.50 passkey-attestation-cef` passed 2 CEF passkey bridge/beacon tests; `.130 passkey-attestation-shell` passed 5 Browser passkey tests; `.90 passkey-attestation-servo` passed the Servo passkey bridge test; `.130 passkey-attestation-mackesd-check` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.170 passkey-attestation-fmt` passed scoped rustfmt for the touched passkey attestation files; local `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Passkey native-like JS result shape slice 2026-07-09:** Servo and CEF passkey completion bridges now resolve page promises with a closer WebAuthn-shaped credential object instead of a plain data bag. The result includes `getClientExtensionResults()`, `toJSON()`, `authenticatorAttachment="platform"`, best-effort `PublicKeyCredential`/`AuthenticatorAttestationResponse`/`AuthenticatorAssertionResponse` prototypes when those constructors exist, attestation `getTransports()` returning `internal`, `getAuthenticatorData()`, and platform authenticator availability statics (`isUserVerifyingPlatformAuthenticatorAvailable=true`, conditional mediation=false) for engines without a native WebAuthn surface. This improves compatibility with site feature detection while remaining honest: it still does not claim true native browser brand checks in all engines, CTAP2 hardware keys, phone-as-authenticator, packed/self attestation signatures, or real-site passwordless login E2E validation. Farm evidence: `.50 passkey-native-cef` passed 2 CEF passkey bridge/beacon tests; `.90 passkey-native-servo` passed the Servo passkey bridge test; `.130 passkey-native-shell` passed 5 Browser passkey tests; `.50 passkey-native-client` passed 2 shared client/session passkey tests; `.170 passkey-native-shell-check` passed `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper`; `.170 passkey-native-fmt` passed scoped rustfmt for the touched helper files.
  - **Passkey relying-party verification slice 2026-07-09:** `browser_passkeys` now has daemon-side coverage that verifies its registration and assertion artifacts the way a relying party would consume them. The test decodes registration `clientDataJSON`, parses the `fmt="none"` attestation object, extracts `authData`, validates RP ID hash, UP/UV/AT flags, credential id, sign counter, and COSE ES256 public key, then performs a login ceremony, decodes assertion `clientDataJSON`, checks RP hash/flags/counter, recomputes the client-data hash, and verifies the ES256 assertion signature with the public key recovered from registration authData. This is still a deterministic local RP proof, not a live third-party site E2E; CTAP2 hardware keys, phone-as-authenticator, packed/self attestation signatures, and fleet/browser UI for live RP smoke remain open. Farm evidence: `.90 passkey-rp-single` passed `registration_and_assertion_outputs_verify_like_a_relying_party`; `.50 passkey-rp-worker` passed 7 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.130 passkey-rp-mackesd-check` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.170 passkey-rp-fmt` passed scoped rustfmt for `browser_passkeys.rs`.
  - **Passkey hardware-key readiness slice 2026-07-09:** `browser_passkeys` status now includes an honest CTAP/FIDO HID readiness probe (`hardware_state`, `hardware_key_count`, `hardware_readable_count`, `hardware_probe_ms`). The daemon scans `/sys/class/hidraw` for likely FIDO/CTAP/U2F security-key descriptors and checks whether matching `/dev/hidraw*` nodes are openable, distinguishing `unknown`, `unavailable`, `present_permission_denied`, and `ready`. This does not implement CTAP2 command exchange, PIN/user-presence flows, hardware-backed credential creation/assertion, packed/self attestation, phone-as-authenticator, or live RP E2E; it only makes hardware-key readiness visible and testable. Farm evidence: `.90 passkey-hw-single` passed `hardware_key_probe_reports_ready_permission_denied_unavailable_and_unknown`; `.50 passkey-hw-worker` passed 8 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.130 passkey-hw-mackesd-check` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.170 passkey-hw-fmt` passed scoped rustfmt for `browser_passkeys.rs`.
  - **Passkey Browser hardware-status display slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now consumes the daemon's hardware-key readiness fields from `state/browser-passkeys/<node>`, validates the four honest states (`unknown`, `unavailable`, `present_permission_denied`, `ready`), keeps old status JSON compatible as `unknown`, and renders a separate status chip for known hardware posture: `Security key ready`, `Security key blocked`, or `Security key unavailable`. Ceremony posture remains a separate passkey chip, so a pending/error WebAuthn ceremony no longer hides the hardware-readiness signal. This still does not implement CTAP2 command exchange, hardware-backed credential creation/assertion, PIN/user-presence flows, packed/self attestation, phone-as-authenticator, or live RP E2E. Farm evidence: `.50 passkey-hw-ui-shell2` passed 5 focused Browser passkey tests; `.90 passkey-hw-ui-chip2` passed `passkey_owner_status_chip_reflects_retained_daemon_state`; `.130 passkey-hw-ui-check2` passed `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper`; `.170 passkey-hw-ui-fmt` passed scoped rustfmt for `web.rs`.
  - **Passkey CTAP HID framing diagnostic slice 2026-07-09:** `browser_passkeys` now owns the first CTAP HID protocol framing seam for hardware keys: it builds broadcast `CTAPHID_INIT` requests, splits longer CTAP HID payloads into 64-byte initialization/continuation frames with sequence bytes, rejects oversized payloads or commands that already include the initialization bit, and validates `CTAPHID_INIT` response shape in daemon tests. Hardware readiness status also reports `hardware_ctaphid_state` plus `hardware_ctaphid_init_frame_count`, using `init_request_ready` only when a readable FIDO/CTAP hidraw node is present. This still does not write to or read from a live hidraw node, allocate a live CTAP HID channel, send CTAP2 CBOR `authenticatorMakeCredential`/`authenticatorGetAssertion`, handle PIN/user-presence, create hardware-backed credentials, produce packed/self attestation, support phone-as-authenticator, or prove live RP E2E. Farm evidence: `.90 passkey-ctaphid-single2` passed 3 focused `ctaphid` tests; `.50 passkey-ctaphid-worker2` passed 11 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.130 passkey-ctaphid-check2` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.170 passkey-ctaphid-fmt3` passed scoped rustfmt for `browser_passkeys.rs`.
  - **Passkey Browser CTAP diagnostic display slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now consumes the daemon's `hardware_ctaphid_state` and `hardware_ctaphid_init_frame_count` fields from `state/browser-passkeys/<node>`, preserves old status JSON as `unknown`, rejects unsupported or contradictory CTAP diagnostic states, and renders a separate `CTAP INIT framed` chip only when the daemon has a readable hardware key and a nonzero framed broadcast INIT request. The existing security-key readiness chip remains separate, so physical HID readiness and protocol-framing readiness are not collapsed. This still does not perform a live hidraw exchange, allocate a CTAP HID channel, send CTAP2 credential commands, handle PIN/user-presence, create hardware-backed credentials, or prove live RP E2E. Farm evidence: `.50 passkey-ctaphid-ui-shell` passed 5 focused Browser passkey tests; `.90 passkey-ctaphid-ui-chip` passed `passkey_owner_status_chip_reflects_retained_daemon_state`; `.130 passkey-ctaphid-ui-check` passed `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper`; `.170 passkey-ctaphid-ui-fmt2` passed scoped rustfmt for `web.rs`.
  - **Passkey opt-in live CTAP HID INIT diagnostic slice 2026-07-09:** `browser_passkeys` now has a reachable live `CTAPHID_INIT` exchange primitive behind `MDE_BROWSER_PASSKEY_CTAPHID_LIVE_PROBE`. The default status poll still only reports framed-request readiness with live probing `disabled`, so it does not block or perturb hardware. When explicitly enabled, the worker opens the first readable FIDO/CTAP hidraw node read/write with nonblocking flags on Unix, writes the broadcast INIT frame, reads one 64-byte response, validates nonce/command/length/allocated channel, and reports live channel, protocol, version, and capabilities or an honest error in separate `hardware_ctaphid_live_*` fields. This still does not send CTAP2 credential CBOR, handle PIN/user-presence, create hardware-backed credentials, produce packed/self attestation, support phone-as-authenticator, or prove live RP E2E. Farm evidence: `.90 passkey-ctaphid-live-single` passed 4 focused `ctaphid` tests; `.50 passkey-ctaphid-live-worker` passed 12 focused `browser_passkeys` tests; `.130 passkey-ctaphid-live-check` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib`; `.170 passkey-ctaphid-live-fmt2` passed scoped rustfmt for `browser_passkeys.rs`.
  - **Security review: honest UP-only flags, no consent gate yet (2026-07-10):** operator review found the authenticator-data flags byte was unconditionally claiming **User-Verified** (hardcoded `0x05`/`0x45`) on every registration/assertion ceremony even though nothing in the pipeline performs user verification or even checks for a user gesture — `handle_passkey_event` in `web.rs` forwards straight to the Bus with no confirmation, and the injected `navigator.credentials.create`/`.get` shims in `mde-web-preview/src/engine.rs` and `mde-web-cef/src/cef_browser.rs` have no gesture check either. Fixed the flag bytes to honest UP-only (`0x01` registration / `0x41` assertion — `browser_passkeys.rs`, asserted by the `auth_data[32]` regressions) so the daemon stops lying to relying parties about a verification step that never happened. The underlying gap — no real per-ceremony consent/verification UI — is **not** fixed, just no longer actively misrepresented. Full writeup: `docs/THREAT_MODEL.md` §7.4 item 1 (not duplicated here). Marker moves `[ ]` → `[>]`: the WebAuthn mechanism is real and now honest, but a real user-consent/verification gate is required before this item is feature-complete or safe to rely on for actual authentication.
  - **Passkey shell-consent gate slice 2026-07-15:** Browser no longer forwards page-origin WebAuthn ceremonies straight to the daemon. `handle_passkey_event` now validates the helper body, holds it as `PendingPasskeyConsent`, renders a shell-owned Approve/Deny prompt in the Browser page prompt lane, and publishes `action/browser/passkey` only after approval. Approval stamps `user_present=true`, `shell_consent=true`, and `presence_source=browser_shell_prompt`, making the shell click the User Present signal; denial returns a `CompletePasskey` error to the helper so the page promise rejects and the daemon never sees the ceremony. Duplicate ceremonies while one approval is pending are rejected immediately without replacing the visible prompt. Pending completion routes now use the stable tab id instead of a reorder-prone tab index. Threat-model and worker comments updated: this is consent/presence, not User Verification; `UV` stays unset. This still does not implement CTAP2 hardware credential commands, PIN/biometric verification, phone-as-authenticator, packed/self attestation, or live third-party passwordless E2E. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-passkey-consent-shell` passed `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_passkey -- --nocapture` (7/7); `.50` slot `browser-passkey-consent-worker` passed `cargo test -p mde-browser-workers browser_passkeys -- --nocapture` (17/17); `.170` slot `browser-passkey-consent-fmt` passed `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/Cargo.toml -- --check`. BigBoy slot `browser-passkey-consent-shell` also passed `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper`.
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-7: mesh sync + follow-me tabs + send-tab.** **Acceptance:** tabs/session + bookmarks + settings/speed-dial + downloads list sync over the Nebula+Syncthing substrate (no external account, E2E); follow-me tabs (your live set on any node); send-tab to nodes AND paired phones; startup restores the synced session. **Send-tab handoff slice 2026-07-08:** Browser now publishes a typed `action/browser/send-tab` Bus handoff for the active tab with target `node` or `phone`, engine, URL, title, preview, source, and host metadata. The Bookmarks menu exposes `Send Tab to Node` and `Send Tab to Phone`, and the page-actions menu uses the same handoff for the live page; delivery remains honestly owned by the session-sync and phone bridge layers. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_send_tab -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_bookmarks_menu_exposes_platform_share_handoffs -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_page_family_items_disable_without_a_live_page -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs docs/WORKLIST.md` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Session snapshot handoff slice 2026-07-08:** Browser now publishes a deduped `action/browser/session-sync` snapshot for the state it already owns: active tab index, per-tab URL/title/engine/container/display target/lifecycle flags, Browser settings (`future_engine`, vertical tabs, page zoom, find/download drawer state), and browser-originated download ledger summaries. The snapshot is emitted after tab open/close/select/reorder, visible Browser setting changes, download ledger refreshes, and helper poll state changes; unchanged bodies are cached so frames do not flood the Bus. This gives the future Nebula+Syncthing follow-me owner a real state stream while startup restore remains open. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_session_sync -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_send_tab -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.170` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper tab_strip_state_switches_closes_and_requests_new_tabs -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs docs/WORKLIST.md` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Startup restore parser slice 2026-07-08:** Browser can now consume the same `action/browser/session-sync` JSON shape as a startup-restore input: it restores Browser-owned settings (`future_engine`, vertical tabs, page zoom, find/download drawer state), parses synced tabs, and enqueues each tab through the existing live-helper `TabOpenIntent::NewForegroundUrl` path instead of inventing a second opener. Multiple restored tabs ride a FIFO queue, and the previously active tab is queued last so the current helper-open behavior foregrounds it after restore. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_session_restore -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_session_sync -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.170` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper tab_strip_state_switches_closes_and_requests_new_tabs -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper saved_pdf_opens_in_a_cef_viewer_tab -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs docs/WORKLIST.md` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Speed-dial sync/restore slice 2026-07-08:** Browser new-tab shortcuts now live in `WebState` instead of being render-only constants, so the `action/browser/session-sync` snapshot carries `settings.speed_dial` entries with label, URL, and hint metadata. Startup restore applies snapshot-provided speed-dial entries through the same parser, drops malformed blank label/URL rows, caps restored entries at 32, and keeps the existing mesh-local defaults for fresh sessions. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_session -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui new_tab_dashboard_mesh_service_shortcuts_use_the_same_load_gate -- --nocapture` passed; a follow-up `.50` rerun passed **4/4** after fixing the test-only `LiveHttpServer` cfg gate. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Daemon session-sync store slice 2026-07-08:** `mackesd` now owns the `action/browser/session-sync` stream through a `browser_session_sync` worker on the Workstation tier. It validates Browser snapshot shape, persists the latest startup-restore JSON per host under the local durable session-sync root, mirrors the same body into the Syncthing-backed workgroup root when writable, publishes `state/browser-session-sync/<node>` status, and keeps local pending state when the shared root is unavailable. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_session_sync --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Startup file restore slice 2026-07-08:** Browser startup now probes the daemon-owned latest session snapshots before opening the default blank helper tab: local durable session-sync root first, then the canonical Syncthing workgroup root. It reads `browser-session-sync/<host>/latest.json`, uses the same host sanitizer as `mackesd` session-sync, feeds the file body into the existing `action/browser/session-sync` parser, and drains the resulting tab-open queue through the existing live-helper path so restored tabs and normal new tabs share one opener. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_startup_restore -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_session_restore -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Send-tab outbox owner slice 2026-07-08:** `mackesd` now drains `action/browser/send-tab` in the same `browser_session_sync` owner. It validates the Browser handoff shape (`op=browser_send_tab`, target class `node` or `phone`, Servo/CEF engine, non-empty URL and host), writes a durable local outbox record under `browser-send-tab/<target>/<source-host>/<event>.json`, and mirrors all pending local outbox records into the Syncthing-backed workgroup root whenever the share is writable. This makes send-tab a daemon-owned replicated handoff instead of a Browser-only Bus event; final destination selection remains a follow-up because the current Browser payload carries a target class, not a specific node/phone id. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_session_sync --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **8/8**; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_session_sync.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_session_sync.rs` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Send-tab concrete destination slice 2026-07-08:** Browser node handoffs now carry `target_id`/`target_label` for the concrete local mesh hostname, and phone handoffs can be armed with `MDE_BROWSER_SEND_PHONE_TARGET`/`MDE_BROWSER_SEND_PHONE_LABEL` until the paired-phone picker lands. `mackesd` preserves backward compatibility for class-only handoffs while routing durable outbox records under `browser-send-tab/<target>/<target-id>/<source-host>/<event>.json`, so downstream node and phone bridges can consume by concrete recipient instead of only target class. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_send_tab -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_session_sync --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **9/9**. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_session_sync.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_session_sync.rs` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued.

  **Remote node destination slice 2026-07-15:** Browser `Send Tab to Node` can
  now publish a concrete remote mesh node destination through
  `MDE_BROWSER_SEND_NODE_TARGET`/`MDE_BROWSER_SEND_NODE_LABEL`, instead of always
  self-addressing the local hostname. The previous self-target remains the
  fallback for follow-me/self-restore, while `mackesd`'s existing
  `target_id`-based outbox path now has a real Browser-side way to address a
  different node. Farm evidence: BigBoy `.130` slot `browser-sendtab-shell`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_send_tab -- --nocapture` passed **7/7**;
  `.50` slot `browser-sendtab-outbox`
  `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_send_tab_outbox -- --nocapture` passed
  **3/3**; BigBoy direct warm-slot
  `CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo test -p mde-browser-workers browser_session_sync -- --nocapture`
  passed **10/10**. `.170` slot `browser-sendtab-fmt`
  `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/Cargo.toml -- --check`
  passed, and local `git diff --check` passed. `.90` and one cold
  BigBoy worker crate attempt hit ENOSPC before project tests; both were
  superseded by the successful BigBoy warm-slot worker run, and the disposable
  failed slots were removed.

### Phase 2 — power + media + chrome
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-8: Power mode + power menu + downloader + scraper.** **Acceptance:** a single Power-mode toggle (off=clean; on=the Power menu) surfacing full Chromium DevTools + view-source + UA-switch + device APIs (prompted) + the native media downloader (HLS/DASH video, all-images, media sniffer, ignore-blocking, auto-rename+metadata) + the full site scraper (extract/crawl/export CSV·JSON·MD) + userscripts.
  **Power-mode entry slice 2026-07-10:** Browser now has a real Power-mode setting instead of a View-menu placeholder: View toggles clean/off vs Power/on, the Power menu appears only when enabled, status shows a Power chip, the setting rides browser session-sync/startup-restore, and the first real power tool opens the active page through the existing live-helper tab path as `view-source:<url>`. Unfinished DD-8 tools remain honest captions until their own slices land: full Chromium DevTools, UA/device overrides, prompted device APIs, native media downloader, full site scraper, and related Power tools. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-power-mode` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper power_mode -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` slot `browser-power-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-power-sync` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_session_sync -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.170` slot `browser-power-fmt` farm-side scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed. Package-wide `cargo fmt -p mde-shell-egui -- --check` remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated `cloud_plane.rs` formatting drift.
  **Power-mode UA override slice 2026-07-10:** The Power menu now includes a real per-tab User Agent switch with Default, Desktop Chrome, and Android Chrome presets. The selected preset is visible in tab chrome/status chips, rides the typed helper protocol as `SetUserAgent`, is preserved in browser session-sync snapshots, and Servo/CEF both apply a bounded page-visible `navigator.userAgent`/`appVersion`/`platform` override through their existing page-script seams. This is intentionally not claimed as native network request-header rewriting; that deeper CEF/Servo hook remains open alongside DevTools, media downloader, scraper, device overrides, and prompted device APIs. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-ua-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-ua-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper power_mode -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` slot `browser-ua-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-ua-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_session_sync_body_carries_tabs_settings_and_downloads -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-ua-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_user_agent_override_script_installs_and_clears_page_visible_ua -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-ua-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml user_agent_override_script_installs_and_clears_page_visible_ua -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-ua-fmt` farm-side scoped rustfmt over the touched Browser/preview/CEF files passed. A stale `.50` `browser-ua-shell-controls` run failed on the pre-fix nested test import and was superseded by the passing `.90` rerun.
  **Power-mode device-profile override slice 2026-07-10:** The Power menu now also includes a real per-tab Device Profile switch with Default, Phone, and Tablet presets. The selected profile is visible in tab chrome/status chips, rides the typed helper protocol as `SetDeviceProfile`, is preserved in browser session-sync snapshots, and Servo/CEF both apply bounded page-visible viewport/screen/touch metadata (`innerWidth`, `screen.*`, `devicePixelRatio`, `navigator.maxTouchPoints`, and a managed viewport meta tag) through their existing page-script seams. This is intentionally not claimed as full native device emulation; compositor viewport/device emulation and lower-level engine hooks remain open alongside DevTools, media downloader, scraper, and prompted device APIs. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-device-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-device-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper power_mode -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.50` slot `browser-device-shell-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-device-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_session_sync_body_carries_tabs_settings_and_downloads -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-device-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_device_profile_script_installs_and_clears_page_visible_device_metadata -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-device-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml device_profile_script_installs_and_clears_page_visible_device_metadata -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-device-fmt` farm-side scoped rustfmt over the touched Browser/preview/CEF files passed.
  **Power-mode CEF DevTools portal slice 2026-07-10:** The CEF helper now pins Chromium remote debugging on loopback port `9222` through both `cef_settings_t.remote_debugging_port` and the early `--remote-debugging-port=9222` switch. When Power mode is enabled, Browser exposes a CEF-only `Chromium DevTools` item that opens `http://127.0.0.1:9222/` in a foreground CEF tab; Servo tabs keep the item disabled and direct action attempts report an honest CEF-only notice. This is intentionally the real Chromium discovery portal, not automatic active-target DevTools frontend selection, Servo DevTools, or packaged extension tooling. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-devtools-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml cef_init -- --nocapture` passed **12/12**; `.90` slot `browser-devtools-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper power_mode -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.130` slot `browser-devtools-view` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-devtools-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-devtools-fmt` farm-side scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/src/cef_init.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed.
  **Power-mode Chromium DevTools active-target slice 2026-07-10:** The Power-menu Chromium DevTools action now queries CEF's loopback `/json/list` endpoint with a bounded timeout, selects the target whose URL matches the active CEF tab, opens the concrete DevTools frontend URL for that page when discovery succeeds, and falls back honestly to the target-list portal when the endpoint is unavailable or no match exists. Servo remains CEF-only gated.   **Power-mode active-page scrape export slice 2026-07-10:** The Power menu now has a real `Export Page Scrape` producer for the current live page. It materializes JSON, CSV, and Markdown active-page metadata exports in a browser scrape spool, then submits all three files through the existing `browser_download` Transfers lane to the Browser capture destination, so the output uses the shared ledger/verify/notification path instead of a second download manager. This is intentionally an active-page metadata export, not full crawl/deep DOM extraction, media sniffing, or native downloader coverage.   **Power-mode scrape crawl-seed export slice 2026-07-10:** `Export Page Scrape` now carries a bounded same-origin crawl seed in all three exported formats instead of only page metadata. The Browser folds retained helper resource telemetry into JSON `crawl_seed` rows plus CSV/Markdown seed sections, de-duplicates and caps candidates at 64 URLs, preserves resource type and allowed/blocked verdicts, excludes cross-origin telemetry, and still submits the JSON/CSV/Markdown artifacts through the existing `browser_download` Transfers lane. This is the first crawl handoff for the scraper; recursive fetching, DOM link extraction, and deep content extraction remain follow-up scraper hooks. Farm evidence: `.90` slot `browser-scrape-crawl-focused-final` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper active_page_scrape_export -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `.90` slot `browser-scrape-crawl-power` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper power_mode -- --nocapture` passed **5/5**; `.130` slot `browser-scrape-crawl-browser` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_ -- --nocapture` passed **83/83**; `.170` slot `browser-scrape-crawl-fmt-final` farm-side scoped `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed.
  **Power-mode scrape text-extract export slice 2026-07-10:** `Export Page Scrape` is no longer metadata-only on the user-facing path. The Power-menu action now sends a bounded `RequestPageText` to the active helper, waits for the `PageText` response, and writes the returned visible page text into the JSON/CSV/Markdown scrape artifacts with `extracted_text_status`, char count, and truncation metadata while retaining the bounded same-origin crawl seed and Transfers enqueue path. Empty helper text is recorded honestly as `no_text`; the direct test/fallback export path records `not_requested`. Recursive fetching, structured DOM extraction, and richer link extraction remain follow-up scraper hooks.   **Power-mode scrape DOM links/headings export slice 2026-07-10:** `Export Page Scrape` now uses a structured helper scrape request instead of only plain page text. The shared helper wire has `RequestPageScrape`/`PageScrape`, Servo returns bounded visible text plus visible DOM anchors/headings through its page-script callback, CEF mirrors the same shape through a bounded synthetic scrape beacon, and the Browser writes `dom_links`/`dom_headings` with same-origin marking into JSON plus DOM link/heading rows/sections in CSV and Markdown. The older direct fallback export remains honest with `dom_extract_status=not_requested`. Recursive network crawling and richer article/body extraction were left as follow-up scraper hooks for later slices.   **Power-mode scrape article/body extraction slice 2026-07-10:** `Export Page Scrape` now captures richer article metadata from the same structured helper scrape response.   **Power-mode scrape crawl-manifest handoff slice 2026-07-10:** `Export Page Scrape` now writes a bounded depth-1 crawl manifest handoff in all three scrape formats. The Browser combines same-origin resource telemetry with same-origin DOM links from the structured scrape body, de-duplicates targets, caps the manifest at 128 URLs, records target source/resource/allowed/depth metadata in JSON `crawl_manifest`, adds `crawl_manifest_status=ready` plus `crawl_execution_status=not_started`, writes `crawl_target` CSV rows, and adds a Markdown `Crawl Manifest` section.   **Power-mode scrape crawl execution lane slice 2026-07-10:** Browser scrape JSON exports with `crawl_execution_status=not_started` now reach a daemon-owned bounded crawl executor instead of only being copied as handoff metadata. `BrowserDownloadLane` recognizes `browser_active_page_scrape` JSON files, copies the original export to the selected destination, validates and de-duplicates same-origin http/https `crawl_manifest` targets, fetches up to 128 depth-1 targets through the existing bounded `HttpWgetLane`, stores them in a sibling `.crawl` package directory, and writes `browser-scrape-crawl-package.json` with source URL, original export, target count, fetched paths, resource type, source, and depth metadata.   **Power-mode HLS package downloader slice 2026-07-10:** HLS Browser media request files now package more than the root manifest. `BrowserDownloadLane` promotes HLS `.download.json` requests into a bounded HLS package mode, fetches the root playlist with the existing `HttpWgetLane`, parses child playlist lines plus `URI=` attributes such as init maps and keys, resolves child URLs through the playlist base URL, recursively fetches up to 16 playlists and 256 assets, de-duplicates URLs, collision-safes filenames, and writes a `browser-hls-package.json` index beside the fetched playlists, init/key files, and segments. Direct image/audio/video media requests still use the single-file fetch path, and normal materialized browser outputs still copy unchanged. This closes the HLS playlist traversal and segment/key/init expansion hook for observed HLS URLs; DASH MPD traversal and richer offline playlist rewriting remain follow-up downloader hooks.   **Power-mode HLS offline playlist rewrite slice 2026-07-10:** HLS Browser media packages are now self-contained for local playback instead of leaving fetched playlists pointed at network-relative child paths. After the bounded playlist/asset traversal completes, `BrowserDownloadLane` rewrites each saved playlist's segment lines and `URI=` attributes to the collision-safe local package filenames, records `offline_rewrite_status=completed` and `root_playlist_path` in `browser-hls-package.json`, and keeps the existing 16-playlist/256-asset caps and duplicate suppression. This closes the HLS offline playlist rewrite hook; DASH offline MPD rewriting remains open.   **Power-mode DASH package downloader slice 2026-07-10:** DASH Browser media request files now package more than the root MPD. `BrowserDownloadLane` promotes `kind=dash` / `.mpd` request files into bounded DASH package mode, fetches the MPD with the existing `HttpWgetLane`, parses MPD `BaseURL`, `Representation` id/bandwidth, `SegmentTemplate` initialization/media templates, `sourceURL`, and `SegmentTimeline` segment numbers, expands `$RepresentationID$`, `$Bandwidth$`, `$Number$`, and `$Number%0Nd$`, resolves child URLs through the MPD/BaseURL context, fetches up to 256 init/source/segment assets, de-duplicates URLs, collision-safes filenames, and writes `browser-dash-package.json` beside the MPD and fetched assets. This closes the first bounded DASH MPD traversal/segment expansion hook for observed DASH URLs; full DASH profile semantics, multi-period/adaptation scoping, DRM/license acquisition, and offline manifest rewriting remain follow-up downloader hooks.   **Power-mode prompted device permission slice 2026-07-10:** The Power menu now exposes prompt actions for camera, microphone, location, notifications, and clipboard on the current first-party site. Each prompt records an explicit per-site deny decision, updates the Privacy permission summary, and publishes a typed `action/browser/permission-prompt` handoff with permission/site/url/engine/decision/enforcement metadata while helper enforcement remains default-deny. This is intentionally not a native engine allow/grant path, browser-device callback implementation, or live hardware/API access until Servo/CEF permission callbacks can honor site decisions.
