  **BROWSER-DD-8 reconciliation 2026-07-10:** the slices above already closed most of this item's acceptance — Power-mode toggle, full Chromium DevTools (loopback discovery + active-target selection), view-source, UA-switch (device-profile presets), prompted device APIs (camera/mic/location/notifications/clipboard, default-deny), the native media downloader (HLS/DASH manifest sniffing + real segment/key/init package fetch + offline playlist/MPD rewrite, all-images batch, ignore-blocking retrieval, auto-rename), and the full site scraper (extract/crawl-seed/text/DOM-links-headings/article-body/crawl-manifest, export to JSON/CSV/MD, plus a bounded depth-1 daemon-owned crawl execution lane) are real, tested, and wired through the shared Transfers queue (crediting TRANSFERS-10's producer hook, not a second download manager). Userscripts are shared credit with BROWSER-DD-11's curated library. Operator review found and fixed one bug: the `TogglePowerMode` View-menu item was missing its page-gate — every sibling page-scoped View item calls `.enabled(...)`, this one didn't, so Power Mode stayed toggleable with no tab open even though its features need a live page — fixed by adding `.enabled(can_tools)` to match the adjacent `CycleContainer` gate. Farm evidence (post-fix): full `mde-shell-egui` `web::tests` module = **139 passed, 0 failed**. **Honestly still open** (self-documented in-app as captions, not invented here): Servo DevTools (CEF-only for now), native device emulation beyond page-visible viewport/UA metadata, native network-header UA rewriting, full DASH/HLS DRM and multi-period/adaptation semantics, and crawl/scrape recursion beyond same-origin depth-1. Marker moves `[ ]` → `[>]` to reflect the real, substantial, tested implementation while those residual gaps stay open.
- [ ] **BROWSER-DD-9: media + mesh conferencing.** **Acceptance:** full codecs (incl. H.264/H.265) + full WebRTC + PiP + background audio + media keys + GPU/HW video decode; block-all autoplay; a built-in MULTI-PARTY mesh conferencing app over WebRTC on the Nebula overlay (screen-share, peer-directory participants, phone ties).
  - **Block-all autoplay default slice 2026-07-14:** the Browser shell now treats autoplay blocking as the per-tab default policy instead of a dormant operator toggle. New Servo/CEF tabs immediately mirror `SetAutoplayBlocked { blocked: true }` into the helper, Clear Current Tab Data resets back to the same block-all default, and respawn-on-reload replays the tab's current autoplay policy into the fresh helper so crash recovery cannot silently drop the media policy. This closes only DD-9's block-all-autoplay policy activation; codecs, WebRTC, PiP, background audio, media keys, GPU/HW decode, and mesh conferencing remain open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-autoplay-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-autoplay-web` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web::tests -- --nocapture` passed **337/337**; `.170` slot `browser-autoplay-fmt` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Background-audio idle-suspend guard slice 2026-07-14:** inactive Browser tabs that report `AudioState { audible: true }` and are not muted are now exempt from the shell's idle-tab suspension path, so the Browser no longer sends `Stop` to a background tab that is actively producing audio. Muted or silent inactive tabs still suspend after the idle timeout, and the existing one-shot `action/browser/tab-suspend` handoff remains intact for truly idle tabs. This is an operational background-audio guard; CEF audio sample playout, media keys, codecs, WebRTC, PiP, GPU/HW decode, and conferencing remain open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-audio-idle` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper idle -- --nocapture` passed **16/16** including `audible_inactive_tabs_are_not_idle_suspended`; `.130` slot `browser-audio-web` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper web::tests -- --nocapture` passed **338/338**; `.170` slot `browser-audio-fmt` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Browser media play/pause control slice 2026-07-14:** Browser now has a first-class `ToggleMediaPlayback` helper control plus `WebSession::toggle_media_playback`; CEF and Servo both execute a bounded `audio,video` page script that pauses currently-playing media or marks the target media element as user-allowed for the Browser autoplay blocker before calling `play()`. The View menu exposes `Play/Pause Media`, and the shell's fixed host-key table now forwards Linux `KEY_PLAY`/`XF86AudioPlay` as a typed `MediaPlayPause` action routed to the Browser active tab. This is an active-tab play/pause media-key slice only; full media-key set/now-playing routing, MPRIS integration, PiP, codecs, WebRTC, GPU/HW decode, and conferencing remain open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-media-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-wire control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-media-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_zoom_and_find_controls_are_framed_for_the_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml media_playback_toggle_script -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-media-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_media_playback_toggle_script -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper media -- --nocapture` passed **18/18**; `.50` slot `browser-media-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-media-seat` `cargo test -p mde-seat hotkeys -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.130` slot `browser-media-hostkeys` `cargo test -p mde-egui hostkeys -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.50` slot `browser-media-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` passed. A `.170` shell hotkey attempt hit a farm disk-full/mold write failure before rerunning the shell gates successfully on `.50`.
  - **Browser media transport key set slice 2026-07-14:** Browser now has a typed `MediaTransport { action }` helper control plus `WebSession::media_transport`, with Play/Pause, Play, Pause, Stop, Next, and Previous encoded on the wire and handled by both CEF and Servo through bounded `audio,video` page scripts. The shell keeps the existing View-menu `Play/Pause Media` compatibility control, but host-first hardware media keys now route active-tab transport actions through the fixed table: Linux `KEY_PLAY`, `KEY_PLAYPAUSE`, and `KEY_PLAYCD` map to Play/Pause; `KEY_PAUSECD` maps to Pause; `KEY_STOPCD` maps to Stop; `KEY_NEXTSONG` maps to Next; and `KEY_PREVIOUSSONG` maps to Previous. This closes the active-tab media-key set transport slice only; MPRIS/now-playing integration, PiP, codecs, WebRTC, GPU/HW decode, conferencing, and native engine-level media-session metadata remain open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-media-transport-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-wire control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-media-transport-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_zoom_and_find_controls_are_framed_for_the_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-transport-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml media_transport_script -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-transport-servo-lib` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml --lib servo_media_transport_script -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-transport-seat` `cargo test -p mde-seat hotkeys -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.130` slot `browser-media-transport-hostkeys` `cargo test -p mde-egui hostkeys -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.50` slot `browser-media-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper hotkeys -- --nocapture` passed **11/11**; `.130` slot `browser-media-shell-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-transport-fmt` `cargo fmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` passed. A broader `.130` Servo test attempt reached link then failed with `mold: failed to write to an output file. Disk full?`; the final `.50 --lib` Servo gate above is the code evidence for this script slice.
  - **CEF camera/microphone media-access permission prerequisite slice 2026-07-14:** CEF's `on_request_media_access_permission` no longer returns a blanket default-deny for device camera/microphone requests. It now maps CEF device audio/video capture bits to the existing session-only Browser permission prompt (`kind` 3 camera, 4 microphone, 5 camera+microphone), holds the correct `cef_media_access_callback_t`, and answers CEF with the requested media bitmask on allow or `0` on deny. Desktop capture remains default-deny. At landing time this did **not** turn WebRTC on because `webrtc_block_script` still removed the JS WebRTC/getUserMedia surface; the follow-up CEF surface/device-visibility slice below supersedes that blocker. Servo remains `dom_webrtc_enabled=false` with the dummy media backend, and remaining DD-9 conferencing work is still open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-webrtc-perm-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml permission -- --nocapture` passed **9/9** including the media-access allow/deny callback tests; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-perm-wire2` `cargo test -p mde-web-wire messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-perm-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client permission -- --nocapture` passed **6/6**; `.50` slot `browser-webrtc-perm-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper permission -- --nocapture` passed **10/10**; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-perm-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  - **CEF browser-page WebRTC surface/device-visibility slice 2026-07-14:** CEF no longer injects `webrtc_block_script` by default; legacy API removal is now explicit via `MDE_CEF_WEBRTC_BLOCKED=1`. The shared sandbox now binds only `/dev/snd` and `/dev/videoN` capture devices when present, with no broad `/dev` exposure, while retaining the existing `/dev/dri` GPU path. Combined with the prior native media permission callback, this makes CEF `RTCPeerConnection`/`getUserMedia` reachable for real browser pages under the IP-handling policy and session-only camera/microphone prompt. Servo remains disabled; mesh conferencing/multi-party, PiP, screen-share, and GPU/HW decode remain open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-webrtc-surface-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml webrtc -- --nocapture` passed **4/4** including the default-operational env parser and opt-in JS block regressions; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-surface-perm` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml permission -- --nocapture` passed **9/9**; `.50` slot `browser-webrtc-sandbox` `cargo test --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-sandbox/Cargo.toml media_device -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-surface-fmt-cef` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-surface-fmt-sandbox` `cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-sandbox/Cargo.toml -- --check` passed; `.130` slot `browser-webrtc-surface-fmt-root` `cargo fmt --check` passed; `.50` slot `browser-webrtc-cef-ui-compile` shell live-smoke compile path passed **1/1**; `.50` slot `browser-webrtc-cef-render` `cargo build --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml` passed, and a direct farm runtime smoke against `/home/mm/mde-cef-active` ran `mde-web-cef render-once --url https://example.com/ --width 800 --height 600`, emitted `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK`, and delivered `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... view=800x600 ... last_paint=800x600`; `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Browser now-playing metadata slice 2026-07-15:** Browser now carries bounded page/media-session metadata over the shared helper protocol as `EventMsg::MediaMetadata`. CEF polls `navigator.mediaSession` plus active `audio,video` elements and publishes through an intercepted `mde-media.invalid` beacon that never reaches the network; Servo mirrors the same bounded script through its `evaluate_javascript` callback. `WebSession` stores the latest body and clears on an empty update, and the Browser tab hover/status cluster surfaces a compact `Now: ...` chip only when real media/session metadata is present, so ordinary non-media pages stay quiet. This is the now-playing/status slice only: MPRIS/desktop now-playing ownership, PiP, full codecs, WebRTC conferencing, screen share, and GPU/HW decode remain open. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-media-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-wire -- --nocapture` passed **10/10**; `.90` slot `browser-media-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client media_metadata -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `.50` slot `browser-media-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml media_metadata -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` slot `browser-media-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_media_metadata -- --nocapture` passed **1/1** after the expected cold Servo build; `.130` slot `browser-media-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper media_metadata -- --nocapture` and `now_playing_metadata -- --nocapture` each passed **1/1**; `.170` slots `browser-media-fmt2`, `browser-media-cef-fmt2`, and `browser-media-servo-fmt2` passed workspace, CEF, and Servo `cargo fmt ... --check` respectively.
  - **Browser retained media status slice 2026-07-15:** the Browser shell now publishes a deduped retained `state/browser-media/<node>` status whenever page/media-session metadata changes, including the selected media tab's stable id/index, engine, URL/title context, audible/muted flags, compact label, public metadata fields, play/pause/idle state, and an idle clear when metadata disappears. Selection prefers the active media tab, then audible background media, then any tab with metadata, so background audio is visible to the later MPRIS/KDE Connect bridge without claiming full desktop media ownership yet. This is a Browser-owned platform state seam only: full MPRIS implementation, PiP, codecs, WebRTC conferencing/screen-share, and GPU/HW decode remain open. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-media-status-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_media_status -- --nocapture` passed **3/3** on BigBoy against the final tree; `.170` slot `browser-media-status-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check` passed; `.50` slot `browser-media-status-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client media_metadata -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `.90` slot `browser-media-status-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-wire control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Browser KDC media-control bridge slice 2026-07-15:** KDC now folds the Browser's retained `state/browser-media/<node>` status into KDE Connect MPRIS reports as `mde-browser`, including player-list and now-playing responses, and Browser-targeted phone MPRIS commands publish `action/browser/media-control/<node>` instead of falling through to `playerctl`. The Browser shell consumes those node-addressed actions and applies them to the explicit tab id when present, otherwise the selected media tab (active media, audible background, any metadata, then active tab), so paired phones can pause/play/next/previous/stop Browser media without inventing a separate phone transport. This is the KDE Connect bridge over the existing Browser state seam, not a full freedesktop MPRIS D-Bus service; volume, PiP, codecs, WebRTC conferencing/screen-share, and GPU/HW decode remain open. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-kdc-media-control` `cargo test -p mackesd workers::kdc_host --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **61/61**; `.50` slot `browser-media-control-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_media -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.90` slot `browser-media-control-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-wire control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `.170` slot `browser-kdc-media-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  - **Design doc landed 2026-07-10:** `docs/design/browser-dd9-webrtc-rescope.md` re-scopes this item — confirmed genuinely **zero prior code** (no worker/test/struct under any webrtc/getUserMedia/conferencing spelling, unlike its shipped DD-1/2/3/5-8/11/12 siblings) — and picks an engine by reading the actual pinned sources, the DD-2 discipline: **CEF, not Servo.** CEF's only blocker is one launch switch, `--disable-webrtc` (`cef_init.rs`'s `chromium_privacy_switches()`), whose real-world effect is itself unverified (not found in a comprehensive community Chromium-switches reference — may be a no-op, a real open question independent of DD-9); Servo has real DOM code (`rtcpeerconnection.rs` et al., 30KB+, verified against the actual pinned 0.3.0 crates.io release the same way DD-2 verified the cancel-load gap) but ships `dom_webrtc_enabled: false` by default upstream (open tracking issue servo/servo#41396 "Enable WebRTC by default," plus a 6-year-open `addTransceiver` gap, #26860), resolves the no-op `servo-media-dummy` backend rather than the real `servo-media-gstreamer` one in this workspace's own `Cargo.lock`, and this codebase separately disables it on purpose already (`engine.rs`: `dom_webrtc_enabled: false, // no local-IP leak`). **Mesh-specific finding:** Nebula's already-shipped direct→relay→HTTPS-tunnel cascade (`mackes-transport::peer_path::PeerPath`/`TransportKind`, fed by the real STUN client in `mackesd/src/stun.rs`) means a mesh-scoped call needs no external STUN/TURN server at all — ICE can scope to the Nebula tun IP only (which also sidesteps the local-IP-leak concern behind today's hardening), signaling rides the existing Bus (the `mesh-chat-icq.md` pub/sub pattern). DD-9 also turns out to bundle two deliverables: browser-page WebRTC compat (CEF-only, mesh-independent) and "phone ties"/1:1 peer calling, which **already ships** via the unrelated-protocol VOIP-GW epic (SIP/RTP, `mde-voice-hud`/`mde-voice-egui`, 7/7 closed, incl. real PSTN bridging) — multi-party is the one part that's genuinely unstarted on every path (grepped: no conference/mixer/SFU/b2bua code anywhere, WebRTC or SIP side). Recommended first slice: CEF-only 1:1 call between two named peers, Nebula-scoped ICE, Bus signaling, received audio tagged `mde.mesh.peer` so it lands in the already-shipped E12-16 mixer's `StripOrigin::MeshRemote` for free (zero mixer changes). Screen-share (no compositor portal exists — this shell is DRM-native, no Wayland) and real PiP (no floating/overlay-window concept anywhere in `mde-shell-egui`'s single-`Surface`-at-a-time dock model) both need new, WebRTC-independent shell capabilities not sized here. Design/options only at that time; later 2026-07-14 DD-9 sub-slices moved media policy/WebRTC prerequisites into code while conferencing remains open.
  - **`--disable-webrtc` open question resolved 2026-07-10:** the "unverified... may be a no-op" flag in the design-doc sub-bullet above is resolved, independent of DD-9's own scope/timeline (DD-9 itself remains not-started, marker unchanged). Confirmed **not** a real Chromium switch (verified against the live Chromium source, not just the community switches reference the design doc cites) and fixed — see the BROWSER-DD-1 sub-bullet above and `docs/THREAT_MODEL.md` §8 for the full evidence trail and the renderer-level replacement mitigation. At the time this was a hardening-correctness fix, not DD-9 feature enablement; the later 2026-07-14 DD-9 slice enables CEF browser-page WebRTC while Servo remains disabled.
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-10: browser chrome + UX.** **Acceptance:** fixed Quasar toolbar; container tabs; force-dark (Quasar-tuned) on light sites; full download manager (fixed folder); built-in PDF viewer; full print/save-as-PDF (CUPS); full capture (full-page/region/PDF-MHTML/annotate); reader mode; per-tab audio mute; page zoom; find-in-page; multi-display. **Compact Chromium-style chrome slice 2026-07-07:** the Browser workspace chrome is now denser and more Chromium-like: horizontal and vertical tab strips render compact tab pills with inline close controls, tab reorder moved into the tab context menu instead of visible per-tab arrow buttons, Servo/CEF new-tab affordances are compact `+Servo` / `+CEF` buttons, and the address row uses a compact arrow Go button. This preserves the existing two-engine tab model and page-action seams while reducing toolbar height and visual clutter. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper tab_strip -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_ -- --nocapture` passed **11/11**, including `browser_body_input_is_localized_and_keyboard_is_focus_gated` and `browser_panel_click_focuses_page_and_sends_keyboard_input_to_helper`, confirming the compact chrome did not regress page keyboard forwarding. **Zoom/find protocol slice 2026-07-08:** added real `SetZoom`, `FindInPage`, and `ClearFind` helper controls plus typed `WebSession` methods; CEF applies bounded page zoom and page search through its JS execution seam, while Servo decodes the controls and honestly no-ops until a real Servo find/zoom hook exists. The Browser View menu now exposes Zoom In, Zoom Out, Actual Size, and Find in Page, the compact find bar submits forward/backward searches, and the status cluster shows active zoom/find state. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_zoom_and_find_controls_are_framed_for_the_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml page_zoom_and_find_scripts_are_bounded_and_escaped -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml` passed. **Per-tab audio mute slice 2026-07-08:** added real `SetAudioMuted` helper control plus `WebSession::set_audio_muted`; CEF applies it through the pinned CEF 149 `cef_browser_host_t::set_audio_muted` slot (`offset=520`, derived from the farm runtime headers and asserted in the ABI layout test), while Servo support is completed in the later page-script mute slice below. The Browser stores mute per tab, exposes Mute/Unmute in the View menu and tab context menus, marks muted tabs/status with a compact indicator, clears mute on Clear Current Tab Data, and sends mute/unmute frames only to the active tab. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_zoom_and_find_controls_are_framed_for_the_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml audio_mute_control_uses_cef_host_audio_slot -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Force-dark slice 2026-07-08:** added real `SetForceDark` helper control plus `WebSession::set_force_dark`; CEF installs/removes a bounded Quasar-tuned forced-dark stylesheet through the existing JS execution seam, while Servo decodes the control and honestly no-ops until a native Servo color-scheme hook exists. The Browser stores force-dark per tab, exposes Enable/Disable Force Dark in the View menu and tab context menus, marks tabs/status with compact dark indicators, clears force-dark on Clear Current Tab Data, and sends enable/disable frames only to the active tab. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_zoom_and_find_controls_are_framed_for_the_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml force_dark_script_installs_and_clears_bounded_style -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Container tabs slice 2026-07-08:** added named per-tab container profiles (No Container, Personal, Work, Banking, Research) on top of the existing one-helper-session-per-tab isolation model. The tab strip context menu can assign a container, the View menu can cycle the active tab container, tab labels/hover text expose compact container markers, and the status cluster shows the active non-default container. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper container_tabs_are_named_per_tab_and_visible_in_chrome -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper tab_strip -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Download-manager UI slice 2026-07-08:** the Browser now owns a compact Chromium-style downloads button and drawer backed by the shared `mde-files-egui::transfers` ledger, filtered to `browser_download` jobs. It shows active/history rows, progress, verify/error state, worker-missing/empty states, and dispatches Pause/Resume/Cancel through the same `TransfersClient` verbs as Files. The View menu can show/hide Downloads and the status cluster marks active browser downloads; the existing browser-output and scraper-output producers are now production helpers over the shared Transfers queue instead of test-only code. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_download -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_toggles_downloads -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_page_family_items_disable_without_a_live_page -- --nocapture` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Native Servo page-tools slice 2026-07-08:** Servo now applies the existing Zoom/Find/Clear Find/Force Dark controls through its `Engine::evaluate_javascript` page seam instead of decoding them as no-ops. The script builders are bounded and escaped, matching the CEF behavior while preserving the existing wire protocol; Servo tab-audio mute is covered by the later page-script mute slice; a deeper native audio-session API remains optional. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml` passed; `.50` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_page_tool_scripts_are_bounded_and_escaped -- --nocapture` passed, compiling the full test harness including the force-dark script regression. A redundant follow-up force-dark-only rerun was stopped when it began a fresh cold Servo rebuild to avoid wasting farm capacity. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Reader mode slice 2026-07-08:** added a real per-tab Reader Mode control across the Browser shell, wire protocol, CEF helper, and Servo helper. The View menu and tab context menus can enable/disable Reader Mode; tabs and the status cluster show compact reader indicators; Clear Current Tab Data disables it. CEF and Servo apply the same bounded reader stylesheet through their page-script seams, hiding common navigation/ad chrome and constraining article text width without changing the wire protocol.   **Node send-tab inbox slice 2026-07-08:** Browser now scans the daemon-owned local and Syncthing-backed send-tab outbox for concrete node records addressed to the current host (`browser-send-tab/node/<this-host>/<source>/<event>.json`), validates the `browser_send_tab` payload, enqueues accepted URLs through the existing live-helper foreground-tab path, dedupes local/share copies of the same event, and unlinks consumed records so they do not replay every launch. Phone-targeted and other-node records are ignored for their owning bridge/node. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_send_tab -- --nocapture` passed **6/6**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_startup_restore -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued.
  **Phone send-tab bridge slice 2026-07-08:** `mackesd` now turns phone-targeted Browser send-tab handoffs into the existing KDE Connect share verb instead of inventing a second phone transport. The `browser_session_sync` owner still writes and mirrors the durable `browser-send-tab/phone/<device-id>/<source>/<event>.json` record, then publishes `action/connect/share` with `device_id=<target-id>`, `url`, `open=true`, source metadata, and the handoff id; the KDC host worker remains the paired-phone delivery owner and continues to reject unpaired devices honestly. Farm evidence: `.90` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_session_sync --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **10/10**; `.50` `cargo test -p mackesd connect_verb_share_requires_paired_and_enqueues_file_or_url --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_session_sync.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_session_sync.rs` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued.
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-11: accessibility + TTS + voice + userscript library.** **Acceptance:** screen-reader (accesskit) + forced-dark + keyboard nav + a best-of-breed open-source TTS engine (Piper/Kokoro-class) + voice commands/dictation (on-device STT) + offline-hunspell spellcheck; a bundled library of ~100 curated userscripts for major sites (YouTube/NPR/Spotify/news…). **Forced-dark + page keyboard-input slice 2026-07-09:** Browser already has a real View-menu/tab-menu force-dark toggle, per-tab force-dark state, status chip, persisted session state, and `ControlMsg::SetForceDark` dispatch to the live helper; both Servo and CEF helpers inject and clear bounded forced-dark page styling. The browser body also has click-latched keyboard focus so address-bar/chrome typing does not leak into pages, while focused pages receive key and committed-text input through the helper control socket. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-dd11-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-dd11-input` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_panel_click_focuses_page_and_sends_keyboard_input_to_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-dd11-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_force_dark_script_installs_and_clears_bounded_style -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-dd11-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml force_dark_script_installs_and_clears_bounded_style -- --nocapture` passed. **Curated userscript library slices 2026-07-09:** added a typed `SetUserScripts` helper control plus `WebSession::set_user_scripts`, implemented shell-curated userscript execution/cleanup in both Servo and CEF page-script seams, and exposed a per-tab Browser View/tab-menu toggle with tab/status indicators and session-sync state. The bundled library now comes from a structured 100-rule Rust registry generated into the helper script, covering host-specific cleanup/readability fixes across video/music, news, docs/developer references, social feeds, commerce, recipes, and learning sites while preserving explicit cleanup on disable. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-userscripts-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control -- --nocapture` passed `3/3`; `.130` slot `browser-userscripts-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper userscript -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-userscripts-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-userscripts-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-userscripts-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml userscript_library_script_runs_and_cleans_the_bundle -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-userscripts-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_userscript_library_script_runs_and_cleans_the_bundle -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-userscripts-100-shell-rerun` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper userscript -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-userscripts-100-control` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-userscripts-100-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-userscripts-formatcheck` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui -p mde-web-preview-client -p mde-vdi-vnc` passed, plus the excluded Servo/CEF helper manifest format checks from the first slice. **Browser AccessKit shell/page semantics slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser now emits a polite AccessKit `Browser status` live region with active tab count, engine, title, URL, security state, container, display target, and enabled page tools, plus a named `Browser page` node over the painted page canvas so screen readers have a stable page region and focus hint instead of only unlabeled texture chrome. This is shell/page-canvas semantics, not a claim that helper DOM trees are bridged yet. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-a11y-shell-rerun` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_page_exports_accesskit_status_and_clickable_page_region -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-a11y-input` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_panel_click_focuses_page_and_sends_keyboard_input_to_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-a11y-format-rerun` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui -p mde-web-preview-client -p mde-vdi-vnc` passed; `git diff --check` passed. **Remaining:** helper DOM-level AccessKit semantics, TTS, on-device STT/voice commands, offline Hunspell spellcheck, and ongoing live-site tuning for the curated userscript rules.
  **Browser spell-runtime packaging slice 2026-07-09:** the full RPM now hard-requires `hunspell` and `hunspell-en-US`, matching the editor `hunspell -a` engine and giving Browser spellcheck a shipped offline runtime/dictionary instead of an operator-installed assumption. This does not claim page spell underlines yet; BROWSER-DD-11 remaining Hunspell work is the helper page-text bridge plus shell/UI integration. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-hunspell-rpm` `cargo test -p mackesd base_rpm_ships_and_enables_the_drm_seat_unit --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-hunspell-rustfmt` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/onboard/role_provision.rs` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser page-text protocol slice 2026-07-09:** added typed `RequestPageText { id, max_bytes }` and `PageText { id, text }` helper frames, client-side request/drain APIs, and a Servo implementation that extracts bounded visible page text via the JavaScript callback with UTF-8 byte clamping. CEF explicitly no-ops the request in this slice until its separate beacon/callback path is wired, so this is the transport seam for Browser Hunspell/TTS, not page underlines yet. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-pagetext-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_text -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-pagetext-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-pagetext-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client page_zoom_and_find_controls_are_framed_for_the_helper -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-pagetext-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_page_text_script_is_bounded_and_utf8_clamp_is_safe -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-pagetext-cef` `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml` passed; `.90` slot `browser-pagetext-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-web-preview-client` passed; `.90` slot `browser-pagetext-servo-fmt2` and `.50` slot `browser-pagetext-cef-fmt2` excluded-helper format checks passed.
  **CEF page-text beacon slice 2026-07-09:** CEF now handles `RequestPageText` instead of no-oping it: the helper injects a bounded `mde-page-text://capture/<id>?text=...` beacon script, intercepts that internal scheme before the normal ad-filter `ResourceRequest` path, decodes/UTF-8-clamps the payload, publishes `PageText`, and cancels the fake resource so it never reaches the network. This closes the CEF half of the Browser page-text transport seam; remaining Hunspell work is shell spellcheck/underline UI and applying misses to page text. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-cef-pagetext-tests` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml page_text -- --nocapture` passed `2/2`; `.90` slot `browser-cef-pagetext-check` `cargo check --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml` passed; `.50` slot `browser-cef-pagetext-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml` passed.
  **Browser offline Hunspell shell slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser View menu now exposes `Check Spelling`, requests bounded helper page text through the typed `RequestPageText` seam, runs the existing `mde-editor-egui` Hunspell engine off the UI thread, and reports no-text/unavailable/no-miss/miss-count plus first-suggestion notices without assuming the runtime is present outside the full RPM. This is a real shell spellcheck path, not visual page underlines or correction application yet; remaining Hunspell work is page-text miss mapping, underlines, walk/corrections, and DOM-aware integration. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-shell-spell-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-shell-spell-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-shell-spell-notice` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper spellcheck_notice_summarizes_results_without_hunspell -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-shell-spell-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
 **Browser spellcheck result drawer slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now retains the latest Hunspell page-text result and renders a `Spelling` drawer with miss words, character ranges, suggestions, copy, close, and unavailable-error states instead of only dropping a transient notice. This still does not claim DOM underlines, page correction application, or DOM-aware miss walking. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-spell-drawer-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_spellcheck -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` slot `browser-spell-drawer-controls` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper page_zoom_and_find_actions_send_helper_controls -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-spell-drawer-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-spell-drawer-fmt` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui` passed.
 **Browser spellcheck page-highlight slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser shell now sends bounded Hunspell miss words back through a typed `SetSpellcheckHighlights` helper control after the page-text spellcheck completes. Servo and CEF consume that control through their existing page-script seams, clear prior marks, and wrap matching visible text in `mde-browser-spell-miss` spans with wavy underline styling. This is a real page-visible highlight path for offline spellcheck misses; correction application and DOM-aware range walking remain separate follow-ups because the current page-text extractor normalizes whitespace before Hunspell. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-spell-highlight-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_spellcheck -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.50` slot `browser-spell-highlight-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-spell-highlight-cef2` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml spellcheck_highlight_script_marks_and_clears_words -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-spell-highlight-servo2` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_spellcheck_highlight_script_marks_and_clears_words -- --nocapture` passed; farm-side format checks passed on `.50` (`cargo fmt --check -p mde-web-preview-client -p mde-shell-egui`), `.170` (`cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check`), and `.130` (`cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --check`).
 **Browser spellcheck correction slice 2026-07-09:** the `Spelling` drawer now turns Hunspell suggestions into real apply actions instead of passive text. Browser results retain the originating tab index, and selecting a suggestion sends a typed `ApplySpellcheckCorrection { word, replacement }` helper control to that tab. Servo and CEF handle the control through bounded DOM scripts that first replace a matching `mde-browser-spell-miss` mark, then fall back to the first matching visible text node while skipping form/script/style nodes. This lands a first correction path without claiming full DOM-aware range walking or multi-occurrence correction UX. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-spell-correct-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_spellcheck -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` slot `browser-spell-correct-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-spell-correct-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml spellcheck_correction_script_replaces_mark_or_text -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-spell-correct-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_spellcheck_correction_script_replaces_mark_or_text -- --nocapture` passed.
 **Browser read-aloud handoff slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser View menu now exposes `Read Aloud`, requests bounded helper page text through the same `RequestPageText` seam used by Hunspell, and publishes a bounded typed `action/browser/read-aloud` Bus request with tab index, engine, URL, title, source host, text length, and truncation metadata. This is a real shell/page-text TTS handoff, not a claim that Piper/Kokoro synthesis or audio playback is implemented yet; remaining TTS work is the daemon/player owner plus offline voice assets. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-readaloud-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_read_aloud -- --nocapture` passed `2/2`; `.90` slot `browser-readaloud-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-readaloud-fmt2` `cargo fmt --check -p mde-shell-egui` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser read-aloud daemon owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now drains `action/browser/read-aloud` on Workstation nodes through a registered `browser_read_aloud` worker, validates the Browser page-text payload, invokes a configured offline TTS command (`MDE_BROWSER_TTS_COMMAND` / `MDE_TTS_COMMAND`) when present, and publishes `state/browser-read-aloud/<node>` with `speaking`/`spoken`/`unavailable`/`error` status. Missing Piper/Kokoro command/model is surfaced as unavailable rather than fake playback; remaining TTS work is packaging voice assets/default command and live audio verification. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-tts-worker4` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_read_aloud --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `5/5`; `.90` slot `browser-tts-role4` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `19/19`; `.130` slot `browser-tts-filefmt3` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_read_aloud.rs` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser read-aloud default command packaging slice 2026-07-09:** the full Workstation RPM now ships `/usr/libexec/mackesd/browser-read-aloud-tts`, and the `browser_read_aloud` worker uses that packaged wrapper as its default TTS command when no `MDE_BROWSER_TTS_COMMAND` / `MDE_TTS_COMMAND` override is set. The wrapper speaks through Piper only when `piper`, a local voice model (`MDE_BROWSER_TTS_MODEL` / `MDE_TTS_MODEL`, default `/usr/share/magic-mesh/tts/browser-read-aloud.onnx`), and `paplay`/`aplay` are present; otherwise it exits with an unavailable code that the worker maps to `state:"unavailable"` rather than fake playback. The headless server RPM does not ship the desktop TTS wrapper. Remaining TTS work is bundling or provisioning an approved offline voice model/runtime and live audio verification. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-tts-default-worker2` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_read_aloud --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed `8/8`; `.90` slot `browser-tts-default-rpm2` `cargo test -p mackesd full_rpm_ships_browser_read_aloud_tts_wrapper_but_server_variant_does_not --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-tts-default-fmt4` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_read_aloud.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/onboard/role_provision.rs` passed; local `bash -n install-helpers/browser-read-aloud-tts.sh` passed; `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser read-aloud voice provisioning slice 2026-07-09:** the full Workstation RPM now ships a role-gated `mde-browser-tts-voice-setup.service`, a sha256-pinned `browser-read-aloud-voice.env` manifest, and `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-browser-tts-voice` to install an approved Piper `.onnx` model plus optional `.onnx.json` config into `/usr/share/magic-mesh/tts/voices/` and activate `/usr/share/magic-mesh/tts/browser-read-aloud.onnx` for the default wrapper. The default manifest intentionally exits `78` until an approved model URL/SHA is supplied, so this is a real provisioning path, not a fake bundled voice claim; remaining TTS work is selecting/bundling or mirroring the approved voice model/runtime and live audio verification.   **Browser voice-command shell handoff slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser View menu now exposes `Voice Command` and `Dictate` actions that publish typed `action/browser/voice-command` requests with active tab index, engine, URL, title, address-bar draft, focus target, mode (`command`/`dictation`), host, and transcript budget. This is the real Browser-side command/dictation request path; it does not fake microphone capture or STT locally.   **Browser voice-command daemon owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_voice_command` worker that drains `action/browser/voice-command`, validates Browser command/dictation payloads, invokes an operator-configured offline STT/capture command (`MDE_BROWSER_STT_COMMAND` / `MDE_STT_COMMAND`) when present, publishes retained `state/browser-voice-command/<node>` with `listening`/`transcribed`/`unavailable`/`error` status, and emits bounded `event/browser-voice-command/<node>` transcript events. Remaining voice work is choosing/filling the approved offline STT runtime/model manifest for production images.   **Browser voice-command transcript consumption slice 2026-07-09:** the Browser shell now polls `event/browser-voice-command/<local-node>` with a retained cursor, validates `browser_voice_transcript` result events from the daemon STT owner, applies a conservative Browser-owned command set (`new tab`, `close tab`, `back`, `forward`, `reload`, `read aloud`, and page find), and forwards dictation into the helper as committed text only when the originating tab is still open and the page canvas owns focus. Unsupported transcripts surface as notices rather than arbitrary automation. Remaining voice work is choosing/filling the approved offline STT runtime/model manifest for production images.   **Browser voice-command STT packaging slice 2026-07-09:** the full Workstation RPM now ships `/usr/libexec/mackesd/browser-voice-command-stt` as the daemon's default STT/capture command, plus a sha256-pinned optional model manifest (`browser-voice-command-stt.env`), installer (`install-browser-stt-model`), and Workstation-gated setup unit (`mde-browser-stt-model-setup.service`). The wrapper exits 69 until a local model, capture path, and `MDE_BROWSER_STT_TRANSCRIBE_COMMAND`/`MDE_STT_TRANSCRIBE_COMMAND` offline runtime are configured; the installer exits 78 while the manifest is intentionally unfilled. The server variant does not ship these desktop STT assets. Remaining voice work is choosing/filling the approved offline STT runtime/model manifest for production images.   **Browser speech owner status display slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now polls the daemon-owned retained `state/browser-read-aloud/<local-node>` and `state/browser-voice-command/<local-node>` topics, validates the TTS/STT worker status records, and surfaces active/problem states in chrome without claiming fake playback or transcripts. The menubar status cluster now shows compact `TTS speaking/spoken/unavailable` and `Voice`/`Dictation` chips when the workers have meaningful state, while a Browser speech drawer shows the active page, counters, transcript length, and real unavailable/error reasons. **Browser spellcheck replace-all correction slice 2026-07-09:** the `Spelling` drawer suggestions now expose an `all` action that sends a typed `ApplySpellcheckCorrectionAll { word, replacement }` helper control through `WebSession`. Servo and CEF handle it with bounded scripts that replace all current `mde-browser-spell-miss` marks for that word, or, when no marks match, replace up to 512 visible text-node occurrences while skipping form/script/style nodes. This narrows the remaining spellcheck tail without claiming full DOM-aware range walking or per-occurrence selection UI. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-spell-all-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_spellcheck -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.90` slot `browser-spell-all-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-spell-all-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml spellcheck_correction_script_replaces_mark_or_text -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-spell-all-servo2` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_spellcheck_correction_script_replaces_mark_or_text -- --nocapture` passed; format checks passed on `.50` (`cargo fmt --check -p mde-web-preview-client -p mde-shell-egui`), `.90` (`cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --check`), and `.170` (`cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check`).
**Browser spellcheck indexed occurrence correction slice 2026-07-09:** ordinary suggestion clicks in the `Spelling` drawer now target the selected miss row instead of always correcting the first visible match. The shell computes a zero-based occurrence index among prior matching miss rows, sends `ApplySpellcheckCorrectionAt { word, replacement, occurrence }` through the typed helper protocol and `WebSession`, and Servo/CEF replace that indexed `mde-browser-spell-miss` mark or, when no marks exist for the word, the matching visible text-node occurrence within the existing 512-match cap. The `all` action remains separate for bulk correction. This reduces the per-occurrence UX gap while still not claiming full DOM-aware range persistence after page mutation. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-spell-at-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_spellcheck -- --nocapture` passed **5/5** and `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper spellcheck_ -- --nocapture` passed **8/8**; `.90` slot `browser-spell-at-wire` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client control_messages_round_trip -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-spell-at-cef` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml spellcheck_correction_script_replaces_mark_or_text -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-spell-at-servo` `cargo test --locked --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml servo_spellcheck_correction_script_replaces_mark_or_text -- --nocapture` passed; format checks passed on `.90` (`cargo fmt --check -p mde-web-preview-client -p mde-shell-egui`), `.130` (`cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-preview/Cargo.toml -- --check`), and `.170` (`cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/desktop/mde-web-cef/Cargo.toml -- --check`).
  **BROWSER-DD-11 speech/spell reconciliation 2026-07-09:** the older in-line "Remaining" sentence above is superseded for shipped software paths: Browser TTS handoff, daemon read-aloud owner, default TTS wrapper/provisioning hooks, voice-command/dictation shell handoff, daemon STT owner, transcript consumption, speech status display, offline Hunspell page-text checking, page highlights, and first suggestion application are now present and farm-gated. The honest remaining BROWSER-DD-11 tail is helper DOM-level AccessKit semantics, approved offline TTS/STT model/runtime selection, live microphone/audio verification, DOM-aware spell range walking/multi-occurrence correction UX, and ongoing live-site userscript tuning. Reconciliation evidence: `.50` slot `browser-speech-shell2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_ -- --nocapture` passed **72/72**; `.90` slot `browser-speech-worker2` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_ --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **90/90**; `.130` slot `browser-speech-role2` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**; `.170` slot `browser-speech-provision` `cargo test -p mackesd role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **24/24**; local `bash -n install-helpers/browser-read-aloud-tts.sh install-helpers/browser-voice-command-stt.sh install-helpers/install-browser-tts-voice.sh install-helpers/install-browser-stt-model.sh` passed.
- [>] **BROWSER-DD-12: platform integration + the long tail.** **Acceptance:** private offline/mesh translation; auto-suspend idle tabs; offline cache + mesh peer cache; protocol handling (mailto→email, tel→VOIP, magnet); web notifications + download/capture events → the mesh notify feed; share to peer/Chat/phone/email/QR; rich bookmarks (folders/tags/search/dead-link); independent security updater. **External protocol handoff slice 2026-07-08:** Browser address submissions for `tel:`, `mailto:`, and `magnet:` no longer navigate Servo/CEF. `tel:` publishes the existing `action/voice/dial` verb with browser source metadata; `mailto:` and `magnet:` publish typed `action/browser/protocol` handoffs for the future Email and Transfers owners; the shell shows a compact handoff notice. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper external_ -- --nocapture` passed **6/6** including `external_tel_urls_handoff_to_voice_without_helper_navigation`; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper mailto_and_magnet_urls_publish_browser_protocol_handoffs -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check -- crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Browser notify feed slice 2026-07-08:** successful browser captures and browser download Done/Failed transitions now publish `event/notify/browser` into the mesh notification feed. Chat alert folding classifies those notifications with the `browser` flag, notification actions deep-link back through `action/shell/goto/browser`, download notifications are once-per-transfer id, and the initial transfer-ledger snapshot does not spam existing jobs. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/toast_bridge.rs crates/services/mde-chat/src/alert.rs --check` and `git diff --check` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Browser share handoff slice 2026-07-08:** the Browser now exposes platform share targets for Peer, Email, and QR beside the existing Chat share path. Toolbar page actions, address-bar context actions, and the shared Browser menubar publish typed `action/browser/share` events with `op=browser_share`, target, URL, title, preview, source, and host metadata; the Browser does not fake downstream delivery owned by the peer, email, and QR surfaces. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_share -- --nocapture` passed **3/3**; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_bookmarks_menu_exposes_platform_share_handoffs -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check` passed. A stale `.170` rerun of the pre-fix test revision was stopped to avoid wasting farm capacity; Eagle excluded and no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Idle-tab suspend shell slice 2026-07-08:** inactive Browser tabs now track last activity, stop their helper load path after 30 minutes idle, skip background polling while shell-suspended, show an idle marker/hover state, reactivate on selection or page input, and publish a once-per-idle-period `action/browser/tab-suspend` handoff with tab index, engine, URL, title, source, host, and timeout metadata for the later engine/process hibernation owner. This is real shell-level suspension plus an honest platform handoff, not a claim that full helper process hibernation/restore exists yet. Farm evidence: `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper idle -- --nocapture` passed **15/15** including both Browser idle-suspend regressions; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper selecting_a_suspended_tab_reactivates_idle_state -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper inactive_idle_tabs_publish_suspend_handoff_and_stop_once -- --nocapture` passed. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs --check` and `git diff --check` passed. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued. **Bookmarks manager reachability slice 2026-07-08:** Browser now mounts the existing `mde-bookmarks-egui` manager as first-class `Surface::Bookmarks`, with a dedicated Quasar `surface-bookmarks` glyph, dock placement beside Browser in the Terminals group, `shell/goto/bookmarks` resolver support, and a Browser Bookmarks menu action that latches shell navigation to the manager. This makes the existing folders/tags/search/import/dead-link-capable manager reachable from Browser chrome instead of only supporting Add Bookmark. Local `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check` and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. Eagle excluded; no encrypted bench-seat reboot was issued.

  **Browser external-protocol daemon owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_protocol` worker that drains Browser's `action/browser/protocol` handoffs for external schemes the shell refuses to navigate. Browser protocol payloads now carry `source=browser` and host metadata; the worker validates scheme/target/URL consistency for `mailto -> email` and `magnet -> transfers`, publishes retained `state/browser-protocol/<node>` with accepted/rejected counters and latest route metadata, and emits bounded `event/browser-protocol/<node>` accepted-route events. This closes the daemon-owned handoff for mailto/magnet routing without faking an Email composer or magnet/torrent transfer lane. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-protocol-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_protocol --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.90` slot `browser-protocol-role` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**; `.130` slot `browser-protocol-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper mailto_and_magnet_urls_publish_browser_protocol_handoffs -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-protocol-fmt2` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_protocol.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/mod.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/worker_role.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed.
  **Browser tel Voice handoff metadata slice 2026-07-09:** Browser `tel:` submissions still publish the existing `action/voice/dial` verb and avoid helper navigation, but the Voice dial payload now also carries the local host beside `peer`, `source=browser`, and URL metadata. This gives the downstream Voice/VOIP owner the same Browser-origin attribution as the mailto/magnet protocol and platform-share handoffs without claiming live SIP call completion. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-tel-host-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper external_tel_urls_handoff_to_voice_without_helper_navigation -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-tel-host-protocol` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper mailto_and_magnet_urls_publish_browser_protocol_handoffs -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-tel-host-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-tel-host-fmt` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed.
  **Browser platform-share daemon owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_share` worker that drains Browser's `action/browser/share` handoffs for Peer, Email, and QR platform targets. The worker validates Browser-origin payloads (`op=browser_share`, `source=browser`, safe host, target, URL, title, preview), publishes retained `state/browser-share/<node>` with accepted/rejected counters and latest route metadata, and emits bounded `event/browser-share/<node>` accepted-route events for downstream owners. Chat share continues to use the existing Chat verb directly; this slice closes the daemon-owned Peer/Email/QR handoff without faking peer delivery, Email composition, or QR rendering. Local `git diff --check` passed; local trailing whitespace scan on the untracked worker file passed.
  **Browser QR share result/render slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now consumes the daemon-owned `event/browser-share/<local-node>` accepted-route stream with a retained cursor, validates only `browser_share_routed` events from the `browser_share` owner, ignores Peer/Email accepted routes inside Browser, and renders accepted local QR routes as a real QR matrix in a compact `QR share` drawer with URL copy/close controls. Oversized or unencodable QR payloads surface as unavailable instead of a placeholder. This closes the Browser-visible QR share path while Peer and Email delivery remain owned by their downstream surfaces.   **Browser phone-share bridge slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now exposes `Share to Phone` beside the existing Peer/Email/QR share actions and publishes `action/browser/share` with `target=phone`; when `MDE_BROWSER_SEND_PHONE_TARGET` / `MDE_BROWSER_SEND_PHONE_LABEL` is configured, the handoff carries the concrete KDE Connect device id/label until a paired-phone picker lands. The `browser_share` worker now accepts phone shares only with a safe concrete `target_id`, records that id in retained status/events, and publishes the existing `action/connect/share` verb with `device_id`, URL, `open=true`, and Browser source metadata. The KDC host remains the paired-phone delivery owner and still rejects unpaired or unreachable devices honestly; this slice does not fake phone delivery.   **Browser bookmarks dead-link daemon slice 2026-07-09:** the existing `bookmarks` worker now owns a bounded `action/bookmarks/check-links` path for rich-bookmark dead-link checks. It probes the current converged bookmark collection with a fixed-argv `curl` HTTP(S) checker in production, uses an injected probe in tests, classifies each checked bookmark as `alive`/`dead`/`unsupported`/`error`, and publishes both retained `state/bookmarks/link-check` and `event/bookmarks/link-check` results without minting bookmark CRDT ops or changing synced bookmark content. This lands the daemon-owned dead-link evidence path; Bookmarks UI display is closed by the slice below. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `bookmarks-linkcheck-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd bookmarks --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **11/11**; `.90` slot `bookmarks-linkcheck-role` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**; `.130` slot `bookmarks-linkcheck-check` `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` passed with existing warnings only; `.170` slot `bookmarks-linkcheck-fmt3` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/bookmarks.rs` passed.
  **Browser bookmarks dead-link UI display slice 2026-07-09:** the Bookmarks egui surface now has a Bus edge that publishes the user-triggered `action/bookmarks/check-links` request and tails the daemon-retained `state/bookmarks/link-check` cursor. The pure manager stores the latest link-check status, indexes per-bookmark records for the detail pane, and keeps the request as a one-shot intent so no UI frame floods the Bus. The toolbar exposes `Check links`, the status line summarizes checked/alive/dead/unsupported/error counts, and focused bookmark details show link health, HTTP status, and probe detail when the daemon has a result. The standalone Bookmarks app and the shell-mounted `Surface::Bookmarks` pump the same bridge. This closes the retained-status UI display follow-up; daemon collection hydration is closed by the slice below. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `bookmarks-link-ui-crate` `cargo test -p mde-bookmarks-egui -- --nocapture` passed **30/30**; `.50` slot `bookmarks-link-ui-final` `cargo test -p mde-bookmarks-egui link_check -- --nocapture` passed **4/4** against the final synced tree; `.90` slot `bookmarks-link-ui-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper bookmarks -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.130` slot `bookmarks-link-ui-check` `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper` passed with existing warnings only; `.170` slot `bookmarks-link-ui-fmt` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-bookmarks-egui/src/model.rs crates/desktop/mde-bookmarks-egui/src/view.rs crates/desktop/mde-bookmarks-egui/src/lib.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/main.rs` passed.
  **Browser bookmarks daemon collection hydration slice 2026-07-09:** the same Bookmarks bus bridge now tails the daemon-retained `state/bookmarks/collection` cursor and deserializes the worker's converged `mde_bookmarks::Collection` directly into the manager before applying link-check overlays. `Manager::replace_collection` swaps in the daemon snapshot while pruning stale current-folder, expanded-folder, selection, detail, rename, and delete-confirmation ids, so the surface shows the real worker-owned tree instead of staying first-run empty. This is a read-side hydration slice only; publishing local manager edits back to the worker's `action/bookmarks/{add,edit,move,delete,...}` verbs remains separate work. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `bookmarks-collection-ui-crate` `cargo test -p mde-bookmarks-egui -- --nocapture` passed **32/32**; `.90` slot `bookmarks-collection-ui-focused` `cargo test -p mde-bookmarks-egui daemon_collection -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.130` slot `bookmarks-collection-ui-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper bookmarks -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.50` slot `bookmarks-collection-ui-final` final synced `cargo test -p mde-bookmarks-egui daemon_collection -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.170` slot `bookmarks-collection-ui-fmt-final` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-bookmarks-egui/src/model.rs crates/desktop/mde-bookmarks-egui/src/view.rs crates/desktop/mde-bookmarks-egui/src/lib.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/main.rs` passed.
  **Browser bookmarks UI write-through slice 2026-07-09:** the Bookmarks manager now queues UI-authored add, add-folder, edit-title/rename, move, and delete operations as typed daemon actions, and the Bookmarks bus bridge publishes them to `action/bookmarks/<verb>` before reading daemon snapshots. Failed bus opens leave queued edits in the model, while publish failures requeue the failed and remaining actions for the next pump. The bookmarks worker action parser now accepts optional client-supplied ids and fractional order keys for optimistic UI adds/moves while preserving worker-minted defaults for other callers, so daemon snapshots can converge on the same ids/order the UI applied locally. This closes the write-side publish binding without claiming conflict-resolution UX beyond CRDT convergence and retry-on-next-pump.   **Browser translate shell handoff slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now exposes `Translate Page` in the shared View menu, extracts bounded helper page text, and publishes `action/browser/translate` with `op=browser_translate`, URL/title/engine/tab metadata, `source_lang=auto`, default target language `en` (env override supported), `privacy=offline_or_mesh_only`, text length, and truncation metadata.   **Browser translate daemon owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_translate` worker that drains `action/browser/translate`, rejects non-private payloads unless `privacy=offline_or_mesh_only`, validates Browser page metadata/language fields, invokes a configured local/mesh translation command (`MDE_BROWSER_TRANSLATE_COMMAND` / `MDE_TRANSLATE_COMMAND`) when present, publishes retained `state/browser-translate/<node>` with `translating`/`translated`/`unavailable`/`error` status, and emits bounded `event/browser-translate/<node>` translation results. Remaining translation work is packaging/provisioning an approved offline/mesh translation runtime and Browser-side result consumption/display.   **Browser translate model packaging slice 2026-07-09:** the full Workstation RPM now ships `/usr/libexec/mackesd/browser-translate` as the `browser_translate` worker's default command, plus a sha256-pinned optional model manifest (`browser-translate.env`), installer (`install-browser-translate-model`), and Workstation-gated setup unit (`mde-browser-translate-model-setup.service`). The wrapper exits 69 until a local model and `MDE_BROWSER_TRANSLATE_ENGINE_COMMAND`/`MDE_TRANSLATE_ENGINE_COMMAND` offline/mesh runtime are configured; the installer exits 78 while the manifest is intentionally unfilled. The server variant does not ship these desktop translation assets. Remaining translation work is selecting and filling the approved offline/mesh translation runtime/model manifest. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-translate-pack-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_translate --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **9/9**; `.90` slot `browser-translate-pack-role` `cargo test -p mackesd role_provision --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **23/23**; `.130` slot `browser-translate-pack-worker-role` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**. Local `bash -n install-helpers/browser-translate.sh` and `bash -n install-helpers/install-browser-translate-model.sh` passed; local unconfigured wrapper probe exited 69 and local unfilled manifest probe exited 78 as expected; `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser offline/mesh cache owner slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now exposes `Save Offline Copy` in the View menu, extracts bounded helper page text through the existing no-store helper seam, and publishes a private `action/browser/offline-cache` snapshot (`op=browser_offline_cache`, `privacy=offline_or_mesh_only`, URL/title/engine/tab metadata, bounded text, truncation metadata). `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_offline_cache` worker that validates only Browser/private payloads, computes a stable SHA-256 cache id, persists local records under `browser-offline-cache/pages/`, mirrors them into the Syncthing-backed workgroup root for peer reuse, and publishes retained `state/browser-offline-cache/<node>` status. The helper HTTP disk cache remains disabled; this is a daemon-owned private offline/mesh cache, not a re-enabled engine cache. Remaining long-tail cache work is rendering cached snapshots when a page is unavailable and adding richer resource/PDF capture. Local `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser offline-cache result/render slice 2026-07-09:** accepted daemon cache records now also publish private `event/browser-offline-cache/<node>` events (`op=browser_offline_cache_record`, `source=browser_offline_cache`, cache id, page metadata, bounded text, cached timestamp) after local persistence succeeds. Browser polls that retained result stream with a cursor, validates only private cache-worker records for the local host, and renders the latest cached snapshot in an `Offline copy` drawer with page metadata, bounded text, copy, and close controls. This makes saved snapshots inspectable inside Browser without re-enabling helper HTTP disk cache; remaining cache work is automatic unavailable-page fallback and richer resource/PDF capture.   **Browser offline-cache fallback slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now indexes accepted private daemon cache records by exact normalized URL and uses them as an unavailable-page fallback: a crashed active tab with a matching cached URL renders a full `Offline copy` body instead of only the crash panel, and a gated no-session open for the address-bar URL renders the cached body instead of only the helper/seat gate. The fallback remains honest and read-only: it labels the live-page failure, shows bounded daemon-owned text/page metadata, offers copy, and does not auto-respawn helpers or re-enable helper HTTP disk cache. Remaining cache work is richer resource/PDF capture and a broader URL/resource matching policy beyond exact URL text snapshots. Farm evidence: `.130` slot `browser-cache-fallback-shell2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_offline_cache -- --nocapture` passed **6/6**; `.50` slot `browser-cache-fallback-menu2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.90` slot `browser-cache-fallback-fmt3` `cargo fmt -p mde-shell-egui -- --check` passed. A stale `.90` pre-fix rerun failed on `Style::WARNING` before the token was corrected to `Style::WARN`; the fresh reruns above passed.
  **Browser offline-cache canonical URL fallback slice 2026-07-09:** offline-cache fallback lookup now keeps exact URL keys and adds conservative HTTP(S) canonical aliases for daemon-owned snapshots, so unavailable-page fallback survives fragment changes, host/scheme casing, default ports, empty-path slash differences, and reordered query pairs. Non-HTTP schemes and userinfo-bearing URLs remain exact-only. Remaining cache work is still richer resource/PDF capture and broader resource matching beyond visible page-text snapshots. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-cache-url-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_offline_cache -- --nocapture` passed **8/8**; `.90` slot `browser-cache-url-canonical` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper canonical_url -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-cache-url-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-cache-url-fmt2` `cargo fmt -p mde-shell-egui -- --check` passed.
  **Browser offline-cache viewport snapshot slice 2026-07-09:** Browser `Save Offline Copy` now attaches the retained viewport frame as an optional bounded private `image/png` payload when one is available. The `browser_offline_cache` worker validates MIME, dimensions, base64, PNG signature, and size; persists the sanitized viewport image in local and mesh cache JSON; and emits it in private cache result events. Browser validates the returned viewport metadata and surfaces the cached PNG dimensions in the `Offline copy` drawer. Text-only cache records still work; this does not claim full resource/PDF capture or inline cached-image rendering.
