Local `git diff --check` passed.
  **Browser offline-cache inline viewport render slice 2026-07-09:** cached viewport PNGs are no longer metadata-only in Browser. The `Offline copy` drawer now decodes the private cached viewport image through the shared PNG decoder, uploads it to a bounded egui texture cached by cache id and payload signature, verifies decoded dimensions against the daemon metadata, and renders the image above the cached text while text-only records continue to render as before. This makes the saved viewport image visibly usable in offline fallback/drawer flows; full resource graph capture and cached PDF rendering remain separate follow-up work. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-cache-image-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_offline_cache -- --nocapture` passed **9/9**; `.90` slot `browser-cache-image-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_offline_cache --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **7/7**; `.130` slot `browser-cache-image-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-cache-image-fmt` `cargo fmt -p mde-shell-egui -- --check` passed.
  **Browser offline-cache bounded MHTML archive slice 2026-07-09:** Browser `Save Offline Copy` now also carries a bounded private `archive_mhtml` payload: a generated MHTML archive containing cached page metadata/text and the retained viewport PNG when available. The `browser_offline_cache` worker validates MIME, safe filename, declared byte count, base64, UTF-8 MHTML signature, and size; persists the sanitized archive in local and mesh cache JSON; and republishes it in private cache result events. Browser validates returned archive records and exposes a drawer `MHTML` action that saves the cached archive to the Browser capture directory. Text-only and viewport-only records still work; this is a bounded visible-page archive, not a claim of full resource graph capture or cached PDF rendering. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-cache-mhtml-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_offline_cache -- --nocapture` passed **10/10**; `.90` slot `browser-cache-mhtml-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_offline_cache --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **9/9**; `.130` slot `browser-cache-mhtml-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-cache-mhtml-fmt2` `cargo fmt -p mde-shell-egui -- --check` passed; farm-side `.170` slot `browser-cache-mhtml-workerfmt` `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_offline_cache.rs` passed. Local `git diff --check` passed; local trailing whitespace scan on the untracked worker file passed.
  **Browser offline-cache PDF artifact slice 2026-07-09:** Browser `Save Offline Copy` now also carries an optional bounded private `pdf_snapshot` when the latest helper-produced user PDF belongs to the active page. The shell tracks non-CUPS PDF save confirmations by path, validates readable `%PDF-` bytes before attaching them, and exposes a drawer `PDF` action that writes the cached artifact back to the Browser PDF directory and opens it through the existing CEF PDF viewer path. The `browser_offline_cache` worker validates MIME, safe filename, declared byte count, base64, PDF signature, and size; persists the sanitized PDF payload in local and mesh cache JSON; and republishes it in private cache result events. Text-only, viewport-only, and MHTML-only records still work; full resource graph capture remains a separate follow-up. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-cache-pdf-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_offline_cache -- --nocapture` passed **11/11**; `.90` slot `browser-cache-pdf-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_offline_cache --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **11/11**; `.130` slot `browser-cache-pdf-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.170` slot `browser-cache-pdf-rustfmt2` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_offline_cache.rs` passed.
  **Browser offline-cache resource manifest slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now retains a bounded per-page resource manifest from the existing helper `ResourceRequest` and ad-filter verdict path, then includes that metadata in private offline-cache snapshots. The manifest records URL, stable resource type, and allowed/blocked verdict only; it does not store cookies, request/response headers, bodies, or perform an external refetch. The `browser_offline_cache` worker validates count/type/url bounds, persists the sanitized manifest in local and mesh cache JSON, and republishes it in private result events; Browser validates returned manifests and shows a resources/blocked summary in the offline-copy drawer. Remaining cache work is replayable full response-body resource graph capture if desired beyond metadata. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-cache-resource-client` `cargo test -p mde-web-preview-client resource_manifest -- --nocapture` passed **1/1**; `.90` slot `browser-cache-resource-shell` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_offline_cache -- --nocapture` passed **11/11**; `.130` slot `browser-cache-resource-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_offline_cache --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **13/13**; `.170` slot `browser-cache-resource-menu` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper the_view_menu_exposes_real_zoom_and_find_actions -- --nocapture` passed; `.50` slot `browser-cache-resource-rustfmt2` farm-side scoped rustfmt passed.
  **Browser idle-tab suspend daemon owner slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_tab_suspend` worker that drains the shell's `action/browser/tab-suspend` handoffs after the shell has already stopped inactive helper load paths. The worker validates Browser-origin payloads (`op=browser_tab_suspend`, source/host/engine/tab/url/title/idle timeout), publishes retained `state/browser-tab-suspend/<node>` with accepted/rejected counters and latest suspended tab metadata, and emits bounded `event/browser-tab-suspend/<node>` accepted-suspend events for diagnostics and future orchestration. This closes the daemon-owned handoff for idle tab suspension without claiming deeper engine/process hibernation beyond the shell stop path. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-tabsuspend-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_tab_suspend --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **4/4**; `.90` slot `browser-tabsuspend-role` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**; `.130` slot `browser-tabsuspend-check` `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` passed with only existing warnings; `.170` slot `browser-tabsuspend-fmt3` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --config skip_children=true --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_tab_suspend.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/mod.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/worker_role.rs` passed. Full `rustfmt --check` on `src/bin/mackesd.rs` remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing import-order drift; local `git diff --check` covered the new spawn hunk.
  **Browser security-update status slice 2026-07-09:** `mackesd` now runs a Workstation-tier `browser_security_update` worker that inspects the packaged CEF fast-update manifest (`MDE_CEF_MANIFEST` or `/usr/share/magic-mesh/browser/cef-linux64-minimal.env`) and the active installed runtime (`CEF_ACTIVE_LINK` or `/opt/mde/cef`). It publishes retained `state/browser-security-update/<node>` with expected CEF/Chromium/channel/asset/SHA-256, active runtime path, installed manifest values, `libcef.so` presence, and an honest `current`/`missing`/`mismatch`/`manifest_missing` state. This is the daemon-owned sentinel for the independent browser-engine updater; it does not fake archive downloads or promotion. Remaining updater work is the actual timed fetch/promote path and Browser/Workbench display of the status. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-security-update-worker` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_security_update --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **5/5**; `.130` slot `browser-security-update-role` `cargo test -p mackesd worker_role --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **19/19**; `.170` slot `browser-security-update-check` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_security_update.rs` and `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` passed with only existing warnings.
  **Browser security-update promotion slice 2026-07-09:** the `browser_security_update` worker now owns the timed updater trigger as well as the status sentinel. On each cadence it publishes the current manifest/runtime posture, invokes the packaged `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime` helper when the runtime is `missing` or `mismatch`, passes through `MDE_CEF_MANIFEST` plus an active-link override when configured, publishes an `installing` state before the attempt, then re-inspects and publishes the post-install posture with updater command, attempt timestamp, exit code, and failure summary. The installer remains the only code that downloads, verifies SHA-256, extracts, and promotes CEF archives, so failed installs stay `missing`/`mismatch` instead of faking `current`. Remaining updater work is Browser/Workbench display of the status. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-security-updater-worker2` `cargo test -p mackesd browser_security_update --features async-services -- --nocapture` passed **7/7**; `.170` slot `browser-security-updater-check` fresh farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/browser_security_update.rs` and `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` passed with only existing warnings.
  **Browser security-update display slice 2026-07-09:** Browser now polls the daemon-owned retained `state/browser-security-update/<local-node>` topic, validates the CEF updater posture, and surfaces it without claiming a fake install result. The shared menubar status cluster shows compact `CEF current`/`CEF missing`/`CEF mismatch`/`CEF manifest` chips, while non-current/installing/failed states also render a Browser engine update drawer with updater state, Chromium version, active runtime path, and real updater/inspection errors. This closes the Browser-side status display; Workbench-level fleet aggregation remains a separate follow-up if broader operator visibility is desired. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `browser-security-display-final2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper browser_security_update -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.90` slot `browser-security-chip-final2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper security_update_status_chip -- --nocapture` passed; `.130` slot `browser-security-bookmarks-final2` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper bookmarks -- --nocapture` passed **2/2**; `.170` slot `browser-security-display-rustfmt-final` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/web.rs` passed.
  **Browser security-update Workbench fleet slice 2026-07-09:** the Workbench Fleet plane now folds every retained `state/browser-security-update/<node>` record into the existing per-node Browser & ad-block policy section, validates daemon CEF updater states, latest-wins by `updated_ms`, and renders compact `CEF current`/`missing`/`mismatch`/`manifest` posture plus Chromium/runtime/updater error details. This closes the Workbench-level operator view without moving CEF install/download ownership out of the daemon installer path. Farm evidence: `.50` slot `workbench-cef-fleet-datacenter` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper datacenter -- --nocapture` passed **20/20**; `.90` slot `workbench-cef-fleet-project-browser` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper project_browser -- --nocapture` passed **5/5**; `.130` slot `workbench-cef-fleet-workbench` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features live-helper workbench -- --nocapture` passed **34/34**; `.170` slot `workbench-cef-fleet-rustfmt2` farm-side `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/datacenter.rs` passed.

## TMUX-FC — first-class tmux control chrome (operator 2026-07-04; 16-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/tmux-first-class.md)

Make tmux first-class in mde-term-egui via iTerm2-style control mode (`tmux -CC`): tmux windows→native tabs, panes→native splits, driven by full GUI chrome, mesh-aware, persistent. Reuse TERM-4 splits + TERM-5 tabs (§6, no second multiplexer).

- [✓] **TMUX-FC-1: control-mode core.** **As** a user, **I want** tmux to drive the native chrome, **so that** it's truly first-class. **Acceptance:** a `tmux -CC` spawn + an incremental control-mode protocol parser (%output/%window-*/%layout-change/%session-changed/%begin-%end); a live sessions→windows→panes model; each tmux window mapped to a `tabs::TabbedTerminal` tab + each pane to a `splits` leaf feeding the TERM-3 widget; GUI ops emit tmux commands and %-events reconcile the GUI; tested against a real tmux (parse + round-trip).
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-2: session management chrome.** **Acceptance:** a session/window/pane sidebar tree; create / attach / detach / kill / rename; a picker listing ALL sessions (attached + detached); tested each op issues the tmux command + the tree reflects %-events. *(mde-term-egui `tmux_ui.rs`: the `TmuxChrome` sidebar tree over the live `TmuxModel`; session ops via `TmuxIntent`→`command_for`→`tmux::commands` (round-trip, never a direct mutation); the all-sessions picker off the control-channel `list-sessions` reply (`TmuxController::all_sessions`). Session cmd builders + `parse_session_list` added to `tmux.rs`; the menubar tmux seam wired (`TmuxMenuChoice`) as its entry point; mounted in `panel.rs`. Opt-in per lock #16 — no auto-attach. Pane-content mount + window/pane mutation ops remain TMUX-FC-3.)*
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-3: window & pane operations.** **Acceptance:** split/close/zoom · break/join/swap/move · drag-resize (→resize-pane) + drag-reorder · rename window/pane titles — each a GUI affordance issuing the tmux command, reconciled by %layout-change; tested. *(done: 66e2ef6 — all lock-#9 ops as round-trip intents (split/close/zoom·break/join/swap/move·renames), drag-resize→resize-pane -x/-y + drag-reorder→move-window, zoom from %layout-change Z-flags, tagged list-windows/panes convergence, AND the FC-2 pane-content mount closed (Session::Tmux + new_tmux through the one TERM-3 grid, send-keys -H). Farm .50: 309 tests incl. the LIVE tmux suite, clippy 0 touched. join-pane -h has no UI trigger yet (FC-4); move-window -a/-b needs tmux≥3.2.)*
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-4: native status bar + toolbar + palette + context menus.** **Acceptance:** a native Quasar status bar (window tabs + session + clock, ignoring tmux status config); a toolbar; a curated ~30-command fuzzy palette; right-click context menus on tabs/panes — all issuing tmux commands; Style tokens (§4); tested. *(done: 3204ca7 — native Quasar status bar (ignores tmux status-*) + toolbar + 32-cmd fuzzy palette + enriched tab/pane context menus incl. the join-pane -h trigger; all through the FC-1/2/3 round-trip. Farm .50: 329 tests --test-threads=1, clippy 0 new.)*
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-5: persistence + templates.** **Acceptance:** auto-reattach remembered sessions on relaunch (detached keep running); session templates ("projects") seed a layout + commands; persisted in the platform-managed config; tested reattach + template open.
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-6: mesh tmux.** **As** an operator, **I want** any node's tmux, **so that** my persistent sessions follow the mesh. **Acceptance:** extend remote.rs/roster.rs (SSH-over-overlay) to enumerate + attach `tmux -CC` on peer nodes; the same chrome controls a remote session; tested list + attach path.
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-7: the 5 mesh-styled layout presets.** **Acceptance:** Mesh Ops (meshctl+peers+logs+shell) · Node Watch (btop+journal+disk/SMART+shell) · Cloud/OpenStack · Dev/Build · AI-CLI (Claude CLI + Codex CLI + shell) — each opens its window/pane layout + seeded commands in the Quasar style; mesh-synced; tested each preset builds its layout.
- [✓] **TMUX-FC-8: config, keys, copy.** **Acceptance:** a platform-managed Quasar tmux config (mesh-synced) + a GUI settings pane (prefix/mouse/history); the tmux prefix passes through AND remappable native GUI chords; native GUI scrollback + selection + search (not tmux copy-mode) with yank into tmux buffers + the mesh clipboard; tested. *(done: TMUX-FC-5/6/7/8 8ed93a1/d3816f4/47e339f1/d5a1ef26 — persistence+templates (Blueprint+tmux_store, auto-reattach), mesh tmux (ControlLink trait: local + broker-backed MeshControlChannel over the Bus PTY broker, live dial integration-gated like TERM-8), the 5 Quasar presets, config/keys/copy (single-line set-buffer bound documented, multi-line→OS/mesh clipboard). Farm .50: 373 tests incl. LIVE tmux-guarded round-trips, clippy 0 net-new. TMUX-FC epic COMPLETE.)*


## TERM-MENUBAR — a top menu bar exposing all terminal features (operator 2026-07-04)

- [✓] **TERM-MENUBAR-1: the terminal menu bar.** **As** a user, **I want** a menu bar at the top of the terminal exposing all features, **so that** everything is discoverable without memorizing shortcuts. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): a top menu bar on the terminal surface (mde-term-egui) with menus covering EVERY feature — File (new tab/window, close), Edit (copy/paste/find/select-all/clear), View (appearance/theme presets, fonts, zoom, toggle status bar), Terminal (new session, reset, broadcast-input, bell settings), Splits (split H/V, navigate, close, zoom, layouts), Tabs (new/close/rename/reorder/detach), Session (mesh/remote roster, attach a peer node), tmux (the TMUX-FC session/window/pane actions — wired as those land), Help (shortcuts). Each item drives the REAL existing feature seam (menu.rs/tabs/splits/search/remote/appearance/presets — §6 glue, no dead items §7); shortcuts shown beside items; Carbon Style tokens (§4). Tested: the menu bar renders all menus, each item dispatches its real action, honest-disabled where a feature needs context. *(mde-term-egui; keep the panel.rs mount minimal — coordinate with TMUX-FC-1)*


### DEVMGR — Device-Manager hardware inspector in the About surface (25-Q survey 2026-07-04; design: docs/design/about-device-manager.md)

Turn `Surface::About` into a Windows-Device-Manager-style hardware inspector: a faithful categorized device tree in Quasar dark, host-switching across the whole mesh (nodes/Cloud/phones/LAN/VyOS), rich per-device properties, MDM problem-code parity, fleet-wide fault notify, and (P2) real device actions over the overlay. **Serialize the shell units (DEVMGR-2..6/10/11 all touch the new `device_manager.rs` + the About mount — land on the settled base, like the VDOCK churn).**

#### Phase 1 — read-only inspector (local + mesh nodes)

- [✓] **DEVMGR-1: the device-inventory worker + publish.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** every node to enumerate + publish its own hardware,
  **so that** any host's device tree is readable across the mesh.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] extend an existing inventory worker (`legacy_inventory`/`compute_registry`, NOT a new worker — #16) to enumerate the local host's **full Linux taxonomy** (CPU/memory/disk+storage-ctrl/network/GPU/USB/PCI/audio/input/sensors/bluetooth/power) via **sysfs/udev first** (+ `pci.ids`/`usb.ids` names) with `lshw -json`/`dmidecode` shelled out for deep DMI/firmware on demand (#4/#15)
    - [✓] each device carries `{name, vendor, model, ids, sysfs_path, driver/module+version, status+problem_code, resources(irq/io/mem), recent_events}`
    - [✓] publish the tree to `<workgroup_root>/device-inventory/<hostname>.json` via the SEC-5 mesh-shunt replication (like node_grade); periodic republish + an on-demand live-re-query seam (#7/#8); rank-0 (every node)
    - [✓] the shared serde schema lives in a mesh-neutral shared crate (or duplicated with a round-trip test) — no §6 boundary crossing
    - [✓] tested: enumeration builds the taxonomy from a fixture sysfs tree, the JSON round-trips, the publish path writes the file
    — DONE (2026-07-04, session 4e2f8f4c): folded into the existing rank-0 **`hardware_probe`** worker (the best "whichever fits best" home — `legacy_inventory` is a file-catalog core module, `compute_registry` publishes VM/container workloads to the Bus; `hardware_probe` is the existing hardware producer already publishing to `<workgroup_root>` via the SEC-5 idiom, so lock #16 is honored with no new worker/census entry). New engine `mackesd::workers::device_inventory` walks the full taxonomy sysfs-first (`/sys/bus/{pci,usb}`, `/sys/block`, `/sys/class/*`, `/proc/{cpuinfo,meminfo,uptime}`) with `pci.ids`/`usb.ids` names + honest status/problem derivation (PCI no-driver → `no-driver`, `enable=0`/de-authorized USB → `disabled`, dmesg error → `degraded`) + IRQ/io/mem resources + dmesg events; `lshw`/`dmidecode` are best-effort tool-availability flags (#15 honest-degrade). Publishes `<workgroup_root>/device-inventory/<hostname>.json` (atomic temp+rename, keyed on the same stem node_grade uses). **Shared schema in `mackes-mesh-types::device_inventory`** (the mesh-neutral crate that already carries `peer_probe` — both `mackesd` + `mde-shell-egui` depend on it, no §6 crossing; DEVMGR-2 reads this). Farm-green (slot devmgr1, host .90): mackes-mesh-types + mackesd build/clippy clean on the new files; `mackes-mesh-types` 5/5 + `mackesd::workers::device_inventory` 11/11 device tests pass; layered-tiers lint clean; rustfmt clean.

- [✓] **DEVMGR-2: About → Device Manager shell (local host, by-type).**
  **As** a user,
  **I want** the About surface to become a Device Manager,
  **so that** I can inspect this machine's hardware like Windows.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] a new `device_manager.rs` renders as the body of `Surface::About`; brand → a compact `◈ <name> v<ver>` title strip + an ⓘ button opening license/credits/mesh-identity in a dialog (#2/#24)
    - [✓] the faithful MDM **by-type** device tree in `mde_egui::Style` dark tokens, **all-collapsed** default (#1/#18), reading the LOCAL published inventory (DEVMGR-1)
    - [✓] a full **menu bar (Action/View/Help) + toolbar** (Scan, View-mode toggle, Expand/Collapse-all) (#19)
    - [✓] a **rich header card** (host · OS/kernel · uptime · CPU/RAM/disk totals · device count + problem badge) (#20)
    - [✓] style-leak grep clean; tested: tree builds from a fixture inventory, categories group correctly, collapse state, header card fields
    — DONE (2026-07-04, session 4e2f8f4c): new `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/device_manager.rs` (`DeviceManagerState`) is the body of `Surface::About` — a compact `◈ MDE Quazar v<ver>` title strip (single-sourced from `mde_theme::brand`, never a hardcoded name) + an ⓘ button opening the canonical QBRAND-6 identity screen (`about::about_panel` reused verbatim, §6) in an `egui::Modal`; a faithful MDM menu bar (Action/View/Help) + toolbar (Scan re-reads the published snapshot, Expand/Collapse-all, the view-mode control) in `mde_egui::Style` dark tokens; the by-type `CollapsingHeader` tree **all-collapsed** by default (#18, external expand-set so Expand/Collapse-all + per-header clicks route through one store), amber-tinted category branches over problem devices + per-device status dot + honest Linux `problem` reason; a rich header card (host · OS · kernel · humanized uptime · CPU model+count · RAM in GiB · device count + problem badge, honest em-dashes for absent fields, lshw-missing hint) fed by `mackes_mesh_types::device_inventory::read_inventory(root, local_host)` on a 30 s cadence + on Scan; honest pre-poll dim "?" before the first read and an honest "nothing published yet" for an empty host (§7). By-connection (DEVMGR-5) + by-node (DEVMGR-4) view modes are honest **disabled** seams, not stubs; the detail drawer (DEVMGR-3), host rail (DEVMGR-4) + export (DEVMGR-6) are clean seams. Farm-green (slot devmgr2, host .130): `mde-shell-egui` build clean; **510/510** tests pass incl. the **10** new `device_manager` tests (fixture tree render, all-collapsed default + expand/collapse-all + toggle, header-card fields, absent-field em-dash, honest pre-poll→empty read, disabled view modes, ⓘ dialog render, uptime/mem formatting, status tones, partial-CPU degrade); clippy clean on both touched files (`device_manager.rs`, `main.rs`); rustfmt + style-leak grep + layered-tiers lint clean. **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-3: the device detail drawer + problem codes.**
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): extended `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/device_manager.rs` with three landed things. **(a) The bottom detail drawer (#9/#10):** a tree-row click opens a resizable `TopBottomPanel::bottom` drawer (via `show_inside`, tree stays full-width above in a `CentralPanel`) with the five MDM tabs **General / Driver / Details(sysfs+IDs) / Events / Resources** as `selectable_label`s, each populated from the selected `DeviceRecord` — honest empty-tab (`muted_note`) when a field/list is absent, em-dash for an absent scalar (§7); selection is a stable `DeviceSelection{category,name,sysfs_path}` resolved against the live inventory each frame, so a re-read never freezes a stale device (a vanished device closes the drawer); a re-click on the open row closes it. **(b) Full MDM problem-code parity (#11):** `problem_code(DeviceStatus)` maps no-driver→**Code 28**, disabled→**Code 22**, degraded→**Code 10**; Ok/Unknown carry **no fabricated code** (honest). The General tab's status box reads "This device is working properly." or "Code N — <real Linux reason>"; the tree rows carry a `Code N` badge beside faulted devices. **(c) About chrome onto the shared `mde_egui::menubar::MenuBar`** — DEVMGR-2's bespoke Action/View/Help bar + toolbar are **replaced** by the shared component (title "About", accent `Style::ACCENT_SYSTEM`, the `◈ Magic-Mesh Quasar v<ver>` brand strip kept beside it per #24): **Action**(Scan) · **View**(the 3 modes as radio items — By type live+checked, the others honest-disabled §7 — + Expand/Collapse-all, gated on a loaded inventory) · **Help**(the ⓘ dialog), each item the mouse-twin of the real seam via `apply()`; right-side **status cluster** = host · N devices · M problems · scanned-time (all off live state). Farm-green (slot devmgr3, host .130): build clean; **562/562** tests (22 `device_manager`, **10** new: the state→code map, `device_status_display` keeps the real reason, the 5-tab drawer renders per record, drawer prunes a vanished selection, the selection key, the MenuBar menus drive the real seams, expand/collapse gate honestly, `apply` dispatch, the status cluster reflects host/devices/problems + is honest pre-read/clean, scanned-freshness humanize); clippy clean on `device_manager.rs`; rustfmt + style-leak grep + layered-tiers lint clean. **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-4: the host rail + mesh-node switching.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** to switch which host I'm inspecting,
  **so that** I can check any node's hardware from one panel.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] a persistent **left host rail** (rail │ tree │ drawer) listing mesh peer nodes with a status dot + the local "you are here" marker (#5)
    - [✓] selecting a host loads its published inventory instantly (**hybrid snapshot**) + a **live-refresh** button re-queries it (#7); a **Scan** action + ~30s auto-refresh (#8); honest dim/stale/offline states
    - [✓] tested: rail lists nodes from the peer directory, switching loads the right tree, stale/offline render honestly
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): extended `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/device_manager.rs` (DEVMGR-4 only — no other file touched). **The persistent left host rail (#5):** a full-height `SidePanel::left` reserved first so the About body is now **rail │ tree │ (bottom drawer)**; it lists every peer that published a `device-inventory/<host>.json` (enumerated via **`mackes_mesh_types::device_inventory::read_all(root)`** — one dir read serves both the rail model and the selected host's tree, no second file read) as a clickable row with a freshness/health **status dot**, the **local "you are here" node pinned first** + marked with a ⌂ glyph, and a header **↻ live-refresh** button. **Host switching (#7 hybrid):** clicking a row `select_host`s it — the device drawer + tab reset (a selection is per-host), then an instant re-read loads THAT host's published snapshot; the **tree + header card + drawer + MenuBar status cluster all reflect the selected host**, with local the default on open. **Honest dim/stale/offline (§7):** a new pure `host_freshness(published_at_ms, now)` classifies **Fresh** (dot = OK/green or Danger on a fault) / **Stale** (amber — published but older than the 180 s `STALE_AFTER`, or an unknown `published_at_ms == 0`, so its health can't be trusted) / **Absent** (dim — nothing published, an honest "?" you can still select → the empty-host read, never a fabricated tree); the row hover carries the real counts + freshness read-out, an absent host invents none. Scan (menu) + the ~30 s auto-refresh cadence (DEVMGR-2's `poll`) both flow through the same rebuilt-rail `refresh`. All colours/spacing via `mde_egui::Style` tokens (§4). Farm-green (slot devmgr4, host .130): `mde-shell-egui` build clean; **568/568** tests (28 `device_manager`, **6** new: rail lists every published host with local pinned first, local pinned first even when it published + sorts late, `refresh` reads the rail + switching loads the right host's tree over a real multi-host dir, an unpublished selected host reads an honest empty tree, freshness → honest dim/stale/offline dots, the rail renders headless + its hover stays honest); clippy clean on `device_manager.rs`; rustfmt + style-leak grep (zero new hits) + layered-tiers lint clean. **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-5: the By-connection view mode.**
  **As** a user,
  **I want** the bus-topology view,
  **so that** I can see what hangs off what.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] a **By-connection** view mode (the View toggle) renders the PCI/USB topology tree (host bridge → bus → device → child) from the same inventory (#3)
    - [✓] tested: the topology tree nests devices under their parent bus from a fixture
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): extended `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/device_manager.rs` (DEVMGR-5 only — no other file touched). **The By-connection view (#3):** `ViewMode::ByConnection` is now wired (`is_available` = ByType | ByConnection; **By node** stays an honest disabled seam §7) and selectable from the shared MenuBar's View menu; the About body branches on the active mode, re-rooting the **same** devices under their **parent bus / controller** (host → PCI/USB bus segment → device) instead of their function category. **Topology source:** the DEVMGR-1 `DeviceRecord` schema carries **no explicit `parent`/`bus` field** — the only topology signal is **`sysfs_path`**, so a new pure `build_connection_tree(&DeviceInventory)` reconstructs the hierarchy via `derive_bus(sysfs_path)`: a PCI address → its `DDDD:BB` bus segment (`/sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:02:00.0` → `PCI bus 0000:02`; a real `/sys/devices/…` path resolves to the device's own bus, not the bridge's), a USB path → its bus number, any other `/sys/bus/<kind>/` → a title-cased `<Kind> bus`. **Honest degradation (§7):** a device whose path resolves no bus (or has no `sysfs_path`) falls under the **host root** as a leaf — never dropped; a host that published **no bus/parent data at all** degrades to a **flat** list under the root with an explicit note (`flat_no_bus`), never a fabricated hierarchy — the note flags that a richer bridge/port tree needs a `parent`/`bus` field in the DEVMGR-1 inventory. Bus branches share the one `expanded` store (Expand-all is now mode-aware: category keys in By-type, bus-branch keys in By-connection) and the **device rows + selection + detail drawer (DEVMGR-3) are reused verbatim** — only the nesting differs, so a mode flip preserves the selected host (DEVMGR-4's rail selection still governs) and its inventory. Farm-green (slot devmgr5, host .130): `mde-shell-egui` build clean; **585/585** tests (**33** `device_manager`, **5** new: the fixture nests each device under its parent PCI bus, a parentless device stays under the host root, no-bus-data degrades flat with the note, switching to By-connection preserves the selected host + Expand-all fills bus branches + renders headless, `derive_bus` reads PCI/USB/generic paths + honest None; plus the renamed disabled-seam test now asserting By-connection wired / By-node disabled); clippy clean on `device_manager.rs` (`use_self` fix); rustfmt + style-leak grep (zero new hits) + layered-tiers lint clean. **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-6: export the device list.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** to export a host's inventory,
  **so that** I can attach it to a ticket or diff it.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] an Export action produces the current host's inventory as machine **JSON** and a human-readable **Markdown report**, plus copy-to-clipboard (#23)
    - [✓] tested: the JSON round-trips, the Markdown report contains every category + problem summary
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): the About → Device Manager **Action** menu grows the MDM `Action → generate a report` seams — **Export inventory (JSON)…**, **Export report (Markdown)…**, and **Copy report to clipboard** (surfaced on the shared MENUBAR-ALL bar, not a hidden verb). All three render the **currently-selected host + active view mode** (`render_json`/`render_report`): JSON serde-serializes the §6 `mackes_mesh_types::device_inventory::DeviceInventory` contract (round-trips); the Markdown report mirrors the on-screen tree — the rich host-header summary (#20 fields) then per-category (By type) or per-bus/controller (By connection, reusing `build_connection_tree`) device rows carrying the same DEVMGR-3 problem-code + status text the drawer shows (`device_status_display`, e.g. `Code 28 — no kernel driver bound`), with the amber `⚠ N` branch suffix. No native file-save dialog seam exists on the DRM seat (grepped: zero `rfd`/`FileDialog`), so a write lands at a deterministic `$XDG_DATA_HOME`/`~/.local/share/mde/device-inventory/<host>-<view>.<ext>` path (`export_dir`, temp-dir fallback; hostile hostnames `sanitize`d) via the tmp-then-rename atomic writer the shell's other JSON writers use, and confirms on the shared KIRON `event/toast/show` toast lane (`raise_toast` → `mde_bus::Persist`, the same lane Chat/Explorer nav toasts use); a **failed write raises a `warning` toast, never a silent no-op** (§7). Copy uses `ctx.copy_text` (the seat clipboard). An **absent host** (nothing published per DEVMGR-4) exports an honest `published:false` JSON + a "no inventory yet" report, never a fabricated tree. Farm-green (slot devmgr6, host .130 BigBoy): `mde-shell-egui` build clean; **593/593** tests pass incl. **8** new `device_manager` DEVMGR-6 tests (JSON round-trips a fixture, the report lists host + every device + `Code 28`, absent host → honest empty report, the report groups to reflect the active view mode, a real file write round-trips, a failed write is an honest error, deterministic export dir, sanitize neutralizes path separators); clippy clean on `device_manager.rs` (`--all-targets`); rustfmt + style-leak grep (0 hits) + layered-tiers lint clean. Stayed strictly within `device_manager.rs` (no `iac.rs` / Cargo.toml touch). **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

#### Phase 2 — actions, notify, By-node, non-PC hosts, reach

- [✓] **DEVMGR-7: local device actions (armed → honest read-only subset).**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** to act on this machine's devices,
  **so that** I can recover a wedged device.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] **Properties** (open the property sheet / drawer), **Scan for hardware changes / rescan** (re-read the inventory — the honest rescan), and **Copy device details** (#12) fire on the selected host (local incl.), from a device-row right-click **context menu** + a drawer Copy button; **Enable/Disable + Reload kernel module OMITTED, not greyed** (§7/§8 — a read-only §6 inventory consumer has no privileged-exec seam; those hardware mutations belong to the node-side producer, a later request-channel unit)
    - [✓] the three honest actions are all **safe** and fire directly; **no destructive action ships**, so no typed-arming gate is needed here (the destructive verbs that WOULD need EXPLORER-5 arming, #14, are exactly the omitted ones, §7/§8)
    - [✓] tested: each honest action maps to its real seam (Properties opens the drawer, Scan re-reads, Copy dumps every field), the offered set is asserted to be exactly the honest read-only subset (no Enable/Disable/Reload/Uninstall/Update verb), the dump carries the Code + honest reason + em-dashes; the arming gate is N/A (nothing destructive)
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): extended `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/device_manager.rs` (DEVMGR-7 only — no other file touched). **The honest read-only device-action set (#12):** every device row now carries a right-click **context menu** (`device_context_menu`) offering the three actions a read-only §6 inventory consumer can perform honestly — **Properties** (open the DEVMGR-3 detail drawer / property sheet), **Scan for hardware changes** (re-read the inventory — the honest rescan, the same `refresh()` seam the Action-menu Scan drives), and **Copy device details** (`render_device_details` → the full field dump: identity, the MDM `Code N — reason` status line reused verbatim from the drawer's General tab, driver, sysfs, resources, events — absent scalars dash, empty lists read `none reported`, §7). A **Copy details** button in the detail-drawer header gives the same Copy-info a non-right-click path. Each action bubbles up a resolved `RowActionRequest` (Properties→selection, Scan, CopyDetails→boxed record) applied after the frame's read borrow ends by `apply_row_action`, exactly like the existing row-selection / header-toggle plumbing (the shared `device_row`/`CategoryOutcome` seams now carry a `RowOutcome`). **Honestly OMITTED (§7/§8), not greyed:** MDM's hardware-mutating verbs — **Enable/Disable** (driver bind/unbind, `ip link up/down`, USB `authorized`) and **Reload kernel module** (rmmod+modprobe) — cannot be performed honestly from this surface: `mde-shell-egui` is a desktop-side consumer of the published `device-inventory/<host>.json` (§6) with **no privileged-exec / worker-request seam**, and a real mutation belongs to the mesh-side producer on the node itself (a later request-channel unit, cf. DEVMGR-8's remote-exec seam). Shipping them greyed would be a §8 placebo, so they are absent, with a muted caption in the context menu (`Read-only inventory — enable/disable + driver reload run on the node`) stating why (§7 honest disclosure). Because **no destructive action ships**, the DEVMGR-7 typed-arming gate (#14) is not needed here — the three honest actions are all safe and fire directly. Farm-green (slot devmgr7, host .50): `mde-shell-egui` build clean; **606/606** crate tests (**46** `device_manager`, **5** new: the offered set IS exactly the honest read-only subset {Properties, Scan, Copy} with no Enable/Disable/Reload/Uninstall/Update verb; the detail dump carries every drawer-tab field + the Code 28 + honest reason identical to the General tab; a sparse device dumps honest em-dashes + two `none reported` lines + no fabricated code; `apply_row_action` dispatches each action to its real seam — Properties OPENS the drawer (never toggles it shut like a row click) + resets to General, Scan re-reads (`seen` flips), Copy touches no selection state; the action-carrying rows + the drawer copy button render headless); clippy clean on `device_manager.rs` (`--all-targets`; boxed the large `CopyDetails` payload to satisfy `large_enum_variant`); rustfmt + style-leak grep (0 hits on device_manager.rs) + layered-tiers lint clean. Stayed strictly within `device_manager.rs` (no `iac.rs` / Cargo.toml touch). **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-8: remote device actions over the overlay.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** to act on a remote node's devices,
  **so that** I can drive any host's hardware from one panel.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] the DEVMGR-7 actions dispatch on a selected **remote** host over the Nebula overlay (reuse the mackesd run-command / QC-verb / TERM remote seam, #13), typed-armed
    - [✓] a "you may lose reach to this host" warning for network-device actions; every action appends to the KDC hash-chained audit log
    - [✓] tested: a remote action round-trips to the target's action seam + the audit append
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): built the **node-side privileged device-exec seam** that makes DEVMGR-7's honestly-omitted mutating verbs live. **New shared §6 contract** `crates/mesh/mackes-mesh-types/src/device_control.rs` — the typed `DeviceControlRequest`/`DeviceControlResult`/`DeviceControlOp{Enable,Disable,ReloadModule,RescanBus}`/`DeviceTarget` + the replicated-dir seam (`write_request`/`take_requests`/`write_result`/`take_result` under `<root>/fleet/device-control/<target>/`), so neither the shell nor mackesd depends on the other (the wire is the shared crate, like `device_inventory`). **New mackesd worker** `crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/device_control.rs` (`DeviceControlExecWorker`, rank-0 universal, mirrors `lifecycle_exec` — no push-SSH, §9): drains this box's replicated request dir, **gates** each device against this node's own published inventory (the L9 rail, like `lifecycle_exec::offered`), maps the typed op onto a **FIXED real seam** (`command_plan`, pure — driver `bind`/`unbind` write, USB `authorized` toggle, the PCI bus `rescan` write, or `ip link set`/`rmmod`+`modprobe`), **executes for real** (sysfs write == `std::fs::write`; the `ip`/`rmmod`/`modprobe` binaries are fixed, never a command string), **hash-chain audits every op** (success OR refusal) via `events::append_and_alert(AdminAction)`, and **notifies on failure** on `event/notify/device-control` (the `chat`-folded lane, mirroring `node_grade`). An op inapplicable to a device kind (reload with no module, enable/disable with no bus/net anchor, rescan on a non-PCI bus) is a **typed error, never a fake success** (§7). **Shell wiring** (`device_manager.rs`): the DEVMGR-7 context menu now offers the four privileged verbs (present, not greyed), each behind **typed-arming** (echo the device name, `render_arming`) with a **"you may lose reach to this host"** caption for network devices; a confirm writes the typed request to the **RAIL-selected** host's replicated dir (`device_control::write_request`, DEVMGR-4 governs), and an **Absent/never-published** host raises an honest error toast + writes nothing (no silent no-op). **Live vs honestly-errored:** USB (`authorized`), PCI/platform driver bind/unbind, PCI bus rescan, and `ip link`-anchored netdevs are genuinely live; enable/bind of a driverless device, reload of a moduleless device, rescan of a non-PCI bus, and any device with no sysfs/net anchor return a typed error. **Farm-green** (slot devmgr8, host .50): `cargo build -p mackesd -p mde-shell-egui`; `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (device_manager +6 DEVMGR-8 tests) + `cargo test -p mackesd device_control` (+13, `--test-threads=1`) + `cargo test -p mackes-mesh-types device_control` (+4); clippy/rustfmt/style-leak/layered-tiers clean. **Shared mackesd files touched (additive only, for hand-merge):** `workers/mod.rs` (+`pub mod device_control;`), `worker_role.rs` (+`("device_control", 0)` census row), `bin/mackesd.rs` (+import + a role-gated spawn block, mirroring `lifecycle_exec`), and `mackes-mesh-types/src/lib.rs` (+`pub mod device_control;`) — **did NOT touch `workers/openstack/` or `iac.rs`**. **NOT pushed** (operator-gated).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-9: fleet-wide device-fault notify.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** to hear when a device fails on any node,
  **so that** I catch hardware problems without watching the panel.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [ ] a device transitioning INTO a problem state (driver drop, disk I/O errors, NIC down) emits `event/notify/<source>` → Chat + phone (CHAT-FIX-2 producer, #21), debounced against flapping (mirror node_grade)
    - [ ] tested: a simulated fault transition fires one debounced notify; a flapping device does not spam

- [✓] **DEVMGR-10: the By-node cross-fleet view.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** every host's devices in one tree,
  **so that** I can scan the whole fleet's hardware at once.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] a **By-node** view mode flattens all hosts' published inventories into one cross-fleet tree (host → its devices), problem hosts near the top (#3)
    - [✓] tested: the By-node tree aggregates multiple fixture hosts + surfaces problem counts per host
    - **Landed (DEVMGR-10, 2026-07-05):** `ViewMode::ByNode` is now wired (`is_available`, the third View-menu radio — no disabled seam left). A new `build_node_tree(read_all, local)` flattens the whole fleet: each published host a top-level `NodeHost` branch carrying its own device tree (categories cloned in), the absent local "you are here" node injected as an honest dim leaf mirroring the rail. `node_order` ranks **problem hosts first** (present-before-absent, any-fault-before-clean, more-problems-higher, then alphabetical), each host branch showing a per-host `⚠ N` badge + device count + a stale flag; a `fleet_header` sums the cross-fleet totals. Device rows/status-dots/problem-codes/context-menu/detail-drawer are the DEVMGR-2..8 seams verbatim (only the outer host→category→device nesting is new); Expand-all is mode-aware (host keys via `NodeTree::host_keys`). The DEVMGR-4 rail selection is cross-fleet-wide: the rail-selected host is accented and a device click on another host is an honest **jump** (`select_node_device` switches the inspected host so the drawer + any armed op resolve against the right node), never a mismatched host or fabricated tree (§7). Farm-green: `mde-shell-egui` builds; **625 tests pass** (7 new/updated By-node tests: fleet aggregation, problem-host ranking + exact per-host counts, honest absent leaf, view-flip preserves the fleet set, cross-fleet jump, renders-despite-absent-selection); clippy clean on `device_manager.rs`; style-leak grep + `lint-layered-tiers.sh` clean. STRICTLY within `device_manager.rs` (IAC-5 owns iac.rs + mackesd).

- [✓] **DEVMGR-11: non-PC host types.** *(done: a1d34e0 (+DEVMGR-9 5b24d67) — worker died AT the clippy gate (session limit): tests 635/635 green, clippy re-gate owed on the integration tree.)*
  **As** a user,
  **I want** phones, routers, cloud instances + LAN hosts in the inspector,
  **so that** the whole mesh's hardware is visible.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [ ] the host rail also lists **Cloud/Nova instances**, **paired phones**, **LAN-discovered hosts**, and **VyOS/router devices** (#6); each renders **only the categories that apply** (router→Network/System/Firmware, phone→Power/Sensors/Radios, Nova→virtio, LAN→remotely-detectable), never an empty category (#22)
    - [ ] each source contributes what it can (Nova via the QC/compute registry, phones via KDC, LAN via probe_nmap, VyOS via SSH/API); honest partial trees
    - [ ] tested: each host type maps to its inventory source + renders only applicable categories

- [✓] **DEVMGR-12: live smoke on the seat.** *(done: SEAT-2 live on .2 — host rail self+peer, real device on the LAN tree (gateway router 172.20.0.1 MAC 18:e8:29:44:27:91), device_control/inventory workers running, real nvme0n1 in local inventory.)*

### MENUBAR-ALL — unified title + menu bar on every surface (15-Q survey 2026-07-04; design: docs/design/menubar-all.md)

Every primary nav-bar surface gets a slim top bar: a large UPPERCASE mono accent title (left) + the File/Edit/View/Help spine + surface menus (inline) + a live per-surface status cluster (right), all via one shared `mde-egui` MenuBar. **Governing principle (operator): the menu bar surfaces EVERY control incl. advanced/complex — comprehensive, honestly-wired, no dead entries (§7); critical for the OpenStack/IaC interfaces.** One big wave; Terminal + Editor refactor onto the shared component.

- [✓] **MENUBAR-ALL-1: the shared `mde-egui` MenuBar component + Terminal/Editor refactor.**
  **As** a mesh operator,
  **I want** one shared title+menu bar widget,
  **so that** every surface reads as one platform.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] a new `mde_egui::menubar` module: a slim `MenuBar` rendering an UPPERCASE mono DISPLAY-tier title (accent-tinted) + an inline menu strip + a right-aligned status-chip cluster, at one consistent height, from `Style`/`Motion`/`fonts` only
    - [✓] a caller-supplied menu model (`Menu{title,mnemonic,entries}`, `Item{id,label,shortcut,enabled,checked}` + `Entry::{Item,Submenu,Separator,Caption}`); dropdowns fade in on the shared motion spring; **Alt-mnemonics** (underlined, `resolve_mnemonics`) + egui keyboard nav + 2px accent focus ring; each item shows its live shortcut hint; honest disable/omit (§7)
    - [✓] backing tests (model, mnemonic resolution, disabled-item non-activatable, status cluster, title casing, reduce-motion, headless render) in mde-egui; style-leak grep clean
    - [✓] **Terminal** (`mde-term-egui/menubar.rs`, incl. the TERM-MENUBAR-1 + TMUX-FC-2 Tmux menu) + **Editor** (Word-97 bar) refactored onto it — every existing menu item + seam preserved, their menu tests green (term 290, editor 388)
    - [✓] renders correctly at 1.0 + a fractional scale (built on egui layout + `Style` tokens only, no hardcoded pixel positions)

- [✓] **MENUBAR-ALL-2..N: per-surface title+menu bars (one big wave).**
    — DONE (2026-07-05, session 4e2f8f4c): **all 13 wave surfaces now embed the shared `mde_egui::menubar::MenuBar`** (Terminal + Editor were the MENUBAR-ALL-1 refactor), so every one of the 15 nav surfaces reads as one platform. Per-surface bars landed: Music (`mde-music-egui/menubar.rs`), Media (`mde-media-egui/menubar.rs`), Files (`mde-files-egui/menubar.rs`), Voice (`mde-voice-egui/lib.rs`), Browser (`mde-shell-egui/web.rs`, later retired from the shared top strip by **BROWSER-CHROME C0** in favor of first-party browser chrome), Chat + Instances + Desktop(`vdi.rs`) + System + Storage + Workbench (`mde-shell-egui/*`), Mesh-View (`mde-mesh-view/menubar.rs`, `MeshMenuBar` over the shared bar), and now **About** (`mde-shell-egui/device_manager.rs`, this unit — DEVMGR-3) — the **14th/last** surface onto the component. Each carries its UPPERCASE title + category accent + a live status cluster, its real File/Edit/View/Help spine (only where real) + surface-specific menus, every visible item bound to an existing seam with honest disable/omit (§7). All farm-green + tested per surface; About closes the wave.

### IAC — the "Infra as Code (IaC)" workspace / OpenStack IaaS control plane (25-Q survey 2026-07-04; design: docs/design/iac-workspace.md)

A new Workloads-group surface "Infra as Code (IaC)": an OpenStack IaaS control plane on the standard APIs — Keystone catalog = the service directory, Heat = the native IaC engine, per-service APIs = status + comprehensive control. Lists all published services (OpenStack catalog + mesh/LAN scan, grouped by kind) under a live OpenStack-API status band. Tabbed Overview/Resources/Heat; typed-arming on all mutations; audit-all + notify-on-failure; comprehensive catalog-driven menu bar (the MENUBAR-ALL governing principle). Everything in one cut. Instances (QC-12) stays the quick VM view; IaC is the full admin.

- [✓] **IAC-1: the OpenStack client foundation.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** a standard-API OpenStack client,
  **so that** the workspace speaks OpenStack natively.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] auth via `clouds.yaml` (openstacksdk standard, single default context); the **Keystone service catalog** (services + public/internal/admin endpoints + region); per-service **API health** (real ping/version probe → up/down + latency + microversion); the standard resource + verb calls (Nova/Neutron/Glance/Cinder/Heat/…) — reuse/extend the QUASAR-CLOUD / mackesd OpenStack integration (§6), not a parallel client
    - [✓] honest absent/unreachable when a service isn't deployed; tested against fixtures + (IAC-7) a live cloud
    - **Landed (IAC-1, 2026-07-04):** the client foundation extends the QUASAR-CLOUD `mackesd::workers::openstack` worker with a new `client/` sub-tree — `config` (clouds.yaml load + single-context select, password redacted, never on argv), `keystone` (v3 auth → token + catalog seam), `health` (per-endpoint version/ping probe seam), `resource` (the list/show/create/update/delete REST seam — the IAC-3 boundary) — plus a testkit of fakes; all pure logic fixture-tested (37 new unit tests). The §6 catalog+health wire contract (`ServiceCatalog`/`ServiceHealth`) lives in `mackes-mesh-types::openstack` so IAC-2's `mde-shell-egui` consumes it off the Bus without depending on `mackesd`; runtime-reachable now via the new `action/cloud/get-catalog` read verb on the existing QC-11 Bus seam (honest `Unconfigured` gate on a node with no clouds.yaml). The live-cloud smoke is IAC-6.

- [✓] **IAC-2: the surface + Overview tab (status band + merged directory).**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** the API status + every published service in one grouped view,
  **so that** I see the whole infrastructure at a glance.
  **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable):
    - [✓] `Surface::InfraCode` in the Workloads dock group, name "Infra as Code (IaC)", its Server (infrastructure) glyph; **Overview shown alone this unit** (the Resources/Heat tab bar lands with those tabs in IAC-3/4 — no disabled-tab or "coming soon" placeholder, §7)

Per-tile actions (logs / restart-armed / self-test / drill) are IAC-3 (they need the Resources verb seam)

The **Mesh services** group (`descriptors` / `probe_nmap` / mackesd registries scan) + the Rescan button fold in at IAC-3 (a code-level seam here, not rendered)

The live-cloud smoke is IAC-6.

- [✓] **IAC-3: the Resources tab — per-service CRUD via the menu bar.**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** to manage every service's resources,
  **so that** the workspace is the full OpenStack admin.

The arming gate + tab/render/menu/fold seams are unit-tested; a live create/delete round-trip is IAC-6 (needs the create/delete verbs above + a throwaway project)
    - **Landed (IAC-3, 2026-07-05):** the shared `list-resources` wire contract — `ResourceTable`/`ResourceRow` + a pure `from_collection_json` parser + `default_collection` in `mackes-mesh-types::openstack` (6 unit tests); the real mackesd `action/cloud/list-resources` Bus verb reusing IAC-1's `ResourceApi`/`Session` client via a new `ResourceSource` seam + a `CloudClient` supertrait (`LiveOpenStack` authenticates → REST `list` → parses; honest `Unconfigured` gate / transport-failure degrade; 3 verb tests — genuinely live end-to-end for Nova servers + Heat stacks on a real Kolla cloud); and `mde-shell-egui/src/iac.rs` — the Overview | Resources | Heat **tab bar**, the **Resources** tab (per-service sortable tables off `list-resources`, row + bulk selection, non-blocking request/reply per the IAC-2 idiom), the **catalog-driven per-service menus** (MENUBAR-ALL governing principle), **typed-arming** on the destructive lifecycle verbs, the **linked jump bar**, and the **Heat** honest-empty state (IAC-4). No shell→mackesd dep (§6). The live-cloud verb-path tuning (per-service collection versioning) is the IAC-6 smoke.

- [✓] **IAC-4: the Heat tab — the native IaC loop.**

    - [✓] **reverse-generate** a HOT template from the live discovered infra (capture reality as code); tested a stack lifecycle + a drift detect + a reverse-generate against fixtures/a live cloud

No shell→mackesd dep (§6). **Every Heat verb is genuinely wired live** through the existing `TokenRestApi` seam (no honest-omitted verb); the live-cloud round-trip tuning (Heat URL/redirect specifics, string-vs-object template body) is the IAC-6 smoke.

- [✓] **IAC-5: audit + notify + comprehensive menu bar.**
  **As** an operator,

    - [✓] every mutating op → the KDC hash-chained audit log; the mesh notify feed fires **only on failure/service-down** (CHAT-FIX-2 producer); tested the audit append + a failure notify

No shell→mackesd dep (§6). And `mackesd/workers/openstack/verbs.rs` — the **`CloudNotifier`** mesh-notify producer publishing the alert-shaped body on `event/notify/cloud` (the CHAT-FIX-2 lane the `chat` worker folds into `alert:<self>`, reusing the `node_grade`/`notify` glue, §6), firing **only** on a mutating-op failure (`mutation_failed`) or a `get-catalog` health-probe `Up→Down` transition (`down_transitions`, first sight seeded silently) — never a routine success or an honest gate; the audit story is closed by `verb_audits` (every lifecycle **and** Heat mutation now flags `audited` + a tracing line, incl. start/stop) with a verb-for-verb test that it agrees with the Bus hash-chain guard (`is_auditable`). Wired into the `OpenstackWorker` drain (`mod.rs`, the notifier rides in/out of the blocking task like the verb cursors). Gate green on the farm: `mde-shell-egui` +29 iac tests, `mackesd workers::openstack` +5 notify/audit tests, clippy clean on the touched files, style-leak + layered-tiers clean. The live-cloud confirmation is the IAC-6 smoke.

  **so that** it's truly done.

## MENUBAR-SWEEP — every workspace fronts the new MenuBar + title (operator directive 2026-07-05, live-seat report)

The shared `mde_egui::menubar` system (`MenuBarModel`: UPPERCASE DISPLAY-tier accent-tinted title · `Menu`/`Entry` trees with Alt-mnemonics · `StatusChip`s — the IAC-5/EDTB-7 idiom) must front EVERY workspace at real per-surface depth. Honest entries only (§7): a gated verb renders disabled-with-reason or is omitted — never a dead item. Operator-named gaps: Workbench (retitle **"State of the Mesh"**), Contacts, Browser (complex — two-engine design), Storage (complex — GParted-class, **"Local Cylinders"**), About (now carrying Device Manager).

- [✓] **MENU-1: Workbench → "State of the Mesh".** **As** the operator, **I want** the Workbench fronted by the new bar under its real name, **so that** the flagship workspace reads like one. **Acceptance:** workbench.rs renders a full `MenuBarModel` titled **"State of the Mesh"** (operator retitle 2026-07-05) with menus at plane depth — View (plane nav), per-plane verb menus (Fleet revisions; the Cloud plane's verbs as QC-12 lands them), Help; status chips (leader, revision, peers); the thin 1-menu bar is retired. *(folded into the in-flight QC-12 worker, session 759c1f91)*
- [✓] **MENU-2: Contacts (chat.rs).** **As** a user, **I want** the roster workspace titled and menued, **so that** Contacts reads like the ICQ-style surface it is. **Acceptance:** chat.rs renders a `MenuBarModel` titled **"Contacts"** — Contacts menu (open 1:1, mark-read, mute/unmute contact — real roster ops only), View (feed filters: alerts/clips/messages, unread-only), Chat (send, clear-unread), Help; chips: unread total + contacts online; all entries drive existing chat/roster seams (§6), honest-gated where a seam is absent.
- [✓] **MENU-3: Browser (web.rs) — the two-engine bar.** **As** a user, **I want** the Browser's bar to carry the two-engine design, **so that** the DD architecture is legible from the chrome. **Acceptance:** an **Engine** menu (Servo ⇄ CEF selection per BROWSER-DD-1's user-selectable engine lock; CEF entries honest-gated "not yet vendored" until BROWSER-DD-1 lands), File/Page (open URL, bookmarks — reuse the existing verbs), View (real zoom/nav verbs only), a Power-mode entry honest-gated per BROWSER-DD-8; chips: active engine + page state; no dead entries. **Superseded in default render path 2026-07-14 by BROWSER-CHROME C0:** Browser keeps this action model but no longer paints the shared MENUBAR-ALL top strip; actions now live under the browser-local toolbar menu.
- [✓] **MENU-4: Storage → "Local Cylinders" (GParted-class).** **As** an operator, **I want** the Storage workspace to be the platform's GParted, **so that** disk surgery has a real pro-tool front. **Acceptance:** storage.rs retitles to **"Local Cylinders"** and grows the GParted menu spine over the E12-20..23 storage plane (UDisks2 + fs toolchain): **Device** (rescan; new partition table — typed-armed), **Partition** (new/delete/resize-move/format-to<fs>/mount-unmount/label — every destructive verb typed-armed, each honest-gated where the live UDisks2 leg is absent), **View** (device rail, geometry/cylinder detail panel), Help; chips: selected device + pending-ops count; §7 — verbs drive the real storage-plane seams, never mock. *(done: 01fc350 — storage.rs → "Local Cylinders", GParted spine Peer·Edit·View·Device·Partition·Help; Resize/Move got real StorageOp legs mirroring the worker wire shapes; typed-armed destructive; live UDisks2 stays E12-23 gated. 723 tests.)*
- [✓] **MENU-5: About + Device Manager coverage.** **As** a user, **I want** the About workspace's bar to cover Device Manager, **so that** the merged surface has one honest chrome. **Acceptance:** the About/DEVMGR bar (device_manager.rs, title "About") covers the host rail (node switch), refresh, category jumps, and the DEVMGR armed-action posture as DEVMGR-11 adds host types; about.rs's non-devmgr face carries the same bar. *(folded into the in-flight DEVMGR-9/11 worker, session 759c1f91)* *(done: a97655d — device_manager.rs bar spine Action·View·Hosts·Device·Help: Hosts=rail twin grouped by kind (Mesh/Cloud/Phones/LAN/Routers, DEVMGR-11 kinds where a real source published), Device=DEVMGR-8 armed verbs on the selection (non-PC hosts honestly omit privileged verbs), View→Jump-to-category. about.rs left bare-by-design (it's the ⓘ modal body, not a naked surface — a bar there = all dead entries). 742 tests, clippy 0.)*
- [✓] **MENU-6: full-coverage backstop.** **As** the shell, **I want** menubar coverage enforced, **so that** no workspace ships bare again. **Acceptance:** an mde-shell-egui test enumerates every routed `Surface` and asserts it renders a `MenuBarModel` with a non-empty title OR appears on an explicit recorded exemption list (with the reason); the remaining thin/missing surfaces (mesh_view, explorer/discovery, editor, terminal, files, music, media, voice, chooser) are inventoried in the test's exemption list as follow-on units. *(done: 4e6f39e — menubar_coverage test, exhaustive match over 17 Surfaces: 9 Covered w/ titles, 8 Exempt w/ recorded MENUBAR-SWEEP follow-on reasons; 4 bars render-proven (UPPERCASE title in painted shapes). Note: mde-shell-egui is binary-only so it sits at storage.rs tail until a main.rs lib-target touch is in scope.)*

## BUG-BROWSER — "no pages rendering" root-caused (live debug 2026-07-05, session 759c1f91)

Root cause CLASS (c): `mde-web-preview/src/engine.rs` called `present()` BEFORE `read_back()` at all three frame sites — surfman swaps unpreserved, so every published frame was all-zeros (proven on Eagle: `render-once` on example.com → `distinct=1 mean_luma=0.0`). The shm seam, shell texture path, and web.rs were innocent. Secondary: both RPM paths re-link the shell WITHOUT `--features live-helper` → fresh installs get a permanently-gated Browser (Eagle only worked at all via a hand-deployed helper build). The BOOKMARKS-5 DoD test only asserted a-frame-arrives — a black frame passed green.

- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-1: the black-frame fix.** *(done: 8ce4d31 — paint→read_back→present reorder + ordering contract documented; landed unverified-live (Servo rebuild gated, below).)*
- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-2: RPM ships a reachable Browser.** *(done: 0ddba6a — xcp-build.sh + build-rpm-fedora43.sh re-link `--features drm,live-helper` before generate-rpm; extends [[strip-verify-replacement-ships-in-rpm]].)*
- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-3: rebuild mde-web-preview + deploy to Eagle + live-verify.** Cold Servo build on BigBoy (crates cached on .90/.130, no net needed) → pull → deploy to Eagle → live-verify. **PARTIAL 2026-07-05 (session 759c1f91):** rebuilt on .130 (7m21s, warm slot `oaksvo`), deployed to Eagle (172.20.146.13, mm/newline-fed-sudo), `render-once https://example.com` now prints **`mean_luma=255.0` (was 0.0)** — the reorder fix is LIVE-PROVEN (black→white: read-back now samples the painted surface). **BUT a SECOND defect remains:** the frame is uniform WHITE (`distinct=1`), and even an instant `data:` URL with an explicit black body + colored text renders uniform white → page CONTENT never composites into the read-back surface. The `render-once` early-return (returns on the first `frame_ready`, the blank clear) is one factor; but the data-URL uniform-white after `load_complete` points at a Servo compositor→surface binding the read_back doesn't sample. **The live shell uses continuous `pump_step` (main.rs:205) so it may accrue content the diagnostic misses — UNVERIFIED on the seat.** → BUG-BROWSER-6 owns the compositor-surface root-cause. Also harden headless_frame to assert non-uniform pixels.
- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-6: content never composites into the read-back frame (Servo compositor-surface).** **As** a user, **I want** actual page content, not a white rectangle, **so that** the browser works. **Acceptance:** root-cause why `read_back()` samples a uniform clear even after `load_complete` for an instant `data:` URL (candidate: the webview paints into a compositor surface distinct from the SoftwareRenderingContext's read surface; or `pump_until_frame`/`pump_step` capture before the content-composite frame — pump N-past-load or gate capture on a non-uniform/`load_complete` frame); fix so `render-once` on a black `data:` URL reports `distinct>1` with real content luma AND the live shell on Eagle shows example.com text; warm slot `oaksvo` on .130 gives fast incremental rebuilds. *(done: ced05eb — render-once captured the FIRST frame_ready which is Servo's EMPTY white scene (fires before the content pipeline); new pump_until_content_frame waits LoadStatus::Complete + 500ms settle + a forced final composite. Surface-mismatch hypothesis DISPROVEN (read_to_image reads the same bound swap-chain surface WR renders into). LIVE on Eagle: example.com distinct=192 luma=237.3, black data: URL distinct=143 luma=2.5 (was distinct=1). headless_frame now asserts distinct>1 AND luma<128 — kills both uniform-frame classes.)*
- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-4: WebSession Drop-kill.** Orphaned `tab` helper process pairs accumulate on Eagle (4 found) — WebSession needs a Drop that reaps its helper; tested spawn→drop→no orphan. *(done: cb26be3 — impl Drop for WebSession (kill+wait reaps the helper child). SCOPE CORRECTION: the fix is in mde-web-preview-client/src/session.rs, NOT web.rs — WebSession + its private child handle live there; the orphan rule forbids impl Drop in the importing crate. std::process::Child detaches on drop → every teardown leaked a live helper; try_wait only reaped already-exited ones. Test spawns sleep 600, records pid, drops, asserts /proc/<pid> gone (distinguishes orphan/zombie/reaped). mde-web-preview-client 42 + mde-shell-egui 761 tests.)*
- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-5: browser_policy is NOT the fd leak (soak-proven) — the EMFILE is process-wide.** *(done: 0a15e6b — a 400-tick fd-soak guard (`after <= before+8`) with a VALIDATED negative control (injected 1-fd/tick leak → assert fires) proves browser_policy holds no leakable handle: every fs call is RAII, no child/socket, the Bus Persist opened once in run() and reused. Live triage: the `Too many open files` hit adfilter\/browser_policy\/kdc_host\/media_server\/mdns_sd\/probe_nmap AT THE SAME INSTANT = whole-process fd exhaustion, browser_policy was a victim not the culprit. Real cause below → BUG-BROWSER-7.)*
- [✓] **BUG-BROWSER-7: process-wide fd budget (the real EMFILE).** **As** an operator, **I want** mackesd to stay under the 1024 nofile limit, **so that** state publishes + socket binds stop failing. **Acceptance:** the systemic cause is that **every async-services worker opens its own `Persist` sqlite connection** (~95 fds floor on Eagle: 33+31 to `/run/mde-bus/index.sqlite`+wal, 17+12 to the local one, +22 sockets) and mdns_sd socket churn tips it over 1024; fix = a **shared process-wide `Persist`** (touches `bin/mackesd.rs` + each worker `run()` signature) and/or bound the mdns_sd socket churn, and/or raise the unit `LimitNOFILE`; tested: mackesd fd count stays well under nofile over a multi-worker soak; live-verify on Eagle no EMFILE over an hour.
  - **Done 2026-07-07:** `mackesd.service` ships `LimitNOFILE=65536`, pinned by a packaging regression test, so the daemon no longer runs under the default 1024-fd ceiling.
  - **Done 2026-07-07:** inbound `mdns_relay` peer republish registrations are capped at 512 unique services, with duplicates still no-op, so noisy/flapping announce traffic cannot grow mdns-sd registrations without bound.
  - **Done 2026-07-07:** L3 stability now gates `LimitNOFILE`, mackesd fd count before/after 1,000 Bus publishes, and fresh EMFILE/Too-many-open-files journal lines. The fd counter now reads `systemctl MainPID` and counts `/proc/$pid/fd` via `sudo`, avoiding false `0→0` results on protected `/proc`.
  - **Done 2026-07-07:** package install hangs found during candidate promotion were removed from the base RPM path: SELinux policy loaders moved out of synchronous `%post` into bounded systemd oneshots that are enabled only, Caddy provisioning is bounded by `timeout 360`, optional `%post` helpers are timeout-bounded, and base weak deps no longer pull `libvirt-daemon-*`/`swtpm` into fresh lighthouse installs.
  - **Candidate evidence:** failed RPM `2b72eea4e14c6e77a97869657733c1fc8ddcca95e67aebf7f47442e63fc0d53d` (112233602 bytes, 2026-07-07 02:51:10 -0400) timed out in `dnf install`; failed RPM `3d284eac32a480ebf23f048cbc73bb9b3b2b160a31cf92ecd0800cd7a273ab8a` still started SELinux policy loading during `%post`; failed RPM `62addc18d543c643d66516ebe3a4fa233e31948c72d98f6af16ccfe52383701e` avoided that but still weak-pulled `swtpm` via libvirt recommends. Passing L3 candidate: `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm`, size `111984624`, sha256 `c9750102cb547b28ba27a75d591ed8d108476894cccd13b75a96f6438f0a214f`; L3 stability passed `14/14` with fd count `131→131`, no fresh EMFILE logs, RSS `29648→29904 KiB`, overlay reachability, chaos, and reboot recovery green.
  - **Promotion evidence 2026-07-07:** same candidate passed L1 install `6/6`, L2 feature mini-mesh `15/15`, and L4 staged lighthouse replacement `33/33`. Promoted in place to Eagle and both DO lighthouses; post-promotion live smoke passed after the expected restart ABRT lines aged out of the 70-second smoke window. Live audit passed: both DO lighthouses report `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64` with `mackesd,nebula,etcd,syncthing` active and no legacy QNM/LizardFS mounts; Eagle reports `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64` with `mackesd,nebula,syncthing` active and no legacy mounts. Immediate live fd sample: `104.131.64.207` fds `138`, `165.227.188.238` fds `139`, Eagle fds `170`; all three have `LimitNOFILE=65536` and `0` EMFILE/Too-many-open-files lines in the recent journal window.
  - **REL-2 final-candidate correction 2026-07-07:** same-NEVRA promotion exposed that Fedora's global `/usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d/10-timeout-abort.conf` can override `TimeoutStopFailureMode=terminate` from the base unit file. The final RPM now also ships `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mackesd.service.d/90-stop-policy.conf` with `TimeoutStopSec=90` and `TimeoutStopFailureMode=terminate`, and `%post` removes only stale local watchdog drop-ins carrying the old `20s`/`abort` stop policy. Farm regression evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services` passed `15/15`.
  - **Final promotion evidence 2026-07-07:** final candidate `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm`, size `112290724`, sha256 `a8e3f464e297ea33239db75bdfc3b4ba3ae9ba7d660474e7e5e73903785a7114`, passed L1 install `6/6`, L2 feature mini-mesh `15/15`, L3 stability `14/14`, and L4 staged lighthouse replacement `33/33`. Promoted in place to Eagle and both DO lighthouses. Effective service policy after promotion: Eagle `TimeoutStopUSec=1min 30s`, `TimeoutStopFailureMode=terminate`, packaged drop-in present; both DO lighthouses `TimeoutStopUSec=1min 30s`, `TimeoutStopFailureMode=terminate`, packaged drop-in present, and no recent `SIGABRT`/`ABRT`/coredump journal lines. Live smoke exited `0`; live audit passed with both DO lighthouses on `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64`, core services active, legacy QNM/LizardFS inactive/not-found, and no FUSE mounts; Eagle is also on `12.0.0-1` with `mackesd,nebula,syncthing` active and no legacy mounts.
  - **Rebuilt final-candidate evidence 2026-07-07:** source changed after the prior final RPM to add durable `%post` cleanup for stale local Quasar `mde-shell.service` units, so the final candidate was rebuilt as `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm`, size `112291230`, sha256 `7e780ab7aee218116865a08b667cf04e7042a6b34d68759f80c3a3439489e251`. Package inspection confirmed `%post` removes only `/etc/systemd/system/mde-shell.service` when it points at `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui`, removes legacy wants, then enables packaged `mde-shell-egui.service`; the RPM also carries `mackesd.service.d/90-stop-policy.conf` with `TimeoutStopSec=90` and `TimeoutStopFailureMode=terminate`. Gates passed again on this exact candidate: L1 install `6/6`, L2 mini-mesh `15/15`, L3 stability/fd budget `14/14`, and L4 staged lighthouse replacement `33/33`. Promoted in place to Eagle and both DO lighthouses. Eagle audit after promotion: `mackesd` stop policy `1min 30s,terminate`, packaged `mde-shell-egui.service` enabled, stale local `mde-shell.service` absent; the same-NEVRA RPM replacement stopped Quasar and Cosmic took the seat, so Cosmic/Xwayland were terminated and `mde-shell-egui.service` was restarted. Final seat proof: `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui` active and owning `/dev/dri/card1` plus `/dev/tty1`, DRM pipe A active. DO promotion verified both lighthouses with `mackesd,nebula,etcd,syncthing` active. Post-roll live smoke passed with both etcd endpoints healthy and Eagle plus both lighthouses online; live audit passed on all three nodes with `STOP_POLICY=1min 30s,terminate`, legacy QNM/LizardFS inactive or not-found/masked, and zero FUSE mounts. The final one-hour fd/EMFILE soak passed (`automation/promotion/live-fd-soak.sh`, start `2026-07-07 17:56:29 UTC`, duration `3600s`). Final sample at elapsed `3603s`: `104.131.64.207` fds `142`, `165.227.188.238` fds `140`, Eagle fds `171`; all promoted services active, all `LimitNOFILE=65536`, all EMFILE/Too-many-open-files counts `0`. Interim fd peaks stayed bounded: `154`, `140`, and `171`.
  - **Pipeline hardening 2026-07-07:** `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh live-audit` now gates the effective systemd stop policy, not just packaged unit text: every DO lighthouse and Eagle must report `STOP_POLICY=1min 30s,terminate`, carry `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mackesd.service.d/90-stop-policy.conf`, and avoid any stale local watchdog drop-in containing `TimeoutStopFailureMode=abort` or `TimeoutStop{Sec,USec}=20s`. Verification after the script change: `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh live-audit` passed on `104.131.64.207`, `165.227.188.238`, and Eagle, each printing `STOP_POLICY=1min 30s,terminate`.
  - **Pipeline hardening 2026-07-08:** `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh cycle` now runs the same post-roll gates the release doctrine requires: after live DO promotion and `live-audit`, it executes MEDIA-LIGHTHOUSE verification and then `automation/promotion/live-fd-soak.sh`. Playlist mutation remains explicitly armed with `MCNF_MEDIA_MUTATE_PLAYLIST=1`; the default cycle proves DNS/account reachability without writing a temporary playlist. The CLI also exposes `fd-soak`/`live-fd-soak` as first-class stages, and `status` now names live audit, media verification, and fd soak as required final-candidate evidence. Verification: `bash -n automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh automation/promotion/live-fd-soak.sh automation/media/verify-media-lighthouse.sh` passed; `automation/media/verify-media-lighthouse.sh --self-test` passed; `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh media-verify` passed non-mutating against `104.131.64.207` (`music.mesh=2`, `music-writer.mesh=1`, Subsonic ping ok); `MCNF_LIVE_FD_SOAK_SECONDS=1 MCNF_LIVE_FD_SOAK_INTERVAL=1 automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh fd-soak` passed on both DO lighthouses plus Eagle with all promoted services active, `LimitNOFILE=65536`, and EMFILE `0`.
  - **Live soak evidence 2026-07-07:** `automation/promotion/live-fd-soak.sh` passed the full one-hour promoted-fleet soak (`duration=3600s`, `since=2026-07-07 08:57:15 UTC`). Final sample at elapsed `3604s`: `104.131.64.207` active `mackesd,nebula,etcd,syncthing`, `LimitNOFILE=65536`, fds `139`, EMFILE `0`; `165.227.188.238` active `mackesd,nebula,etcd,syncthing`, `LimitNOFILE=65536`, fds `140`, EMFILE `0`; Eagle `172.20.146.13` active `mackesd,nebula,syncthing`, `LimitNOFILE=65536`, fds `167`, EMFILE `0`. Interim samples stayed bounded (`104.131.64.207` peaked at `149`, `165.227.188.238` peaked at `142`, Eagle peaked at `174`), with no upward leak trend and no Too-many-open-files journal hits.
  - **Status command 2026-07-07:** `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh status` is now the canonical read-only statrep for the production candidate. Current output shows worklist open/in-progress `42`, release declaration missing (`docs/ops/production-release-declaration.md`), candidate `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` version `12.0.0-1` sha256 `7e1bb539a35ed88620cac154d1e76804282735a98151accaa411b4c244cc79fb`, DO account `active=3 limit=25 free=22 tagged_lighthouses=2` with gate green, lighthouses `104.131.64.207` and `165.227.188.238` on `12.0.0-1`, Eagle `172.20.146.13` on `12.0.0-1`, and farm capacity `0/9` active, `9` free, with all 4 nodes up.
  - **Closure 2026-07-07:** acceptance is satisfied by the shipped `LimitNOFILE=65536` guard, bounded mdns-sd registration churn, L3 fd/EMFILE gate, L1-L4 promotion gates, live audit on both DO lighthouses plus Eagle, and the one-hour promoted-fleet fd soak above. Shared process-wide `Persist` is not required by this candidate unless later live/runtime bug hunting finds a new fd trend; the operator bug-hunt declaration remains a release-level gate, not a BUG-BROWSER-7 blocker.

## PLAT-FONT-2 — platform font → Intel One Mono (operator directive 2026-07-05)

- [✓] **PLAT-FONT-2: Intel One Mono is the platform font.** **As** the operator, **I want** Intel One Mono everywhere, **so that** the platform reads in it.
