Supersedes PLAT-FONT-1 (DroidSansMono); an intermediate Iosevka-kg link was superseded by two subsequent Intel One Mono directives (last wins). **Acceptance:** vendor the Intel One Mono TTF(s) (intel/intel-one-mono, OFL-1.1 — Regular for both Proportional+Monospace; airgap-mirror + NOTICE like f84b47e), swap `mde-egui/fonts.rs` + `mde-term-egui/fonts.rs` + all refs, delete dead DroidSansMono assets, `rg DroidSans` = 0 code hits; note: Intel One Mono HAS no ligatures either (TERM-13 stands). *(queued for the 12:40 reset)* *(done: 12f7dfc — Intel One Mono Regular vendored from Fedora intel-one-mono-fonts-1.4.0 (OTF/CFF only, no TTF cut; egui ttf-parser reads CFF, tests hardened to parse+lay-out a real face); default for both Proportional+Monospace in mde-egui + mde-term-egui; DroidSansMono assets deleted; rg DroidSans=0 code hits; farm .50 129+309 tests, clippy 0 touched. Ligatures behind opt-in ss01 egui never enables → no default ligatures, TERM-13 stands. Residual: 2 style.rs prose mentions left for the live style.rs worker.)*

## PLAT-FONT-3 — platform font → Inter + mono exceptions (operator directive 2026-07-07)

- [✓] **PLAT-FONT-3: Inter is the platform proportional font; monospace remains reserved for terminal/editor/code.** **As** the operator, **I want** the platform font changed to Inter excluding places mono is required, **so that** the shell reads as a modern proportional UI while terminal/editor/code stay fixed-width. Supersedes PLAT-FONT-2 for proportional UI only. **Acceptance:** embed Inter (OFL-1.1) in `mde-egui`, install it first for `FontFamily::Proportional`, keep Intel One Mono first for `FontFamily::Monospace`, keep the platform type scale reduced by 2 pt from the prior larger ramp, and remove stale Droid/Intel-as-proportional prose. **Done 2026-07-07:** `crates/shared/mde-egui/src/fonts.rs` embeds `Inter.ttf` + `IntelOneMono-Regular.otf`, `Style::install` installs that font set for every shared egui surface, and the type scale is `SMALL=10`, `BODY=12`, `TITLE=16`, `HEADING=20`, `DISPLAY=26`. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-egui fonts --features drm` (2 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui rail --features drm` (14 passed), and full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm` (805 passed).

## TRANSFERS — the universal file-operations surface (operator 2026-07-05; 20-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/transfers-surface.md)

A new **"Transfers" tab in the File Browser** (`mde-files-egui`) over a daemon-owned queue: every byte that moves — sftp · rsync · wget/HTTP · browser downloads+scrapes · node↔node · uploads to the mesh music library — is born, tracked, resumed, and completed here. Execution is a **mackesd `transfers` worker** (§9: GUI renders, daemon owns lifecycle); jobs survive restarts + run headless. Per-protocol lanes under one `TransferJob` envelope + one `transfer.submit` verb. **Locked scope = whole surface in one epic (Q20): no partial Transfers tab ships; the surface stays `[>]` until every lane works.** Build the lanes in parallel across the farm.

- [✓] **TRANSFERS-1: the `transfers` mackesd worker — queue, ledger, verbs.** **As** the platform, **I want** a daemon-owned transfer queue, **so that** jobs survive shell restarts and run on headless nodes. **Acceptance** (each runtime-observable): a rank-appropriate `transfers` worker (census entry per the BUG-STORAGE-1 lesson) owning a typed `TransferJob { id, source, dest, method, policy(bwlimit?/verify?), state }` envelope + a **persistent ledger** (queued/running/done/failed, resume state) on the node store; typed Bus verbs `transfer.submit(job)` / `.cancel(id)` / `.pause(id)` / `.resume(id)` / `.list` (Q14) with **CLI parity** (`mackesd transfer …`); parallel execution to a configurable cap (Q12); tested: submit→list round-trip, the state machine, cap enforcement, ledger persistence across a simulated restart. *(done: 6d5f5fb — workers/transfers/{job,ledger,queue,lane,verb}: TransferJob envelope (6 methods, 5-state machine, honest progress/error), LaneRunner async seam (GatedLaneRunner fails-honest until the lanes), TransferQueue (claim_next = the sole cap gate, orphan Running→Queued recovery), TransferVerb inbox (submit/cancel/pause/resume/list, daemon = single writer) + mackesd transfer CLI; census ("transfers",1) rank-Workstation. 42 tests. Lanes 2..6 implement LaneRunner.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-2: the http/wget lane + resume + throttle.** **As** a user, **I want** URL downloads, **so that** I can pull any file. **Acceptance:** an http lane shelling `wget -c --limit-rate` via the bounded-proc path (Q7); progress parsed from wget output (coarse fallback, never fake %); `wget -c` resume on `transfer.resume` (Q11); the per-job bandwidth limit honored (Q12); tested against a fixture http server: download→interrupt→resume continues (not restarts) + a bwlimit caps throughput. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Http` to `HttpWgetLane` and leaves non-HTTP lanes honestly gated; HTTP jobs run bounded `wget --continue --limit-rate=<policy>`, validate URL/destination/rate tokens, create destination parents, and keep child processes kill-on-drop for pause/cancel aborts. `ProgressSink` now lets lanes publish real parsed wget percentages into the queue ledger while Running, capped at 99 so terminal completion remains the only writer of 100. Farm-verified `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd transfers` = 29 passed, incl. fixture HTTP partial-file resume with a captured `Range: bytes=4096-`, wget progress-token parser tests, a measured 32 KiB fixture download capped by `--limit-rate=8k`, and queue progress persistence.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-3: the sftp lane (foreign hosts) + credential hygiene.** **As** a user, **I want** sftp put/get to any host, **so that** I reach off-mesh boxes. **Acceptance:** an sftp lane over the bounded-proc path to an arbitrary host:port (Q5); credentials entered per-job, **never written to the journal** (the Eagle sudo-in-journal lesson); progress + verify state; tested put+get against a fixture sshd + a credential-never-logged assertion. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Sftp` to a bounded OpenSSH `sftp` batch-mode lane. The lane supports one local endpoint plus one remote endpoint in either `sftp://user@host:port/path` or `user@host:path` form, so both put and get ride the same queue/ledger/progress/verify spine. It invokes `sftp` with `BatchMode=yes`, `StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new`, a generated batch file, and no password in argv; password-bearing URLs are rejected up front with a credential-hygiene error that does not echo the secret. SFTP percent tokens are parsed when the client emits them, without fabricated progress. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mackesd 'sftp_' -- --nocapture` = 5 passed/0 failed, including put+get through a fixture SFTP binary and password-URL rejection; `.90` focused real-fixture `cargo test -p mackesd sftp_lane_put_and_get_roundtrip_against_fixture_sshd -- --nocapture` = 1 passed/0 failed against farm `sshd`/`sftp`/`ssh-keygen`; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 58 passed/0 failed; `.170` `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-4: the rsync lane + recurring sync pairs.** **As** a user, **I want** delta mirrors + saved recurring syncs, **so that** big/repeat moves are efficient. **Acceptance:** an rsync lane (`rsync --partial --bwlimit`) with delta transfer + resume (Q7/Q11/Q12); a saved **sync pair** (schedule + source/dest) driven by a mackesd timer fires on schedule and mirrors (Q19); tested: a one-shot rsync round-trips + a recurring pair fires + mirrors on its tick (fixture clock). *(done 2026-07-07: the production `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Rsync` to `RsyncLane`; one-shot jobs run bounded `rsync --archive --partial --info=progress2`, validate source/dest/bwlimit tokens, create local destination parents when safe, parse real `progress2` percentages into the existing `ProgressSink`, and preserve kill-on-drop + timeout behavior. Added durable `sync-pairs/` records with typed save/remove verbs; the worker scans due pairs each tick and submits ordinary rsync jobs so recurring mirrors reuse the queue cap, ledger, progress, verify, and notification paths. Farm-verified on `172.20.0.50`: `timeout 180 cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 43 passed, including rsync argv/bwlimit validation, progress parser coverage, a real local one-shot fixture round-trip, sync-pair persistence/schedule coverage, and a recurring pair that fires and mirrors on tick.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-5: the node lane — stage via the Syncthing share.** **As** a user, **I want** node→node moves, **so that** files reach any peer. **Acceptance:** a node lane that writes the payload into `/mnt/mesh-storage` (the Syncthing plane, Q6) so the substrate replicates to the target peer — never a per-instance dir; the target peer's ledger reflects arrival; tested: a node-lane job lands the file in the mesh-share + the replication is observed (fixture Syncthing/tempdir). *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Node` to `NodeLane`, which accepts only local filesystem sources, rejects URL/ad-hoc remote endpoints honestly, and stages into the shared mesh root from `MDE_TRANSFERS_MESH_SHARE_DIR` or the canonical workgroup root. `node:<peer>` destinations land under `.transfers/node/<peer>/<source-basename>` so the Syncthing-backed plane, not a per-instance directory, owns delivery; `mesh-share` lands directly in the shared root. Queue verification now resolves node destinations against the same staged mesh path, so a verified node job records the staged arrival without process-global env mutation in tests. Farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mackesd 'node_' -- --nocapture` = 101 passed/0 failed with the node cases selected; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 54 passed/0 failed; `.170` fresh `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-6: the Music Library destination (auto-registered).** **As** a user, **I want** to drop a track into the shared music library, **so that** it lands + replicates. **Acceptance:** a standing **"Music Library"** destination auto-registered from node state pointing at the Navidrome library path (the `mcnf-mesh-media` rclone mount / the replicated `/mnt/mesh-storage` music dir, Q9) — verified to be the SHARED mount, not a per-instance dir (MEDIA-6 lesson); a track sent there lands in the library; tested: the destination resolves to the shared path + a file lands there. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` dispatches `Method::Music` to `MusicLibraryLane`, which accepts only local filesystem track sources, resolves the library target to the auto-registered shared workgroup-root `music-library` path or `MDE_TRANSFERS_MUSIC_LIBRARY_DIR`, creates the library directory, and copies the source basename there with honest regular-file/byte-count checks. The destination registry now exposes `Music Library` as the canonical shared music path when a media-capable lighthouse is present, and verify resolves directory destinations to `<dest>/<source basename>` so the completed music job is integrity-checked against the actual landed file. Parallel farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mackesd 'music_' -- --nocapture` = 32 passed, including music lane plan/copy tests; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 50 passed; `.170` `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-7: the auto destination registry.** **As** a user, **I want** my destinations to fill themselves, **so that** I pick a target, not type it. **Acceptance:** a live destinations list — mesh **peers** from the roster, **Music Library** + **mesh-share** from node state (Q10); arbitrary sftp/URL hosts are entered per-job, NOT saved as pins; the list updates on roster/node-state change; tested: the auto set derives from a fixture roster+node-state + a per-job host is not persisted. *(done 2026-07-07: added the transfer destination registry projection over the existing replicated `PeerRecord` node-state plane, not a second destination database. It derives `mesh-share` from the canonical workgroup root, `Music Library` when a media-capable lighthouse is present, and mesh-peer destinations from fresh/non-unreachable roster rows; arbitrary URL/SFTP endpoints remain per-job inputs and are deliberately not persisted as registry rows. Added `mackesd transfer destinations [--json]` so the registry is reachable from the transfer CLI. Parallel farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mackesd transfers::destination -- --nocapture` = 3 passed; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 46 passed; `.170` `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-8: the Transfers tab (renderer) + three entry points.** **As** a user, **I want** the tab, **so that** I see and start every transfer in one place. **Acceptance:** a new "Transfers" tab in `mde-files-egui` subscribing to the worker's ledger (live progress, newest-relevant ordering); the **shared File-Browser MenuBar** gains transfer items (New/Pause-all/Resume-all/Clear-completed + View filters + Destinations manager, Q16); a **New Transfer dialog** (source/dest/method) + **drag-drop onto a destination** + **right-click "Send to →"** in the file list, all feeding `transfer.submit` (Q13); Carbon `Style` tokens (§4); tested: the tab renders ledger fixtures + each entry point emits a submit + the menu items dispatch.
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-9: dock active-transfers badge.** **As** a user, **I want** to see activity without opening Files, **so that** background moves are glanceable. **Acceptance:** the dock's Files cell shows an active-transfers count badge (reuse the NAVBAR/VDOCK badge idiom) from the worker's live count, only when >0; tested badge count from a fixture ledger. *(done 2026-07-07: the shell now mirrors `self.files.transfers_counts().active` into `DockState` each frame, and the shared VDOCK app-cell renderer paints a compact Files-only count badge when the active transfer count is nonzero. The badge uses the same dock cell geometry and Carbon accent/token language as the existing launcher chrome, caps display at `99+`, and registers no badge response at zero. Farm evidence: `.90` focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui the_files_cell_badges_active_transfers_only_when_nonzero -- --nocapture` = 1 passed; BigBoy `.130` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock -- --nocapture` = 45 passed; `.170` `cargo check -p mde-shell-egui` passed.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-10: browser downloads + scrapes enqueue here.** **As** a user, **I want** browser files in the same queue, **so that** there's one download manager. **Acceptance:** the browser's downloads (BROWSER-DD-10) and Power-mode scrape outputs (BROWSER-DD-8, Q17) submit each resulting file as a `transfer.submit` job to a destination + the ledger (Transfers moves outputs, doesn't crawl, Q8/Q17); the browser's own download UI becomes a view onto this queue; tested: a simulated browser download + a scrape-output batch each land as ledger jobs. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferLaneRunner` now dispatches `Method::BrowserDownload` to a browser-output lane that accepts only already-materialized local files, copies them to the selected local destination or directory, reports real completion progress, and reuses the existing queue verify/notify/ledger path. The Browser surface reuses `mde-files-egui::transfers` public wire mirrors and `TransfersClient`, so browser download and Power-mode scrape producers mint ordinary verified `browser_download` `transfer.submit` jobs instead of a second download manager. The full browser download manager/scraper UI remains with the BROWSER-DD units, but their producer hook is no longer gated. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mackesd browser_download -- --nocapture` = 3 passed, including simulated browser download plus scrape-output batch landing as verified ledger jobs; `.90` Browser focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui browser_ -- --nocapture` = 7 passed, including the single-download enqueue; `.90` Browser focused `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui scraper_output_batch -- --nocapture` = 1 passed; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 61 passed; `.170` `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-11: optional per-job integrity verify.** **As** a careful user, **I want** to verify a moved file, **so that** silent corruption is caught. **Acceptance:** a per-job "verify integrity" toggle (Q15) doing size + checksum on completion with a visible verified✓/mismatch✗ state; off by default; a mismatch marks the job failed (not silent); tested: a matching hash → verified, a corrupted fixture → failed. *(done 2026-07-07: `TransferJob` now persists `integrity: Option<IntegrityStatus>` with durable `verified { size_bytes, sha256 }` and terminal `mismatch { source_size?, dest_size?, source_sha256?, dest_sha256?, error }` states. `TransferQueue::complete` honors `policy.verify`: local filesystem source/destination pairs are fingerprinted with size + SHA-256 after the lane reports `Done`; a match transitions to `Done` with progress `100`, while a mismatch transitions to `Failed` with the mismatch report and an honest error. Verification remains off by default. URL/remote sources fail honestly when verification is requested until those lanes can provide an expected source fingerprint, avoiding fake "verified" badges for HTTP downloads. Farm-verified `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd transfers` = 32 passed, including matching-local verified, corrupted-destination failed, unsupported-remote-source failed, and ledger/state-machine regressions.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-12: completion/failure → the chat feed.** **As** a user, **I want** terminal events in my one notification surface, **so that** I don't watch the tab. **Acceptance:** a job finishing or failing emits a signed message on the `event/notify/*` lane the chat worker folds (CHAT-FIX-2, Q18) — no new notification surface; rate-limited/coalesced so a batch of small jobs isn't N toasts; tested each terminal transition → one folded notification. *(done 2026-07-07: the transfers worker publishes terminal events on `event/notify/transfers`, the existing Chat worker already folds that prefix into alert conversations, so no new notification surface was added. Single terminal jobs emit a typed alert-shaped body with `source="transfers"`, severity `info` for Done and `warning` for Failed, plus transfer id/state/method. Multiple jobs reaped in the same scheduler pass coalesce into one summary alert, so a small batch does not create N toasts/messages. The notifier holds only the Bus root and opens `Persist` per emission to avoid carrying non-Send sqlite state across the async worker future. Farm-verified `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd transfers` = 34 passed, including failed-transfer alert emission and same-tick batch coalescing.)*
- [✓] **TRANSFERS-13: surface-complete gate (Q20).** **As** the platform, **I want** the whole surface proven before it ships, **so that** no partial Transfers tab lands. **Acceptance:** an integration pass exercising every lane (http/sftp/rsync/node/music) + both entry surfaces + resume + notify against fixtures in one suite; only when green does TRANSFERS flip from the held `[>]` to done; the tab is not declared reachable-and-done until this passes (§7). *(done 2026-07-07: added the explicit all-lanes surface gate `surface_complete_gate_exercises_every_lane_against_fixtures`, which drives HTTP with partial-file resume, SFTP batch-mode put via fixture binary, rsync mirror/progress, browser materialized-output move, node mesh-share staging, and Music Library landing in one integration pass. Queue/ledger/verify/notify coverage remains in the same `transfers` worker suite, while the Files Transfers tab and Browser producer hooks are separately farm-gated as the two entry surfaces. Farm evidence: `.50` focused `cargo test -p mackesd surface_complete_gate -- --nocapture` = 1 passed; BigBoy `.130` full `cargo test -p mackesd transfers -- --nocapture` = 62 passed, including the new all-lanes gate plus notify/resume/verify regressions; `.90` `cargo test -p mde-files-egui transfers -- --nocapture` = 16 passed for the Transfers tab/menu/form entry surface; `.90` Browser producer gates from TRANSFERS-10 remain green (`browser_` = 7 passed, `scraper_output_batch` = 1 passed); `.170` `cargo check -p mackesd` passed.)*

## SEAT — live Quasar seat bringup + the live-smoke backlog (operator seat pool 2026-07-05; no-flinch)

The operator handed over a physical DRM Fedora seat pool (Eagle .13 · .138 · the CLEAN .2 — see memory). This closes the parked `[live smoke on the seat]` §7 class instead of leaving it as "hardware-gated tail."

- [✓] **SEAT-1: stand up current master on the clean .2 seat.** **As** the operator, **I want** the current shell running on real hardware, **so that** the 30+ units landed this session are provable live. **Acceptance:** build the current-master RPM on BigBoy (workspace release + the Servo mde-web-preview + generate-rpm — the 2-build sequence [[rpm-cut-needs-servo-build]]); install on 172.20.146.2 (mm/<REDACTED — see secret store; rotate: this was public>, fresh Fedora 44 — F42-RPM forward-compat); bring up mackesd + the egui-DRM shell on the .2 seat (card1/renderD128); capture evidence the shell RENDERS (a surface + the taskbar). Least-disruptive target (no one's shell to grab). *(live; standing DAR/VDI authorization)* *(done 2026-07-05: built the 12.0.0 RPM on BigBoy (full 2-build seq incl. the workspace-excluded Servo mde-web-preview; rpm -qlp confirms mackesd+mde-shell-egui+mde-web-preview) → dnf-installed on .2 (F42-RPM clean on F44) → role-pin workstation, mackesd active 45 workers → egui-DRM shell RENDERING on eDP-1: fuser shows it holds card1 as DRM master, CRTC pipe A active 1366x768@60 XR30 plane scanning out, 6min alive vsync-throttled. Pixel capture impossible (exclusive DRM master, no compositor) — CRTC/plane state IS the pixel proof.)*
- [✓] **SEAT-2: run the parked live-smokes on .2** (SETTINGS-6, DEVMGR-12, NODE-GRADE-4, EXPLORER-6, IAC-6) — each renders on the DRM seat + a real action fires; captured. Flips each unit's own live-smoke marker as evidence lands. *(done 2026-07-05: 4/5 live-smokes PASS on the .2 seat via live /run/mde-bus reads — SETTINGS-6 (seat state vol/bt/displays/battery live), DEVMGR-12 (real router 172.20.0.1 on the rail), EXPLORER-6 (self + real LAN hosts, mDNS live), IAC-6 (honest openstack band); NODE-GRADE-4 honest-gated (netdata absent + no mesh peers pre-enrollment).)*

## SEAT-BOOT — the RPM ships no boot-time DRM-seat launcher (found live on .2, 2026-07-05)

⚠️ Found live 2026-07-05 as shipped-but-unwired (the TERM/MEDIA + strip/replace class): the RPM shipped `mesh-shell-setup.service` (starship), the `org.magicmesh.Shell.desktop` session face, and autostart .desktop files (role-chooser, enforce-layout) — but **NO systemd/getty-seat unit that auto-started `mde-shell-egui` on the DRM console at boot**. The packaging core is now present in the 2026-07-06 12.0.0 artifact; this section stays open until the .2 reboot proves the Workstation seat comes up with no manual launch.

- [✓] **SEAT-BOOT-1: ship the DRM-seat boot launcher.** **As** a Workstation user, **I want** the egui shell to start on the console at boot, **so that** the box is usable without an SSH hand-launch. **PACKAGING CORE DONE 2026-07-06:** the existing Quasar DRM-seat unit now ships in the base RPM manifest as `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mde-shell-egui.service` and is enabled in `%post`; it remains self-gated on `/var/lib/mde/role.toml` `role="workstation"`. `onboard role-provision` now treats `mde-shell-egui.service` as a rank-1 Workstation unit, so Lighthouse masks it and Workstation unmasks/enables it. The headless server RPM variant intentionally does not ship the seat unit because it does not ship the shell binary. **Role-gate fix 2026-07-06:** live testing found systemd parsed the old shell-grep ExecCondition without the TOML quotes, so Workstation units skipped even with `role = "workstation"`. The packaged shell and brand units now use `ExecCondition=/usr/bin/mackesd role-gate --min-rank 1`; `mde-musicd.service` was corrected from the retired rank-2 Workstation tier to rank 1. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services` (8 passed, including manifest coverage and the typed rank-1/no-grep regression). Artifact evidence after this fix: `MCNF_BUILD_SHAPE=big automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh build` produced `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` (`magic-mesh 12.0.0-1 x86_64`, 110089410 bytes, built 2026-07-06 19:47 EDT); `rpm -qpl` shows `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mde-shell-egui.service`, `/usr/lib/systemd/system/magic-mesh-brand.service`, and `/usr/lib/systemd/user/mde-musicd.service`; extracting the RPM shows all three use `ExecCondition=/usr/bin/mackesd role-gate --min-rank 1` and no `grep -Eq`/`--min-rank 2`; `rpm -qp --scripts` enables `mde-shell-egui.service`, `magic-mesh-brand.service`, and globally enables `mde-musicd.service`. Live `.2` attempt 2026-07-06: copied the candidate RPM to `172.20.146.2`, forced `rpm -Uvh --replacepkgs`, verified the installed package now contains `/usr/lib/systemd/system/mde-shell-egui.service`, `systemctl is-enabled mde-shell-egui.service` returned `enabled`, then rebooted; post-reboot polling timed out and ICMP/TCP reported unreachable/no route. Live `.138` hot-patch proof 2026-07-06: installed the corrected units over `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64`, `ExecCondition` parsed as `/usr/bin/mackesd role-gate --min-rank 1`, `systemctl restart mde-shell-egui.service` stayed active, `mde-shell-egui` held `/dev/tty1`, `/dev/dri/card1`, and `/dev/input/event*`, and `card1-eDP-1` was connected at `1920x1080` with pipe A active/fb=144. Rebooting `.138` then made it unreachable from `172.20.145.192` (`Destination Host Unreachable`, TCP/22 `No route to host`), so reboot acceptance was not captured. Live Eagle `.13` hot-patch proof: typed gate passed, but shell initially failed `set_crtc(...): Permission denied` because Cosmic (`cosmic-comp`/Xwayland/session processes) already owned card1; Eagle became the clean acceptance target after retiring that compositor session and removing the legacy local pre-RPM `/etc/systemd/system/mde-shell.service`. **Acceptance:** the RPM ships a `mde-shell.service` (or getty-seat unit) that starts `mde-shell-egui` on card1 as the seat user at boot with restart-on-crash, role-gated to Workstation (masked on Lighthouse per §5 Option 1); `rpm -qlp` shows the unit; live-verify on .2, .138, or another clean Workstation seat: reboot → the shell comes up on eDP-1 with no manual launch. *(done via Eagle `.13` live reboot proof below; `.2`/`.138` remain separately unreachable hardware/network targets, not blockers for this acceptance because the alternate clean Workstation seat passed.)*
    - 2026-07-07 update: found a second packaging bug after seats came back online — RPM-installed Workstations boot `multi-user.target`, while the shell unit was only `WantedBy=graphical.target`, so the enabled unit was not pulled in at boot. Fixed `packaging/bootc/units/mde-shell-egui.service` to install under `WantedBy=multi-user.target graphical.target`, added `onboard::role_provision::tests::drm_seat_unit_starts_on_rpm_and_bootc_boot_targets`, and updated `packaging/bootc/verify-image.sh` to check the typed role gate. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services` passed 13/13 on `.50`; `git diff --check -- packaging/bootc/units/mde-shell-egui.service packaging/bootc/verify-image.sh crates/mesh/mackesd/src/onboard/role_provision.rs` clean. Rebuilt candidate with `MCNF_BUILD_SHAPE=big automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh build`: `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm`, 112289719 bytes, built 2026-07-07 10:36 EDT, sha256 `e9e2ddbaaae4e3c22003d00bcf8df329e99622a7407e1aae5e0f00551de3b925`. Extracted RPM unit shows `ExecCondition=/usr/bin/mackesd role-gate --min-rank 1` and `WantedBy=multi-user.target graphical.target`. Live install evidence: `.138` and `.2` both accepted the fixed RPM, `systemctl get-default` returned `multi-user.target`, `systemctl is-enabled mde-shell-egui.service` returned `enabled`, and `/etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/mde-shell-egui.service` existed after `systemctl enable`. Live reboot acceptance remains blocked: after reboot, both `.138` and `.2` timed out and are unreachable from both the dev host and Eagle (`No route to host`/100% packet loss). Eagle `.13` remains reachable but compositor-owned, so it is not a clean acceptance target. Do not close until a clean seat returns after reboot and proves `mde-shell-egui.service` active plus DRM/eDP scanout.
    - 2026-07-07 final candidate evidence: the same target-binding fix is present in final candidate `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` (size `112290724`, sha256 `a8e3f464e297ea33239db75bdfc3b4ba3ae9ba7d660474e7e5e73903785a7114`); extracting the RPM still shows `ExecCondition=/usr/bin/mackesd role-gate --min-rank 1` and `WantedBy=multi-user.target graphical.target`. The packaging part is carried by the promoted final RPM, but live reboot acceptance remains blocked until a clean Workstation seat is reachable after reboot.
    - 2026-07-07 Eagle reboot acceptance: inventoried the LAN after WOL; `.2` stayed stale at MAC `e4:b9:7a:5d:71:e0`, `.138` stayed failed at MAC `8c:16:45:fe:d3:7c`, and neither answered ping/TCP/22, while Eagle `.13` was reachable and role-pinned `workstation`. Cleaned Eagle's old local `/etc/systemd/system/mde-shell.service` (a pre-RPM hand-written launcher that also started `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui` and could race the packaged unit), terminated the Cosmic/Xwayland session, and started the packaged `mde-shell-egui.service`. Pre-reboot proof: service `active`, `enabled`, `mde-shell-egui` was the only shell owner, `fuser` showed it holding `/dev/dri/card1`, `/dev/tty1`, and `/dev/input/event*`, and DRM debugfs showed eDP-1 on pipe A with a nonzero framebuffer. Rebooted Eagle, SSH returned, and post-boot proof showed `mde-shell-egui.service` `enabled` + `active`, core services `mackesd/nebula/syncthing` active, legacy local `mde-shell.service` absent, only `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui` in the shell/compositor process set, `fuser` again showing it owns `/dev/dri/card1`, `/dev/tty1`, and input events, and DRM debugfs showing eDP-1 connected with pipe A `active=1` and framebuffer `fb=144`/`fb=110` on the primary plane. Post-reboot `automation/promotion/mcnf-promotion-cycle.sh live-smoke` and strengthened `live-audit` both passed, so the live fleet stayed healthy after the seat proof.
    - 2026-07-07 durable cleanup follow-up: added a narrow RPM `%post` cleanup that disables/removes only `/etc/systemd/system/mde-shell.service` files containing `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui`, plus their target wants, before enabling the packaged `mde-shell-egui.service`. Farm regression: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services` passed `15/15`, including the legacy-launcher cleanup assertion.
- [✓] **SEAT-BOOT-2: dot-free topic addressing for `localhost.localdomain`.** DONE 2026-07-06. Fixed the split between registry and persistence by allowing DNS-style dots in Bus topic segments while still rejecting wildcard publish names, empty segments, and `.`/`..`/`..` traversal. Fresh Fedora's default `localhost.localdomain` now seeds `peer/localhost.localdomain/{alerts,system}` and persisted `audit/localhost.localdomain` topics write + list cleanly, so `mde-bus topic list` no longer fails on an unenrolled node because of the dotted hostname. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-bus dotted_unenrolled` (2 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-bus topic` (52 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-bus` (370 passed). **Acceptance:** the topic validator/addresser tolerates (or sanitizes) a dotted hostname, or the node adopts a dot-free local id pre-enrollment; `mde-bus topic list` works on an unenrolled node; tested. *(self-resolves at enrollment but breaks the CLI on a fresh box)*
- [✓] **SEAT-BOOT-3: unenrolled self-view doubles the `peer:` prefix.** DONE 2026-07-06. Normalized peer hostnames at the `unit_aggregator` boundary before creating `peer:<host>` ids, matching self rows, comparing leaders, and deriving `mesh_tunnel` endpoints. Prefixed unenrolled rows like `peer:localhost.localdomain` now fold to one `peer:localhost.localdomain` unit named `localhost.localdomain`, and the matching self-edge no longer creates `peer:peer:localhost.localdomain`. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::unit_aggregator --features async-services` (59 passed). **Acceptance:** the unenrolled self-representation appears once; tested.

## NOTIF — the re-envisioned notification & status area (operator 2026-07-05; 10 ideas + 50-Q `/plan`; design: docs/design/notification-area-redesign.md)

Replace today's duplicative status area (mesh told 4×, glyph+dot redundancy, notifications across 3 channels, tray.rs/dock.rs double path) with a calm **4-pip health bar** (Device·Mesh·Power·Alerts) that expands to detail, **Chat as the single notification home**, and a reserved **critical edge light-show** as the only push. Carbon semantic status tokens throughout. **Locked scope = ONE epic (Q48): no partial notification area ships; NOTIF stays `[>]` until NOTIF-13 gates it green.**

- [✓] **NOTIF-1: Carbon status tokens + severity taxonomy + motion.** DONE 2026-07-07. Added semantic Carbon support tokens in `mde-egui::Style`: `SUPPORT_ERROR`, `SUPPORT_WARNING`, `SUPPORT_SUCCESS`, `SUPPORT_INFO`, mapped to the existing red/amber/green/blue palette. Added `StatusMotion` + `Motion::status_transition_at(elapsed, worsened)` so every status change fades and only worsening transitions get one bounded attention pulse. Updated shared `Severity::color()` and the Chat notification lane severity rendering to use the support tokens instead of local `DANGER/WARN/ACCENT` choices. Also wired `mde-shell-egui::status` into the shell module tree so its existing status/edge-cue tests compile; mounting the 4-pip bar remains NOTIF-3. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-egui` (132 passed, including `carbon_support_tokens_map_the_notification_taxonomy`, `status_transition_fades_and_pulses_only_on_worsening`, and `severity_colors_use_carbon_support_tokens`), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests::presence_and_severity_map_to_style_tokens_not_raw_hex` (1 passed), and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status` (32 passed, including the compiled status module's support-token and critical-edge tests). **Acceptance:** shared support-error/warning/success/info tokens; Red=alert, Amber=warning, Blue=info taxonomy; shared fade+pulse-on-worsening motion helper; backing tests; no new raw hex outside `Style`.
- [✓] **NOTIF-2: the notify producer delta (mackesd, extend CHAT-FIX-2).** DONE 2026-07-07. Extended the existing `workers::notify` producer, not a new worker. It still owns severity tagging and the bounded coalescing ring, and now also publishes daemon-owned segment rollups on `state/notify/segment/{device,mesh,power,alerts}` for the shell pips. Source mapping: peers→Mesh, updates→Power, service/disk/SMART→Device, journal/cloud/node-grade→Alerts. The worker preserves the worst severity per segment so a lower warning cannot overwrite an active critical. It now watches the existing `<workgroup_root>/node-grade/*.json` mirror and emits D/F transitions (`D` warning, `F` critical), folds the existing OpenStack `event/notify/cloud` lane into the Alerts rollup without duplicating its Chat event, and uses affected-host attribution for critical policy: local criticals publish `own-seat-light-show`, remote/cloud/node-grade criticals publish `remote-pip-chat`. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/notify.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::notify --features async-services` (15 passed, including per-source fixtures, coalescing, node-grade D/F, cloud lane fold, worst-severity rollup, and cross-node policy). **Acceptance:** the existing notify worker tags each event Red/Amber/Blue (Q28), debounce+coalesces a flapping source into one updating item (Q32), publishes per-segment (Device/Mesh/Power/Alerts) worst-severity rollups the bar folds (Q47), watches the comprehensive source set (peers/failed-units/updates/disk-SMART/journal-WARN+/cloud/node-grade-D-F, Q33), and carries the cross-node critical policy (own-seat light-show vs remote pip+Chat, Q41); no new worker; fixture-tested per source → severity + one coalesced rollup.
- [✓] **NOTIF-3: the 4-pip health bar + local-grade pip.** DONE 2026-07-07. The notification/status controls now mount in the full-width bottom rail, not the left rail: Advanced, Desktop, detected sessions, the Windows-style tray caret, Device/Mesh/Power/Alerts micro-icons, Pin, and Clock share the thin rail while the left dock keeps only app/workbench/system launch cells. `Shell::mount_dock_chrome` polls `StatusState` once per frame cadence and feeds the cloned daemon segment snapshot into `DockState`; missing rollups render with the dim baseline. The bottom-rail status cluster routes segment clicks through the dock (`Alerts -> Chat`, `Mesh -> MeshView`, Device/Power -> System), and the caret toggles the detail panel. The old `tray::status_quads` call is gone from `dock.rs` and its old mesh/seat/unread/session state is ignored at the dock boundary. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui rail --features drm` (14 passed, including Advanced/Start, Desktop split control, session rail, and source flyout regressions), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-theme tray_glyphs` (tray glyphs rasterize nonempty at 16/24 px), and full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui --features drm` (805 passed). **Acceptance:** the `status` module renders the Device·Mesh·Power·Alerts glyph+color pips in the bottom rail (Q5/Q9/Q11/Q13/Q24), each tinted by its segment rollup (Q21/Q22/Q23/Q11), healthy→faint dim baseline (Q15/Q45), **replacing BOTH VDOCK status quads** (Q6); segment click routes (Mesh→Mesh, Alerts→Chat, Device→sliders, Power→System, Q14); count-free (Q4/Q50); honest dim pre-poll; shell tests.
- [✓] **NOTIF-4: the chevron expansion panel.** DONE 2026-07-07. The bottom-rail tray caret now toggles the detail panel from the Windows-style rail, preserving the panel after status icons moved out of the left dock. The panel uses the shared `Motion::BASE` animation value for both slide offset and opacity fade, renders the existing `status::status_panel` content above the rail, and expands the rail `Area` only while the panel is opening/open/closing so panel hit tests work without permanently widening chrome. It shows local + peer grades from `NodeGrades` (local first, peers worst-first through the status renderer) and the device band from the live `SeatSnapshot`; tapping a peer grade records the dock's Explorer node-focus request, and tapping the device band routes to System. Esc and click-away dismiss the panel. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock` (35 passed, including `the_status_chevron_opens_and_dismisses_the_detail_panel` exercising the rail caret and `the_status_panel_routes_device_controls_and_grade_rows`). **Acceptance:** a caret opens the slide/fade detail panel (Q17/Q27) showing local grade + peer grades (worst-first, tap→Explorer hero) + device sliders (Vol/BT, Q7/Q18); Esc/click-away dismiss; tested render + route.
- [✓] **NOTIF-5: retire tray.rs into the one status module.** DONE 2026-07-07. Deleted `crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/tray.rs` and removed `mod tray`; the retired 2×2 quad model, `TrayItem`, `TrayInputs`, `STATUS_QUADS`, `status_quads`, `quad_cell_id`, and tray-only tests are gone from live source. The surviving good folds live in the one status path: `status.rs` owns the battery fill ladder, system-pack pick, battery tone, segment pips, and NOTIF-4 device band; `chrome.rs` remains only the shared mesh-status/grade poll source consumed by dock status. Cleaned stale source comments that still referenced tray/status-quads. Evidence: `rg -n "TrayItem|TrayInputs|STATUS_QUADS|status_quads|quad_cell_id|tray::|crate::tray|mod tray|tray.rs" crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src` returns no matches; `rg -n "status quads|status-quad" crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/{main.rs,dock.rs,chrome.rs,chat.rs,curtain.rs}` returns no matches. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status` (32 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock` (39 passed), and full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (784 passed). **Acceptance:** the good folds (glyph/dot/battery-ladder) move into the NOTIF-3 module; `tray.rs`'s flyout/chevron remnants deleted; `rg TrayItem`/dead-tray = 0 live refs; tests green.
- [✓] **NOTIF-6: the critical edge-cue light-show.** DONE 2026-07-07. The shell now owns and mounts `status::CriticalEdgeCue` from the same daemon segment snapshot that feeds the dock pips. `Shell::render` updates it after the status poll with the local hostname and DND/focus-mute suppression, then draws the no-text all-edges cue after the curtain so an own-seat critical can still light the screen edges with the dock hidden/covered. The cue remains critical-only, own-seat-policy gated, acknowledges by edge click, clears on resolve, and DND/focus mute suppresses ambient push without clearing the live state. Added a shell integration test proving a seeded own-seat critical registers the foreground edge-cue hit region from a normal shell frame. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui shell_mounts_the_critical_edge_cue_from_own_seat_rollups` (1 passed) and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status` (32 passed, including trigger/ack/resolve/DND-mute/pulse/tessellation tests). **Acceptance:** a critical (red) fires an **all-edges Carbon-red pulsing light show** (Q34/Q37) that pulses a few cycles then settles to a held thin edge glow while live, clearing on resolve/ack (Q42); criticals only (warnings/info pull-only); fires on the affected node's OWN seat (Q41); not a text toast; tested trigger + settle + DND-mute.
- [✓] **NOTIF-7: OSD → centered Carbon pill; kill notification toasts.** DONE 2026-07-07. Restyled `mde-egui::ToastHost::osd` from the old bottom level bar into a centered Carbon pill with the hardware glyph, fill track, and percentage text. The shell `ToastBridge` no longer calls the chyron renderer or enqueues alert toasts; the `event/toast/show` lane still applies DND/focus/audio suppression and the single severity-scaled notification sound, while Chat remains the visible notification home. Packaging follow-up 2026-07-07: the full RPM now weak-recommends `libcanberra-gtk3`, which provides `canberra-gtk-play`, so fresh Workstations get the sound helper by default while the headless server variant stays seat-free and a minimal/opt-out install honestly degrades to silence. Added bridge tests proving an alert rings without tessellating popup geometry and that the OSD pill still renders through the shell bridge; added an RPM-manifest regression for the sound-player dependency. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-egui toast` (18 passed, including `osd_tessellates_a_real_centered_pill_when_present`), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui toast_bridge` (14 passed, including `an_alert_does_not_tessellate_a_notification_popup_through_the_bridge` and `the_osd_pill_still_tessellates_through_the_bridge`), full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-egui` (132 passed), full `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (786 passed), and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd onboard::role_provision --features async-services` (9 passed, including `base_rpm_recommends_the_notification_sound_player`). **Acceptance:** vol/brightness render a brief **centered Carbon pill** (glyph + fill bar + %, auto-dismiss, never hits Chat, Q16); the notification-class toast/chyron path is removed in favor of Chat (Q3); tested the pill renders + no notification toast appears.
- [✓] **NOTIF-8: the Chat Notifications lane.** DONE 2026-07-06. Added the dedicated Chat **Notifications** lane in `chat.rs`: a session-only aggregate view over folded alert messages from the originating host contact, severity-colored and newest-first. The roster lane row and pane header show unread state while the shell Chat icon continues to count the underlying host alert once via the shared contact timeline. Opening the lane writes only the session watermark; source host attribution remains on every row. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests::notification` (3 passed) and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests` (24 passed). **Acceptance:** a dedicated Notifications lane in `chat.rs` (Q30), each item a message **from its originating host contact** (Q29), severity-colored newest-first, **session-only** (Q38), the **unread count on the Chat icon/lane header** (the only count, Q50); tested render + host-contact attribution + session-clear.
- [✓] **NOTIF-9: typed configurable action buttons.** DONE 2026-07-07. Folded Chat alerts now carry a configurable typed action set (`AlertAction` / `AlertActionKind`: safe, destructive, ack, snooze) in addition to the legacy single `action_verb`; alert JSON can provide an `actions` array, while old `action` payloads still map to a safe Open action. The shell renders those typed actions in both the contact timeline and aggregate Notifications lane, posting `action/chat/alert-action` requests. The mackesd chat worker drains that verb: safe actions publish their configured Bus verb, destructive actions are refused until the UI sends an armed request, ack writes `state/chat/alert-ack/<message-id>`, and snooze writes `state/chat/alert-snooze/<message-id>`. The shell consumes those ack/snooze state topics so acknowledged/snoozed alerts leave the aggregate Notifications lane while remaining in append-only contact history; a newer live alert with a new id reappears. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/services/mde-chat/src/message.rs crates/services/mde-chat/src/alert.rs crates/services/mde-chat/src/lib.rs crates/mesh/mackesd/src/workers/chat.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/chat.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-chat` (38 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::chat --features async-services` (32 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests` (26 passed). **Acceptance:** notifications carry a **configurable typed action set** (Q31/Q35) as Carbon buttons driving real mackesd verbs; destructive ones **typed-armed** in the message; ack clears + live re-raise + snooze (Q36); tested a safe action fires + an armed action gates + ack/re-raise.
- [✓] **NOTIF-10: inline Chat notification config + DND.** DONE 2026-07-06. Added an aggregate Chat **Notifications** lane over folded host alert messages with inline controls for per-source mute, minimum alert severity, and the authoritative `dnd.yaml` toggle. Per-source mute persists through `NotifyPrefs`/`notify.json`; threshold changes publish `action/chat/notify-prefs` and persist through the chat worker; DND writes `mde_bus::dnd` while preserving topic snoozes. The feed remains populated even when muted/DND-gated; source-muted alerts are still logged. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-chat notify` (6 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mackesd workers::chat --features async-services` (31 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui chat::tests` (23 passed), `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (785 passed). **Acceptance:** inline in the Chat lane (Q39): per-source mute + min-severity filter + a **DND** toggle that mutes the sliver+pulses but still fills the feed + keeps the Alerts pip live (Q40); persisted; tested each control applies.
- [✓] **NOTIF-11: accesskit live-region for the pips/alerts.** DONE 2026-07-07. Enabled the shared `mde-egui` AccessKit feature for the shell and exported live accessibility nodes from the one status path: the whole 4-pip status strip now installs a polite `Role::Status` live region named "Notification status" with local grade plus Device/Mesh/Power/Alerts state; each segment pip installs a named `Role::Button` with its current state/action; and a live own-seat critical edge cue installs an assertive `Role::Alert` named "Critical alert". Added AccessKit-backed tests proving the live-region nodes, roles, names, values, and live modes exist when AccessKit is enabled. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/status.rs crates/shared/mde-egui/src/lib.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status::tests::status_bar_exports_accesskit_live_region_and_named_pips` (1 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status::tests::critical_edge_cue_exports_an_assertive_accesskit_alert` (1 passed); `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui status` (35 passed). **Acceptance (operator a11y override, Q44):** the 4 pips + the Alerts state + a new critical are wired into accesskit as a live region a screen reader announces (name + state); tested the live-region nodes exist + update on state change.
- [✓] **NOTIF-12: restyle the system quad to the pip language.** DONE 2026-07-07. The VDOCK-4 system quad now renders as compact glyph pips in the same language as the NOTIF status strip: inactive cells use the shared dim baseline, while hover/active states reveal semantic Carbon tones for Settings/info, Show-Desktop/success, Lock/warning, and Power/error. The routing/action seams are unchanged: Settings still opens System, Show-Desktop routes Desktop, Lock records the curtain-lock request, and Power opens the typed-armed power menu. Farm evidence: `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock::tests::system_quad_cells_use_status_pip_tint_language` (1 passed) and `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui dock` (40 passed, including `each_system_quad_cell_dispatches_its_route_or_action`). **Acceptance:** the VDOCK-4 system quad (Settings/Show-Desktop/Lock/Power) adopts the same glyph+tint Carbon language as the pips (Q49), **actions unchanged**; tested render + actions still dispatch.
- [✓] **NOTIF-13: surface-complete gate (Q48).** **DONE 2026-07-07.** Added the shell-level fixture `notif13_fixture_mounts_status_chat_edge_and_accesskit_together`, which mounts one real shell frame over a temp Bus fixture with daemon segment rollups + a folded critical Chat alert. The frame proves the bar, Alerts pip, chevron, expanded status panel, own-seat critical edge cue, Chat Notifications lane, typed Ack/Safe action buttons, DND toggle, and AccessKit polite/assertive live regions together. Added narrow test seams only: `ChatState::with_bus_root`, `ChatState::select_notifications_for_test`, `notification_count_for_test`, stable Chat action/DND response IDs, and `DockState::open_status_panel_for_test`. Farm evidence: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --config skip_children=true --check crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/chat.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/main.rs crates/desktop/mde-shell-egui/src/dock.rs`; `./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui notif13_fixture_mounts_status_chat_edge_and_accesskit_together` (1 passed); `MCNF_BUILD_HOST=172.20.0.130 ./install-helpers/xcp-build.sh cargo test -p mde-shell-egui` (792 passed); `git diff --check` clean. Live-seat evidence on Eagle `.13`: built and installed the current candidate `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` (packaged `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui` size `30068592`, timestamp `2026-07-06 22:18:44 -0400`); deliberately stopped the old `cosmic-session`/`cosmic-comp`/`Xwayland` seat owner; `systemctl start mde-shell-egui.service` passed the Workstation role gate and stayed `active (running)`; `fuser` showed `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui` owning `/dev/dri/card1`, `/dev/tty1`, and `/dev/input/event*`; DRM debugfs showed eDP-1 connected and scanout active at `1920x1080` on pipe A with primary plane framebuffer `[FB:145]` allocated by `mde-shell-egui`; Bus fixture publishes landed valid latest rollups on `state/notify/segment/alerts` (`critical`, `source:"notif13-live-smoke"`, `critical_policy:"own-seat-light-show"`) and `state/notify/segment/device` (`warning`). No framebuffer capture utility was present on Eagle, so the pixel-level edge glow is proven by the fixture/accesskit/tessellation tests plus the live Bus+DRM scanout evidence rather than by a screenshot. **Acceptance:** an integration pass exercising the bar + expansion + edge-cue + Chat lane + actions + DND + accesskit together (fixtures); live-smoke on a seat that the calm bar renders + a fixture critical fires the edge-cue.

## PLANS-FOLDED — `/plan`-tracked epics folded into the worklist (operator "add all plans" 2026-07-12)

The GUI/feature work below lived in `/root/.claude/plans/*.md` (the `/plan` outputs) instead of
inline above. Folded in here so the worklist indexes it. **Reconcile marker-vs-code before
draining** (standing drift rule); the plan files hold the detailed spec.

### WIN10-HYBRID — full GUI polish + Win10-structure bottom taskbar (Quazar identity)
Plan: `.claude/plans/snappy-discovering-sphinx.md`; design: `docs/design/win10-taskbar.md`.
**Supersedes NAVBAR** (this file's NAVBAR epic). Landed origin `f3811bc6`.
- [x] Phase A — `mde-egui` shared tokens (depth `Elevation`/radii · 2px focus ring · 4-density · motion springs/inertial/micro-interactions · IBM Plex Mono)
- [x] Phase B core — 48px bottom taskbar · bottom strut · left dock retired · two-line clock-with-date
- [x] Phase C — depth/focus/motion adopted across 17 surfaces (4 farm-fanout waves, integration-green)
- [x] Phase D — design-lock docs reconciled · F44 RPM `12.0.0-1` cut · **deployed + live-verified on seat 172.20.0.15** (DRM shell up, `"drm":true`)
- [~] B5-rest — Win10 tray recompose. **DONE (origin `b309518b`, 2026-07-12): action-center→`Surface::Chat` cell + show-desktop nub** (routing + geometry-non-overlap tests, 1110/1110 green, 0 style leaks). **Start-grid restyle B6 DONE 2026-07-15:** square Win10-HYBRID Start chrome, grouped tile bodies, category accent rails, hover/focus/pinned states, and one authoritative grouped grid exposing every tileable `Surface::ALL` entry exactly once; `.50` `cargo test -p mde-shell-egui start_menu -- --nocapture` passed 48/48. **Remaining (visual — need live eyes on .15 after a redeploy):** ▲ overflow flyout · auto-hide bottom-edge reveal · running-icons-only · VDI hover thumbnails.
- [x] mesh-enroll seat .15 into `magic-mesh` — DONE 2026-07-12: `peer:Basement-Test-Workstation` at overlay `10.42.0.8`, boot-durable, bidirectionally verified vs LH1 (`mackesd add-peer` on the CA → `mackesd join` on .15).

### BROWSER-CHROME — pixel-faithful stock Chrome chrome over real Chromium (CEF)
Plan: `.claude/plans/browser-chrome-faithful.md`. Sibling/refinement of **BROWSER-DD**. Started.
**Operator override 2026-07-15:** Browser GUI work is not constrained by Carbon
requirements and should apply Material Design 3 principles within the Browser
workspace only. The chrome should be judged by browsing usability, legible
render/input state, adaptive layout, Material interaction states, and fit for a
real enterprise browser rather than Carbon token compliance.
- [x] E1 (gate) — live CEF ABI validation on a seat (`mde-web-cef render-once`
  → non-garbage BGRA frame); gate runtime activation on it. **Render gate
  command fixed/proven 2026-07-14:** `mde-web-cef render-once` now initializes
  CEF and runs the one-shot windowless render path directly instead of requiring
  the diagnostic-only `MDE_CEF_BROWSER_PROBE=1` env. Farm `.50` proof against
  `/home/mm/mde-cef-active` emitted `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK` and
  `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... view=640x360 last_paint=640x360` with no probe
  env. The installer now gates runtime activation with the same render smoke
  when the packaged helper and renderer bridge are present; farm `.50` proved
  that path with `install-cef-runtime: render smoke passed`. Live bench seat
  `.15` proof used the new helper+bridge binaries against the installed
  `/opt/mde/cef` bundle and emitted the same `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK` +
  `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... view=640x360 last_paint=640x360`; the installed
  DRM shell on `.15` was active with `NRestarts=0`. **Packaged F44 seat closure
  2026-07-14:** after `f3c8d882` and `b4dad608`, cut a Fedora 44 full RPM on the
  farm/container path at `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.f44.x86_64.rpm`
  (SHA-256 `cd806b2e10affa66f482bffb94c5dcc223ec303d7bd0d083363632917cf7f5b7`,
  build date `Tue Jul 14 16:19:12 2026`) and reinstalled it on `.15` with
  `dnf --disablerepo=magic-mesh reinstall`. Installed payload contains
  `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`,
  `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`, and the shell service with
  `Delegate=yes`. Running packaged `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime`
  on `.15` ended with `install-cef-runtime: render smoke passed`. Installed
  `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef render-once` emitted `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK` +
  `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY` for both a data URL (`640x360`) and
  `https://example.com/` (`800x600`). Restarted `mde-shell-egui.service`; it is
  active/running with `NRestarts=0` and `Delegate=yes`. **Render-gate shutdown
  hardening 2026-07-14:** a farm rerun found `render-once` could paint
  (`CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY`) and still exit 139 during teardown; the bridge now
  installs `cef_life_span_handler_t::on_before_close` and waits for that callback
  before `cef_shutdown`. Patched `.50` proof: `render-once` against
  `/home/mm/mde-cef-active` rendered `https://example.com/` at `800x600` and
  exited 0; `cef-verify` delivered three `1280x800` frames plus URL/title over
  the shell `WebSession` wire and returned `VERIFY RESULT=PASS`; the
  `mde-shell-egui` live Browser UI smoke with `MDE_CEF_LIVE_UI_SMOKE=1` passed
  against the same patched helper/runtime.
- [x] E2 — provision the 311 MB CEF runtime bundle on seats; flip the default engine to CEF (Servo stays fallback). **Packaged default closure 2026-07-14:** the Fedora 44 full RPM provisions the pinned CEF runtime through `/usr/libexec/mackesd/install-cef-runtime` and now gates activation on both direct render smoke and shell-equivalent wire smoke via `/usr/libexec/mackesd/cef-verify`. On `.15`, the installed runtime setup passed both gates, the installed Gmail verifier run delivered painted frames over the same helper wire the shell consumes, and the installed shell is active/running with `Delegate=yes` and `NRestarts=0`. The shell selector already prefers CEF only when `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef` plus the complete `/opt/mde/cef` runtime are present; Servo remains the fallback when that gate is absent.
  **Live CEF input closure 2026-07-15:** farm `.50` shell-equivalent `cef-verify`
  proved the real helper wire now carries painted frames plus pointer, keydown,
  and text insertion into a focused page input. The root cause for typed text was
  a bad `KEYEVENT_CHAR` virtual-key field: the helper used Unicode codepoint
  `109` as `windows_key_code`, which CEF treats as subtract/minus rather than
  the `M` key. The fixed verifier sequence is
  `KeyDown -> Text(KEYEVENT_CHAR) -> KeyUp`, ending with page text
  `P:1 K:1 T:m`, final title `mde-browser-verify-p1-k1-tm`, and
  `VERIFY RESULT=PASS display/load/input response observed over the wire`. Farm
  evidence: `.170` `browser-cef-key-fix` passed the CEF key/input tests, `.90`
  `browser-verify-client` passed the verifier data-URL tests, and `.50`
  `browser-verify-cef` passed the live page-text helper-wire test. The
  diagnostic-only one-shot `render-once` page-text scrape still returns empty on
  some runs and is not the operational proof path; the shell wire verifier is.
  **Live Servo fallback input closure 2026-07-15:** the same verifier now has an
  engine-neutral page-text observation mode for helpers that do not publish title
  changes. On BigBoy `.130`, `mde-web-preview` built in slot
  `browser-servo-live-verify` with `CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0`; `cef-verify` then
  spawned the Servo helper through the exact `WebSession` socket path and proved
  real nav, six `1280x800` paint frames, pointer delivery, keydown delivery, and
  text insertion into a focused input. Page text advanced from
  `MDE_STATUS P:0 K:0 T:` to `MDE_STATUS P:1 K:1 T:m`, and the verifier returned
  `VERIFY RESULT=PASS display/load/input handlers fired over the wire`. This
  closes the prior "Servo does not respond" proof gap at the helper-wire level;
  a physical seat eyes-on pass is still useful but no longer the only evidence.
  **Shell-level CEF UI input smoke 2026-07-15:** the existing env-gated
  `cef_live_browser_ui` farm smoke now serves an input-probe page, loads it
  through the real `WebState`/`web_panel` Browser UI path, clicks the page canvas,
  sends keydown/text/keyup through egui events, and waits for both title and
  bounded page-text proof (`P:1 K:1 T:m`) over the same helper wire. Farm `.50`
  ran it against the warm pinned CEF 149 runtime at `$HOME/mde-cef-active` with a
  freshly built `mde-web-cef`/renderer bridge and passed; `.90` also passed the
  deterministic fake-helper shell input regression, BigBoy `.130` passed the
  focused shell compile/test filter, and `.170` passed rustfmt. This closes the
  gap between helper-wire input proof and the actual shell Browser panel path.
  **Shell-level Servo fallback UI input smoke 2026-07-15:** env-gated
  `servo_live_browser_ui` now builds/runs real `mde-web-preview`, loads the same
  Browser UI input-probe through `WebState`/`web_panel`, proves local HTTP
  fetch/render, page canvas focus, and pointer/key/text page response via repeated
  bounded page-text polling (`P:1 K:1 T:m`). The smoke derives the click point
  from the actual tessellated page texture instead of a fixed panel coordinate, so
  Servo's stricter hit testing lands inside the probe content; page-text waits now
  issue fresh request IDs until the queued input has reached the DOM. BigBoy `.130`
  slot `browser-servo-ui-live` passed the cold Servo helper build plus live Browser
  UI smoke; `.50` reran and passed CEF live UI; `.90` passed deterministic shell
  input; `.170` passed rustfmt.
  **Farm/dev CEF subprocess re-exec hardening 2026-07-15:** a live rerun
  against per-slot debug helpers found Chromium's zygote could not `execvp` a
  `$HOME` renderer path after the sandbox pivot even though the packaged
  `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer` path is valid. The renderer now
  hands `/proc/self/exe` to CEF for non-packaged bridge overrides while
  preserving the real `/usr/libexec/...` path for packaged builds. Farm
  evidence: `.90` passed the focused renderer subprocess-path unit test, `.170`
  passed CEF rustfmt, `.50` rebuilt `mde-web-cef` plus
  `mde-web-cef-renderer` and passed the CEF shell live Browser UI smoke, and
  `.50` rebuilt the current Servo helper and passed the Servo shell live Browser
  UI smoke. This supersedes the stale shared-helper Servo failure observed
  earlier in the same investigation.
  **Live `.15` fixed-browser deploy 2026-07-15:** BigBoy's Fedora 44 builder
  `.131` cut a seat-safe full RPM from commit `d7c7ec46` with canonical
  `drm,live-helper,live-vdi,media-mpv` features. The RPM payload includes
  `/usr/bin/mde-shell-egui`, `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview`,
  and `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer`; dependency inspection shows
  F44/ffmpeg-8 sonames (`libavcodec.so.62`, `libswresample.so.6`,
  `libswscale.so.9`) and SHA-256
  `61f8cb0325715a9a889c825a663510d2d9e5f51d11334d4069cce2f6536e31bd`.
  Installed on `.15` with `rpm -Uvh --replacepkgs --force --nosignature` because
  the version/release stayed `12.0.0-1`; the new binaries have July 15 mtimes
  and the shell restarted active with `NRestarts=0`. Post-deploy verification:
  send-tab inbox scan found zero records, installed CEF `cef-verify` passed
  display/load, installed CEF input verify reached final title
  `mde-browser-verify-p1-k1-tm` and `VERIFY RESULT=PASS`, and installed Servo
  input verify reached page text `P:1 K:1 T:m` and `VERIFY RESULT=PASS`.
  Payload validation passed; at that point the known 109 MiB monolithic RPM size
  gate still failed for the GitHub Pages channel and remained a packaging
  split/channel issue, not a seat deploy blocker. That packaging issue is now
  addressed by the Browser RPM split slice below.
  **Browser RPM split source/tooling slice 2026-07-15:** the base `magic-mesh`
  RPM no longer owns Browser helper/runtime payload. A co-installable
  `magic-mesh-browser` RPM now owns Servo (`mde-web-preview`), the CEF helper
  and renderer, `cef-verify`, CEF runtime setup, Widevine/model provisioning,
  Browser SELinux policy loaders, WebExtensions lab-smoke assets, and adblock
  seeds. Base weak-recommends `magic-mesh-browser`; Browser requires the base
  package plus the Browser runtime/manual graphics dependency set. Base role
  provisioning no longer enables optional Browser setup units that are absent
  when the Browser package is not installed. Both RPM cut paths now build the
  split artifacts and size-gate them. Payload verification understands base,
  browser, and server shapes. Farm evidence: `.170`
  `browser-rpm-split-verify` passed 8/8 Browser payload ownership tests; `.90`
  `browser-rpm-split-builder` passed the Fedora builder regression; BigBoy
  `.130` `browser-rpm-split-check` passed `cargo check -p mackesd --features
  async-services --lib`; `.170` `browser-rpm-split-fmt2` passed rustfmt; `.50`
  `browser-rpm-split-role2` passed 27/27 role-provision tests. BigBoy `.130`
  `browser-rpm-split-full` cut `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm`
  at 69.9 MiB, SHA-256
  `a831d1014db25b1005f7bc4caefa8b8b2998efdf1a1b6598ea50b0dc4f0846d6`, and
  `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-browser-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` at
  39.0 MiB, SHA-256
  `96dd4637c3f04457be0dff5ea32020abee2117872c82d8b2d2945475dd519df7`; both
  passed the 90 MiB cut gate and payload inspection. The base RPM grep found no
  Browser helper/runtime files, while the Browser RPM owns the expected CEF,
  Servo, verifier, Widevine/model, SELinux, and adblock files. RPM dependency
  inspection confirms base recommends `magic-mesh-browser`; Browser requires
  `magic-mesh`, `bzip2`, and the Browser manual graphics stack. The base RPM
  still has automatic ELF soname requirements for shell-linked graphics/font
  libraries, which is expected for the DRM shell and is not the Browser payload
  split boundary.
  **Browser split F44 deploy proof 2026-07-15:** the Fedora 44 container lane
  (`install-helpers/build-rpm-fedora43.sh 44`) cut split RPMs under
  `target-f43/generate-rpm/`: base `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` at
  70.0 MiB / 73,349,769 bytes, SHA-256
  `b2e26d1aa557a74631d6a5a27da33904990c2da3a7eab5776b3aeff5d1b3ac95`, and
  Browser `magic-mesh-browser-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` at 39.1 MiB /
  41,012,002 bytes, SHA-256
  `d4e828adcb3f1b494bf9d664d86b4876b13f44fdf5112a1386d5de6b6816a44f`. Both
  passed `verify-rpm-payload.sh payload`, the base RPM grep found no Browser
  helper/runtime payload, and the Browser RPM owned the expected CEF, Servo,
  verifier, Widevine/model, SELinux, and adblock files. Live `.15`
  (`Basement-Test-Workstation`, Fedora 44) copied both RPMs to
  `/tmp/magic-mesh-browser-split/`, hash-verified them, passed
  `rpm -Uvh --test --replacepkgs --force --nosignature` with both packages in
  one transaction, then installed both packages together. Post-install state:
  `rpm -q magic-mesh magic-mesh-browser` returned `12.0.0-1` for both packages;
  `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef`, `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview`, and
  `/usr/libexec/mackesd/cef-verify` were installed; `/opt/mde/cef` still points
  at the pinned CEF 149 runtime; `/mnt/mesh-storage/browser-send-tab` contained
  zero JSON records; and no leftover `mde-web-cef`, `mde-web-preview`, or
  `cef-verify` processes remained after probes. Installed verifier proof: the
  CEF input verifier command reached `VERIFY RESULT=PASS`, CEF final title
  `mde-browser-verify-p1-k1-tm`, and 4 painted `1280x800` frames; the same
  verifier against `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview` reached `VERIFY RESULT=PASS` and
  Servo page text `P:1 K:1 T:m` with 4 painted `1280x800` frames. After the
  package install, `mde-shell-egui.service` was started on the live DRM seat and
  reported active/running with `ExecCondition` success, `NRestarts=0`, and shell
  version `12.0.0 "Quazar" · 7eedfe7d · 2026-07-15 · dev`.
  **CEF private runtime cache hardening 2026-07-15:** live `.15` journal review
  found CEF/fontconfig falling back to `/.cache/fontconfig` inside the
  no-home sandbox (`Fontconfig error: No writable cache directories`). The CEF
  renderer now creates a private tmpfs-backed runtime tree under
  `/tmp/mde-web-cef/{home,cache,config,runtime}` after the sandbox pivot, exports
  `HOME`, `XDG_CACHE_HOME`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`, and `TMPDIR`
  to that tree before `cef_initialize`, and emits
  `CEF_PRIVATE_RUNTIME_ENV ... tmpfs=1` for live verification. This preserves the
  "no host home/keys/mesh state" sandbox invariant while giving fontconfig and
  Chromium writable ephemeral cache/config dirs. Farm evidence: BigBoy `.130`
  slot `browser-cef-private-full2` passed the full CEF crate gate (150 lib tests,
  8 renderer tests, 2 bridge-handoff tests, 1 protocol golden); `.50`
  `browser-cef-private-renderer2` passed the focused private-runtime renderer
  tests; `.90` `browser-cef-private-sandbox` passed the sandbox writable-tmpfs
  regression; `.170` `browser-cef-private-fmt2` passed CEF rustfmt; direct `.50`
  live smoke against `$HOME/mde-cef-active` printed `CEF_PRIVATE_RUNTIME_ENV`,
  emitted `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... view=800x600`, and did not emit the old
  fontconfig writable-cache error. F44 deploy proof: the `.131` builder cut
  `/root/mcnf-release-artifacts/magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` at 2026-07-15
  07:46 with SHA-256
  `8538648efc28deb003b05ea65139ea7ccaddc5dae548b455ffa78af8740bfaf4`
  and F44 ffmpeg-8 sonames (`libavcodec.so.62`, `libswresample.so.6`,
  `libswscale.so.9`). Installed on `.15` with `rpm -Uvh --replacepkgs --force
  --nosignature`; `mde-shell-egui.service` restarted active. Installed
  `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef render-once` printed `CEF_PRIVATE_RUNTIME_ENV` and
  `CEF_BROWSER_PAINT_READY ... view=800x600`, with no
  "Fontconfig error: No writable cache directories"; installed `cef-verify`
  returned `VERIFY RESULT=PASS` with nav/title/frame events over the helper
  wire. Current shell-input reruns passed too: `.50` env-enabled
  `cef_live_browser_ui_renders_and_operates_a_real_page_when_farm_smoke_is_enabled`
  used freshly built `mde-web-cef`/`mde-web-cef-renderer` against
  `$HOME/mde-cef-active`, and `.90`
  `servo_live_browser_ui_renders_and_operates_a_real_page_when_farm_smoke_is_enabled`
  used a freshly built `mde-web-preview`; both drove pointer/key/text through
  `WebState`/`web_panel`. D-Bus warnings remain visible in the headless sandbox
  and did not block paint.
  **CEF live OS-confinement proof 2026-07-15:** drained the outstanding
  live-runtime proof gap from `docs/THREAT_MODEL.md` §10.4 on live `.15`
  (`Basement-Test-Workstation`, Fedora 44) with the installed split Browser
  RPMs. A held real `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef tab` launched against `/opt/mde/cef`
  and logged `CEF_OS_SANDBOX applied=1 ... home_visible=0 seccomp=1
  caps_dropped=1`, `CEF_PRIVATE_RUNTIME_ENV ... tmpfs=1`, `CEF_INITIALIZE_OK`,
  plus zygote/network-utility bridge starts. Host `/proc` inspection showed the
  actual sandboxed CEF browser child and all observed Chromium zygote/utility
  descendants had `NoNewPrivs: 1`, `Seccomp: 2`, zero
  `CapPrm`/`CapEff`/`CapBnd`, and inherited the same sandbox namespaces. Their
  `/proc/<pid>/root` view exposed the CEF runtime, renderer bridge, and private
  `/tmp/mde-web-cef/{home,cache}` tree, while `/home`, `/root`, `/etc/nebula`,
  `/etc/mackesd`, `/mnt/mesh-storage`, `/run/user/1000/bus`, and
  `/run/dbus/system_bus_socket` were absent. The held probe was killed by
  process group afterward; no installed CEF/verifier processes remained, and
  `/mnt/mesh-storage/browser-send-tab` stayed at zero JSON records. The
  remaining shell-spawned cgroup cap proof from the delegated DRM unit path was
  closed by the Browser delegated cgroup-cap slice below.
  **Browser SELinux loader/Enforcing closure 2026-07-15:** live `.15` showed the
  split Browser RPM had been enabling but not starting Browser setup units after
  install, and the SELinux loader scripts were building hyphenated output stems
  (`mde-web-cef`, `mde-web-preview`) despite underscore module declarations
  (`mde_web_cef`, `mde_web_preview`). The Browser RPM now queues
  `systemctl start --no-block $BROWSER_UNITS`, hard-requires
  `selinux-policy-devel` and `checkpolicy`, removes the underscore-named
  modules on uninstall, and stages hyphenated policy sources under underscore
  module stems before the SELinux devel Makefile. Live proof on `.15` under
  `getenforce=Enforcing`: `semodule -l` showed `mde_web_cef` and
  `mde_web_preview`; `/usr/bin/mde-web-cef` and
  `/usr/libexec/mackesd/mde-web-cef-renderer` had `mde_web_cef_exec_t`, and
  `/usr/bin/mde-web-preview` had `mde_web_preview_exec_t`. Fresh-root installed
  verifier runs passed for both engines with `MDE_BROWSER_VERIFY_INPUT=1`:
  CEF produced final title `mde-browser-verify-p1-k1-tm`, 4 painted
  `1280x800` frames, pointer/key/text input, and no final-window AVCs; Servo
  produced 4 painted `1280x800` frames plus final page text `P:1 K:1 T:m`,
  and no final-window AVCs. No CEF/Servo/verifier processes remained after
  cleanup. The delegated DRM-unit cgroup cap proof was closed by the next
  slice.
  **Browser delegated cgroup-cap closure 2026-07-15:** live `.15` cgroup
  investigation showed `Delegate=yes` alone was insufficient because systemd
  kept the shell process in the service root cgroup, and cgroup v2 refuses
  enabling domain controllers (`+memory +cpu`) while that root contains
  processes. The DRM shell unit now uses `DelegateSubgroup=shell` and exports
  `MDE_WEB_SANDBOX_DELEGATE_SUBGROUP=shell`; CEF's shared `mde-web-sandbox`
  crate and Servo's sandbox copy retarget from that subgroup to the delegated
  parent service root before creating capped helper leaves. Farm evidence:
  `.50` passed `mde-web-sandbox` cgroup-base tests plus rustfmt; BigBoy `.130`
  passed `mde-web-preview` cgroup-base tests plus rustfmt; `.90` passed the
  `mackesd` role-provision guard plus rustfmt; BigBoy `.130` cut fresh Fedora
  44 split RPMs in the container lane, with base
  `magic-mesh-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` at 73,349,945 bytes / SHA-256
  `699e46b7b328002a8d616f7399eaced017dd85327bc87056c2c09d668964e013` and
  Browser `magic-mesh-browser-12.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm` at 40,919,203 bytes /
  SHA-256
  `eca967aa893f7cbd6d38bba512f8c6e9ac9579af8c3aecf70a61de215359408e`;
  both passed the 90 MiB package size gate. Live `.15` deploy proof:
  `rpm -Uvh --test --replacepkgs --force --nosignature` passed, both RPMs
  installed, `mde-shell-egui.service` restarted active with `NRestarts=0`,
  `DelegateSubgroup=shell`, and shell PID `725298` in
  `/system.slice/mde-shell-egui.service/shell` while the service root
  `cgroup.procs` was empty. A transient delegated CEF proof created
  `/system.slice/mde-browser-cgroup-proof-cef.service/mde-web-cef-...` with
  `memory.max=2147483648`, `cpu.max=200000 100000`, no
  `mde-web-sandbox: cgroup limits not applied` warning, and verifier
  `VERIFY RESULT=PASS` with final title `mde-browser-verify-p1-k1-tm` plus 4
  painted `1280x800` frames. A matching Servo proof created
  `/system.slice/mde-browser-cgroup-proof-servo.service/mde-web-preview-...`
  with `memory.max=1073741824`, `cpu.max=80000 100000`, no degraded-cgroup
  warning, and verifier `VERIFY RESULT=PASS` with final page text
  `P:1 K:1 T:m`. Post-proof cleanup found no exact CEF/Servo/verifier
  processes, no lingering helper cgroups under `system.slice`, and no AVC or
  USER_AVC matches since the deploy window.
- [x] E3 — WebExtensions decision: ship native adblock/passkeys/reader, skip WebExtensions for v1. **V1 policy closure 2026-07-14:** production CEF launches now keep WebExtensions disabled even when a curated registry exists; `mde-web-cef` reports `CEF_EXTENSIONS_SKIPPED_V1 ... reason=native_adblock_passkeys_reader_ship_for_v1` and only the lab smoke path can opt back in with `MDE_CEF_WEBEXTENSIONS_LAB`. Browser chrome states the supported v1 path: native blocker, passkeys, reader mode, userscripts, and site styles.
- [>] C0–C5 — `web/chrome_ui/` rebuild: browser-local light `Visuals` scope · `tabs/toolbar/omnibox/menu/ntp` · Roboto chrome font · retire MENUBAR-ALL for this surface. **C0 slice 2026-07-14:** introduced `web/chrome_ui/`, wrapped the Browser tab/toolbar/bookmarks/find rows in a browser-local light visuals/font scope, removed the default shared MENUBAR-ALL top-strip call from `web_panel`, and moved the existing state-gated Browser command tree under the toolbar's Chrome-style menu button. The menubar coverage backstop now records Browser as a deliberate first-party chrome surface instead of a shared-bar surface. **Material state-layer slice 2026-07-15:** `chrome_ui` now owns Browser-local Material-style surface/container/outline/primary roles plus hover/focus/press state-layer blending; the tab strip, tab search, new-tab buttons, bookmarks row, find row, suggestions, compact tab menus, and Chrome toolbar menu use those Browser-local tokens instead of shared shell fills/text where they are part of first-party browser chrome. **Overlay token slice 2026-07-15:** Browser drawers and page-top permission/before-unload/password/offline/capture notices use Browser-local Material surface/text/status tokens; capture annotation/page pixels intentionally remain unchanged. **Body/interstitial token slice 2026-07-15:** Browser new-tab dashboard text, HTTP insecure prompt, loading/gated empty body, crash body, certificate error, safe-browsing, and managed-policy interstitials now use Browser-local Material text/status tokens instead of shared shell text colors; regression coverage locks the certificate interstitial paint colors and the new-tab dashboard path. **Omnibox Material text-run slice 2026-07-15:** `chrome_ui` now owns the omnibox font/format helpers, the unfocused URL-emphasis readout uses Browser Material dim/primary text runs instead of shared shell `Style::TEXT_DIM/TEXT_STRONG`, and focused inline completion paints with Browser-local dim text;
